rule: API documentation and payloads should be semantically accurate, future-proof, and include human-readable fields. Avoid implementation-specific details in docs, ensure required fields are never silently empty, and use consistent PATCH semantics.
trigger: When writing or modifying API schemas, documentation, PATCH endpoints, or audit event payloads
rationale: Implementation-specific docs break when internals change. Silently empty required fields confuse API consumers. Missing human-readable names force users to do extra lookups. Reviewers consistently flag these across nucleus, apps, and extensions.
mode_rationale: General convention about API payload/doc quality, not a multi-step procedure

rule: When a method encounters an unsupported type, impossible state, or unresolvable required field, throw an explicit error rather than silently returning empty/no-op results or continuing execution.
trigger: When handling unexpected types in match/switch expressions, resolving required fields that may be absent, or encountering states that should be impossible
rationale: Silent returns mask bugs and make debugging harder. Three instances across two PRs from different reviewers show this is a strongly-held team convention. Reviewers explicitly asked for throws over silent skips.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence coding convention: throw instead of silently returning

rule: Sorting, pagination, and relational lookups should be performed in database queries, not in application memory. Avoid N+1 query patterns by combining lookups into single query pipelines.
trigger: When implementing list endpoints with sorting or pagination, joining related entities, or fetching collections that are filtered or sorted after retrieval
rationale: In-memory sorting of unbounded collections causes performance degradation at scale. N+1 queries multiply latency. Reviewers explicitly asked to push sorting/paging into persister methods and combine lookups into single query pipelines.
mode_rationale: Performance convention: prefer DB-side operations over in-memory

rule: Routine operational messages should use DEBUG, not INFO. Warnings should only fire on genuinely unexpected conditions, and log messages must accurately reflect the condition that triggers them.
trigger: When adding or modifying log statements, especially when choosing between INFO/DEBUG/WARN levels or writing log message text
rationale: Overly verbose INFO logs create noise in production. Warnings on legitimate conditions cause alert fatigue. Inaccurate log messages mislead on-call engineers. Reviewers enforce logging discipline across multiple PRs.
mode_rationale: Simple style rule about choosing correct log levels

rule: When code looks up an entity that may not exist (deleted, archived, never created), handle the absence explicitly — clean up stale references, provide fallback behavior, or defend against null returns.
trigger: When fetching entities by ID that could be deleted, archived, or not yet created, or when processing collections that reference other entities
rationale: Unhandled absent entities cause NullPointerExceptions or stale data in indexes. Reviewers flagged cases where archived apps remained in search indexes and where missing instances or commits had no fallback handling.
mode_rationale: Defensive coding convention for null/missing entity handling

rule: TODOs must reference tracked tickets. Forward-looking assumptions must be documented inline. Interface-layer docs must not reference implementation details from other modules.
trigger: When adding TODO comments, writing docstrings on interface-layer classes, or making assumptions about current system behavior that may change
rationale: Untracked TODOs are never resolved. Undocumented assumptions become invisible tech debt. Cross-module implementation details in interface docs create coupling and break when internals change.
mode_rationale: Convention about TODO format and doc boundaries, not a workflow

rule: Error messages shown to users should explain the probable cause in plain language and suggest resolution steps. Don't surface raw internal errors.
trigger: When writing error messages that will be displayed in the UI or returned in user-facing API responses
rationale: Raw internal errors confuse users and generate support tickets. Reviewers asked for messages like 'User hasn't logged in yet' instead of technical errors, so users can self-resolve.
mode_rationale: Single guideline: error messages should explain cause and suggest resolution

rule: Fields required for operation should be non-optional types. When fields are nullable at creation but resolved at runtime, resolve and persist them before execution to ensure reproducibility.
trigger: When defining case classes or data models where fields are Optional but always expected to have values at execution time
rationale: Optional types for required fields push validation to every call site. Unresolved nullable fields at execution time break reproducibility. Reviewer asked to make non-optional fields actually non-optional in case classes and resolve all such fields before launching.
mode_rationale: Type design convention about nullability and required fields

rule: When introducing new allowed behaviors or wrapping service calls in NonFatal recovery, add explicit tests for those specific scenarios.
trigger: When adding new state transitions, changing allowed behaviors between entities, or adding error recovery wrappers around service calls
rationale: New behaviors without tests are invisible regressions waiting to happen. Reviewers specifically asked for tests covering new allowed version transitions and fallback behavior when taxonomy service fails.
mode_rationale: Testing convention: cover new behaviors with explicit tests

rule: Variable and map names must precisely describe their key-value semantics; rename when the actual key type differs from what the name implies (e.g., containerName vs imageName).
mode_rationale: Naming convention: names must match actual key-value semantics

rule: Do not match on null in Scala code; use Option types and idiomatic Scala constructs instead of null checks carried over from Java interop.
mode_rationale: Language idiom rule: use Option instead of null checks

rule: Move computations inside the conditional branch where their result is actually used, rather than computing eagerly outside the branch.
mode_rationale: Simple performance convention about lazy evaluation placement

rule: Extract the result of a method call into a variable when it is used multiple times; use pattern matching on the extracted value instead of calling the method again.
mode_rationale: Convention: extract repeated method calls into a variable

rule: Add inline comments explaining the purpose and meaning of parameters or expressions when the code is non-obvious, especially for future-proofed or indirectly mapped values.
Remove all debugging statements (print, log, temporary outputs) from production code before submitting a PR for review.
mode_rationale: Hygiene rule: no debug prints in production code

rule: When testing new fields or computed values, assert that the values are correct, not just that they exist or are non-null.
mode_rationale: Testing convention: assert correctness, not just non-null

rule: When fixing or updating logic in one code path, search for and apply the same change to all analogous code paths in the codebase.
mode_rationale: Convention: propagate fixes to all analogous code paths

rule: Add comments explaining behavioral invariants and design decisions (e.g., why a value is not updated once set) so future developers understand the intent.
mode_rationale: Documentation convention about commenting design decisions

rule: Keep test code inline and straightforward; do not extract test logic into separate helper functions unless it is reused across multiple test cases.
mode_rationale: Testing style rule: keep tests inline unless reused

rule: Use non-optional collection types (e.g., Map instead of Option[Map]) with empty defaults unless None carries distinct semantic meaning different from an empty collection.
mode_rationale: Type design convention: use empty collections over Option[Collection]

rule: When reading optional collection fields from external sources (databases, APIs), convert them to non-optional with getOrElse(empty) at the domain boundary rather than propagating Option throughout the domain model.
mode_rationale: Boundary convention: normalize optionals at the edge

rule: Replace deeply nested map/flatMap/match chains with for-comprehensions for readability, especially when chaining multiple Optional value extractions.
mode_rationale: Scala readability convention for chained Optional extractions

rule: Never call .get on Option types; use flatMap, map, getOrElse, or pattern matching to safely handle the None case.
mode_rationale: Safety rule: always handle None case idiomatically

rule: When transforming a Map's entries, call .map directly on the Map with a partial function instead of converting to a List first with .toList.map.
mode_rationale: Scala idiom: avoid unnecessary .toList before .map on Maps

rule: When the same literal value is used in multiple places within a file, extract it into a named constant defined at module or function scope to avoid duplication and ease future changes.
mode_rationale: DRY convention: named constants over repeated literals

rule: Always include a descriptive label or title in print and log statements so that output can be easily identified when debugging.
mode_rationale: Logging convention: always include descriptive labels in output

rule: API calls in the frontend must include error handling that surfaces feedback to the user (such as an error toast) when the call fails, rather than failing silently.
mode_rationale: Frontend convention: show error toast on API failure

rule: When the same computed or literal value appears multiple times in a function, extract it into a named variable to avoid repetition and improve readability.
mode_rationale: DRY convention: extract repeated computed values

rule: When styling UI components, prefer using theme helpers and design tokens over hardcoded values so that styles remain consistent and centrally maintainable.
mode_rationale: Styling convention: use design tokens over hardcoded values

rule: Use a consistent import style for shared dependencies (e.g., React) across all files in a module to maintain codebase uniformity.
mode_rationale: Style rule: uniform import conventions per module

rule: Use theme helper tokens and design system constants for styling values (colors, font sizes, spacing) instead of hardcoding raw pixel or hex values.
mode_rationale: Styling convention: design system tokens over raw values

rule: Control font-family at the global/theme level rather than setting it in individual component styles, so font changes (e.g., during a visual refresh) propagate automatically.
mode_rationale: Architecture convention: font-family at theme level only

rule: Remove commented-out code from test scripts and production files to improve legibility and reduce confusion during triage.
Before creating new test fixture files, check if an equivalent fixture already exists in the test suite and reuse it to avoid duplication.
mode_rationale: Testing convention: check for existing fixtures before creating new ones

rule: When adding new API-based test steps to a feature file, check whether they can replace existing UI-based or duplicated steps in the same file to reduce redundancy.
mode_rationale: Testing convention: consolidate duplicate step definitions

rule: Do not add retry logic for operations that are already retried at a lower layer, as this compounds retry attempts and can cause excessive delays or cascading failures.
mode_rationale: Error handling convention: avoid compounding retries across layers

rule: When a code block is repeated across multiple call sites, extract it into a shared utility function to reduce duplication and improve maintainability.
mode_rationale: DRY convention: shared utility for repeated code blocks

rule: Keep retry counts and backoff configurations consistent across the codebase; when a standard exists (e.g., 5 retries), new retry policies should match unless there is a documented reason to differ.
mode_rationale: Convention: match existing retry counts unless documented otherwise

rule: When introducing a new shared policy, utility, or abstraction, migrate existing callers that should use it rather than leaving them on the old approach.
mode_rationale: Convention: migrate existing callers when introducing new shared abstractions

rule: Use specific, descriptive function names that convey intent (e.g., createStatusMismatchException) rather than generic names (e.g., createException) especially when multiple overloads exist.
Use a query string library (e.g., qs) to build URL search parameters instead of complex manual string interpolation, which is error-prone and hard to read.
mode_rationale: Convention: use qs library over manual string interpolation for URLs

rule: Add initialization logging to services so that their deployment placement (which pod/node they run on) can be verified, especially when services are moved between modules.
mode_rationale: Logging convention: log init for deployment verification

rule: Avoid hardcoding validation rules for third-party models and endpoints that change frequently; prefer open-ended validation or keep the list externally configurable.
mode_rationale: API design convention: keep third-party validation configurable

rule: When modifying dependency injection bindings (e.g., adding @Named annotations), verify that the change does not silently swap the implementation injected into consumers, and test that the affected component still behaves correctly.
mode_rationale: Convention: verify DI binding changes don't silently swap implementations

rule: When a component fetches data on mount, initialize the loading state to true immediately (or before the first render) to prevent a brief flash of the error or empty state before data arrives.
mode_rationale: UI convention: initialize loading=true to prevent flash of error state

rule: Adding a new shared library or provider (e.g., React Query) to a high-level component should be discussed at the team/architecture level first to agree on conventions, key management, and integration strategy.
mode_rationale: Process convention: discuss new shared library adoption at team level first

rule: Use React Hook Form's useWatch (or equivalent) to observe form field values in child components instead of setting dynamic form items at a high level, which causes unnecessary re-renders of the entire form tree.
mode_rationale: React performance convention: useWatch over high-level dynamic form items

rule: In E2E tests, use a retry-with-timeout pattern (poll until a condition is met within a deadline) instead of a fixed sleep, so tests are both faster on success and resilient to timing variation.
mode_rationale: Testing convention: poll-until-condition instead of fixed sleep

rule: Do not embed domain-specific logic (e.g., HTML escaping for files) in a generic controller that serves multiple resource types; instead, place that logic in the appropriate domain service layer.
mode_rationale: Architecture convention: domain logic belongs in service layer, not generic controllers

rule: Before reading a stream fully into memory, check or limit its size to prevent out-of-memory kills from unexpectedly large payloads.
mode_rationale: Performance/safety convention: limit stream size before full read

rule: Before adding a new configuration flag, search for existing flags with similar intent; if one exists, extend or properly deprecate it with release notes and a removal timeline rather than creating a near-duplicate.
mode_rationale: Multi-step procedure: search for existing flags, then decide to extend or deprecate with release notes and removal timeline

rule: When replacing a configuration flag, add a release note announcing the replacement, document the migration path, and schedule removal for the next minor release so customers have time to migrate.
mode_rationale: Multi-step workflow: add release note, document migration path, schedule removal

rule: When adding a new variant of an existing configuration or test target, follow the established naming convention to keep the system predictable (e.g., if standard has a vcluster variant, the new target should too).
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention to follow established patterns

rule: After modifying API dependencies or package versions, run the project's code-generation or update scripts to ensure all downstream package.json files and generated types stay in sync.
mode_rationale: Single-action reminder to run scripts after dependency changes

rule: In E2E tests, verify the absence of an element by first confirming the page has fully loaded and then asserting the element is not present, rather than using a wait-to-not-see timeout which is slow and unreliable.
mode_rationale: Simple testing convention about how to assert element absence

rule: Use a shared noop utility function instead of inline empty arrow functions for default or placeholder callbacks to improve readability and avoid creating new function instances on every render.
mode_rationale: Simple code style rule to use shared noop instead of inline empty functions

rule: Expose service endpoints through the shared API package (@domino/api) rather than making ad-hoc HTTP calls from individual components.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to use the shared API package

rule: When introducing random delays or jitter in loops that process many items, calculate the aggregate worst-case time to ensure it remains acceptable at production scale.
mode_rationale: Single-check guideline to calculate aggregate worst-case time

rule: Name methods, classes, and variables to accurately describe their actual behavior, not a legacy or loosely related concept.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention

rule: When iterating over a collection to perform side effects (e.g., publishing events), catch errors per item inside the loop so that a failure for one item does not prevent processing the rest.
mode_rationale: Simple error-handling pattern: catch per item in loops

rule: Long-lived objects such as actors or background workers must have an explicit cleanup or shutdown mechanism to prevent memory leaks, rather than relying on implicit garbage collection.
mode_rationale: Simple best practice for long-lived objects

rule: In E2E tests, navigate to a new URL within the existing browser session instead of opening a new browser window, unless the test specifically requires a fresh session.
mode_rationale: Simple testing convention about browser session reuse

rule: In E2E tests, add an explicit wait or assertion step (e.g., 'I wait to see' or 'I wait to click') before interacting with UI elements to avoid race conditions from asynchronous rendering.
mode_rationale: Simple testing convention to wait before interacting

rule: Use clearly distinct variable and directory names in tests to avoid confusion when the same value is reused in different contexts, even if it is technically correct.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for test variables

rule: When modifying a shared or reusable component, verify that the change does not break or degrade other views that consume the same component.
When rendering data that is fetched asynchronously, guard against undefined or loading states to prevent displaying 'undefined' strings in the UI.
mode_rationale: Simple defensive coding convention for async data rendering

rule: Use existing global utility functions (e.g., getAppName) instead of reimplementing the same logic inline to ensure consistency and reduce duplication.
Components should not rely on knowing the internal implementation details of callback props (e.g., assuming a callback will never throw) when determining control flow such as success/error feedback.
mode_rationale: Simple component design principle

rule: Never use the `any` type in TypeScript; use the actual data type, a specific interface, or `unknown` when the type is not known.
Remove state variables (and their setters) that are declared but never read, as they add unnecessary complexity and can mislead future readers.
mode_rationale: Simple code cleanup convention

rule: Assign safe default values (e.g., empty array `[]`) to props or destructured parameters that may be undefined according to their type definition.
mode_rationale: Simple defensive coding convention

rule: Do not reset submitting or loading flags until all dependent async operations (including data refetches) have completed, to prevent users from operating on stale data.
mode_rationale: Simple state management rule

rule: Use feature flags (FF) for short-lived, temporary behavioral changes and configuration constants (CC) for permanent configuration choices.
mode_rationale: Simple classification guideline for FF vs CC

rule: Use a consistent approach for constructing API base URLs across the codebase (e.g., always use `window.location.origin` or never use it) rather than mixing patterns.
mode_rationale: Simple consistency convention for URL construction

rule: Use consistent term ordering and phrasing in test step definitions so that steps for similar concepts are predictable and discoverable.
mode_rationale: Simple naming/phrasing convention for test steps

rule: Keep terminology in code (function names, variable names, test steps) aligned with the current product naming conventions, updating references when product terms change.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention to match product terms

rule: Before creating new mock data or test fixtures, check for existing mock generators and data factories in the codebase and reuse them to avoid duplication.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to check for existing test fixtures before creating new ones

rule: Do not mix API version prefixes (e.g., v1 and v4) in the same endpoint path; keep versioning consistent within each API surface.
mode_rationale: Simple API design convention

rule: Prefer spec-first API design (manually defining the OpenAPI/Swagger YAML) over code-first generation, as it makes code reviews easier and provides an extra layer of validation.
mode_rationale: Simple preference for spec-first over code-first approach

rule: Design API endpoints using RESTful resource hierarchy patterns (collection/id/collection) and model capabilities as subresources rather than ad-hoc endpoints.
mode_rationale: Simple API design convention for resource paths

rule: Prefer classes over singleton objects for service-like components so they can be easily mocked and tested in unit tests.
mode_rationale: Simple design preference for testability

rule: Place each class or major type definition in its own file, named after the class, to improve discoverability and keep files small.
mode_rationale: Simple file organization convention

rule: Name modules and packages with descriptive terms that convey their intent (e.g., 'utils' or 'commons') rather than generic names like 'lib'.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for modules

rule: REST API endpoints returning collections must use a consistent wrapper format with totalCount, paginationDetails, and items array, even when pagination is not needed, so all Domino APIs share the same structure for consumers.
mode_rationale: Simple API response format convention

rule: API clients with rate limiting must be injected in singleton scope so all threads share the same instance and respect the rate limit collectively.
mode_rationale: Simple dependency injection convention

rule: When introducing deprecated function replacements, create a follow-up ticket to remove usages of the deprecated functions in a future release cycle.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to create follow-up tickets for deprecations

rule: Do not import components from another team's or domain's section directory; instead, extract shared functionality into a common reusable package to avoid unintended coupling and breaking changes.
mode_rationale: Simple import boundary rule

rule: Do not import from internal or transitive dependencies that are not explicitly listed in the package's package.json; create custom types or add the dependency explicitly instead.
mode_rationale: Simple dependency management rule

rule: When adding new API calls to a rate-limited external service, evaluate whether other services or pods share the same rate limit and could cause contention.
mode_rationale: Simple performance consideration when adding API calls

rule: API filter parameters should default to returning all results (no filter) when no filter value is specified, rather than requiring explicit opt-in to see everything.
mode_rationale: Simple API design convention for filter defaults

rule: Access other modules' services through adapter interfaces rather than taking direct dependencies on their internal implementations, preserving module boundary separation.
mode_rationale: Simple architecture convention for module boundaries

rule: Extract utility functions and helper logic out of component files into dedicated utils files to keep components focused and helpers reusable.
mode_rationale: Simple code organization convention

rule: Use existing authorization methods (e.g., canRetrieveProject) rather than implementing custom permission checks, to ensure consistent authorization rules across endpoints.
mode_rationale: Simple security convention to use existing auth methods

rule: Access authorization through service-layer APIs that bundle it with data retrieval, rather than calling lower-level authorizers directly, to ensure consistent permission enforcement.
mode_rationale: Simple architecture convention for authorization access

rule: When converting class components to functional components, wrap event handler functions in useCallback to prevent unnecessary re-renders of child components.
Use established utility functions like Ramda's pick for selecting a subset of object properties instead of writing manual property extraction logic.
mode_rationale: Simple code style preference for using established utilities

rule: Wrap expensive or derived computations in useMemo to avoid recalculating on every render when the inputs have not changed.
Always include the JIRA ticket number in TODO comments so they remain traceable and do not become orphaned technical debt.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation convention for TODO traceability

rule: When multiple functions perform related but distinct operations, their names must clearly convey the difference to avoid confusion for other developers.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention

rule: Return a spinner or empty placeholder while async data is loading rather than rendering the component with incomplete or undefined data.
mode_rationale: Simple UI convention for async data loading

rule: Do not mix analytics or tracking logic directly inside UI components; extract it into a dedicated hook or utility function to avoid duplication and keep components focused on rendering.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence separation-of-concerns guideline for UI components

rule: Extract each React component into its own file rather than defining multiple components in a single file.
Avoid using mutable object references as useEffect dependencies; extract the specific primitive value you depend on into a variable and use that as the dependency instead.
mode_rationale: Single coding convention about React hook dependencies

rule: Do not use template literal syntax when there is no string interpolation; use a plain string or direct function call instead.
Split E2E test specifications into multiple files when cumulative wait times and execution durations risk exceeding CI timeout limits.
mode_rationale: Single guideline about test file size management

rule: Use full terms or define abbreviations on first use in test descriptions and feature files; do not assume readers know domain-specific acronyms.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation convention about acronym clarity

rule: Use idiomatic empty collection expressions (e.g., List.empty or Nil in Scala) instead of constructing empty instances manually.
mode_rationale: Simple style preference for idiomatic Scala

rule: Represent fixed sets of operation types (CREATE, UPDATE, DELETE, etc.) as enums rather than raw strings to get compile-time safety and exhaustiveness checks.
mode_rationale: Single type-safety convention for operation constants

rule: When a query method filters out certain records (e.g., archived items), make the filtering behavior explicit in the method name to avoid ambiguity.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for query methods

rule: Extract shared query filter clauses (e.g., isArchived checks) into reusable variables and compose them with query-specific criteria to avoid duplicating filter logic across methods.
mode_rationale: Single DRY principle for query filter reuse

rule: In E2E tests, use explicit wait-then-act steps (e.g., wait to click) after any action that changes the visible page content, rather than immediately interacting with elements that may not yet be rendered.
mode_rationale: Single testing convention about wait-then-act patterns

rule: Remove redundant or orphaned code comments that no longer serve a purpose rather than leaving them in place.
mode_rationale: Simple code hygiene rule

rule: Delete unused code rather than leaving it in the codebase for potential future use; if follow-up is planned, reference a ticket in a comment.
Verify that test fixtures, hooks, and framework callbacks actually execute at the expected lifecycle phase; unused teardown logic that never runs is a silent bug.
mode_rationale: Single awareness guideline about test fixture execution

rule: When adding retry logic, filter on specific exception types or messages rather than catching all exceptions, to avoid masking real intermittent failures.
mode_rationale: Single error-handling convention for retry logic

rule: Session-scoped pytest fixtures must have unique names across the test suite; duplicate names with different implementations cause unpredictable behavior across modules.
mode_rationale: Single naming uniqueness rule for pytest fixtures

rule: Do not add ts-ignore or ts-expect-error directives when the type error can be resolved by providing the correct arguments or types.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to fix types instead of suppressing errors

rule: Before creating a new component or utility, check if an existing one already provides the needed functionality, even if minor adaptation is required.
mode_rationale: Single guideline to check for existing components first

rule: When updating shared configuration values like image tags, ensure the same value is updated in all repositories and charts that reference it to maintain congruence.
mode_rationale: Single awareness rule about shared config consistency

rule: Use unit tests instead of end-to-end system tests when the behavior can be verified without exercising the full application, as e2e tests are expensive to run and maintain.
Keep PRs focused on a single concern; tooling, scripts, and infrastructure changes should be in a separate PR from feature or bug fix changes for clearer change tracking.
mode_rationale: Single PR hygiene convention

rule: Services should have clear responsibility boundaries; avoid adding methods that bypass the service's core abstraction layer (e.g., direct DB queries in a search service meant to abstract over Elasticsearch).
mode_rationale: Single architecture principle about service boundaries

rule: Use string interpolation instead of string concatenation for constructing strings with dynamic values, as it improves readability.
mode_rationale: Simple style preference for string construction

rule: Every BDD/Gherkin scenario must include explicit assertion steps using 'Then' keywords that verify expected outcomes, not just action steps using 'And' or 'When'.
mode_rationale: Single BDD convention about Then steps

rule: In BDD scenarios, clicking a button or performing a UI action is a 'When' progression step, not an assertion; use 'Then' steps to verify expected text or state after actions.
mode_rationale: Single BDD keyword usage convention

rule: When a legacy template system (e.g., Scala HTML templates) is being phased out, avoid adding new content to it; instead, place new content in the replacement system (e.g., React components).
mode_rationale: Single migration-awareness guideline

rule: Use the CSS `gap` property for spacing between flex/grid children instead of margin-based approaches, and consider adding a linting rule to enforce consistent gap usage.
mode_rationale: Simple CSS layout convention

rule: Do not wrap JSX in a React Fragment (<>...</> or <Fragment>...</Fragment>) when there is only a single child element; return the child directly.
Use existing shared utility functions and hooks (e.g., date formatting helpers like `getFromNowString` from `useDate`) instead of reimplementing the same logic in new components.

When a client needs both a list of entities and a related aggregate (e.g., total count), return them together in a single API response rather than requiring a separate call to the service and database.
mode_rationale: Single API design principle about response bundling

rule: API list endpoints should default to sorting by creation timestamp in descending order (newest first) rather than having no default sort, providing a predictable and useful ordering out of the box.
mode_rationale: Single API convention for default sort order

rule: When a function returns multiple related values, define a named type or case class instead of returning a tuple, because positional access (e.g., `._1`) harms readability.
mode_rationale: Simple readability convention for return types

rule: Avoid using numeric indexes to select UI elements in E2E tests; instead, use stable selectors (data-testid, ARIA roles, or meaningful text) and file UI tickets to improve element testability when needed.
mode_rationale: Single testing convention about stable selectors

rule: Tag E2E feature files and scenarios with component tags (e.g., @projects, @dfs, @workspace) and team tags (e.g., @team-develop) so tests can be triggered selectively by PR scope and owned by the responsible team.
mode_rationale: Single test organization convention for tagging

rule: Prefer unit tests and API-level system tests over expensive E2E/UI tests for backend logic like permissions; reserve E2E tests for scenarios that genuinely require browser-level validation.
mode_rationale: Single test pyramid guideline

rule: Name test helper functions that verify conditions with an `assert_` prefix so their intent is immediately clear.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for test helpers

rule: Extract resource creation and setup logic out of test functions into separate helper functions to keep tests focused on assertions.
mode_rationale: Single guideline about test function focus

rule: A microservice's local config file should only contain settings required to bootstrap the service; all other settings must be defined in the parent platform (nucleus) so they can be overridden via central config.
mode_rationale: Single architecture rule about config location

rule: Include the exception type or error category in error/log messages so that different failure modes can be distinguished from each other.
mode_rationale: Simple error message convention

rule: Name configuration settings and variables to clearly indicate which domain entity they belong to, preventing ambiguity with similarly named concepts elsewhere.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for config settings

rule: Rely on the framework's built-in error reporting (e.g., exceptions with key names) instead of writing verbose manual detection code for missing configuration keys.
mode_rationale: Single convention to prefer framework built-ins

rule: When naming default values, include the entity they apply to so readers do not mistake them for generic or service-level defaults.
mode_rationale: Simple naming clarity rule for defaults

rule: Do not hardcode values that may need to change; extract them into configuration settings so they can be adjusted without code changes.
mode_rationale: Single configuration convention

rule: Name boolean flags with general, intent-based names like `isEnabled` rather than implementation-specific names like `isInScope` that leak internal details.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for boolean flags

rule: Error messages should clearly describe what went wrong using complete, human-readable sentences rather than terse or ambiguous phrasing.
mode_rationale: Single convention about error message readability

rule: When adding new parameters to existing methods, provide default values to maintain backward compatibility so existing callers are not broken.
mode_rationale: Single API compatibility convention

rule: Default container image references should point to the organization's own registry (e.g., quay.io/domino/) rather than upstream third-party images.
mode_rationale: Single infrastructure convention for image sources

rule: Replace magic numbers (especially z-index values, pixel sizes, and thresholds) with named constants, and add a comment explaining why the specific value was chosen.
mode_rationale: Simple code clarity rule about magic numbers

rule: Method names should accurately reflect their actual scope and behavior; if a function only works for a specific provider or context, the name should indicate that restriction.
mode_rationale: Simple naming accuracy convention

rule: Do not catch exceptions only to rethrow them without adding context or handling; either handle the exception meaningfully, wrap it in the appropriate type (e.g., Left for Either), or let it propagate naturally.
mode_rationale: Single error-handling convention about meaningful catch blocks

rule: When multiple test scenarios share identical setup steps, extract those steps into a shared fixture or Background block to reduce duplication and simplify maintenance.
mode_rationale: Single guideline about extracting shared setup, not a multi-step procedure

rule: When tests involve asynchronous operations like deletion or state transitions, consider potential race conditions and order operations to minimize timing-dependent failures.
mode_rationale: General awareness principle about ordering async operations

rule: Break tests that validate multiple independent behaviors into separate test cases so that failures can be isolated and one failing assertion does not mask others.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: one behavior per test case

rule: Tag test scenarios with the owning team's label (e.g., @team-data) so that test failures can be quickly routed to the responsible team.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence tagging convention

rule: Error messages must accurately describe the specific operation that failed; do not copy-paste error messages between handlers without updating them to reflect the actual context.
mode_rationale: Simple rule: error text must describe the actual failed operation

rule: When authorization is deliberately delegated to another layer (e.g., a proxy), add a code comment explaining the delegation and why the current layer does not perform the full check.
mode_rationale: Single convention: add a comment when delegating auth

rule: Use union types or enums to constrain string attributes (such as ARIA roles) to their set of valid values, preventing typos and invalid usage at compile time.
mode_rationale: Simple rule: use union types/enums instead of raw strings

rule: Use java.time.Instant for timestamp fields rather than DateTime or other date-time types to ensure consistency and precision across the codebase.
mode_rationale: Single type preference for timestamp fields

rule: Create shared, reusable test data factory functions in a common test utilities module instead of duplicating test object construction across individual test files.
mode_rationale: Convention to centralize test data construction

rule: Only allow service or superuser accounts to perform actions on behalf of other users; regular user requests must always use the authenticated principal's own identity to prevent impersonation risks.
mode_rationale: Single authorization rule about impersonation boundaries

rule: Name error types and utility classes based on their general scope of applicability, not after the specific feature or module where they were first introduced, so they can be reused across the codebase.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for reusable error types

rule: Prefer positive test assertions that verify expected behavior over negation tests that wait for something to not happen, as negation tests incur timeout costs on every run and may pass incorrectly if a different failure occurs.
mode_rationale: Testing style preference, single guideline

rule: When combining multiple test cases into a single scenario, add section comments that label what each block verifies so readers can quickly understand the test structure.
mode_rationale: Simple readability convention for test structure

rule: Avoid installing packages from upstream repositories during every e2e test run; prefer pre-built Docker images or cached environments to prevent flakiness from upstream outages or breaking changes.
mode_rationale: Single principle about test environment isolation

rule: Use dedicated data-test attributes with descriptive, component-scoped names (e.g., data-test="billing-tag-add-button") as stable selectors for tests instead of relying on CSS classes or DOM structure.
mode_rationale: Simple selector convention for test stability

rule: Include key entity identifiers (such as names, titles, and IDs) in log messages to make debugging easier when scanning logs for specific items.
mode_rationale: Single logging best practice

rule: Name variables and parameters to accurately reflect the data they contain; do not name a field userIds if it holds organization IDs, as misleading names cause bugs and confusion.
mode_rationale: Basic naming accuracy rule

rule: Never use a user ID where an organization ID is expected or vice versa; mixing identity types across domain boundaries introduces authorization bugs and security vulnerabilities.
mode_rationale: Single security rule about identity type boundaries

rule: Service adapter classes that wrap external APIs or infrastructure should be declared as singletons to avoid unnecessary instantiation and ensure consistent state.
mode_rationale: Simple instantiation convention for service adapters

rule: When multiple tests create the same domain objects, extract shared test data fixtures into a reusable TestUtil or test helper file rather than duplicating construction logic across test classes.
mode_rationale: Convention to centralize test data into helpers

rule: When using dictionary lookups for required mappings (e.g., test IDs, configuration keys), omit default values so that missing entries cause immediate failures rather than silently proceeding with incorrect data.
mode_rationale: Simple rule: no default values for required lookups

rule: Delete operations on persisted entities should set an archived or soft-delete flag rather than performing hard deletes, and reviewers should verify the flag is actually being set in the implementation.
mode_rationale: Single convention for delete operations on entities

rule: Test cleanup code (stopping services, deleting resources) must be placed in a try/finally block so that cleanup runs even when the test fails, preventing resource leaks in CI.
mode_rationale: Simple rule: cleanup belongs in finally blocks

rule: When managing pagination, sorting, or filter state that triggers data fetching, ensure that state changes during an in-flight request are not dropped or ignored -- do not gate state updates on a loading flag.
mode_rationale: Single principle about not gating state updates on loading flags

rule: State variables should be named to accurately reflect all the data they contain; a state object holding pagination, sorting, and filter state should not be named just 'pagination' as it misleads readers about its scope.
mode_rationale: Naming accuracy rule for state objects

rule: Do not force-fit a complex abstraction (like a generic data-fetching hook) when the resulting workarounds outweigh the benefits; prefer simpler, direct implementations when the abstraction adds more complexity than value.
mode_rationale: General design principle about abstraction tradeoffs

rule: Do not commit empty or placeholder files (e.g., empty test files, scratch files) to the repository; remove them before merging.
mode_rationale: Simple repo hygiene rule

rule: When an API uses non-obvious parameter encodings (e.g., boolean true for ascending, false for descending, undefined for none), add an inline comment documenting the mapping so future readers do not have to guess.
mode_rationale: Single convention: comment non-obvious encodings

rule: Always verify that the rowKey property in table components is bound to a truly unique field to prevent rendering bugs and key collisions.
mode_rationale: Simple rule to verify uniqueness of row keys

rule: When a code change introduces a known follow-up task that is not blocking release, file a ticket immediately to ensure it is tracked and not forgotten.
mode_rationale: Single process convention for tracking follow-ups

rule: Add unit tests for newly introduced or modified logic paths, especially when handling new data variants or edge cases.
mode_rationale: Basic testing expectation for new code paths

rule: Review IDE-generated import paths to ensure they follow the project's established import conventions before committing.
mode_rationale: Simple review step for import paths

rule: Reference defined constants instead of duplicating magic strings in test files to keep tests in sync with production code.
mode_rationale: Single convention about referencing constants

rule: Define static configuration objects (such as table column definitions) outside the render path or memoize them to avoid unnecessary recomputation on every re-render.
mode_rationale: Performance convention: hoist or memoize static config

rule: Define dependency versions in a single variable and reference it everywhere to keep related tool versions synchronized and avoid drift.
mode_rationale: Simple rule: one variable for shared version strings

rule: Avoid using --break-system-packages for pip installs; use virtual environments instead to isolate Python dependencies from the system Python.
mode_rationale: Single rule against a specific pip flag

rule: Install Python dependencies in virtual environments rather than at the system level to avoid implicit dependencies and ensure reproducibility.
mode_rationale: Simple environment isolation convention

rule: Pass only the specific data needed (e.g., a user ID) rather than an entire rich object (e.g., AuthenticatedPrincipal) to reduce coupling and narrow the interface.
Always call throwIfNotAuthorized() (or the equivalent enforcement method) after performing a permission check to ensure authorization is actually enforced, not just evaluated.
mode_rationale: Single security rule: always call the enforcement method

rule: Do not mark API fields as Optional when the enclosing context guarantees they will be present; align nullability with the actual domain invariants.
mode_rationale: API design rule about nullability alignment

rule: Use functional control flow (e.g., Either chaining or validated applicatives) to accumulate validation errors rather than throwing exceptions, so all field errors can be returned at once.
mode_rationale: Single convention for validation error handling style

rule: When storing or sending data to external services (e.g., Vault, databases), validate input length and size against the service's documented limits before making the call.
mode_rationale: Simple rule: check limits before calling external services

rule: Use the framework's built-in query parameter binding mechanisms (e.g., QueryBindable in Play) instead of manually parsing query parameters in controllers.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use built-in parameter binding

rule: Use existing domain utility methods (e.g., DominoId.fromString) for type conversions instead of reimplementing conversion logic inline.
mode_rationale: Simple rule: reuse existing conversion methods

rule: Keep class and type names concise; avoid redundant qualifiers that duplicate the package namespace, and maintain consistent suffixes (e.g., Dto for API models) across the codebase.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for class/type brevity

rule: When replacing system dependencies or tools (e.g., switching from wget to curl), verify that the new dependency is available in all supported environments to avoid backward incompatible changes.
mode_rationale: Single check: verify dependency availability across environments

rule: Add section header comments in test scenarios to organize test steps into logical groups, making test failures easier to triage.
mode_rationale: Simple readability convention for test organization

rule: When fixing flaky or failing tests, ensure the fix does not reduce coverage or hide potential bugs in the system under test; prefer more specific test inputs over broadening acceptance criteria.
mode_rationale: Single principle about preserving coverage when fixing tests

rule: Only inject feature-specific configuration (volumes, env vars, config files) into runtime environments when the corresponding feature is enabled.
mode_rationale: Simple rule: gate config injection on feature enablement

rule: Before writing system-generated configuration files, consider whether users may have made their own modifications that should not be silently overwritten.
mode_rationale: Single principle: check for user modifications before writing config

rule: Follow the target language's established naming conventions (e.g., camelCase for Scala identifiers) consistently across the codebase.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention to follow consistently

rule: Use early return (guard clause) patterns to exit functions early when preconditions are not met, instead of wrapping the entire function body in a conditional block.
mode_rationale: Single coding style preference for guard clauses

rule: Add comments explaining the rationale behind non-obvious priority or ordering logic in data structures like maps and lists, even if the code is somewhat self-documenting.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation convention for non-obvious logic

rule: When extracting a new service to break cyclic dependencies, move all related operations (not just the new one) into the extracted service to maintain coherent responsibility boundaries.
mode_rationale: Architectural guideline about service extraction responsibility

rule: Before creating a new enum or type definition, check for existing ones across modules to avoid duplication that will need later reconciliation.
mode_rationale: Simple check-before-creating convention

rule: Use an empty collection rather than Option[Collection] to represent the absence of items, as it simplifies consumer code and avoids nested unwrapping.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence type modeling preference

rule: Variable names should clearly indicate their type; avoid names that sound like booleans (e.g., prefixed with 'is' or 'has') when they hold collections or other non-boolean values.
mode_rationale: Naming convention about type-name alignment

rule: In RESTful APIs, name schema/DTO types as entities (e.g., 'NewComputeTier') not actions (e.g., 'ComputeTierCreation'), and ensure the name is appropriate for all operations that use it.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for REST schema types

rule: Use PATCH-based partial updates instead of PUT with full-object replacement to avoid concurrency issues where simultaneous requests overwrite each other's changes.
Trait fields that do not apply to all implementations should be declared as Optional rather than requiring implementations to use runtime exceptions (e.g., ???) as placeholders.
mode_rationale: Type design convention for trait fields

rule: Search and index operations must handle archived or deleted entities gracefully (e.g., by removing stale index entries) and should include tests covering those states.
mode_rationale: Single rule about handling archived entities in search

rule: Use debug log level for routine, high-frequency operations like indexing individual records; reserve info level for significant state changes or events operators need to see.
mode_rationale: Simple log level selection guideline

rule: Bulk data operations (e.g., reindexing all records) should use streaming or pagination rather than loading all entities into memory at once, to avoid memory issues at scale.
mode_rationale: Single performance convention for bulk operations

rule: Disabled UI elements should display a message explaining why they are disabled and what the user can do to resolve the situation, enabling self-service without contacting support.
mode_rationale: Single UX convention for disabled elements

rule: TODO comments in code must reference a tracking ticket or issue so that the technical debt is visible and prioritizable in the project tracker.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation convention for TODOs

rule: Variable names should accurately reflect the semantic state of the data they hold (e.g., 'mergedUser' for data that has been merged from multiple sources, not a generic name like 'user' or a misleading name like 'idpUser').
mode_rationale: Naming convention about semantic accuracy

rule: When a function only applies to a subset of input types, explicitly restrict or throw on unsupported types rather than silently returning nothing.
mode_rationale: Single error handling convention

rule: When a parameter is not actually used in a method's implementation, remove it from the method signature all the way through the call chain rather than leaving dead parameters.
Error messages that may be displayed in the UI should be written in clear, user-friendly language that describes both the problem and possible cause.
mode_rationale: Single convention about error message tone

rule: When fetching collections to sort and take a subset, push the sorting and limiting logic to the database query rather than loading all records into application memory.
mode_rationale: Single performance convention for queries

rule: When a method executes one query per item in a list (N+1 pattern), replace it with a batch query or join to avoid performance degradation as the list grows.
mode_rationale: Single performance convention about batching queries

rule: When fields are effectively required for an operation, make them non-optional in the type signature rather than leaving them as Option types that obscure which fields are truly required.
mode_rationale: Type design convention about optionality

rule: When a data model allows optional fields for flexibility but an operation requires them, validate and throw at the operation entry point rather than silently proceeding with missing data.
mode_rationale: Single validation placement guideline

rule: When code relies on an implicit assumption that may change in the future (e.g., only one active instance), add a comment documenting the assumption and what would need to change.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation convention for assumptions

rule: When a precondition (such as resolving an ID) must succeed for the operation to be meaningful, throw an error instead of logging a warning and continuing with incomplete data.
mode_rationale: Single error handling convention

rule: Push sorting, limiting, and pagination logic into database queries instead of fetching all records and processing in application memory, especially for endpoints that serve growing datasets.
mode_rationale: Single performance convention for pagination

rule: When new behavior is introduced that was previously disallowed (e.g., starting an older version stops a newer one), add explicit tests that codify and document this newly permitted behavior.
mode_rationale: Single testing convention for behavioral changes

rule: When creating a versioned record, resolve all runtime-dependent fields (e.g., environment ID, commit ID) at creation time and persist the resolved values to ensure reproducibility.
mode_rationale: Single architectural rule about versioned records

rule: Do not add validation checks that guard against states which are technically valid and do not affect correctness, as they add maintenance burden and complexity without meaningful safety benefit.
mode_rationale: Single guideline against unnecessary validation

rule: API documentation should describe behavior abstractly rather than referencing current implementation details (such as specific upsert keys or internal identifiers) that may change as the system evolves.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation convention for API docs

rule: PATCH endpoint descriptions should concisely state what happens to absent fields and call out any non-standard merge semantics (such as upsert behavior for nested collections).
mode_rationale: Single API documentation convention

rule: Method and function arguments in Scala should start with a lowercase letter, following standard Scala naming conventions.
mode_rationale: Simple Scala naming convention

rule: When transformation logic is only used in one place, prefer keeping it inline rather than extracting it into a separate method, to reduce indirection and keep the code easier to follow.
mode_rationale: Single code organization preference

rule: When changing a DTO field from required to optional, verify backward compatibility with existing consumers by running end-to-end tests before merging.
mode_rationale: Single testing convention for DTO changes

rule: When creating an actor system, always register a shutdown hook to ensure graceful shutdown, following the established pattern used elsewhere in the codebase.
mode_rationale: Single convention for actor system lifecycle

rule: Internal implementation details such as API client instances and invokers should be declared as private to prevent unintended external coupling.
mode_rationale: Simple encapsulation convention

rule: Move feature flag checks and expensive service calls inside the branch that actually needs them, so they are skipped when the result would be unused (e.g., when a list is empty).
mode_rationale: Single performance convention for conditional logic

rule: When an API schema marks fields as required, do not silently map missing values to empty strings or defaults; either ensure the values are present or make the schema reflect the actual optionality.
mode_rationale: Single API correctness convention

rule: When a dependency call is wrapped in NonFatal exception handling, add a test that verifies the caller degrades gracefully (e.g., returns a default value) when the dependency throws.
mode_rationale: Single testing convention for error paths

rule: Use None to represent 'unknown/legacy/not-applicable' and Some(Seq.empty) to represent 'intentionally empty' when modeling optional collection fields, and document the semantic distinction.
mode_rationale: Single type modeling convention for optional collections

rule: Utility and resolver methods should handle rare edge cases (such as null returns for empty repositories) defensively, especially if they may be reused in broader contexts beyond their original caller.
mode_rationale: Single defensive coding convention

rule: Log a warning when the system encounters an unexpected but non-fatal state (such as an archived project being resolved at runtime) to aid debugging and provide visibility into edge cases.
mode_rationale: Single logging convention for unexpected states

rule: Keep docstrings focused on the module where they live; do not reference cross-module implementation details (such as callers from other packages) in interface-level documentation.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation scoping convention

rule: Throw an exception for supposedly impossible cases instead of silently skipping them, so broken assumptions surface immediately at runtime.
mode_rationale: Single error handling convention

rule: Docstrings in interface/API modules should describe the contract, not reference low-level implementation details or types from other modules.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation abstraction convention

rule: Verify end-to-end behaviors like query parameter propagation through integration or e2e tests when unit tests cannot exercise the full request path.
mode_rationale: Single testing strategy guideline

rule: Log warnings only for actual service failures, not for legitimately empty results, so operators can distinguish between 'no data found' and 'upstream call failed'.
mode_rationale: Single logging convention for result vs failure

rule: When passing a potentially large list of IDs to a database $in operator, verify query size limits and ensure the target field is indexed for performance.
mode_rationale: Single performance convention for large query lists

rule: Use 'sortBy' rather than 'sortField' as the query parameter name for sort operations in REST APIs for clarity and consistency.
mode_rationale: Simple API naming convention

rule: Keep status enums for durable lifecycle states (active, expired, revoked) and represent transient or computed conditions like 'expiring soon' as separate boolean fields.
mode_rationale: Single API design convention for status enums

rule: Always include a human-readable name alongside entity IDs in audit trail events so that audit logs are meaningful without requiring additional lookups.
When accessing a field assumed to always be present (e.g., via .get on an Option), add defensive handling or document the invariant, since 'always present' assumptions can silently break at runtime.
mode_rationale: Defensive coding guideline for accessing optional fields

rule: When introducing temporary code or workarounds, add a comment with the follow-up ticket number so the cleanup does not get lost.
mode_rationale: Simple comment convention for temporary code

rule: Log messages emitted on a recurring schedule (e.g., every few seconds) should use debug log level, not info or higher, to avoid noisy production logs.
mode_rationale: Log level convention for recurring messages

rule: Use a structured logger with an appropriate log level instead of println or stdout for diagnostic messages; println writes unconditionally and creates noisy logs.
When a security-sensitive behavior (e.g., disabling SSL verification) is conditionally applied, add a unit test that verifies the behavior is only active for the intended case.
mode_rationale: Testing convention for security-conditional behavior

rule: Never leave SSL verification disabled without a tracked ticket for re-enabling it; disabling SSL opens the door to man-in-the-middle attacks and data exposure.
mode_rationale: Security convention to track SSL verification disabling

rule: When removing a behavior or skipping a code path (e.g., disabling a connection check), add a regression test that verifies the old behavior does not resurface.
mode_rationale: Testing convention when removing code paths

rule: When a conditional check encodes domain knowledge that is not self-evident from the code, add a comment explaining the business reason and its relationship to the broader system.
mode_rationale: Documentation convention for domain-specific conditionals

rule: Do not use `object` as a TypeScript type; instead, define an interface or type alias with the minimum set of required attributes.
mode_rationale: Simple TypeScript typing convention

rule: Each PR should contain only changes related to its stated purpose; unrelated cleanup or refactoring should be split into a separate PR for clarity and reviewability.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence PR hygiene guideline

rule: When naming configuration properties or variables, prefer specific names that identify exactly what the value represents rather than vague umbrella terms that could refer to multiple things in the same domain.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for configuration properties

rule: Do not infer the URL scheme (HTTP vs HTTPS) from the port number; instead, store and propagate the scheme explicitly in configuration.
mode_rationale: Simple convention about explicit URL scheme propagation

rule: When constructing URLs by concatenating a base URL and a path, normalize the result to prevent malformed URLs with double slashes or other artifacts.
mode_rationale: Defensive coding guideline for URL construction

rule: Extract repeated literal values in tests (such as URLs, IDs, or config strings) into named fixture attributes for reuse and maintainability.
mode_rationale: Test hygiene convention for repeated literals

rule: Test files should have the same code ownership (CODEOWNERS) as the source files they test, so the owning team is notified of changes.
mode_rationale: Code organization convention for CODEOWNERS alignment

rule: Name boolean parameters and flags with a verb prefix (e.g., `useJson`, `isEnabled`, `shouldRetry`) to make their intent immediately clear at call sites.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for boolean parameters

rule: Do not add dependency injection annotations (such as @Named) unless they are required to disambiguate bindings; unnecessary annotations add noise and mislead readers.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to avoid noisy DI annotations

rule: Pass only the specific values a class needs (e.g., a boolean flag) rather than injecting an entire settings or configuration object, to reduce coupling and simplify testing.
mode_rationale: Dependency injection convention to reduce coupling

rule: Do not use environment-specific values (such as internal AWS account IDs) as hardcoded fallback defaults in application code, because they mask real configuration propagation failures in other environments.
mode_rationale: Configuration convention against hardcoded env-specific values

rule: When the same conditional check (e.g., feature-flag gating) appears in multiple places, extract it into a shared helper method to reduce duplication and ensure consistency.
mode_rationale: DRY convention for repeated conditional checks

rule: Remove or reduce log statements that produce excessive output; verbose logging in hot paths degrades signal-to-noise ratio and can impact performance.
mode_rationale: Logging convention about signal-to-noise ratio

rule: Keep log messages concise by abbreviating well-known terms (e.g., 'repo' instead of 'repository'), especially when the message will include long values like URIs.
mode_rationale: Simple log message style convention

rule: When adding a feature flag or central config value, also guard the corresponding API endpoints in the same PR rather than leaving them unprotected; the exception thrown should indicate that the feature could be enabled but is not currently active.
mode_rationale: Convention to guard endpoints when adding feature flags

rule: When a feature is gated behind a config flag, add a unit test that verifies the correct behavior when the flag is disabled, not just when it is enabled.
mode_rationale: Testing convention for feature flag coverage

rule: Do not introduce direct dependencies from implementation modules to the monolith server package; use adapter classes or named injection to keep implementation code decoupled and preserve the ability to eventually remove monolith dependencies.
mode_rationale: Architecture convention to avoid direct monolith dependencies

rule: When adding a new configuration value, always provide a sensible default fallback in the application code and add the value to deployment chart configs; a missing config value should not cause the application to crash with an opaque error.
mode_rationale: Configuration convention requiring sensible defaults

rule: Mark deprecated code paths with WARN-level log messages that explain the deprecation, so that anyone encountering the log in production knows to investigate and can help confirm whether the path is still in use.
mode_rationale: Logging convention for deprecated code paths

rule: When objects are being drilled through several layers of classes to reach their point of use, prefer a dependency injection framework (e.g., Guice) over manual parameter passing to keep constructors clean and avoid coupling between layers.
mode_rationale: Architecture convention favoring DI over parameter drilling

rule: Place configuration settings in the component that owns the decision they control, not in lower-level classes that merely execute the behavior.
mode_rationale: Configuration placement convention

rule: Write test names that clearly describe the scenario and expected behavior, reading as a complete specification of what is being tested.
When a service restart triggers many concurrent operations (e.g., spawning actors for all active entities), stagger or jitter the initialization to avoid a thundering herd that spikes resource usage.
mode_rationale: Performance convention to stagger concurrent operations

rule: When renaming a class, update all related names (events, messages, methods, variables) that reference the old name to maintain naming consistency across the codebase.
mode_rationale: Naming consistency convention when renaming

rule: Make behavioral constants (e.g., batch sizes, delays, thresholds) configurable rather than hardcoded, so that risky changes can be reverted via configuration without requiring a full code rollback.
mode_rationale: Architecture convention for tuneable constants

rule: When binding service classes in a Guice injection module, scope them as Singletons to avoid creating multiple instances of stateful or expensive service objects.
mode_rationale: Guice scoping convention for service classes

rule: Use semantically correct HTTP status codes in API responses -- return 201 for resource creation and 200 for retrieval or updates -- so that clients can distinguish the type of operation that occurred.
mode_rationale: API convention for correct HTTP status codes

rule: Shared resources like ActorSystem should be injected via dependency injection rather than instantiated within individual classes, to avoid creating redundant instances and ensure proper lifecycle management.
mode_rationale: DI convention for shared resources like ActorSystem

rule: Internal service-to-service callbacks and endpoints should be defined on internal API surfaces, not exposed through public APIs, to maintain clear API boundaries and avoid leaking implementation details.
mode_rationale: API boundary convention for internal vs public endpoints

rule: Question and minimize dependencies between modules in build files; a module depending on 'server' or other large upstream modules may indicate a leaky abstraction that should be refactored.
mode_rationale: Build hygiene convention to limit module coupling

rule: Use a consistent version pinning strategy (e.g., caret ranges like ^29.7.0 vs exact pins) across all dependencies in a package manifest to avoid confusion about update behavior.
mode_rationale: Package manifest convention for version pinning

rule: Name parameters using precise domain terminology; do not call a URL path segment 'url' when it is only a path component.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for domain-accurate parameter names

rule: When wrapping or mirroring a well-known library's functionality, use the same parameter names that library uses so callers can transfer existing knowledge.
mode_rationale: Naming convention to match upstream library terminology

rule: Never silently replace or override user-provided values; at minimum emit a warning so future developers can diagnose unexpected behavior.
mode_rationale: API convention against silent value replacement

rule: When concatenating base URLs with path segments, normalize trailing and leading slashes to prevent subtle double-slash or missing-slash bugs.
mode_rationale: Defensive coding guideline for URL path joining

rule: Error messages should include the specific entity name, identifier, and relevant counts so operators can diagnose issues without additional lookups.
mode_rationale: Error message convention for diagnostics

rule: Do not wrap synchronous operations inside Future or async blocks when only the return type needs to be async; keep synchronous work outside the async boundary.
mode_rationale: Performance convention about unnecessary async wrapping

rule: When querying paginated APIs, limit the page size to the minimum number of records needed rather than fetching default or maximum page sizes.
mode_rationale: Performance convention for paginated API queries

rule: Do not use abbreviations that collide with existing domain terms; spell out or choose a distinct short form to prevent confusion.
mode_rationale: Naming convention against colliding abbreviations

rule: Derive configuration values from existing data or context rather than adding new parameters; reduce the configuration surface area.
mode_rationale: Architecture convention to minimize configuration surface

rule: Replace hardcoded sleep calls with condition-based polling or explicit state checks, since sleeps provide no guarantee the awaited condition is met.
mode_rationale: Testing convention to avoid flaky sleep-based waits

rule: Use context managers (with-blocks) when temporarily changing state (e.g., window size, config overrides) so the original state is always restored, even on failure.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use with-blocks for temporary state changes

rule: Rebase or merge from the base branch before pushing to ensure your PR does not accidentally revert changes recently landed by other engineers.
mode_rationale: Single rule: rebase/merge before pushing to avoid reverting others' work

rule: Keep UI state as close as possible to the component that uses it to avoid unnecessary prop drilling and reduce coupling between components.
mode_rationale: Single principle: keep state close to its consumer

rule: Include units or type context in variable names (e.g., widthPx, timeoutMs) instead of relying on comments to convey that information.
mode_rationale: Naming convention: include units in variable names

rule: Move static arrays, objects, and configuration data outside React components and use TypeScript's `as const` or explicit type annotations for better type inference and to avoid unnecessary re-creation on each render.

Use Ant Design's `Form.useWatch` hook instead of `formInstance.getFieldsValue()` to reactively track form field values, since `getFieldsValue()` is not reactive and will not trigger re-renders when values change.
mode_rationale: Single API preference: use Form.useWatch instead of getFieldsValue

rule: Avoid the "previous value + useEffect" pattern for tracking state changes in React; instead follow the React team's recommended approach of computing derived values during rendering or using event handlers.
mode_rationale: Single anti-pattern to avoid in React

rule: When modifying shared/base components (e.g., a common Modal wrapper), verify that the change does not break or visually alter existing consumers of that component across the codebase.
mode_rationale: Single guideline: check existing consumers when modifying shared components

rule: Before adding or re-adding code, check whether concurrent or recently merged PRs have intentionally removed or modified the same code to avoid reverting others' changes.
mode_rationale: Single practice: check concurrent PRs before adding/re-adding code

rule: When tracking previous values or derived state, always update the tracked value unconditionally rather than gating the update behind a condition, to prevent stale state and logic bugs.
mode_rationale: Single rule: update tracked values unconditionally to prevent stale state

rule: Ensure CODEOWNERS entries for test files and supporting code match the team that owns the production code being tested, so that the correct team reviews changes.
mode_rationale: Single rule: CODEOWNERS entries should match production code ownership

rule: In switch statements or if-else chains, explicitly match all known cases rather than using a meaningful value as the default/fallback; return the raw value or throw for truly unknown cases to avoid silently masking unexpected inputs.
mode_rationale: Single convention: explicitly match all known cases instead of using meaningful defaults

rule: Keep PRs focused on the task at hand; avoid including unrelated reformatting or cosmetic changes that make the diff harder to review and may require additional reviewers.
mode_rationale: Single guideline: avoid unrelated formatting changes in PRs

rule: When introducing a new function or API endpoint, add corresponding tests to verify its behavior rather than relying solely on tests for the old code path.
mode_rationale: Single rule: add tests when introducing new functions

rule: When writing files to a directory path, verify that the parent directory exists or will be created before the write operation to avoid runtime failures in edge cases.
mode_rationale: Single defensive practice: ensure parent directory exists before writing

rule: Structure boolean expressions with the positive/enabling condition first to improve readability.
mode_rationale: Single readability convention for boolean expressions

rule: Add unit tests for functions containing complex filter or predicate logic, especially when the correctness of negation or matching is non-obvious.
mode_rationale: Single rule: add unit tests for complex filter/predicate logic

rule: Keep Scaladoc and API doc comments updated to accurately describe the current behavior and parameters of public service methods.
mode_rationale: Single guideline: keep doc comments in sync with current behavior

rule: When upgrading a dependency to get new behavior, ensure the pinned version actually includes the feature you need by checking the dependency's changelog or release notes.
mode_rationale: Single practice: verify pinned version includes the needed feature

rule: If a function parameter is always passed the same constant value at every call site, remove the parameter and inline the constant.
mode_rationale: Single refactoring rule: inline constants when parameter never varies

rule: Extract repeated literal values in tests (such as IDs, strings, or configuration) into named variables or constants for reuse and clarity.
mode_rationale: Single convention: extract repeated literals in tests into named variables

rule: When fixing a styling issue that affects multiple instances of a component, apply the fix in the shared component definition rather than patching individual usages.
mode_rationale: Single rule: fix shared styling in the component definition, not per-usage

rule: Use descriptive, user-facing aria-label values that accurately name the element for screen readers; do not repurpose aria-label for test identification or use generic values like 'test'.
mode_rationale: Single accessibility convention for aria-label values

rule: Before removing or replacing data-test attributes or test selectors, verify that no existing end-to-end or integration tests depend on them.
mode_rationale: Single practice: check for dependent tests before removing selectors

rule: Use existing shared library utilities (e.g., domino-pytk) for common operations like resource fetching and data traversal instead of writing custom implementations.
