mode_rationale: Single rule: prefer existing shared utilities over custom implementations

rule: When querying for resources, use the most specific identifier available (e.g., execution ID) rather than broad identifiers (e.g., project name) to avoid ambiguity when multiple resources exist.
mode_rationale: Single principle: use the most specific identifier available for queries

rule: Remove commented-out code rather than leaving it in the codebase; if the code is not used, delete it entirely.
Do not assume build artifact directories (e.g., /dist) will always exist when configuring package links; validate or document the build dependency chain.
mode_rationale: Single rule: validate build artifact directories exist before depending on them

rule: When injecting helpers or dependencies via dynamic module loading, evaluate whether race conditions can occur between modules loading and the helpers becoming available.
Never import mock or test utilities in production code files; this causes build issues and increases bundle size. Extract shared logic into a utility in a shared package instead.
mode_rationale: Single rule: never import test utilities in production code

rule: Prefer defining default values via destructuring assignment in component props rather than using wrapper utilities like withDefaultProps.
mode_rationale: Single convention: prefer destructuring defaults over wrapper utilities

rule: Wrap function definitions passed as props or used in dependency arrays in React.useCallback() to prevent unnecessary re-creation on every render.
mode_rationale: Single performance rule: memoize callbacks passed as props

rule: When the same TypeScript type is needed across packages, define it once in a shared package and import it rather than duplicating the definition. Prefer interface with extends over type aliases for extensibility.
mode_rationale: Single rule: define shared types once in a shared package

rule: Always add explicit error handling around API calls in frontend code so failures are caught and surfaced to users rather than silently ignored.
mode_rationale: Single rule: always add error handling around frontend API calls

rule: Lambda functions should be lightweight routing layers; push complex logic (config processing, branching) into downstream services or build steps that can be tested independently.
mode_rationale: Single architecture principle: keep lambdas as thin routing layers

rule: Distinguish between null and empty collections (e.g., Seq.empty) in Scala; they are not equivalent, and confusing them causes incorrect mock behavior and subtle bugs.
mode_rationale: Single rule: distinguish null from empty collections in Scala

rule: Use human-readable, screen-reader-friendly names for aria-label values (e.g., 'chart metric selector') rather than dash-separated ID-like strings (e.g., 'chart-metric-selector').
mode_rationale: Single naming convention: use human-readable aria-label values

rule: Place commonly-used fields for filtering and sorting at the top level of a domain model, and nest type-specific definitions in dedicated sub-fields, to enable efficient querying and pagination.
mode_rationale: Single modeling convention: place filterable fields at top level

rule: Use domain-level identifiers (e.g., DominoId) in service interfaces and keep persistence-specific details (e.g., MongoDB ObjectId) confined to the persistence implementation layer.
mode_rationale: Single architecture rule: use domain IDs in service interfaces

rule: Remove annotations (such as @unused) that are no longer applicable after code changes to avoid misleading future readers.
mode_rationale: Single hygiene rule: remove annotations that no longer apply

rule: Write Storybook stories using CSF3 format instead of deprecated MDX format to stay current with the framework and avoid migration debt.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use CSF3 instead of deprecated MDX format

rule: Import from lodash-es instead of lodash to enable tree-shaking and reduce bundle size in ES module-based builds.
mode_rationale: Single import rule: use lodash-es for tree-shaking support

rule: Name functions to accurately reflect all behaviors they perform; if a function both applies and removes tags, either rename it or split it into separate single-responsibility functions.
mode_rationale: Single naming rule: function names must reflect all behaviors performed

rule: Do not add partial or poorly-implemented HATEOAS fields (like URI links) to API responses; remove unused API fields and add them properly when there is a concrete consumer need.
mode_rationale: Single API design rule: don't add partial HATEOAS fields without a consumer

rule: Create endpoints should produce deterministic results for the same payload; prefer upfront validation over silently skipping items, so two identical requests yield identical resources rather than different outcomes.
mode_rationale: Single API design principle: create endpoints should be deterministic

rule: The service layer should not reference REST API DTOs; returning API-specific types from service methods couples all internal consumers to the API definition and propagates changes unnecessarily.
mode_rationale: Single architecture rule: service layer must not reference API DTOs

rule: Place dependency injection bindings in the module they belong to (e.g., template bindings in TemplatesInjectionModule) rather than in a parent or sibling module to maintain cohesion.
mode_rationale: Single organization rule: place DI bindings in the owning module

rule: Add log statements to new or modified operations so that behavior is observable in production.
mode_rationale: Single observability rule: add logs to new operations

rule: When a file contains functions for multiple subsystems, include the subsystem name in each function name to make the scope obvious at the call site.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention: include subsystem name in shared-file functions

rule: When a function's arguments and return types do not make its scope obvious, prefix the function name with the domain or subsystem it belongs to.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention: prefix with domain when scope isn't obvious from types

rule: Remove all console.log statements before merging; use a proper logging framework instead of ad-hoc console output.
Review and remove TODO comments that are no longer valid before merging to prevent confusion for future readers.
Do not commit empty files; remove them if the contents have been moved or are no longer needed.
Place new tests in a dedicated test module rather than appending them to an unrelated test file, especially when they test a distinct feature area.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: place tests in appropriate dedicated modules

rule: Variable and parameter names should accurately describe the value they hold; rename misleading identifiers (e.g., use 'env_image' instead of 'env_uri' when the value is a container image reference).
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention: names should match what the value actually represents

rule: Functions should return their results rather than accepting mutable containers as parameters to be filled in; callers should not need to pre-allocate empty data structures.
mode_rationale: Simple design convention: prefer return values over mutating parameters

rule: When multiple tests perform the same comparison logic, extract a shared assertion function that each test calls with its specific inputs.
Break long lines into multiple lines for readability; do not let single expressions stretch beyond the project's line-length convention.
mode_rationale: Simple formatting convention: respect line-length limits

rule: Avoid using `object` as a TypeScript type; use a specific interface or type alias that describes the expected shape.
Do not ignore or suppress useEffect dependency array warnings; refactor the effect or memoize dependencies instead of disabling the lint rule.
mode_rationale: Simple React convention: fix dependencies instead of suppressing warnings

rule: Avoid using @ts-ignore in test files; fix the underlying type mismatch or add proper type declarations instead.
Separate unrelated changes (e.g., API client generation, library upgrades) into distinct PRs to keep each PR focused and easier to review and bisect in history.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: keep PRs focused on one concern

rule: When naming variables that reference external resources like S3 buckets, include enough context to disambiguate which specific resource is being referenced.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention: include enough context to identify the resource

rule: Name variables to reflect their lifecycle state (e.g., `persistedDeployment` instead of a generic name) to make the code's intent clearer at the call site.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention: reflect lifecycle state in variable names

rule: Never catch all Throwables; catch only NonFatal exceptions to avoid swallowing fatal JVM errors like OutOfMemoryError.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: catch NonFatal only, not all Throwables

rule: When deleting resources that span multiple systems, delete the database record only after external resource cleanup (e.g., Kubernetes deletes) succeeds, so the record is preserved for retry if cleanup fails.
mode_rationale: Simple ordering convention: delete DB record after external cleanup succeeds

rule: Use idiomatic Option combinators like filter, map, and getOrElse instead of verbose if/else chains when working with optional values in Scala.
mode_rationale: Simple Scala convention: use filter/map/getOrElse over if/else

rule: When hardcoding values or using temporary implementations, add a TODO comment explaining what the correct source should be and link to a tracking issue if available.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: annotate temporary code with TODO and tracking issue

rule: Follow the Databricks Scala style guide for curly brace usage in multi-line expressions and control structures.
mode_rationale: Simple style convention: follow Databricks Scala guide for braces

rule: Never use `.get` on an Option in Scala; use pattern matching, `.getOrElse`, or `.fold` with an explicit error to handle the None case safely.
mode_rationale: Simple Scala convention: never call .get on Option

rule: Add comments to non-obvious dependency injection module bindings so that readers can understand why a binding exists without tracing through the entire dependency graph.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: comment non-obvious DI bindings

rule: In Scala, catch only NonFatal exceptions rather than catching all exceptions indiscriminately, to avoid swallowing fatal JVM errors like OutOfMemoryError.
Add trace-level logging to all client/integration methods, including the method name and all parameters, to aid debugging of cross-service calls.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: add trace logging with method name and params to client methods

rule: Use library-provided case-insensitive matching methods (e.g., enumeratum's withNameInsensitiveOption) instead of manually converting strings to uppercase or lowercase for comparison.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: use library-provided methods instead of manual reimplementation

rule: Use getOrElse with an explicit error thrown for required values rather than silently proceeding with a missing or default value.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: throw explicitly for missing required values

rule: Use idiomatic Scala collection methods like isEmpty instead of manually checking collection size or comparing to empty values.
When wrapping exceptions in custom exception types, always pass the original exception as the cause parameter to the parent Exception constructor to preserve the full stack trace.
Custom exception classes should name the wrapped exception parameter 'cause' (not 'ex') and pass it to the parent Exception constructor so the JVM exception chain is properly maintained.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: name wrapped exception param 'cause' and pass to parent

rule: Avoid hardcoding behavior for a specific provider type in shared service layers; prefer generic, extensible designs that support multiple providers without code changes.
mode_rationale: Simple design convention: keep shared layers provider-agnostic

rule: Method names should accurately reflect all behaviors the method performs; if a method both creates and updates, name it createOrUpdate rather than just create.

When logging exceptions, pass the full exception object as a parameter (which includes the stack trace) and write a descriptive log message about the operation context, rather than just logging ex.getMessage.
mode_rationale: Simple logging convention: pass full exception object and describe context

rule: Name variables that hold configuration values to match or closely mirror the configuration key they are sourced from, so the mapping between code and config is immediately obvious.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention: mirror config key names in variables

rule: Keep persistence logic (e.g., database saves) in a consistent location for all code paths (create, update, delete) within a service to avoid confusion about where state changes happen.
Use separate try-catch blocks for independent operations that require different rollback logic, rather than grouping them in a single try-catch where partial failure handling becomes ambiguous.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: use separate try-catch blocks for independent rollback logic

rule: A service method for a create or update operation should encapsulate its full lifecycle (database persistence and external resource management) rather than splitting responsibilities across the controller and service layers.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: service methods own the full create/update lifecycle

rule: When using try-catch-finally patterns, ensure that fatal errors are handled distinctly from non-fatal errors so that finally blocks do not execute unintended logic after catastrophic failures.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: distinguish fatal from non-fatal in catch handling

rule: Do not place success-only logic in finally blocks; put it in the try block after the operation that must succeed, since finally runs regardless of whether an exception occurred.
Follow the project's adopted language style guide for syntax conventions such as curly brace usage, even when the code compiles without them.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: follow the project's adopted style guide

rule: When refactoring or restructuring code, verify that all existing tests are preserved or re-added rather than silently dropped.
When a method can return None/null for a resource lookup, decide explicitly whether to throw an exception or handle the absence gracefully rather than silently ignoring it.
When the logic for create and update operations diverges significantly, split them into separate methods rather than using a single method with conditional branches.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: split divergent create/update logic into separate methods

rule: Apply retry policies consistently to all external service calls (create, update, delete), not just selectively to some operations.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: apply retry policies to all external calls, not just some

rule: When adding authentication or authorization wrappers to components, update existing tests to mock the auth layer to prevent unintended API calls during testing.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: mock auth layers when testing authenticated components

rule: Before moving or deleting a file during refactoring, check whether it had unit tests and ensure those tests are migrated or updated accordingly.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: migrate tests when moving or deleting files

rule: In end-to-end and integration tests, poll on status changes instead of using fixed sleep/delay calls, which are flaky and waste time.
When adding a new dependency to a BUILD file or build configuration, be prepared to explain why it is needed; unnecessary dependencies increase build times and coupling.
Use existing utility functions (e.g., getAppName) instead of hardcoding or re-implementing values that already have a canonical source.
When implementing object copy, fork, or clone operations, ensure all relevant attributes are transferred -- review the source model field-by-field to avoid missing properties.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: review all fields when implementing copy/clone operations

rule: Use dedicated accessor methods (e.g., getTemplateType) to simplify handling of optional fields rather than manually unwrapping Option types.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: use accessor methods to simplify optional handling

rule: In REST API models, make new fields optional to maintain backward compatibility with existing clients; plan to make them required in a future API version.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: make new API fields optional for backward compatibility

rule: Validation logic that applies to all code paths (e.g., billing tag validation for all project creation scenarios) should be centralized rather than duplicated across different branches.
mode_rationale: Single principle about where to place validation logic

rule: Ensure that exception types map to the correct HTTP status codes; IllegalArgumentException should produce a 400 (Bad Request), not a 500 (Internal Server Error).
mode_rationale: Simple rule about correct exception-to-status-code mapping

rule: Service methods that look up entities should return Optional/Option types and let the caller decide how to handle absence, rather than throwing exceptions internally.
mode_rationale: Convention for service method return types

rule: When multiple async operations are independent of each other, execute them in parallel rather than awaiting them sequentially to reduce latency.
mode_rationale: Single performance guideline about parallel execution

rule: When adding a new feature path that parallels an existing one, ensure it has equivalent observability (counters, metrics, logging) as the original path.
mode_rationale: Convention to maintain observability parity

rule: Use more specific pattern matches (e.g., Some(value) instead of raw Option) to simplify downstream code and avoid unnecessary map/foreach calls.
mode_rationale: Simple coding style preference for pattern matching

rule: When modifying shared utility code (e.g., error handling utilities), run the broader test suite to catch regressions in all dependent sections.
mode_rationale: Single testing guideline about test scope for shared code

rule: Include a unique scenario ID or test identifier in dynamically created test resource names (e.g., workspace names) to avoid collisions and improve traceability.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for test resources

rule: Avoid asserting exact values for environment-dependent quantities in tests; instead assert relative conditions (e.g., greater than a baseline) or check units to avoid brittle tests that break across different infrastructure configurations.
mode_rationale: Testing convention about assertion style

rule: When removing a code step or block, also remove any associated comments that reference the removed code to avoid stale documentation.
mode_rationale: Simple cleanup rule when removing code

rule: Remove unnecessary interfaces from implementation classes when you want to prevent other services from directly injecting the implementation; force consumers to use the higher-level service layer instead.
mode_rationale: Architecture convention about interface exposure

rule: Domain entities must not reference API DTOs or models; the API is one presentation layer and domain should not depend on it, as this creates a coupling in the wrong direction.
mode_rationale: Layering rule about dependency direction

rule: When a file contains only a single class, name the file after the class for discoverability and consistency.
mode_rationale: Simple file naming convention

rule: Domain entities and validation logic should not depend on web framework classes (e.g., Play Forms); extract validations into a framework-agnostic layer to avoid coupling the domain to a specific web framework.

Domain entities should not contain JSON serialization mappings; serialization concerns belong in the infrastructure or presentation layer to avoid constraining the domain with a specific serialized format.
mode_rationale: Layering rule keeping serialization out of domain

rule: When different entity subtypes have distinct sets of fields, use polymorphism (separate subclasses) instead of making all fields optional on a single type; this enforces correctness at compile time.
mode_rationale: Design preference for type modeling

rule: Validation helpers should live in a separate file from domain entities, scoped to the package that uses them and marked as package-private to prevent misuse elsewhere.
mode_rationale: Code organization convention for validation placement

rule: Database persistence records should be clearly readable from code and decoupled from other layers (API DTOs, domain entities); sharing types between persistence and API layers creates hidden coupling where changes in one layer unexpectedly break the other.
mode_rationale: Layering rule about persistence isolation

rule: Remove fields from an entity or record that do not apply to that entity's type; storing inapplicable fields (always None/null) creates confusion and violates the principle that data models should accurately represent their domain.
mode_rationale: Data modeling convention about field relevance

rule: When communicating between services, use dedicated DTOs rather than sending internal domain representations to avoid coupling internal models with external contracts that may have different lifecycles.
mode_rationale: Convention for inter-service contracts

rule: Place API-facing DTOs and internal mapping DTOs (e.g., remote hub models) in separate packages so that backward compatibility implications are immediately clear from the package path.
mode_rationale: Package organization convention for DTOs

rule: Do not introduce code that has no current usages; add it in the PR where it is actually needed to keep changes minimal and reviewable.
mode_rationale: Simple principle against premature code additions

rule: Domain and service layers should never depend on API-layer DTOs; use domain entities instead to prevent unrelated API changes from rippling into core business logic.
mode_rationale: Layering rule about dependency direction

rule: When adding authorization checks, follow the established patterns used by similar methods in the codebase rather than inventing new approaches, to maintain consistency and reviewability.
When fixing a bug in one location, search for structurally similar code nearby that may have the same issue and fix all instances together.
mode_rationale: Simple practice to fix all similar instances together

rule: Use aria-label attributes instead of custom testId attributes for element identification in tests, which simultaneously improves accessibility and eliminates the need for custom test step definitions.
mode_rationale: Testing convention for element identification

rule: Aria-label values should use full, descriptive words (not abbreviations or IDs) because they are read aloud by screen readers for visually impaired users.
mode_rationale: Accessibility convention for label content

rule: Use dict.get() with a default value parameter to simplify conditional logic instead of checking for key existence separately.
mode_rationale: Python idiom for dictionary access

rule: In Python, prefer @dataclass (or NamedTuple) for simple data containers over custom classes with manual __init__ methods; keep data structures explicit and avoid hiding logic behind wrapper classes.
mode_rationale: Python convention for data containers

rule: Avoid using singletons or global mutable state unless there is a clear justification; consider thread-safety and testing implications before introducing shared state.
mode_rationale: Architecture convention against global state

rule: In Python, use list comprehensions for transforming collections instead of imperative loops with manual list building, as they are more idiomatic and concise.
mode_rationale: Python idiom for collection transforms

rule: When a utility function is almost always invoked alongside the same side effect (e.g., logging), encapsulate that side effect inside the function to reduce repetitive call-site boilerplate.
When recording a failure or error condition, always emit a corresponding log statement so that failures are visible in logs, not just in internal data structures.
mode_rationale: Logging convention for failure visibility

rule: Before removing or renaming environment variables from shared CI scripts, check whether other repositories or projects depend on those variables.
mode_rationale: Simple check before removing shared variables

rule: In CI scripts, reference shared environment variables instead of hardcoding values so that changes propagate consistently across all consumers.
mode_rationale: CI convention against hardcoded values

rule: Write BDD/feature test descriptions as user stories (As a [role], I'd like to [action], So that [benefit]) to clarify intent and maintain a user-centric perspective.
mode_rationale: Test writing convention for BDD descriptions

rule: Name API client classes to reflect the actual API version they communicate with, not an internal or legacy version, to avoid confusion.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for API client classes

rule: Name test fixtures after the resource or data they provide (e.g., `git_credentials`) rather than using generic or action-oriented names.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for test fixtures

rule: Move complex inline expressions into named utility functions to improve readability and testability.
Replace magic numbers with named constants (e.g., `MAX_GOALS_SHOWN`) and reuse the constant wherever the same value appears.
Always await async function calls inside try-catch blocks; unawaited async calls will not have their errors caught by the surrounding try-catch.
Ensure that the test description accurately reflects what the test actually validates; if it claims to test a behavior change, the test must trigger that change and assert the result.
In React components, consider memoizing expensive computed values or derived data to prevent unnecessary recalculations on re-render.
mode_rationale: React performance convention for derived values

rule: Use standard or existing utility classes (e.g., AutoRecordMapperCodec) for serialization instead of writing manual mapping code.
mode_rationale: Convention to use existing serialization utilities

rule: When processing events that trigger repeated lookups of the same data, introduce a low-complexity cache to avoid multiplying database queries per event.
mode_rationale: Performance convention for repeated data access

rule: In end-to-end tests, use API calls rather than UI interactions for test data setup (e.g., creating data sources) to make tests faster and more reliable.
mode_rationale: E2E testing convention for setup method

rule: When a function or hook accepts several parameters, group them into a single options object to improve readability and reduce confusion at the call site.
Move utility functions that do not depend on component state or props outside the React component body to improve readability and separation of concerns.
mode_rationale: React code organization convention

rule: Define related constant values as an enum (or a structured type map) rather than loose string literals to improve type safety and discoverability.

Instead of using useEffect to synchronize state with changing props (which can cause extra renders and missing-dependency warnings), use the React pattern of tracking the previous prop value and updating state during render.

When spreading rest props onto nested elements, verify that accessibility attributes like aria-label are not unintentionally duplicated across wrapper and child elements, which can confuse screen readers.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence guideline about checking accessibility attribute duplication when spreading props

rule: When a component depends on asynchronously loaded data (e.g., locale configuration), consider delaying the render until the data is available to avoid rendering in an incomplete or incorrect state.
mode_rationale: Single coding convention: delay render until async data is ready

rule: Domain modules must not import or reference API/web layer models (e.g., DTOs); the dependency direction should be from API layer toward domain, not the reverse.
mode_rationale: Architectural layering rule expressible in one sentence

rule: When the backend has structured data (e.g., git provider name), expose it as an explicit field in the API response rather than requiring clients to parse or derive it from other fields like URIs.
mode_rationale: API design convention: expose structured data rather than forcing client parsing

rule: Avoid chaining multiple useEffects to trigger data refreshes; instead extract the fetch function and call it imperatively from the event handler that requires the refresh.
mode_rationale: Single React convention about avoiding chained useEffect for data fetching

rule: Use imperative reload functions instead of boolean reload flags that trigger useEffect chains, as flag-based patterns cause unnecessary re-renders and unmount/remount cycles.
mode_rationale: Performance convention: prefer imperative reload over boolean flag patterns

rule: Do not use state flags that unmount and remount components just to trigger a data refresh; show a loading indicator within the existing component instead.
mode_rationale: Performance convention: don't unmount/remount to refresh data

rule: Use existing shared test utility helpers (e.g., layoutTestUtils.checkBreadcrumbs from @domino/test-utils) instead of reimplementing common test assertions inline.
Do not use deprecated or removed concept names (e.g., 'detwirl') when naming new variables, functions, or components; use current domain terminology.
mode_rationale: Naming convention: use current domain terms, not deprecated ones

rule: Use React route links (e.g., Link from react-router) instead of plain anchor tags for navigation within the React single-page application to avoid full page reloads.
Do not spread-clone an entire props object just to inject a single additional property; pass the original props through and add the extra prop individually.
mode_rationale: Coding style rule: don't spread-clone props just to add one property

rule: Use kebab-case for data-test (data-testid) attribute values to maintain consistency across the codebase.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for data-test attribute values

rule: Each React component should be in its own file; do not export multiple components with similar interfaces but different implementations from the same file, as it creates confusion.
Do not prefix internal component names with third-party library names (e.g., 'Ant') as it couples the component identity to the underlying library.
mode_rationale: Naming convention: don't prefix components with library names

rule: When a prop is optional and has no default value, do not assume it will exist; add a null/undefined check or provide a default value.
mode_rationale: Defensive coding rule: null-check optional props without defaults

rule: Add comments or improved logging to explain non-obvious control flow decisions, especially around retry logic, forced termination, or safety mechanisms.
When building endpoints or features, design them to support all known future use cases (e.g., multiple backing store types) rather than hard-coding for a single case.
mode_rationale: API design principle: support known future use cases rather than hard-coding

rule: Never hard-code the product name (e.g., 'Domino') in UI strings; always use the app name getter to support white labelling.
Before introducing a new constant, check if an existing one already serves the same purpose and adjust its value if needed rather than creating a duplicate.
When multiple match/case branches share the same structure, extract the differing parts into a tuple or data object and handle the common logic once to reduce duplication.
mode_rationale: Code organization convention: deduplicate shared logic across match branches

rule: Do not add explicit imports for types already covered by a wildcard import in the same scope.
mode_rationale: Simple import hygiene rule

rule: Use domain-level abstractions (e.g., DominoId.newInstance) instead of implementation-specific types (e.g., MongoDB ObjectId) outside of persistence code to maintain proper layering.
mode_rationale: Architectural layering convention: use domain types outside persistence code

rule: Module-scoped or session-scoped pytest fixtures that are shared across tests should be extracted into a separate conftest or fixture file rather than defined inside a specific test file.
mode_rationale: Test organization convention: shared fixtures go in conftest files

rule: Before writing custom test setup logic, check for existing helper functions (e.g., create_user()) that already encapsulate the required setup.
Tests should clean up resources they create (e.g., archive projects, delete temporary data) to avoid polluting the test environment for subsequent runs.
mode_rationale: Testing convention: clean up created resources after tests

rule: When a variable is an alias for another concept (e.g., selectedRevision is actually activeRevision), add a comment explaining the relationship to prevent confusion.
mode_rationale: Documentation convention: comment variable aliases to prevent confusion

rule: Review error messages and log strings for typos before committing, as these are user-facing or debugging-critical text.
mode_rationale: Simple quality convention: check error/log strings for typos

rule: Define static configuration objects like table column definitions outside the React component body to avoid recreating them on every re-render.
mode_rationale: Single rule: move static data outside components to avoid re-creation

rule: Formatting and display utility functions should gracefully handle undefined or null inputs, returning a sensible fallback instead of rendering 'Invalid date' or similar broken output.
mode_rationale: Error handling convention: formatters should handle null/undefined gracefully

rule: Write BDD test step definitions in natural, readable language that clearly conveys the action and expected outcome without ambiguity.
Use generated enum types from API specifications instead of hardcoded string values with @ts-ignore comments.
Use shared, white-label-aware UI components (e.g., wait spinners, loaders) instead of custom or non-themed implementations to ensure consistent branding across deployments.
Do not commit commented-out code or commented-out tests; remove dead code before merging.
Do not leave commented-out test cases in test files; either fix them or remove them entirely.
mode_rationale: Test hygiene rule: don't leave commented-out test cases

rule: Avoid unnecessary type assertions and casts; if the API typing is correct, use the types as-is rather than forcing casts.
Extract complex inline logic into named functions to improve readability and reusability.
Use rem units instead of px for margin and padding values to support consistent scaling and accessibility.
Pass function references directly as callbacks instead of wrapping them in unnecessary arrow functions.
mode_rationale: Performance convention: pass function refs directly instead of wrapping in arrow functions

rule: When a service already returns the needed type, use it directly rather than adding unnecessary conversion methods.
mode_rationale: Simplicity convention: don't add conversion methods when the type already matches

rule: Do not place internal service models in API model packages; keep non-API entities in the package where they are consumed (e.g., alongside the service interface).
mode_rationale: Code organization rule: keep internal models out of API packages

rule: Service method signatures should separate pagination, sorting, and filtering into distinct parameters rather than bundling them into a single ambiguous object.
Verify that API parameters (filters vs. sort orders) accurately reflect the product design and requirements before implementing them.
mode_rationale: API design convention: verify parameters match product requirements

rule: Use a consistent, short parameter name (e.g., `q`) for free-text search across all API endpoints; internally map it to a descriptive name like `searchQuery`.
mode_rationale: API naming convention: use consistent short name for free-text search

rule: Name binder or helper objects/functions after the parameter they actually bind, not after unrelated concepts.
mode_rationale: Naming convention: name binders after the parameter they bind

rule: Disable IDE 'align when multiline' formatting rules to avoid excessive whitespace that creates noisy diffs and makes side-by-side code review harder.
mode_rationale: Formatting convention: disable alignment rules to reduce noisy diffs

rule: Name API filter parameters to match the corresponding domain model attribute names so the mapping is immediately obvious to consumers.
mode_rationale: API naming convention: filter params should match domain model attribute names

rule: Replace bare `.get` calls on Option/nullable values with `getOrElse` throwing an `IllegalStateException` with a clear message describing the unexpected state, even for cases that should never occur.
mode_rationale: Error handling convention: use getOrElse with descriptive exception instead of bare .get

rule: The totalCount field in paginated API responses must reflect the total number of items matching the filter in the database, not just the current page size.
Move reusable test fixture creators and builders into shared test utility modules (e.g., testlib) so they can be used across multiple test suites.
Use domain-specific types (e.g., DominoId) instead of raw primitives (e.g., String) even when implicit conversions would make primitives compile, to preserve type safety and intent.
Only use Provider-based (lazy) dependency injection when resolving circular dependencies; use direct injection for all other cases.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence convention about when to use lazy DI

rule: Start with the narrowest API parameter type that satisfies current requirements (e.g., single value instead of list); widening later is a non-breaking change.
mode_rationale: Simple design principle about API parameter types

rule: Keep test-only utilities, injection modules, and fixtures in test source directories rather than in production source sets.
mode_rationale: Straightforward code organization rule

rule: When a class is hard to name because it represents multiple concerns, it is a signal the class should be split into separate classes with clear single responsibilities.
mode_rationale: Naming/design heuristic expressible in one sentence

rule: Do not perform expensive IO on every request for data that changes rarely; use caching or a lightweight staleness check to avoid unnecessary database load.
Replace N individual database queries with a single bulk query (e.g., using $in) and perform filtering or diffing in memory to reduce IO overhead.
mode_rationale: Performance best practice about bulk queries

rule: When checking or updating multiple records, consolidate into a single bulk query rather than issuing separate database calls per item.
mode_rationale: Performance convention to consolidate DB calls

rule: Name fields and functions after the domain concept they represent, not a loosely related concept; using the wrong domain term creates confusion for other developers.
mode_rationale: Naming convention about using correct domain terms

rule: Use strict equality (=== instead of ==) to avoid unexpected type coercion in JavaScript and template languages.
When commenting out code or disabling tests, include a reference to the tracking ticket (e.g., Jira link) explaining why the code is disabled and when it should be re-enabled.
mode_rationale: Documentation convention for commented-out code

rule: Define magic numbers (such as page sizes) as named constants rather than inlining them, so the value is documented and easy to change in one place.
When the same value (e.g., page size) is used in multiple places, reference a single shared constant to ensure consistency and prevent drift.
mode_rationale: Convention to reference a single shared constant

rule: Review interface and type names for typos before merging; a misspelled name that resembles an existing name creates confusing collisions and hinders code discoverability.
mode_rationale: Simple naming hygiene rule

rule: Do not import the same module twice, and prefer named imports over wildcard ("* as") imports in React/TypeScript files.
mode_rationale: Import style convention

rule: Always use strict equality (===) instead of loose equality (==) for comparisons in JavaScript/TypeScript.
Extract reusable utility functions and logic out of component files into dedicated utility modules to keep components focused on rendering.
mode_rationale: Architecture convention about separating logic from rendering

rule: Separate styled-component definitions with an empty line between them for visual clarity and readability.
mode_rationale: Formatting/style convention

rule: When introducing a new configuration dimension (e.g., deployment flavor), ensure it is captured in observability and reporting data so results can be traced back to their source.
mode_rationale: Observability convention for new config dimensions

rule: Assign boolean literals to descriptively named variables before passing them as function arguments instead of passing raw True/False values.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for boolean arguments

rule: Always provide a default value for environment variable expansions in shell scripts (e.g., ${VAR:-default}) to prevent failures when the variable is unset.
mode_rationale: Shell scripting safety convention

rule: When modifying shared test steps or code paths, verify the change works correctly in all environments and test configurations that use it, not just the one you are targeting.
mode_rationale: Testing best practice about verifying all environments

rule: Always surface API errors to the user; ensure that components consuming async data display error states from failed API calls.
When reading from a cache, handle the case where the expected entry is missing -- ensure the operation degrades gracefully and will be retried on the next cycle rather than failing silently.
mode_rationale: Error handling convention for cache reads

rule: Choose descriptive, self-documenting variable names that communicate purpose; avoid generic or abbreviated names that require additional context to understand.
Align and format complex expressions (e.g., chained calls, multi-line parameters) so they are easy to scan and understand at a glance.
When working with Option types in Scala, prefer pattern matching on the value rather than using `.isDefined` checks followed by `.get`, as it leads to safer and more readable code.
mode_rationale: Scala idiom preference expressible in one sentence

rule: Use the default import syntax (`import React from 'react'`) rather than named or namespace imports for React, as modern React and bundler configurations support this simpler form.
mode_rationale: Simple import style convention for React

rule: Extract reusable helper logic out of component or route files into dedicated utils files to improve reusability and keep route/component files focused.
mode_rationale: Code organization convention

rule: Use the React fragment shorthand syntax (`<>...</>`) instead of `<React.Fragment>` or wrapping divs when no key or attributes are needed.
When refactoring removes or changes the behavior of a function, rename it to accurately reflect the new behavior and avoid confusing future readers.

Avoid hardcoding magic number constants (e.g., pixel widths) in child components that parent components are unaware of; instead, derive UI values from explicit boolean or enum state managed at the parent level.
mode_rationale: Architecture convention about deriving UI values from state

rule: When an optional API field has a destructive side effect (e.g., omitting a field removes an existing value), document this behavior explicitly in the API docs and add unit tests to cover it.
Remove unused variables, imports, and code artifacts to keep the codebase clean and avoid confusion about what is actively used.
Permission-denied error messages should clearly state what the user cannot do and direct them to contact an administrator for resolution, using a consistent format across the application.
mode_rationale: Error message convention for permission denials

rule: When adding behavior to a shared/base component for a targeted fix, prefer creating a wrapper component that composes the original rather than modifying the shared component directly, to limit blast radius.
mode_rationale: Architecture convention about wrapper components for hotfixes

rule: Do not commit empty test files; either write meaningful tests or remove the test file until tests are ready to be implemented.
Use trace-level logging for high-frequency operational messages (e.g., per-message processing) so logs are not too verbose in production but can be enabled when debugging.
Use a structured retry policy with backoff and max attempts instead of bare infinite loops for resilience logic, to avoid unbounded resource consumption and provide better observability.
mode_rationale: Error handling convention about structured retries

rule: Message consumers and event processors must include exception handling to prevent unhandled errors from crashing the consumer loop or losing messages silently.
mode_rationale: Error handling convention for message consumers

rule: In Scala, place constants and static-like values in a companion object rather than in the class body to separate instance state from shared values.
mode_rationale: Scala code organization convention

rule: Name variables and parameters after the domain concept they represent in the current service, not after terminology borrowed from other services or systems.
mode_rationale: Naming convention about domain-local terminology

rule: Add unit tests to illustrate expected inputs and outputs, especially for non-trivial processing logic, so reviewers and future developers can quickly understand the behavior.
mode_rationale: Testing convention to illustrate behavior

rule: When defining status enums, include an explicit error state and ensure the corresponding error message is propagated so consumers can understand what went wrong.
mode_rationale: Error handling convention for status enums

rule: Prefer explicit boolean props or render props over using optional function props to conditionally show or hide UI elements, as optional function props obscure intent and reduce readability.
mode_rationale: React API design convention about explicit boolean props

rule: Use the same term for the same concept across all APIs and services to avoid confusion; do not introduce synonyms (e.g., 'authors' vs 'owners') for the same entity.
For non-paginated endpoints, set the internal query limit high enough that it is effectively unreachable, to prevent items from being silently dropped from the response.
mode_rationale: API design convention about internal query limits

rule: Make internal limits (such as max result sizes) configurable via parameters with sensible defaults, so tests can set low values to verify limit enforcement without needing large datasets.
mode_rationale: Testing convention about configurable limits

rule: Pass the full domain object to a validation method rather than individual fields, so the method can evolve its checks without requiring callers to know which fields are validated.
mode_rationale: Architecture convention about method signatures

rule: Use configurable variables or environment-derived values instead of hardcoding version tags, image references, or other values that may change over time in test fixtures.
mode_rationale: Testing convention about configurable test fixtures

rule: In end-to-end tests, avoid placing long-running API steps before browser interactions, as the browser session may time out and cause unrelated failures.
mode_rationale: E2E testing convention about browser timeout avoidance

rule: When consuming internal packages, import from the dist (compiled) output rather than directly from source files to ensure proper module resolution and avoid compilation issues.
Remove console.log and other console statements before merging; do not use console logging as a workaround for unused variable errors.
mode_rationale: Simple rule to remove console statements before merging

rule: Define table column configurations in or near the table component file rather than in a parent view, to keep rendering logic co-located with the component that uses it.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence guideline about code colocation

rule: When using a global store (e.g., useStore), access it at the consuming component level rather than at the router level and passing values down as props.
mode_rationale: Simple convention about where to access global state

rule: Import UI components from the internal design system wrapper library rather than directly from the underlying component library (e.g., Ant Design) to maintain consistency and enable centralized customization.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence rule about importing from design system wrappers

rule: When a render function or column renderer grows complex, extract it into its own standalone component rather than keeping it in a utility file.
mode_rationale: Simple guideline about extracting complex render logic

rule: When the same set of string literal values is used across multiple files, define a union type to centralize them and make future additions easier.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence typing convention

rule: When logging service operations, include the filter or query parameters in the log message to aid debugging and traceability.
When adding new fields to a public API, check whether existing system or integration tests need to be updated to cover the new field.
mode_rationale: Simple reminder to update tests when adding API fields

rule: Prefer using fake implementations over mocking frameworks in tests, as fakes provide more realistic behavior and are easier to maintain.
When adding log statements with contextual data, include all relevant fields rather than a subset, so that logs provide complete diagnostic information.
mode_rationale: Simple logging completeness guideline

rule: Use the correct semantic HTML element for interactive controls; do not use a Link component when the behavior is that of a button.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence rule about correct HTML element usage

rule: Do not commit empty files to the repository; remove them or add the intended content before merging.
Mark temporary workarounds, skipped functionality, or deferred work with a TODO comment so it is discoverable and trackable.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence convention about marking temporary code

rule: Use built-in framework components (e.g., react-router's Redirect) instead of writing custom replacements unless you have a documented reason the built-in cannot satisfy the requirement.
When testing multiple expected failures, assert each one individually rather than in a loop that stops at the first failure.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence testing convention

rule: In E2E tests, use element-type-specific selectors (e.g., 'I wait to see the checkbox') rather than generic text selectors to ensure the correct UI element is present.
mode_rationale: Simple E2E test selector guideline

rule: In E2E tests, verify that the element type in your assertion matches the actual rendered element; asserting a checkbox when the element is not a checkbox will cause a false failure.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence E2E assertion check

rule: Avoid using 'any' as a type in TypeScript; use proper typed interfaces or generics to maintain type safety.
Do not mark test functions as async unless the test body actually awaits something; unnecessary async adds confusion and may mask timing issues.
Maintain consistent import ordering: absolute/external imports should come before relative imports, and rely on auto-formatting tools like Prettier to enforce this.
Keep React state as close as possible to the component that uses it; do not lift state to a parent container unless the parent actually needs it.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence React state management guideline

rule: When displaying code snippets in the UI, use the CodeEditor component in read-only mode rather than plain text or custom markup for consistent syntax presentation.
When removing or simplifying wrapper elements, verify that text overflow and ellipsis behavior still works correctly in constrained layouts.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence verification reminder during refactoring

rule: Do not mark test functions as async if they contain no await expressions; unnecessary async adds confusion and overhead.
When migrating between SDK versions behind a feature flag, keep the old implementation unchanged so it remains a valid fallback; do not modify the old path with new SDK calls.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence rule about keeping old code unchanged behind feature flags

rule: Do not expose vendor-specific version numbers (e.g., V2) in public API names; treat SDK version details as implementation internals.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention to hide vendor versions

rule: Do not embed configuration or feature-flag values in domain data objects; keep configuration concerns separate from the data model.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence separation of concerns guideline

rule: When wrapping an input stream in a data object, store a factory function (lambda) to regenerate the stream on each access rather than holding a single stream reference that can only be read once.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence pattern for re-readable stream wrappers

rule: In Scala, prefer case classes over regular classes with @unused annotations; case class constructor parameters are automatically public vals, eliminating the need for boilerplate getters and suppression annotations.
In Scala, use case classes with public vals instead of Java-style getter methods (getX); access fields directly by name following Scala naming conventions.
Place related types in the same package or directory even if they are in different build modules; do not scatter related abstractions across unrelated packages.
mode_rationale: Simple code organization guideline

rule: When renaming a field or method during a refactor, update all call sites to use the new name rather than adding compatibility aliases that obscure the actual type.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence refactoring convention

rule: Do not add production API surface (methods, wrappers, interfaces) solely to support test code; move test-only utilities to the test module or use the underlying client directly in tests.

When designing a client wrapper, choose a consistent configuration strategy: either make the client a singleton with per-call parameters, or make one client instance per configuration set; avoid mixing constructor-level and method-level configuration for the same concern.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence API design convention about configuration consistency

rule: Unpack settings objects and pass only the specific settings a component needs rather than the entire settings object.
mode_rationale: Simple guideline to avoid passing entire settings objects

rule: Place new configuration settings alongside existing related settings and follow the established pattern rather than creating new configuration locations.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence convention about config placement

rule: Use enumerated types instead of boolean flags when a field represents more than two meaningful states to improve clarity.
mode_rationale: Simple typing guideline for multi-state fields

rule: When modifying API endpoints, preserve the old endpoint for backward compatibility rather than replacing it, especially when external consumers may depend on it.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence API stability rule

rule: Include negative test cases that verify behavior does not occur when it shouldn't, not just positive tests for expected behavior.
mode_rationale: Simple testing completeness reminder

rule: Use proper synchronization (e.g., AtomicReference) for mutable state that can be accessed by concurrent calls.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence concurrency safety rule

rule: Prefer simple, direct implementations for straightforward operations rather than routing them through complex abstractions like state machines.
Document user-facing timeouts and configurable limits with comments explaining their full behavior and impact on users.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence documentation convention

rule: Update architecture documentation when introducing new build tools or infrastructure changes.
mode_rationale: Simple reminder to update docs with infrastructure changes

rule: Import aliases should resolve to the correct type of code — aliases pointing to compiled output paths should not redirect to source code.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence rule about import alias correctness

rule: Use namespaced package imports (e.g., @org/package) to be explicit about the nature and origin of imports.
mode_rationale: Simple import naming convention

rule: Do not store computed values in the database that can be derived from other fields; compute them on read instead.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence data modeling guideline

rule: When exposing configuration or internal data through API endpoints, review whether sensitive values (secrets, credentials) could leak outside their intended security boundary.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence security review reminder

rule: Add a blank line between logically distinct code blocks (e.g., before an if statement following variable assignments) to improve readability.
mode_rationale: Simple code formatting convention

rule: Conversion or mapping logic between representations should live on the domain class or its companion object, not in an unrelated service class.
Define traits (interfaces) that are clean of external library dependencies so they can be shared across modules without pulling in transitive dependencies.
Use relative imports for files within the same module or feature directory rather than absolute imports to keep modules self-contained and portable.
mode_rationale: Simple import style convention

rule: Before creating new mock data files, check if shared mocks already exist in a centralized location (e.g., api/__mocks__) and reuse them to avoid duplication.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence guideline to check for existing mocks before creating new ones

rule: When adding a new field or flag to a data model, update or add a test case that explicitly verifies the field gets set correctly.
mode_rationale: Simple rule to add tests when adding model fields

rule: After a mutative action (restore, update, archive) on a detail view, refetch the current entity's data rather than navigating the user away from the page.
mode_rationale: Single behavioral convention for post-mutation UI updates

rule: When using API-imposed limits or magic numbers (e.g., max page size), add a comment explaining the constraint and its source so future developers understand why the value was chosen.
mode_rationale: Simple commenting convention

rule: When transforming collections, verify the return type matches the expected shape (e.g., avoid accidentally nesting arrays by mapping into arrays).
Prefer a single .map transformation over chaining .map and .zip when both values can be computed in one pass.
mode_rationale: Simple performance preference for collection operations

rule: Create domain-specific authorization roles rather than repurposing generic ones, to avoid unintended permission drift when the generic role evolves.
mode_rationale: Single architectural guideline about authorization roles

rule: Always include all referenced values in React useEffect dependency arrays, especially after stabilizing callbacks.
mode_rationale: Simple React convention about dependency arrays

rule: Use optional parameters with sensible default values for commonly used arguments to simplify call sites.
Extract repeated string literals in test files into shared variables to reduce duplication and ease maintenance.
mode_rationale: Simple code hygiene rule for tests

rule: Do not assert on exact UI copy in end-to-end tests; prefer checking key content or semantic selectors to avoid brittle tests that break on copy changes.
Make data-testid attributes and button labels dynamic based on component state so they accurately describe the current action.
Verify that shared state modifications in tests do not impact other tests running in parallel.
Every new class should have a corresponding test spec file before merging.
Set pagination defaults based on real-world data volumes rather than arbitrary numbers, considering the largest known customer deployments.
mode_rationale: Single guideline about pagination defaults

rule: Remove debug print statements (println, console.log) before merging code.
Run IDE project generation commands when adding new build targets to keep the development environment in sync.
mode_rationale: Simple reminder to regenerate IDE config

rule: Add comments explaining why security-sensitive operations (like user impersonation) are safe in their specific context.
mode_rationale: Simple commenting convention for security-sensitive code

rule: Use eager singleton binding (.asEagerSingleton()) for dependency injection modules to ensure only one instance is created.
mode_rationale: Single DI configuration convention

rule: Dependencies must be injected via class constructor arguments rather than instantiated inside the class, to enable unit testing.
mode_rationale: Single architectural convention about how to inject dependencies

rule: Add trace-level logging at service entry points to aid debugging without cluttering production logs.
mode_rationale: Simple logging convention

rule: Use structured logging with key-value pairs in API controllers, logging both success and failure with entity IDs and operation context.
mode_rationale: Single logging style guideline

rule: Write unit tests that verify dependencies are called with the correct arguments, not just that they are called.
mode_rationale: Simple unit test assertion convention

rule: Review log statements to ensure they do not inadvertently log authentication secrets, tokens, or sensitive headers.
mode_rationale: Single security review guideline

rule: Catch NonFatal exceptions instead of RuntimeException to avoid swallowing fatal errors that should propagate.
mode_rationale: Simple error-handling convention for Scala

rule: Error log messages must include relevant entity IDs and the name of the operation that failed for effective debugging.
mode_rationale: Single logging content requirement

rule: Avoid calling .get on Option types; refactor the return type or use safer alternatives like .getOrElse or pattern matching.
mode_rationale: Simple safe-coding convention for Option types

rule: API response type names must be semantically accurate for all endpoints that use them; do not reuse a type name that only makes sense for one endpoint.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention for API response types

rule: Pass the exception object as a parameter to the logger so that the full stack trace is logged, rather than only logging the message string.
mode_rationale: Single logging convention about exception parameters

rule: Capitalize Domino-specific entity names (e.g., Workspace, Datasets) in UI copy but use lowercase for generic terms (e.g., published apps, docs).
mode_rationale: Simple capitalization convention for UI copy

rule: Capitalize product feature names (e.g., 'Exports', 'Workspace') when referring to the named feature, but use lowercase when referring to the generic action.
mode_rationale: Simple capitalization convention for feature names

rule: Use a consistent format for help links and instructional copy across the application (e.g., 'Learn more about <feature>').
Do not create special-case handling for entities that can be treated uniformly with other entities of the same type.
mode_rationale: Simple design principle about uniform treatment

rule: Before implementing custom logic, check if an existing service method or API already provides the needed functionality and prefer reusing it.
mode_rationale: Single guideline to check for existing APIs first

rule: Every TODO comment in the codebase must reference a tracked ticket (e.g., Jira) so it does not get lost or forgotten.
mode_rationale: Simple rule requiring ticket references on TODOs

rule: When a system or integration test fails, automatically collect debugging artifacts (e.g., support bundles, logs) to aid post-failure investigation.
In API and integration tests, explicitly assert the HTTP status code returned by the server, not just the response body.
mode_rationale: Simple test assertion convention

rule: Replace magic numbers (especially shared constants like sleep durations and timeouts) with named variables or configuration values.
mode_rationale: Simple code hygiene rule about named constants

rule: Do not declare multiple conflicting version constraints for the same dependency in a single requirements file; consolidate them into a single compatible range.
When adding a feature flag or toggle, write unit tests covering the behavior under each flag state to prevent regressions.
mode_rationale: Simple testing convention for feature flags

rule: Do not leave tests commented out or skipped without filing a tracking ticket to ensure they are re-enabled or properly removed.
mode_rationale: Simple rule requiring tickets for skipped tests

rule: In React components, use useRef instead of useState for values that do not need to trigger re-renders and have no reactive dependencies.
Components should inject their own known values into callbacks rather than requiring parent components to pass curried functions.
mode_rationale: Single component design convention

rule: Do not add new exceptions to the useEffect dependency lint rule; always include all dependencies, especially for simple value types like booleans.
mode_rationale: Simple React lint rule convention

rule: Use label="value" notation for JSX string attributes instead of template literals or expressions wrapping plain strings.
mode_rationale: Simple JSX syntax style convention

rule: Wrap React context provider values in useMemo to prevent unnecessary re-renders of all consuming components.
mode_rationale: Single React performance convention

rule: Do not place React components in style files; components with their own logic belong in separate files, even if they started as styled components.
mode_rationale: Simple file organization convention

rule: Reserve the 'use' prefix for React hooks; do not name component props or regular functions with the 'use' prefix.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for React hooks

rule: React component names must accurately describe what the component is, not what it was derived from or an unrelated UI element.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention for React components

rule: Use NonFatal pattern matching in Scala catch blocks to avoid catching fatal errors that should crash the application.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence Scala error handling convention

rule: Extract repeated string literals used as defaults across multiple locations into a shared constant.
mode_rationale: Simple code organization rule about deduplicating literals

rule: Use semantically correct HTTP status codes in API responses (e.g., 403 for forbidden, not other 4xx codes).
Do not create container components unless they have heavy logic or backend calls; simple pass-through containers add unnecessary abstraction.
In BDD-style tests, use 'Then' for verification/assertion steps, not 'And' or other keywords.
mode_rationale: Single convention about BDD keyword usage

rule: Add comments to test steps whose purpose is not immediately obvious from the step description.
Use Option/Optional types (e.g., Option[String]) instead of empty strings to represent absent values, as this makes the absence of a value explicit and type-safe.
Extract entity IDs from API response payloads rather than parsing them out of URLs, which is fragile and couples tests to URL structure.
mode_rationale: Simple testing convention about parsing response payloads

rule: When introducing new configuration values that affect routing or paths, default to the safe/backward-compatible value first and change it in a follow-up PR to prevent breaking production.
mode_rationale: Single rule about defaulting to backward-compatible values

rule: When adding configuration files that must be present in a Docker image, add a corresponding container structure test to verify the file exists and update the Dockerfile COPY instructions.
mode_rationale: Multi-step procedure: add config file, add structure test, update Dockerfile COPY

rule: Place new configuration keys in the same section as related existing settings rather than adding them in an unrelated location in the config file.
Do not expose internal retry/backoff handler callbacks to callers; instead add logging inside the polling function itself, so the retry library can be swapped without breaking the public API.
Use existing shared test helper functions (e.g., wait_for_status) instead of reimplementing polling or status-checking logic inline in individual tests.
Add debug logging inside retry/polling loops to capture the current state on each iteration, aiding troubleshooting of intermittent failures.
mode_rationale: Single logging convention for retry/polling loops

rule: In React components, reset all related state variables (flags, content) in error handlers to prevent stale state from a previous successful response leaking into the error state.
Add explanatory comments for non-obvious or tricky logic so future readers understand the intent without reverse-engineering the code.
Create styled icon components instead of using nested CSS class selectors to style child elements, keeping styles co-located and explicit.

Place data classification or detection logic (e.g., whether a file is truncated) on the backend where the information is already known, rather than reimplementing complex detection heuristics on the frontend.
mode_rationale: Single architectural guideline about where to place detection logic

rule: Define static constants outside the React component function scope to avoid unnecessary re-creation on every render.
Be aware that checking loading=false and data=undefined together can cause a brief flash of incorrect UI state before data arrives; guard against this race condition in data-fetching patterns.
mode_rationale: Single convention about guarding against loading/data race conditions

rule: Use white-label-aware UI components (e.g., WaitSpinner) instead of brand-specific ones (e.g., spinning domino logo) to avoid additional white-labelling logic.
Absolute imports must appear before relative imports; rely on ESLint/Prettier auto-fix to enforce consistent import ordering.
Do not mark a function as async if its body contains no await expressions.
Add an explanatory comment when a function has a non-obvious or early return to clarify the intent for future readers.
Never use explicit `any` as a TypeScript type; use a specific type, `unknown`, or a generic instead.
Define styled components outside the React component function, never inline within JSX.
mode_rationale: Simple rule about styled component placement

rule: Remove commented-out code before merging; use version control history to recover old code if needed.
Define static constants outside of React component functions to avoid unnecessary re-creation on every render.
Use styled components instead of inline styles, especially when the same style is repeated across multiple elements.
When modifying code near ESLint disable comments, verify the disable directive still covers its originally intended suppression.
Remove CI pipeline parameters that are only needed for integration testing before merging to the main branch.
mode_rationale: Simple cleanup rule for CI parameters

rule: When deleting files, also remove or update all references to those files in CI configs, tests, and other dependent code.
mode_rationale: Single convention about cleaning up dangling references

rule: Before writing custom utility code, check if existing internal libraries (e.g., domino-pytk) already provide the needed functionality.
mode_rationale: Simple rule about checking for existing utilities before writing new ones

rule: When querying or operating on resources that can have multiple instances (e.g., pods, jobs), target by a specific identifier (e.g., execution ID) rather than a broad scope like project name.
mode_rationale: Single convention about precise resource targeting

rule: Do not leave commented-out code in the codebase; remove it entirely and rely on version control for history.
When linking monorepo packages, verify that assumed build output directories (e.g., /dist) will exist at resolution time and document the dependency on the build step.
mode_rationale: Single convention about verifying build output paths

rule: When injecting scripts or helpers via modules at runtime, evaluate whether race conditions can occur between module loading and script availability.
mode_rationale: Single guideline: evaluate race conditions in dynamic module loading

rule: Never import mock modules or test fixtures in production source files; this causes build issues and increases bundle size. Extract shared logic into a utility instead.
Wrap callback functions defined inside React components with React.useCallback() to prevent unnecessary re-creation on every render.
mode_rationale: Single React performance convention

rule: Use TypeScript interface with extends instead of redefining identical types; share type definitions from a common package rather than duplicating across app and package boundaries.
mode_rationale: Single TypeScript convention about type reuse

rule: Lambda functions that integrate with CI/CD systems should be thin routing layers; push business logic and config processing closer to where the code is checked out.
mode_rationale: Single architectural guideline about lambda responsibility

rule: In Scala, distinguish between empty collections (Seq.empty) and null; they are not the same and mock setups must match the actual return types to avoid false test behavior.
Aria-label values should be human-readable names with spaces (suitable for screen readers), not id-like strings with dashes.
Domain interfaces should use domain IDs (e.g., DominoId) and keep database-specific details (e.g., MongoDB ObjectId) in the persister implementation layer.
Use CSF3 (Component Story Format 3) for Storybook stories instead of the deprecated MDX format.
mode_rationale: Single convention about Storybook format

rule: Function names should accurately describe all behaviors; if a function both applies and removes tags, split it into separate single-concern functions rather than overloading one name.
In polymorphic data models, place commonly-used fields for filtering and sorting at the top level and nest type-specific definitions in dedicated fields to enable efficient querying and pagination.
mode_rationale: Single data modeling convention for polymorphic schemas

rule: Do not ship half-implemented architectural patterns (e.g., HATEOAS URI fields); either implement them properly or remove them entirely and add them back when there is a real need.
mode_rationale: Single quality convention against shipping incomplete patterns

rule: Service layer interfaces should not return REST API DTOs; this couples all service consumers (including internal callers) to API definition changes.
mode_rationale: Single architectural layering convention

rule: Place dependency injection bindings in the injection module that owns the related functionality, not in a neighboring module's injector.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence convention about where to place dependency injection bindings

rule: When an operation can partially succeed, return what was included and what failed to include with reasons, rather than failing the entire request or silently dropping items.
mode_rationale: Design principle for API response handling, not a multi-step procedure

rule: Include logging statements in methods that perform significant operations (e.g., project forking, template creation) so that failures and execution flow can be traced.
mode_rationale: Simple best practice to include logging in significant methods

rule: When a file contains methods serving multiple subsystems, prefix method names with the subsystem or domain they belong to (e.g., makeFlyteCommand instead of makeCommand) so readers can identify purpose without inspecting signatures or return types.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for methods in multi-concern files

rule: Remove all console.log statements from production code before submitting a PR; use proper logging infrastructure instead of console output.
Review and remove TODO comments that are no longer valid before merging; stale TODOs create confusion about whether work is still pending.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: clean up outdated TODO comments before merge

rule: Delete files that become empty after refactoring rather than leaving them in the codebase.
Helper functions should live in the module that owns their concern; do not add unrelated helpers to a generic helper file when a more specific test module or utility file is appropriate.
mode_rationale: Code organization principle about function placement

rule: Variable names should precisely describe what they hold; rename variables when their meaning drifts from the actual content (e.g., use env_image instead of env_uri when the value is a container image reference).
mode_rationale: Naming convention to keep variable names aligned with their content

rule: Design functions to return their computed results rather than accepting a mutable container as an argument to be filled; this makes the data flow explicit and the function easier to reason about.
mode_rationale: API design principle preferring return values over mutation

rule: When multiple tests perform similar verification, extract the shared logic into a common assertion function and have each test call it with its specific parameters, reducing duplication and improving maintainability.
mode_rationale: Testing best practice about reducing duplication via shared helpers

rule: When a line of code (especially string formatting or assertions) is too long to read at a glance, break it into multiple lines rather than leaving it as one long horizontal scroll.
mode_rationale: Simple formatting convention for line length

rule: Do not create empty containers that are immediately passed to a function for population; instead, capture the function's return value directly in the variable.
mode_rationale: Code style rule to avoid redundant temporaries
