rule: Do not use `object` as a type annotation in TypeScript; use a specific interface, type alias, or Record<string, unknown> instead.
Include all dependencies in the useEffect dependency array rather than using eslint-disable; if a dependency causes infinite loops, refactor the callback to be stable.
Do not use @ts-ignore to suppress type errors; instead define correct TypeScript types or type declarations for the component or module.
mode_rationale: TypeScript convention to fix types rather than suppress errors

rule: Separate unrelated changes (e.g., client generation updates vs. feature logic) into distinct PRs to make review easier and git history clearer.
mode_rationale: PR hygiene guideline about separating unrelated changes

rule: Name configuration variables to clarify which specific resource they refer to rather than using generic names like s3Bucket; prefer names like dominoBlobsS3Bucket.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for configuration variables

rule: Choose variable names that communicate the object's lifecycle state, e.g., use persistedDeployment for an entity that has been saved to the database.
mode_rationale: Naming convention to communicate object lifecycle state

rule: Never catch all Throwables or Exceptions broadly; use scala.util.control.NonFatal to avoid swallowing fatal errors like OutOfMemoryError.
mode_rationale: Error handling convention for Scala exception catching

rule: When deleting a resource that has both a database record and external dependencies (e.g., Kubernetes resources), delete the external resources first so the record is preserved if cleanup fails.
mode_rationale: Ordering principle for resource cleanup, single rule not a workflow

rule: Do not call .get on Option types; use .getOrElse with a meaningful exception, pattern matching, or .fold to handle the missing-value case explicitly.
When a hardcoded value or workaround exists because upstream functionality is not yet available, add a TODO comment referencing what needs to change and ideally the tracking ticket.
mode_rationale: Documentation convention for tracking workarounds

rule: Follow the Databricks Scala style guide and add curly braces around multi-line expressions for clarity and consistency.
mode_rationale: Scala style convention following Databricks guide

rule: Simplify Option handling using combinators like .filter and .getOrElse rather than verbose if/else chains; e.g., use s3DefaultBucket.filter(_.nonEmpty).getOrElse(throw ...).
mode_rationale: Scala idiom preference for Option combinators

rule: Catch only NonFatal exceptions in Scala; never use a bare catch block that could swallow fatal errors like OutOfMemoryError.
mode_rationale: Duplicate of catch-nonfatal rule, same single-sentence convention

rule: Add trace-level logging to all client/service boundary methods, including the method name and all parameters.
Custom exceptions must accept and pass a cause Throwable to the Exception constructor so the full stack trace is preserved, not just the message.
When defining custom exception case classes, name the throwable parameter 'cause' and pass it to the Exception superclass constructor for proper exception chaining.
mode_rationale: Exception design convention for proper chaining

rule: Log messages should describe the action and context (e.g. 'error creating model deployment secret, rolling back') rather than just echoing the exception message; pass the full exception object as a separate parameter for the stack trace.
mode_rationale: Logging convention about message content and exception passing

rule: If a method performs create-or-update logic, name it accordingly (e.g. 'createOrUpdateModelDeployment') so callers understand the full behavior from the name alone.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for methods with multiple behaviors

rule: Name variables and configuration keys to reflect their actual domain purpose rather than generic infrastructure names (e.g. 'mlflowS3ArtifactBucket' instead of a generic blob bucket name).
Use getOrElse with an explicit error thrown when a required value is missing, rather than silently proceeding with a default or None.
mode_rationale: Error handling convention to throw on missing required values

rule: Do not hardcode behavior or settings for a specific compute provider type; design abstractions that are provider-agnostic to avoid adding new settings or branches for every new provider.
mode_rationale: Architecture principle about provider-agnostic design

rule: Use Scala's idiomatic collection methods (e.g. isEmpty, nonEmpty) instead of manual equivalents for cleaner, more readable code.
mode_rationale: Scala style convention for collection methods

rule: When matching string input to an enum, use enumeratum's withNameInsensitiveOption instead of manually converting to uppercase before matching.
mode_rationale: Scala convention for enum matching with enumeratum

rule: Keep create and update persistence logic at the same abstraction level; if one path persists inside a service method, the other should too.
mode_rationale: Architecture convention about matching abstraction levels

rule: Use separate try-catch blocks for independent operations that require different rollback logic, rather than grouping them in a single block.
Service methods should encapsulate their own persistence calls and side effects rather than requiring the controller to orchestrate them.
Ensure finally blocks do not perform persistence or state-changing operations that should only run on success; fatal errors will still execute the finally block.
Do not place success-only logic in a finally block; put it in the try block after the operation that must succeed first.
mode_rationale: Error handling convention about correct placement of success logic

rule: Use idiomatic Option combinators like getOrElse instead of verbose pattern matching when the logic is a simple value-or-throw.
When refactoring or restructuring code, ensure existing tests are preserved or re-added to maintain coverage.
mode_rationale: Testing convention to preserve coverage during refactoring

rule: When a lookup method returns Option/None, decide explicitly whether to throw an exception or handle the missing case rather than silently ignoring it.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: explicitly decide how to handle None/null returns

rule: When a combined create-or-update method has mostly different logic for each path, split it into separate create and update methods for clarity.
Apply retry policies consistently across similar operations; if update uses a retry policy, create should too.
When adding new components that make API calls, ensure a test suite exists and mock external dependencies to prevent real API calls in tests.
When moving or refactoring files, verify that associated unit tests exist and are migrated along with the code.
mode_rationale: Testing convention to migrate tests alongside refactored code

rule: Never use fixed sleeps in test code; poll on the expected status or condition instead.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: poll for status changes instead of fixed sleeps

rule: Use existing utility functions (e.g., getAppName) instead of hardcoding values or reimplementing logic that is already available.
When implementing entity copy or fork operations, ensure all attributes from the source are carried over; review for missing fields.
Use existing helper methods (e.g., getTemplateType) to avoid manually unwrapping optional types when a convenience accessor already exists.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to prefer existing accessors over manual unwrapping

rule: Do not ship functionality that will not be tested by QE; if a feature is supported, it must work and have test coverage.
mode_rationale: Single rule about ensuring test coverage for shipped features

rule: Fields should only be optional in REST API models for backward compatibility; in domain models, required fields must not be optional.
mode_rationale: Convention about when fields should be optional vs required

rule: Validation logic (e.g., billing tags) should be applied consistently across all creation or update code paths, not just some.
Ensure exceptions map to the correct HTTP status code; IllegalArgumentException should produce a 400 Bad Request, not a 500 Internal Server Error.
mode_rationale: Convention to map exceptions to correct HTTP status codes

rule: Services should return Optional values and let callers decide how to handle absence, rather than failing internally with a fixed error strategy.
mode_rationale: Architectural convention about Optional return values

rule: When multiple independent async calls exist, combine and run them in parallel rather than awaiting them sequentially.
mode_rationale: Performance convention to run independent calls in parallel

rule: When adding a new feature path (e.g., customer template creation), add corresponding observability counters to track usage.
mode_rationale: Single rule to add observability counters with new feature paths

rule: When adding a new dependency to a BUILD file, be prepared to explain why it is needed; unexplained dependencies raise review flags.
mode_rationale: Convention to explain new dependencies in BUILD files

rule: When using a Provider instead of direct injection to break a circular dependency, document the reason and file a tech debt ticket for the proper fix.
mode_rationale: Convention to document Provider usage and file tech debt tickets

rule: When modifying shared utility code (e.g., error handling utilities), run the full end-to-end test suite to catch unintended regressions across features that depend on the utility.
mode_rationale: Single rule to run full e2e suite when modifying shared utilities

rule: Include the test scenario ID in dynamically created resource names (e.g., workspace names) to ensure uniqueness and traceability when debugging test failures.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for test resource uniqueness and traceability

rule: In tests that depend on infrastructure-specific values (e.g., memory sizes, CPU counts), assert relative conditions or unit-based checks rather than exact absolute values, since those values vary by platform and node configuration.
mode_rationale: Convention to use relative assertions instead of platform-specific absolutes

rule: When removing a code step or block, also remove any associated comments that reference the removed code to avoid stale or misleading documentation.
mode_rationale: Simple cleanup rule when removing code blocks

rule: Domain entities should never depend on API DTOs; the API is one presentation of the domain, not the only one, so coupling the domain to a specific API layer creates a dependency in the wrong direction.
mode_rationale: Architectural boundary rule about dependency direction

rule: When a file contains a single class or object, name the file after that class to make it easy to locate and reduce cognitive overhead.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for file-class alignment

rule: Domain entities and business logic should not depend on web framework constructs (e.g., Play Forms, HTTP-specific libraries); isolate framework dependencies to the presentation or validation layer to avoid coupling the domain to a specific web technology.
mode_rationale: Layering rule about framework independence

rule: Domain entities should not include JSON serialization mappings; coupling the domain to a specific serialized format constrains its evolution and creates backwards-compatibility concerns that belong in a separate mapping layer.
mode_rationale: Convention to keep serialization out of domain entities

rule: When different entity variants have distinct sets of fields, use type polymorphism (subclasses or sealed traits) rather than making all variant-specific fields optional on a single type, to enforce invariants at the type level.
mode_rationale: Design convention favoring type polymorphism over optional fields

rule: Validation helpers and required-field definitions are not domain entities; extract them into a dedicated file (e.g., ValidationHelpers) scoped to the package that uses them, keeping domain entity files focused on business concepts.
Database persistence records should not reuse API DTOs for field types; define dedicated persistence types so that changes to the API layer do not break database serialization and vice versa.
mode_rationale: Architectural boundary rule separating persistence from API types

rule: When a type hierarchy has variants with different field sets, do not carry fields from one variant into another as always-empty optionals; remove fields that do not apply to a given subtype to keep the data model honest.
mode_rationale: Convention to keep subtype fields honest by removing irrelevant ones

rule: Remove or restrict interfaces from shared modules when direct injection should be prevented; keeping implementation classes in the implementation module (rather than exposing them via an interface module) ensures consumers use the intended service facade.
mode_rationale: Convention about controlling injection scope via interface visibility

rule: When sending data between services, use a dedicated DTO rather than the internal domain representation, to decouple the internal model from the external contract and support independent evolution.
mode_rationale: Convention to use dedicated DTOs between services

rule: In Scala, use pattern matching on Option[Boolean] instead of nested conditionals to make the intent of each branch explicit and exhaustive.
mode_rationale: Scala idiom preference for explicit pattern matching

rule: Place DTOs in packages that reflect their boundary context (e.g., `api.models` for REST API DTOs, `remotehub.models` for external service mapping DTOs) so reviewers can quickly identify which compatibility constraints apply.
mode_rationale: Package organization convention for DTOs by boundary

rule: Do not add classes, DTOs, or other code that has no current usages; introduce them in the PR where they are actually needed.
mode_rationale: Simple rule against adding code with no current usages

rule: Domain services should depend on domain entities, not on API-layer DTOs; coupling services to API models causes unrelated API changes to ripple into core business logic.
When adding permission checks, follow the existing authorization patterns in similar methods rather than inventing new approaches; consistency reduces review burden and prevents subtle security gaps.
When fixing a bug caused by a specific code pattern, scan the same file and nearby files for identical patterns that may have the same issue.
mode_rationale: Simple practice to scan for duplicate bug patterns

rule: Prefer `aria-label` over `data-testid` for identifying interactive elements in tests; this serves the dual purpose of improving accessibility and simplifying test selectors.
Write `aria-label` values as full human-readable phrases (not terse IDs or abbreviations) since they are read aloud by screen readers for visually impaired users.
Use `dict.get(key, default)` with an appropriate default value instead of separate null-checking logic to simplify data access in Python.
mode_rationale: Python idiom to simplify null checks with dict.get defaults

rule: Use `@dataclass` (or `NamedTuple`) with auto-generated constructors instead of custom wrapper classes with manual builder methods; prefer explicit construction over hidden convenience methods.
mode_rationale: Convention to use dataclasses over custom builder patterns

rule: Avoid using singleton/global state patterns unless there is a clear need and thread-safety implications have been considered for the test runner or runtime environment.
mode_rationale: Convention to avoid global state without thread-safety analysis

rule: Use list comprehensions instead of imperative for-loops when transforming a collection of items into a new list in Python.
mode_rationale: Python idiom preferring comprehensions over imperative loops

rule: When a helper function is almost always called alongside a logging statement, encapsulate the logging inside the helper to reduce repetition and prevent missed log calls.
Before removing environment variables, scripts, or shared resources, search for usages across all consuming repositories and CI pipelines to avoid breaking downstream dependencies.
Use existing environment variables for version strings and configuration values rather than hardcoding them, especially in CI scripts where values are already exported.
mode_rationale: Convention to use environment variables over hardcoded strings

rule: Write BDD feature file scenarios as user stories (As a [role], I'd like to [action], So that [benefit]) to clearly communicate the intent and value of each test.
mode_rationale: Writing convention for BDD scenario descriptions

rule: Domain mapping code (e.g., converting template models to project models) should live in the service that owns the source domain, not in the consuming service, to avoid cross-service coupling.
mode_rationale: Architectural rule about where mapping code should live

rule: Name test fixtures to describe the resource they provide (e.g., `git_credentials`) rather than using generic or action-oriented names.
Define named constants for magic numbers and reuse them across related logic to improve readability and maintainability.
Always await async functions inside try-catch blocks; a try-catch cannot catch errors from unawaited async calls, leading to silently unhandled rejections.
mode_rationale: Error handling rule for async/await correctness

rule: Ensure test `it` descriptions accurately reflect what is actually being asserted; a test that only validates initial state should not claim to validate state changes.
mode_rationale: Convention that test names must reflect actual assertions

rule: Use framework-provided codecs like AutoRecordMapperCodec instead of manually writing serialization/deserialization code.
mode_rationale: Convention to use framework-provided codecs

rule: When processing events that trigger repeated database lookups, introduce a low-complexity cache to avoid redundant queries.
mode_rationale: Performance convention to cache redundant queries

rule: In e2e tests, prefer API steps over UI interactions for test data setup to improve speed and reliability.
mode_rationale: Testing convention to prefer API over UI for test data setup

rule: Move utility and helper functions outside of React component bodies to improve readability and separation of concerns.
mode_rationale: Convention to move helpers outside React component bodies

rule: Define related string or numeric constants as enums or typed mappings rather than loose constants to enforce type safety and discoverability.
mode_rationale: Type safety convention for constant values

rule: Instead of useEffect to sync state with prop changes, use the React pattern of tracking previous props in state and updating during render to avoid missing dependency warnings and extra re-renders.
mode_rationale: React convention for prop-to-state synchronization

rule: When spreading rest props containing aria attributes onto compound components (e.g., a button wrapping a link), verify that screen readers correctly interpret the duplicated attributes across nested elements.
mode_rationale: Single accessibility guideline to verify when spreading aria props

rule: When a component depends on asynchronously loaded configuration (e.g., locale data), consider delaying the component render until the data is available to avoid flash of incorrect content.
mode_rationale: Single rendering convention for async dependencies

rule: Domain/interface modules must not reference API/web module types; mapping between domain and API models should flow in the correct dependency direction as enforced by the build system.
mode_rationale: Single architectural rule about dependency direction

rule: In API responses, provide explicit fields (e.g., provider name) rather than expecting consumers to parse or derive values from other fields (e.g., extracting provider from a URI).
mode_rationale: Single API design convention

rule: Use imperative functions (e.g., extracted fetchData callbacks) for data refresh instead of chaining boolean state flags through multiple useEffects, which causes unnecessary re-renders.
mode_rationale: Single React pattern preference for data fetching

rule: Within the React SPA, use route links (React Router Link components) instead of plain anchor tags or window.location for internal navigation.
mode_rationale: Single convention about navigation components

rule: Prefer existing shared test helpers (e.g., `@domino/test-utils` layoutTestUtils.checkBreadcrumbs) over reimplementing common assertions in individual test files.
mode_rationale: Single convention to prefer existing helpers

rule: Do not use deprecated or legacy product terminology (e.g., 'detwirl') in new code, variable names, or route definitions — use current domain language instead.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention

rule: Each component should be in its own file; do not export multiple components with similar interfaces but different implementations from the same file, and avoid vendor-prefixing component names (e.g., 'Ant').
mode_rationale: Single file organization convention

rule: Use kebab-case for data-testid attribute values to maintain consistency across the codebase.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention for test attributes

rule: Do not spread-clone an entire props object just to add one property; instead pass the original props and add the extra prop individually at the call site.
mode_rationale: Single performance convention about prop passing

rule: If a prop is optional and has no default value, do not assume it will exist — add a null/undefined check or provide a default.
mode_rationale: Single defensive coding convention

rule: Prefer setting default values inline at the destructuring site rather than using separate conditional logic or fallback assignments later in the function body.
mode_rationale: Single code style convention

rule: When code contains non-obvious defensive logic (e.g., forcefully killing a process on repeated stop requests), add a comment explaining why it is necessary.
mode_rationale: Single documentation convention

rule: When building generalized endpoints or services, ensure they support all planned use cases (e.g., multiple backing store types) from the start, not just the immediate one.
mode_rationale: Single API design principle

rule: Never hardcode the product name (e.g., 'Domino') in UI strings; always use the app name getter to support white-labelling.
Before creating a new constant, check if an existing one already serves the purpose; if a default constant's value is insufficient, increase it rather than introducing a confusingly-named duplicate.
When multiple match/case branches repeat the same structure with different parameters, extract the varying parts into a tuple and handle the common logic once to improve readability.
mode_rationale: Single refactoring convention for match statements

rule: Use domain-level ID constructors (e.g., DominoId.newInstance) instead of database-specific types (e.g., ObjectId) outside of persistence code to maintain abstraction boundaries.
mode_rationale: Single architectural boundary convention

rule: Module-scoped or broadly-shared test fixtures should be defined in dedicated fixture files, not inline in specific test files, to clarify ownership and reusability.
mode_rationale: Single test organization convention

rule: Before writing inline test setup code, check for existing helper functions (e.g., create_user()) that already encapsulate the operation.
Tests that create resources (projects, users, etc.) should archive or clean them up after execution to avoid polluting shared environments.
When a variable is an alias for another concept (e.g., selectedRevision aliasing activeRevision), add a clarifying comment to prevent confusion for future readers.
mode_rationale: Single documentation convention for aliases

rule: Define static configuration objects like table column definitions as constants outside the component to avoid recreating them on every re-render.

When calling formatting utilities (e.g., date formatters) with potentially undefined values, verify the function handles undefined gracefully or add a guard to prevent displaying 'Invalid date' or similar artifacts.
mode_rationale: Single defensive coding convention

rule: Write BDD test step definitions that read as natural English sentences, rewording awkward phrasing for clarity.
mode_rationale: Single test writing style convention

rule: Use enums generated from API specs (e.g., OpenAPI YAML) instead of suppressing type errors with ts-ignore comments.
Use the project's design-system components (e.g., the white-label-aware wait spinner) instead of hardcoded or third-party alternatives to ensure consistent branding.
Remove commented-out code and commented-out tests before merging; use version control history to recover deleted code if needed.
Avoid unnecessary type casting; if you find yourself casting API response types, verify the API typing is correct and use proper types directly.
Extract complex inline logic into named functions for readability and reuse.
Use rem units instead of px for margin and padding to support responsive scaling and accessibility.
mode_rationale: Single CSS unit convention

rule: Don't add conversion or mapping methods when the service layer already returns the correct type; use the returned type directly.
mode_rationale: Single code simplicity convention

rule: Place internal service models in the service interface package, not in the API models package; the api.model package should only contain API model entities.
mode_rationale: Single package organization convention

rule: Separate pagination, sorting, and filtering into distinct parameters in service interfaces rather than bundling them into a single object.
Model query parameters according to their actual semantics: use sort order for ranking/ordering concerns and filters for inclusion/exclusion criteria; don't conflate the two.
mode_rationale: Single API semantics convention

rule: Use `q` as the query parameter name for search/filter endpoints across all APIs to maintain consistency, and use a more descriptive name like `searchQuery` only internally.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention for search parameters

rule: API parameter types should match their actual usage (e.g., single-select UI means a singular parameter, not a sequence); expanding from singular to multi-valued later is a non-breaking change.
mode_rationale: Single API typing convention

rule: API filter parameter names should match the corresponding domain model attribute names so the mapping is intuitive (e.g., use `owner` not `author` if the model property is `owner`).
mode_rationale: Single naming alignment convention

rule: Name binders, helpers, and utility methods after what they actually do, not what they were copied from or vaguely related to.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention for utility methods

rule: Use `getOrElse` with a descriptive `IllegalStateException` message instead of bare `.get` on Options; even for impossible states, clear error messages make debugging easier.
mode_rationale: Single error handling convention for Options

rule: Pagination responses must compute totalCount from the database query reflecting all filtered results, not just the size of the current page.
Test fixture creators and builders should live in the shared test utilities library (e.g., `testslib`) so they can be reused across test suites, not duplicated in individual spec files.
mode_rationale: Single test organization convention

rule: Use the proper domain type (e.g., `DominoId`) directly instead of relying on implicit conversions from primitive types like String.
mode_rationale: Single type usage convention

rule: Do not use `Provider` for dependency injection unless there is an actual circular dependency; import the dependency directly for simpler, more readable code.
mode_rationale: Single dependency injection convention

rule: Avoid heavy IO on every request for data that changes infrequently; use caching or lazy refresh strategies to reduce database load.
mode_rationale: Single performance convention about caching

rule: Replace multiple individual database queries with a single bulk query using operators like $in, then diff results in memory.
mode_rationale: Single performance convention about query batching

rule: When comparing or filtering datasets against a database, fetch all relevant records in one query and perform filtering in memory rather than issuing per-record lookups.
mode_rationale: Single performance convention about reducing database calls

rule: Name functions and fields after the domain concept they represent, not a loosely related concept; mismatched naming creates confusion when the API exposes those names.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention rule

rule: Use strict equality operators (=== instead of ==) to avoid type coercion bugs in JavaScript and Scala templates.
mode_rationale: Single coding style rule about operator choice

rule: When commenting out or disabling code due to a known issue, include a reference to the tracking ticket so the workaround can be cleaned up when the issue is resolved.
mode_rationale: Single documentation convention

rule: Define named constants for magic numbers like page sizes and reuse them across components to ensure consistency and ease of change.
mode_rationale: Single code organization convention

rule: Fix typos in type and interface names promptly; a typo that resembles another type's name creates confusion and blocks future corrections due to name collisions.
mode_rationale: Single naming hygiene rule

rule: Avoid wildcard imports (`import * as`) and duplicate imports from the same module; use named imports instead.
mode_rationale: Single import style convention

rule: Always use strict equality operators (=== and !==) instead of loose equality (== and !=) in JavaScript/TypeScript.
Extract utility functions and non-UI logic out of React component files into separate utility modules.
mode_rationale: Single code organization convention

rule: Separate styled component definitions with an empty line between each component for readability.
mode_rationale: Single formatting convention

rule: Always provide default values for environment variables in shell scripts using the `${VAR:-default}` syntax to prevent unbound variable errors.
mode_rationale: Single shell scripting convention

rule: Ensure API errors are surfaced to the user rather than silently swallowed, even when a hook or wrapper handles fetching.
mode_rationale: Single error handling principle

rule: Use descriptive, meaningful variable names that convey purpose; avoid generic or abbreviated names.
Align and format complex expressions and chained calls for readability, especially in functional pipelines and multi-line expressions.
mode_rationale: Single formatting convention

rule: Use modern default imports (`import React from 'react'`) instead of namespace imports for React.
Move reusable utility and helper functions into dedicated utils files rather than co-locating them in route or component files.
mode_rationale: Single code organization convention

rule: Use the React fragment shorthand syntax (`<>...</>`) instead of `<React.Fragment>` when no key prop is needed.
When a function's behavior changes (e.g., event handlers removed), rename the function to accurately reflect its new purpose and avoid confusion.
mode_rationale: Single naming hygiene rule

rule: Avoid hardcoding dimension constants (e.g., pixel widths) in child components that the parent knows nothing about; instead derive state from a boolean or enum at the parent level to prevent fragile implicit coupling.
mode_rationale: Single architecture principle about prop design

rule: When an optional API field being absent causes destructive behavior (e.g., removing an existing value), document this explicitly in the API and add unit tests, because callers may assume absent fields are simply skipped.
mode_rationale: Single API documentation convention

rule: Remove unused variables, imports, and code rather than leaving them in the codebase.
Permission denial messages should be specific about what the user cannot do and include actionable resolution steps such as contacting an administrator.
mode_rationale: Single error message convention

rule: When making changes for a cloud fix or hotfix, prefer creating a wrapper/composition component rather than modifying a shared base component to minimize risk to other consumers.
mode_rationale: Single architecture principle for hotfixes

rule: Do not commit empty test files; either write meaningful tests or remove the file until tests are ready.
Use trace-level logging for high-frequency or verbose operational messages so they are off by default but can be enabled when needed for debugging.
Use a structured retry policy with backoff and max attempts instead of infinite loops for error recovery and reconnection logic.
In Scala, place constants and static-like members in a companion object rather than inline in the class body.
mode_rationale: Single Scala code organization convention

rule: Name variables and types using terminology from the current domain context, not carried over from unrelated services or modules.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention

rule: Add unit tests that serve as executable examples showing how a component works, especially for non-trivial stream processing or data transformation logic.
mode_rationale: Single testing principle

rule: Status enums should include an explicit error state and propagate error details so consumers can handle failures gracefully.
mode_rationale: Single enum design convention

rule: Use explicit boolean props rather than optional function props to control whether UI elements are shown, as optional functions are less verbose and harder to reason about.
mode_rationale: Single React prop design convention

rule: Use the same term for the same concept across all API endpoints to avoid confusion; do not introduce synonyms (e.g., 'authors' vs 'owners') for the same entity.
mode_rationale: Single API naming convention

rule: When an endpoint is not paginated, set the internal query limit high enough that it's effectively unreachable to prevent data from being silently truncated in the UI.
mode_rationale: Single API design convention

rule: Extract hardcoded limits (like max result size) into parameters with default values so tests can set low limits to verify boundary behavior without needing large datasets.
mode_rationale: Single testability convention

rule: Validation methods should receive the full objects they need to validate rather than relying on callers to extract specific fields; callers should not assume which fields will be checked.
mode_rationale: Single method signature convention

rule: Use configuration variables or environment-level constants for values like Docker image tags in tests rather than hardcoding them, to reduce maintenance burden when they change.
mode_rationale: Single test maintenance convention

rule: In a monorepo, import shared package components from their dist (built) files rather than directly from source to ensure consistent module resolution and avoid compilation issues.
mode_rationale: Single monorepo import convention

rule: Remove console.log and other console statements before merging; do not use console output as a workaround for unused variable warnings.
mode_rationale: Single code hygiene rule

rule: Define table column configurations in a file co-located with the table component rather than in a parent view; pass data, not column definitions, as props.
mode_rationale: Single component colocation convention

rule: When a store (e.g., useStore) is globally accessible, consume it directly in the component that needs it rather than prop-drilling it from a router or parent.
mode_rationale: Single state management convention

rule: Import UI components from the Domino design system wrapper library instead of directly from Ant Design; if the wrapper doesn't support a needed variant, flag it for extension.
mode_rationale: Single component import convention

rule: When a column renderer or inline JSX block grows complex, extract it into its own component rather than leaving it as a function in a utility file.
mode_rationale: Single code organization convention

rule: Define a TypeScript union type for sets of related string literal keys instead of using raw strings, so new values are easy to add and type-checked.
mode_rationale: Single TypeScript typing convention

rule: When logging service operations, include the request filters and parameters in the log message so that issues can be diagnosed from logs alone.
mode_rationale: Single logging content convention

rule: When adding or modifying fields in a public API contract, check whether existing system or integration tests need to be updated to cover the new field.
mode_rationale: Single testing maintenance convention

rule: Use fake implementations (in-memory, lightweight stand-ins) instead of mocks in integration and unit tests for more realistic behavior and easier maintenance.
mode_rationale: Single testing strategy convention

rule: When adding log statements to a method, include all relevant state values (e.g., flags, IDs, configuration) rather than logging only a subset.
mode_rationale: Single logging completeness convention

rule: Use the correct semantic component for the interaction — do not use a Link component as a button or vice versa.
Do not commit empty files to the repository; remove them before merging.
Add TODO labels to mark known issues or temporary workarounds in test code so they can be tracked and addressed later.
mode_rationale: Single test annotation convention

rule: Use framework-provided components (e.g., react-router's Redirect) instead of building custom equivalents unless there is a clear justification.
mode_rationale: Single architecture principle about framework usage

rule: When testing multiple expected failures, assert each one individually rather than relying on a loop that stops at the first failure.
Use specific selectors in e2e tests (e.g., 'I wait to see the checkbox "Label"') rather than generic text checks, to ensure the correct element type and content are verified.
Avoid using `any` type in TypeScript; use proper types or generics instead.
Do not mark test functions as async unless they contain awaited expressions.
Maintain consistent import ordering with absolute imports before relative imports, and ensure Prettier/linting enforces it automatically.
mode_rationale: Passive style rule about import ordering

rule: Keep React state as local as possible to the component that uses it, rather than lifting it to a container that doesn't directly need it.
Use a CodeEditor component (in read-only mode) to display code snippets rather than plain text elements, for proper syntax highlighting and formatting.
Do not mark test cases as async unless they contain an await expression; unnecessary async obscures which tests are truly asynchronous.
When migrating between SDK versions behind a feature flag, keep the v1 code path unchanged so it can serve as a reliable rollback; only modify the v2 path.
mode_rationale: Single guideline about not modifying v1 code paths during migration

rule: When creating wrapper abstractions over third-party SDKs, use generic names (e.g., listObjects) rather than leaking vendor-specific version details (e.g., listObjectsV2) into public APIs.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for wrapper abstractions

rule: Do not store an InputStream as a class attribute because it can only be read once; instead hold a factory lambda that produces a fresh stream on each call, or use a stream-plus-metadata pattern like AWS ResponseInputStream.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence design rule about InputStream handling

rule: In Scala, use case classes instead of regular classes with private fields and @unused annotations; case classes provide public getters automatically and eliminate boilerplate.
mode_rationale: Simple Scala idiom preference

rule: In Scala, use case class val accessors (obj.key) instead of Java-style getter methods (obj.getKey); follow Scala naming conventions for accessors and mutators.
mode_rationale: Simple Scala naming convention

rule: Keep related abstractions (interfaces, wrappers, models) in the same package/directory even when build-system module boundaries differ from language package boundaries.
When renaming an attribute or method, update all call sites directly rather than adding a compatibility alias that mismatches the return type or semantics.
mode_rationale: Simple refactoring convention about updating call sites

rule: Do not add or maintain production wrapper methods that are only used in tests; instead, use the underlying client directly in test code to keep the production API surface minimal.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence rule about production API surface

rule: Only include fields in wrapper/DTO types that are actually consumed by callers; remove fields carried over from the underlying SDK that have no current use.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to keep wrapper types minimal

rule: When injecting configuration, unpack the settings object and pass only the specific settings a component needs rather than passing the entire settings object.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence dependency injection guideline

rule: New configuration settings should be added to the most relevant existing settings class rather than a generic top-level Settings file, using optional fields with sensible defaults to avoid touching application.conf.
mode_rationale: Simple convention about config class placement

rule: When a flag represents more than a simple on/off state, use an enumeration with descriptive values instead of a boolean to make the intent and transitions clear.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence naming/type convention

rule: When modifying or replacing API endpoints, keep the old endpoint available for backward compatibility, especially when external clients or agents may depend on it.
mode_rationale: Simple backward compatibility guideline

rule: When testing state-dependent operations, include negative test cases that verify the operation does NOT trigger in states where it should be inactive.
Mutable state accessed from multiple threads must be properly synchronized using thread-safe constructs like AtomicReference, not plain mutable variables.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence concurrency safety rule

rule: Do not route simple operations through complex machinery like state machines when a direct implementation would suffice; choose the simplest approach that meets actual requirements.
mode_rationale: Simple architecture guideline about choosing simplest approach

rule: When documenting configurable values or behaviors in comments, ensure the comment describes the full behavior including edge cases, not just the common case.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence documentation convention

rule: When introducing configurable timeouts that affect user-facing behavior, ensure public documentation exists so users know about the limits and how to adjust them.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation guideline about timeout visibility

rule: When introducing new build tools or changing the build pipeline, update the corresponding architecture documentation to reflect the changes.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence documentation convention

rule: Import path aliases that reference a /dist directory should resolve to compiled output, not source code; mixing these causes broken React contexts and confusing build behavior.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence build configuration rule

rule: Values that can be computed from other stored data should not be persisted as separate database fields; instead, compute them at read time from their source data to avoid synchronization issues.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence data modeling convention

rule: When exposing configuration values through API endpoints, assess whether sensitive secrets could leak and document the security boundary (e.g., superuser-only access).
mode_rationale: Single-sentence security review guideline

rule: Add a blank line between logically distinct blocks (e.g., before an if-statement that follows unrelated code) to improve readability.
mode_rationale: Simple formatting/readability convention

rule: Place conversion or mapping logic on the domain class or its companion object rather than in an unrelated service, so the logic is co-located with the type it transforms.
When moving code into shared/common modules, extract a clean trait or interface that is free of external library dependencies so consumers are not forced to pull in transitive dependencies.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence architecture guideline about dependency isolation

rule: Within a frontend module's own components, use relative imports instead of absolute/aliased imports to keep the module self-contained and avoid circular dependency risks.
Use existing centralized mock data (e.g., from api/__mocks__) instead of creating duplicate mock files in individual component directories.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence testing convention about mock reuse

rule: Do not mark fields as optional without a clear reason; unnecessary optionals introduce null-checking burden and weaken type safety.
mode_rationale: Simple type design convention

rule: When adding or changing a field mapping or business rule, add or update a corresponding test to verify the new behavior is exercised.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence testing convention about coverage

rule: After a state-restoring mutation (e.g., restore, unarchive), refetch the current entity's data and stay on the detail page rather than navigating the user back to the list view.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence UX/data-fetching guideline

rule: When modifying data-fetching or transformation expressions, verify the return type matches what consumers expect (e.g., avoid accidentally returning an array of arrays instead of a flat array).
mode_rationale: Single-sentence type safety convention

rule: Prefer a single map operation over separate map and zip calls when transforming collections, to reduce intermediate allocations and improve readability.
mode_rationale: Simple performance/readability convention

rule: Create purpose-specific authorization roles for distinct use cases rather than repurposing existing roles with similar-but-different semantics, to avoid coupling and accidental permission drift.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence authorization design guideline

rule: When a callback passed to useEffect becomes stable (e.g., wrapped in useCallback with empty deps), add it to the useEffect dependency array rather than omitting it with an eslint-disable.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence React hooks convention

rule: When a function parameter has a sensible default value, make it optional with that default rather than requiring callers to always provide it.
mode_rationale: Simple API design convention about parameter defaults

rule: When the same literal string or value appears multiple times in a test scenario, extract it into a variable defined in the Background section to reduce duplication and ease maintenance.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence test maintenance convention

rule: Minimize assertions on specific UI copy in E2E tests; assert on key functional outcomes rather than exact text strings to make tests resilient to copy changes.
mode_rationale: Simple e2e testing resilience guideline

rule: Make data-testid attributes and accessibility labels dynamic to reflect component state (e.g., expanded vs collapsed) so tests and assistive tools can distinguish between states.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence accessibility/testing convention

rule: When tests modify shared configuration (feature flags, system settings), verify that the changes are scoped so they do not interfere with other tests running in parallel.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence test isolation convention

rule: Every new domain class or service should have a corresponding test spec file; do not merge new logic without test coverage.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence test coverage requirement

rule: Remove all debug print statements (println, console.log, etc.) before merging; use structured logging instead if runtime output is needed.
When an API endpoint can return a large number of items, use a configurable page size (with a sensible default) and document known cases where the default may be insufficient.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence API design guideline about pagination

rule: When code impersonates a user or uses elevated privileges, add an inline comment explaining why it is safe to do so and what authorization mechanism protects the call.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence convention to add inline comments for privilege escalation

rule: Service implementations in Guice injection modules should be bound as eager singletons using .asEagerSingleton() to ensure only one instance is created.
mode_rationale: Simple coding convention for Guice binding style

rule: API controller methods should log success at info level and errors at error level, including structured context (userId, entity IDs, request parameters) in both cases; extract a reusable executeOperation helper to reduce verbosity.
mode_rationale: Multi-step procedure: log at specific levels, include structured context, extract reusable helper

rule: Add trace-level log statements at service method entry points to aid debugging without polluting info-level logs.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to add trace logging at entry points

rule: When a method delegates to a dependency, write a unit test that verifies the dependency is called with the correct arguments, not just that the method succeeds.
mode_rationale: Single testing guideline about verifying dependency arguments

rule: When logging request or response data (headers, payloads), verify that authentication tokens, secrets, or credentials are not included in the log output.
mode_rationale: Simple security check to apply when logging request/response data

rule: In Scala, catch NonFatal(ex) instead of RuntimeException to avoid swallowing fatal errors like OutOfMemoryError while still handling all recoverable failures.
mode_rationale: Single coding convention for Scala exception handling

rule: Error log messages must include the operation that failed and relevant entity identifiers (e.g., modelId, userId) to enable effective debugging.
mode_rationale: Simple convention for error log content

rule: Calling .get on an Option is a code smell; refactor the return type or use pattern matching, getOrElse, or fold to handle the None case safely.
mode_rationale: Single coding convention to avoid unsafe Option.get

rule: API response type names must make sense for every endpoint that uses them; if a response is shared across endpoints, choose a generic name rather than one tied to a specific operation.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for shared API response types

rule: When logging exceptions, pass the exception object as a parameter to the logger method so the full stack trace is captured, rather than only logging the message string.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to pass exception objects to loggers

rule: In user-facing text, capitalize Domino-specific entity names (Workspace, Model API, Artifacts, Datasets) but use lowercase for generic terms (published apps, docs).
mode_rationale: Simple capitalization rule for UI text

rule: Capitalize product feature names (e.g., 'Exports', 'Feature Store') but use lowercase for generic actions (e.g., 'export files', 'import data').
Use a consistent format for contextual help links across the product, such as 'Learn more about {feature}' without trailing punctuation.
mode_rationale: Simple formatting convention for help links

rule: Every TODO comment in code must have an associated Jira ticket to ensure it gets tracked and eventually addressed.
mode_rationale: Simple rule: every TODO needs a Jira ticket

rule: System tests and integration tests should automatically collect debug artifacts (e.g., support bundles, logs) when jobs or operations fail, to aid post-failure investigation.
API tests should explicitly assert HTTP status codes rather than only checking response bodies, to catch unexpected error responses early.
Magic numbers (e.g., sleep durations, timeouts) that are used in multiple places should be extracted into named constants or shared configuration to keep them in sync.
Do not place conflicting version constraints for the same dependency in a single requirements file; use a single combined constraint (e.g., `>=1.0,<2.3`) or separate requirements files per context.
When adding behavior gated by a feature flag, add unit tests that validate the code path under the new flag to prevent regressions from untested branches.
mode_rationale: Simple testing guideline for feature-flagged code

rule: Do not skip or comment out tests without documenting the reason and creating a Jira ticket to track re-enabling them.
mode_rationale: Simple rule: no skipped tests without reason and ticket

rule: In React components, prefer useRef over useState for values that do not need to trigger re-renders, reserving useState for values that affect the rendered output.
mode_rationale: Simple React convention for useRef vs useState

rule: Components should inject their own data into callbacks rather than receiving pre-curried functions from parent levels; keep data binding close to the source.
mode_rationale: Single architecture guideline about data binding in callbacks

rule: Do not add new eslint-disable exceptions for useEffect dependency arrays; include all dependencies, especially simple values like booleans that are unlikely to cause issues.
mode_rationale: Simple rule: no new eslint-disable for useEffect deps

rule: Wrap React Context provider values in useMemo to prevent unnecessary re-renders of all child components consuming the context.
Files named styles.tsx should only contain styled components; standalone React components with their own logic belong in separate files.
The `useX` naming convention is reserved for React hooks; do not use it for component props or callback names.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for React hooks

rule: Name components after what they actually are; do not include 'Button' in a component name if the component is not a button.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for component accuracy

rule: In Scala, use `NonFatal` in catch blocks to avoid catching fatal errors (like OutOfMemoryError) that should cause the service to fail.
mode_rationale: Duplicate of catch-nonfatal pattern; simple Scala convention

rule: When a default string value is used in multiple places, extract it into a shared constant to ensure consistency and ease of maintenance.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to extract repeated default values

rule: Return the correct HTTP status code for the error condition; use 403 for forbidden access, not arbitrary 4xx codes.
mode_rationale: Simple rule to return correct HTTP status codes

rule: Do not create container/wrapper components when there is no heavy logic or backend calls to justify the separation; keep the component tree flat when possible.
In BDD feature files, use `Then` to begin verification/assertion steps, not `And` or other keywords, to clearly distinguish actions from verifications.
mode_rationale: Simple BDD convention for Then keyword usage

rule: Add explanatory comments for test steps whose purpose is not immediately clear, especially workarounds for previous bugs or non-deterministic behavior.
Use Option/Maybe types (e.g., Option[String]) to represent optional values instead of empty strings or other sentinel values, even when it requires additional validation work.
mode_rationale: Simple coding convention for optional values

rule: When test steps need entity IDs, extract them from API response bodies rather than parsing them out of URLs, which is fragile and couples tests to URL structure.
mode_rationale: Simple testing convention for extracting entity IDs

rule: When introducing new environment variables or config that changes routing or behavior, default to the safe/no-op value initially and change it in a follow-up PR to prevent breaking production.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to default new config to safe values

rule: When adding new files that must be present in Docker images (e.g., .env files, config), add a corresponding container-structure-test entry to verify the file exists in the built image.
mode_rationale: Simple rule to add structure tests for new Docker artifacts

rule: When adding new configuration keys, place them in the existing section for that feature or domain rather than adding them in an unrelated location in the config file.
mode_rationale: Simple organization convention for config files

rule: Keep retry/polling implementation details (e.g., backoff handlers) internal to helper functions rather than exposing them to callers, so the underlying retry library can be swapped without breaking consumers.
mode_rationale: Simple encapsulation guideline for retry logic

rule: Before writing custom polling or wait logic in tests, check if an existing helper method already handles the use case (e.g., wait_for_status) to avoid duplicating logic and keep tests concise.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to check for existing helpers before writing new ones

rule: When fetching data that sets multiple state variables, ensure error handlers also reset dependent state (e.g., flags and content) to prevent stale values from a previous successful fetch from persisting.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to reset dependent state in error handlers

rule: When styling icons or child elements within a React component, create a dedicated styled component rather than using nested class selectors, which are harder to maintain and less explicit.
mode_rationale: Simple styling convention preferring styled components over nested selectors

rule: Place data detection and validation logic on the backend where the authoritative information exists, rather than implementing complex client-side heuristics to infer server-side state.
mode_rationale: Simple architecture guideline to keep validation on the backend

rule: Static constants (arrays, objects, config values) that don't depend on props or state should be defined outside the component function to avoid unnecessary re-creation on each render.
mode_rationale: Simple performance convention for static values in React

rule: Use white-label-aware UI components (e.g., WaitSpinner) instead of brand-specific alternatives to avoid additional white-labelling logic.
Do not mark functions as async if they contain no await expressions.
mode_rationale: Simple rule: don't mark functions async without await

rule: Add a comment explaining non-obvious return statements so reviewers and future readers understand the intent.
Never use explicit `any` as a TypeScript type; use proper types, generics, or `unknown` instead.
Define styled components outside the JSX render tree, not inline within component markup.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to define styled components outside JSX

rule: Remove commented-out code before merging; use version control history to retrieve old code if needed.
Do not commit empty files; either add content or remove the file entirely.
Define static constants outside of React component functions to avoid unnecessary re-creation on each render.
When moving or modifying code near ESLint disable comments, verify the disable directive still applies to the intended line.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence lint discipline convention

rule: When deleting files, verify that no CI configs, scripts, or other files still reference the deleted path.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence deletion hygiene rule

rule: Place reusable test utilities in the shared tests-lib module rather than duplicating them in individual adapter or service test directories.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence code organization convention about where to place test utilities

rule: When an enum value's name no longer matches its semantic meaning due to backward compatibility, add a comment to the enum explaining the mapping (e.g., 'bitbucketServer' actually means 'datacenter').
mode_rationale: Single-sentence documentation convention for legacy enum values

rule: When the same transformation or sanitization logic appears in multiple places, move it onto the relevant domain object so consumers get the behavior for free.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence DRY principle for domain object transformations

rule: When an operation can fail for multiple reasons, write error messages that describe the observed symptom generically rather than asserting a specific cause that may be wrong.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence error message writing convention

rule: Do not mark a value as lazy if it is referenced on the very next line; the deferred initialization adds complexity with no benefit.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence Scala performance convention

rule: When filtering one collection by membership in another, convert the lookup collection to a Set first to achieve O(n) instead of O(n²) runtime.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence performance convention for collection lookups

rule: Use Scala's collect with a partial function instead of chaining filter and map when both selecting and transforming collection elements.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence Scala idiom preference

rule: Place methods specific to a single external provider (e.g., Bitbucket, GitHub) on that provider's implementation class rather than on the shared base client or trait.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence architecture convention for provider abstractions

rule: When using string replacement or pattern matching on URLs or paths, consider edge cases where the target substring may appear multiple times and ensure the logic handles all occurrences correctly.
When a single integration has fundamentally different implementations (e.g., cloud vs. datacenter), use separate feature flags for each so they can be enabled, disabled, and debugged independently.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence feature flag scoping convention

rule: Use map/filter/reduce instead of manually creating arrays and pushing elements into them.
Define styled components outside the component body to avoid re-creating them on every re-render.
Show a loading indicator while asynchronous data (e.g., dropdown options, form defaults) is being fetched.
Import shared UI components (tooltips, modals, etc.) from internal wrappers rather than directly from third-party libraries like antd.
Use the project's shared FormItem components for form fields to maintain consistent styling across the application.
Configuration key names must exactly match the names referenced in the code that reads them; mismatches cause silent failures.
Define configuration defaults in one canonical location (e.g., application.conf) and remove duplicate defaults from code to avoid divergence.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence DRY convention for configuration defaults

rule: Remove developer names, nicknames, and personal debug markers from code before merging.
mode_rationale: Simple hygiene rule: no developer names in code

rule: Name feature flags and configuration parameters descriptively so their purpose is immediately clear (e.g., uploadFileBatchCount instead of a vague abbreviation).
mode_rationale: Single-sentence naming convention for feature flags

rule: All user-facing pages and components must support white-labeling; never hard-code product-specific branding.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence branding convention

rule: Ensure every element in a React list has a unique key; duplicated keys cause rendering bugs and reconciliation issues.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence React correctness convention

rule: Scope CSS and styled-component overrides to the specific element or variant that needs them; avoid broad selectors that unintentionally affect unrelated components.
Remove all debugging print statements and temporary logging before submitting a PR for review.
mode_rationale: Simple hygiene rule: no debug logging in merged code

rule: Related API endpoints (e.g., list and get-by-id) should use the same DTO models to avoid divergent representations of the same resource.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence API design convention for DTO consistency

rule: Design domain models and DTOs so that invalid combinations of fields cannot be constructed; validate at creation time rather than relying on downstream checks.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence domain modeling convention

rule: Error messages, validation messages, and other user-facing strings should start with a capitalized word.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence copy style convention

rule: When the same logic appears in multiple files, extract it into a shared utility function to reduce duplication and ensure consistency.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence DRY principle

rule: When a component has multiple conditional branches based on feature flags, consider splitting it into separate components — one per flag state — to reduce complexity and make cleanup easier when the flag is removed.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence component organization convention for feature flags

rule: Test mock data should live in the shared mocks package rather than co-located with individual test files, so mocks can be reused across test suites.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence test organization convention

rule: Always use color variables or design tokens from the design system instead of hardcoded hex values in component styles.
Never use .get() on Scala Option values; use .map/.getOrElse or pattern matching to safely handle the None case with a meaningful fallback.
When introducing new log markers or event names, choose names that will not collide with planned features to avoid confusion when both coexist in production logs.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence naming convention for log markers

rule: End-to-end test steps that verify backend or API behavior should not depend on being on a specific UI page; use API-based approaches or parameterized steps to avoid brittle coupling.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence test architecture convention

rule: Use the project's shared/common modal component instead of creating custom one-off modals, to ensure visual consistency and make future restyling feasible.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence UI component reuse convention

rule: When passing a boolean prop with value true in JSX, use the shorthand form (e.g., `<Route exact>` instead of `<Route exact={true}>`).
When adding retry/wait logic for tests, extend the existing helper function rather than creating a new one, so all retry behavior lives in one place.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence DRY convention for test helpers

rule: When a test needs to wait for an asynchronous operation, identify the root cause of the delay and use a targeted wait (e.g., wait_for_status) rather than a generous blanket timeout.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence test quality convention for async waits

rule: When implementing exponential backoff or retry delay calculations, validate that the computed delay cannot overflow numeric limits at high attempt counts.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence defensive coding convention for retry math

rule: Use Option types to represent optional updates instead of pairing a value field with a boolean flag; this eliminates invalid states and reduces the API surface.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence API design convention for optional values

rule: When changing API method signatures or adding new parameters, update the corresponding scaladoc/swagger documentation in the same PR to describe the new behavior.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence documentation convention to keep docs in sync

rule: When adding or changing fields in a backend API, verify whether frontend mocks, TypeScript types, or swagger docs also need updating to stay in sync.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence cross-layer sync convention

rule: When conditionally applying an update based on an Option value in Scala, use `option.foreach` instead of if/else checks for more idiomatic and concise code.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence Scala idiom preference

rule: Every bug fix must include a test that reproduces the bug and verifies the fix.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence testing convention for bug fixes

rule: When gating a feature behind a feature flag, ensure all related UI elements (buttons, checkboxes, table columns) are hidden, not just the primary component.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence feature flag completeness convention

rule: Use established libraries (e.g., python-box) for data access and transformation instead of writing manual attribute-mapping code.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence code quality convention to use established libraries

rule: When polling for status completion, short-circuit on all terminal states (error, stopped, failed) not just the success state, to avoid infinite loops or wasted resources.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence rule about checking all terminal states during polling

rule: Drive hardcoded values (URLs, endpoints, magic strings) from constants or a config file rather than embedding them inline.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to avoid hardcoded values

rule: Keep CI pipeline steps minimal by combining related commands into a single step, and always enable shell debug flags (set -exuo pipefail) to aid troubleshooting.
mode_rationale: Coding convention for CI script structure

rule: When calling functions with positional boolean arguments, use named variables or code comments to document what each value means.
mode_rationale: Simple readability convention for boolean arguments

rule: Model authorization using the existing roles and permissions system rather than ad-hoc conditional checks; create tech debt tickets if refactoring is needed to unblock a release.
Split authorization checks to match the scope of each query — don't require broad permissions when the user only needs access to a narrow subset of data.
mode_rationale: Single principle about scoping authorization checks

rule: Add explicit type annotations to variables holding complex expressions (tuples, mapped collections) to improve readability.
mode_rationale: Simple readability convention for type annotations

rule: Use case-insensitive comparison (e.g., .lower) for user-facing search and filtering to avoid missed matches.
mode_rationale: Single-rule convention for search/filter comparisons

rule: Follow established project naming conventions (e.g., directory-per-hook) even when the resulting names are long, to maintain codebase consistency.
mode_rationale: Convention to prefer project naming patterns over short names

rule: Use the project's standard shared UI components (e.g., Domino modal) rather than creating custom one-off implementations, to maintain consistency and reduce duplication.
mode_rationale: Convention to use standard shared components

rule: Do not update local state or flags until the corresponding remote/async operation completes successfully, to avoid inconsistent state on failure.
mode_rationale: Single rule about ordering state updates after async ops

rule: Critical business logic (e.g., cache refresh, data synchronization) must have dedicated tests even when mocking is difficult; find a way to test it rather than skipping coverage.
mode_rationale: Convention that critical logic must have test coverage

rule: When adding or changing operational configuration (feature flags, cache TTLs, service parameters), document them in the relevant runbook so on-call engineers can find and adjust them.
mode_rationale: Convention to document operational config in runbooks

rule: Test helper functions that handle failures should call record_failure (or equivalent) so that support bundles and diagnostic artifacts are captured for debugging.
mode_rationale: Convention for test helpers to capture diagnostic artifacts

rule: When implementing retry logic for opaque exceptions, match on specific error messages rather than broad exception types to avoid retrying non-transient failures.
mode_rationale: Convention to match specific error messages in retry logic

rule: Log messages and status labels must use terminology that accurately reflects the operation being performed (e.g., 'Stopped' not 'Delete' when stopping a run).
mode_rationale: Simple rule about accurate log message wording

rule: Example manifests and sample configurations that use permissive security settings (e.g., wide-open NetworkPolicies) should include comments warning that production usage should be more targeted and restricted.
mode_rationale: Convention to comment on permissive security in examples

rule: In TypeScript, prefer `unknown` or generic type parameters over `any` to preserve type safety and enable type checking at call sites.
mode_rationale: TypeScript type safety convention

rule: When two methods return the same result, consolidate them into a single method to reduce maintenance burden and confusion.
mode_rationale: Simple DRY convention for methods

rule: Do not overload a DTO with fields that conceptually belong to a different entity; place each field on the DTO that owns that concept.
mode_rationale: Convention about DTO field ownership

rule: Error messages thrown from lookups should name the entity type and ID so operators can quickly identify what was not found.
mode_rationale: Simple convention for error message content

rule: When a find-entity-or-throw pattern appears multiple times, extract it into a dedicated `findOrThrow` method on the relevant manager/repository.
mode_rationale: Convention to extract repeated find-or-throw patterns

rule: When a refactor empties or nearly empties a file, delete it entirely rather than leaving a skeleton behind.
mode_rationale: Simple cleanup convention during refactors

rule: When a chained or nested expression is hard to parse at a glance, assign it to a descriptively-named local variable to improve readability.
When the same few lines of logic appear in multiple places within a service, factor them out into a dedicated method to reduce duplication.
mode_rationale: DRY convention for repeated logic

rule: When an Ant Design table column simply renders the field value as-is, rely on `dataIndex` alone and omit the `render` prop.
mode_rationale: Ant Design convention to avoid unnecessary render props

rule: Styled components should live in dedicated component or styles files, not in utility modules.
Route path constants and link builders should be co-located in the central routes definition, not scattered across utility files.
mode_rationale: Convention to co-locate route paths in central module

rule: Function and test step names should include the domain or system they interact with (e.g., 'keycloak') so readers can understand them without surrounding context.
BDD/Cucumber step definitions should be descriptive enough to understand when listed outside their specific test scenario, such as in step catalogs or documentation.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for BDD step definitions

rule: If a function is called only once and there is no reason to expect reuse, inline it at the call site rather than extracting it into a separate function.
Magic values and literals embedded in the middle of functions should be moved to configuration files and referenced from there.
mode_rationale: Convention to externalize magic values

rule: Debug log messages should include the relevant data being operated on (e.g., the config object) so developers can trace behavior without stepping through code.
When mapping a fixed set of values to corresponding outputs (e.g., icons, components), use a dictionary/map rather than if-else chains or switch statements.
mode_rationale: Code style convention for value-based mapping

rule: Boolean fields and variables should use the 'is' prefix (e.g., isWorkloadServiceExecution) to make their boolean nature immediately clear at the call site.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for booleans

rule: When iterating over items that require remediation or processing, extract the loop body into its own function with independent error handling so a failure on one item does not prevent processing the rest.
mode_rationale: Error handling convention for loop resilience

rule: Route components should use lazy loading so they are only fetched when the user navigates to that route, reducing the initial bundle size.
New routes for a feature area should be added to the existing module router rather than creating a parallel navigation entry, to avoid fragmenting routing logic.
mode_rationale: Architecture convention to avoid fragmenting routing

rule: In tests, use DOM API methods like getAttribute('href') to read element attributes instead of casting the element to a specific type to access properties directly.
If a function returns the same result on every call and takes no arguments, define its output as a module-level constant to avoid re-creating it on every render.
mode_rationale: Performance convention to avoid re-creation on render

rule: When a class only needs a subset of a configuration object, accept that narrow type directly instead of depending on the full configuration, to reduce coupling and simplify construction.
mode_rationale: Architecture convention to reduce coupling

rule: Pin third-party dependency versions in package.json to avoid unexpected breaking changes from upstream updates.
Reference color values from the shared colors.ts (or equivalent design token file) instead of hardcoding hex values in components.
Use the project's existing shared components (modals, buttons, etc.) rather than creating ad-hoc replacements, to maintain visual and behavioral consistency across the app.
mode_rationale: Convention to use shared components for consistency

rule: Extract magic numbers into named constants with meaningful names that explain the value's purpose.
When changing a variable's semantics (e.g., removing patch from a version string), update the variable name to reflect its new meaning.
mode_rationale: Naming convention to update names when semantics change

rule: When a test checks for an error condition, use explicit assertion mechanisms (e.g., pytest.raises) so the test actually fails when the error is absent, rather than silently passing.
mode_rationale: Testing convention for explicit error assertions

rule: Use the project's structured audit event system instead of ad-hoc logger.info calls for actions that need to be auditable, ensuring consistent format and queryability.
mode_rationale: Logging convention to use structured audit system

rule: Do not add logging to intermediate steps (e.g., per-chunk upload) when the concluding step already logs the complete action; log at the boundary that represents the completed operation.
When renaming a parameter in one file, update the corresponding parameter names in all related interfaces and adapters to maintain consistency across the codebase.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for cross-layer parameter consistency

rule: Before adding a new audit event, consider how frequently the triggering action occurs (e.g., every file preview click) and assess whether the logging volume is acceptable for the system.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence reminder to consider logging volume before adding audit events

rule: Use the correct HTTP status code for each operation: 201 for resource creation, 200 for responses with a body, and 204 for delete operations with no response body.
