mode_rationale: Simple convention mapping HTTP methods to status codes

rule: Use 'preview' instead of 'beta' in API path prefixes to align with the organization's established terminology for pre-GA endpoints.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention for API path prefixes

rule: Use PATCH instead of PUT for update operations to support partial updates and avoid requiring clients to send the full resource representation.
Name creation request DTOs using the pattern 'New[EntityName]' (e.g., NewResourceConfiguration) to clearly distinguish them from read models and update DTOs.
mode_rationale: Single naming pattern for creation DTOs

rule: Name update request DTOs using the pattern 'Updated[EntityName]' (e.g., UpdatedResourceConfiguration) to clearly distinguish them from creation and read models.
mode_rationale: Single naming pattern for update DTOs

rule: Write OpenAPI operation descriptions in third person (e.g., 'Gets all Deployment Target Types') rather than imperative form (e.g., 'Get all Deployment Target Types').
mode_rationale: Simple style convention for OpenAPI description phrasing

rule: Reference related entities by their primary key (ID) rather than by name or other mutable fields to avoid synchronization complexity if renaming is ever supported.
mode_rationale: Single rule about using IDs instead of mutable fields

rule: When a UI wizard creates a parent entity with its children (e.g., a deployment target with resource configurations), use a single creation endpoint rather than multiple sequential calls to avoid partial success states.
mode_rationale: Single design principle about atomic creation endpoints

rule: When removing entities that are referenced by other records (e.g., resource configurations referenced by model deployments), archive them instead of deleting to preserve referential integrity.
mode_rationale: Single rule about archiving vs deleting referenced records

rule: When modifying an API or data model, take the opportunity to fix misleading names in the same area rather than perpetuating them further.
mode_rationale: Simple guideline to fix bad names opportunistically

rule: Maintain separate admin and user-facing API endpoints when they require different field visibility or permission filtering, even if they access the same underlying data.
mode_rationale: Single architectural principle about API layer separation

rule: Ensure OpenAPI spec status codes match the actual runtime behavior of the API framework; if the framework always returns 200, document 200 rather than a theoretically correct but unimplemented status code.
mode_rationale: Simple rule to keep specs aligned with actual framework behavior

rule: Model child entities as subresources (e.g., /deploymentTargets/{id}/resourceConfigurations) when they need independent CRUD operations, rather than embedding them only in the parent.
mode_rationale: Single API design convention for child entity endpoints

rule: Use consistent phrasing and structure across similar API endpoint descriptions in OpenAPI specs.
Update/PATCH endpoints should not mark all fields as required; an update should allow modifying only a subset of fields.
mode_rationale: Single rule about PATCH field requirements

rule: When introducing a new API spec alongside an old one, create a ticket to track the removal of the old spec to prevent indefinite accumulation.
mode_rationale: Simple reminder to create cleanup tickets for old specs

rule: Add debug-level logging to status polling and readiness checks that includes the resource identifier, current count, and expected count.
mode_rationale: Single logging convention for polling/readiness checks

rule: When a design involves non-obvious resource allocation tradeoffs (e.g., volume sizing, memory splitting), document the behavior clearly for users.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation guideline for resource allocation decisions

rule: In pattern matching, let redundant or default-behavior cases be covered by the wildcard (_) case instead of listing them explicitly.
mode_rationale: Single pattern matching style convention

rule: Do not hardcode navigation paths inside UI components; define routes in a centralized location and import them.
mode_rationale: Simple rule to centralize route definitions

rule: Before reimplementing behavior manually, check if the component library already provides a prop for it (e.g., destroyInactiveTabPane for tab cleanup).
mode_rationale: Simple reminder to check library props before reimplementing

rule: Before writing new state management logic, check if a shared hook or utility already exists for the pattern (e.g., useSearchParamTabController for URL-synced tabs).
mode_rationale: Simple reminder to check for existing hooks before writing new ones

rule: Prefer idiomatic Scala collection operations like `.map(...).toMap` over imperative loop-and-accumulate patterns when building maps from collections.
mode_rationale: Single style convention for Scala collection operations

rule: When filtering and transforming a collection simultaneously, use `.collect` with pattern matching instead of chaining `.filter` and `.map` separately.
mode_rationale: Single Scala idiom preference for filter+map

rule: Initialize data structures with their intended values at declaration rather than creating empty structures and mutating them afterward.
mode_rationale: Simple coding convention about initialization style

rule: New external provider integrations (e.g., BitBucket, GitHub) must include system tests that exercise the real API endpoints, not just unit tests with mocks.
mode_rationale: Single testing requirement for external integrations

rule: Scenario and test names should precisely describe what behavior is under test, not make broader claims the test doesn't actually verify.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for test descriptions

rule: Use ES module imports (import/export) instead of CommonJS require; use .mts file extensions when needed to enable ESM syntax in TypeScript.
mode_rationale: Single import style convention

rule: Before adding a new dependency injection binding, check whether the same binding already exists in another injection module to prevent duplicate registrations.
Methods should live in the service that owns the relevant domain concept — don't add launcher-related logic to a launcher-job service or vice versa.
mode_rationale: Single architectural principle about service boundaries

rule: Class names should clearly communicate their purpose and domain concept; avoid vague, cryptic, or 'weird' names that force readers to inspect the implementation to understand intent.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for class clarity

rule: Add a description comment to entity classes and data structures whose purpose is not immediately obvious from the name, especially integration-specific DTOs.
Order import statements with absolute (third-party/package) imports first, followed by relative (local) imports.
Prefer separate styled components for each element instead of nesting style selectors within a parent styled component.
Use the shorthand <> fragment syntax instead of React.Fragment when no key prop is needed.
mode_rationale: Single JSX syntax preference

rule: Side effects like changing user selection or UI state after an async operation must be placed in the success handler, not unconditionally after the operation completes.
mode_rationale: Single error-handling rule about side effect placement

rule: Place components in subdirectories that reflect their domain hierarchy rather than leaving them at the feature root level.
mode_rationale: Simple file organization convention

rule: Use JSX expression interpolation instead of the + operator for string concatenation inside JSX templates.
Keep test fixture JSON files trimmed to the minimum data needed for the test scenario rather than using full production-sized responses.
mode_rationale: Simple testing convention about fixture data size

rule: When a match on Option returns None for None and transforms Some, use .map() instead of an explicit pattern match.
mode_rationale: Single Scala idiom preference for Option transforms

rule: E2E test scenarios that create resources (apps, workspaces, etc.) should include cleanup steps to stop or remove them after the test completes.
Before implementing inline utility logic, check for and use existing global helper functions (e.g., getAppName) already available in the codebase.
mode_rationale: Simple reminder to use existing utility functions

rule: When updating configuration values for a new version, ensure the same change is applied consistently to all relevant version entries and their defaults.
Always validate YAML files for structural errors like duplicate keys using an IDE or linter before submitting for review.
mode_rationale: Simple validation reminder for YAML files

rule: Do not resolve PR review comments until the underlying issue has actually been fixed in a commit.
mode_rationale: Simple PR workflow convention

rule: Ensure PR descriptions and summaries accurately reflect the actual code changes, including correct names for resources referenced.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation accuracy rule

rule: Use flexible version constraints (>=) in requirements files rather than compatible release (~=) to avoid breakage when other dependencies or vulnerability fixes require newer versions.
mode_rationale: Single dependency versioning convention

rule: Compute derived boolean values in the component that uses them rather than passing them as props from a parent, unless multiple children need the same value.
mode_rationale: Simple component design principle about derived values

rule: Use established package import aliases (e.g., @domino/core-ui/Link) instead of relative paths when importing shared components.
mode_rationale: Single import path convention

rule: When introducing new environment variables or setup requirements for running tests, document them in the README so developers know what to configure.
mode_rationale: Simple reminder to update docs when adding test prerequisites

rule: When configuration values fail to resolve, make downstream error messages actionable so developers can trace failures back to the missing configuration.
mode_rationale: Single guideline about error message quality

rule: Do not maintain multiple configuration keys that hold the same value; consolidate to a single canonical key used everywhere.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to consolidate duplicate config keys

rule: Add descriptive comment sections to test scenarios (e.g., linking to test case IDs) to improve readability and make failure triage easier.
mode_rationale: Single convention about adding descriptive comments to tests

rule: Async data fetches in UI components should handle errors gracefully and show loading feedback (spinner or disabled state) while the request is in flight.
mode_rationale: Standard UI convention for async data fetching

rule: Prefer explicit, typed data contracts (defined case classes/interfaces) over storing arbitrary JSON blobs, as defined contracts are easier to work with in code, tests, and database inspection.
mode_rationale: Simple preference for typed contracts over loose JSON

rule: When designing metadata structures for external integrations, capture all available identifying fields even if currently redundant, to avoid breaking changes when future features need them.
mode_rationale: Single guideline about capturing all available fields

rule: Avoid unnecessary intermediate nesting levels in data model field names; prefer flatter, clearer naming when the context is already established by the parent structure.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for flat data models

rule: When a React component's render method grows complex, extract logically distinct sections into dedicated sub-components for better readability and maintainability.
mode_rationale: Standard refactoring guideline for React components

rule: Before writing a new utility or computation, search the codebase for existing utilities that already compute the same value to avoid duplication.
Always use version specifiers (e.g., `>=`) when declaring Python dependencies in requirements files to avoid unexpected breakage from upstream changes.
mode_rationale: Single rule about version pinning in requirements files

rule: When modifying a shared test data file or fixture, verify that all other tests depending on that file still pass and are unaffected.
mode_rationale: Simple reminder to check downstream consumers of shared fixtures

rule: When validating values that cross service boundaries (e.g., Kubernetes quantities), prefer existing SDK/library parsing utilities over custom validation to ensure correctness.
mode_rationale: Single preference for SDK utilities over custom validation

rule: When fixing a bug in a class that lacks tests, create a test file and add tests covering the fix rather than leaving the class untested.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to add tests alongside bug fixes

rule: Prefer function-level test fixture scope over session scope unless sharing state across tests is explicitly required; place shared fixtures in a common fixtures file so other tests can discover them.
mode_rationale: Single guideline about test fixture scoping

rule: Route and utility function names should clearly describe their destination or purpose (e.g., `getProjectLauncherPath` instead of a generic name) so callers understand the navigation target.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for functions

rule: Use `2>/dev/null` to suppress stderr output silently; do not use `2>&1` when the intent is to discard errors rather than merge them with stdout.
mode_rationale: Single shell scripting convention

rule: Generate and include a correlation ID (e.g., UUID) in error responses so operators can correlate client-facing errors with server-side log entries for debugging.
mode_rationale: Single convention about including correlation IDs in errors

rule: When modifying or renaming a shared configuration file, audit and update every service that includes or depends on that file to avoid runtime failures.
mode_rationale: Simple reminder to audit consumers when modifying shared config

rule: Before renaming shared configuration files or resources, get explicit alignment from the domain owners to ensure the new name is semantically correct and agreed upon.
mode_rationale: Single guideline about getting alignment before renaming

rule: Correct typos in type names, interface names, and other identifiers immediately when discovered to prevent propagation through the codebase.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to fix typos immediately

rule: Do not nest configuration providers inside feature components; instead, use prop composition (e.g., passing a ReactNode to a prop like emptyMessage) to achieve the same behavior without tight coupling.
Use JSDoc-style comments (/** ... */) for component and function documentation so that IDEs can display them in hover hints and autocomplete.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation style convention

rule: When removing or renaming fields from serialized models, assess whether the change could break serialization compatibility and create a separate ticket to track the migration if so.
mode_rationale: Single guideline about tracking breaking changes separately

rule: Use template literals instead of string concatenation to build strings, and guard against potentially undefined values that would produce 'undefinedsome-str' when concatenated.
mode_rationale: Simple defensive coding convention

rule: Define named constants for magic numbers (e.g., max lengths, limits) so the value can be reused consistently and the intent is self-documenting.
Maintain consistent import ordering: absolute (third-party/package) imports must come before relative imports.
Keep all styled components in a single location per feature — either all in a dedicated styles file or all co-located in the component file, not split across both.
mode_rationale: Single convention about where to place styled components

rule: Do not pass empty no-op functions as event handlers when the framework or parent component already handles the behavior (e.g., form item onChange).
mode_rationale: Simple convention to avoid no-op handlers

rule: When the same logic appears in multiple components, extract it into a single shared function rather than duplicating it.
mode_rationale: Standard DRY principle guideline

rule: Repeated string literals (e.g., field names, keys) used in query building or data access should be defined as named constants within the class.
mode_rationale: Simple convention about named constants for strings

rule: When writing tests for serialization or type conversion logic, include edge case tests that verify correct behavior across type boundaries (e.g., retrieving an integer value as a Double).
mode_rationale: Single testing guideline about edge cases

rule: When adding boolean props to shared UI components, make them optional with a default value of false so callers only need to pass them when opting in.
mode_rationale: Simple API design convention for boolean props

rule: When adding logging inside nested or helper functions, verify the logger is within scope and actually producing output, as loggers invoked inside nested functions can silently fail.
Remove all commented-out code before submitting a PR; do not leave dead code in the codebase as it creates noise and confusion about intent.
Do not wrap child components in a render function unless the parent is actually injecting props through it; use the component directly as a child instead.
Never suppress or skip required dependencies in useEffect; if a function is used inside the effect, define it inside the effect body or properly declare it as a dependency.
Every async operation that can fail (API calls, form submissions) must have user-visible error feedback; never silently swallow failures.
mode_rationale: Single guideline about user-visible error feedback

rule: When using Ant Design Form components, let the Form manage field state rather than using it purely as a layout/styling wrapper with separate state management.
Do not wrap components in React fragments when there is only a single child element; fragments are only needed to group multiple sibling elements without adding a DOM node.
If a function does not reference any variables computed inside a React component (props, state, hooks), define it outside the component to avoid unnecessary re-creation on each render.
In tests, prefer using waitFor or similar async utilities to assert state changes rather than hardcoded setTimeout delays, which are flaky and slow.
mode_rationale: Single testing convention about async assertions

rule: Use React hooks (e.g., useHistory) to access framework-provided values instead of passing them through component props.
mode_rationale: Single React architecture convention

rule: Before building new UI elements, check if an existing shared component (e.g., HelpLink) already provides the needed functionality.
New admin UI entries and user-facing features must be gated behind a feature flag before merging.
mode_rationale: Single gating convention for new features

rule: Implement UI and styling in React components rather than server-side Scala templates to keep rendering logic consistent and maintainable.
mode_rationale: Single architectural preference for React over server templates

rule: When using well-known specifications (e.g., JSON Schema meta-schemas), reference the official spec rather than creating a custom copy.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to reference official specs

rule: When adding advanced OpenAPI schema features (e.g., additionalProperties with complex types), verify they are supported by the project's code generation tooling.
mode_rationale: Single reminder to check codegen support for schema features

rule: API schema fields with a known set of valid values should use an enum rather than a freeform string type.
mode_rationale: Simple API design convention for constrained fields

rule: API schema descriptions should be detailed and include examples so that complex structures are easy to understand without reading the implementation.
mode_rationale: Single documentation convention for API schemas

rule: When removing a property from an API schema, also remove it from the required array to avoid invalid specs.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence convention about keeping API spec fields in sync

rule: In meta-schemas (schemas that define schemas), avoid field names like 'description' or 'type' that conflict with the actual data fields they constrain, as this creates ambiguity.
mode_rationale: Naming convention to avoid ambiguity in meta-schemas

rule: API specifications should define the minimal contract; put validation logic and known-constant enforcement in application code rather than embedding it in the spec.
mode_rationale: Design principle about where validation logic belongs

rule: When an API spec represents a collection of schemas, model it as an array with typed items rather than nesting objects with redundant wrapper properties.
mode_rationale: Simple modeling convention for API spec collections

rule: When a type field in an API schema has a known fixed value, constrain it with an enum declaration to prevent invalid values.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence convention for constraining known values

rule: When handling process termination or state transitions, branch on the specific signal that caused the transition (e.g., stop properties being set) rather than the current broad state, to avoid catching unrelated exit reasons.
mode_rationale: Coding convention about precise conditional branching

rule: If a React component prop is always passed by every caller, declare it as required rather than optional to accurately represent the component's contract.
Test step definitions should be explicit about what they do and named intuitively so other engineers will discover and reuse them; avoid combining unrelated behaviors into a single opaque step.
mode_rationale: Best practice for test step naming and reuse

rule: Prefer explicit constructor parameters over Scala implicits because implicit constructors create hidden nullary constructors that cause surprising instantiation errors and obscure the dependency graph.
Follow the project's established import ordering convention to keep files consistent and diffs clean.
When multiple authorization paths perform nearly identical permission checks, combine them into a single code path to reduce duplication and prevent drift.
mode_rationale: Architectural guideline to reduce duplication

rule: When modifying role-based access controls, explicitly verify with the product manager which user tiers (e.g., lite users) should or should not have access to the affected endpoints.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence reminder to verify RBAC changes with PM

rule: Use React's useId() hook to generate stable, unique IDs for DOM elements instead of manual ID generation or random strings.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to use a specific React hook for IDs

rule: In end-to-end tests, ensure the user performing an action and the entity being tested are not the same or co-located, to avoid false positives from implicit permissions.
mode_rationale: Testing principle about separating actor from subject

rule: When adding configuration values to a service, verify whether existing config-fetching code could pull and override those values from a remote source like Nucleus, and ensure the override precedence is correct.
mode_rationale: Reminder to check config override precedence when adding values

rule: Use the framework's default timeout values in tests unless there is a specific, documented reason to override them; arbitrary custom timeouts add unnecessary divergence.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to use framework defaults

rule: Use useMemo to create a debounced handler and useCallback for the onChange, rather than syncing debounced values through useEffect.
Maintain consistent import ordering with absolute imports grouped before relative imports.
When a conditional structure checks against multiple discrete values and is likely to grow, use an array with .includes() instead of if/else or switch chains.
mode_rationale: Refactoring guideline for extensible conditionals

rule: Do not add explicit cleanup steps in tests when the test setup or fixture already handles that cleanup automatically.
API endpoint implementations should live in the shared @domino/api package rather than being defined inline in feature modules.
mode_rationale: Convention about where API endpoints should live

rule: Index files should only re-export from other modules; do not define function implementations directly in index files.
When a type is used across module boundaries, export it from the shared package rather than re-defining or importing it from an internal path.
mode_rationale: Convention for cross-module type sharing

rule: When a user action triggers multiple sequential API calls, the UI must indicate that work is in progress so the user knows something is happening.
Modals should call generic callbacks (onClose, onSuccess) provided by the parent, not directly trigger side effects like data refetching that are the parent's concern.
mode_rationale: Architectural convention about modal responsibility boundaries

rule: Never use explicit `any` as a type annotation when TypeScript can infer the type automatically; rely on type inference instead.
When modifying a handler or function used by multiple callers, check existing integration/e2e tests that cover all call sites before merging.
mode_rationale: Reminder to check test coverage when changing shared code

rule: Move generic utility code (e.g., AWS client wrappers) into common shared modules rather than domain-specific packages; remove domain-specific dependencies (like config objects) in favor of primitive parameters to keep the code reusable.
mode_rationale: Architectural convention for utility code placement

rule: Name wrapper types using consistent Request/Response pairs — if input types are named 'Request', output types should be named 'Response' (not 'Result') to match SDK v2 conventions.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for API wrapper types

rule: When wrapping exceptions across SDK versions, define proper custom exceptions that set both message and cause on the parent Throwable so stack traces remain useful for debugging.
Do not include code in a PR that cannot be validated without the context of a future PR; defer it so reviewers can assess correctness with full context.
mode_rationale: PR hygiene rule about reviewable scope

rule: API and wrapper methods should return typed data structures rather than pre-serialized strings; let callers decide how to format or log the data. If the return value is not needed, return Unit.
mode_rationale: API design convention about return types

rule: When the same conversion or transformation logic (e.g., safe number parsing that prevents NaN) is repeated multiple times, extract it into a shared utility function.
When adding new event types to an existing set, follow the established naming convention of the existing events (e.g., if existing events use 'Change' suffix, don't switch to 'Updated' or 'Edit').
mode_rationale: Naming convention for event type consistency

rule: Before adding authentication redirects or route guards to a React component, verify the component's mounting lifecycle — if it is always mounted while the app is active, the guard may unintentionally affect unrelated routes.
mode_rationale: Architectural awareness check before adding route guards

rule: Always roll back temporary or unstable package versions and use a stable develop version before merging a PR.
mode_rationale: Simple pre-merge convention for dependency versions

rule: Use static Object methods like Object.keys().forEach() instead of calling prototype methods directly on objects.
mode_rationale: Coding style preference for static Object methods

rule: Only include dependencies that are actually used inside the useEffect callback; extra dependencies cause unnecessary re-runs and potential bugs.
mode_rationale: React convention for accurate dependency arrays

rule: Order import statements so that absolute imports come before relative imports.
Use themeHelper tokens (e.g., themeHelper('margins.tiny')) for margins and spacing instead of hardcoded values.
Ensure all useEffect dependency arrays are complete and correct; missing deps should produce linting errors that must be resolved, not suppressed.
mode_rationale: Linting convention to fix rather than suppress warnings

rule: Let API utility functions throw exceptions and handle errors with try/catch or .catch() at the call site rather than passing onSuccess/onError callback parameters.
mode_rationale: Error handling convention preferring try/catch over callbacks

rule: API request functions should not handle error notification (e.g., console.error or toast); error display is the responsibility of the calling component or hook.
When catching errors from API calls, consider whether the user needs to be informed through UI feedback (e.g., a toast or inline message), not just a console.error.
mode_rationale: UX convention to inform users of errors visually

rule: Replace nested ternary expressions with if/else blocks, early returns, or helper functions for better readability.
Move constant values outside of component functions and name them using UPPER_SNAKE_CASE (e.g., MAX_RETRY_COUNT).
mode_rationale: Naming convention for constant extraction and casing

rule: Give meaningful names to numeric literals so their purpose is clear without additional context.
Add documentation to functions that use domain-specific numbers or non-obvious logic, explaining what the values represent and what the function returns.
mode_rationale: Documentation convention for domain-specific logic

rule: When a function's return type is explicitly declared, avoid redundant type casts on the returned value — the type annotation already provides type safety.
mode_rationale: TypeScript convention to avoid redundant casts

rule: When a React component has nothing to render, return `null` instead of an empty or meaningless DOM element like `<span />`.
When using styled-components, define all styles in the styled component definition rather than using inline `style` props — this keeps styling centralized and consistent.
In JSX, pass string literal props without curly braces — use `prop="value"` instead of `prop={"value"}`.
Prefer built-in array methods like `.some()` over manual loops or reduce patterns when checking a condition across array elements.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence coding style preference

rule: Keep utility and helper functions in dedicated utility files rather than defining them inside component files.
mode_rationale: Simple file organization convention

rule: Move constant values and static derived data outside the component body to avoid recomputing them on every render.
Define colors in the project's centralized color constants file (e.g., `colors.ts`) rather than hardcoding hex values in components.
mode_rationale: Simple convention about where to define colors

rule: When using styled-components, do not use `className` for styling — use styled-component composition instead. Reserve `className` as a last resort for deeply nested third-party components you cannot reach otherwise.
mode_rationale: Single styling convention for styled-components

rule: When the same style override appears in multiple views, extract it into the shared component that owns the styling rather than duplicating it across consumers.
mode_rationale: Simple DRY principle for style overrides

rule: When a piece of UI (like a modal or dialog) is defined inline within another component, extract it into its own component file and render it from the parent.

When adding ScalaDoc to a method, document all parameters and include a summary of what the method does — partial documentation is worse than none because it implies the undocumented parts are unimportant.

If a method mutates a builder or performs side effects and callers discard the return value, the return type should be Unit — a non-Unit return type implies a pure transformation the caller should use.
mode_rationale: Single convention about return type accuracy

rule: Write test names as human-readable behavioral descriptions (e.g., 'should return empty list when no items match') rather than mirroring method names (e.g., 'should testGetItems').
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for tests

rule: Data wrapper or result types should not extend Exception or RuntimeException unless they are genuinely intended to be thrown as exceptions.
mode_rationale: Single type design convention

rule: When a function may not handle all input cases, return a functional error type like Try or Either instead of throwing exceptions for unmatched cases.
mode_rationale: Simple error handling convention

rule: Cross-cutting dependencies like ExecutionContext that are already available in the dependency graph should be taken as implicit constructor parameters rather than passed explicitly at each call site.
mode_rationale: Single dependency injection convention

rule: When a method returns a generic type (like String) that represents domain-specific content, add documentation explaining what the returned value represents.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation rule for return values

rule: When consolidating similar exception handling branches during refactoring, verify with the original author or through analysis that the differences between branches were not intentional.
mode_rationale: Single caution rule during refactoring

rule: Integration tests should use real implementations (e.g., real persisters and repositories), not mocks; mocking in integration tests defeats their purpose and reduces coverage of real interactions.
Capitalize domain-specific terms (e.g., Dataset, ID) and proper abbreviations in client-facing error messages and API route descriptions for consistency and professionalism.
mode_rationale: Simple naming/capitalization convention

rule: API controllers should include a catch-all exception handler that returns a 500 status code to prevent unhandled exceptions from leaking implementation details.
mode_rationale: Single error handling convention for controllers

rule: When migrating between SDK versions, verify that string representations and serialized outputs match between old and new versions, especially if those outputs are consumed downstream.
mode_rationale: Single verification rule during migrations

rule: Include a `DeletedAt` (or equivalent) timestamp field in entities that support soft deletion, so deletions are auditable and reversible.
mode_rationale: Simple data modeling convention

rule: All CRUD service methods should include telemetry events for observability; plan telemetry from the start even if it ships in a follow-up PR.
mode_rationale: Single observability convention

rule: CRUD integration tests should include explicit authorization test cases to verify that unauthorized users cannot access or modify protected resources.
mode_rationale: Simple testing convention about auth coverage

rule: Add a comment explaining why a value is intentionally discarded (e.g., `val _ = ...` in Scala) so future developers do not accidentally remove the side-effecting call.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation rule for discarded values

rule: A field's name must accurately reflect the type of value it holds — e.g., if the value is a resource name, the field should be called `pvName`, not `pvId`.
mode_rationale: Single naming accuracy convention

rule: When a field's context already implies its type, use the shorter name to reduce redundancy (e.g., `pvcName` instead of `persistentVolumeClaimName` when inside a PVC-related object).
mode_rationale: Simple naming brevity convention

rule: Always capture the return value of `setTimeout` (the timeout ID) and use it with `clearTimeout` for proper cleanup; cancel the timeout when the async operation completes.
mode_rationale: Single cleanup convention for timeouts

rule: Define static constants (values that never change between renders) outside the React component body to avoid unnecessary re-creation on every re-render.
mode_rationale: Simple React performance convention

rule: Replace magic numbers with named constants that convey the value's meaning and make future changes easier.
Configuration files of the same kind (e.g., ESLint configs) should use a consistent file format and extension across all packages in a monorepo.
mode_rationale: Simple consistency convention for config files

rule: Magic numbers and arbitrary constants must include a comment explaining how the value was chosen and what it relates to.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation rule for constants

rule: Every TODO comment in the codebase must reference a Jira ticket so the work is tracked and not forgotten.
mode_rationale: Single convention for TODO comments

rule: PRs should only contain changes related to the ticket or feature described in the title; unrelated modifications should be split into separate PRs.
mode_rationale: Simple PR scoping convention

rule: Debug print statements (println, console.log, etc.) must be removed or replaced with proper logging before a PR is merged.
Enum values in Scala should follow camelCase naming convention to stay consistent with the codebase style.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention for enums

rule: When changing serialization formats, markup patterns, or data structures, verify that existing persisted data will still be correctly interpreted after the change.
mode_rationale: Single verification rule for format changes

rule: When setting multiple fields on the same form or state object, combine them into a single call rather than making multiple sequential calls.
mode_rationale: Simple performance convention for state updates

rule: When renaming or reformatting identifiers, apply the same convention uniformly across all related files to avoid inconsistency.
mode_rationale: Single consistency convention for naming

rule: When catching exceptions, always preserve the original exception as the cause; never silently swallow or discard exception information.
Within a single test scenario, use a consistent interaction approach (UI or API) rather than mixing both without justification.
mode_rationale: Simple testing consistency convention

rule: When introducing new console methods or APIs, verify they pass the project's ESLint rules before submitting the PR.
mode_rationale: Single pre-submit verification rule

rule: When a new UI pattern (like a status tag) is introduced in a feature module, extract it as a reusable component in the shared packages/ui library.
When a test library target (e.g., tests-lib) already bundles common dependencies like the core library and test frameworks, do not re-declare those dependencies in consuming BUILD targets.
When filtering, sorting, and pagination logic is duplicated across modules, extract it into a shared commons library and have modules extend it rather than re-implementing it.
mode_rationale: Simple DRY convention for shared logic

rule: Do not use unexplained magic numbers in code; when a reviewer has to ask 'why this number?', the value should be a named constant or accompanied by a comment explaining its derivation.
mode_rationale: Single readability convention for constants

rule: API route parameter definitions should include $ref or enum constraints to enable automatic API request validation rather than accepting unvalidated strings.
mode_rationale: Single API validation convention

rule: API method signatures should use typed enums (e.g., SortableColumn) instead of raw String parameters, and the enum should be referenced in API docs for validation.
Scala imports should follow a consistent ordering convention (java/javax first, then scala standard library, then project imports) and group related imports together.
mode_rationale: Simple import ordering convention

rule: Test specs should not contain unused test fixtures, setup data, or helper objects; clean up any test data that is no longer exercised by assertions.
mode_rationale: Simple test hygiene convention

rule: Write Storybook stories in CSF3 format, not MDX, which is no longer supported in Storybook 8.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use CSF3 format for stories

rule: Extract complex sub-renders and inline logic into separate components or pure functions to improve testability and readability.
mode_rationale: Single guideline about component decomposition

rule: Avoid nested ternary expressions in JSX; use early returns, variables, or helper functions for complex conditionals.
Use styled-component props for dynamic styling instead of toggling CSS class names.
mode_rationale: Single convention for dynamic styling approach

rule: Use semantic color tokens from the theme palette (e.g., colorPalette.textPrimary, colorPalette.bg) instead of raw color values or hardcoded hex codes.
Import colors from the canonical colors.ts file, not from alternative color files, to ensure theme switching works correctly.
Components should reference the dynamic color palette rather than implementing hardcoded dark/light theme branches.
mode_rationale: Simple rule to reference dynamic palette

rule: Use Ant Design's data-driven items array pattern instead of deprecated JSX children for Menu and similar components.
mode_rationale: Single convention to use items array over JSX children

rule: Leverage the base component library's built-in props and APIs (e.g., icon prop on Ant Design SubMenu) rather than re-implementing equivalent functionality.
mode_rationale: Simple guideline to use built-in props instead of reimplementing

rule: Provide sensible default values for component props (e.g., orientation defaults to horizontal) while allowing callers to override via props.
mode_rationale: Single convention about providing defaults with override capability

rule: Component prop types should extend or pick from the base library (Ant Design) types rather than defining loose, unconstrained prop interfaces.
Style customizations should go through the Ant Design Theme Provider rather than manually overriding component styles in styled-components or CSS.
mode_rationale: Simple rule to use Theme Provider for customizations

rule: Use the themeHelper utility for accessing theme values in styled-components instead of hardcoding values or accessing the theme object directly.
Always define theme keys for both light and dark modes explicitly, even when the values are identical, to support future design divergence.
mode_rationale: Simple rule to always define both light and dark keys

rule: Always reference colors from the theme palette rather than hardcoding hex values or using colors that may not exist in the current theme.
mode_rationale: Single convention to reference theme palette colors

rule: Import UI components from Domino's design system wrappers, not directly from the antd package.
mode_rationale: Simple import path rule for UI components

rule: Do not use useMemo or React.memo unless wrapping an expensive computation; unnecessary memoization adds complexity without performance benefit.
Use spacing and dimension tokens from the theme instead of raw CSS units (rem, px) so that layout values stay consistent with the design system.
mode_rationale: Simple rule to use spacing tokens from theme

rule: Prefer async/await with try/catch over .then/.catch promise chains for consistency across the codebase.
mode_rationale: Single coding style preference for async patterns

rule: Shared route definitions should live in a centralized package location (e.g., packages/ui/src/core/routes) rather than within individual app modules.
mode_rationale: Single convention about where routes should live

rule: Use proper TypeScript generics on utility methods like reduce() to eliminate the need for type assertions/casting downstream.
Mock data should use realistic example values that reflect the actual shape and format of real data (e.g., realistic IDs) to make tests and development more representative.
mode_rationale: Simple guideline for test data quality

rule: Use themeHelper utilities for font-size, margin, and other styling values instead of hardcoding them directly in styled components.
Remove commented-out code before merging; do not leave dead code in the codebase.
Replace switch statements or if-else chains that map values to strings with a dictionary/map object for cleaner, more maintainable code.
mode_rationale: Single refactoring preference for value mappings

rule: Avoid inline styles in React components; use styled components or CSS classes instead.
Favor template literal string interpolation over string concatenation for readability and consistency.
Use union types of string literals with the UnionToMap utility instead of TypeScript enums.
mode_rationale: Single TypeScript convention preferring union types

rule: Do not commit secrets, tokens, or credentials to version control; use a secrets management solution instead.
Import from canonical module paths rather than deprecated aliases to avoid breakage when aliases are removed.
mode_rationale: Single convention about import paths

rule: Maintain consistent casing conventions for identifiers across a codebase (e.g., if existing code uses PascalCase like CustomSVG, new related identifiers should follow the same convention).
mode_rationale: Simple naming consistency rule

rule: Components that consume global store via hooks like useStore should be memoized to prevent unnecessary re-renders.
mode_rationale: Single performance convention for store consumers

rule: Use a dictionary or mapping for aria-label values so the same icon always gets the same label, ensuring consistency for QA automation selectors.
mode_rationale: Simple accessibility/testing convention

rule: API parameter documentation should enumerate the allowed or expected values, not just describe the parameter abstractly.
mode_rationale: Single documentation convention for API params

rule: When adding fields to API response DTOs, verify that all downstream clients are tolerant of extra fields before merging.
mode_rationale: Requires checking downstream clients, a multi-step verification workflow

rule: Never import from a package's own dist/build output folder; use relative paths within the same package.
mode_rationale: Simple import path rule within packages

rule: Use color values from the design system palette (e.g., colors.palette.danger) instead of hardcoded hex values in styled components.
When multiple async requests are independent of each other, use Promise.all to execute them in parallel instead of sequentially.

Use the same domain terminology at all layers of the application (ubiquitous language from DDD); don't invent new names for concepts that already have established terms in documentation and user-facing surfaces.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention about domain terminology

rule: Configuration settings should be grouped with their related domain module, not added to a monolithic Settings class.
Use the structured logging framework (logger) instead of raw println statements, even for temporary debugging output.
When a file format or technology is no longer supported (e.g., MDX in Storybook), remove the files rather than refactoring them into the deprecated format.
Don't repeat parent context in nested configuration key names; if a setting is already scoped under 'workflows.storage', don't prefix its children with 'flows' or 'storage' again.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention against redundant prefixes

rule: Avoid inline styles in React components; use styled-components, CSS modules, or Ant Design's component props for styling.
Before creating custom UI elements, check if an existing component in the shared library (e.g., ActionDropdown.Button) already provides the needed functionality.
mode_rationale: Single guideline to check for existing components first

rule: Do not render a separate modal instance for every table row; use a single shared modal at the table level and pass the selected row's data to it.
When a function name collides with or closely resembles an existing function in the codebase, use a more specific name that conveys scope, version, or return type to avoid confusion.
mode_rationale: Single naming clarity convention

rule: When accessing properties on array elements or objects that may be undefined, use optional chaining (?.) with nullish coalescing (?? '') instead of assuming the value exists.
mode_rationale: Single defensive coding convention

rule: Use Array.reduce() or other functional methods instead of declaring mutable variables with let and imperatively building up values.
mode_rationale: Single coding style preference for functional patterns

rule: Do not import from your own package's published name (e.g., @domino/ui) within that same package; use relative imports instead.
mode_rationale: Single import convention within monorepo packages

rule: Do not copy data from a custom hook (e.g., useRemote) into local state via useEffect; use the hook's returned data directly to avoid unnecessary re-renders and state synchronization bugs.
mode_rationale: Single convention about not copying hook data into local state

rule: When a function argument is unused, remove it from the function signature rather than adding an eslint-disable comment to suppress the warning.
mode_rationale: Simple rule: remove unused args rather than suppressing lint

rule: When a component receives data from a single object, pass the object as one prop rather than spreading its fields, to keep the component's prop interface simple and explicit.
mode_rationale: Single convention about prop passing style

rule: Component props should be typed so that downstream consumers do not need to cast values; if a prop is expected to have a specific type, define it in the component's prop interface rather than casting at the call site.
mode_rationale: Simple typing convention to avoid call-site casts

rule: Place TypeScript type and interface definitions in a dedicated types file (e.g., types.ts), not in a utils file which should contain runtime logic.
mode_rationale: File organization convention for type definitions

rule: Avoid adding eslint-disable comments when the code can be written in a way that satisfies the lint rule naturally; fix the code rather than silencing the linter.
mode_rationale: Simple rule: fix code instead of silencing linter

rule: If code must be left commented out, add a TODO comment explaining why it is retained and under what conditions it should be restored or removed.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation convention for commented-out code

rule: Never mutate backend-fetched data when applying client-side filters; store the original data as an immutable source of truth and compute filtered views from it using separate filter state.
mode_rationale: Single architectural principle about data immutability

rule: When adding try/catch blocks to prevent crashes, also provide user-visible feedback (e.g., a toast or error message) so the user knows something went wrong rather than silently swallowing the error.
mode_rationale: Simple rule: show user feedback instead of silent catch

rule: Always use strict equality (===) instead of loose equality (==) to avoid type coercion bugs, unless there is an explicit reason for loose comparison.
mode_rationale: Single coding convention: === over ==

rule: If a component is no longer used and there are no planned additions, delete it rather than leaving dead code in the codebase.
mode_rationale: Simple housekeeping rule about dead code

rule: Use the nullish coalescing operator (??) instead of logical OR (||) for default values when only null/undefined should trigger the fallback.
Pure API call functions should throw errors and let the caller handle error messaging; API layers should have no concern about user-facing error messages.
mode_rationale: Single separation-of-concerns rule for API layers

rule: Components that can be used globally should be moved to shared packages (e.g., packages/ui) with proper Storybook documentation rather than kept in feature-specific modules.
mode_rationale: Single architectural guideline about component placement

rule: When the same placeholder or sentinel value appears in multiple places, extract it into a named constant (e.g., EMPTY_PLACEHOLDER) and reuse it across related utility functions.
Always use themeHelper or theme token values for colors and fonts in styled components instead of hardcoded hex values or raw numbers.
Style definitions should reference design system theme values rather than hardcoded numbers, even when maintaining existing code.
mode_rationale: Single styling convention: theme values over hardcoded numbers

rule: Keep import statements sorted in a consistent order within each file.
mode_rationale: Simple import ordering convention

rule: Avoid using negative margins in CSS; prefer restructuring layout with proper spacing, padding, or flexbox/grid alignment instead.
mode_rationale: Single CSS convention about layout approach

rule: When using batched MongoDB queries with $in, document the chosen batch size, the reasoning behind it, and the implicit BSON document size limits that constrain it.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation convention for batch queries

rule: When an API schema field has implicit constraints (e.g., nesting depth limits), document those constraints explicitly in the schema description everywhere the assumption applies.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation rule for schema constraints

rule: API schema definitions should include concrete examples to clarify expected structure and values for consumers.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: add examples to API schemas

rule: Use `unknown` instead of `any` in TypeScript to preserve type safety and force explicit type narrowing before use.
Remove code that is no longer needed rather than leaving it in place; do not keep dead code on the assumption it might be useful later.
When a field accepts multiple value types (e.g., not just strings), add test cases covering non-string and non-default value types to ensure correct handling.
mode_rationale: Simple testing guideline about covering multiple types

rule: A class name must accurately reflect its actual behavior; if a class named 'Logger' also enforces authorization or throws exceptions, rename it to reflect its broader responsibility (e.g., 'Checker' or 'Enforcer').
mode_rationale: Single naming convention about accuracy

rule: Log messages should include contextual identifiers (e.g., controller name, operation name) and use trace-level logging before and after a block to aid debugging.
mode_rationale: Simple logging convention about including context

rule: When making API fields optional, write at least two tests: one that modifies all optional fields and one that modifies none, to verify correct behavior at both boundaries.
mode_rationale: Simple testing convention about boundary coverage

rule: Maintain consistent formatting conventions (e.g., punctuation, capitalization) across method descriptions and documentation comments within a codebase.
mode_rationale: Simple formatting convention for doc comments

rule: Include example values (e.g., example hostnames, URLs) in API documentation and configuration descriptions to help consumers understand expected formats.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation convention about examples

rule: Use theme helper functions for styling values like font sizes and colors instead of hardcoding raw pixel values in component files.
Do not use explicit `return` statements in Scala; rely on expression-based control flow as the language intends.
When a component file contains multiple distinct components or variants (e.g., controlled and uncontrolled versions), split them into separate files.
Define default callback functions and static helper functions outside the React component body to prevent unnecessary re-creation on each render.
mode_rationale: Single React performance convention

rule: Use `unknown` instead of `any` for TypeScript type parameters when the values are not actually used, to preserve type safety.
Extract repeated test setup logic (e.g., creating test fixtures like unauthorized principals) into reusable helper methods to reduce duplication in test files.
When caller already has the needed data (e.g., a Principal), pass it directly to downstream services rather than forcing a redundant database or API lookup.
mode_rationale: Single performance convention about avoiding redundant lookups

rule: Prefer `find -exec rm -rf {} +` over piping `find -print0 | xargs -0 rm -rf` for batch file deletion — it's simpler and faster, and always use `-mindepth 1` to protect the parent directory from deletion.
mode_rationale: Single shell command preference convention

rule: Order import statements so that absolute (package/library) imports appear before relative imports.
Reuse existing route/path generator functions from shared utility modules instead of hardcoding URL paths inline; export them if not already exported.
mode_rationale: Simple convention about reusing shared utilities

rule: When constructing URLs from variables, validate that all interpolated values are defined before building the URL to avoid generating broken paths like `/u/undefined/undefined/browse-git`.
mode_rationale: Simple validation rule for URL construction

rule: Name methods to precisely reflect their behavior — a method that updates a resource should not be named `create`, and a method that creates or updates should be named `createOrUpdate`.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention about method accuracy

rule: When soft-deleting entities that have associated secrets or credentials, explicitly decide whether those credentials should be purged or retained, since users may expect their stored credentials are fully removed when they delete a resource.
When suppressing compiler or linter warnings, target the specific warning being suppressed rather than using broad suppression annotations.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: narrow suppression over broad

rule: Extract interactions with external services (e.g., Vault, secret stores) into separate classes rather than mixing them into the main service implementation, for better organization and testability.
Mock data used in tests or storybook should be typed using the types generated from the API schema, not left untyped or manually typed.
mode_rationale: Simple typing convention for mock data

rule: Remove all debug identifiers, test strings, and temporary values (e.g., hardcoded test IDs) from code before submitting a PR.

When a value can be computed from existing state or props, calculate it directly (or use `useMemo`) instead of synchronizing it via `useEffect` + `useState`, which adds unnecessary complexity and re-renders.

When a form renderer uses a switch/case or conditional to render different input types, extract each form control type into its own component to keep the renderer manageable as new input types are added.
Never reference color variables directly in component styles; always consume colors through the theme helper to ensure consistency and support theming.
mode_rationale: Single styling convention: theme tokens only

rule: When a manual conversion pattern is repeated (e.g., enum to constants), create a utility function to automate it rather than doing it one by one.
Use common code comments or markers to flag configuration lines that must be updated when cutting a release branch, so they are easy to find and not missed.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use code comments to flag release-branch config lines

rule: Name parameters explicitly to convey their meaning — e.g., use `pageNumber` instead of a vague abbreviation like `page` when the context could be ambiguous.
mode_rationale: Naming convention: prefer explicit parameter names over abbreviations

rule: When an operation requires a record to exist and its absence would be a logic error, use a get-or-create pattern instead of a plain get that can fail.
mode_rationale: Single pattern choice: use get-or-create instead of plain get for required records

rule: When adding a lifecycle state transition method like `pause`, always provide its complement (e.g., `resume`) rather than overloading an existing method like `start`.
mode_rationale: API design convention: always provide complementary lifecycle methods

rule: When an operation can fail for multiple similar reasons, the error message should specify which state caused the failure rather than being vague.
mode_rationale: Single guideline: make error messages specific about which state caused the failure

rule: If a feature (like a lease holder check) was designed for a use case that doesn't exist in the current PR, remove it to reduce complexity.
mode_rationale: Single convention: remove unused features to reduce complexity

rule: Do not enable background services (watchers, schedulers, periodic jobs) in all pod types when they are only needed in a specific one (e.g., dispatcher) — this avoids unnecessary k8s API load, heap/CPU waste, and future side-effect multiplication bugs.
mode_rationale: Single guideline: only enable background services in the pod types that need them

rule: When watching Kubernetes resources, use field selectors to limit the watch to only the specific resources you need, reducing memory footprint and API load.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use field selectors to limit k8s watches

rule: When state can be accessed by multiple threads, add explicit synchronization — protect both reads and writes using a dedicated lock object or volatile + synchronized pattern.
mode_rationale: Single rule: add explicit synchronization for shared mutable state

rule: When protecting shared mutable state with a lock, synchronize on both read and write paths — a write lock alone does not prevent stale reads from other threads.
mode_rationale: Single rule: synchronize both read and write paths for shared state

rule: When creating a resource that must be cross-referenced in another system, create the primary record first to obtain its ID before creating the secondary record that references it.
mode_rationale: Single convention: create primary record first to obtain ID before creating referencing record

rule: When constructing or logging domain objects, place the entity ID as the first parameter (or immediately after the creator) so the most important identifier is immediately visible.
mode_rationale: Naming/ordering convention: put entity ID first in parameter lists

rule: Prefix class names with the specific domain or subsystem they belong to (e.g., IdpUserReconciler instead of UserReconciler) to disambiguate purpose and avoid collisions.
mode_rationale: Naming convention: prefix class names with domain or subsystem

rule: When parsing strings into structured types (e.g., UUIDs, URIs, enums), validate the format at the point of parsing and handle failures explicitly rather than letting them propagate as unexpected runtime errors.
mode_rationale: Single rule: validate format at the point of parsing

rule: Delete code that is not actively used instead of leaving it in the codebase undocumented; unused code adds maintenance burden and confusion without providing value.
mode_rationale: Single convention: delete unused code instead of keeping it around

rule: When introducing polling or periodic checks against external services, expose the interval as a configurable parameter (e.g., via Central Config) rather than hardcoding it, enabling tuning without redeployment.
mode_rationale: Single guideline: expose polling intervals as configurable parameters

rule: When adding periodic or synchronous calls to external services, consider whether requests can queue up and block other operations; apply timeouts to bound the impact.
mode_rationale: Single rule: apply timeouts to prevent request queuing on external calls

rule: When exposing a new endpoint or service method, explicitly verify that appropriate authentication and authorization checks are in place, even when adapting code from existing patterns.
mode_rationale: Single rule: verify auth checks are in place on new endpoints

rule: When suppressing deprecation or compiler warnings in build files, document which specific deprecations are being silenced and why, so reviewers and future maintainers understand the trade-off.
Do not merge pull requests that point package dependencies at custom or feature branches; all dependencies must reference stable, published versions before landing.
mode_rationale: Single gate rule: dependencies must reference stable versions before merging

rule: Use configurable white-label product names instead of hardcoding the product name in UI components.
Before adding a dependency, verify it is not deprecated and follow the recommended migration path if it is.
mode_rationale: Single rule: verify a dependency is not deprecated before adding it

rule: Before adding a dependency, verify it supports the project's framework version (e.g., React 18) and do not add packages that lack support.
mode_rationale: Single rule: verify framework version compatibility before adding a dependency

rule: In Vite apps, use import.meta.env.MODE to check the application mode instead of process.env.NODE_ENV, and compare with strict equality.
Use dynamic import() instead of require() for conditional module loading in ES module codebases.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use dynamic import() instead of require() in ESM codebases

rule: When adding new ESLint rules, start them as warnings rather than errors to avoid forcing developers to use --no-verify or add excessive exceptions.
mode_rationale: Single convention: start new ESLint rules as warnings, not errors

rule: Component prop types must accurately reflect which props are actually used; either Pick the forwarded props or use the rest operator to pass through unhandled ones.
Use styled-components instead of inline styles for component styling.
Extract sub-components (e.g., tab contents) into their own files early, even if they are small, to prevent a single file from growing unwieldy.
mode_rationale: Single convention: extract sub-components into their own files early

rule: Do not declare the same utility, constant, or type in multiple locations; consolidate into a single shared definition.
Define styled-components in a dedicated styled.ts file rather than co-locating them in the component file.
Use NavTabs for full-page tab navigation and Segmented controls only for toggling between views within a section.
mode_rationale: Single convention: NavTabs for page navigation, Segmented for in-section toggles

rule: Use Ant Design's Flex component for layout instead of creating custom divs with flexbox styles.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use Ant Design Flex component instead of custom flexbox divs

rule: When a feature flag controls visibility of a component in one view, ensure it is consistently applied in all other views that render the same component.
mode_rationale: Single rule: apply feature flags consistently across all views rendering the same component

rule: Use optional chaining (?.) combined with nullish coalescing (??) to safely access nested properties and provide fallback values instead of verbose null checks.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use ?. and ?? for null safety instead of verbose checks

rule: TypeScript utility and helper files should follow camelCase naming conventions (e.g., getTheme.ts, not get-theme.ts) to match the project's frontend naming standards.
Color tokens should be used according to their semantic meaning — do not repurpose a token like 'color-link' to style non-link elements; use the semantically correct token (e.g., 'primary') instead.
When a React component contains self-contained logic (e.g., side effects, state management), extract it into a separate custom hook for better separation of concerns and reusability.
mode_rationale: Single convention: extract self-contained logic into custom hooks

rule: Remove commented-out code before merging — version control preserves history, so dead code should not live in the active codebase.
Do not include files that are not imported or referenced anywhere in the codebase; remove dead files to keep the project clean.
mode_rationale: Single rule: remove files not imported or referenced anywhere

rule: Remove outdated version numbers from file and component names when they no longer reflect the current product version, to avoid confusion.
mode_rationale: Naming convention: remove outdated version numbers from file names

rule: Instead of using 'any', leverage TypeScript utility types like ReturnType<typeof fn> to derive proper types and maintain type safety.
mode_rationale: Single rule: use TypeScript utility types instead of any

rule: Package.json dependencies must not reference custom or feature branches when merging to develop/main — always point to published versions or the main branch.
API specification fields should include example values to clarify expected formats and usage, especially for non-obvious types like stringified values or comma-delimited arrays.
mode_rationale: Single convention: include example values in API spec fields

rule: API schemas should not define the same concept (e.g., 'type') in multiple places; consolidate to a single source of truth to avoid ambiguity.
mode_rationale: Single rule: consolidate duplicate type definitions to a single source of truth

rule: Do not wrap JSX in React.Fragment (or <Fragment>) when a shorthand fragment (<>) or no wrapper at all suffices.
mode_rationale: Single convention: avoid unnecessary Fragment wrappers in JSX

rule: Default values and initial state objects should be extracted to named constants or a shared constants file rather than inlined in components.
mode_rationale: Single convention: extract default values to named constants

rule: When a result is unexpectedly absent or unknown (e.g., a missing exit code), default to failure rather than assuming success to avoid silently passing broken states.
Use modern React Router hooks instead of deprecated higher-order components like withRouter.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use React Router hooks instead of deprecated HOCs like withRouter

rule: When creating similar page variants, explicitly evaluate whether duplicated components or a single component with conditionals is the better trade-off before proceeding.
mode_rationale: Requires explicit evaluation/decision process before proceeding with implementation

rule: Functions with multiple parameters should accept a single options object instead of positional arguments to avoid ordering errors and improve readability.
mode_rationale: Simple API design convention for function signatures

rule: All form fields should have explicit name attributes to ensure proper form state management, validation, and accessibility.
Use a single Ant Design form instance to manage related form state and validation rather than creating multiple form instances or tracking values in component state.
mode_rationale: Simple convention preferring single form instance over multiple

rule: Before creating a new custom hook, check whether an existing hook in the codebase already handles the same concern (e.g., URL-synced tab state).
mode_rationale: Simple check-before-creating convention

rule: Before defining new enums or type constants, check if an equivalent type already exists in the codebase and reuse it.
API endpoint summaries and descriptions should accurately describe what the endpoint does, not use vague or misleading language.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation accuracy convention

rule: Boolean-returning methods should use names that clearly convey both the condition being checked and its specificity (e.g., `areMultipleStoragesRegistered` instead of ambiguous alternatives).
mode_rationale: Naming convention for boolean methods

rule: When you only need to know the count of records, use a COUNT query at the persistence layer instead of fetching full objects into memory.
mode_rationale: Simple performance convention for count-only queries

rule: Do not leave commented-out code in the codebase; remove it before merging since version control preserves history.
Always consume colors from the theme (or palette) rather than importing them directly from a color constants file, so that theming and rebranding propagate automatically.
Use a <button> element (not an <a> or link) when the element triggers an action rather than navigating to a URL; if the link-styled button pattern recurs, create a dedicated button variant.
mode_rationale: Simple HTML semantics convention

rule: When a component is a specialization of another, place it in its own file rather than co-locating it inside the base component's file.
mode_rationale: Simple file organization convention

rule: Do not leave commented-out code in the codebase; delete it and rely on version control to recover it if needed.
When a JSX conditional involves multiple chained expressions, extract them into a descriptively named boolean variable defined before the return statement to improve readability.
mode_rationale: Simple readability convention for JSX conditionals

rule: When a function's behavior is extended (e.g., adding a filter parameter), update its doc comment to reflect the new functionality.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation maintenance convention

rule: Use consistent casing and spacing in API route descriptions and doc comments — match the variable name's word boundaries (e.g., 'data plane' not 'dataplane') and capitalize acronyms like 'ID' consistently.
mode_rationale: Naming and casing consistency convention

rule: When adding a new filtering or query parameter to an existing endpoint, add integration or unit tests that verify the filtering behavior.
When a component is used across multiple sections or modules, move it to a shared package (e.g., a common UI library) rather than importing across section boundaries.
mode_rationale: Simple code organization convention for shared components

rule: When a parent already manages a form field's state, use the `value` prop instead of `defaultValue` to make the component fully controlled and avoid needing to sync state manually.
mode_rationale: Simple React component pattern convention

rule: Components consumed by multiple modules should be placed in packages/ui so that no single module owns a shared dependency.
Extract repeated expressions into utility functions and avoid nested ternary expressions for readability.
mode_rationale: Simple readability convention against nested ternaries

rule: Group related data-fetching and transformation logic in one place rather than scattering it across the function, to improve readability.
Do not wrap lightweight operations in useMemo or useCallback; inline them in JSX and use a simple variable if the expression is reused.
When adding log-and-rethrow error handling, investigate whether a higher-level caller is swallowing exceptions and fix the root cause rather than only improving logging.
Replace unused variables with underscore (_) to signal intent and avoid confusion about whether the value is needed.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for unused variables

rule: Avoid nesting CSS class selectors inside styled components; apply styles directly to the styled component definition instead.
Use styled components instead of inline styles for consistency and to keep styling concerns in one place.
Extract non-trivial filter or transformation functions into separate utility modules for reuse and testability.
When theme values are available through the theme provider or feature flags, use them directly instead of reading from localStorage.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to prefer theme provider over localStorage

rule: When declaring @font-face rules, set font-weight to match the actual weight of the font file so the browser selects the correct face.
mode_rationale: Simple CSS convention for font-face declarations

rule: When a magic value (e.g., version string) is used across multiple files, extract it into a single shared constant rather than duplicating literals.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to extract shared constants

rule: Name functions to match how their return value is used at call sites; if callers assign the result to `currentVersion`, the function should be named `getCurrentThemeVersion`, not something ambiguous.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for function names

rule: When creating theme or layout components scoped to a specific release version, include the version identifier in the component name (e.g., `AppPanelThemeRelease5`) for clarity.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for versioned components

rule: All touched React files should include an explicit `import React from 'react'` for consistency across the codebase.
mode_rationale: Simple import convention for React

rule: Extract complex inline JSX expressions or conditional rendering blocks into named variables (e.g., `mainContent`) to improve readability.
mode_rationale: Simple readability convention for complex JSX

rule: Avoid creating multiple similar DTOs (e.g., StorageDto, StorageInfoDto, StorageViewDto) when a single DTO with an additional field would be simpler and clearer.
mode_rationale: Simple architecture convention against DTO sprawl

rule: When exposing data that may vary by user permissions, explicitly verify whether unauthenticated or non-user callers should have access, and flag any security concerns in review.
mode_rationale: Simple security review convention for access control

rule: Name map/dictionary variables to indicate what the key represents (e.g., `dataPlaneHwTierMap` when the key is a data plane ID) so readers understand the lookup semantics at a glance.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for map/dictionary variables

rule: When a collection is filtered by the requesting user's permissions, encode that in the variable or field name (e.g., `accessibleHardwareTiers` instead of just `hardwareTiers`).
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention encoding permission context

rule: Order imports so that absolute (library/package) imports appear before relative imports for consistent readability.
Consume colors from the theme provider rather than hardcoding hex values, especially once the design system's color palette is available.
Move non-trivial inline calculations in components to a utility function to keep component code focused on rendering.
mode_rationale: Simple code organization convention for inline calculations

rule: Avoid using `let` with subsequent value overwriting; instead, extract the logic into a function that returns the final value to promote immutability and clarity.
mode_rationale: Simple immutability convention against let reassignment

rule: When consuming a stream of events that each triggers a database write, consider batching writes (e.g., update after N events or within a time window) to reduce write churn.
mode_rationale: Simple performance convention for write batching

rule: Name database collections, queues, and other shared resources with specific prefixes that identify the subsystem (e.g., 'mongoevents_' instead of generic 'events_') to avoid ambiguity when multiple subsystems use similar concepts.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for shared resources
