rule: Add comments explaining why retry or reconnection loops exist, including the failure scenarios they handle (e.g., cursor closed due to node failover), so future developers don't accidentally remove them.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation convention for resilience logic

rule: Use theme helper functions and design tokens for spacing, colors, and typography instead of hardcoded pixel values or raw color references, even when the exact value isn't available in the token set.
mode_rationale: Simple styling convention to use design tokens

rule: React components that render JSX should live in their own component files, not inside utility modules — even if they started small.
Route paths like '/admin' should not be hardcoded in component files; use constants or a route configuration to keep navigation paths centralized and maintainable.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to centralize route path constants

rule: All values used inside a useMemo (or useCallback) computation must appear in its dependency array; do not force re-renders by adding unrelated dependencies that the memo body does not consume.
mode_rationale: Single rule about dependency array correctness in useMemo/useCallback

rule: React hooks must only be called from React function components or custom hooks — never from plain utility functions, as this violates the Rules of Hooks and can cause crashes when hook call order changes at runtime.

If a file exports a React component, name it as a component (PascalCase, no 'Render' prefix or 'Util' suffix); file names should reflect what the module actually is, not how it was originally conceived.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for file names matching exports

rule: When a theme function already receives colors or tokens as parameters, use those parameters instead of importing and referencing the color constants directly, to ensure theme overrides and dark mode work correctly.
mode_rationale: Single rule about using theme function params over direct imports

rule: CSS for React components should be co-located with the component (CSS modules, styled-components, or CSS-in-JS), not placed in global stylesheets, unless it genuinely applies globally.
mode_rationale: Convention about CSS co-location with components

rule: Call React hooks at the top level of a component and assign results to variables before using them in arrays, objects, or other data structures — never invoke hooks inline within data structure definitions.
mode_rationale: Single rule about hook call placement

rule: When an error occurs in the UI, don't just display an error message — design a recovery path such as redirecting to an error page or offering a retry action, and coordinate with UX on the appropriate user experience.
mode_rationale: Principle about error UX design; guidance not a multi-step procedure

rule: Business logic such as validation and URI construction belongs in the domain layer, not the infrastructure layer.
mode_rationale: Architectural layering convention

rule: Remove println and other debug print statements before merging; use proper logging instead.
Choose the exception type that most accurately describes the underlying error condition rather than using a generic or loosely related exception.
mode_rationale: Convention about choosing the right exception type

rule: Before implementing ad-hoc solutions for cross-cutting concerns like obfuscation or redaction, check for and reuse existing platform utilities and patterns.
mode_rationale: Guideline to check for existing utilities before writing new ones

rule: Do not explicitly assign a property to its default value; it adds noise without changing behavior.
mode_rationale: Code cleanliness rule about avoiding noise from default assignments

rule: Place entity classes in packages that reflect their source or ownership — entities retrieved from an external API belong in the upstream package, not alongside internal domain entities.
mode_rationale: Package organization convention based on entity source

rule: Use named parameters consistently within a file — either always include them or always omit them for the same function calls.
When computing derived values from optional fields, handle the case where the field is not set to avoid inconsistent or misleading results.
mode_rationale: Defensive coding convention for optional fields

rule: Before creating a new class, check if an identical or equivalent class already exists in another module and reuse or consolidate it to avoid duplication.
mode_rationale: DRY principle applied to cross-module class deduplication

rule: Consider caching responses from service calls that return data that changes infrequently, even if call volume is currently low, to protect against future scaling.
mode_rationale: Performance guideline about caching stable data

rule: Keep CSS class selectors flat rather than nesting them; nested classes increase specificity and make styles harder to override and maintain.
When working on migration tasks, verify that files already migrated are not accidentally reverted to their pre-migration state.
mode_rationale: Vigilance rule during migration work, not a step-by-step procedure

rule: When a component supports props like color and fontSize directly, pass values through props rather than wrapping the component in a Styled component.
mode_rationale: Convention preferring props over styled wrappers

rule: Order import statements with absolute (library/package) imports first, followed by relative imports.
Express sizes in px rather than em unless there is a specific reason for relative sizing; the codebase convention is px.
mode_rationale: Codebase convention for sizing units

rule: Use the project's spacing utilities (e.g., getMargins, themeHelper) for margin and padding values instead of hardcoding inline styles.
mode_rationale: Convention to use spacing utilities instead of hardcoded values

rule: Name styled components and wrappers to accurately describe what they render; a component named 'Span' should not render an icon.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for styled components

rule: Reference semantic theme tokens (e.g., palette.borderLight) instead of hardcoding hex values or raw CSS color strings.
mode_rationale: Convention to use theme tokens over hardcoded colors

rule: When assigning colors to components, verify that the color matches the intended semantic meaning in the current theme (e.g., primary color is blue, not another color).
mode_rationale: Convention to verify semantic color correctness

rule: Do not cast a value to a type it has already been cast to; check if an earlier cast already covers the needed type assertion.
Instead of passing null for required parameters, use a proper placeholder or sentinel value (e.g., an 'uncorrelated' context) to maintain type safety and avoid null pointer issues.
mode_rationale: Type safety convention for required parameters

rule: Only audit events that represent user-initiated actions; system-driven status changes (e.g., job status transitions not triggered by a user) should be excluded from audit trails.
mode_rationale: Scoping rule for what qualifies as an auditable event

rule: Avoid making additional database queries solely for auditing purposes; capture audit data from context already available at the call site rather than fetching it after the fact.
mode_rationale: Performance guideline to use existing context for audit data

rule: Parameter names should be aligned with their corresponding type names for clarity and consistency.
mode_rationale: Naming convention aligning parameters with their types

rule: When adding new dependency injection bindings, follow the same pattern used by existing modules (e.g., @provides with named annotations) rather than introducing a different abstraction for the same purpose.
mode_rationale: Convention to follow existing DI patterns

rule: Use direct imports (e.g., import package.ClassName) instead of qualified references through intermediate objects when the direct import is available.
mode_rationale: Import style convention preferring direct imports

rule: When moving a class to a different module, move its corresponding test spec to the same module to keep tests co-located with the code they exercise.
mode_rationale: Convention to keep tests co-located with their code

rule: When implementing rollback or cleanup stacks, write tests that verify execution order and behavior when individual rollback callbacks throw errors.
mode_rationale: Testing guideline about what to cover, not a step-by-step procedure

rule: Use the appropriate log level for the situation: expected recovery operations (like rollback after an exception) should log at info, not warn; actual failures should log at error level.
mode_rationale: Logging convention for choosing correct log levels

rule: Each class should use its own logger instance rather than accepting another class's logger as a parameter; log correlation should be done via shared context (e.g., DominoContext), not by sharing logger instances.
mode_rationale: Logger ownership convention

rule: Critical operational failures (such as rollback failures) should trigger admin-facing alerts via supportAlerter, not just log messages, because logs alone are likely to go unnoticed.
mode_rationale: Alerting convention for critical failures

rule: API endpoints that advertise feature support should check that all prerequisites (e.g., PAT configuration) are actually met before reporting the feature as available.
mode_rationale: API correctness convention about prerequisite checking

rule: In React components, derive computed values with useMemo instead of storing them in state via setState; use early-return if-statements for clearer conditional logic.
mode_rationale: React convention preferring useMemo over derived setState

rule: Wrap event handler functions in React components with useCallback to prevent unnecessary re-renders of child components.
mode_rationale: React performance convention for event handlers

rule: When multiple objects or parameters share the same shape, define a TypeScript interface to avoid repetition and improve type safety.
mode_rationale: DRY convention using TypeScript interfaces

rule: Event handler functions defined inside React components should be wrapped with useCallback to prevent unnecessary re-renders of child components.
Modal components should extend the base modal's prop interface (e.g., open, onClose) rather than redeclaring those props, reducing duplication and ensuring consistency.
mode_rationale: DRY convention for modal component prop interfaces

rule: Use simple parameter names (e.g., datasetIds) and only add qualifiers like 'toInclude' or 'toExclude' when disambiguation is actually needed.
mode_rationale: Naming convention to keep parameter names simple

rule: When a method's return value is not immediately obvious from the signature, add a comment above it explaining what is being returned.
Generic, reusable UI components (e.g., cards, modals) should be placed in the shared packages/ui library, not in app-specific module directories.
mode_rationale: Code organization convention for shared UI components

rule: Do not add extra wrapper div elements in React components unless they serve a structural or styling purpose; unnecessary nesting hurts readability and can affect performance.
User-facing strings should not hardcode the product name (e.g., 'Domino'); use generic phrasing like 'A user' instead of 'A Domino user' to support whitelabel deployments.
mode_rationale: Localization/whitelabel convention for user-facing strings

rule: In TypeScript, prefer 'interface' over 'type' for defining component prop shapes and object structures, as interfaces are more extensible and produce clearer error messages.
mode_rationale: TypeScript convention preferring interface over type for objects

rule: When the same logic appears in multiple React components, extract it into a shared custom hook to eliminate duplication and centralize maintenance.
mode_rationale: Single convention: extract shared logic into custom hooks

rule: Before modifying interactive UI behavior (e.g., hover states, disabled styling), confirm the intended behavior with the UX team to avoid unintended regressions.
mode_rationale: Single guideline: confirm UI behavior changes with UX team

rule: When storing a value that does not need to trigger a re-render, use useRef instead of useState to avoid unnecessary component updates.
When using spacing utility functions like getMargin, verify the multiplier logic (e.g., getMargin(16) may compute 16 * BASE, not 16px) to avoid unintended values.
mode_rationale: Single caution: verify multiplier logic in spacing utilities

rule: Use the same CSS unit system (px or rem) consistently throughout the application; do not mix units unless there is an established convention for when each is appropriate.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: don't mix CSS unit systems

rule: When computing layout values based on viewport or container size, use a ResizeObserver to recalculate on resize rather than only computing once on mount.
mode_rationale: Single rule: use ResizeObserver for dynamic layout calculations

rule: New DynamicField field types must check whether the field is editable and only render interactive controls when editable; return null or a read-only view otherwise.
mode_rationale: Single constraint: DynamicField types must gate on editability

rule: Components added to reusable systems like DynamicField should be generic and simple; if a component is highly complex or domain-specific, extract it into its own module rather than embedding it in the reusable system.
mode_rationale: Simple guideline: keep reusable system components generic

rule: Always use the IconResolver abstraction for rendering icons rather than importing icon components directly, to ensure consistent icon handling across the application.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use IconResolver abstraction for icons

rule: Remove commented-out code before merging; if the code is needed later it can be retrieved from version control.
mode_rationale: Simple hygiene rule: remove commented-out code

rule: When adding conditional behavior based on an enum or provider type, ensure all variants are handled including unknown or unrecognized values.
mode_rationale: Single rule: handle all enum variants including unknown

rule: When a service makes repeated lookups for the same data, apply a caching pattern to avoid redundant calls and improve performance.
mode_rationale: Single guideline: cache repeated lookups

rule: Non-function constants (such as table column definitions or configuration arrays) should be named using UPPER_SNAKE_CASE to distinguish them from functions and variables.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for non-function constants

rule: Do not use explicit `return` statements in Scala; instead, restructure the control flow (e.g., invert the if condition) to rely on expression-based returns.

Ensure that type annotations match the actual data representation; avoid using narrower types (e.g., Int) when the source data uses a wider type (e.g., BigDecimal) to prevent silent data loss from implicit conversions.
mode_rationale: Single rule: match type widths to prevent data loss

rule: When modeling JSON Schema enum fields, support JsValue (or equivalent generic JSON type) rather than only String, since enum values may be numbers or other non-string types.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use JsValue for enum fields

rule: Move hardcoded constant values into companion objects, and reference existing shared constants from other modules rather than redefining them locally.
mode_rationale: Single rule: centralize constants in companion objects

rule: When checking for duplicate or existing versions of a resource, compare the actual schemas or content for equality rather than relying solely on version identifiers.
mode_rationale: Single guideline: compare content not just version IDs

rule: Define pure utility functions (like sorting or data transformation) outside the component body and have them fully encapsulate their logic, including any copying or spreading of data.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: define pure functions outside components

rule: Avoid rendering empty or unnecessary DOM elements (e.g., empty spans) when the underlying data is null or empty; conditionally render only when there is content to display.
mode_rationale: Single rule: conditionally render only when content exists

rule: Move reusable helper and utility functions out of component files and into dedicated utility files to improve discoverability and reuse.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: move helpers to utility files

rule: Replace hardcoded numeric literals with named constants that convey the value's purpose and meaning.
Use the project's design system spacing functions (e.g., getMargin, getSpacing) instead of hardcoded CSS spacing values to maintain consistency.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use design system spacing functions

rule: In Scala, use `val` instead of `var` when the reference itself is never reassigned, even if the underlying collection is mutated.
mode_rationale: Simple Scala idiom: prefer val over var

rule: When mapping between two enums, use a validated bidirectional mapping builder (e.g., StrictEnumBiMapBuilder) with explicit overrides rather than manual match/map expressions, so that missing mappings are caught at class-load time.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use validated bidirectional enum mappers

rule: Wrap API calls in error handling (try/catch or .catch()) to prevent unhandled exceptions from propagating and crashing the UI.
mode_rationale: Single rule: wrap API calls in error handling

rule: Use the nullish coalescing operator (??) or optional chaining (?.) to simplify conditional expressions that handle null or undefined values.
mode_rationale: Simple style preference: use ?? and ?.

rule: Access colors through themeHelper or theme tokens instead of importing color variables directly.
mode_rationale: Single convention: access colors via themeHelper

rule: Use theme spacing utilities like getMargin and getPadding instead of hardcoded margin/padding values in styled-components.
Use Ant Design Form, Form.List, and shouldUpdate instead of building custom form state management with nested conditionals.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use AntD Form over custom state management

rule: Use AntD Form.List for dynamic array fields instead of implementing custom add/remove logic.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use Form.List for dynamic arrays

rule: Use native JavaScript methods (Array.find, Array.filter, etc.) instead of utility library equivalents like Ramda when the native API provides the same functionality.
Do not create a named type or interface for values that are only used inline with hardcoded literals — pass the object directly.
mode_rationale: Single rule: don't name types used only inline

rule: Do not check the same condition twice in contiguous lines — consolidate the logic into a single conditional block.
mode_rationale: Simple rule: consolidate duplicate conditionals

rule: Use word-break: break-word instead of break-all to avoid cutting words in the middle when wrapping text.
mode_rationale: Single CSS convention: break-word over break-all

rule: Use the AntD Form onFinish/submit handler and Form change events instead of manually collecting and submitting form values.
Pass boolean props using JSX shorthand (e.g., <Collapse accordion />) instead of explicitly setting the value to true.
When a function has four or more parameters, convert to an options object to avoid relying on positional argument order.
Sort React component props alphabetically for consistent readability and easier diffing.
Do not leave commented-out code in PRs; use TODOs with ticket references instead if future work is planned.
Remove redundant null/undefined checks when the subsequent condition already handles the falsy case.
mode_rationale: Single rule: remove redundant null checks

rule: Prefer explicit if-statement null checks over TypeScript non-null assertion operators (!) to make safety guarantees visible in the code.
mode_rationale: Single convention: if-checks over ! assertions

rule: Wrap expensive computations in lambdas or lazy values so they are only evaluated when actually needed.
mode_rationale: Single rule: wrap expensive computations in lazy/lambda

rule: When introducing new policies or behaviors, use no-op or passthrough implementations for unrelated components to avoid unintended side effects.
mode_rationale: Single guideline: use no-op implementations to isolate changes

rule: Ensure all resources (RevWalk, TreeWalk, streams) are properly disposed even within Using blocks, since close() and dispose() may have different semantics.
mode_rationale: Single rule: ensure resources are properly disposed

rule: Add doc comments explaining the rationale behind magic numbers and non-obvious default values.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: document rationale for magic numbers

rule: Use existing well-tested libraries for common operations (e.g., unit conversions) rather than writing custom utility code that must be maintained and tested.
mode_rationale: Single guideline: use existing libraries over custom utilities

rule: When pattern matching on enums or change types, explicitly consider all variants including edge cases like deletes, and add tests to verify behavior for each.
Place private methods at the bottom of the class so the public API and core logic are visible first.
mode_rationale: Simple ordering convention: private methods last

rule: Before implementing new logic, check for existing utility methods that already provide the same functionality to avoid code duplication.
Move validation checks out of API service classes into dedicated validator services (e.g., ModelDeploymentValidator) to separate concerns.
mode_rationale: Single architectural convention about where validation logic belongs

rule: Methods that don't depend on instance state should be static or moved to the companion object.
Regex patterns and other compiled constants should be defined as static fields on companion objects so they are only compiled once, not on every method invocation.
mode_rationale: Single performance convention for compiled constants

rule: Avoid accessing tuple elements via positional indices (_1, _2); use case classes or named fields instead for readability.
mode_rationale: Simple readability convention preferring named fields over positional access

rule: When the same logic appears in multiple places within a class, extract it into a dedicated helper function to reduce duplication.
mode_rationale: Standard DRY principle expressed as a single guideline

rule: Each custom React hook should live in its own file rather than being co-located in a shared hooks utility file.
Ensure functions passed as props or used in dependency arrays are memoized (useCallback/useMemo) to prevent unnecessary re-renders.
mode_rationale: Single performance convention about memoizing props

rule: Replace switch/if-else chains that map status values to icons or components with a dictionary/object lookup for cleaner, reusable code.
mode_rationale: Simple code style preference for lookups over conditionals

rule: Controllers should delegate to domain services for business logic like lookups and data assembly rather than implementing it directly.
mode_rationale: Single architectural boundary rule

rule: Service methods should return domain-specific objects (case classes) rather than raw tuples or maps so callers know what each field represents.
When the same UI component appears in multiple places, extract it into a shared general-purpose component rather than duplicating it.
mode_rationale: Standard DRY principle for UI components

rule: When adding documentation to one function in a group of related functions, add docstrings to all of them for consistency.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation consistency rule

rule: Memoize sorting and other computationally expensive operations in React components using useMemo to avoid re-computing on every render.
mode_rationale: Single performance convention for useMemo usage

rule: Use theme helper tokens (e.g., themeHelper('palette.textPrimary')) instead of hardcoded color values to ensure consistency with the design system.
Before building a new UI element, check if an equivalent shared component already exists in the codebase (e.g., TooltipRenderer, empty state components).
Maintain correct import ordering with absolute paths before relative paths, and ensure linting is properly configured to enforce import sorting.
Avoid explicit type casting when using autogenerated API clients; rely on the generated types unless the API definition is known to be incorrect.
Replace magic numbers with named constants (e.g., MAX_TEMPLATES_TO_SHOW) and reuse the constant wherever that value is needed.
Challenge every @ts-ignore directive; remove it if the underlying type issue can be fixed, or document why it is necessary and track removal in a tech debt task.
mode_rationale: Simple code quality rule about suppressed type errors

rule: Do not add more logic to already-large components; encapsulate new functionality within its own component or a dedicated hook to keep parent components manageable.
Use styled-components for styling React components instead of inline styles to maintain consistency with the codebase's styling approach.
Never define a React component inside another component; move it to its own declaration outside, or assign the JSX directly to a variable without wrapping in an arrow function.
Do not store derived values in useState with a useEffect to sync them; compute the value directly from its source during render.
mode_rationale: Single React pattern preference for derived state

rule: Never mutate arrays received as props or stored in state; clone them before filtering or modifying to avoid subtle React rendering bugs.
mode_rationale: Fundamental React immutability rule

rule: When a permission is not found or undefined, default to hiding the item rather than showing it; fail closed on unknown authorization state.
mode_rationale: Single security convention about authorization defaults

rule: When filtering child items from a parent menu or group, also hide the parent if all children are filtered out to avoid empty submenus.
mode_rationale: Simple UI behavior rule for filtered menus

rule: File names should accurately reflect the scope and specificity of their contents; a file containing admin-specific logic should be prefixed with Admin, not given a generic name.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for files

rule: Never merge a PR to develop or main while package.json dependencies point to a custom or feature branch; resolve all branch-based references first.
mode_rationale: Single merge-readiness rule

rule: Isolate dependency or API client changes into a separate PR and merge it promptly to minimize merge conflicts with concurrent work.
mode_rationale: Simple PR scoping convention

rule: Every API call in the UI must have user-visible error feedback; never silently swallow fetch failures.
mode_rationale: Single error handling convention for UI API calls

rule: When two utility functions have identical logic, combine them into a single shared function with a configuration flag rather than maintaining duplicates.
Permission-gated UI must account for the loading and error states of the permissions fetch; do not render all items as visible while permissions are still loading or if the request fails.
mode_rationale: Single security/UX convention about permission fetch states

rule: Use the project's existing spacing utility functions (e.g., getMargin) consistently instead of hardcoding spacing values, even when setting padding.
mode_rationale: Simple styling convention about spacing functions

rule: Do not explicitly set CSS properties to their default values (e.g., flex-direction: row) as it adds noise without changing behavior.
mode_rationale: Simple CSS hygiene rule

rule: Use existing custom hooks (e.g., useInterval) instead of manually wiring up setInterval with useEffect cleanup for common recurring patterns.
mode_rationale: Simple code reuse convention

rule: When migrating functionality to a new implementation, remove the old files if they are no longer referenced; verify by deploying after removal.
mode_rationale: Simple cleanup convention during migrations

rule: Move styled component definitions out of the main component file and into a separate file to keep component files focused on logic and markup.
Reference traits or interfaces rather than concrete service implementations to maintain loose coupling and testability.
Before building a new UI component, check if an equivalent component already exists in the codebase and consult UX if the existing one can be adapted.
mode_rationale: Simple check-before-building convention

rule: When importing modules within the same package, use relative import paths instead of absolute paths.
Add comments specifying the expected units (e.g., milliseconds, bytes, seconds) for numeric fields that represent time or measurements.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation convention for numeric fields

rule: Add try/catch blocks around async operations and display meaningful error feedback to the user when failures occur.
mode_rationale: Single error handling convention

rule: Access colors through the themeHelper utility rather than hardcoding hex values or referencing theme props directly, ensuring consistency with the theming system.
mode_rationale: Simple theming convention

rule: Use the theme context to resolve colors instead of importing from static color constants, so that colors respond correctly to theme changes.
mode_rationale: Simple theming convention about dynamic color resolution

rule: Declare fixed configuration values (timeouts, limits, thresholds) as constants rather than variables to communicate immutability.
mode_rationale: Simple immutability convention for config values

rule: When adding environment variables or metadata to Kubernetes workloads, also add corresponding Kubernetes labels so admins and tooling can filter and identify workloads.
mode_rationale: Single infrastructure convention about K8s metadata

rule: Do not assume optional or dynamically-provided values are always defined; add explicit checks or fallbacks for cases where the value may be absent.
mode_rationale: Simple defensive coding convention

rule: Do not wrap a field in Option when the unwrapped type already has a valid empty/default value (e.g., empty string for String); prefer the simpler type unless None carries distinct semantic meaning.
mode_rationale: Simple type design convention about Option usage

rule: When adding feature-specific behavior (logs, files, UI elements), gate it on the relevant configuration flag so it does not appear when the feature is disabled.
mode_rationale: Single feature flag convention

rule: When a user's permission or role state is unknown (e.g., request pending or failed), default to the most restrictive access level rather than granting elevated privileges.
mode_rationale: Single security convention about default access levels

rule: When calling a service that can fail, provide user-visible feedback (error message, toast, fallback UI) rather than silently swallowing the error.
mode_rationale: Single convention: show error feedback on service failures

rule: Replace magic HTTP status code numbers with named constants from the framework (e.g., play.api.http.Status.CONFLICT instead of 409) for readability and maintainability.
mode_rationale: Simple style rule: use named constants instead of magic numbers

rule: Name method parameters to describe their purpose or behavior (e.g., filterByUserAndOrgIds instead of userAndOrganizationIds) so callers understand how the argument is used without reading the implementation.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for method parameters

rule: Extract inline event handler functions into memoized callbacks using useCallback to avoid unnecessary re-renders of child components.
mode_rationale: Simple performance convention: memoize callbacks with useCallback

rule: Before creating custom styled components or layout utilities, check for existing shared components in the UI package (e.g., FlexLayout) and reuse them.

Do not hardcode shared values (URLs, support links, config strings) in feature components; place them in the appropriate shared utility module (e.g., supportUtil.ts) so they can be maintained in one place.
mode_rationale: Code organization rule: shared values go in utility modules

rule: Code should be understandable to other engineers without requiring the author to explain it; if a reviewer cannot follow the logic, refactor for clarity.
mode_rationale: General clarity convention

rule: Do not explicitly pass `true` to boolean JSX props; use the shorthand attribute name alone (e.g., `<Comp disabled />` instead of `<Comp disabled={true} />`).
mode_rationale: JSX style rule: use shorthand boolean props

rule: Hide pagination controls when there is insufficient data to paginate rather than displaying an empty or single-page paginator.
mode_rationale: UI convention: hide pagination when not needed

rule: When modifying a shared or base component, validate the change across all consumers to ensure no regressions, since it can affect many downstream components.
Never import from a package's own dist output within that same package; use relative imports to the source files instead.
Prefer built-in component props (e.g., Ant Design's `noStyle`) over custom CSS overrides when the framework already provides the desired behavior.
Do not hardcode URLs that vary across deployments; use configuration hooks like `helpLink` or its associated hook to resolve environment-specific URLs.
Use the simpler `get` method for required configuration values instead of manual error handling; it throws an exception with the key name if the value is missing.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: use get method for required config

rule: Remove commented-out code before merging; if it must stay, add a comment explaining why and link the tracking ticket.
Revert exploratory or debugging changes to shared config files (e.g., package.json) that did not resolve the issue before merging.
Files containing only styled-components should be named `styled-components.tsx` rather than generic names like `styles.tsx`.
Always wrap JSON.parse in a try-catch even when form validation exists, because optional or defaulted fields may still produce invalid JSON at runtime.
When a periodic task has multiple independent stages, catch NonFatal exceptions in each stage independently so that a failure in one does not prevent the others from executing.
mode_rationale: Error-handling convention: catch exceptions per stage independently

rule: When fixing a bug, add a regression test that reproduces the original bug scenario to prevent future regressions.
mode_rationale: Testing convention: add regression test with bug fixes

rule: Test names should use positive statements that match what the test actually asserts (e.g., 'these providers are supported') rather than negative exclusions (e.g., 'all except X are supported').
mode_rationale: Naming convention: use positive statements in test names

rule: Bug fixes in serialization or mapping logic should include a unit test verifying the correct behavior, even if it requires adjusting method visibility.

Do not modify shared utility methods (e.g., adding case-insensitive matching) to fix a single call site; instead, create a separate method with a comment explaining why the relaxed behavior is needed.
mode_rationale: Architecture convention: don't modify shared methods for single call sites

rule: Do not allow invalid string representations (like the string "None" from an Option's toString) to propagate through the system; validate and throw at the boundary instead.
mode_rationale: Validation convention: throw at boundary for invalid string representations

rule: Test data should match realistic API input values (e.g., use the actual casing the API sends) to catch case-sensitivity bugs.
mode_rationale: Testing convention: use realistic API values in test data

rule: Types and interfaces should be moved to a dedicated `types` file rather than defined inline in utility or component files.
mode_rationale: Code organization rule: move types to dedicated file

rule: Use idiomatic collection operations like drop(offset).take(limit) for pagination instead of manual slice arithmetic.
Method names should accurately reflect their behavior; if a method performs pagination, include that in the name (e.g., getPaginatedModelDeployments).
Extract repeated parsing and transformation logic into a single helper function to avoid duplication and reduce error-prone inline parsing.
Do not retrieve all records from the database on every request; apply server-side filtering or pagination to avoid fetching excessive data.
mode_rationale: Performance convention: always apply server-side filtering or pagination

rule: Keep import statements organized and properly ordered in source files.
Place modal instances at the table level rather than inside each table row; row actions should only trigger the modal, not own their own instance.
Add an explicit guard condition when logic only applies to a specific subtype, making the precondition obvious to readers.
mode_rationale: Readability convention: add explicit guards for subtype-specific logic

rule: Add DEBUG or TRACE level log statements before key decision points so the decision-making process is observable in logs.
New service classes with non-trivial logic should have a corresponding unit test Spec class.
mode_rationale: Testing convention: new service classes need unit tests

rule: Function names must accurately describe the scope of their behavior; if a function stops an entire workflow rather than a single task, the name and documentation must make that clear.
mode_rationale: Naming convention: function names must reflect actual scope of behavior

rule: Choose field names that are clearly distinct from existing fields on the same type; when a name is ambiguous, add a comment explaining its purpose.
mode_rationale: Naming convention: choose clearly distinct field names

rule: Do not pass arguments to a function that are not declared in its parameter list; relying on closure scope while passing a value positionally is misleading.
In JSX, omit the `={true}` for boolean props; use the shorthand attribute form instead.
mode_rationale: JSX style rule: use shorthand boolean props (duplicate of implicit-boolean-props)

rule: Use a component's built-in loading mechanism rather than wrapping it in an additional loading spinner, unless the loading state is for data outside the component's responsibility.
mode_rationale: Convention: use built-in loading mechanism instead of extra spinners

rule: When rendering lists in React, use a unique identifier (e.g., entity ID) as the key prop instead of the array index to ensure correct reconciliation and avoid subtle rendering bugs.
mode_rationale: React convention: use unique IDs as keys, not array indices

rule: When designing APIs that support multiple providers or backends, accept generalized parameter names from the user and map them internally to provider-specific fields, reducing user-facing complexity.
Instead of commenting out code to disable functionality, create a new variable or configuration that achieves the desired state cleanly, keeping the codebase free of dead commented-out blocks.
mode_rationale: Code hygiene rule: create clean alternative instead of commenting out

rule: When an RPC or service call already receives an entity ID, assemble the full request payload within the RPC context rather than passing redundant data from the caller.
mode_rationale: Architecture convention: assemble payload in RPC context, avoid redundant params

rule: When adjusting visual layout such as spacing, indentation, or dividers, prefer CSS rules on the appropriate container or group elements rather than restructuring component hierarchy or adding JavaScript logic.
mode_rationale: Convention: use CSS for layout/spacing instead of JS restructuring

rule: When modifying permission or role definitions, explicitly verify that each role (especially restricted roles like limitedAdmin) has the expected access level and cannot view or modify resources beyond its intended scope.
mode_rationale: Security convention: verify each role's access level when modifying permissions

rule: Review conditional branches and guard clauses to identify code that can never be reached due to earlier conditions, and remove any debugging or test code that was accidentally left in.
mode_rationale: Code hygiene rule: remove dead code and debugging artifacts

rule: When overriding a pattern match to customize handling for specific cases, delegate all other cases to the superclass or default implementation rather than duplicating or restricting the existing logic.
mode_rationale: Architecture convention: delegate non-custom cases to superclass

rule: When modifying authorization, configuration, or behavior for one entity type (e.g., templates, projects), verify whether the same changes need to be applied to related entity types (e.g., apps, models) to avoid inconsistent behavior.
mode_rationale: Consistency convention: apply changes across all related entity types

rule: In tests that do not depend on real-world values (e.g., real time, real network), use simulated or mocked dependencies instead of real ones to clearly signal that the test is not relying on those external factors.
mode_rationale: Testing convention: use mocked dependencies to signal test intent

rule: Prefer structured or typed data access over manual string parsing; string parsing is fragile and should be a last resort with a clear comment explaining why.
mode_rationale: Single convention preferring typed access over string parsing

rule: Explicitly consider and handle cases where expected data may be absent, and decide whether that is a valid state or an error.
mode_rationale: Simple guideline to handle absent data explicitly

rule: When using undocumented or hard-to-find API endpoints, add a comment explaining where the endpoint was discovered and link to the closest available documentation.
mode_rationale: Single rule to comment on undocumented endpoint sources

rule: Do not add dependencies that are no longer actively maintained; prefer actively maintained alternatives to reduce risk from unpatched bugs and vulnerabilities.
Access colors through the theme helper abstraction rather than importing directly from the colors map, ensuring consistent theming and easier future changes.
Use existing library functions (e.g., Ramda, Lodash) for common operations like unique/set instead of writing custom implementations.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to prefer existing library functions

rule: Do not extract single-use expressions into named functions; place the logic inline at the call site to reduce indirection.
mode_rationale: Single rule about not extracting one-off expressions

rule: When logic is reusable across components or modules, extract it into a shared utility function rather than keeping it inline in a single component.
Before creating a new utility, check for existing implementations and extend them rather than duplicating constants or logic.
mode_rationale: Single rule to check for existing utils before creating new ones

rule: Use reduce instead of forEach when building or accumulating a result, as it makes the data transformation intent explicit and avoids side-effect-driven mutation.
mode_rationale: Simple style preference for reduce over forEach

rule: Replace hard-coded numeric literals with named constants to improve readability and maintainability.
When domain terms are overloaded, introduce method names that make the specific use case explicit rather than relying on generic names with parameters.
mode_rationale: Simple naming guideline for overloaded domain terms

rule: Test names should unambiguously describe the scenario under test; when a reviewer has to ask 'is this X or all X?', the name is too vague.
mode_rationale: Single convention about descriptive test names

rule: Never hardcode font-family values in component styles; remove inline font-family declarations or reference the value from the theme.
mode_rationale: Simple rule to never hardcode font-family

rule: Avoid introducing custom color values in theme definitions or components; map to existing palette tokens (e.g., palette.danger) to keep the design system consistent.
mode_rationale: Single convention to use palette tokens over custom hex

rule: When replacing icons from one library (e.g., AntD) with another (e.g., FontAwesome), the aria-label must match the original to avoid breaking existing tests.
mode_rationale: Simple rule to maintain aria-labels during icon migration

rule: When migrating components or icons, verify that conditional rendering logic and visual states remain identical to the original implementation.
mode_rationale: Single guideline to verify rendering logic stays identical

rule: Theme object entries for color attributes should reference existing semantic palette tokens, not non-standard hex values, to maintain consistency across the application.
mode_rationale: Simple convention for theme color references

rule: Do not explicitly set style properties that already match the theme defaults; remove redundant declarations like fontSizes.medium or fontWeights.normal when they duplicate the default values.
mode_rationale: Single rule to not re-declare default theme values

rule: Before defining custom color values in theme files, check if the default palette already provides the needed token to avoid duplication and inconsistency.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to check defaults before defining custom colors

rule: When implementing paginated API responses, use the existing PaginatedResponse traits from the shared utilities rather than creating ad-hoc pagination wrappers.
mode_rationale: Single rule to use shared PaginatedResponse traits

rule: Scaladoc and code comments must accurately describe the class or method semantics; do not add documentation that attributes behavior or constraints that are actually imposed by callers rather than the type itself.
mode_rationale: Simple convention for accurate documentation

rule: Be cautious when adding feature-specific traits or changes to shared infrastructure files (e.g., telemetry events); ensure the additions are generic enough to be reused by other consumers of that module.
mode_rationale: Single guideline about keeping shared code generic

rule: When introducing a v2 implementation alongside a legacy version, create a separate test spec file for the new version rather than adding v2 tests to the existing legacy spec file.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to separate v2 tests from legacy specs

rule: Use the standard DominoLoggable trait or lazy val logger pattern instead of manually creating loggers, unless there is a specific cross-service requirement for a named logger.
Use concise entity type names (e.g., 'user' instead of 'userAccount') in APIs and domain models unless the longer name disambiguates between genuinely different concepts.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for concise entity types

rule: When migrating to new icon components, apply color via a StyledComponent wrapper rather than passing color as a direct prop, as the new icon components may not accept color props the same way.
mode_rationale: Single rule to apply icon color via styled wrapper

rule: Move complex serialization logic to the model's companion object or a dedicated serializer in the same namespace, and add serialization tests to verify the output format.
mode_rationale: Simple convention for serialization placement

rule: Remove print statements and debug logging added during development before merging; use proper logger calls at appropriate log levels instead.
mode_rationale: Single rule to remove debug prints before merging

rule: Review logging statements to ensure they use appropriate log levels; remove debug/trace logging or bump it down from info level before merging to production.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to verify appropriate log levels

rule: Add tests for all significant code paths and features, not just the primary one; if a module handles multiple cases (e.g., both 'target' and 'location'), each should have dedicated test coverage.
Centralize repeated encoding/transformation logic (such as double URL encoding) into a single shared utility function so it can be updated in one place when the underlying framework changes.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to centralize repeated encoding logic

rule: When adding encoding logic to shared/common components, verify that already-encoded values are not encoded again to prevent double-encoding bugs across the application.
mode_rationale: Single rule to prevent double-encoding bugs

rule: In property-based tests, use the most specific generator that matches the domain constraint (e.g., use relativeFilePathGen for values that must always be relative paths) rather than a more general generator.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to use specific generators in property tests

rule: When registering scheduled tasks or injection modules, verify which service instances they will run in and ensure they only execute where needed to avoid unnecessary resource consumption.
mode_rationale: Single guideline to verify task registration scope

rule: When adding new customer-facing features or modifying visibility/access logic, update the corresponding runbook documentation so operations teams can troubleshoot issues.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to update runbooks with new features

rule: Do not set both `data-test` and `testId` props on the same component; use only the one that your test framework requires to keep test selectors consistent and avoid confusion.
mode_rationale: Single rule to not duplicate test selectors

rule: Use the shared `getErrorMessage` utility from `@domino/ui/dist/utils/errorUtil` to extract error messages instead of writing custom error parsing logic.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to use getErrorMessage utility

rule: Absolute imports must appear before relative imports; enforce consistent import ordering even when the linter misses it.
Extract utility functions, constants, and sub-components into separate files; keep the main component file focused on its primary responsibility.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to keep component files focused

rule: Use styled-components instead of inline styles for consistent, maintainable styling.
Use the shared mock utility pattern (e.g., `createMockComponent`) for mocking components in tests so you can access props and call callbacks on the mock.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to use createMockComponent pattern

rule: Create reusable mock data generator functions in `frontend/packages/mocks/generators` instead of inlining test data, so mock data can be shared across test suites.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to use shared mock data generators

rule: Prefer `slice` over `splice` for array operations to avoid mutating the original array, even when working with a copy.
mode_rationale: Single rule to prefer slice over splice

rule: When intentionally omitting dependencies from a React useEffect/useCallback dependency array, leave a comment explaining why to prevent future confusion.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to comment omitted React deps

rule: Avoid creating separate React state for values that can be derived or computed from existing state to reduce synchronization bugs.
mode_rationale: Single rule to compute values from existing state

rule: Before creating custom styled elements, check if an existing shared component (e.g., Link components) already provides the needed functionality.
Import style modules using namespace imports (`import * as Styles from './styles'`) so styled components are clearly identifiable as components in JSX.
mode_rationale: Single import convention for style modules

rule: Use theme or palette colors instead of hardcoded color values, and avoid `!important` overrides in styled-components.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to use theme colors over hardcoded values

rule: When encountering an error, investigate and fix the root cause rather than disabling or bypassing the failing code path, especially when the workaround has unintended side effects on other features.
mode_rationale: Single guideline to fix root cause not symptoms

rule: When refactoring code that moves responsibility from one class to another, ensure equivalent unit tests are created for the new class covering the same behavior.
mode_rationale: Single guideline: ensure tests follow moved logic

rule: When implementing policies that enforce uniqueness or singleton constraints, account for race conditions where multiple concurrent requests could bypass the check, especially in distributed systems with multiple service instances.
mode_rationale: Single principle about accounting for race conditions in uniqueness checks

rule: Query methods that filter, scope, and sort results should have tests verifying each dimension: correct entity type, correct scope (e.g., project), correct status filtering, and correct sort order.
mode_rationale: Single guideline about what dimensions query tests should cover

rule: Do not wrap values in useMemo unless the computation is expensive or the reference identity matters for downstream effects; unnecessary memoization adds complexity without benefit.
Use theme helper constants (e.g., margins.tiny, margins.small) instead of hardcoded pixel values in styled components to maintain consistency with the design system.
mode_rationale: Simple style rule: use theme constants over hardcoded pixels

rule: When a value can be computed directly from existing state or props, calculate it inline rather than synchronizing it through a separate useState and useEffect pair.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: compute derived values inline instead of syncing via state+effect

rule: Documentation and help links should use centralized support utilities (e.g., HelpLink component or docs base path hook) rather than hardcoded URLs, so links update automatically when docs locations change.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: use centralized utilities for doc links

rule: When the same conditional logic is duplicated across multiple components in the same file or module, extract it into a shared utility function to avoid repetition and ensure consistency.
mode_rationale: Simple refactoring principle: DRY up duplicated conditionals

rule: When multiple styled components share the same styles (e.g., icon wrappers with identical styling), consolidate them into a single reusable styled component.
When a component is already a styled-component, set all its styles (including fontSize) in the styled definition rather than passing them as inline style props.
mode_rationale: Simple style rule: define all styles in styled definition, not inline

rule: If a function is fully replaced by another (e.g., update replaced by patch), delete the unused function rather than keeping both to avoid confusion about which to use.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: remove replaced functions

rule: Configuration flags that control independent behaviors should remain independent; disabling one feature flag should not silently disable an unrelated feature, even if the code paths are nearby.
mode_rationale: Single principle: keep independent feature flags independent

rule: When implementing a feature that operates on project files, verify it works across all supported project types (e.g., DFS and GBP) rather than only the default type.
mode_rationale: Single principle: verify features across all supported project types

rule: Name constants to clearly indicate their domain or purpose, not just their structural role.
Do not add DI framework dependencies (e.g., Guice) directly in library classes; initialize bindings in injection modules to prevent transitive dependency leakage into downstream services.
mode_rationale: Single architecture rule about DI framework isolation

rule: When logic is extracted into a new class, tests must follow the logic to the new class rather than relying on indirect coverage from the original caller.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: tests follow extracted logic

rule: New code paths and branches should have corresponding test cases, even if added in a follow-up PR.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: new branches need tests

rule: Domain models should only contain fields that are actively used; do not add fields speculatively for future use.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: no speculative fields in domain models

rule: Service implementations that make HTTP calls or depend on external infrastructure belong in the infrastructure layer, not in the domain or server layer.
mode_rationale: Single principle: HTTP/infra code belongs in infrastructure layer

rule: Entity type identifiers should be specific enough to distinguish between the entity and its parent or container (e.g., `centralConfigSetting` vs `centralConfig`).
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for entity type identifiers

rule: Reference defined constants or enum values (e.g., `EventTarget.Project`) instead of inline string literals for type identifiers.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: use enums/constants over string literals

rule: Field names like `Id` should contain actual identifiers, not display names; rename fields whose values don't match the semantic meaning of the name.
mode_rationale: Simple naming rule: field names must match their semantic content

rule: Telemetry and audit trail events should include all available identifying fields (e.g., both ID and name) to make events useful for debugging and auditing.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: include all available IDs in telemetry events

rule: In React/TypeScript files, leave a blank line between type/interface declarations and the component function definition for readability.
Order imports so that absolute (package) imports come before relative imports.
Use styled components instead of inline styles, reference colors from the theme rather than hardcoding hex values, and use named constants for magic numbers in calculations.
mode_rationale: Simple style convention: styled components with theme refs over inline styles

rule: When a common/shared component exists (e.g., a shared link component), prefer reusing it over creating a local duplicate.
Use router links instead of anchor tags when navigating to React pages to avoid full page reloads.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: router links over anchor tags for SPA navigation

rule: Use semantic color tokens (textPrimary, textSecondary) from the design system instead of hardcoded or arbitrary color values in styled components.
Use design system palette border colors for dividers and borders instead of hardcoded color values.
mode_rationale: Simple style rule: design system border tokens over hardcoded colors

rule: Use the getMargin spacing utility instead of deprecated paddings.* keys for calculating spacing values.
Use the themeHelper utility for accessing theme values in styled components instead of raw theme property access.
Use existing route helper functions to build navigation URLs instead of constructing URL strings manually.
Simplify conditional logic by using early returns instead of if/else chains when the if-branch sets a value and returns.
Before writing custom state management or effects, check if an existing hook (e.g., useOnChange, useRemoteData.refetch) already provides the needed behavior.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: check for existing hooks before writing custom ones

rule: Use the refetch method from data fetching hooks (e.g., useRemoteData) instead of copying remote data into local state to trigger re-renders after mutations.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: use refetch instead of copying remote data to local state

rule: Define event handler functions with useCallback to prevent unnecessary re-creation on every re-render.
Apply the same post-mutation side effects (e.g., page reload, refetch) consistently across all similar async operations in a component.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: apply same side effects across similar mutations

rule: If a method's return type includes values it can never actually produce (e.g., always returns true), refactor the return type to accurately reflect actual behavior (e.g., Unit).
mode_rationale: Simple convention: return types should reflect actual behavior

rule: Before modifying or removing props from shared UI components, verify that no other consumers depend on the changes, especially for long-lived branches.
When replacing legacy Ant Design icons with custom icons, preserve the same aria-label values to avoid breaking existing tests.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: keep aria-labels when swapping icon implementations

rule: Verify that new UI layouts render correctly at the minimum supported viewport dimensions (1366x768) before merging.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: verify layouts at 1366x768 minimum

rule: Leverage existing internal utility libraries (e.g., domino-pytk) for common operations like resource traversal instead of writing custom implementations.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: use internal utility libraries over custom implementations

rule: When operating on resources that can have multiple instances (e.g., jobs in a project), target by a unique identifier (e.g., execution ID) rather than a broad parent identifier (e.g., project name).
mode_rationale: Simple convention: use unique IDs over broad parent identifiers

rule: Do not leave commented-out code in the codebase; either remove it entirely or explain why it must remain.
When linking to internal packages, do not assume build output directories (e.g., /dist) will always exist; validate or document the dependency on prior build steps.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: don't assume build output directories exist

rule: When injecting helpers or scripts via modules at runtime, evaluate whether race conditions can occur due to load ordering or timing.
mode_rationale: Single principle: evaluate load-order race conditions for injected scripts

rule: Never import mock modules or test utilities in production source files; this causes build issues and increases bundle size. Duplicate the needed functionality as a utility instead.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: never import test utilities in production files

rule: Wrap callback functions passed as props or used in effects with React.useCallback() to prevent unnecessary re-creation on every render.
mode_rationale: Simple performance convention: useCallback for callback props

rule: Use TypeScript interface with extends to compose types rather than defining duplicate type aliases; if a type already exists in a shared package, reuse it.
mode_rationale: Simple TypeScript convention: compose types via extends, reuse shared types

rule: When a function already computes or retrieves a value that callers need, return it directly rather than forcing callers to re-derive it separately.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence convention about function return values

rule: Lambda handlers should be lightweight routers that validate and forward requests, pushing business logic and config processing to downstream services.
mode_rationale: Architectural guideline about handler responsibility

rule: In Scala, distinguish between empty collections (Seq.empty) and null — they are not the same and mock argument matchers must account for this difference.
trigger: When setting up mock return values for methods that return collections in Scala tests
rationale: Without this distinction, mocks returning null instead of Seq.empty cause NullPointerExceptions in production code that calls .map or .filter, while mocks returning Seq.empty instead of null miss null-safety bugs — both lead to false test passes.
mode_rationale: Single convention about null vs empty in Scala mocks
steps: Step 1: Check the actual method's return type and behavior — does it return null or an empty collection when no results exist?
Step 2: Configure mock to return exactly what the real implementation returns (Seq.empty, None, or null)
Step 3: If testing the 'no results' path, use Seq.empty — not null — unless the real code actually returns null
Step 4: Add a separate test case for null if the upstream API can return null, to verify null-safety

rule: When storing polymorphic domain entities, put commonly-used fields for filtering and sorting at the top level, and nest type-specific definitions in dedicated sub-fields.
mode_rationale: Data modeling convention for polymorphic entities

rule: Domain interfaces should use domain IDs (e.g., DominoId) and keep persistence-specific details (e.g., MongoDB ObjectId) confined to the persister implementation layer.
mode_rationale: Layer boundary convention about domain vs persistence types

rule: Use Storybook CSF3 format for stories instead of the deprecated MDX format.
mode_rationale: Simple format preference rule

rule: Import from lodash-es instead of lodash to enable proper tree-shaking and reduce bundle size.
mode_rationale: Single import convention for bundle optimization

rule: Function names should accurately describe all behaviors — a function named onApplyTag that also removes tags should be split into separate functions with single concerns.
Remove poorly-implemented HATEOAS uri fields rather than keeping them — if nobody currently needs the feature, delete it and implement it properly when needed.
mode_rationale: Design principle about removing dead code vs keeping stubs

rule: When a create operation involves copying multiple sub-resources, do not fail the entire operation if some items cannot be copied — instead track what succeeded and report failures with reasons.
mode_rationale: Error handling convention for batch operations

rule: Service layer interfaces should not return REST API DTOs — this couples all service consumers (including internal ones) to the API definition and forces changes in the API layer to propagate to unrelated consumers.
mode_rationale: Layer separation convention about service return types

rule: Dependency injection bindings should be placed in the module that owns the feature (e.g., TemplatesInjectionModule), not in a parent or sibling module (e.g., ProjectsInjectionModule).
mode_rationale: DI placement convention

rule: Add logging to new service operations and API endpoints so that behavior can be traced in production.
mode_rationale: Simple observability convention

rule: When a file contains functions for multiple subsystems, prefix function names with the subsystem name to make their scope clear from the call site.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for multi-concern files

rule: Remove all console.log statements and other debug logging artifacts before submitting a PR for review.
Review and remove TODO comments that are no longer valid when modifying or moving code.
Do not commit empty or unused files; delete them as part of the PR cleanup.
Place new tests in a dedicated test module rather than appending them to an existing unrelated test file; group related tests together by concern.
mode_rationale: Test organization convention

rule: Functions should return computed results rather than requiring callers to pass in empty containers to be filled.
mode_rationale: Function design convention about immutability

rule: When multiple tests share the same assertion logic, extract a common assertion function and have each test call it with its specific parameters.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: DRY up repeated assertion logic into shared helpers

rule: Use descriptive, domain-specific variable names (e.g., env_image instead of env_uri) that accurately reflect the value's meaning.
mode_rationale: Naming convention

rule: Do not use `object` as a type annotation in TypeScript; use a specific interface, type alias, or `Record<string, unknown>` instead.
mode_rationale: Simple TypeScript convention: use specific types instead of object

rule: Include all dependencies in React useEffect dependency arrays rather than using eslint-disable; refactor the effect or extract stable references to avoid infinite loops.
mode_rationale: React hooks convention

rule: Use proper TypeScript types for test props instead of @ts-ignore; convert JSX components to TSX or provide type declarations so tests are type-safe.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: fix type mismatches instead of using @ts-ignore

rule: Disentangle unrelated concerns (e.g., client generation, dependency updates, feature logic) into separate PRs so each change is easier to review and reason about in history.
mode_rationale: PR hygiene convention

rule: When a variable refers to one of several possible resources of the same kind (e.g., S3 buckets), include a qualifier in the name to clarify which resource it refers to.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for disambiguation

rule: Name variables to reflect their lifecycle state (e.g., `persistedDeployment` instead of a generic name) so readers immediately know whether the value is in-memory, persisted, or pending.
mode_rationale: Naming convention about state clarity

rule: Never catch all Throwables; use `scala.util.control.NonFatal` to avoid swallowing fatal JVM errors like OutOfMemoryError.
mode_rationale: Scala error handling convention

rule: When deleting a resource that has dependent external resources (e.g., Kubernetes objects), delete the database record only after the external cleanup succeeds so the record is preserved for retry if cleanup fails.
mode_rationale: Ordering convention for cleanup operations

rule: Do not call `.get` on Option values; use `.getOrElse`, `.fold`, pattern matching, or throw a descriptive exception to handle the None case explicitly.
mode_rationale: Scala Option handling convention

rule: Use curly braces for multi-expression blocks and follow the Databricks Scala style guide conventions for brace placement.
mode_rationale: Code style convention

rule: Add trace-level logging to all client/infrastructure methods, including the method name and all parameters.
mode_rationale: Logging convention for infrastructure methods

rule: Catch only NonFatal exceptions in Scala; never catch all exceptions with a bare catch block, as this can swallow fatal errors like OutOfMemoryError.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: catch NonFatal only in Scala (duplicate of narrow-exception-catching)

rule: Custom exceptions must accept and pass the underlying cause Throwable to the Exception constructor so stack traces and root causes are preserved in logs.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: always pass original exception as cause

rule: Name the exception parameter 'cause' (not 'ex') and pass it to the parent Exception constructor so the JVM exception chain is properly linked.
mode_rationale: Exception parameter naming convention

rule: Log messages should describe the operation context (e.g. 'error creating model deployment secret, rolling back'), not just echo exception.getMessage, since the full exception is already logged via the second parameter.
mode_rationale: Logging message content convention

rule: If a method performs create-or-update logic, its name should reflect both operations (e.g. 'createOrUpdateModelDeployment') rather than implying only one.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for multi-action methods

rule: Name variables after their domain purpose (e.g. 'mlflowS3ArtifactBucket') rather than the config key they're read from, so the code is self-documenting about intent.
mode_rationale: Naming convention about domain intent

rule: Use enumeratum's withNameInsensitiveOption for parsing enum values from external input instead of manually converting to uppercase, to handle mixed-case input safely.
mode_rationale: Enum parsing convention

rule: Use getOrElse with an explicit error thrown for required values rather than silently returning None or a default, so missing data fails fast with a clear message.
mode_rationale: Fail-fast convention for required values

rule: Service layers should use generic, provider-agnostic abstractions rather than hardcoding behavior for a specific compute provider; design for dynamic discovery and configuration.
mode_rationale: Abstraction convention for provider independence

rule: Use idiomatic Scala collection methods like isEmpty instead of manual length or size checks for cleaner, more readable code.
mode_rationale: Scala idiom convention

rule: Keep persistence logic (e.g., MongoDB saves) in the same architectural location for both create and update paths of the same entity to avoid confusion and inconsistency.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: keep persistence logic in a consistent layer

rule: Use separate try-catch blocks for independent operations that require different rollback logic, rather than grouping them into a single block.
mode_rationale: Error handling scoping convention

rule: Controllers should delegate to a single service method that encapsulates both persistence and downstream calls, rather than orchestrating persistence and business logic separately.
mode_rationale: Layer responsibility convention

rule: Ensure finally blocks do not execute unintended side effects on fatal errors; guard finally logic with appropriate flags that account for both fatal and non-fatal failure paths.
mode_rationale: Finally block safety convention

rule: Do not place success-only logic in finally blocks; code that should only run when no exception occurred belongs in the try block after the operation that may fail.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: put success-only logic in try, not finally

rule: Follow the Databricks Scala style guide for curly brace usage in conditionals and control structures.
mode_rationale: Duplicate of curly brace style convention

rule: Use idiomatic Scala Option methods like getOrElse instead of verbose if/else or match expressions when extracting a value or throwing on None.
When refactoring or rewriting code, verify that all existing tests are preserved or re-added; do not silently drop test coverage.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: verify all tests survive refactoring

rule: When a method returns Option/None, explicitly handle the absent case — decide whether to throw an exception, return a default, or propagate the Option.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence convention about handling absent values

rule: When create and update paths share a method but have mostly different logic, split them into separate methods for clarity and maintainability.
mode_rationale: Simple guideline to split methods when logic diverges

rule: Apply retry policies consistently across analogous operations (e.g., if update uses a retry policy, create should too) unless there is a deliberate reason for the difference.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence convention about applying retry policies uniformly

rule: When adding new components that make API calls, ensure a test suite exists and mock external calls to prevent unintended network requests in tests.
mode_rationale: Simple rule to ensure tests exist and mock external calls

rule: When moving or refactoring files, check whether the original file had unit tests and ensure equivalent coverage exists for the new location.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence reminder to check test coverage after refactoring

rule: Use polling on status instead of fixed sleep/wait calls in tests and automation, as sleeps are fragile and waste time.
mode_rationale: Simple convention preferring polling over fixed sleeps

rule: Use existing utility functions (e.g., getAppName, getTemplateType) instead of manually handling raw values or reimplementing logic that a utility already encapsulates.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: use existing utils instead of hardcoding or reimplementing

rule: When copying or forking an entity, ensure all attributes are included in the copy — review the source model field by field to avoid silently dropping properties.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence guideline about completeness when copying entities

rule: When adding a new dependency to a BUILD or build file, be prepared to explain why it is needed; unnecessary dependencies increase build times and coupling.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: justify why a new dependency is needed

rule: Fields that are optional in REST API DTOs for backward compatibility should be required in the domain model; keep the distinction clear and plan to version the API to tighten optional fields.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence rule about field optionality across layers

rule: Do not allow a code path that enables functionality which has not been tested by QE; if a feature is not supported in MVP, the code should not permit it.
mode_rationale: Simple gate rule: no code paths for untested functionality

rule: Ensure that exception types map to the correct HTTP status codes — user input errors (e.g., IllegalArgumentException) should return 400, not 500.
mode_rationale: Simple convention about mapping exceptions to correct HTTP status codes

rule: Services should return Optional values and let the caller decide how to handle absence, rather than throwing exceptions internally — the correct failure mode (IllegalArgument vs IllegalState) depends on the caller's context.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence convention about service return types

rule: Validation logic (e.g., billing tags, permissions) should be applied consistently across all code paths that create or modify the same entity, not duplicated in different places with divergent behavior.
mode_rationale: Simple rule to apply validation consistently across all code paths

rule: When adding counters or metrics for one variant of a feature, ensure equivalent metrics exist for all variants to maintain observability parity.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence guideline about observability parity

rule: Use existing accessor methods on domain objects (e.g., d.getTemplateType) instead of manually unwrapping optional fields, to reduce boilerplate and avoid null-handling bugs.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to use accessor methods instead of manual unwrapping

rule: When modifying shared utility code that is used across multiple sections, run the full relevant test suite to catch unintended regressions.
mode_rationale: Simple reminder to run full test suite when touching shared code

rule: Include scenario IDs or other unique identifiers in test resource names (e.g., workspace names) to avoid collisions and improve traceability.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for test resources

rule: In tests that validate environment-dependent values (e.g., memory, CPU), assert on relative properties (unit suffix, greater-than threshold) rather than exact values to avoid brittleness across different node types or platforms.
mode_rationale: Simple guideline about assertion style for environment-dependent values

rule: When removing a code step or block, also remove any associated comments that reference the deleted code to avoid stale documentation.
mode_rationale: Simple cleanup rule when removing code

rule: Domain entities should never reference API model classes; the API is one presentation of the domain and coupling the domain to it creates a dependency in the wrong direction.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence architectural constraint about dependency direction
