rule: When a file contains only one class or object, name the file after that class to make it easy to locate.
mode_rationale: Simple file naming convention

rule: Domain entities and validation logic should not depend on web framework constructs (e.g., Play Forms); if the framework changes or the domain is reused in a non-web context, the domain should remain unaffected.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence architectural boundary rule

rule: Domain entities should not include JSON or other serialization mappings because it couples the domain with a specific serialized format and constrains future evolution of the entity.
mode_rationale: Simple decoupling rule for domain entities

rule: When different subtypes of an entity have distinct fields, use polymorphism (subclasses) rather than making all fields optional on a single type, to enforce correctness at the type level.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence design preference for type modeling

rule: Validation helpers and required-field definitions should live in their own file, separate from the domain entity definitions, and be scoped (e.g., package-private) to the consuming code.
mode_rationale: Simple organizational rule about file separation

rule: Database record definitions should clearly show what is stored without requiring developers to trace through codecs and external DTOs; do not reuse API DTOs as persistence record fields since changes in the API layer should not affect the persistence layer.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence rule about persistence layer independence

rule: When a data type represents a specific variant, remove fields that never apply to that variant rather than leaving them as unused optional fields.
mode_rationale: Simple guideline to remove unused fields from variants

rule: Remove public interfaces from shared modules when a class should only be consumed through a higher-level service, preventing other services from bypassing the intended access layer.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence encapsulation rule

rule: Place REST API DTOs and remote/internal mapping DTOs in separate packages so reviewers can immediately identify what backward-compatibility guarantees apply to each.
mode_rationale: Simple packaging convention for DTO separation

rule: Do not include code (classes, files, endpoints) that has no current usages; add it in the PR where it is actually needed.
mode_rationale: Simple YAGNI principle about not adding unused code

rule: Domain services should depend on domain entities, not on API-layer DTOs; coupling services to API models causes unrelated API changes to ripple into business logic.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence architectural dependency rule

rule: When adding permission checks, follow the existing pattern used by similar methods in the codebase rather than inventing a new approach.
mode_rationale: Convention to follow existing auth patterns

rule: When fixing a bug in one code path, check whether the same problematic pattern exists in nearby or analogous code and fix those instances too.
mode_rationale: Simple habit rule to check for similar bugs nearby

rule: Prefer aria-label over data-testid for identifying interactive elements in tests; this simultaneously improves accessibility and simplifies test selectors.
mode_rationale: Simple selector preference for tests

rule: Write aria-label values as full, human-readable words (not abbreviations or IDs) because they are read aloud by screen readers for visually impaired users.
mode_rationale: Simple writing convention for aria-label values

rule: Use built-in default-value mechanisms (e.g., dict.get with a default) to eliminate unnecessary conditional branches.
mode_rationale: Simple code style preference for defaults over conditionals

rule: Use @dataclass with its auto-generated __init__ for simple data holders instead of writing custom wrapper classes that hide straightforward construction behind unnecessary abstraction.
mode_rationale: Simple convention preferring dataclasses for data holders

rule: Do not use module-level singletons or global-like variables unless there is a clear, justified need; prefer passing dependencies explicitly.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence architectural guideline against global state

rule: Use list comprehensions instead of imperative for-loops with append when transforming a collection into a new list in Python.
mode_rationale: Simple Python style preference

rule: When a function is almost always invoked alongside a logging call, move the logging inside the function to avoid repetitive boilerplate and missed log statements.
mode_rationale: Convention to encapsulate co-occurring side effects

rule: Before removing shared configuration like environment variables or build scripts, check for dependencies in other repositories that may consume them.
mode_rationale: Simple safety check before removing shared config

rule: When an environment variable already exists for a value, reference the env var instead of hardcoding the value in other scripts or config files.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to reference existing env vars instead of hardcoding

rule: Phrase BDD feature file scenarios as user stories (As a [role], I'd like to [action], So that I can [benefit]) to clarify intent and audience.
mode_rationale: Simple BDD writing convention for scenario descriptions

rule: Cross-domain mapping logic (e.g., template-to-project) should live in the originating service, not the consuming service, to avoid coupling unrelated services.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence rule about where cross-domain mapping logic belongs

rule: Name test fixtures to describe the data or resource they provide (e.g., `git_credentials`) rather than using generic or action-oriented names.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for test fixtures

rule: Move complex inline expressions into named utility functions or variables to improve readability and testability.
mode_rationale: Readability convention for inline complexity

rule: Replace magic numbers with named constants (e.g., `MAX_GOALS_SHOWN`) so the intent is clear and the value can be reused consistently.
mode_rationale: Simple convention replacing magic numbers with named constants

rule: Ensure the test's `it` description accurately reflects what is being asserted; if it claims to test a behavior change, the test must actually trigger and verify that change.
mode_rationale: Testing convention for description accuracy

rule: Use existing utility classes like AutoRecordMapperCodec for serialization instead of writing manual mapping code, unless there is a specific technical reason not to.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to use existing serialization utilities

rule: When processing a stream of events that each trigger a database or service lookup, introduce a low-complexity cache to avoid redundant lookups that scale linearly with event volume.
mode_rationale: Single guideline about caching redundant lookups in event streams

rule: Prefer API-based setup steps over UI-based steps in end-to-end tests to reduce flakiness and speed up test execution, unless the test specifically validates UI-only behavior.
mode_rationale: Single preference for API-based over UI-based test setup

rule: When a function or hook accepts several parameters, group them into a single options/params object to improve readability and reduce confusion at call sites.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to group parameters into an options object

rule: Move pure utility functions and mappings outside of React component bodies into separate utils files or module-level declarations to improve readability and testability.
mode_rationale: Single rule about where to place pure utility functions

rule: When defining a set of related string or numeric constants, use an enum (or a const object with an extracted type) rather than loose constants, and tie the type to the enum keys for type safety.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to prefer enums over loose constants

rule: Instead of using useEffect to react to prop changes (which can cause extra renders and missing-dependency warnings), use the 'previous value comparison during render' pattern recommended by the React docs.
mode_rationale: Single guideline to use previous-value comparison instead of useEffect

rule: When using rest/spread props that include accessibility attributes like aria-label, verify the attribute is interpreted correctly on each target element, especially when both a link and a button receive the same spread props.
mode_rationale: Single accessibility check when spreading props

rule: When a component depends on asynchronously loaded configuration or locale data, consider delaying child rendering until that data is available to prevent render inconsistencies.
mode_rationale: Single guideline about guarding renders on async config

rule: Domain/interface modules must not reference API/web module types (e.g., DTOs). Mapping between domain and API models should live in the appropriate layer, and build tools like Bazel should enforce this boundary.
mode_rationale: Single architectural rule about dependency direction between layers

rule: When the frontend needs a derived value (e.g., provider name from a URI), include it as an explicit field in the API response rather than requiring consumers to parse or compute it, especially when the mapping already exists on the backend.
mode_rationale: Single API design convention to expose derived values explicitly

rule: Use imperative functions (e.g., a callable `fetchData`) for data refreshes instead of chaining multiple useEffect hooks with boolean flags, which causes unnecessary re-renders.
mode_rationale: Single preference for imperative fetch over chained useEffects

rule: Before writing custom test assertions, check for existing shared test helpers (e.g., `@domino/test-utils`) that already cover common verification patterns like breadcrumbs, layout, and routing.
Do not use deprecated or legacy product terminology (e.g., 'Detwirl') in new code, variable names, route names, or comments — even when refactoring code that originally used those terms.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention to avoid legacy terms

rule: Use React Router route links instead of raw anchor tags or `window.location` for in-app navigation within the React SPA to preserve client-side routing and avoid full page reloads.
mode_rationale: Single rule to use React Router links instead of raw anchors

rule: Do not clone or spread an entire props object just to inject one additional value; pass the original props through and add the extra prop individually.
mode_rationale: Simple convention against cloning props to inject one value

rule: Use kebab-case for `data-testid` attributes to maintain consistency across the codebase.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention for test IDs

rule: Each React component should live in its own file; do not export multiple components with similar interfaces but different implementations from the same file. Avoid prefixing component names with vendor library names (e.g., 'Ant').
mode_rationale: Simple file organization and naming convention

rule: If a prop is optional and has no default value, do not assume it will exist at runtime — add a null/undefined guard before accessing it.
mode_rationale: Single defensive coding rule for optional props

rule: Set default values for optional parameters at the destructuring site or function signature rather than with conditional logic later in the function body.
mode_rationale: Simple convention about where to set default values

rule: Add explanatory comments or improved logging for non-obvious control flow, especially around retry logic, race conditions, or multi-step shutdown sequences where the 'why' is not self-evident from the code.
mode_rationale: Single guideline to document non-obvious logic

rule: When removing all usages of a module during a refactor, also delete the now-unused source file to prevent dead code from accumulating.
mode_rationale: Single cleanup convention during refactors

rule: When building a generalized endpoint, ensure it supports all intended backing types (e.g., DFS and Git), not just the immediately needed one.
mode_rationale: Single API design principle about supporting all intended types

rule: Never hardcode the product name in UI strings; always use the app name getter to support white-labelling.
mode_rationale: Single rule to never hardcode the product name

rule: Before creating a new constant (especially for timeouts/thresholds), check if an existing one can be reused or adjusted instead.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to check for existing constants first

rule: When a large match/case statement repeats the same function call structure in every branch, extract the varying parts into a tuple and call the function once after the match.
mode_rationale: Single refactoring guideline for match/case statements

rule: Do not add explicit imports for classes that are already covered by a wildcard import in the same file.
mode_rationale: Simple import hygiene rule

rule: Use domain-level ID abstractions (e.g., DominoId.newInstance) instead of database-specific types (e.g., ObjectId) outside of database layer code.
mode_rationale: Single convention to use domain-level ID abstractions

rule: Module-scoped pytest fixtures that could be shared across tests should be defined in a separate fixtures file, not inline in a specific test file.
mode_rationale: Single convention about where to define shared fixtures

rule: Use established test helper utilities (e.g., create_user() from user_helpers.py) instead of reimplementing test setup logic inline.
trigger: When writing inline object construction or setup code in a test method
rationale: Without checking for helpers first, developers duplicate setup logic that already exists, creating maintenance burden and risk of tests using stale construction patterns.
good_example:
// Found existing helper in TestFixtures
val user = TestFixtures.create_user(role = Viewer)
bad_example:
// Manually constructing what a helper already does
val user = User(
  id = UUID.randomUUID(),
  name = "viewer",
  email = "viewer@example.com",
  role = Viewer,
  orgId = testOrg.id
)
mode_rationale: Simple reminder to use established test helpers
steps: Step 1: Before writing any inline setup, search the test directory for helper/factory functions matching the object type
Step 2: Check test base classes and trait mixins for inherited helpers
Step 3: Use the discovered helper, passing only the fields relevant to your test scenario
Step 4: If no helper exists and you need the same setup in 2+ tests, extract one now

rule: Archive or delete test-created resources (projects, users, etc.) after tests complete to avoid polluting the test environment.
mode_rationale: Single convention to clean up after tests

rule: When a variable is an alias for another concept (e.g., selectedRevision aliasing activeRevision), add a comment explaining the relationship to avoid confusion.
mode_rationale: Single documentation rule for aliased variables

rule: Define static configuration objects like table column definitions as constants outside the component to avoid re-creating them on every re-render.
When calling date formatting utilities, verify they handle undefined or invalid inputs gracefully and do not render 'Invalid date' to the user.
mode_rationale: Single defensive coding rule for date formatting

rule: Use TypeScript enums generated from API/YAML specs instead of @ts-ignore or hardcoded string literals.
mode_rationale: Single convention to use generated enums instead of suppressions

rule: Use white-label-aware UI components (e.g., waitSpinner) instead of hardcoded or generic alternatives to ensure theming consistency.
mode_rationale: Single rule to use theming-aware components

rule: Remove all commented-out code and commented-out tests before merging; do not leave dead code in the codebase.
Do not use type casting to work around API type mismatches; fix the underlying type or remove the cast.
Move complex inline expressions into named functions for readability and reusability.
mode_rationale: Simple readability convention

rule: Use rem units instead of px for margin and padding values to support scalable, accessible layouts.
mode_rationale: Single CSS convention for spacing units

rule: Write BDD/Gherkin test steps in clear, natural language that reads as a straightforward English sentence.
mode_rationale: Single convention about test step readability

rule: Do not place internal service models in the API model package; keep API models and internal domain models in separate packages.
mode_rationale: Single architectural rule about model package separation

rule: Design service interfaces with distinct parameters for pagination, sorting, and filtering instead of bundling them into a single object.
mode_rationale: Single API design convention for parameter separation

rule: Return the most specific type directly from service methods instead of adding unnecessary conversion methods when the service already produces the correct type.
mode_rationale: Single guideline to return specific types directly

rule: Use the same query parameter name (e.g., `q` for search) across all APIs to maintain a consistent developer experience.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention for API query parameters

rule: Use `getOrElse` with an `IllegalStateException` containing a clear message instead of calling `.get` on Options, even in cases that should never fail, to make debugging easier.
mode_rationale: Single error handling convention for Options

rule: API filter parameter names should match the corresponding domain model attribute names so the mapping is easy to understand; fix naming mismatches early because they become harder to change later.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention for API-to-model alignment

rule: When a simpler API shape (e.g., single-value parameter) satisfies current requirements and expanding it later (e.g., to multi-value) is not a breaking change, ship the simpler version first.
mode_rationale: Single API design principle to ship the simplest version first

rule: Use the proper domain type (e.g., `DominoId`) explicitly rather than relying on implicit conversions from primitive types to ensure type safety and code clarity.
mode_rationale: Single type safety convention against implicit conversions

rule: Move reusable test fixture creators into shared test utility modules (e.g., `testslib`) so they can be used across multiple test suites.
mode_rationale: Single convention about test fixture placement

rule: The `totalCount` in a paginated API response must reflect the total number of results matching the filter in the database, not just the current page size.
mode_rationale: Single API correctness rule for paginated responses

rule: Name variables and binders to accurately reflect what they bind or represent; misleading names create confusion during code review and maintenance.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence naming convention

rule: When upstream data changes rarely (e.g., once every few months), avoid performing heavy IO on every endpoint call; use caching or a check-before-fetch strategy to reduce database load.
mode_rationale: Performance guideline about caching strategy

rule: When checking existence or fetching multiple records, use a single bulk query with $in or similar operators and do in-memory diffing, rather than issuing one query per item.
mode_rationale: Performance convention preferring bulk queries

rule: When synchronizing upstream data with a local database, consolidate queries into bulk operations rather than performing per-record IO; consider strategies like remove-all-and-reinsert for simplicity.
mode_rationale: Performance guideline to consolidate IO

rule: Function and field names should match their domain-level meaning (e.g., use 'hub' when filtering by hub, not 'author') to avoid confusion between API concepts and internal filters.
mode_rationale: Naming convention about domain-level terminology

rule: Use strict equality (=== instead of ==) to avoid implicit type coercion bugs, especially in template and frontend code.
mode_rationale: Simple coding convention for === over ==

rule: When commenting out code or disabling tests due to a known issue, include a reference to the tracking Jira ticket so future readers know why it was disabled and when it can be re-enabled.
mode_rationale: Documentation convention for disabled code

rule: Define named constants for magic values like page sizes, and reuse the same constant everywhere the value is referenced to ensure consistency and single-source-of-truth.
Choose clearly distinct names for types and interfaces; if a name looks like a typo of another type in the same file, rename it to reflect its actual purpose to avoid confusion.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for type distinctness

rule: Avoid duplicate imports from the same module and prefer named imports over wildcard (* as) imports in React files.
mode_rationale: Import style convention

rule: Always use strict equality operators (=== and !==) instead of loose equality (== and !=) for comparisons.
mode_rationale: Simple coding convention for strict equality

rule: Prefer the 'unknown' type over 'any' in TypeScript to maintain type safety, especially in test mocks and type assertions.
Assign boolean literals to descriptively named variables before passing them as function arguments to improve readability.
mode_rationale: Readability convention for boolean arguments

rule: Always provide a default value for shell/environment variable expansions using ${VAR:-default} to prevent unset variable errors.
mode_rationale: Shell scripting convention for defaults

rule: Ensure API errors are surfaced to the user or handled explicitly, not silently swallowed by the calling component.
mode_rationale: Error handling convention against silent swallowing

rule: Use clear, descriptive variable names that convey the purpose and content of the variable.
Align and format complex expressions or chained operations for readability, especially in functional pipelines.
mode_rationale: Formatting convention for readability

rule: Use default imports for React (`import React from 'react'`) instead of namespace imports.
mode_rationale: React import style convention

rule: Move reusable utility functions out of component files and into dedicated utils files.
Use the short fragment syntax `<>` instead of `React.Fragment` when no key prop is needed.
mode_rationale: React syntax preference

rule: When refactoring changes a function's behavior, rename the function to accurately reflect its new behavior and avoid confusion.
mode_rationale: Naming convention during refactoring

rule: Avoid hardcoding magic numbers (e.g., pixel widths) in child components; instead derive UI state from parent-controlled boolean or enum props to prevent fragile coupling.
mode_rationale: Component design convention against hardcoded values

rule: When an optional API field has a destructive implicit behavior (e.g., omitting it deletes existing data), document that behavior in the API contract and add a unit test covering it.
Remove unused variables, imports, and dead code rather than leaving them in the codebase.
Permission denial messages should specify what the user lacks permission to do and direct them to contact their administrator for resolution.
mode_rationale: Error message content convention

rule: When making a targeted fix (especially for cloud/hotfix), create a wrapper component that composes the shared component instead of modifying the shared component directly.
mode_rationale: Architecture convention for hotfix isolation

rule: Do not commit empty or placeholder test files; either write meaningful tests or omit the file until tests are ready.
mode_rationale: Testing convention against placeholder files

rule: Use trace-level logging for verbose operational messages so they are off by default but can be enabled when debugging; reserve info/debug for significant state changes.
trigger: When adding log statements inside loops, per-message handlers, or high-frequency code paths
rationale: Without trace-level logging, verbose per-item messages flood production logs at INFO or DEBUG, increasing log storage costs and making it harder to find meaningful entries during incidents.
mode_rationale: Logging level convention
steps: Step 1: Identify whether the log statement will fire per-item, per-message, or per-request in a high-throughput path
Step 2: Use TRACE level for per-item operational messages (e.g., 'processing message X')
Step 3: Use DEBUG level for aggregate summaries or decision-point logs (e.g., 'processed batch of 50 messages')
Step 4: Reserve INFO level for lifecycle events (startup, shutdown, configuration changes)

rule: Use a bounded retry policy with backoff instead of an infinite loop when waiting for an external resource or condition.
mode_rationale: Error handling convention for bounded retries

rule: Add exception handling around operations that interact with external systems or can fail at runtime; do not leave them unguarded.
mode_rationale: Error handling convention for external calls

rule: Place constants and static-like values in a companion object rather than inline in the class body.
mode_rationale: Scala code organization convention

rule: Name variables and types using the language of the current domain context, not terms inherited from upstream or adjacent services.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for domain context

rule: When adding a new consumer, parser, or non-trivial logic, include a unit test that demonstrates the expected input/output to make the behavior concrete for reviewers and future maintainers.
mode_rationale: Testing convention to include tests with new logic

rule: When defining status enums, always include an explicit error state and ensure error messages from downstream systems are passed through rather than silently dropped.
mode_rationale: Enum design convention for error states

rule: Do not use optional function props as the mechanism to show or hide UI elements; use an explicit boolean prop or a more verbose pattern that makes the conditional rendering obvious.
mode_rationale: React component API convention

rule: When multiple APIs refer to the same concept, use the same term everywhere to avoid confusion between equivalent names.
mode_rationale: Naming consistency convention

rule: For unpaginated endpoints, set the internal query limit high enough that results are never silently truncated in practice, preventing mysterious missing data in the UI.
mode_rationale: API design convention for query limits

rule: Extract hard-coded limits (e.g., max result size) into method parameters with sensible defaults so tests can set low values to verify limit enforcement without relying on large data sets.
mode_rationale: Testability convention for extracting parameters

rule: When a method's responsibility is to validate an entity, pass the entire entity to it rather than having the caller decide which fields will be checked — the validation method should own that decision.
mode_rationale: Architecture convention for validation method signatures

rule: Use variables or configuration references for environment-specific values (image tags, versions, URLs) in test files instead of hardcoding them, to prevent silent test failures when those values change.
mode_rationale: Testing convention against hardcoded env values

rule: Remove console.log and other console statements from production code before merging; do not use console output as a workaround for unused variables.
mode_rationale: Code cleanup convention

rule: Define table column configurations in a separate file near the table component that renders them, not in the parent view that passes them as props.
mode_rationale: Code organization convention for table configs

rule: When a store (e.g., useStore) is globally accessible, consume it directly in the component that needs it rather than threading it through router or parent props.
mode_rationale: State management convention for direct store access

rule: Import UI components from the Domino design system library rather than directly from Ant Design; document the reason if a direct Ant import is necessary due to missing design system support.
mode_rationale: Architecture convention for design system usage

rule: When a column renderer or inline render function grows complex, extract it into its own component rather than keeping it in a utility file.
mode_rationale: Code organization convention for render complexity

rule: Define a TypeScript union type for a fixed set of string keys so new values are caught at compile time rather than silently passing.
mode_rationale: TypeScript type safety convention

rule: Import from the built dist output of internal packages rather than reaching into source files directly.
mode_rationale: Import convention for internal packages

rule: When logging service operations, include the filter or query parameters in the log message so issues can be traced to specific inputs.
mode_rationale: Logging convention for traceability

rule: Use fake implementations rather than mocks for test doubles; fakes exercise more realistic behavior and are easier to maintain.
mode_rationale: Testing convention for test double strategy

rule: When logging an operation or decision point, include all relevant contextual values (e.g., flags, IDs, status) so logs are self-contained for debugging.
mode_rationale: Logging convention for self-contained debug info

rule: Use the correct semantic HTML element or component for its purpose — do not use a Link component as a button or vice versa.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence convention about using correct HTML elements

rule: Do not commit empty files to the repository; remove them or add their intended content before merging.
When leaving temporary workarounds or placeholders in test files, annotate them with a TODO label so they are trackable and not forgotten.
mode_rationale: Single convention about annotating workarounds with TODO

rule: Use framework-provided components (e.g., react-router's Redirect) instead of writing custom equivalents unless there is a clear, documented reason the built-in cannot work.
mode_rationale: Simple guideline to prefer built-in framework components

rule: When testing multiple expected failures, assert each one individually rather than relying on a loop that short-circuits on the first failure.
mode_rationale: Single testing convention about individual assertions

rule: In E2E tests, use the most specific selector available (e.g., `I wait to see the checkbox "Practitioner"`) rather than generic text matching, to make tests more resilient and intention-clear.
mode_rationale: Simple guideline about selector specificity in E2E tests

rule: Verify the actual UI element type (checkbox, button, text, etc.) before writing E2E test assertions against it to avoid false passes or misleading test logic.
mode_rationale: Single convention to verify element type before writing assertions

rule: Avoid using `any` in TypeScript; use proper types or generics to maintain type safety.
Do not mark test functions as async unless they actually contain awaited expressions; unnecessary async adds confusion about test intent.
mode_rationale: Single convention about async usage in tests

rule: Maintain consistent import ordering: absolute/external imports should come before relative imports, enforced by Prettier or ESLint.
Keep React state as close as possible to the component that uses it rather than lifting it to a parent container that doesn't need it.
mode_rationale: Single React state placement guideline

rule: When displaying code snippets in the UI, use a CodeEditor component (read-only) rather than plain text elements, to provide proper syntax highlighting and visual context.
mode_rationale: Simple UI component choice convention

rule: Do not mark test cases as async when they contain no await expressions.
mode_rationale: Duplicate of no-unnecessary-async-tests; same single convention

rule: When introducing a feature toggle to switch between old and new implementations, keep the old implementation unchanged so it remains a valid rollback target.
mode_rationale: Single guideline about keeping old code intact behind feature toggles

rule: Public API method names should not expose underlying implementation details like SDK version numbers; use generic names that describe the operation.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for public API methods

rule: When wrapping an input stream, provide a way to regenerate it (e.g., via a factory lambda) rather than holding a direct reference that can only be read once.
mode_rationale: Single design guideline about stream regeneration

rule: In Scala, prefer case classes with public vals over regular classes with private fields and explicit Java-style getter methods.
mode_rationale: Simple Scala idiom preference

rule: If fields in a Scala class require @unused annotations because they are private, convert to a case class to make them public getters and eliminate the suppression.
mode_rationale: Single Scala convention about case classes and annotations

rule: Keep related source files in the same package or directory even when module/build boundaries differ, to improve discoverability.
mode_rationale: Simple file organization guideline

rule: When renaming an attribute, update all usage sites directly instead of adding compatibility aliases that obscure the actual type and add unnecessary indirection.
mode_rationale: Single convention about direct renaming vs aliases

rule: Remove fields, methods, and wrapper operations that are not used in production; do not include code that has no current consumers.
mode_rationale: Simple code hygiene rule

rule: Do not add production wrapper methods solely to support test code; instead, have tests use the underlying client or SDK directly.
mode_rationale: Single convention about production code purity

rule: When a method duplicates the logic of an existing method with only minor argument wrapping, consolidate callers to use the canonical method instead of maintaining both.
mode_rationale: Simple guideline to consolidate duplicate methods

rule: Feature flags and configuration concerns should not be embedded in domain or data wrapper objects; keep them at the infrastructure or injection layer.
mode_rationale: Single separation-of-concerns convention

rule: Unpack settings objects and pass only the specific values a component needs, rather than passing the entire settings object, to minimize coupling and clarify dependencies.
mode_rationale: Simple guideline about minimizing coupling

rule: New configuration settings should be added to the appropriate domain-specific settings class (e.g., ComputeGridSettings) rather than the global Settings file, using optional fields with sensible defaults.
mode_rationale: Single convention about configuration placement

rule: When a value has more than two meaningful states (e.g., waiting, requested, completed), use an enum or sealed type rather than a boolean, and name it to capture the intent of each transition.
mode_rationale: Simple type design convention

rule: When modifying or replacing an API endpoint, keep the old endpoint functional for backward compatibility, especially when external consumers or agents may depend on it.
mode_rationale: Single backward compatibility guideline

rule: When testing state-dependent logic, include negative test cases that verify the system does NOT perform an action when it shouldn't, not just that it performs correctly when it should.
mode_rationale: Single testing convention about negative cases

rule: Mutable state accessed by concurrent callers must be properly synchronized using atomic references or other concurrency primitives; a plain mutable variable is not safe.
mode_rationale: Single concurrency safety rule

rule: Don't route simple operations like writing a file through heavyweight abstractions (state machines, actor messages) when a direct call from the request handler would suffice.
mode_rationale: Simple design guideline about appropriate abstraction

rule: Comments on configuration fields and methods must describe the complete behavior, not just part of it; if a timeout triggers both deduplication and forced termination, both must be documented.
mode_rationale: Single documentation convention about comment completeness

rule: When introducing configurable timeouts or limits that affect user-facing behavior (e.g., post-run script execution windows), ensure public documentation exists so users understand the constraints.
mode_rationale: Single documentation convention about user-facing constraints

rule: When adding new build tools or CLI utilities, update the relevant architecture documentation (wikis, ADRs) so the team understands how the new tool fits into the system.
mode_rationale: Single convention about keeping docs in sync

rule: Import aliases for internal packages (e.g., @domino/ui/dist) should resolve to compiled output, not source code, to avoid breaking React contexts and causing build inconsistencies.
mode_rationale: Single build configuration convention

rule: Use scoped package names (e.g., @domino/ui, @domino/components) for import aliases to make the nature and origin of the import explicit.
mode_rationale: Simple import naming convention

rule: When migrating to a new build tool (e.g., tsc to Vite), replace all relevant scripts (build, watch, type-check) with the new tool's equivalents rather than leaving legacy scripts alongside the new ones.
mode_rationale: Single convention about complete migration

rule: Fields whose values are derived from other data (e.g., a path computed from a container config) should not be stored in the database; compute them at read time to avoid staleness and maintenance burden.
mode_rationale: Single data storage convention

rule: When exposing configuration values through API endpoints, assess whether sensitive secrets could leak outside their intended security boundary.
mode_rationale: Single security guideline about secret leakage

rule: Use relative imports for files within the same frontend module rather than absolute imports to maintain module encapsulation.
mode_rationale: Simple import style convention

rule: Reuse existing shared mock data from centralized mock directories (e.g., api/__mocks__) instead of creating duplicate mocks within individual component directories.
mode_rationale: Single testing convention about mock reuse

rule: Do not mark fields or parameters as optional unless there is a concrete reason they may be absent; unnecessary optionals weaken type safety.
mode_rationale: Simple type safety convention

rule: When adding a new field to a domain mapping or data transformation, update or add a test that verifies the field is correctly set.
mode_rationale: Single testing convention about covering new fields

rule: After a state-changing action on a detail view (e.g., restore, archive), refetch the entity and stay on the detail page rather than navigating back to the list.
mode_rationale: Single UI behavior convention

rule: Keep core traits and interfaces clean of external library dependencies; refactor shared classes so a dependency-free trait can be extracted.
mode_rationale: Single interface design guideline

rule: Conversion and mapping logic should live on the relevant domain class or its companion object rather than in unrelated service code.
mode_rationale: Single code organization convention

rule: When transforming a collection, prefer a single map/traversal that produces the final result rather than chaining separate map, zip, and toMap calls that create unnecessary intermediate collections.
mode_rationale: Single performance convention about collection traversals

rule: When an existing role or entity has a semantically loaded name that doesn't exactly match the new use case, create a new purpose-specific one rather than repurposing it, to avoid future bugs when the original's permissions evolve independently.
mode_rationale: Single design guideline about role semantics

rule: When a useEffect callback dependency becomes stable (e.g., wrapped in useCallback with empty deps), add it to the dependency array rather than leaving it out, to keep the hook correct and avoid stale-closure bugs.
mode_rationale: Single React hooks convention

rule: When a function parameter has an obvious default value used by most callers, make it optional with that default rather than requiring every caller to pass it explicitly.
mode_rationale: Single API design convention about defaults

rule: When the same literal string or value appears multiple times in a test file, extract it into a shared variable (e.g., in the Background section for feature files) to reduce duplication and ease future updates.
mode_rationale: Simple coding convention to reduce duplication in test files

rule: E2E tests should not assert on excessive specific UI copy; reduce text-matching assertions to only what is essential for validating behavior, so tests are resilient to copy changes.
mode_rationale: Single guideline about minimizing text assertions in E2E tests

rule: When a component toggles between states (e.g., expand/collapse), its data-testid and accessible label should update dynamically to reflect the current state, improving both testability and screen-reader experience.
mode_rationale: Convention for keeping test IDs and labels in sync with component state

rule: When adding or changing feature flags, configuration, or shared resources in tests, verify that the change does not create side effects for other tests running in parallel.
mode_rationale: Awareness guideline about side effects in parallel test environments

rule: Every new service or domain class (e.g., a Resolver or Handler) should have a corresponding *Spec.scala test file; do not merge new logic without tests.
mode_rationale: Simple rule requiring test files for new service classes

rule: Remove all println, console.log, or other debug output statements before submitting code for review; use structured logging instead if runtime output is needed.
mode_rationale: Basic hygiene rule to remove debug output before merge

rule: When introducing a default page size or limit for a list endpoint, research actual production data volumes (e.g., number of environments, EDVs, snapshots) to ensure the default serves the majority of users without requiring extra pagination.
mode_rationale: Design guideline to research production data when setting defaults

rule: When adding a new Bazel project/module, run the project generation script (e.g., dev/bazel/gen_bazelproject.py) so that IDE tooling like IntelliJ picks up the new target.
mode_rationale: Procedural step that must be invoked when adding Bazel modules to update IDE tooling

rule: When impersonating a user or using elevated privileges, add a comment explaining why it is safe and what authorization context is being used.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to comment on impersonation/privilege escalation code

rule: API controller operations should use structured logging that includes all relevant context (user ID, entity IDs, operation name) on both success and failure paths, ideally via a reusable helper that captures context once.
mode_rationale: Convention for including structured context in API controller logs

rule: When a method delegates to another service, write a unit test that verifies the delegate is called with the correct arguments.
mode_rationale: Simple testing convention for verifying delegate call arguments

rule: Before logging variables that may contain HTTP headers or request metadata, verify that authentication secrets and tokens are not included in the output.
mode_rationale: Security guideline to check logged variables for secrets

rule: Catch `NonFatal` exceptions instead of broad exception types like `RuntimeException` to avoid swallowing fatal JVM errors.
mode_rationale: Simple rule to catch NonFatal instead of broad exception types

rule: Error log messages must include the operation that failed and all relevant entity identifiers to enable effective debugging.
mode_rationale: Logging convention for error message content

rule: Calling `.get` on an `Option` is a code smell; if the value is always expected to be present, refactor the return type to a non-optional type instead.
mode_rationale: Simple code smell rule about Option.get usage

rule: API response type names should make sense for every endpoint that uses them; avoid naming a shared response after a single operation (e.g., 'SagasStartedResponse' is wrong for a stop endpoint).
mode_rationale: Naming convention for shared API response types

rule: When logging errors, pass the exception object as a parameter to the logger method so the stack trace is captured, rather than only logging the message string.
mode_rationale: Simple logging convention to pass exception objects for stack traces

rule: Capitalize Domino-specific product entity names (Workspace, Model API, Artifacts, Datasets) but use lowercase for generic terms (published apps, docs) in UI copy.
mode_rationale: Style rule for capitalizing Domino product entity names

rule: Capitalize product feature names (e.g., 'Exports', 'Feature Store') when referring to the named feature, but use lowercase for generic actions (e.g., 'export files from a project').
mode_rationale: Style rule distinguishing feature names from generic actions

rule: Use a consistent format for help links across the UI: 'Learn more about [feature name]' without trailing punctuation.
mode_rationale: Simple formatting convention for help links

rule: Every TODO comment in code must have an associated JIRA ticket to ensure it gets tracked and resolved.
mode_rationale: Convention requiring JIRA tickets for TODO comments

rule: Tests that launch jobs or long-running operations should automatically collect debugging artifacts (e.g., support bundles) when the operation fails.
mode_rationale: Testing convention to auto-collect artifacts on failure

rule: Tests that make HTTP requests should explicitly assert the response status code rather than only checking the response body.
mode_rationale: Simple rule to assert status codes in HTTP tests

rule: Magic numbers used in multiple places (e.g., sleep durations, timeouts) should be extracted into named constants or a shared configuration file.
mode_rationale: Convention to replace magic numbers with named constants

rule: Do not specify multiple conflicting version constraints for the same dependency in a requirements file; combine them into a single constraint expression.
mode_rationale: Simple rule about combining dependency version constraints

rule: When adding a feature flag or configuration toggle, add unit tests that validate the correct behavior under each flag state to prevent regressions.
mode_rationale: Convention to test both flag states when adding toggles

rule: Do not skip or comment out tests without documenting the reason and creating a JIRA ticket to re-enable them.
mode_rationale: Simple rule requiring documentation and tickets for skipped tests

rule: Components should inject their own parameters into callbacks rather than requiring parent components to pass curried functions down through multiple levels.
mode_rationale: React pattern for components to inject their own params into callbacks

rule: Do not add eslint-disable comments for useEffect dependency arrays, especially when the dependencies are simple value types like booleans that are cheap to compare.
mode_rationale: Simple rule against suppressing useEffect dependency warnings

rule: Wrap React context provider values in useMemo to prevent unnecessary re-renders of all child components that consume the context.
Files named styles.tsx should only contain styled-components; if a file contains a standalone component with its own logic, it should be extracted to its own file.
mode_rationale: File organization rule keeping styles and components separate

rule: The `useX` naming convention is reserved for React hooks; do not use it for component props, as it misleads readers into thinking it is a hook.
mode_rationale: Naming convention reserving useX for React hooks

rule: Name components to accurately reflect what they render; do not include 'Button' in a component name if the component is not a button.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for accurate component names

rule: In Scala catch blocks, use `NonFatal` pattern matching to avoid catching fatal errors (like OutOfMemoryError) that should propagate and crash the application.
mode_rationale: Scala-specific convention for NonFatal pattern matching in catch blocks

rule: When the same string literal is used as a default value in multiple places, extract it into a shared constant to avoid inconsistency and duplication.
mode_rationale: Convention to deduplicate repeated string literals into constants

rule: E2E tests should assert the semantically correct HTTP status code (e.g., 403 for forbidden access) rather than accepting any 4xx error.
mode_rationale: Testing rule to assert specific HTTP status codes

rule: Do not create a container/wrapper component when there is no significant business logic or backend calls to justify the separation from the presentational component.
mode_rationale: Architecture guideline against unnecessary wrapper components

rule: In BDD/Gherkin feature files, steps that verify or assert an outcome should start with 'Then', not 'And' or other keywords.
mode_rationale: BDD convention for using Then keyword in assertion steps

rule: When a test step exists for a non-obvious reason (such as verifying the absence of a previously known bug), add a comment explaining the intent.
mode_rationale: Convention to document non-obvious test step intent

rule: Use Option/Optional types to represent absent values instead of defaulting to empty strings.
mode_rationale: Type design convention preferring Option over empty strings

rule: Obtain entity IDs from API responses rather than parsing them out of URLs.
mode_rationale: Simple rule to get IDs from API responses not URL parsing

rule: When introducing a new config value that changes behavior, deploy with a safe no-op default first, then change the value in a follow-up PR to prevent breaking production.
mode_rationale: Two-step deployment workflow: deploy with safe default first, then change value in follow-up PR

rule: When adding config files (e.g. .env.*), ensure they are COPY'd in the Dockerfile and validated with a container structure test.
mode_rationale: Multi-step procedure: add config file, COPY in Dockerfile, validate with container structure test

rule: Place new configuration keys in the existing section designated for that feature area rather than adding them in an arbitrary location.
mode_rationale: Simple organization rule for placing config keys in correct sections

rule: Do not expose internal implementation details (such as retry library callbacks) in utility function signatures; keep them encapsulated so the underlying library can be swapped later.
mode_rationale: Encapsulation convention for utility function signatures

rule: Use existing helper methods (e.g. wait_for_status) instead of reimplementing polling or status-check logic inline in tests.
In error handlers for async data fetching, reset all dependent state variables (e.g. flags and content) to safe defaults, not just the error state.
mode_rationale: Error handling convention to reset all dependent state on failure

rule: Create a styled component for icons and elements instead of targeting them with nested CSS class selectors.
mode_rationale: CSS-in-JS convention preferring styled components over nested selectors

rule: Place data detection and validation logic on the backend where the source of truth exists, rather than reimplementing complex matching logic on the frontend.
mode_rationale: Architecture guideline placing validation logic on the backend

rule: Define static constants outside the React component's function scope to avoid unnecessary re-creation on each render.
Absolute imports must come before relative imports; rely on eslint/prettier auto-fix to enforce consistent import ordering.
mode_rationale: Simple import ordering convention enforced by tooling

rule: Do not mark a function as async if it contains no await expressions.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence coding convention about async/await usage

rule: Add an explanatory comment for return statements whose purpose is not immediately clear from the surrounding code.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation guideline for return statements

rule: Styled components must be defined outside the React component body, not inline within JSX.
mode_rationale: Single rule about styled component placement

rule: Remove commented-out code before merging; use version control history to recover deleted code if needed.
Define static constants (arrays, objects, config values) outside the React component function to avoid unnecessary re-creation on each render.
mode_rationale: Single performance convention for React components

rule: Prefer styled components over inline styles, especially when the same style is applied to multiple elements.
When modifying code near eslint-disable comments, verify the directive still applies to the correct line and does not break the original suppression intent.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence caution about eslint-disable comments

rule: When deleting a file, also remove all references to it (CI configs, imports, test steps) to prevent broken builds.
mode_rationale: Simple cleanup convention when deleting files
Placeholder/unclassified pattern with no rule content

rule: When changing how configuration values are parsed or validated, verify the change does not break upgrades for customers with existing configurations.
mode_rationale: Single guideline about config change safety

rule: Place reusable test helpers in the shared tests-lib module rather than duplicating them in individual service test directories.
mode_rationale: Simple code organization convention for test helpers

rule: When a provider has multiple distinct implementations (e.g., cloud vs. datacenter), use separate feature flags so each can be toggled independently.
mode_rationale: Single architectural guideline about feature flag granularity

rule: When an enum value's name does not clearly convey what it represents (e.g., a legacy key), add a comment explaining the mapping.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation convention for enum values

rule: When the same transformation or sanitization logic appears in multiple places, move it to a shared domain object so consumers get the behavior for free.
mode_rationale: Single DRY principle for sanitization logic

rule: When parsing or manipulating strings (e.g., stripping suffixes), consider edge cases where the target pattern appears multiple times in the input.
mode_rationale: Single defensive coding guideline for string parsing

rule: Avoid marking a value as lazy when it is used on the very next line; the deferred evaluation adds overhead with no benefit.
mode_rationale: Simple performance convention about lazy evaluation

rule: When an operation can fail for multiple reasons, write error messages that are general enough to be accurate for any failure mode rather than assuming a single cause.
mode_rationale: Single guideline about error message generality

rule: When matching elements between two collections, convert one to a Set for O(1) lookups instead of nested iteration that produces O(n²) runtime.
mode_rationale: Single performance convention about collection lookups

rule: Prefer idiomatic collection operations like collect with partial functions over manual filter-then-map chains for cleaner, more readable code.
mode_rationale: Simple Scala convention: use isEmpty instead of manual size checks

rule: Name variables to reflect their domain meaning (e.g., workspaceId instead of a generic name) so readers immediately understand the value's purpose.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention about domain-driven names

rule: Provider-specific methods should live on the provider's own implementation class, not on a shared base client used by all providers.
mode_rationale: Single code organization rule about provider methods

rule: When adding a new provider, verify it handles the same edge cases (e.g., missing branches) as existing providers to maintain consistent behavior.
mode_rationale: Single architectural guideline about provider consistency

rule: Use functional array methods like map, filter, and reduce instead of imperatively creating arrays and pushing elements into them.
mode_rationale: Simple style preference for functional array methods

rule: Define styled components outside the React component body to prevent them from being regenerated on every re-render.
mode_rationale: Single performance convention identical to define-styled-components-outside-jsx

rule: Show a loading indicator while asynchronous data (e.g., dropdowns, selectors) is being fetched to give users feedback that content is on the way.
mode_rationale: Single UX convention about loading feedback

rule: Import shared internal component wrappers (e.g., tooltipRenderer) instead of importing directly from third-party libraries like antd to maintain consistency and allow centralized customization.
mode_rationale: Single architectural rule about import indirection

rule: Use shared form components like FormItem for consistent styling and layout across the application rather than ad-hoc form markup.
mode_rationale: Simple convention about shared form component usage

rule: Configuration keys must exactly match the names referenced in the code that reads them; mismatches cause silent failures.
mode_rationale: Single correctness rule about config key naming

rule: Define default values in one canonical location (e.g., the config file) and remove duplicate defaults from code to avoid drift between them.
mode_rationale: Single DRY principle for default values

rule: Remove personal identifiers, debug print statements, and temporary markers from code before merging a PR.
mode_rationale: Simple code hygiene convention

rule: Name variables and config flags to precisely describe what they represent (e.g., use 'Count' suffix for counts, 'Size' for byte sizes).
mode_rationale: Single naming convention about precise suffixes

rule: Do not mark route parameters as required if the controller already handles the missing/empty case with a default value.
mode_rationale: Single API design rule about parameter optionality

rule: When a method's parameter list or behavior changes, update the method name to accurately reflect the new contract.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention about method name accuracy

rule: Scope CSS and style overrides to the specific component or element that needs them, rather than applying broad selectors that affect unrelated components.
mode_rationale: Single CSS scoping convention

rule: Remove all debugging print/log statements before submitting a PR for review.
Use the same DTO model structure across related endpoints (e.g., list and get-by-id) to avoid divergence and inconsistency.
mode_rationale: Single API design consistency guideline

rule: Design DTOs and domain models so that invalid combinations of fields are unrepresentable at the type level, rather than relying on runtime validation.
mode_rationale: Single type-safety architectural principle

rule: User-facing error and validation messages should start with a capitalized word.
mode_rationale: Simple formatting convention for error messages

rule: When the same logic appears in multiple files, extract it into a shared utility or hook to avoid duplication.
mode_rationale: Single DRY principle for duplicated logic

rule: When a component has multiple conditional branches for a feature flag, consider splitting it into separate components — one per flag state — to reduce complexity.
mode_rationale: Single code organization guideline about component complexity

rule: Test mocks that could be reused across test suites should live in a shared mocks package, not alongside individual test files.
mode_rationale: Single code organization convention for test mocks

rule: Always use design system color variables/tokens instead of hardcoded hex or color values in component styles.
Never use .get() on Scala Option values; use .map/.getOrElse or pattern matching to handle the None case safely.
mode_rationale: Single safety convention for Scala Option handling

rule: When naming log events or event types, check that the name won't conflict or cause confusion with upcoming features that will use similar naming.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention about forward-compatible event names

rule: E2E test steps should not depend on specific UI page state when the same data can be obtained via API, as this makes tests brittle and limits reusability.
mode_rationale: Single testing guideline about API vs UI dependencies

rule: Always use the project's shared/common UI components (e.g., Domino modal) instead of creating custom one-off implementations, to ensure visual consistency and simplify future restyling.
mode_rationale: Single convention about using shared UI components

rule: When an API field is conditionally present, use Option/Optional types to encode optionality rather than adding separate boolean flags that duplicate intent and force callers to provide unused values.
mode_rationale: Single API design rule about encoding optionality

rule: When adding retry/wait logic to tests, extend the existing helper function rather than creating a new wrapper; tests that don't need retries can call the underlying API directly.
mode_rationale: Single convention about extending vs duplicating test helpers

rule: In tests, wait for a specific status transition (e.g., wait_for_status(stopped)) rather than using an overly generous max timeout, to make tests faster and failure causes clearer.
mode_rationale: Single testing convention about wait strategies

rule: When implementing exponential backoff or retry delay calculations, ensure the math cannot overflow numeric type limits as attempt counts grow large.
mode_rationale: Single defensive coding guideline for math safety

rule: When modifying the semantics of API parameters (e.g., making fields optional or changing nullability), update the corresponding scaladoc/documentation to describe the new behavior (e.g., 'Ignore if None, Unassign if Some(None)').
mode_rationale: Simple rule to keep docs in sync with code changes

rule: When setting a boolean prop to true in JSX, use the shorthand form (e.g., `<Route exact />`) instead of the explicit form (`exact={true}`).
mode_rationale: Single-sentence JSX style convention

rule: When adding or changing fields in a backend API, verify whether frontend mocks, TypeScript types, or Swagger/OpenAPI docs also need to be updated to stay in sync.
mode_rationale: Reminder to check related contracts when changing APIs

rule: Every bug fix PR should include a test that reproduces the bug and verifies the fix.
mode_rationale: Simple policy: every bug fix needs a regression test

rule: When gating a feature behind a feature flag, ensure all related UI elements (checkboxes, buttons, columns) are also hidden, not just the primary component.
mode_rationale: Single rule about feature flag completeness

rule: In Python test helpers, use python-box (or equivalent established library) for dict-to-attribute access instead of writing manual conversion logic.
mode_rationale: Prefer library over hand-rolled code for dict access

rule: When polling for a status change, short-circuit on all terminal statuses (error, stopped, failed, etc.), not just the success state.
mode_rationale: Single defensive coding rule for polling loops

rule: Drive hardcoded values (URLs, thresholds, environment-specific strings) from constants defined in a config file rather than inlining them.
mode_rationale: Standard code organization convention

rule: CI shell scripts should use `set -exuo pipefail` for debuggability and fail-fast behavior, and consolidate related commands into a single step to reduce complexity.
mode_rationale: Simple CI scripting convention

rule: Replace or annotate magic literals (like `true true`) with named variables or code comments so their purpose is immediately clear.
mode_rationale: Readability convention for unexplained literals

rule: Use the roles-and-permissions system to model access control rather than writing ad-hoc conditional authorization checks; create tech debt tickets if refactoring is needed to unblock a release.
mode_rationale: Prefer existing authz system over ad-hoc checks

rule: Split authorization checks so each query only requires the permissions it actually needs, rather than demanding broad authorization for narrowly scoped operations.
mode_rationale: Principle of least privilege for authorization

rule: Add explicit type annotations to vals/variables that hold complex expressions (tuples, mapped collections) to improve readability.
mode_rationale: Readability convention for complex type expressions

rule: When implementing search or filter functionality on user-provided strings, normalize to lowercase before comparison to avoid case-sensitivity bugs.
mode_rationale: Simple defensive coding rule for string comparison

rule: Follow established project naming conventions (e.g., directory-per-hook with matching names) consistently, even when the resulting names are long.
mode_rationale: Consistency convention for naming

rule: Use the standard Domino modal and other shared design system components instead of building custom implementations, to maintain UI consistency.
mode_rationale: Single rule: use design system, don't build custom

rule: Only update state or flags after the associated asynchronous operation (e.g., remote call, database write) completes successfully, not before.
mode_rationale: Single defensive coding rule for async state updates

rule: Critical business logic such as cache refresh and invalidation must have dedicated tests, even if mocking is difficult — find a way to test it.
mode_rationale: Testing policy for critical business logic

rule: When adding or changing operational configuration (timeouts, feature flags, cache settings), document it in the relevant runbook so on-call engineers can find it.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation habit for operational changes

rule: Test helper functions that detect failures should call record_failure (or equivalent) so that support bundles and diagnostic artifacts are captured for debugging.
mode_rationale: Single testing convention for failure recording

rule: When implementing retry logic for opaque exceptions, match on specific known error messages rather than broad exception types to avoid retrying non-retryable failures.
mode_rationale: Defensive coding rule for retry specificity

rule: Log messages and status values should accurately describe the operation being performed (e.g., use 'Stopped' not 'Delete' when stopping a run).
mode_rationale: Simple accuracy rule for log messages

rule: Sample or example deployment configurations should include comments warning where production configs need to be more restrictive (e.g., network policies, RBAC).
mode_rationale: Documentation convention for example configs

rule: Use generic type parameters or `unknown` instead of `any` in TypeScript component APIs to preserve type safety.
mode_rationale: TypeScript type safety convention

rule: When two methods return the same result, consolidate them into a single method to reduce confusion and maintenance burden.
mode_rationale: DRY principle for method consolidation

rule: Place fields on the DTO that conceptually owns them rather than overloading a related DTO with cross-cutting concerns.
mode_rationale: Single API design principle for field placement

rule: Error messages thrown from domain-specific code should include the domain entity name so the error is immediately identifiable in logs and stack traces.
When a find-then-throw-if-missing pattern repeats across a codebase, extract it into a dedicated `findOrThrow` method on the relevant manager or repository.
mode_rationale: DRY convention for find-or-throw patterns

rule: When a refactoring removes most of a file's contents, delete the file entirely rather than leaving a near-empty shell.
mode_rationale: Simple cleanup rule after refactoring

rule: When an inline expression grows complex (nested maps, Options, chained accessors), assign it to a descriptively named variable to improve readability.
When the same few lines of logic appear multiple times in a file, extract them into a dedicated method to reduce duplication and maintenance risk.
mode_rationale: Standard DRY principle

rule: When an Ant Design table column simply displays the field value without custom formatting, use `dataIndex` alone and omit the `render` prop.
mode_rationale: Ant Design table simplification convention

rule: Styled components should live in their own component files or alongside the component that uses them, not in shared utility files.
mode_rationale: File organization convention for styled components

rule: Application route paths and link constants should be defined in the core routing module, not scattered across utility or feature files.
mode_rationale: Single rule about route definition placement

rule: Function and step names should include the domain or service they interact with (e.g., 'keycloak') to avoid ambiguity, even if it feels redundant — clarity for readers outweighs brevity.
mode_rationale: Naming clarity convention

rule: If a function is called only once and there is no clear reason to expect reuse, inline it rather than extracting it into a separate function.
mode_rationale: Code organization preference against premature extraction

rule: Magic literals (strings, URLs, file paths) embedded in the middle of functions should be extracted to a config file and referenced from there.
mode_rationale: Standard config management convention

rule: When iterating over items to perform operations that can fail, extract the loop body into a function that fails gracefully so that a failure on one item does not prevent processing the remaining items.
mode_rationale: Defensive coding rule for graceful loop iteration

rule: Route components should use lazy loading to avoid loading unnecessary code upfront and improve initial page load performance.
mode_rationale: Performance convention for route code splitting

rule: When a component maps between a fixed set of values (e.g., type to icon), use a dictionary/map rather than conditional logic to make the mapping explicit and easy to extend.
mode_rationale: Code pattern preference for value mapping

rule: When accessing DOM element attributes in tests, prefer getAttribute('href') over type casting to avoid unnecessary type assertions and keep test code idiomatic.
mode_rationale: Test code style convention

rule: Debug log messages should include sufficient context (e.g., the object being operated on) so that logs are useful for troubleshooting without additional instrumentation.
mode_rationale: Logging quality convention

rule: If a function returns the same result every render and takes no arguments, define its output as a constant outside the component to avoid unnecessary recomputation.
mode_rationale: React performance convention

rule: Pin new dependency versions in package.json to avoid unexpected breaking changes from semver-range updates.
Reference color values from the shared design token file (e.g., colors.ts) instead of hardcoding hex values in component files.
mode_rationale: Design system convention for color values

rule: When building new UI features, use the project's existing shared components (modals, buttons, etc.) to maintain visual consistency across the application.
mode_rationale: UI consistency convention

rule: Replace magic numbers with named constants that convey the value's purpose.
When changing the semantics of a value, update all variable and string references to reflect the new meaning — stale names mislead future readers.
mode_rationale: Naming accuracy convention post-refactor

rule: When a test checks for the presence of unwanted resources or states, ensure it actually fails (e.g., raises an exception or calls pytest.fail) when the condition is detected, rather than silently passing.
mode_rationale: Testing correctness rule for failure assertions

rule: Use the project's structured audit event system instead of logger.info with hand-formatted strings for actions that need to be auditable.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use structured audit events instead of ad-hoc log strings

rule: Do not add audit or log events at intermediate processing steps if the completing step already records the same information.
mode_rationale: Single convention: don't log at intermediate steps if the final step already logs

rule: When renaming a parameter in one file, update the corresponding parameter names in all related interfaces, traits, and adapter files to keep the codebase consistent.
mode_rationale: Single convention: keep parameter names consistent across interfaces when renaming

rule: Before adding audit events to an operation, evaluate how frequently it fires in practice — high-frequency triggers (e.g., file preview clicks) can create excessive noise.
mode_rationale: Single convention: evaluate frequency before adding audit events

rule: Use 'preview' instead of 'beta' for pre-release API path segments to maintain consistent terminology across the platform.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use 'preview' not 'beta' for pre-release API paths

rule: Use 201 for resource creation, 200 for responses with a body, and 204 for delete operations with no response body — do not mix them up.
mode_rationale: Single convention: match HTTP status codes to operation semantics

rule: Name creation request DTOs as `New[EntityName]` and update request DTOs as `Updated[EntityName]` to clearly distinguish their purpose in the API contract.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention: New[Entity] for creation, Updated[Entity] for updates

rule: Use PATCH instead of PUT for update endpoints to support partial updates and avoid requiring clients to send the full resource representation.
mode_rationale: Single API design preference for partial updates

rule: Write OpenAPI operation descriptions in third person (e.g., 'Gets all resources') rather than imperative or first person.
mode_rationale: Single style rule: write OpenAPI descriptions in third person

rule: Use primary key IDs rather than names when referencing other entities in API contracts to avoid sync complexity if entities are renamed.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use IDs not names for entity references in API contracts

rule: When a parent and its children should be created as a unit (e.g., from a single UI confirmation), include child resources in the parent creation request rather than splitting across multiple endpoints to avoid partial success states.
mode_rationale: Single design principle: include children in parent creation to avoid partial success

rule: When replacing child resources during a parent update, archive the old records instead of deleting them to preserve referential integrity from existing associations.
mode_rationale: Single convention: archive old records instead of deleting to preserve referential integrity

rule: Verify that status codes declared in the OpenAPI spec match what the server actually returns at runtime, especially when code generators may silently override spec values.
mode_rationale: Single convention: verify spec status codes match actual server responses

rule: Do not reuse the same endpoint for admin and regular user access when the response shape, visible fields, or permission model differs — create distinct endpoints to avoid leaking admin-only data.
mode_rationale: Single convention: don't reuse endpoints when response shapes differ by role

rule: Use consistent phrasing across similar API endpoint descriptions (e.g., if one list endpoint says 'based on the provided filters', all similar list endpoints should use the same wording).
mode_rationale: Single style rule: use consistent phrasing across similar endpoint descriptions

rule: Update/PATCH request schemas should not mark fields as required, since an update may only modify a subset of fields.
mode_rationale: Single convention: PATCH schemas should not mark fields as required

rule: When introducing a replacement for existing code (new API spec, new module, etc.), create a ticket to track removal of the old version so it doesn't get forgotten.
mode_rationale: Single convention: create a ticket when introducing replacements for old code

rule: Add debug logs at key checkpoints (e.g., readiness checks, polling loops) that include identifying context such as resource name, current count, and expected count.
mode_rationale: Single convention: include identifying context in debug log messages

rule: Internal infrastructure errors and retry failures should not bubble up to user-facing output; keep them in system logs only.
mode_rationale: Single convention: keep infrastructure errors in system logs, not user-facing output

rule: Avoid embedding route paths directly inside UI components; centralize route definitions so they can be maintained in one place.
mode_rationale: Single convention: centralize route definitions instead of embedding paths in components

rule: Before implementing custom behavior (e.g., manually destroying inactive tabs), check if the component library already provides a prop or built-in feature for it.
mode_rationale: Single convention: check for built-in library features before implementing custom behavior

rule: Before writing new state management logic for common UI patterns (tabs, search params, filters), check if an internal hook or utility already exists in the codebase.
mode_rationale: Single convention: check for existing hooks/utilities before writing new state management

rule: In pattern matches or switch statements, let removed or legacy cases fall through to the default/wildcard handler rather than listing them explicitly.
mode_rationale: Single convention: let removed cases fall through to default handler

rule: Use idiomatic Scala collection methods like `.map(...).toMap` and `.collect` with pattern matching instead of imperative loop-and-accumulate patterns.
mode_rationale: Single style rule: use idiomatic collection methods instead of imperative loops

rule: Initialize collections with their intended values at declaration rather than creating an empty collection and mutating it afterward.
mode_rationale: Single convention: initialize collections with values at declaration

rule: New or modified integration endpoints (especially third-party provider integrations) must have corresponding system tests that exercise the real endpoint.
mode_rationale: Single convention: new integration endpoints must have system tests

rule: Test scenario titles should precisely describe the behavior being validated, not a vague or inaccurate summary of the feature area.
mode_rationale: Single convention: test titles must precisely describe the behavior being validated

rule: Use ES module syntax (import/export) and appropriate file extensions (.mts) instead of CommonJS require() when the toolchain supports it.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use ES module syntax instead of CommonJS require()

rule: Before adding a new dependency injection binding, check whether the same binding already exists in another injection module to avoid duplicates.
mode_rationale: Single convention: check for existing DI bindings before adding new ones
