rule: Methods should be placed in the service that owns the domain concept, not in a related but semantically incorrect service (e.g., launcher config belongs in the launcher service, not the launcher job service).
mode_rationale: Single convention: methods belong in the service that owns the domain concept

rule: Class names should clearly communicate the entity's purpose; avoid vague, cryptic, or 'weird' names that force readers to inspect the implementation to understand intent.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention: class names must clearly communicate purpose

rule: Add a description or doc comment to entity classes and data structures whose purpose is not immediately obvious from the name alone.
mode_rationale: Single convention: add doc comments to entity classes with non-obvious purposes

rule: Always use strict equality (===) instead of loose equality (==) for comparisons in JavaScript and TypeScript code.
mode_rationale: Single convention: always use === instead of ==

rule: Order import statements so that absolute (library/package) imports come before relative (local module) imports.
Prefer separate, flat styled components over nesting styles within a single styled component when you control the child elements.
mode_rationale: Single style rule: prefer flat styled components over nested styles

rule: Use the short syntax <> instead of React.Fragment when no key or attributes are needed.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use <> instead of React.Fragment when no key is needed

rule: Side effects like changing user selection or UI state after an async operation must be gated on the success path, not executed unconditionally after the operation completes.
mode_rationale: Single convention: gate side effects on the success path of async operations

rule: Place feature components in descriptive subfolders (e.g., guardrails/project/models/) rather than dumping them at the module root.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use descriptive subfolders instead of dumping at module root

rule: Use JSX interpolation with template expressions instead of the + operator for string concatenation inside JSX.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use template expressions instead of + in JSX

rule: Trim JSON test fixtures to the minimum data needed to exercise the test case; avoid committing large, unredacted API responses.
mode_rationale: Single convention: trim test fixtures to minimum needed data

rule: When pattern matching an Option only to return None for None and transform Some, use .map() instead of an explicit match expression.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use .map() instead of explicit match on Option

rule: E2E test scenarios that create resources (apps, workspaces, etc.) should include cleanup steps to stop or remove them after the scenario completes.
mode_rationale: Single convention: E2E tests should clean up created resources

rule: Before writing inline utility logic, check for existing global or shared helpers (e.g., getAppName) and use them instead of reimplementing.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use existing shared helpers instead of reimplementing

rule: When renaming or removing a configuration flag or central config key, evaluate whether a database migration is needed for existing persisted values.
mode_rationale: Single convention: evaluate DB migration needs when renaming/removing config flags

rule: Code review comments should not be marked as resolved until the underlying question or concern has been actually addressed or answered.
mode_rationale: Single convention: don't mark comments resolved until the concern is addressed

rule: When updating configuration values for a new version, ensure the same change is applied consistently across all version entries that should share the value.
mode_rationale: Single convention: apply config changes consistently across all relevant version entries

rule: Always validate YAML files in an IDE or linter before submitting to catch duplicate keys that silently override values.
mode_rationale: Single convention: validate YAML for duplicate keys before submitting

rule: Never resolve PR review comments until the underlying issue has actually been fixed in code.
mode_rationale: Single convention: never resolve PR comments until the fix is in code

rule: Ensure the PR summary accurately reflects entity names and references used in the actual code diff.
mode_rationale: Single convention: PR summary must accurately reflect actual code changes

rule: Use >= rather than ~= for Python dependency pinning in requirements files to avoid breakage when transitive dependencies shift, unless a known incompatibility requires a tighter bound.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use >= instead of ~= for Python dependency pinning unless needed

rule: If a boolean or derived value is only used by one child component, compute it inside that child rather than passing it down as a prop from the parent.
mode_rationale: Single rule about where to compute derived values

rule: Use the canonical package alias paths (e.g., @domino/core-ui/Link) instead of deep relative imports to keep imports consistent and refactor-friendly.
mode_rationale: Simple import convention

rule: When adding new environment variable dependencies to tests, document them in the project README so other developers know to configure them.
mode_rationale: Single documentation reminder, not a multi-step procedure

rule: Do not maintain duplicate configuration keys that hold the same value; consolidate to a single canonical key used everywhere.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to avoid duplication

rule: Group test scenarios with descriptive comment headers to improve readability and make triage faster when tests fail.
mode_rationale: Single formatting convention for tests

rule: When calling an async API in a React component, catch errors gracefully and show a loading indicator (spinner or disabled state) while the request is in flight.
mode_rationale: Standard React async handling convention

rule: Define explicit typed data structures for API contracts rather than storing arbitrary JSON blobs, to keep code readable, testable, and self-documenting.
mode_rationale: Simple design preference for typed contracts

rule: When designing metadata schemas, capture all available identifiers even if currently redundant, to avoid breaking changes when future features require them.
mode_rationale: Single design guideline about schema completeness

rule: When a React component's render method grows complex, extract logical sections into dedicated sub-components for readability and reusability.
mode_rationale: Simple refactoring convention

rule: Before writing a new helper, search the codebase for existing utilities that compute the same value to avoid duplication and inconsistency.
Always use version constraints (e.g., `>=`) when specifying Python dependencies in requirements files to prevent unexpected breakage from new releases.
mode_rationale: Simple convention for requirements files

rule: When modifying shared test data files, verify all other tests that depend on the same file still pass before merging.
mode_rationale: Single verification step, not a multi-step workflow

rule: When changing shared configuration (e.g., environment images, tags), notify downstream test owners who may be affected by the change.
mode_rationale: Simple communication reminder

rule: When working with external system values (e.g., Kubernetes resource quantities), use the official SDK's parsing and validation utilities rather than hand-rolling string conversions.
mode_rationale: Simple preference for SDK utilities over hand-rolled code

rule: When fixing a bug in a class that lacks tests, add a test class and cover at least the fixed scenario rather than leaving it untested.
mode_rationale: Single testing convention

rule: Use the narrowest fixture scope possible (function over session) to avoid unnecessary environment setup when running subsets of tests.
mode_rationale: Simple convention about fixture scope

rule: Name route-building or utility functions to reflect their specific purpose (e.g., `getProjectLauncherPath` instead of a generic name) so callers understand intent without reading the implementation.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention

rule: Use `2>/dev/null` to suppress stderr output; do not use `2>&1` which merges stderr into stdout instead of discarding it.
mode_rationale: Single shell convention

rule: Include a correlation ID in error responses so operators can match the user-facing error to the corresponding entry in server logs.
mode_rationale: Simple convention for error responses

rule: When renaming or modifying a shared configuration file, search for all services that include or reference it and update them in the same PR.
mode_rationale: Single verification rule for config changes

rule: Before renaming shared configuration files, align with the original authors or domain owners on the new name to ensure it accurately reflects the file's purpose.
mode_rationale: Simple communication convention

rule: If a function's name implies a return value (e.g., `isSomethingEnabled` suggesting a boolean), its actual behavior must match — rename it if it throws exceptions or has side effects instead.
mode_rationale: Simple naming integrity rule

rule: When reviewing tests, verify that both positive (valid input producing expected output) and negative (invalid input returning undefined/error) cases are covered.
mode_rationale: Simple testing convention about positive and negative cases

rule: Do not nest configuration providers (e.g., ConfigProvider) inside feature components; instead, use existing component props to pass customization.
mode_rationale: Single architectural rule about provider placement

rule: Use JSDoc block comment format (/** ... */) for component and function documentation so IDEs display it in hover hints.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation format convention

rule: When removing or renaming fields in serialized models, explicitly verify serialization compatibility and create a separate ticket to track the breaking change.
mode_rationale: Single rule about verifying compatibility on field removal

rule: Boolean props or parameters that modify default behavior should be optional with a sensible default value (typically false), rather than required.
mode_rationale: Simple API design convention

rule: Use template literals for string concatenation and guard against undefined values, since undefined + 'string' produces 'undefinedstring'.
mode_rationale: Simple defensive coding convention

rule: Extract magic numbers (e.g., max lengths, limits) into named constants so they can be reused consistently across related logic.
mode_rationale: Simple code organization convention

rule: Maintain consistent import ordering: absolute/library imports must come before relative imports.
Keep all styled components in a single location per module — either all in a dedicated styles file or all co-located in the component file, never mixed.
mode_rationale: Simple file organization convention

rule: Do not pass empty no-op callback functions (e.g., onChange={() => {}}) when the framework or parent component already handles the behavior.
mode_rationale: Simple cleanup convention

rule: When identical logic appears in multiple components, extract it into a single shared utility function rather than duplicating it.
mode_rationale: Simple DRY convention

rule: Extract repeated string literals (especially query field names and filter keys) into named constants at the class or module level.
mode_rationale: Simple code organization convention

rule: Add test cases for implicit type coercion boundaries (e.g., integer-to-double conversion) in serialization and deserialization logic.
mode_rationale: Simple testing convention for edge cases

rule: When using loggers inside nested or helper functions, verify they actually produce output, as loggers can silently fail when invoked outside their configured context.
mode_rationale: Simple logging verification convention

rule: Review hardcoded timeout and wait values critically; suspiciously long durations (e.g., one hour) should be justified or reduced, even if inherited from existing code.
mode_rationale: Simple code review convention

rule: Remove all commented-out code before submitting a PR; version control preserves history, so dead code should be deleted, not commented.
Do not wrap child components in render functions when the parent is not injecting anything through the render prop; use the component directly as a child instead.
mode_rationale: Simple React convention

rule: Never skip required dependencies in useEffect; if a function references external variables, either declare them as dependencies or define the function inside the useEffect to keep the dependency array honest.
mode_rationale: Simple React hooks convention

rule: Every async operation that can fail must surface feedback to the user; silent failures erode trust and make debugging impossible for end users.
mode_rationale: Simple UX convention for error handling

rule: When using Ant Design Form components, let the Form manage field state rather than using it only as a styling/layout wrapper with separate state management.
mode_rationale: Simple convention about Ant Design Form usage

rule: Always set the name property on Ant Design Form.Item components so the form can properly track and manage field values.
mode_rationale: Simple Ant Design Form.Item convention

rule: Do not wrap JSX in a React Fragment when only a single child element is returned; fragments should only be used to group multiple siblings without adding a DOM node.
mode_rationale: Simple JSX cleanup convention

rule: Functions that do not reference component state, props, or hooks should be defined outside the component body to avoid unnecessary re-creation on each render and to clarify that they are pure utilities.
mode_rationale: Simple performance convention about function placement

rule: In UI tests, prefer async waitFor assertions over hardcoded setTimeout delays; fixed delays are flaky and slow, while waitFor polls until the condition is met.
mode_rationale: Simple testing convention for async assertions

rule: Review PRs for leftover debug statements, temporary scaffolding, and test artifacts that were not intended to be committed.
mode_rationale: Simple code hygiene convention

rule: Do not encode known/fixed values (e.g., type: object) in API specs when they can be enforced programmatically; keep specs declarative and lean.
mode_rationale: Simple API spec design convention

rule: When designing schemas that may eventually be user-defined, minimize surface area now to reduce future validation burden.
mode_rationale: Simple schema design guideline

rule: When a field in an API spec has a fixed set of allowed values, always declare an enum constraint to prevent invalid input.
mode_rationale: Simple API spec validation convention

rule: Use explicit state flags (e.g., stopProps being set) to determine intent rather than inferring meaning from a status enum, which may conflate different scenarios.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence guideline about preferring explicit flags over inferred status

rule: If a component prop is always passed by every caller, declare it as required; optional props should only be used when callers genuinely omit the value.
mode_rationale: Simple convention about prop optionality declarations

rule: Write test step definitions that clearly describe their action; avoid 'clever' steps that bundle multiple behaviors in non-obvious ways, as future authors won't reuse them.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence guideline about test step clarity

rule: Review test scenarios for steps that duplicate assertions or setup already performed by prior steps, and remove them to keep scenarios concise.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to remove duplicate test assertions

rule: Prefer explicit constructor parameters over Scala implicits, as implicit params create hidden nullary constructors that produce surprising instantiation behavior.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence preference for explicit over implicit constructor params

rule: Follow the project's established import ordering convention; keep imports grouped and sorted consistently across files.
When multiple permission check paths (e.g., All vs. ByName) have nearly identical logic, consolidate them into a single check to reduce duplication and maintenance burden.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence guideline to reduce duplication in permission logic

rule: When assigning or restricting permissions for user roles (especially lite/restricted roles), explicitly verify the expected access level with the product manager before merging.
mode_rationale: Simple checkpoint rule to verify permissions with PM before merging

rule: Use React's useId() hook to generate stable, unique IDs for DOM elements instead of manual ID generation to avoid collisions and hydration mismatches.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence convention to use useId() for DOM element IDs

rule: In e2e tests, ensure test entities (orgs, projects) are created by the appropriate user role so that the entity owner is not part of the group being tested, avoiding false positives from implicit owner permissions.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence guideline about test entity ownership to avoid false positives

rule: When adding new configuration values, verify whether existing code pulls and overrides these values from a remote source (e.g., nucleus), and ensure the override precedence is correct.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to verify config override precedence when adding values

rule: Use default wait timeouts in e2e tests unless there is a specific, documented reason to override them; custom timeouts add unnecessary complexity and divergence.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to use default timeouts in e2e tests

rule: Use useMemo to create a debounced handler and useCallback for the change handler instead of wiring debounce logic through useEffect.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence preference for useMemo/useCallback debounce pattern

rule: Absolute imports must appear before relative imports in the import block.
Replace growing if/else or switch chains with a list and Array.includes() to keep conditional structures from expanding unboundedly.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to replace growing conditionals with array lookup

rule: Do not add explicit cleanup steps in tests when the test framework or setup already handles that cleanup automatically.
trigger: When adding teardown or cleanup logic (e.g., deleting records, closing connections) in a test method or afterEach block
rationale: Without checking what the framework already handles, redundant cleanup adds noise, slows test execution, and can interfere with framework-managed lifecycle (e.g., double-closing a connection).
mode_rationale: Simple convention to not duplicate framework-provided cleanup
steps: Step 1: Check if the test fixture, base class, or framework (e.g., @Transactional, withDatabase) already handles cleanup
Step 2: Verify by reading the fixture's afterEach/afterAll or teardown method
Step 3: Only add explicit cleanup for resources the framework does NOT manage
Step 4: If you must add cleanup, add it in the fixture/base class so all tests benefit

rule: Index files should only re-export; do not define function implementations directly in index.ts files.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence rule about index file purpose

rule: API endpoint wrappers should be added to the shared @domino/api package rather than defined inline in feature modules.
mode_rationale: Simple convention about where to place API endpoint wrappers

rule: When a type is useful beyond the component that defines it, export it from the shared package so consumers don't have to re-declare it.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence guideline about type export location

rule: Modals should be agnostic of parent-level side effects like data refetching; pass an onClose or onSuccess callback from the parent instead.
mode_rationale: Simple architectural convention about modal responsibility boundaries

rule: When a mutation affects multiple entities, expose an onSuccess callback so parent components can coordinate follow-up actions like refetching related data.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence API design convention about onSuccess callbacks

rule: When a user action triggers multiple sequential API calls, display a loading indicator so the user knows the operation is in progress.
mode_rationale: Simple UX convention to show loading state during sequential API calls

rule: Before creating a new utility function, check whether adding a parameter to an existing function achieves the same result with less surface area.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence guideline to extend existing functions before creating new ones

rule: In authorization-related classes and methods, place the principal or identity parameter at the top of the parameter list for consistency.
mode_rationale: Simple parameter ordering convention for auth code

rule: Do not use explicit `any` as a type annotation when TypeScript can infer the type automatically; rely on type inference instead.
When modifying a handler or function used by multiple callers, verify that existing integration/e2e tests covering all call sites still pass before merging.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence guideline to check test coverage across call sites

rule: Generic infrastructure code (e.g., AWS SDK wrappers) should live in shared modules like common-core, not in domain-specific packages; replace domain-specific config objects with primitive parameters to remove coupling.
mode_rationale: Simple architectural convention about where generic code belongs

rule: When wrapping SDK types, adopt the naming conventions of the target SDK version (e.g., use 'Response' instead of 'Result' when migrating to AWS SDK v2).
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for SDK wrapper types

rule: When wrapping exceptions across SDK versions, define proper custom exceptions that set message and cause on the parent Throwable; never throw wrapper objects that lose the original stack trace.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence rule about preserving exception cause chains

rule: Do not include code in a PR that cannot be exercised or validated until a future PR; defer it to keep the PR reviewable and testable.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to defer unexercisable code to future PRs

rule: Generic trait methods should return structured types or Unit, not serialized strings; let callers decide how to serialize if needed.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence API design rule about return types

rule: When the same conversion or transformation logic (e.g., safe number parsing) is duplicated across multiple places, extract it into a shared utility function.
mode_rationale: Simple DRY convention for repeated transformation logic

rule: Use consistent naming suffixes and verb choices across related type families (e.g., all event types should follow the same pattern like 'BulkUpdate' / 'BulkDelete' rather than mixing 'Change', 'Updated', 'UpdateChange').
mode_rationale: Single-sentence naming consistency rule for type families

rule: Authentication redirect logic must be scoped to the specific routes that require it; do not place auth guards in always-mounted wrapper components where they can inadvertently redirect anonymous-accessible routes to login.
mode_rationale: Simple architectural convention about auth guard placement

rule: Always roll back temporary or unstable dependency versions to a stable develop branch version before merging a PR.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to roll back unstable dependency versions before merge

rule: Avoid using prototype method calls directly on objects; prefer static methods like Object.keys(obj).forEach() instead.
mode_rationale: Simple style rule preferring static methods over prototype calls

rule: useEffect dependency arrays must only include values actually used inside the effect; do not add extra dependencies that are not referenced.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence React convention about dependency array correctness

rule: Absolute imports should appear before relative imports in the import block.
Use theme helper tokens (e.g., themeHelper('margins.tiny')) for spacing values instead of hardcoded CSS values.
Do not use onSuccess/onError callback patterns for API requests; let exceptions propagate and handle them with try/catch or .catch() at the component/hook level.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence error handling convention against callback patterns

rule: API request functions should not handle error notification concerns; error alerting and UI feedback should be the responsibility of the consuming component or hook.
mode_rationale: Simple separation of concerns rule for API error handling

rule: When catching errors in the UI, consider whether the user should be alerted rather than silently logging to the console.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence guideline to alert users rather than silent console logging

rule: Avoid nested ternary expressions; use if/else blocks, early returns, or helper functions for readability.
mode_rationale: Simple readability convention against nested ternaries

rule: Move constant values outside of function bodies and name them using UPPER_SNAKE_CASE.
mode_rationale: Simple naming and placement convention for constants

rule: Functions that use magic numbers or non-obvious numeric thresholds should include documentation explaining what the numbers represent and what the function is expected to return.
Replace raw numeric literals with named constants that convey their meaning.
mode_rationale: Standard coding convention against magic numbers

rule: Use explicit TypeScript return type annotations on functions instead of casting the return value, which provides better type safety and cleaner code.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence TypeScript convention for return type annotations

rule: When conditionally rendering in React, return null rather than an empty wrapper element like <span> or <div> to avoid polluting the DOM.
mode_rationale: Simple React convention for conditional rendering

rule: When using styled-components, define all styles in the styled component definition rather than using inline style props, to keep styling centralized and maintainable.
In JSX, pass string literal props without curly braces (e.g., prop="value" not prop={"value"}) for cleaner syntax.
mode_rationale: Simple JSX syntax convention for string literal props

rule: Use Array.some() instead of filter-and-check-length when you only need to know whether any element matches a condition.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence convention preferring one array method over another

rule: Keep utility/helper functions in separate utility files rather than defining them inside component files, to improve reusability and separation of concerns.
mode_rationale: Simple organizational guideline about file placement

rule: Move constant/static data and derived values that don't depend on props or state outside the React component body to avoid unnecessary recalculation on every render.
mode_rationale: Single rule about where to place constant data relative to components

rule: Reference colors from the centralized colors.ts (or design token) file rather than defining ad-hoc color values in components.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to reference shared color tokens instead of ad-hoc values

rule: Do not use className-based styling when styled-components are available; classNames should only be a last resort for unreachable nested elements.
mode_rationale: Single styling convention about preferring styled-components over className

rule: When the same style override appears in multiple views, move it to the shared component that renders the styled element to keep styling DRY.
mode_rationale: DRY principle applied to style overrides — one-sentence guideline

rule: When a piece of UI is self-contained, extract it into its own component file and render it from the parent, rather than inlining the full markup.
mode_rationale: Simple component extraction guideline, not a multi-step procedure

rule: When writing ScalaDoc, document all parameters and include a sentence describing what the method does, not just a subset of the information.
mode_rationale: Documentation completeness convention expressed in one sentence

rule: Ensure return types are consistent and intentional across related methods; if callers discard a return value, the method should return Unit to avoid confusion.
mode_rationale: Single rule about return type consistency

rule: Write test descriptions that describe expected behavior in plain language (e.g., 'should return empty list when no items match') rather than referencing method names (e.g., 'should testGetItems').
mode_rationale: Naming convention for test descriptions

rule: Wrapper or data classes should not extend RuntimeException unless they genuinely represent throwable errors.
mode_rationale: Single inheritance guideline for exception classes

rule: Use Try or Either to represent operations that may fail instead of throwing exceptions, so callers can pattern-match on success/failure.
mode_rationale: Convention preferring Try/Either over thrown exceptions

rule: When a method returns a generic type (e.g., String) that represents something domain-specific, add scaladoc or equivalent documentation explaining what the returned value actually is.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation convention for generic return types

rule: When migrating between SDK versions, verify that serialized representations (toString, JSON, etc.) remain compatible, especially if downstream consumers depend on the format.
mode_rationale: Single verification reminder during SDK migrations, not a full checklist

rule: Before removing or merging code that appears duplicated, verify with the original author whether the duplication was intentional to avoid silent behavior changes.
mode_rationale: Simple social convention — check before removing apparent duplication

rule: When changing how assets are bundled or built, verify the output works in the deployed environment, not just in local development.
mode_rationale: Single reminder to verify in deployed environment, not a multi-step procedure

rule: Integration tests should use real implementations (e.g., real persisters/databases), not mocks; if mocks are used, the test should be reclassified as a unit test.
mode_rationale: Single rule about mock vs real implementation in integration tests

rule: Capitalize domain terms (e.g., Dataset, ID) consistently in client-facing error messages, API route descriptions, and user-visible strings.
mode_rationale: Simple capitalization convention for domain terms

rule: Only add TODO comments for work that is planned within the current initiative or near-term roadmap; avoid speculative TODOs that may linger in the codebase indefinitely.
mode_rationale: Single guideline about when TODOs are appropriate

rule: Remove redundant words from field names when the context already conveys the meaning (e.g., use `pvcName` instead of `persistentVolumeClaimName` when the type is already a PVC).
mode_rationale: Naming convention to remove redundant words from field names

rule: Field names must accurately reflect what the value represents; do not name a field `pvId` if it holds a volume name rather than an identifier.
mode_rationale: Single naming accuracy rule

rule: Include a `deletedAt` timestamp field on entities that support soft deletion to maintain an audit trail of when records were removed.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to include deletedAt on soft-deletable entities

rule: Add telemetry events to CRUD methods so that create, read, update, and delete operations are observable in production.
mode_rationale: Single observability convention for CRUD methods

rule: Add explicit tests that verify authorization checks are enforced, ensuring unauthorized users cannot access protected resources.
mode_rationale: Single testing requirement for authorization checks

rule: Add a comment explaining why a value is intentionally discarded (e.g., `val _ = ...`) so it is not accidentally removed during future refactoring.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation convention for discarded values

rule: When retrieving an entity by ID, throw an error with a descriptive message that includes the ID, rather than silently returning a default or an opaque failure.
mode_rationale: Single error message convention including the entity ID

rule: When using setTimeout as a fallback, store the timeout ID and cancel it with clearTimeout if the primary operation completes first, to prevent stale side effects.
mode_rationale: Single cleanup convention for setTimeout fallbacks

rule: Assign the return value of `setTimeout()` (the timeout ID) to the variable, not the delay duration; mixing these up makes `clearTimeout` silently fail.
mode_rationale: Simple correctness rule about setTimeout assignment

rule: Define static constants (thresholds, config values, timeout durations) outside the React component body so they are not re-created on every render.
mode_rationale: Single rule about placing constants outside component body

rule: Replace magic numbers with named constants that convey intent (e.g., `const MAX_FILE_SIZE_MB = 64` instead of a bare `64`).
Hardcoded numeric constants must have a comment or named constant explaining how the value was chosen.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation rule for hardcoded numeric values

rule: Every TODO comment in code must reference a JIRA ticket so the work is tracked and not forgotten.
mode_rationale: Single convention requiring JIRA references on TODOs

rule: Debug print/println statements must be removed or replaced with proper logging before merging to the main branch.
Enum values in Scala should use camelCase to match the project's naming conventions, even though other languages may use UPPER_SNAKE_CASE.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention for Scala enum values

rule: When catching or re-throwing exceptions, always preserve the original exception as the cause so stack trace and root cause information are not lost.
mode_rationale: Single error handling convention about preserving cause chains

rule: When setting multiple form or state values, combine them into a single call rather than making redundant sequential calls.
mode_rationale: Simple performance convention for state updates

rule: When upgrading libraries that change data serialization or markup formats, verify that existing persisted data still renders or functions correctly.
mode_rationale: Single verification reminder, not a multi-step procedure

rule: When introducing a new UI element that could be reused across modules, create a shared component in packages/ui rather than defining it inline in a feature module.
mode_rationale: Simple organizational guideline about shared component placement

rule: Maintain a consistent ordering of imports in Scala files, typically grouping by package hierarchy (java/scala stdlib first, then third-party, then internal packages) with alphabetical ordering within groups.
mode_rationale: Single convention about import group ordering

rule: Do not re-declare dependencies that are already included transitively through other targets (e.g., if tests-lib already includes common-core and scalatest, don't list them again).
mode_rationale: Simple build file hygiene rule

rule: Move reusable cross-cutting concerns like filtering, sorting, and pagination into a commons module and extend them in downstream modules instead of duplicating the logic.
mode_rationale: Single architectural guideline about commons module extraction

rule: Do not use unexplained magic numbers in code; extract them into named constants or add a comment explaining the rationale so future readers understand the value's origin.
mode_rationale: Same as no-magic-numbers — single convention about named constants

rule: API route definitions should include $ref or enum constraints on parameters to enable automatic API validation rather than accepting unconstrained strings.
mode_rationale: Single API design convention about parameter constraints

rule: Use domain-specific typed enums (e.g., SortableColumn) instead of raw String types for API parameters to enable compile-time safety and API documentation validation.
mode_rationale: Single type-safety convention for API parameters

rule: Remove test data, fixtures, and helper entities that are no longer referenced by any test to keep the test suite lean and avoid confusion about what is actively tested.
mode_rationale: Simple test hygiene rule about removing unused fixtures

rule: Components should define sensible default values for props (e.g., orientation defaulting to 'horizontal') while allowing consumers to override via props.
mode_rationale: Single convention about providing default prop values

rule: Style values (colors, spacing, fonts) must come from the theme object, not be hardcoded in component styles.
mode_rationale: Simple styling convention to use theme tokens

rule: Write Storybook stories in Component Story Format 3 (CSF3), not MDX, which is no longer supported in Storybook 8.
mode_rationale: Single convention about Storybook story format

rule: When a section of JSX represents a distinct, self-contained UI block, extract it into its own component for clarity and testability.
mode_rationale: Simple component extraction guideline

rule: Do not use nested ternary expressions in JSX or logic; use early returns, if/else blocks, or lookup maps for readability.
Pass variant or state information as props to styled-components rather than toggling CSS class names.
mode_rationale: Single convention about how to pass state to styled-components

rule: Inline logic and render helpers should be extracted into pure functions or standalone components so they can be tested independently.
mode_rationale: Single guideline about code structure for testability

rule: When wrapping a library component (e.g., Ant Design), maximize use of its built-in API to keep wrapper code minimal rather than reimplementing features.
mode_rationale: Single convention about using library APIs over custom reimplementation

rule: Define Ant Design Menu items using the items array configuration (MenuItem[]) instead of the deprecated JSX children pattern (<Menu.Item>).
mode_rationale: Single convention about preferred Ant Design API pattern

rule: Components must reference the semantic color palette (e.g., colorPalette.textPrimary, colorPalette.bg) rather than specific color constants, to ensure correct behavior when switching themes.
mode_rationale: Single rule about referencing semantic colors instead of constants

rule: Always import colors from the canonical colors.ts file, not from alternative files like colorsNew.ts, to ensure theme switching works correctly.
mode_rationale: Single import convention for color definitions

rule: When wrapping Ant Design components, extend or pick/omit from the library's prop types instead of defining loose custom prop interfaces.
mode_rationale: Single convention about extending library types instead of custom interfaces

rule: Use the Ant Design Theme Provider to customize component appearance instead of manually overriding styles in styled-components.
mode_rationale: Single convention about using theme provider over manual style overrides

rule: Use the themeHelper utility for accessing design tokens (colors, spacing, fonts) instead of hardcoding values in styled-components.
Always define theme keys in both light and dark mode configurations, even when the values are identical, to ensure flexibility for future design changes.
mode_rationale: Single rule about always defining both light and dark theme keys

rule: Always reference colors from the theme palette rather than using hardcoded hex values or deprecated color tokens.
mode_rationale: Single convention about referencing theme palette over hardcoded values

rule: Import UI components from Domino's component library, not directly from Ant Design — Domino wrappers enforce consistent theming and behavior.
mode_rationale: Single import convention for UI components

rule: Only use useMemo for genuinely expensive computations; avoid wrapping simple derivations that don't benefit from memoization.
Use spacing and dimension tokens from the theme (via themeHelper or theme dimensions) instead of raw rem or px values in styled-components.
mode_rationale: Single convention about using theme tokens for spacing

rule: Prefer async/await with try/catch over .then()/.catch() promise chains to maintain a consistent async error-handling style across the codebase.
mode_rationale: Single style convention about async error handling

rule: Route-related code used across apps should live in the shared packages directory (e.g., packages/ui/src/core/routes), not in individual app modules.
mode_rationale: Single convention about where shared route code should live

rule: Prefer adding proper TypeScript generics (e.g., on reduce, map) to eliminate type assertions rather than using `as` casts.
mode_rationale: Single convention about preferring generics over type assertions

rule: Mock data should use realistic example values that match the shape and format of real data to make tests and development more representative.
mode_rationale: Single guideline about mock data quality

rule: Use themeHelper utilities for font-size, margin, and other styling values instead of hardcoding them in styled components.
mode_rationale: Single convention about using themeHelper for styling values

rule: Replace switch statements or chained conditionals that map values with a dictionary/map lookup for clarity and maintainability.
mode_rationale: Single convention about replacing conditionals with dictionary lookups

rule: Avoid inline styles in React components; use styled-components or CSS classes instead.
Use template literal string interpolation instead of string concatenation for readability.
mode_rationale: Single style convention about string formatting

rule: Do not commit secrets, tokens, or credentials (e.g., in .npmrc) to version control; use a secrets management solution instead.
mode_rationale: Single security rule about not committing secrets

rule: Avoid casting to `any` in TypeScript; find proper type definitions or workarounds to maintain type safety.
mode_rationale: Single convention about maintaining type safety

rule: Import from canonical module paths (e.g., ui/globalStore/useStore) rather than deprecated aliases that may be removed.
mode_rationale: Single convention about importing from canonical module paths

rule: Maintain consistent casing conventions across related identifiers (e.g., if the component is CustomSVG, related types and variables should follow the same casing pattern).
mode_rationale: Single convention about consistent casing across identifiers

rule: Use a dictionary or mapping for aria-label values so the same icon always gets the same label, enabling reliable QA/automation selectors.
mode_rationale: Single convention about using a dictionary for aria-labels

rule: API doc comments for parameters that accept a fixed set of values should enumerate those values explicitly.
mode_rationale: Single documentation convention for API parameter values

rule: When adding fields to a DTO or API response, verify that all downstream clients are tolerant of extra fields before merging.
mode_rationale: Single guideline about checking downstream client tolerance

rule: Never import from a package's dist/build output folder within the same package; use relative paths instead.
mode_rationale: Single import convention against dist folder imports

rule: Use color values from the design token palette (e.g. colors.palette.danger) instead of hardcoded hex or raw color values.
When making multiple API requests that don't depend on each other, use Promise.all to execute them in parallel.
mode_rationale: Single performance convention about using Promise.all

rule: Use the same established term for a concept at all layers of the application (DDD ubiquitous language); don't invent new names for things that already have official names.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention about consistent terminology

rule: Place configuration settings alongside other related settings rather than dumping them into a monolithic Settings class.
mode_rationale: Single convention about configuration organization

rule: Always use the structured logger for output; never leave raw println statements in production code.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to use structured logging over println

rule: When a file format is no longer supported (e.g. MDX), remove the file rather than updating it to the deprecated format.
mode_rationale: Single convention about cleaning up unsupported file formats

rule: Don't repeat parent scope names in nested configuration keys; if a key is already inside a 'workflows.storage' namespace, don't prefix child keys with 'flows' or 'storage' again.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention about avoiding prefix repetition in nested keys

rule: Use themeHelper utilities for colors and styling instead of raw values to ensure consistency with the design system.
Avoid inline styles in React components; use styled-components, CSS modules, or theme utilities instead.
mode_rationale: Single convention against inline styles in React components

rule: Before creating custom UI elements, check if an existing component (e.g. ActionDropdown.Button) already provides the needed functionality.
Don't render a modal instance per table row; lift modal state to the parent so only one modal is ever mounted regardless of row count.
mode_rationale: Single performance convention about lifting modal state to parent

rule: Use optional chaining with nullish coalescing (`arr[0]?.id ?? ''`) when accessing array elements, even when you expect the array to be non-empty.
mode_rationale: Single convention about safe array element access

rule: Use `reduce` or other functional transforms instead of `let` variables with mutation when building derived values.
mode_rationale: Single convention about functional transforms over mutation

rule: Do not import from your own package's published name (e.g., `@domino/ui`) within the same package; use relative imports instead.
Do not copy data from a custom hook (e.g., `useRemote`) into local state via `useEffect`; use the hook's returned data directly.
mode_rationale: Single convention about using hook data directly instead of copying to state

rule: Remove unused function parameters from the definition instead of suppressing the ESLint rule with a disable comment.
mode_rationale: Single convention about removing unused params instead of suppressing lint

rule: When a component needs multiple fields from a single object, pass the object as one prop rather than spreading its properties into individual props.
mode_rationale: Single convention about passing objects as props instead of spreading

rule: Place TypeScript type and interface definitions in a dedicated `types.ts` file, not in `utils.ts` or other non-type files.
mode_rationale: Single convention about where to place type definitions

rule: Do not use eslint-disable comments to work around type compatibility issues; fix the types so the code is naturally compatible.
mode_rationale: Single convention about fixing types instead of disabling lint rules

rule: Do not leave commented-out code without explanation; either remove it or add a TODO comment explaining why it is kept and when it should be restored.
mode_rationale: Single convention about handling commented-out code

rule: Frontend filters must not mutate the original API data; keep a single immutable data source and compute filtered views from it using pure functions.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence convention about not mutating API data

rule: When adding try/catch blocks, ensure the catch path provides visible feedback to the user (e.g., a toast or error message) rather than silently swallowing the error.
mode_rationale: Simple rule: catch blocks must show user-visible feedback

rule: Always use strict equality (`===`) instead of loose equality (`==`) for comparisons, unless there is an explicit reason to coerce types.
mode_rationale: Single coding convention: use === over ==

rule: Use the nullish coalescing operator (??) instead of logical OR (||) for default values to avoid incorrectly treating falsy values like 0 or empty string as missing.
mode_rationale: Single operator preference rule

rule: Define named constants (e.g., EMPTY_PLACEHOLDER) for repeated default or sentinel values instead of using inline literals across multiple locations.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for default values

rule: Use themeHelper or theme token values for colors and fonts instead of hardcoded hex values or magic numbers in styled components.
Pass select/dropdown options as a data prop (array of objects) rather than as JSX children to keep components declarative and data-driven.
mode_rationale: Simple component API convention

rule: Remove commented-out code before merging; use version control history to retrieve old code instead of leaving dead code in the codebase.
Style values like colors and spacing should reference the design system's theme tokens rather than raw numbers, even when preserving existing code.
mode_rationale: Single styling convention about theme tokens

rule: When introducing batched database queries, document why the chosen batch size is safe (e.g., won't exceed MongoDB's 16MB BSON document limit) and consider how it scales for large deployments.
mode_rationale: Single documentation requirement when introducing batch queries

rule: When an API schema field has implicit constraints (e.g., nesting depth limits), document those constraints explicitly in the field description everywhere the assumption applies.
mode_rationale: Single documentation convention for API fields

rule: API schema fields, especially those with complex or non-obvious formats, should include inline examples to clarify expected values.
mode_rationale: Single API documentation convention

rule: Do not leave unused imports, variables, or code in the codebase unless there is a concrete near-term need; remove them to reduce noise.
mode_rationale: Simple code hygiene rule

rule: When a field accepts multiple types or has a default value, add test cases that exercise non-string and non-obvious value types to ensure correct handling.
mode_rationale: Single testing guideline about value type coverage

rule: If a class enforces invariants or throws exceptions, its name should reflect that responsibility (e.g., Checker or Enforcer), not imply a passive role like Logger.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention

rule: Log messages should include the relevant identifiers (controller name, operation name) and indicate the lifecycle phase (started, completed) to support debugging and tracing.
mode_rationale: Single logging convention about including identifiers and phase

rule: When fields become optional, write tests for both extremes: all fields provided and no fields provided, not just partial combinations.
mode_rationale: Single testing guideline about boundary coverage

rule: Method descriptions and doc comments within a file or module should follow the same punctuation and formatting conventions for consistency.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation consistency rule

rule: When defining API endpoints or configuration fields, include example values (e.g., example hostnames, URLs) to make usage clear.
mode_rationale: Single documentation convention

rule: Prefix class and type names with their owning module to prevent confusion with similarly-named concepts from other modules during cross-module refactors.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for cross-module clarity

rule: Use theme helpers and design tokens for styling values (font sizes, colors, spacing) instead of hardcoding raw values like '14px'.
mode_rationale: Single styling convention about theme helpers

rule: Avoid using explicit `return` statements in Scala; rely on expression-based control flow instead, as `return` has surprising semantics in Scala.
mode_rationale: Single Scala coding convention

rule: Split React components into separate files rather than defining multiple components (especially controlled and uncontrolled variants) in a single file.
Define default or constant callback functions outside the React component body and pass them as references, rather than defining inline functions in component props.
mode_rationale: Single React performance convention

rule: Use `unknown` instead of `any` in TypeScript when the typed values are not actually accessed, to maintain type safety.
mode_rationale: Single TypeScript type safety convention

rule: Extract repeated test setup (e.g., creating test fixtures like unauthorized principals) into reusable helper methods to reduce duplication in test files.
trigger: When the same test setup pattern (e.g., creating an unauthorized principal, building a specific fixture) appears in two or more test methods in the same file
rationale: Without extraction, duplicated setup logic means a single change to the object model requires updating every test that inlines the construction, increasing the chance of inconsistent or broken tests.
mode_rationale: Simple testing convention about reducing duplication
steps: Step 1: Identify the repeated setup pattern across test methods in the file
Step 2: Extract it into a private helper method with a descriptive name (e.g., createUnauthorizedPrincipal())
Step 3: Parameterize only the fields that vary between callers; hardcode sensible defaults for the rest
Step 4: Replace all inline occurrences with calls to the new helper
Step 5: If the helper is useful across test files, move it to a shared test utilities trait or object

rule: When caller context (e.g., a Principal) is already available, pass it through the call chain rather than performing a redundant lookup downstream.
mode_rationale: Single performance convention about passing context through

rule: Prefer `find -exec rm -rf {} +` over piping to `xargs` for batch file deletion; it is simpler, faster, and avoids pitfalls like swallowing output with >/dev/null or forgetting -mindepth.
mode_rationale: Single shell command preference

rule: Order import statements with absolute (package) imports before relative imports.
Before writing inline path construction or URL building logic, check for existing utility or generator functions in shared packages and reuse them (exporting if necessary).
When constructing URLs from dynamic values, validate that all parameters are defined before interpolation to avoid generating URLs with literal 'undefined' segments that lead to 404 errors.
mode_rationale: Single validation rule for URL construction

rule: Method names should accurately describe the operation they perform; a method that updates a resource must not be named 'create' and vice versa.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention

rule: When implementing soft-delete for entities that store credentials in an external vault, explicitly consider whether retaining those credentials aligns with user expectations around deletion, and document the decision.
mode_rationale: Single security consideration to document during soft-delete

rule: When suppressing compiler warnings, target the specific warning being suppressed rather than using broad suppression annotations that may hide future issues.
mode_rationale: Simple convention about targeted warning suppression

rule: Interactions with external services like Vault should be extracted into a dedicated class rather than mixed into the main service implementation, improving testability and separation of concerns.
mode_rationale: Single architectural convention about separation of concerns

rule: Mock data and test fixtures should be typed using the types generated from the API schema, not left untyped or manually defined, to ensure mocks stay in sync with the actual API contract.
mode_rationale: Single testing convention about type consistency

rule: Remove all debug and testing artifacts (hardcoded test IDs, console.logs, temporary strings) from code before submitting a PR for review.

When a value can be computed from existing props or state, calculate it directly (or use useMemo) rather than syncing it through a useEffect into a separate useState, which adds unnecessary complexity and re-renders.
mode_rationale: Single React performance convention

rule: When a form renders dynamic input types from a schema, extract each input type into its own component early to keep the rendering logic manageable as the number of input types and their validations grow.
mode_rationale: Simple component organization convention

rule: Reference color values through the theme helper rather than importing color constants directly, ensuring consistent theming and easier future theme changes.
mode_rationale: Single styling convention about theme indirection

rule: When changing an infrastructure reference (e.g., username, path, host), audit and update all related configuration points in the same PR.
mode_rationale: Single convention: update all config points in one PR

rule: Use standardized code comments to mark lines that must be updated when cutting a release branch, so they are easy to find and not missed.
mode_rationale: Single convention about marking release-sensitive lines

rule: When manually converting values one by one (e.g., enum members to constants), create a utility function to automate the conversion instead of doing it individually.
mode_rationale: Simple convention preferring utility functions over manual repetition

rule: Use explicit names like `pageNumber` instead of ambiguous short names for pagination parameters to improve readability.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention

rule: Use get-or-create patterns instead of separate get-then-create flows when a missing value should never cause a failure.
mode_rationale: Single error-handling pattern preference

rule: Lifecycle methods must come in symmetric pairs (pause/resume, start/stop); do not add one side without the other.
mode_rationale: Single API design convention about symmetric pairs

rule: Error messages for state-management code should distinguish between possible invalid states rather than using generic messages.
mode_rationale: Single error-handling convention

rule: Do not keep code for hypothetical use cases that are not needed in the current PR; remove unused abstractions to reduce complexity.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: no code for hypothetical use cases

rule: Features that only need to run in a specific pod (e.g., dispatcher) should be gated to that pod to avoid unnecessary resource consumption, k8s API load, and risk of unintended side effects when multiplied across all instances.
mode_rationale: Single deployment convention about pod-scoping

rule: When watching Kubernetes resources, use field selectors to narrow the watch to only the specific resources needed rather than watching all resources in a namespace.
mode_rationale: Single Kubernetes performance convention

rule: Any state that can be accessed by multiple threads must have explicit synchronization; use a dedicated lock object and protect both read and write access.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence concurrency convention about locking shared state

rule: When protecting shared mutable state with locks, synchronize on read as well as write; prefer a dedicated lock object over synchronizing on `this` or the class.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence locking best practice covering read/write synchronization

rule: When creating entities that span multiple systems, create the canonical record in the primary datastore first, then use its ID to propagate to external systems like identity providers.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence ordering convention for cross-system entity creation

rule: Place important identifiers like IDs as the first (or near-first) parameters in method signatures so they are immediately visible.
mode_rationale: Simple parameter ordering convention

rule: Prefix class names with their domain scope (e.g., Idp, Billing) to clearly communicate responsibility and prevent ambiguity.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for class prefixes

rule: When parsing strings into structured types (e.g., UUIDs, enums), validate the format at the point of conversion and handle failures explicitly rather than letting them propagate as runtime exceptions.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence validation convention at parse boundaries

rule: Remove unused code rather than keeping it around speculatively; if it is not in use and not documented, it should be deleted.
mode_rationale: Simple code hygiene rule about deleting dead code

rule: Externalize polling intervals and timeout values as central configuration flags rather than hardcoding them, so they can be tuned without code changes.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence configuration convention for tunable values

rule: When adding polling or repeated cross-service calls, evaluate whether requests can queue up and block other requests; add timeouts to mitigate cascading failures.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence resilience convention about timeouts for polling

rule: Verify that every endpoint has appropriate authentication and authorization checks, especially when copying patterns from existing code that may have different access requirements.
mode_rationale: Simple security convention to always verify auth on endpoints

rule: When suppressing deprecation warnings in build files, document which specific deprecations are being silenced and why they cannot be addressed in the current scope.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence documentation convention for suppressed warnings

rule: Keep imports and dependency declarations in a consistent, conventional order within files.
mode_rationale: Simple style rule about import order

rule: Use white-label / configurable product names instead of hardcoded brand names in UI components.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention to avoid hardcoded brand names

rule: In Vite apps, use `import.meta.env.MODE` to check the application mode instead of `process.env.NODE_ENV`, and use strict equality (===) for safety.
trigger: When checking the application environment (development vs. production) in a Vite-based frontend application
rationale: Without using import.meta.env.MODE, the code relies on process.env.NODE_ENV which is not natively available in Vite's ESM environment and may be undefined or incorrectly polyfilled, causing environment checks to silently fail.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence convention for Vite environment checking
steps: Step 1: Replace any process.env.NODE_ENV references with import.meta.env.MODE
Step 2: Use strict equality (===) for comparison, not loose equality (==)
Step 3: Compare against 'development' or 'production' string literals (Vite's default modes)
Step 4: For custom modes, ensure they are defined in the Vite config and matched exactly

rule: Use ES module dynamic `import()` instead of CommonJS `require()` for conditional or lazy-loaded modules.
mode_rationale: Simple module system preference for ES imports

rule: Before adopting a package, verify it supports your project's framework version (e.g., React 18) by checking the package documentation.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence convention to check compatibility before adopting packages

rule: Do not add new ESLint rules as 'error' severity without a migration plan — it forces developers to use --no-verify or add excessive exceptions.
mode_rationale: Simple convention about ESLint rule severity levels

rule: When wrapping a component, only declare prop types you actually forward; use Pick to select forwarded props, or use the rest operator to pass through remaining props.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence typing convention for component prop forwarding

rule: Proactively extract sub-components (e.g., tab contents) into their own files before they grow too large, even if they are small initially.
mode_rationale: Simple code organization convention about component file extraction

rule: Use NavTabs for full-page top-level navigation and Segment/toggle for in-context view switching within a section.
mode_rationale: Simple convention for choosing NavTabs vs Segment components

rule: Do not declare the same utility or constant in multiple files; consolidate into a single shared location.
mode_rationale: Simple code hygiene rule about shared utilities

rule: Define styled-components in a dedicated `styled.ts` file rather than co-locating them in the component file.
Use Ant Design's `Flex` component for flex layouts instead of creating custom styled divs with flexbox properties.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to use Ant Design Flex instead of custom styled divs

rule: When a feature is gated behind a feature flag, ensure the flag is checked consistently across all views that render or reference the feature.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence convention about consistent feature flag gating

rule: Use self-closing JSX syntax (`<Component />`) for components with no children instead of explicit open/close tags.
mode_rationale: Simple JSX syntax style rule

rule: TypeScript files should use camelCase naming (e.g., getTheme.ts) rather than kebab-case (get-theme.ts) to match the exported function or class name.
mode_rationale: Simple file naming convention for TypeScript

rule: Do not repurpose semantic color tokens (e.g., 'color-link') to replace unrelated colors; use the correct semantic token (e.g., 'primary') that matches the element's purpose.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence convention about correct semantic token usage

rule: When a React component contains complex logic (e.g., theme setup, side effects), extract it into a separate custom hook to improve readability and reusability.
mode_rationale: Simple code organization convention for extracting hooks

rule: Remove commented-out code before merging; use version control history to recover old code rather than leaving dead comments in the codebase.
Do not include files that are not imported or referenced anywhere in the codebase; verify that new or modified files are actually used before submitting a PR.
mode_rationale: Simple code hygiene rule about unused files

rule: Avoid hardcoding version numbers in file names when the content is not version-specific; use generic names so the file doesn't need renaming with each product version.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention to avoid version numbers in filenames

rule: Instead of casting to `any`, use TypeScript utility types like `ReturnType<typeof fn>` to derive proper types from existing functions and maintain type safety.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence TypeScript typing convention

rule: Package.json dependencies must never point to a feature branch when merging to develop or main; always use published package versions or the default branch.
mode_rationale: Simple dependency management convention

rule: API spec fields (especially those with non-obvious formats like string-encoded arrays) must include concrete examples to clarify expected values and usage.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence API documentation convention

rule: When a value cannot be determined at runtime, default to the safer outcome (e.g., assume failure rather than success) to avoid silently masking errors.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence error handling convention for unknown states

rule: When a function accepts multiple arguments, pass them as a named object so callers don't need to know the parameter order.
mode_rationale: API design convention for function signatures

rule: Use React Router hooks instead of the legacy withRouter HOC; higher-order components are no longer necessary with modern React Router.
mode_rationale: Simple modernization convention preferring hooks over HOCs

rule: Use a single Ant Design form instance per logical form scope; multiple instances fragment state management and bypass validation enforcement.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence Ant Design form convention

rule: Before creating a new custom hook, check whether an existing hook already covers the use case to avoid duplication and inconsistent behavior.
Before defining new enums or type constants, check the codebase for existing definitions that cover the same domain to prevent duplication and drift.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to check for existing type definitions

rule: Do not wrap JSX in a React Fragment when the component returns a single element or when no wrapper is needed.
Name boolean-returning methods to clearly express the predicate they check, including the condition and subject (e.g., areMultipleStoragesRegistered instead of ambiguous alternatives).
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for boolean methods

rule: When only the count or existence of records is needed, use a count query at the persistence layer instead of fetching and counting full objects.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence performance convention for count-only queries

rule: API endpoint summaries and descriptions must accurately reflect what the endpoint does; update them when the implementation changes.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation convention for API endpoint summaries

rule: Extract hardcoded default values and initial state into named constants or a shared initial-state definition to improve readability and reuse.
mode_rationale: Simple code organization convention for named constants

rule: All form fields must have explicit name attributes to ensure proper form state tracking, validation, and submission.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence form convention for name attributes

rule: API schemas should define a type in exactly one place; having the same concept defined at multiple levels (e.g., top-level and inside a validationSchema) creates ambiguity.
mode_rationale: Simple API schema convention about single-source type definitions

rule: Always consume colors from the theme rather than importing them directly from a color constants file, so that palette changes (e.g., theme updates) propagate automatically.
Use a <button> element for actions and a <a> element for navigation; do not style a link to behave as a button or vice versa.
Feature-specific specializations of a shared component should live in their own file, not inside the base component's file.
mode_rationale: Simple file organization convention for component specializations

rule: Do not leave commented-out code in pull requests; remove dead code and rely on version control history if it is needed later.
mode_rationale: Simple code hygiene rule: don't leave dead code in PRs

rule: When a JSX conditional involves multiple chained expressions, extract it into a descriptively named boolean variable defined before the return statement.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence readability convention for JSX conditionals

rule: When a function's behavior is extended (e.g., adding a filter parameter), update its doc comment to describe the new functionality.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation convention: update docs when behavior changes

rule: API route descriptions and parameter docs should use consistent casing, spacing, and terminology (e.g., 'data plane' as two words, 'ID' fully capitalized) matching the variable names they describe.
mode_rationale: Naming/style convention for API docs consistency

rule: When adding new filtering or query capabilities to an endpoint, include integration or unit tests that verify the filtering behavior.
mode_rationale: Testing convention: new query capabilities need tests

rule: When a component is used across multiple sections or modules, move it to a shared component library (e.g., domino/ui) to avoid cross-section imports.
mode_rationale: Architectural convention about component placement

rule: Use the 'value' prop to make a form component fully controlled by React state instead of using 'defaultValue' with additional synchronization logic.
mode_rationale: React best practice: prefer controlled components

rule: Components consumed by multiple modules should be placed in packages/ui rather than owned by a single module, so changes driven by one module's requirements don't break others.
mode_rationale: Architectural convention about shared component ownership

rule: When the same code construction is repeated across a file or module, extract it into a shared utility function.
mode_rationale: DRY principle: extract repeated code into utils

rule: Avoid nested ternary expressions; use early returns, if/else blocks, or helper functions for multi-branch conditionals to preserve readability.
Group related logic (e.g., all variables for a single feature/flag) into one block rather than scattering it across the function, to improve readability.
mode_rationale: Code organization convention: group related logic together

rule: Do not memoize (useMemo/useCallback) cheap operations; place lightweight computations directly in JSX or as simple variables instead.
mode_rationale: Performance convention: don't memoize cheap operations

rule: When adding log-and-rethrow patterns, investigate whether something higher in the call stack is swallowing exceptions; fix the root cause rather than papering over it with extra logging.
mode_rationale: Debugging principle: fix root cause rather than adding logging

rule: In Scala pattern matches and destructuring, replace named bindings that are never used with underscore (_) to signal intent and avoid dead-code warnings.
mode_rationale: Scala naming convention for unused bindings

rule: Avoid nesting CSS class selectors inside styled components; apply styles directly to a new or existing styled component instead.
mode_rationale: CSS-in-JS convention: avoid nested class selectors

rule: Use styled components instead of inline styles for all styling in React components.
Non-trivial filter/transform functions used inside components should be extracted into separate utility modules for reuse and testability.
mode_rationale: Code organization convention: extract filter logic for reuse

rule: When declaring @font-face rules, the font-weight value must reflect the actual weight of each font file so the browser selects the correct face.
mode_rationale: CSS correctness rule for font-face declarations

rule: When version identifiers are used across multiple files, extract them into a single constants file rather than duplicating string literals.
mode_rationale: DRY convention: single source for version identifiers

rule: Name functions to reflect how their return value is used at call sites; if callers assign the result to `currentVersion`, the function should be named `getCurrentThemeVersion`, not something ambiguous.
mode_rationale: Naming convention: function names should reflect call-site usage

rule: When creating theme or style variants tied to a specific release, include the release version in the component name (e.g., `AppPanelThemeRelease5`) so the version context is immediately clear.
mode_rationale: Naming convention: include version in variant component names

rule: In frontend files, always place absolute/package imports before relative imports to maintain a consistent and scannable import order.
When a JSX block or expression grows complex, extract it into a descriptively named variable (e.g., `mainContent`) to improve readability and make the render tree scannable.
mode_rationale: Readability convention: name complex JSX blocks

rule: Avoid proliferating near-identical DTO classes (e.g., StorageDto, StorageInfoDto, StorageViewDto); prefer adding optional fields to a single DTO over creating multiple overlapping types.
mode_rationale: Architecture convention: avoid DTO proliferation

rule: When an endpoint returns data filtered by user permissions, explicitly verify that unauthenticated or non-user callers cannot access restricted data, and raise security concerns in review if unclear.
mode_rationale: Security convention: verify access control on filtered endpoints

rule: When naming a Map or dictionary variable, encode the key type in the name (e.g., `dataPlaneHwTierMap` when the key is a data plane ID) so readers immediately understand the lookup semantics.
mode_rationale: Naming convention: encode key type in Map variable names

rule: When a field contains items filtered by the requester's permissions, name it to convey that scope (e.g., `accessibleHardwareTiers` instead of `hardwareTiers`) so consumers understand the data is user-scoped.
mode_rationale: Naming convention: field names should convey permission scope

rule: Use colors from the design system theme rather than hardcoding hex values; when the color palette is available in the theme, components should reference theme tokens.
mode_rationale: Theming convention: use tokens, not hardcoded hex values

rule: When a component accumulates multiple derived calculations, extract them into a utility function or module to keep the component focused on rendering.
mode_rationale: Code organization: keep components focused on rendering

rule: Instead of using `let` with value overwriting, extract the logic into a function that returns the computed value to avoid mutation and improve clarity.
mode_rationale: Code style convention: prefer computed values over mutation

rule: Event streaming or cursor-based loops must include retry logic so that transient failures (e.g. cursor closure, node failover) are automatically recovered without dropping events.
mode_rationale: Resilience convention: streaming loops must include retry logic

rule: When consuming a stream that triggers a write per event, consider batching writes (e.g. after N events or within a time window) to reduce write churn.
mode_rationale: Performance convention: batch writes from high-frequency streams

rule: Name shared resources (collections, queues, tables) with domain-specific prefixes to avoid ambiguity when multiple subsystems use similar concepts.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for shared resource disambiguation

rule: Add a comment explaining why a retry or reconnection loop exists, including the failure scenarios it handles (e.g. cursor closed, node failover).
mode_rationale: Documentation convention: explain why retry loops exist

rule: Use the project's theme helper or design token system for spacing, padding, and margin values instead of raw pixel literals.
mode_rationale: Theming convention: use design tokens for spacing

rule: Files named 'util' should contain pure utility functions, not React components. Move components to their own files with component-appropriate names.
mode_rationale: File organization convention: utils vs components

rule: Route paths like '/admin' should be defined as constants or derived from configuration, not hardcoded inline in components.
mode_rationale: Architecture convention: route paths as constants

rule: All values used inside a useMemo callback must appear in its dependency array. Instead of reading window.location directly, use values from useLocation() so React can track changes and trigger re-renders correctly.
mode_rationale: React correctness rule for useMemo dependency arrays

rule: React hooks must only be called from React function components or custom hooks, never from plain utility functions. Calling hooks from non-hook functions violates the Rules of Hooks and can crash the app.
mode_rationale: React Rules of Hooks: hooks only in components/custom hooks

rule: Hooks must be called at the top level of a component, not inside arrays, objects, or conditional structures. Assign hook return values to variables first, then reference them in data structures.
mode_rationale: React Rules of Hooks: call at top level only
