rule: If a file exports a React component, name it as a component (PascalCase, descriptive of what it renders), not as a utility or render function.
mode_rationale: File naming convention: PascalCase for component files

rule: In theme configuration functions, use the colors parameter passed to the function rather than importing the colors module directly, to ensure theming remains configurable.
mode_rationale: Theming convention: use passed parameters, not direct imports

rule: Component-specific styles should live with the component (CSS modules, styled-components, etc.), not in global CSS files.
mode_rationale: Styling convention: component styles live with the component

rule: When an error occurs in navigation or critical UI paths, provide a meaningful recovery experience (e.g. redirect to an error page) rather than just rendering an inline error message.
mode_rationale: UX convention: provide recovery paths on errors

rule: Keep domain logic (validation, business rules, entity construction) in the domain layer, not in infrastructure or service implementation layers.
mode_rationale: Layered architecture convention: domain logic stays in domain layer

rule: Always use curly braces in Scala control structures and expressions, following the Databricks Scala style guide.
mode_rationale: Scala style convention per Databricks guide

rule: Choose the exception type that most accurately describes the underlying error (e.g., RecordNotFoundException for missing records rather than a generic exception).
mode_rationale: Error handling convention: match exception type to error

rule: When obfuscating or redacting sensitive values, use existing platform redaction mechanisms (e.g., the redacted method on telemetry events) rather than rolling custom obfuscation logic.
mode_rationale: Security convention: use platform redaction, not custom logic

rule: When deriving values from optional fields, ensure consistent behavior when the field is unset to avoid mismatched or unexpected output.
mode_rationale: Defensive coding convention for optional field handling

rule: Place domain entities in packages that reflect their data source — entities retrieved from an upstream API belong in the upstream package, not in the local entity package.
mode_rationale: Package organization convention: entities reflect their data source

rule: Be consistent within a file about whether named parameters are used in function calls; don't mix named and positional styles for the same parameter across call sites.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence style rule about parameter consistency

rule: When updating frontend component styles or icons, verify the change renders correctly in both the current and legacy themes.
mode_rationale: Single verification step, not a multi-step procedure

rule: When a class or model exists in one module, do not recreate an identical version in another module; extract it to a shared location or reuse the existing one.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence convention about avoiding duplication

rule: Remote calls that retrieve configuration or schema data should be cached, even if call frequency seems low today, to guard against future usage growth.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence performance guideline about caching

rule: Before changing a component that has already been migrated to a new pattern, verify its migration status and consult the migration owner rather than rolling it back to the legacy approach.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence rule about respecting migration status

rule: Keep CSS class selectors flat; avoid nesting classes when possible to maintain readability and reduce specificity issues.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence CSS style convention

rule: Pass color and fontSize directly to IconResolver via its props rather than creating Styled component wrappers around icons.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence convention about icon component usage

rule: In frontend files, always place absolute (library/package) imports before relative imports and apply this consistently across all changed files.
When setting icon or component colors, verify they match the intended design token in the current theme rather than assuming a color name maps to the expected visual.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence verification rule about theme tokens

rule: Use the project's spacing utilities (e.g., getMargins, themeHelper) for margins and padding rather than hardcoding pixel or em values inline.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence convention about spacing utilities

rule: Express sizes in px rather than em to maintain consistency with the codebase's established convention; do not introduce em without a specific justification.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence unit convention

rule: A styled component's name must describe what it actually renders; do not name a component 'Span' if it renders an icon or other non-span element.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence naming convention

rule: Reference palette tokens (e.g., palette.borderLight) instead of hardcoding hex or RGB color values in styled components.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence convention about color tokens

rule: Do not re-cast a value that has already been cast earlier in the same scope; use the previously cast variable instead.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence code cleanliness rule

rule: When an API requires a context object, use a proper default (e.g., DominoContext.uncorrelated) instead of passing null, even if the context is not currently used.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence rule about proper defaults

rule: Audit trails should only capture user-initiated actions, not system-driven state changes like automated job status transitions.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence architectural guideline about audit scope

rule: Do not introduce additional database queries solely for audit or telemetry purposes; capture audit data from information already available in the call path.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence performance rule about audit data sourcing

rule: Parameter names should be aligned with their corresponding type names for readability and consistency.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence naming convention

rule: When adding new injection modules, follow the same DI pattern (e.g., @provides with named annotations) used by existing similar modules for consistency.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence consistency rule about DI patterns

rule: Import classes directly rather than using intermediate package-qualified access (e.g., `import events.AuditTrailV2Transformer` should be `import AuditTrailV2Transformer`).
mode_rationale: Single-sentence import style convention

rule: When moving a class to a different module, move its corresponding test spec to the same module to keep tests co-located with the code they verify.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence convention about test placement

rule: When implementing rollback or cleanup stacks, write tests for execution order and for behavior when individual rollback callbacks throw errors.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence testing guideline about what to cover

rule: Expected operational behavior like rollbacks should be logged at info level, not warn; reserve warn/error for genuinely unexpected conditions.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence logging convention

rule: Do not pass another class's logger instance into a utility class; each class should use its own logger, and correlation should be handled via DominoContext.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence logging convention about logger ownership

rule: Critical operational failures (like failed rollbacks) should trigger active admin notifications via supportAlerter.error(), not rely solely on log output which will likely go unnoticed.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence rule about critical failure notification

rule: When adding support for a new provider or integration, ensure all related feature gates and requirement checks include the new provider to maintain consistent behavior.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence consistency rule about feature gates

rule: Replace useState-driven derived values with useMemo to avoid unnecessary re-renders and simplify component logic; use early-return if-statements over complex ternary chains.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence React performance convention

rule: When multiple components or functions share the same data shape, define a shared interface or type alias rather than repeating the structure.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence DRY convention about type definitions

rule: Move complex inline expressions out of JSX and into named variables or functions defined before the return statement for readability.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence readability convention for JSX

rule: Wrap inline event handler functions passed as props with useCallback to prevent unnecessary re-renders of child components.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence React performance convention

rule: Modal wrapper components should extend the base modal's props interface rather than redeclaring common props like open and onClose.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence convention about props interface inheritance

rule: Use the simplest accurate name (e.g. datasetIds) and only add qualifiers like toInclude/toExclude when a complementary parameter is actually introduced.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence naming simplicity rule

rule: When a function's return value is not immediately clear from the signature, add a comment above it describing what it returns.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence documentation convention

rule: New routes and pages must include feature flag validation so they can be toggled without code changes.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence architectural rule about feature gating

rule: Reusable UI components like cards should live in packages/ui, not in app-specific module directories.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence code organization convention

rule: Do not add extra wrapper div elements when the existing component structure or a Fragment would suffice.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence DOM structure convention

rule: Use generic terms like 'A user' instead of 'A Domino user' in user-facing copy to support white-labeling.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence white-labeling convention

rule: When the same logic is repeated across multiple components, extract it into a shared custom hook.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence DRY convention about custom hooks

rule: When a value does not need to trigger a re-render, use useRef instead of useState to avoid unnecessary render cycles.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence React performance convention

rule: When using spacing utilities like getMargin, verify the multiplier base value (e.g., BASE = 4px) so the computed result matches the intended pixel value.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence awareness rule about utility base values

rule: When a reviewer identifies a misuse of a utility function, systematically review all call sites for the same mistake rather than fixing only the commented instance.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence thoroughness rule about fixing all call sites

rule: Use the same CSS unit system (px vs rem) consistently throughout the application; do not introduce a different unit convention in isolated components.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence CSS consistency convention

rule: When computing layout values based on viewport size, use a ResizeObserver instead of a one-time measurement so the layout updates when the viewport is resized.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence technique rule about responsive measurement

rule: New DynamicField types must check whether the field is editable and only render controls when editable; return null otherwise.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence convention about editable state checks

rule: Inline magic values (numbers, strings) used in component logic should be extracted into named constants for clarity and maintainability.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence naming/readability convention

rule: DynamicField component types should remain simple, reusable form controls; complex domain-specific components should be extracted into their own module rather than embedded as a field type.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence architectural boundary rule

rule: Do not leave commented-out code in the codebase; remove dead code and rely on version control to recover it if needed.
When adding conditional logic for specific providers or enum values, ensure all variants are handled — including edge cases like 'unknown' or unsupported values.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence exhaustiveness rule about enum handling

rule: Name non-function constants (arrays, config objects, column definitions) using UPPER_SNAKE_CASE to distinguish them from functions and variables.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence naming convention

rule: Do not use explicit `return` statements in Scala; instead, restructure control flow (e.g., invert the if condition) so the last expression is the return value.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence Scala idiom convention

rule: When declaring types for fields or parameters, match the type used in the upstream definition (e.g., use BigDecimal if the source case class defines BigDecimal, not Int) to avoid silent precision loss.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence convention about matching types to upstream definitions

rule: Define pure utility functions (sorting, filtering, formatting) outside the component body to avoid unnecessary re-creation on each render and improve testability.
mode_rationale: Simple placement rule for pure functions

rule: Move reusable helper and utility functions out of component files into dedicated utils files to improve discoverability and reuse.
mode_rationale: Single convention about file organization for helpers

rule: Use the project's design system spacing helpers (e.g., getMargin, getSpacing) instead of raw numeric values to maintain consistent spacing across the UI.
mode_rationale: Simple rule to use spacing helpers instead of raw values

rule: Place constants in companion objects rather than inline in class bodies, and reference existing constants from other modules instead of redefining them.
mode_rationale: Straightforward convention about constant placement

rule: When modeling JSON Schema enums, support JsValue (not just String) to accommodate numeric and other non-string enum values from upstream schemas.
mode_rationale: Single typing convention for JSON Schema enums

rule: Always wrap API requests in error handling (try/catch or .catch) to prevent unhandled promise rejections from crashing the component.
mode_rationale: Simple rule to always wrap API calls in error handling

rule: Use optional chaining (?.) with nullish coalescing (??) instead of verbose conditional expressions for accessing potentially null/undefined properties.
mode_rationale: Style preference for optional chaining syntax

rule: Use type-safe enum mapping builders (e.g., StrictEnumBiMapBuilder) to validate bijection between related enums at class-load time, ensuring new enum values are never silently missed.
mode_rationale: Convention to use type-safe enum mapping builders

rule: Do not render empty or meaningless DOM elements (e.g., empty spans) when the underlying data is absent; conditionally render only when there is content to display.
mode_rationale: Simple rule about conditional rendering

rule: Do not create type definitions or intermediate variables for values that are hardcoded inline — pass them directly as object literals.
mode_rationale: Style convention to avoid redundant type definitions

rule: Access colors through themeHelper (e.g., themeHelper('colors.white')(props)) instead of importing and referencing the colors variable directly.
mode_rationale: Simple rule to use themeHelper for color access

rule: Always use colors from the theme system rather than hardcoded color values in styled components.
mode_rationale: Straightforward convention to use theme colors

rule: Use object destructuring when accessing multiple properties from props or other objects for cleaner, more readable code.
mode_rationale: Simple style preference for object destructuring

rule: Use theme spacing utilities like getMargin and getPadding instead of hardcoded margin/padding values in styled components.
mode_rationale: Convention to use theme spacing utilities

rule: Use Ant Design's built-in form capabilities (Form, Form.List, Form.Item shouldUpdate, onFinish handler) instead of building custom form state management with nested conditionals.
mode_rationale: Convention to prefer Ant Design built-in form capabilities

rule: Use Ant Design Form's built-in submit handler and form change events rather than manually wiring up submit and change logic.
mode_rationale: Simple rule to use Ant Design's built-in form handlers

rule: Do not check the same condition twice in contiguous lines — consolidate duplicate conditional checks into a single block.
mode_rationale: Simple code style rule about consolidating conditions

rule: Large render blocks and complex JSX fragments within utility files should be extracted into their own separate components.
mode_rationale: Convention about extracting large render blocks into components

rule: Use native JavaScript methods (e.g., Array.find, Array.includes) instead of utility library equivalents (Ramda, lodash) when the native API provides the same functionality.
mode_rationale: Simple preference for native JS over lodash/Ramda

rule: Use JSX boolean prop shorthand — write <Component accordion /> instead of <Component accordion={true} />.
When a function or route helper has many positional parameters, convert to an options object so callers are not sensitive to argument order.
mode_rationale: API design convention for function signatures

rule: Sort JSX component props alphabetically for consistency and easier scanning during code review.
mode_rationale: Simple alphabetical ordering convention

rule: Keep JSX component props sorted alphabetically for consistency and readability.
mode_rationale: Duplicate of sort-jsx-props, same alphabetical ordering convention

rule: Remove commented-out code before merging; use TODOs or tickets to track future work instead of leaving dead code.
mode_rationale: Simple hygiene rule about removing dead code

rule: Do not add explicit null/undefined checks when the falsy value already produces the correct result (e.g., `undefined !== 'blank'` is already false).
mode_rationale: Code simplification convention

rule: Use an explicit `if` guard clause instead of the TypeScript non-null assertion operator (!) to handle potentially null values.
mode_rationale: Simple convention preferring if guards over ! operator

rule: Wrap expensive computations in lambdas or by-name parameters so they are only evaluated when actually needed.
mode_rationale: Performance convention about deferred evaluation

rule: When adding cross-cutting policies or behaviors, use a no-op/null implementation for modules that should not be affected to prevent unintended side effects.
mode_rationale: Design pattern convention for cross-cutting concerns

rule: When a resource's cleanup requires more than `close()` (e.g., `dispose()`), ensure it is explicitly called since `Using` blocks only invoke `close()`/`release()`.
mode_rationale: Resource cleanup convention

rule: Add a code comment explaining the reasoning behind non-obvious default values and magic numbers (e.g., why a multiplier is 1.2).
mode_rationale: Simple documentation convention for non-obvious values

rule: Before writing custom utility code, check if an existing library already in the project can handle the same task — it will be documented, tested, and maintained externally.
mode_rationale: Convention to check for existing libraries before writing custom code

rule: When pattern matching on enum-like types (e.g., file change types), explicitly consider all variants including less obvious ones like DELETE to avoid silent bugs.
mode_rationale: Simple rule about exhaustive pattern matching

rule: Before implementing new logic, check if an existing method already provides the needed functionality to avoid duplication.
mode_rationale: Convention to check for existing methods before writing new ones

rule: Move validation checks out of API service classes into a dedicated validator domain service (e.g., `ModelDeploymentValidator`).
mode_rationale: Architectural convention about validator placement

rule: Methods that don't depend on instance state should be made static by moving them to the companion object.
mode_rationale: Simple convention about static method placement

rule: Regex patterns and other compile-once values should be defined as static fields on the companion object so they are only compiled once.
mode_rationale: Performance convention for compile-once values

rule: Avoid accessing tuple fields by positional index (`_1`, `_2`); use a named case class instead for readability and type safety.
mode_rationale: Readability convention preferring named fields over tuple indices

rule: When the same logic appears multiple times in a method, extract it into a separate function to reduce duplication.
mode_rationale: DRY convention about extracting duplicated logic

rule: Each React custom hook should live in its own file rather than being grouped in a shared hooks utility file.
mode_rationale: Simple file organization convention for React hooks

rule: Verify that callback functions passed as props or used as effect dependencies are memoized (e.g., with useCallback) to prevent unnecessary re-renders.
mode_rationale: Performance convention about memoizing callbacks

rule: Replace switch statements or chained conditionals that map values (e.g., status to icon) with an object/dictionary lookup.
mode_rationale: Code style convention preferring dictionary lookups

rule: When multiple components use the same value-to-element mapping, extract the dictionary into a shared constant and reuse it.
mode_rationale: Convention to extract shared mappings into constants

rule: Controllers should not contain domain lookup or enrichment logic; encapsulate it in a domain service so the controller remains thin.
mode_rationale: Architectural convention about thin controllers

rule: Methods that return multiple related fields should use a named domain object (case class) rather than tuples or generic types, so each field's purpose is clear.
mode_rationale: Convention to use named domain objects over tuples

rule: When a component is visually and functionally identical to one used elsewhere, extract it into a shared general component rather than duplicating it.
mode_rationale: DRY convention about shared component extraction

rule: When adding documentation to one function or command in a group, add equivalent documentation to all sibling functions in the same file.
mode_rationale: Documentation consistency convention

rule: Memoize expensive computations like sorting or filtering with useMemo to avoid re-running them on every React re-render.
mode_rationale: Performance convention about useMemo for expensive computations

rule: Use theme helper tokens (e.g. themeHelper('palette.textPrimary')) instead of hardcoded color values to ensure consistency and support theming.
Before building a new UI element, check if an existing shared component already provides the needed functionality and use it instead.
Maintain correct import ordering with absolute paths before relative paths; verify linting is enforcing import sort rules in all files.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence import ordering convention

rule: Avoid casting types when the auto-generated API client already provides correct type definitions; only cast when the API types are provably wrong.
mode_rationale: Simple guideline about when to cast types

rule: Extract magic numbers into named constants (e.g. MAX_TEMPLATES_TO_SHOW) so the intent is clear and the value is reusable.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence naming convention

rule: Do not add @ts-ignore without a justifying comment; audit existing @ts-ignore directives and resolve them via dedicated tech-debt tasks.
mode_rationale: Simple rule about requiring justification for ts-ignore

rule: When a component is already large, do not add more state or business logic to it; encapsulate new logic within the child component or a dedicated hook.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence architecture guideline about component size

rule: Remove default values for props or parameters that are always explicitly provided by callers, as dead defaults obscure the actual data flow.
mode_rationale: Simple cleanup convention for unused defaults

rule: Use styled-components for styling React components instead of inline style objects or style props.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence styling convention

rule: Never define a React component inside another component's render body; extract it to module scope or assign JSX directly to a variable without the arrow function wrapper.
mode_rationale: Simple rule about component definition placement

rule: Do not use separate state and useEffect for values that can be directly calculated from existing props or state; use derived/computed values instead.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence React state management convention

rule: Never mutate original arrays or objects passed as props or stored in state; clone data before performing filtering or other mutations to prevent React rendering issues.
mode_rationale: Simple immutability rule for React props/state

rule: When a permission is not found or undefined, default to hiding/denying access rather than showing/allowing, to prevent accidental exposure of restricted features.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence security default convention

rule: After filtering children from a parent container (submenu, group), check if the parent has any remaining children and hide it if empty.
mode_rationale: Simple UI cleanup rule after filtering

rule: Name files to accurately reflect their content scope; a file containing admin-specific logic should be prefixed or named accordingly, not given a generic name.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence file naming convention

rule: Keep API changes, dependency updates, and other unrelated modifications in separate PRs to avoid merge conflicts and simplify review.
mode_rationale: Simple PR hygiene guideline

rule: Always add error handling UI (error states, notifications, or fallback messages) when making API calls so users are informed when something fails.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence error handling convention

rule: When two utility functions have identical logic, combine them into a single shared function with optional parameters to handle differences.
mode_rationale: Simple code reuse guideline

rule: When UI visibility depends on async permission data, implement loading and error states to prevent items from flickering or being incorrectly shown while data loads or if the request fails.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence rule about async UI states

rule: Use the project's design system spacing functions (e.g., getMargin) for padding and margin values instead of hardcoded pixel values to maintain consistency.
mode_rationale: Simple styling convention for spacing

rule: Avoid explicitly setting CSS properties to their default values (e.g., flex-direction: row) as it adds unnecessary code and can mislead readers into thinking a non-default value is intentional.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence CSS cleanliness rule

rule: Prefer existing utility hooks (e.g., useInterval) over manual setInterval/setTimeout with useEffect cleanup to reduce boilerplate and avoid common timer bugs.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to prefer existing hooks

rule: Move styled component definitions out of the component file and into a separate file to keep component logic clean and styles isolated.
Reference traits (interfaces) rather than concrete service implementations to maintain loose coupling and testability.
mode_rationale: Simple dependency inversion guideline

rule: Before creating a new UI component, check if a similar shared component already exists and consult UX to confirm whether the standard component can be used.
When importing modules from within the same package, use relative import paths instead of absolute paths.
mode_rationale: Simple import path convention

rule: Add comments specifying the expected units for numeric fields (e.g., milliseconds, bytes, seconds) to prevent misinterpretation.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence documentation convention

rule: Wrap asynchronous API calls in try/catch blocks and surface errors to the user with appropriate error UI.
mode_rationale: Simple error handling convention for async calls

rule: Access color values through the themeHelper utility rather than directly accessing theme properties, to ensure consistent theming support.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence theming convention

rule: Never use hardcoded color constants directly; always access colors through the theme context so that values respond correctly to theme selection.
mode_rationale: Simple theming convention about color access

rule: Use px units for margin and padding values to stay consistent with the rest of the codebase, unless there is a specific reason to use relative units.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence CSS units convention

rule: Declare values that do not change as constants (const) rather than variables to communicate intent and prevent accidental mutation.
mode_rationale: Simple variable declaration convention

rule: When adding environment variables or annotations to Kubernetes workloads, also add corresponding labels so administrators and tooling can easily filter and query workloads.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence Kubernetes labeling convention

rule: Do not assume runtime metadata or configuration values will always be defined; guard against missing values with explicit checks or Option types.
mode_rationale: Simple null-safety convention

rule: Code should be readable and understandable without requiring the author to explain it; refactor complex logic until a reviewer can follow it unaided.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence readability guideline

rule: Do not wrap a field in Option when an empty string is a valid value; use a plain String type instead to avoid ambiguity between None and Some("").
mode_rationale: Simple type design convention

rule: When adding artifacts tied to an optional feature (e.g., log files, config entries), gate their creation on whether the feature is enabled to avoid confusing users with irrelevant output.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence feature gating convention

rule: When user permission state is unknown or a permission check fails, default to the most restrictive access level rather than assuming full privileges.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence security default convention

rule: When a UI component depends on a service call, provide user-visible feedback when that service fails rather than silently swallowing the error.
mode_rationale: Simple error visibility convention

rule: Use named constants (e.g., Status.CONFLICT) instead of magic numbers (e.g., 409) for HTTP status codes to improve readability and maintainability.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence naming convention for HTTP statuses

rule: Double-check that authorization checks map to the correct permission level; flipped or mismatched auth checks silently grant or deny the wrong access.
mode_rationale: Simple security review convention

rule: Name parameters that filter query results with a prefix like 'filterBy' to clearly communicate their purpose and distinguish them from identity or lookup parameters.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence parameter naming convention

rule: Extract inline event handler functions into named functions wrapped with useCallback for improved readability and to avoid unnecessary re-renders.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence React performance convention

rule: Before creating custom styled components for common layouts (e.g., flex containers), check if an equivalent shared component already exists in the UI package.
Do not hardcode shared values (URLs, support links, config strings) in feature modules; define them in the appropriate shared utility file.
mode_rationale: Simple convention about constant placement

rule: In JSX, omit the explicit `={true}` when passing boolean props; the prop name alone implies true.
Hide pagination controls when there is insufficient data to paginate rather than showing an empty or non-functional paginator.
mode_rationale: Simple UI display convention

rule: When modifying a shared or base component, validate the change across all consuming components to ensure no regressions.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence testing convention for shared components

rule: Never import from a package's own dist output within that same package; use relative source imports instead.
mode_rationale: Simple import path convention

rule: Prefer built-in component props (e.g. Ant Design's `noStyle`) over custom styled-component overrides to reset or modify component styling.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence convention to prefer built-in APIs

rule: Do not hardcode URLs that vary across deployments; use the HelpLink component or its hook to resolve environment-specific links.
mode_rationale: Simple environment-awareness convention

rule: When a configuration API provides a method like `get` that throws with the key name on missing values, prefer it over manual error handling with `getOptional` plus custom exceptions.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence API usage convention

rule: Do not leave commented-out code in the codebase unless it is tracked by a ticket; prefer removing it and referencing the ticket if the code will be needed later.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence convention about removing commented-out code

rule: Remove or revert exploratory dependency or configuration changes that did not resolve the original issue before merging.
mode_rationale: Simple guideline to clean up exploratory changes before merging

rule: Name files containing styled-components as `styled-components.tsx` rather than generic names like `styles.tsx`.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for styled-components files

rule: Wrap JSON.parse calls in try/catch even when form validation exists, to guard against edge cases where the value may be undefined or an empty string.
mode_rationale: Simple defensive coding practice for JSON.parse

rule: When a periodic task has multiple independent stages, catch NonFatal exceptions in each stage independently so a failure in one does not prevent the others from executing.
mode_rationale: Error-handling convention for independent stages in periodic tasks

rule: When fixing a bug, add a regression test that reproduces the original failure scenario to prevent the bug from reoccurring.
mode_rationale: Simple practice: add a regression test when fixing a bug

rule: Test names and assertions should use positive statements ('these are supported') rather than negative exclusions ('all except X'), especially when the excluded set may grow independently.
mode_rationale: Testing style convention preferring positive assertions

rule: Bug fixes should include a unit test covering the corrected behavior; make methods package-private if needed to enable focused testing.
mode_rationale: Simple practice: include unit tests with bug fixes

rule: Instead of changing the behavior of a widely-used method (e.g., adding case-insensitivity), create a new method variant and use it only where needed, with a comment explaining why the variant is necessary.
mode_rationale: Design guideline to create variants instead of modifying shared methods

rule: Do not allow language-specific sentinel values like the string "None" to propagate through the system as valid data; throw an exception or use a proper domain-level default instead.
mode_rationale: Simple rule against propagating language-specific sentinels as valid data

rule: Validate and normalize external input (e.g., enum string values) at the API layer rather than adding defensive workarounds deeper in the codebase.
mode_rationale: Convention to validate input at API boundaries, not deep in the codebase

rule: Move TypeScript types and interfaces to a dedicated `types` file rather than defining them inline in utility or component files.
mode_rationale: File organization convention for TypeScript types

rule: Use idiomatic collection operations like `drop(offset).take(limit)` for in-memory pagination instead of manual index arithmetic with `slice`.
mode_rationale: Coding style preference for idiomatic collection operations

rule: Method names should accurately describe their behavior; if a method performs pagination, include that in the name (e.g., `getPaginatedModelDeployments`).
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention: method name should describe all its behaviors

rule: Consolidate related parsing steps (e.g., splitting, field extraction, default handling) into a single function rather than scattering them across the call site.
mode_rationale: Code organization guideline to group related parsing steps

rule: Never retrieve all records from a datastore on every request; apply filters, pagination, or scoping at the query level to avoid fetching too much data.
mode_rationale: Performance convention to always scope queries

rule: Keep imports organized and properly sorted in every file; disorganized imports signal careless code and make files harder to scan.
mode_rationale: Simple style convention for import ordering

rule: Render modal instances at the table level (once), not inside each table row; row actions should only trigger the shared modal to open.
mode_rationale: UI architecture convention for modal placement

rule: Add explicit conditional guards before type-specific operations to make the code's intent clear and prevent unintended execution on wrong types.
mode_rationale: Defensive coding convention for type checks

rule: Add DEBUG or TRACE level log statements before key decision points and side-effecting operations to aid troubleshooting in production.
trigger: When writing code that branches on a condition, selects a strategy, or makes a routing decision that affects downstream behavior
rationale: Without logging at decision points, debugging production issues requires guessing which branch was taken; adding these logs after the fact requires a redeploy, delaying incident resolution.
mode_rationale: Logging convention for key decision points
steps: Step 1: Identify the key decision point (if/match/strategy selection) in your code
Step 2: Add a DEBUG-level log statement immediately before or after the decision, including the input values and the chosen branch
Step 3: Include enough context to reconstruct the decision (e.g., feature flag value, user attribute, config setting)
Step 4: Keep the message concise — one line per decision, not a paragraph

rule: Create a dedicated unit test spec class for every new service or domain class introduced.
mode_rationale: Simple practice: create test specs for new services

rule: Function names must accurately describe the scope of their side effects; if a function stops an entire workflow rather than a single task, name it accordingly and document the blast radius.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for functions with side effects

rule: Choose field names that are unambiguous in context; when a name could be confused with existing fields, either pick a clearer name or add a comment explaining its purpose.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for unambiguous field names

rule: Do not pass arguments to a function that are not declared in its signature; relying on closure scope while appearing to pass a parameter is misleading.
mode_rationale: Code cleanliness convention against misleading arguments

rule: Use JSX boolean shorthand for true props (write `<Comp disabled />` instead of `<Comp disabled={true} />`).
mode_rationale: JSX syntax convention for boolean props

rule: User-facing APIs should accept generalized parameter names and map them to provider-specific equivalents internally, rather than exposing implementation details.
mode_rationale: API design convention to hide provider-specific details

rule: Instead of commenting out code, create a new version of the variable or logic with the desired behavior and use it directly.
mode_rationale: Code cleanliness convention against commented-out code

rule: When calling an RPC or service method, pass only the essential identifier and assemble the full request from available context on the receiving side, rather than forwarding redundant data the callee already has.
mode_rationale: API design convention to avoid passing redundant data

rule: Use CSS rules to handle visual layout concerns like spacing and dividers rather than restructuring JSX or adding JavaScript logic.
mode_rationale: Styling convention preferring CSS over JS for layout

rule: When modifying authorization roles and permissions, explicitly verify that each role grants only the intended access and does not inadvertently expose resources to restricted roles.
mode_rationale: Security convention to verify role permissions on change

rule: Remove conditional checks that can never be triggered due to earlier guard clauses, and clean up leftover test or debug code before merging.
mode_rationale: Code cleanliness convention to remove dead code

rule: When overriding a method to change behavior for one specific case, delegate all other cases to the parent implementation (e.g., super.method) rather than hardcoding every case.
mode_rationale: OOP convention to use super for unmodified cases

rule: In tests that don't depend on real system behavior (e.g., real clock), use simulated or mocked dependencies to make it clear the test is not coupled to external state.
mode_rationale: Testing convention to mock dependencies for clarity

rule: When consuming external service responses, prefer structured/typed APIs over string parsing to reduce fragility and improve maintainability.
mode_rationale: API consumption convention preferring typed APIs

rule: When accessing optional data (e.g., roles, credentials), explicitly handle the case where the value is absent and determine whether absence is a valid state or an error.
mode_rationale: Error-handling convention for optional data

rule: When using undocumented or hard-to-find external API endpoints, add a comment explaining where the endpoint came from and where to find related documentation.
mode_rationale: Documentation convention for undocumented endpoints

rule: Do not introduce dependencies that have not been updated in years; prefer actively maintained libraries to reduce risk from unpatched bugs and vulnerabilities.
mode_rationale: Security convention against stale dependencies

rule: Never reference colors directly from the colors map; always access them through the themeHelper to ensure consistent theming and support theme switching.
mode_rationale: Theming convention to use themeHelper for color access

rule: Use existing utility library functions (e.g., ramda, lodash) for common operations like unique/set/filter instead of writing custom implementations.
mode_rationale: Convention to use existing library functions over custom implementations

rule: Do not extract single-use helper functions; place the logic directly in the JSX where it is consumed to reduce indirection.
mode_rationale: Code organization convention against over-extracting one-off logic

rule: When logic is reusable across components or modules, extract it into a shared utility function rather than leaving it inline in a single component.
mode_rationale: Code organization convention for shared utility extraction

rule: When you need a variant of an existing utility (e.g., a different ID type), extend the existing utility rather than creating a duplicate with a hardcoded copy of the same value.
mode_rationale: Architecture convention to extend rather than duplicate utilities

rule: When building or transforming a collection into a new structure, use reduce (or equivalent functional method) instead of forEach with mutation.
mode_rationale: Functional style convention for data transformations

rule: Extract magic numbers into named constants to improve readability and maintainability.
mode_rationale: Readability convention to name constants

rule: Use built-in unique ID generation (e.g., Date.now(), crypto.randomUUID()) instead of manually maintaining counter variables for keys.
mode_rationale: Convention to use built-in ID generation over manual counters

rule: When a method serves a specific use case within an overloaded domain concept, name it to convey that specific intent rather than reusing a generic name.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for specific method intent

rule: Test names should precisely describe the scope being tested so readers can understand coverage without reading the test body.
mode_rationale: Testing convention for descriptive test names

rule: Never hardcode font family values; use the theme's typography tokens so fonts remain consistent and centrally configurable.
mode_rationale: Theming convention against hardcoded font families

rule: Use existing design system semantic color tokens (e.g., palette.danger) instead of introducing custom hex values; minimize custom colors in the theme.
mode_rationale: Design system convention to use semantic color tokens

rule: The theme should remain general-purpose; do not add component-specific one-off colors to the theme object — map to existing semantic tokens instead.
mode_rationale: Theme architecture convention against component-specific colors

rule: When migrating icon libraries (e.g., AntD to FontAwesome), preserve the original aria-label values to avoid breaking existing integration/E2E tests.
mode_rationale: Single convention: keep aria-labels intact when swapping icon libraries

rule: When a color maps to a known semantic role (danger, warning, etc.), use the palette token rather than a raw hex value, even if they resolve to the same color.
mode_rationale: Simple rule: prefer semantic palette tokens over raw hex values

rule: Do not explicitly set style properties (fontSize, fontWeight, colors) that already match the theme or framework defaults — remove them to reduce noise and prevent drift.
mode_rationale: Simple rule: don't explicitly set styles that match theme defaults

rule: Before adding a color, spacing, or other design token, check whether the default theme/palette already defines it — avoid duplicating values that are already provided.
mode_rationale: Simple rule: check for existing theme tokens before adding new ones

rule: When implementing paginated API responses, use the codebase's standardized pagination traits (e.g., PaginatedResponse) rather than creating ad-hoc pagination wrappers.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: use the codebase's standardized pagination traits

rule: Use the simplest accurate name for entity types (e.g., "user" not "userAccount") unless disambiguation is genuinely needed.
mode_rationale: Naming convention: use simplest accurate name unless disambiguation needed

rule: Be cautious when adding feature-specific changes to shared/generic modules (e.g., telemetry, events) — evaluate whether the additions are truly generic or if they belong in a feature-specific module.
mode_rationale: Architectural guideline: keep feature-specific logic out of shared modules

rule: When building a v2 or replacement implementation, create a dedicated test spec file rather than appending tests to the legacy version's spec.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: new versions get their own test spec files

rule: Use the project's standard logging trait (e.g., DominoLoggable) or lazy logger pattern rather than manually creating Logger instances, unless there is a specific reason to use a custom logger name.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: use project's standard logging trait

rule: Place serialization logic in the domain type's companion object or a dedicated serializer in the same namespace, rather than in consuming services.
mode_rationale: Code placement rule: serialization lives in companion object or dedicated serializer

rule: Add dedicated serialization tests for any non-trivial custom serialization logic to verify the output matches expectations.
mode_rationale: Simple rule: add tests for non-trivial custom serialization

rule: Remove temporary debug logging and print statements before merging, or at minimum reduce them to trace level.
mode_rationale: Simple hygiene rule: remove debug logging before merge

rule: When testing a feature with multiple behaviors or branches (e.g., target and location), add tests covering each path for completeness.
mode_rationale: Simple testing principle: cover each branch/behavior path

rule: When binding a service or scheduler in a shared injection module, verify which deployment instances actually need it to avoid unnecessary resource consumption.
mode_rationale: Simple check: verify which deployments need a service before binding

rule: In test generators, use the most semantically specific generator available (e.g., relativeFilePathGen for a sub-directory) rather than a more general one.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: use most specific test generator available

rule: Remove development print statements (println, console.log, etc.) from production code before submitting a PR.
mode_rationale: Simple hygiene rule: remove print statements from production code

rule: When applying encoding workarounds (like double-encoding for framework bugs), centralize the logic in a single utility so it can be changed in one place when the framework is updated.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: centralize encoding workarounds in one utility

rule: When adding encoding logic to shared/common components, ensure already-encoded inputs are not encoded again to prevent corruption of URLs and paths.
mode_rationale: Simple defensive rule: prevent double-encoding in shared components

rule: When adding customer-facing features or changing operational behavior, update the relevant runbook so on-call engineers have current documentation.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: update runbooks when changing operational behavior

rule: Avoid setting both `data-test` and `testId` props on the same component; use only the one required by your testing framework.
mode_rationale: Simple rule: use one test selector prop, not both

rule: Use the shared `getErrorMessage` utility from `@domino/ui/dist/utils/errorUtil` to parse error messages instead of writing custom error extraction logic.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: use shared getErrorMessage utility

rule: Maintain consistent import ordering: absolute (third-party and aliased) imports must appear before relative imports.
When a component file contains utility functions, constants, and sub-components alongside the main component, split them into separate files (utils, sub-component, main component).
mode_rationale: Simple organizational rule: separate utils, sub-components, and main component

rule: Use styled-components for all styling instead of inline styles to maintain consistency, reusability, and separation of concerns.
mode_rationale: Simple styling convention: styled-components over inline styles

rule: When mocking components in tests, use the shared mock utilities pattern (as in `frontend/packages/mocks`) to retain access to props and callbacks on mocked components.
mode_rationale: Simple testing convention: use shared mock utilities pattern

rule: When mocking data in tests, create generator functions in `frontend/packages/mocks/generators` so the mock data can be reused across test suites.
mode_rationale: Simple testing convention: create reusable mock data generators

rule: Prefer immutable array methods like `slice` over mutating methods like `splice`, even when working with copies, to signal immutable intent and prevent accidental mutation bugs.
mode_rationale: Simple coding convention: use slice over splice to signal immutable intent

rule: When intentionally omitting dependencies from a React useEffect dependency array, leave a comment explaining why to prevent future developers from mistakenly adding them.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation rule: comment omitted useEffect dependencies

rule: Avoid creating separate React state for values that can be computed or derived from existing state; use useMemo or inline computation instead.
mode_rationale: Simple React convention: derive state via useMemo or inline computation

rule: Before creating custom styled elements (e.g., styled anchor tags, custom link wrappers), check if an existing shared component already provides the needed functionality.
mode_rationale: Simple rule: check for existing shared components before creating custom ones

rule: Import styled-components files using namespace imports (`import * as Styles from './styles'`) so styled components are clearly distinguishable as style elements in JSX.
mode_rationale: Simple import convention: namespace import for styled-components files

rule: Use theme tokens or palette colors instead of hardcoded color values, and avoid using `!important` overrides in styled-components.
mode_rationale: Simple styling rule: use theme tokens, avoid hardcoded colors and !important

rule: Verify that new exports, components, and utility functions are actually imported and used somewhere; remove any dead code before merging.
mode_rationale: Simple hygiene rule: verify exports are used, remove dead code

rule: Calculate derived state directly from existing values instead of using useState + useEffect; use `const isDisabled = isSubmitting || !definition` rather than syncing state in an effect.
mode_rationale: Simple React convention: compute derived state directly instead of useState+useEffect

rule: Use theme helper tokens (e.g., margins.tiny, margins.small) instead of hardcoded pixel values in styled components.
mode_rationale: Simple styling rule: use theme tokens instead of hardcoded pixel values

rule: When encountering an error, investigate and fix the root cause rather than disabling or working around the failing code path.
mode_rationale: Simple debugging principle: fix root cause, don't work around it

rule: When refactoring moves responsibility from one class to another, ensure equivalent unit tests are created for the new owner.
mode_rationale: Simple testing rule: tests follow refactored responsibility

rule: Do not wrap simple or static computations in useMemo; reserve memoization for genuinely expensive calculations or referential equality needs.
Never hardcode documentation or support URLs; use the project's support utils, HelpLink component, or docs base path hook so links stay correct across environments.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: use support utils and hooks for documentation URLs

rule: When the same conditional logic is used in multiple places, extract it into a shared utility or variable to avoid repetition and reduce divergence risk.
When multiple styled components share the same styles (e.g., icon wrappers), consolidate them into a single reusable styled component.
mode_rationale: Simple rule: merge duplicate styled components into one reusable component

rule: If a component is already a styled component, define all its styles (including fontSize) in the styled definition rather than passing them as inline style props.
mode_rationale: Simple styling rule: define all styles in styled definition, not inline props

rule: When implementing operations that can be triggered concurrently (e.g., from multiple frontend instances), design for race conditions by detecting and resolving conflicts downstream rather than relying on optimistic checks.
mode_rationale: Architectural guideline: design for race conditions in concurrent operations

rule: Query methods that filter, scope, and sort results must have tests verifying each dimension: correct entity type, correct scope, correct exclusions, and correct ordering.
mode_rationale: Simple testing principle: verify each dimension of query methods

rule: When a function's actual behavior is narrower or different from what its name implies, rename it to accurately reflect what it does.
mode_rationale: Naming convention: rename functions whose names don't match actual behavior

rule: When implementing a feature that touches project-type-specific code paths, verify it works across all supported project types (e.g., DFS and GBP).
mode_rationale: Simple verification rule: test across all supported project types

rule: When a function is fully replaced by a new implementation, delete the old function rather than leaving dead code.
mode_rationale: Simple hygiene rule: delete old functions when fully replaced

rule: Name constants to clearly indicate which domain or subsystem they belong to, so readers can understand their purpose without navigating to the definition.
mode_rationale: Naming convention: constant names should indicate their domain/subsystem

rule: Keep dependency injection framework imports (e.g., Guice) confined to injection/wiring modules rather than spreading them into domain or service classes, to avoid transitive dependency leakage.
mode_rationale: Architectural guideline: keep DI framework imports in injection modules only

rule: When logic is extracted into a new class, that class must have its own dedicated unit tests — do not rely on tests that existed for the original caller.
mode_rationale: Simple testing rule: extracted classes need their own unit tests

rule: Every new code path or branch must have a corresponding test case, even if it means adding the test in a follow-up PR.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence convention: every new code path needs a test

rule: Do not add fields, configuration, or dependencies to a data model until they are actually needed — defer additions until there is a concrete consumer.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence YAGNI principle about deferring additions

rule: Service implementations that depend on external communication (HTTP, gRPC, etc.) belong in the infrastructure layer, not in the server/application layer.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence layering convention for service implementations

rule: When naming entity types in events or audit trails, be specific enough to distinguish the granularity level (e.g., a single setting vs. an entire config page).
mode_rationale: Naming convention about specificity in entity type names

rule: A field named with a suffix like 'Id' must actually contain an identifier, not a display name or other value — misleading suffixes cause bugs when developers trust the name.
mode_rationale: Naming convention about suffix accuracy in field names

rule: Reference defined constant values (e.g., EventTarget.Project) instead of raw string literals in tests and production code to catch refactoring errors at compile time.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to use constants over string literals

rule: When emitting telemetry or audit trail events for an entity, include all available identifying attributes (e.g., both ID and name) so events are useful for debugging and reporting.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence rule about completeness in telemetry events

rule: Use TypeScript `interface` rather than `type` for defining React component props, as interface is the team convention and provides better extensibility and error messages.
mode_rationale: Team convention for TypeScript prop definitions

rule: Order import statements so that absolute (package/library) imports appear before relative (local file) imports.
mode_rationale: Simple import ordering convention

rule: Prefer styled components over inline style attributes; use colors from the design theme rather than hardcoded values; and replace magic numbers with named constants for readability.
mode_rationale: Styling convention to avoid inline styles and magic numbers

rule: When a common/shared component already exists (e.g., a Link component), use it instead of creating a local duplicate with equivalent functionality.
mode_rationale: Simple DRY convention for shared components

rule: In React/TypeScript files, leave a blank line between type/interface declarations and component definitions, and between successive styled component definitions, for visual separation.
mode_rationale: Formatting convention for visual separation in React files

rule: Use router links instead of anchor tags when navigating to React pages to avoid unnecessary full page reloads.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to avoid full page reloads

rule: Use semantic color tokens from the design system (e.g., textPrimary, textSecondary) instead of hardcoded color values in styled components.
Use border color tokens from the theme palette for dividers and borders instead of hardcoded color values.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence styling convention for border color tokens

rule: Use theme spacing helpers (e.g., getMargin) instead of deprecated padding/margin constants for calculating spacing values.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to use theme spacing helpers

rule: Use the project's themeHelper utility for all styled-component values instead of accessing theme properties directly or using hardcoded values.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence convention to use themeHelper utility

rule: Use existing route helper functions to build internal navigation URLs instead of constructing URL strings manually.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to use route helpers over manual URL strings

rule: Before removing or renaming props on shared/core UI components, verify that no other consumers depend on them, especially when the change won't merge soon.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence caution about checking consumers before removing props

rule: When replacing legacy Ant Design icons with custom icons, preserve the original aria-label values to avoid breaking existing tests.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to preserve aria-labels during icon replacement

rule: When calling setState inside useEffect, ensure the dependency condition is strong enough to prevent infinite render loops.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence React performance convention

rule: When a conditional branch sets a value and returns, drop the else clause and let code fall through to improve readability.
mode_rationale: Simple readability convention about early returns

rule: Before writing custom useEffect + useState patterns, check if existing hooks (e.g., useOnChange, useRemoteData) already provide the needed state management and callbacks.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence convention to check for existing hooks first

rule: When you need to refresh data after a mutation, use the refetch method from useRemoteData instead of duplicating remote state into local state.
mode_rationale: Simple convention about data refresh strategy

rule: When multiple async operations exist in the same component, handle their side effects (e.g., page reload, refetch, notifications) consistently unless there is an explicit reason to differ.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence convention about consistent side-effect handling

rule: Define event handler functions with useCallback to prevent unnecessary re-creation on every render, especially when passed as props to child components.
mode_rationale: Simple React performance convention for event handlers

rule: If a method's return value can never vary (e.g., always returns true), refactor the return type to reflect actual behavior (e.g., Unit/void) rather than suggesting a branching outcome.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence convention about truthful return type signatures

rule: Give configuration files meaningful, distinct names rather than generic names like application.conf; they can reference each other by resource classpath.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for configuration files

rule: Use standard platform mechanisms (e.g., classpath includes) instead of file-manipulation workarounds to manage configuration layering.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence convention to use platform-native config mechanisms

rule: Split configuration into a managed/standardized file and a separate user-customizable file so custom settings layer on top without conflicting with managed defaults.
mode_rationale: Simple architecture convention for config file separation

rule: Include the unit of measurement in constant names (e.g., MAX_DROPDOWN_HEIGHT_PX) instead of relying on comments to convey the unit.
mode_rationale: Naming convention to include units in constant names

rule: Do not import from a package's own published name (e.g., @domino/ui) from within that package; use relative paths or package aliases instead to avoid circular dependencies.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to avoid circular self-imports

rule: React hooks should live in their own dedicated files, not in util files; a file named *Util should contain pure utility functions, not hooks.
mode_rationale: File organization convention for React hooks

rule: Call React hooks at the top level of the component function, not inside JSX; inline hook calls in JSX break if early returns or conditional rendering change hook order.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence React rules-of-hooks convention

rule: Style Ant Design components directly via their component API or styled-components wrappers rather than targeting internal class names like .ant-input or .ant-input-affix-wrapper with nested selectors.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to avoid targeting internal Ant Design class names

rule: Only use dangerouslySetInnerHTML when the content is actually HTML; if it is plain text, render it as a normal React child to avoid XSS risk.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence security convention about XSS risk

rule: Use generic/brand-neutral UI elements (e.g., a standard wait spinner) instead of brand-specific assets (e.g., spinning Domino logo) so the component works in white-labeled deployments.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to use brand-neutral UI elements

rule: Keep mock data in a separate file from Storybook story definitions to maintain clean separation of concerns.
mode_rationale: File organization convention for Storybook

rule: Use the project's themeHelper utility for style values instead of hardcoding colors, spacing, or other design tokens directly in styled-components.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence convention to use themeHelper for style values

rule: Name functions with verb-based names that describe the action they perform (e.g., getOrdered or orderOptions) rather than adjective or noun names like ordered.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for function names

rule: Core-ui packages should contain only foundational UI components with no API request logic; components that make API calls belong in the higher-level ui package to avoid cyclic dependencies when packages are split.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence architecture boundary rule for core-ui packages

rule: Move static values, constants, and computations that don't depend on props or state outside the component function to avoid unnecessary re-creation on each render.
mode_rationale: Simple performance convention about static value placement

rule: Frameworks should default to the most restrictive security posture (e.g., deny anonymous access) so developers must explicitly opt in to less secure behavior rather than accidentally forgetting to opt out.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence security convention about default-deny posture

rule: Interfaces (e.g., authorizers) should accept canonical types like Principal; callers that only have a User should perform the conversion themselves rather than polluting the interface with convenience overloads.
mode_rationale: Simple convention about keeping interfaces clean of convenience overloads

rule: Do not merge context objects that have different lifecycles or scopes; a per-request context (DominoContext) and a per-operation context (authorization context) should remain separate parameters even if it looks redundant.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence convention about not merging different-scoped contexts

rule: When inline logic chains are hard to follow, extract them into a named private method to improve readability, even if the logic is only used once.
mode_rationale: Simple readability convention about extracting inline logic

rule: Functions that perform validation or checks should be named with a verb prefix (e.g., checkFormHasErrors) rather than reading as a property or state description (e.g., formHasErrors).
mode_rationale: Naming convention for validation function names

rule: Use a dictionary/object lookup instead of switch statements or if-else chains when mapping a static set of known keys to values.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to prefer object lookup over switch/if-else

rule: Array.prototype.sort() mutates the original array in place; when sorting data that may be referenced elsewhere, sort a copy (e.g., [...items].sort()) to avoid silent bugs.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence convention about sorting copies to avoid mutation

rule: Use design system theme tokens or component props for colors and styling instead of overriding styles with !important.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to avoid !important overrides

rule: Organize imports so that external/library imports come before relative/local imports.
mode_rationale: Simple import ordering convention

rule: Use styled components (or CSS-in-JS abstractions) instead of inline styles for consistency, reusability, and theming support.
Name telemetry and audit events to precisely describe the action they capture; rename ambiguous event names that could be confused with related but different operations.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for telemetry events

rule: Telemetry and audit events should include all relevant context identifiers (e.g., projectId) so events can be correlated and traced back to their source entity.
mode_rationale: Simple rule to include IDs in telemetry payloads

rule: Use trait composition (e.g., extending a base trait like ProjectThroughIdLocatedEvent) to automatically include shared fields in telemetry events rather than manually adding them to each event class.
mode_rationale: Single convention preferring trait composition over manual field duplication

rule: Events must extend the correct trait or interface (e.g., AuditableEvent) to be processed by downstream handlers; inheriting from a base class alone may not activate all intended behaviors.
mode_rationale: Simple rule about extending the correct trait for event processing

rule: Exception messages should use string interpolation to include the relevant entity ID so the error is actionable in logs and debugging.
mode_rationale: Single convention for exception message content

rule: When modifying a shared or base component, manually verify all affected places in the application still behave correctly.
mode_rationale: Multi-step verification workflow across all consumers when modifying shared components

rule: Always define and access colors through the theme system (e.g., useThemeColor hook and theme keys) rather than hardcoding hex values or referencing the palette directly.
Use the project's spacing utility functions (e.g., getMargin) instead of raw CSS values to maintain consistent spacing across the application.
mode_rationale: Simple rule to use spacing utilities instead of raw values

rule: When querying collections, apply status or type filters (e.g., Active-only) to narrow results rather than fetching everything and filtering in application code.
mode_rationale: Simple performance convention to filter at the query level

rule: When aggregating data across entities, consolidate into a single query instead of issuing per-entity queries; create a follow-up ticket if immediate optimization is not feasible.
mode_rationale: Single rule to consolidate per-entity queries into batch queries

rule: When a request spans multiple resources with varying permissions, return results for authorized resources and an empty set for unauthorized ones rather than failing the entire request with a 403.
mode_rationale: Single API design convention for mixed-permission responses

rule: API endpoints should follow RESTful conventions — use resource-oriented paths (e.g., GET /v4/workspaces) rather than action-oriented or verbose custom paths.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for REST endpoints

rule: When aggregating results from multiple partitions or sub-queries, apply sorting and pagination to the combined result set rather than independently per partition.
mode_rationale: Single rule about where to apply pagination

rule: When adding sort functionality to an endpoint, verify the sort field matches the product requirement (e.g., 'last started') and document which field is being sorted on.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to verify sort fields match requirements

rule: Define only one React component per file to improve readability and maintainability.
Never access color values directly in styled-components; always use the themeHelper utility to ensure consistency and theme support.
Use the themeHelper for all theme-related values (colors, spacing) rather than hardcoding them, even in seemingly small usages.
mode_rationale: Simple rule to use themeHelper for all theme-related values

rule: Use spacing utility functions like getMargin instead of hardcoding margin values to maintain consistent spacing across the UI.
mode_rationale: Simple rule to use spacing utility functions

rule: Use styled-components instead of inline styles or ad-hoc CSS to keep styling consistent and co-located.
Variables that are not React components should use camelCase naming, not PascalCase, to clearly distinguish them from components.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for non-component variables

rule: A component's name must accurately describe its actual behavior; if it doesn't contain a link, don't call it a Link.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for component accuracy

rule: Use ampersand (&) nesting in styled-components to increase CSS specificity and avoid style conflicts.
mode_rationale: Simple CSS convention for styled-components specificity

rule: Check for and use existing data-fetching hooks (e.g., useRemoteData) before writing custom fetch logic in components.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to check for existing hooks before writing new fetch logic

rule: Cache project authorization contexts to reduce redundant computation, especially when the same auth checks are needed across multiple features.
mode_rationale: Simple performance convention to cache auth contexts

rule: When reporting authorization or action failures, ensure the error context (entity, action code) matches the specific action that failed to make errors easier to debug.
mode_rationale: Simple rule about error context accuracy

rule: Use sessionStorage instead of localStorage for user-specific preferences, since localStorage is shared between different users on the same browser; prefer the UX/UI backend endpoint for persisting user preferences.
mode_rationale: Simple storage convention for user-scoped data

rule: Do not wrap values in useMemo or useCallback unless there is a clear performance reason; unnecessary memoization adds complexity without benefit.
mode_rationale: Simple performance convention against premature memoization

rule: Extract localStorage/sessionStorage key strings into named constants so they can be reused consistently and changed in one place.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for storage keys

rule: All user-facing strings that reference the product name must be passed through the whitelabel function to support custom branding.
mode_rationale: Simple rule to pass product name strings through whitelabel function

rule: Always wrap JSON.parse calls in a try/catch block to handle malformed data gracefully, especially when reading from storage or external sources.
mode_rationale: Simple error-handling convention for JSON parsing

rule: Include all referenced variables in useEffect/useCallback/useMemo dependency arrays; missing dependencies cause stale closures and subtle bugs.
mode_rationale: Simple rule about React hook dependency completeness

rule: When implementing multi-step business logic, refactor the code so that each documented rule maps to a clearly named method, making the code read like the requirements.
mode_rationale: Single refactoring convention to name methods after business rules

rule: Use existing styling utility functions (e.g., getMargin) for consistent spacing rather than writing ad-hoc margin/padding values in component styles.
mode_rationale: Simple rule to use existing styling utilities

rule: Use the framework-provided Link component for in-app navigation instead of raw anchor tags or manual window.location calls.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to use framework Link component

rule: Use shared route/path utility functions to construct URLs rather than hardcoding path strings inline.
mode_rationale: Simple rule to use route utilities instead of hardcoded paths

rule: When a concept already has an established name in one part of the codebase, use the same name in other modules to maintain consistency.
mode_rationale: Simple naming consistency convention

rule: If a method returns unexpected duplicates, fix the method to not return duplicates rather than compensating with groupBy or deduplication at the call site.
mode_rationale: Simple rule to fix duplicates at the source rather than compensating downstream

rule: When a method operates on a single ID, refactor it to accept a list of IDs (or use parametric polymorphism) to support both single and batch use cases.
mode_rationale: Simple API design convention for method signatures

rule: When an API endpoint returns paginated data, use the project's standardized paginated response trait rather than ad-hoc collection types.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to use standard pagination types

rule: Move URL construction logic into shared route utility functions (e.g., ui/core/routes) instead of computing URLs inline in components.
mode_rationale: Simple code organization rule for URL construction

rule: Absolute (package/library) imports must appear before relative imports in every file.
mode_rationale: Simple import ordering convention

rule: Do not nest class selectors inside styled-components unless styling third-party components (e.g., Ant Design); prefer gap, direct component styling, or wrapping the target in its own styled component.
mode_rationale: Simple CSS convention except for third-party component styling

rule: Before building a new UI element, check whether a global/shared component already exists for that pattern (e.g., run status indicators) and reuse it.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to check for existing components before creating new ones

rule: When a value represents one of a known set of states, define a union type or enum instead of using raw strings.
mode_rationale: Simple typing convention for finite state values

rule: Static values (objects, arrays, configuration) that do not depend on props or state should be defined outside the component body to prevent re-creation on every render.
mode_rationale: Simple performance convention for static values

rule: When a collection is already filtered to matching items, use headOption (or equivalent) instead of an additional find call.
mode_rationale: Simple performance convention to avoid redundant collection operations

rule: Consolidate all external data fetching needed for DTO construction in one place and pure attribute mapping in another; do not split data-fetching across both the service and the mapper.
mode_rationale: Single architectural convention about separation of mapping and fetching

rule: When mapping a list of N entities that each require related data, gather all needed IDs and perform one batch query instead of N individual queries.
mode_rationale: Simple performance convention to batch queries in mappers
