rule: Before adding a database query to enforce a constraint, verify the constraint isn't already enforced by the UX or calling code — skip the query if it adds latency without user-visible benefit.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence guideline about avoiding unnecessary DB queries when constraints are already enforced

rule: Do not add a validation query to reject invalid IDs if the downstream logic already handles them gracefully (e.g., returns no results); save the extra service call.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence convention to skip redundant validation when downstream handles it

rule: Custom React hooks (useX) should live in a folder named 'hooks', not a generic 'utils' folder, and should have accompanying test suites.
mode_rationale: Simple file organization convention for React hooks

rule: Do not set default values in OpenAPI route definitions unless you intend the API to use that value when the parameter is omitted, as per the OpenAPI spec.
mode_rationale: Single rule about OpenAPI default value semantics

rule: API parameters should only affect the dimension they describe; if a parameter like `limit` changes query semantics beyond just limiting results, create a separate endpoint instead of overloading the existing one.
mode_rationale: Design principle about API parameter scope — one sentence guideline

rule: Ensure metric names used in code match exactly between the metric definition and the metric recording call to avoid silent metric loss.
mode_rationale: Simple naming consistency rule for metrics

rule: Never use `any` in TypeScript; generate proper types from the API or define them on the UI side to maintain type safety.
mode_rationale: Straightforward type safety convention

rule: Do not attach click events to non-interactive elements like `<span>`; use a button component (e.g., InvisibleButton) for proper keyboard and screen reader accessibility.
mode_rationale: Simple accessibility rule about interactive elements

rule: When calling an async function inside a try-catch block, you must `await` the call; otherwise the catch block will not intercept errors from the async operation.
mode_rationale: Single coding convention about async/await in error handling

rule: When a component accepts a customizable trigger or child element, use a render prop that injects callbacks (e.g., onClick) rather than accepting a Function Component, to allow consumers to pass additional properties.
mode_rationale: Component API design convention expressible in one sentence

rule: Action-oriented UI components (e.g., row actions, toolbar actions) should be defined in their own files because they tend to accumulate logic over time.
mode_rationale: Simple file organization guideline

rule: Do not define React components inside another component's render body; the inner component is recreated on every re-render, causing unnecessary unmount/remount cycles and state loss.
mode_rationale: Single performance rule about React component definition placement

rule: Name variables, keys, and identifiers by their semantic meaning (e.g., tabDetails, tabResources) rather than ordinal position (tab1, tab2) to avoid cascading renames when items are inserted or reordered.
mode_rationale: Naming convention — use meaning over position

rule: When defining enum values in an OpenAPI spec, verify that the casing matches what the API actually returns, because code generators may silently alter the casing.
mode_rationale: Single verification rule about enum casing consistency

rule: When writing tests, do not mock the component whose behavior you are trying to verify; mocking the expected answers means the test feeds itself correct results and provides no evidence the real code works.
mode_rationale: Single testing principle about what not to mock

rule: Before writing custom null/error handling logic, check for existing utility helpers in the codebase that already handle the same edge case (e.g., K8s API null responses).
mode_rationale: Simple convention to check for existing utilities before writing new ones

rule: Use configured path aliases (e.g., `ui`) instead of long relative import paths to improve readability and maintainability.
mode_rationale: Simple import style convention

rule: When code intentionally deviates from the expected flow (e.g., preserving stale data during a close animation), add an inline comment explaining why.
mode_rationale: Single documentation convention about inline comments

rule: Always use optional chaining or a guard when accessing array elements by index, unless you can guarantee the element exists.
mode_rationale: Simple defensive coding rule

rule: When a DTO field is not present on all variants of a type, model it as Option[T] rather than using an empty string or null sentinel value.
mode_rationale: Type modeling convention for optional fields

rule: When authorization or data loading can result in an empty UI (e.g., empty navbar), provide user-visible feedback explaining why nothing is shown.
mode_rationale: Single UX guideline about empty state visibility

rule: When building a new version of an existing feature (e.g., new sidebar navigation), cross-check with the old implementation to ensure no items or capabilities are missing.
mode_rationale: Single guideline to cross-check old vs new implementations

rule: When naming methods like `map`, respect the established functional programming contract — `map` on a type should return the same type; use a more specific name if the method does something different.
mode_rationale: Naming convention about established method semantics

rule: Name utility methods to reflect their specific domain purpose (e.g., `composeOrdering` instead of `compose`) and add documentation explaining their intended use.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for utility methods

rule: In aggregation endpoints that span multiple projects, authorize the project list once upfront and then query child resources in bulk, rather than re-authorizing each project individually.
mode_rationale: Single performance guideline about batching authorization

rule: When only identifiers or minimal metadata are needed, use a lightweight query or endpoint instead of a full DTO-building query that joins unnecessary data.
mode_rationale: Simple performance convention about query weight

rule: Do not import from a package's own public API barrel within that package's internal files; use direct relative imports instead.
mode_rationale: Single import rule to prevent circular dependencies

rule: Use centralized route helper functions from the routes module instead of constructing URL paths inline.
Place provider-specific validation logic inside the service layer so it is enforced regardless of the calling context, rather than scattering it across callers.
mode_rationale: Single architectural guideline about validation placement

rule: When adding a new feature or field, ensure all exposed API versions (v1, v4, public) are updated to include it.
mode_rationale: Simple rule to keep all API versions in sync

rule: Do not create multiple DTOs or case classes that represent the same domain concept; consolidate into a single model and reuse it.
mode_rationale: Single code organization rule about DTO consolidation

rule: In mapper and serialization tests, define expected input and output as separate class-level fixtures so the test body is a single assertion line, making it clear what is being tested versus what is test data.
mode_rationale: Simple test structure convention

rule: Do not leave unresolved TODO comments in code that ships; either address them in the current PR or remove them and track the work in a ticket.
mode_rationale: Single code hygiene rule about TODO comments

rule: Order imports so that absolute (package/library) imports appear above relative imports.
When a data class has multiple optional fields where only certain combinations are valid, document the valid states explicitly via comments or a sealed trait hierarchy.
mode_rationale: Single documentation guideline for complex data classes

rule: Use shorthand syntax for boolean JSX props that are true (e.g., `closable` instead of `closable={true}`).
mode_rationale: Simple JSX syntax convention

rule: Use the `stringify` method from the `query-string` library instead of manually constructing query strings.
mode_rationale: Single convention to use a library instead of manual string building

rule: API service functions should accept raw key-value objects rather than pre-built query strings, matching the pattern used by domino-api for easier future migration.
mode_rationale: Simple API design convention about parameter formats

rule: Place utility functions near the code that uses them rather than in distant shared utility files, especially when the logic is tightly coupled.
mode_rationale: Single code organization guideline about proximity

rule: Add comments to explain non-standard patterns or conventions (e.g., sort direction encoding) that differ from the usual codebase patterns.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation convention for non-standard patterns

rule: Do not wrap JSX in a React Fragment (`<>...</>` or `<Fragment>`) when it is not needed (e.g., when there is only one child element).
Order imports with external/package imports above relative imports, and keep import groups sorted consistently.
mode_rationale: Simple import ordering convention

rule: Always provide visible UI feedback (e.g., error message, toast, or inline banner) when an API or service call fails, rather than silently swallowing errors.
mode_rationale: Single UX guideline about error visibility

rule: Replace hard-coded numeric values (magic numbers) with named constants to improve readability and maintainability.
Pin exact versions for new dependencies in package.json rather than using semver ranges to ensure reproducible builds.
mode_rationale: Single dependency management rule

rule: Scrutinize yarn.lock or package-lock.json changes in PRs to ensure they are intentional and necessary, not accidental artifacts.
mode_rationale: Simple PR review guideline

rule: Always pass the caught error object to error utility functions like `getErrorMessage(err)` so they can extract meaningful information.
mode_rationale: Single error handling convention

rule: When adding validation or guardrails to one code path, apply the same guardrails to all analogous code paths that perform similar operations.
mode_rationale: Single consistency rule about applying guardrails uniformly

rule: Keep CODEOWNERS and directory ownership assignments aligned with the team that actually owns the code; reassign ownership when code migrates between teams.
mode_rationale: Simple governance rule about CODEOWNERS accuracy

rule: Maintain a consistent error response format (e.g., JSON with an 'error' key) across all endpoints; do not regress structured error responses to raw strings.
mode_rationale: Single API convention about error response structure

rule: Add scaladocs or doc comments to non-trivial private methods, especially when their role in the request lifecycle is not immediately obvious from the signature.
mode_rationale: Single convention: add docs to non-trivial private methods

rule: Within a module, use a single error handling strategy (exceptions OR Either/Try) consistently across analogous methods rather than mixing approaches.
mode_rationale: Single convention: don't mix error handling approaches within a module

rule: Do not silently mask parsing or authentication errors by falling through to an alternative method; surface the error as a BadRequest or Unauthorized response.
mode_rationale: Single convention: surface errors instead of silently falling through

rule: When a parsing function can throw exceptions, ensure all call sites that invoke similar parsing handle the same exceptions consistently.
mode_rationale: Single convention: handle exceptions consistently across all call sites

rule: When a constructor or factory method already handles edge cases (e.g., single-element sequences), remove redundant conditional branching that duplicates that logic.
mode_rationale: Single convention: remove redundant conditionals when constructors handle edge cases

rule: When a method name repeats context already implied by the class or surrounding scope, shorten it for readability.
mode_rationale: Single convention: shorten method names that repeat class context

rule: Telemetry events must include the complete set of entity identifiers for the target; if the event targets a versioned resource, include both the resource ID and the version ID.
mode_rationale: Single convention: always include complete entity identifiers in telemetry events

rule: Place domain-to-DTO conversion logic in the API/DTO layer (e.g., `fromServiceDomain` on the DTO), not as `toApi` methods on internal domain models, to keep the dependency arrow pointing inward and avoid recompiling internals when API models change.
mode_rationale: Single convention: place DTO conversion in the API layer, not domain models

rule: Do not force all API collection responses to include a totalCount; only include it when the pagination strategy (e.g., page-number-based) requires it, since cursor-based pagination does not need it and computing totalCount can be expensive.
mode_rationale: Single convention: only include totalCount when pagination strategy requires it

rule: Prefer hand-crafted test cases with explicit corner cases over random data generators, which are a major source of test flakiness and do not reliably cover edge cases like zero, negative numbers, or empty inputs.
mode_rationale: Single convention: prefer explicit test cases over random generators

rule: When parsing request bodies in controllers, defensively handle missing or malformed bodies (e.g., wrap in `Try(...).toOption`) to avoid unexpected 500 errors on requests that legitimately have no body.
mode_rationale: Single convention: defensively handle missing or malformed request bodies

rule: Break large code blocks into private methods with meaningful names to improve readability, so that callers can understand the logic without navigating into each method's implementation.
mode_rationale: Single convention: break large blocks into named private methods

rule: When splitting or destructuring strings/arrays into expected parts, validate the resulting length matches expectations before accessing indices to prevent silent bugs from malformed input.
mode_rationale: Single convention: validate length before accessing indices

rule: When adding functionality to an existing component that doesn't align with its primary purpose, extract the new functionality into its own dedicated component to preserve single responsibility.
mode_rationale: Single convention: extract unrelated functionality into dedicated components

rule: When comparing or sorting date values from API responses, parse them into proper date objects using dayjs rather than comparing raw strings, to avoid bugs if the backend changes the date format.
mode_rationale: Single convention: parse dates with dayjs instead of comparing raw strings

rule: Use the project's custom design-system components instead of importing raw Ant Design components directly, to maintain visual consistency and leverage project-specific customizations.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use project design-system components over raw Ant Design

rule: When a value can only be one of a known finite set (e.g., status filters like 'Open' | 'Resolved'), define a union type or enum rather than using raw strings, to get type safety and a single source of truth.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use union types/enums instead of raw strings for finite sets

rule: Keep React components thin by moving data-fetching, error handling, and state logic into context providers, exposing only methods and state to components.
mode_rationale: Single convention: keep components thin by moving logic to context providers

rule: Extract styled components out of feature component files and into a dedicated styles file to keep component files focused on logic and rendering.
mode_rationale: Single convention: extract styled components into a dedicated styles file

rule: Wrap object or array literals created inside React component render paths with useMemo to avoid creating new instances on every re-render.
mode_rationale: Single convention: wrap render-path object literals in useMemo

rule: Sort operations and other expensive transformations in render should be memoized or moved to a context/hook so they don't recompute on every re-render.
mode_rationale: Single convention: memoize or hoist expensive transformations out of render

rule: Do not import utility libraries like Ramda for operations that native JavaScript handles equally well (map, filter, etc.); prefer built-in array methods to reduce bundle size and cognitive overhead.
mode_rationale: Single convention: prefer native JS methods over utility libraries like Ramda

rule: Never call .toString on an Option type to extract its value; use .getOrElse, pattern matching, or .map to avoid producing stringified wrapper values like "None" or "Some(value)" instead of the inner value.
mode_rationale: Single convention: never call .toString on Option; use getOrElse or pattern match

rule: When accepting optional filter parameters as Option[String], guard against the string literal "None" leaking through as a valid value by validating or filtering before use.
mode_rationale: Single convention: guard against literal "None" string leaking through Option[String]

rule: Public API documentation should describe how to use filter parameters to retrieve specific subsets of data, not just list the parameters.
mode_rationale: Single convention: describe how to use filter params, not just list them

rule: When blocklisting or allowlisting file types, include all related format variants (e.g., all Office formats: doc, docx, ppt, pptx, xls, xlsx, rtf) rather than just the one that triggered the ticket.
mode_rationale: Single convention: include all related format variants in block/allowlists

rule: Avoid deep nesting selectors (e.g., `&&&&&`) to override library styles; prefer `!important` or targeted overrides when overriding Ant Design styles.
mode_rationale: Single convention: avoid deep nesting selectors to override library styles

rule: Define semantic color constants (e.g., neutral, warning, success) instead of using inline hex or color values throughout component code.
mode_rationale: Single convention: define semantic color constants instead of inline hex values

rule: Create Styled components instead of using inline styles for consistent, maintainable styling.
Pass context methods directly to child components when the context already memoizes them, instead of wrapping in an additional useCallback.
mode_rationale: Single convention: pass already-memoized context methods directly

rule: When an if-block contains a return statement, use a standalone if instead of an else block to reduce code complexity.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use standalone if instead of else after return

rule: Prefer using built-in component properties (e.g., `isExternalLink`) rather than implementing custom solutions for behavior the component already supports.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use built-in component properties before custom solutions

rule: Ensure import statements reference the exact filename on disk, including casing and spelling, to prevent build errors and confusion.
mode_rationale: Single convention: import paths must match exact filename casing and spelling

rule: Review filenames for spelling errors before committing, especially for story files and test files that may not cause build failures.
mode_rationale: Single convention: review filenames for spelling errors before committing

rule: Do not wrap values in useMemo unless they are passed as props or used as dependencies in useCallback/useMemo/useEffect hooks.
mode_rationale: Single convention: only useMemo values passed as props or used as dependencies

rule: Order import statements with external/library imports first, followed by relative/local imports, separated by a blank line.
mode_rationale: Single convention: external imports first, then relative imports

rule: Use the project's spacing utility functions (e.g., `getMargin`) for margins and padding instead of hardcoded CSS values to maintain consistency.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use project spacing utilities instead of hardcoded CSS values

rule: Never leave data-test attributes empty; always provide a meaningful, descriptive test identifier.
mode_rationale: Single convention: always provide meaningful data-test attribute values

rule: Return null instead of rendering an empty div when a React component has nothing to display.
mode_rationale: Single convention: return null instead of empty div when nothing to display

rule: Return children directly instead of wrapping them in an unnecessary React fragment.
mode_rationale: Single convention: return children directly without unnecessary fragments

rule: Use built-in component props and APIs (e.g., styled-components .attrs()) before writing custom CSS overrides.
Extract commonly useful UI components to a shared/global location instead of keeping them module-local.
mode_rationale: Single convention: extract commonly useful components to shared location

rule: Apply theme or style overrides at the most local scope possible rather than modifying global providers, to avoid unintended side effects.
mode_rationale: Single convention: apply theme overrides at local scope, not global

rule: Avoid CSS !important; increase specificity through selector nesting or repetition instead.
mode_rationale: Single convention: increase specificity instead of using !important

rule: Import from specific module paths within a package instead of the root index to prevent circular dependency cycles.
mode_rationale: Single convention: import from specific module paths to prevent circular deps

rule: Remove unused files and dead code rather than leaving them in the codebase alongside their replacements.
mode_rationale: Single convention: remove unused files and dead code

rule: Extract repeated style values (e.g., icon size objects) into named constants to reduce duplication and improve maintainability.
mode_rationale: Single convention: extract repeated style values into named constants

rule: Use a component's built-in props via styled-components .attrs() instead of manually overriding styles with custom CSS.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use .attrs() instead of manual style overrides

rule: Order imports with absolute/package imports before relative imports.
Apply feature flag checks at the specific endpoint or component boundary where the feature is used, not at a coarse wrapper level.
mode_rationale: Single convention: apply feature flags at specific boundaries, not coarse wrappers

rule: Before defining new constants or lists (e.g., filename patterns), check the codebase for existing definitions and reuse them to avoid drift.
Invalid or unmatched routes should render a 404 page, not silently redirect to another section of the app.
mode_rationale: Simple routing convention: render 404 for invalid routes

rule: When displaying conditional content based on async data, add a loading-state guard (e.g., `hasLoaded && !data`) to prevent a flash of the 'not found' view while data is still being fetched.
mode_rationale: Single guard condition pattern to prevent flash of not-found state

rule: Use Ant Design Select's `options` array prop instead of manually rendering `<Option>` children for a simpler, more declarative approach.
mode_rationale: Simple preference for declarative options prop over manual children

rule: Use Ant Design Form instances and structured arrays of objects for form state instead of loose individual state variables.
mode_rationale: Convention to use Form instances instead of loose state variables

rule: Wrap event handler functions passed as props in `useCallback` to prevent unnecessary re-renders of child components.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence React performance convention

rule: Avoid using `any` in TypeScript; define proper types or use `unknown` to maintain type safety.
mode_rationale: Simple type safety rule to avoid any

rule: Reset pagination to page 1 whenever the user changes a filter, search query, or sort order to avoid showing an empty page.
mode_rationale: Single behavioral rule: reset page on filter change

rule: Use case-insensitive comparison (e.g., `.toLowerCase()`) when matching user-facing strings like labels, names, or identifiers that may vary in casing.
mode_rationale: Simple comparison convention for user-facing strings

rule: Combine equivalent conditional branches using logical OR instead of duplicating the same return value across multiple conditions.
mode_rationale: Code style rule to combine equivalent branches

rule: Do not set a sort direction without a corresponding sort key; ensure sort-related state fields are always logically consistent.
mode_rationale: Simple invariant: sort direction requires sort key

rule: Use the platform's standard page size options (e.g., 50, 100, 200) for tables; non-standard values like 5 may not appear in the pagination control's dropdown.
mode_rationale: Convention to use platform-standard page sizes

rule: Telemetry events must target the correct entity type — if the operation acts on a Model API, extend the Model API event base, not the Model API Version event base.
mode_rationale: Simple correctness rule for telemetry event targeting

rule: Do not introduce base classes or traits that add little value or that each subclass must immediately override — they obscure intent and introduce bugs.
mode_rationale: Design convention against unnecessary base classes

rule: Telemetry events should contain only data useful for analysis — exclude descriptions, large lists, and other fields that won't be queried or aggregated.
mode_rationale: Simple filtering rule for telemetry event payloads

rule: When a telemetry field would contain a large or unbounded list, record an aggregate (e.g. count) instead of the full list.
mode_rationale: Single convention to use counts instead of full lists

rule: All sensitive fields (names, file paths, function names) in telemetry events must be redacted before recording.
mode_rationale: Security convention: always redact sensitive telemetry fields

rule: Entity types in event targets must use predefined constants (e.g. EventTarget.Project) rather than inline string literals.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention to use constants over string literals

rule: Record exactly one telemetry event per service operation — do not emit an event per item in a list result.
mode_rationale: Single cardinality rule for telemetry events

rule: Pick one approach for composing event behavior — either override methods directly or use trait mixins — and apply it consistently; do not mix both in a way that cancels inherited behavior.
mode_rationale: Consistency convention: don't mix inheritance approaches

rule: Utility or factory methods related to a type should be placed in its companion object, not in unrelated serializer or service classes.
mode_rationale: Code organization convention for utility method placement

rule: In distributed leader election, fall back to a meaningful node identifier (e.g. pod name via DOMINO_NODE_NAME) so the current lease holder is easily identifiable.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for lease holder identifiers

rule: Leader election should be scoped per service mode (e.g. nucleus mode) rather than electing across all pods and modes, for predictability in where background tasks run.
mode_rationale: Architecture convention for leader election scope

rule: Mutable state accessed by multiple threads must be synchronized using an appropriate lock (e.g., ReentrantReadWriteLock), not left unprotected.
mode_rationale: Fundamental concurrency safety rule

rule: Create separate DTO classes for API responses rather than reusing internal domain models, to prevent cross-team breakage and keep API contracts independent of internal refactors.
mode_rationale: Architecture convention to separate API DTOs from domain models

rule: General-purpose UI components (e.g., DynamicFieldDisplay) should never be aware of or consume feature-specific React context providers; feature-specific state should be managed outside the general-purpose component boundary.
mode_rationale: Component boundary rule: no feature-specific context in general components

rule: Always reference colors through the theme helper rather than hardcoding color values in component styles.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to use theme helpers instead of hardcoded colors

rule: Summarized or aggregated API data should be fetched once and cached in the provider, not re-fetched on every render or component mount.
mode_rationale: Performance convention: fetch once and cache in provider

rule: When multiple operations are always performed together (e.g., fetch data + set state), encapsulate them into a single method to reduce coupling and improve readability.
mode_rationale: Code organization convention to group related operations

rule: When a function's return value is not self-explanatory, either keep a clarifying comment or create a named type to make the return shape explicit.
mode_rationale: Documentation convention for unclear return values

rule: When a shared UI component (e.g., a table) needs style adjustments, make the change in the shared component itself rather than applying CSS overrides from consuming components.
mode_rationale: Simple ownership rule for style changes

rule: Pagination should be handled consistently either entirely on the backend or entirely on the frontend; hybrid approaches where some sort keys paginate server-side and others client-side create unpredictable behavior and should be avoided.
mode_rationale: Architecture convention: pagination must be handled in one layer

rule: When a search or filter parameter changes, reset the pagination offset to zero because the previous page position may no longer exist in the filtered result set.
mode_rationale: Single behavioral rule: reset offset on filter change

rule: Hardcoded layout dimensions (e.g., topbar height) that are referenced across multiple components should be defined as shared constants or exposed by the owning component.
mode_rationale: Convention to extract shared dimensions into constants

rule: When a UI element toggles between two directional states (e.g., expand/collapse), use a single icon and rotate it with a CSS transition rather than swapping between two separate icon components.
mode_rationale: UI convention: rotate one icon instead of swapping two

rule: Set SVG fill attributes to `currentColor` so the icon inherits its color from the parent element's font color, making icons reusable across different color contexts.
mode_rationale: Simple SVG convention for color inheritance

rule: Remove all console.log statements (including commented-out ones) before submitting a PR for review; they should not be committed to the codebase.
mode_rationale: Hygiene rule: no console.log in committed code

rule: Navigation identifiers and keys that may be needed for API calls should be defined in a separate data structure rather than inlined in UI components, enabling reuse across UI and API layers.
mode_rationale: Convention to externalize navigation keys for reuse

rule: Colors should come from the design system palette or theme rather than being hardcoded; if a semantic color token doesn't exist, request one rather than embedding raw hex values.
mode_rationale: Convention to use design system colors, not raw hex values

rule: Name components specifically enough to distinguish them from other similar components in the codebase (e.g., `PlatformNavBar` instead of generic `TopBar` when multiple top bars exist).
mode_rationale: Naming convention for component specificity

rule: When accessing theme values, use the existing utility hooks (e.g., `useThemeColor`) rather than accessing theme objects directly, to maintain consistency and simplify future refactors.
mode_rationale: Convention to use utility hooks for theme access

rule: Don't manually pick individual properties from an object to pass as props when the extra properties would simply be ignored by the receiving component; pass the object directly to reduce boilerplate.
mode_rationale: Code style rule against redundant prop picking

rule: Remove placeholder strings, test artifacts, and debug-only code (e.g., hardcoded debug labels like 'ROUTER LINK') before submitting a PR.
mode_rationale: Hygiene rule: no debug artifacts in committed code

rule: Review and remove stale TODO comments that are no longer valid before merging; leftover TODOs create confusion about whether the work is actually incomplete.
Boolean component props should have explicit default values (typically `false`) so callers don't need to specify them when the default behavior is desired.
mode_rationale: API design convention for boolean prop defaults

rule: When a utility function exists for a layout calculation (e.g., `getMargin`), use it instead of writing inline calculations to maintain consistency and single-source-of-truth.
mode_rationale: Convention to reuse existing layout utilities

rule: Functions with multiple parameters should accept a single object argument instead of positional parameters to improve readability and make the call site self-documenting.
mode_rationale: API design convention for multi-parameter functions

rule: Keep PRs focused by rolling back unrelated or accidental changes to files that are not part of the feature being implemented.
mode_rationale: PR hygiene rule: keep changes focused

rule: Reference colors through the theme or design token system rather than importing and using color constants directly.
mode_rationale: Convention to use theme tokens over color constants

rule: Analytics/tracking event names should follow a consistent naming pattern across the codebase; do not add ad-hoc qualifiers to some events that are absent from others.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for analytics event consistency

rule: Use Array.some() instead of manual iteration or reduce when checking whether any element in a collection satisfies a condition.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence coding convention about which array method to use

rule: Hard-coded limits and thresholds (e.g., max page fetch counts) should be extracted into named configuration values so they can be tuned without code changes.
mode_rationale: Simple rule to externalize hard-coded values

rule: Never assume the default branch is called 'main'; use the API to fetch the repository's actual default branch.
mode_rationale: Single rule about always fetching the actual default branch

rule: Use ES6 template literals (backticks) instead of the + operator when concatenating strings in JavaScript/TypeScript.
mode_rationale: Simple style convention for string interpolation

rule: When determining active navigation state, use prefix matching (e.g., startsWith) rather than exact URL equality to correctly highlight parent menu items for nested/detail routes.
mode_rationale: Single convention about using prefix matching for nav state

rule: When multiple code paths can trigger similar behavior, make test assertions specific enough (e.g., checking message content or parameters) to verify the expected path was exercised, not just that a side effect occurred.
mode_rationale: Single testing principle about assertion specificity

rule: Before implementing common logic (e.g., pluralization, formatting), check whether an existing utility already handles the case to avoid duplication and inconsistency.
Never commit secrets, tokens, or credentials to source code — even for local testing purposes.
mode_rationale: Blanket security rule — never commit secrets

rule: Before building custom UI solutions, check whether the component library already exposes a prop or slot for the use case.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to check library APIs before building custom solutions

rule: Use the most specific TypeScript type available instead of defaulting to `string`; review AI-generated code for type accuracy.
mode_rationale: Single rule about using specific types over string

rule: Use full, descriptive words in type and file names instead of abbreviations — names should be self-explanatory without requiring team knowledge.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention

rule: Use cursor-based pagination (not offset-based) when aggregating results from multiple data sources that need cross-source sorting.
mode_rationale: Single architectural guideline about pagination strategy

rule: Prefer a generic, homogeneous representation for API response types over proliferating per-variant subtypes — work with design to simplify requirements if needed.
mode_rationale: Single API design preference for generic response types

rule: Be mindful of Scala case class field limits (22 fields) when designing DTOs — use nested groupings or polymorphism to stay under the limit.
mode_rationale: Single awareness rule about Scala 22-field limit

rule: When the old version of a module is removed, rename the V2/V3 successor to drop the version suffix from interface names and file names.
mode_rationale: Simple naming cleanup rule after removing old versions

rule: Name methods to reflect their actual behavior — use 'update' when the entity must already exist, not 'upsert'.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention about matching behavior

rule: Return the semantically correct HTTP status code — use 404 when a resource is not found, not 400.
mode_rationale: Simple rule about using semantically correct status codes

rule: Avoid mapping the same source value to multiple DTO fields — each field should represent a distinct piece of data.
mode_rationale: Single data modeling rule against duplicate mappings

rule: When using setTimeout or other timing hacks, add a comment explaining why the timeout is needed.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation convention for timing hacks

rule: Extract complex mapping or transformation logic out of form submit handlers into separate utility functions to keep the handler readable.
mode_rationale: Single refactoring convention for handler readability

rule: Limit the use of `as any` type casts in TypeScript; prefer proper typing or generics to maintain type safety.
mode_rationale: Simple type safety rule against as-any casts

rule: Always log errors in catch blocks so failures are observable and debuggable.
mode_rationale: Single logging convention for observability

rule: Before writing new handler logic, check if an existing handler can be reused or extended with minor changes to avoid duplication.
mode_rationale: Simple DRY convention for handlers

rule: Wrap any function defined inside a React component with `useCallback` to prevent unnecessary re-creation on each render.
mode_rationale: Single React performance convention

rule: When forced to use `any` due to pending backend API changes, add a TODO comment referencing when it should be fixed.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation rule for tracking temporary type workarounds

rule: When an async operation can fail and the UI state depends on it (e.g., form gets locked), always show user-visible feedback explaining the failure.
mode_rationale: Single UX rule about surfacing async errors

rule: Do not show error toasts for form validation failures; rely on inline field-level validation feedback instead.
mode_rationale: Single UI convention for form error placement

rule: Before publishing a new API, evaluate whether upcoming dependency upgrades will require breaking changes, and use API versioning to manage transitions.
mode_rationale: Single API design principle about versioning

rule: Add inline comments explaining what non-obvious parameters do, especially for aggregation or query configurations, so future developers can understand intent quickly.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation convention for query configs

rule: Use react-router's `matchPath` (or equivalent framework utilities) to extract route params instead of manually parsing URLs.
mode_rationale: Single convention to use framework utilities over manual parsing

rule: Never commit valid JWT tokens, API keys, or other secrets to the codebase, even in test or mock files.
mode_rationale: Blanket security rule — same as no-secrets-in-source

rule: Components likely to be reused across modules should be placed in the shared packages/ui library rather than nested in a feature-specific directory.
mode_rationale: Single code organization rule about component placement

rule: When modifying a shared component, check all existing usages across the codebase to ensure changes don't break other consumers.
mode_rationale: Single safety convention when touching shared code

rule: Keep authorization (authZ) and authentication (authN) as separate concerns; place authN-related logic in the authn-adapter module, not in the authZ interface.
mode_rationale: Single architectural separation-of-concerns rule

rule: When a PR touches code owned by another team, tag a specific accountable person from that team and do not merge until they explicitly approve.
mode_rationale: Single process rule about cross-team review

rule: Authorizer methods must return an AuthorizationResult type, not a plain boolean; do not accommodate or work around authorizers that return unsupported types.
mode_rationale: Single type contract rule for authorizer return types

rule: When code needs future revisiting, create a Jira ticket and reference it in the comment rather than leaving open-ended TODO comments that will be forgotten.
mode_rationale: Single process convention for tracking debt

rule: Remove unused method parameters (such as implicit DominoContext) rather than keeping them speculatively for possible future use; reduce boilerplate over speculative compatibility.

When multiple action classes need to extract the same values from a JSON context (e.g., projectId), define a shared case class with Json macros in a companion object rather than duplicating extraction logic across each Actions class.
mode_rationale: Single DRY rule for shared JSON extraction

rule: When a framework requires boilerplate for the common case (e.g., actions that don't need a context parameter), provide a simplified method signature to avoid unnecessary dependencies and placeholder underscores.
mode_rationale: Single API design principle to reduce boilerplate

rule: When modifying API schemas, CRDs, or enum definitions, verify that dependent UIs still render correctly before merging.
mode_rationale: Single testing convention to check dependent UIs

rule: When building abstractions that will serve multiple use cases, name them for the general capability (e.g., 'LogRetriever') rather than the first specific use case (e.g., 'BuildLogRetriever').
mode_rationale: Single naming convention for reusable abstractions

rule: API controller methods and endpoint names should reflect the general capability, not just the first feature that uses them, to avoid renaming when scope expands.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention for API endpoints

rule: If code introduces a dependency or integration that isn't tested or currently intended for use, either remove it or add tests — don't ship dead code that may silently fail in production.
mode_rationale: Single rule against shipping untested dead code

rule: When renaming or restructuring fields in a persisted data model (e.g., MongoDB documents), provide a runnable migration script for upgrading existing deployments.
mode_rationale: Multi-step workflow: assess impact, write migration script, test on existing data, deploy

rule: When code is no longer needed for the current or foreseeable future use case, remove it rather than leaving it in place.
mode_rationale: Simple cleanup convention

rule: Only use Promise.all when resolving multiple concurrent promises; wrapping a single promise in Promise.all adds unnecessary complexity.
mode_rationale: Single code simplicity rule

rule: When setting initial/default values for async state (e.g., React state or query results), ensure the initial shape matches what the resolver actually returns to avoid runtime type mismatches.
mode_rationale: Single type consistency rule for async state

rule: When code has fallback behavior (e.g., returning 'Unknown' when a lookup fails), add explicit unit tests covering those edge cases.
mode_rationale: Single testing convention for fallback paths

rule: Use optional chaining (?.) when accessing nested properties on objects that may be null or undefined, especially for feature flag checks and user/principal objects.
mode_rationale: Single defensive coding convention

rule: Prefer existing shared UI components (e.g., TooltipWrapper) over re-implementing common UI patterns inline, as they improve legibility and consistency.
mode_rationale: Single convention: prefer shared components over inline reimplementations

rule: Never consume fontSizes, colors, or other design tokens directly; always access them through the themeHelper utility to ensure theming consistency and whitelabel support.
mode_rationale: Single convention: always use themeHelper for design tokens

rule: Never hardcode the application name (e.g., 'Domino'); use the getAppName utility with whitelabelSettings from useStore to support whitelabelling.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use getAppName utility instead of hardcoded strings

rule: When styling elements that function as links, use palette.primary from the theme system rather than arbitrary color values.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use theme palette for link colors

rule: Do not add all component props as dependencies to useCallback, as this defeats the purpose of memoization — the function will be regenerated on every re-render just as if useCallback were not used.
mode_rationale: Single convention: don't list all props as useCallback dependencies

rule: API calls that can throw exceptions should be wrapped in try-catch blocks to handle errors gracefully instead of letting them propagate unhandled.
mode_rationale: Single convention: wrap API calls in try-catch

rule: When async data is used to guard destructive actions (e.g., allowing deletion), handle loading and error states explicitly — a falsy default during fetch or on failure can incorrectly enable the action.
mode_rationale: Single convention: handle loading/error states when async data guards destructive actions

rule: Hardcoded numeric values (e.g., pixel dimensions) should be extracted into named constants to communicate intent and make them easier to maintain.
When an operation (especially an API call or async action) can fail, provide visible feedback to the user rather than failing silently.
mode_rationale: Single convention: show visible feedback on failure

rule: When logging telemetry or audit trail events, include human-readable names (e.g., policy name, project name) alongside ID fields to improve end-user usability of audit logs.
mode_rationale: Single convention: include readable names in audit events

rule: Name boolean variables and props with an `is` prefix (e.g., `isAddResource`, `isDuplicate`, `isInvisibleButton`) to clearly communicate their type and intent.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention: prefix booleans with 'is'

rule: Refactor deeply nested ternary expressions into early returns, if/else blocks, or extracted helper functions to improve readability.
mode_rationale: Single convention: refactor nested ternaries for readability

rule: When a form or modal depends on pre-loaded data, show an error message and prevent the user from interacting with an empty or broken form if the data load fails.
mode_rationale: Single convention: show error and block interaction when data load fails

rule: Remove all debug print/println/console.log statements before merging; use proper logging frameworks instead.
mode_rationale: Single convention: remove debug statements before merging

rule: When modifying existing logic paths, explicitly verify that upstream invariants still hold (e.g., guaranteed cardinality, non-null results) to avoid breaking dependent use cases.
mode_rationale: Single convention: verify upstream invariants still hold when modifying logic

rule: Tests should clearly communicate their setup, action, and expected outcome so that a reviewer can understand the scenario without reading the implementation.
mode_rationale: Single convention: test descriptions should communicate setup, action, and outcome

rule: Authorization should run at the top of a service method before any business logic executes, and must cover all code paths — avoid conditional auth that can be bypassed if no condition matches.
mode_rationale: Single convention: auth checks run first, covering all paths

rule: In PATCH/update DTOs, make all fields optional so that `None` means 'do not update this field', keeping persistence logic simple and avoiding unintended dependencies between fields.
mode_rationale: Single convention: make PATCH DTO fields optional

rule: Do not implement manual undo/rollback logic for non-transactional multi-step operations — concurrent requests can cause the rollback to undo another request's work. Instead, make operations idempotent and easy to retry.
mode_rationale: Single convention: prefer idempotent retry over manual rollback

rule: Use PATCH for partial updates and POST for creation — do not use POST for update endpoints.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use PATCH for updates, POST for creation

rule: Use the correct domain-specific term (e.g., 'Searchable' instead of 'Public' for project visibility within Domino) to avoid ambiguity and misinterpretation.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use correct domain terms to avoid ambiguity

rule: Do not leave commented-out code in the codebase; remove it and rely on version control for history.
Before writing new utility logic, check if an equivalent function already exists in the codebase's shared utility modules and reuse it.
Global/shared components must never depend on feature-scoped components; if a feature-specific component is needed by a shared component, extract it into the common layer first.
mode_rationale: Single convention: shared components never depend on feature-scoped code

rule: Don't add components to a shared abstraction (e.g., DynamicField) unless they genuinely fit the abstraction's purpose; forcing unrelated components into a pattern erodes the abstraction's meaning.
mode_rationale: Single convention: only add components that fit an abstraction's purpose

rule: API schema fields with conditional behavior (e.g., presence determines create vs. update) must include documentation explaining the semantics and when each value is expected.
mode_rationale: Single convention: document fields with conditional behavior

rule: When dead or unused code is identified during review, create a cleanup ticket to remove it rather than leaving it in the codebase undocumented.
mode_rationale: Single convention: create tickets for dead code instead of leaving it

rule: Combine multiple import statements from the same module into a single import statement.
mode_rationale: Single convention: combine imports from the same module

rule: Add explicit TypeScript type annotations even when types can be inferred, especially for function parameters and variables used across boundaries.
mode_rationale: Single convention: add explicit TypeScript type annotations

rule: When changing file-handling code, verify behavior with file names containing spaces and special characters, and understand existing workarounds (like double-encoding) before modifying them.
mode_rationale: Single convention: test file handling with special characters

rule: When removing a feature from one surface, audit all downstream consumers (e.g., PDF reports, exports, APIs) to ensure the removal is complete and consistent.
mode_rationale: Single convention: audit all consumers when removing a feature

rule: Use specific, prefixed CSS class names scoped to their feature or page to avoid style collisions with other parts of the application.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use prefixed CSS class names to prevent collisions

rule: When the same condition is evaluated in multiple places, extract it into a descriptively named boolean constant for readability and maintainability.
mode_rationale: Single convention: extract repeated conditions into named booleans

rule: Replace magic numbers with named constants that describe their purpose or the component they relate to.
mode_rationale: Single convention: replace magic numbers with named constants

rule: Use TypeScript discriminated unions and type guards (e.g., checking a `type` field) to narrow types instead of manual type assertions or casting.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use discriminated unions instead of type assertions

rule: Move nested ternary expressions out of JSX and into utility functions or helper variables for readability and maintainability.
mode_rationale: Single convention: move nested ternaries out of JSX

rule: Ensure data-test attributes and form names are consistent and clearly related to the component or feature they belong to.
mode_rationale: Single convention: data-test attributes should match component names

rule: Always use the `getAppName` utility instead of hardcoding the application name, to support white-labeling.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use getAppName for white-label support

rule: Remove commented-out code before merging; if it may be needed later, track it in a ticket rather than leaving dead code in the codebase.
mode_rationale: Single convention: remove commented-out code before merging

rule: Ensure error messages accurately describe the operation that failed, not a different or parent operation.
mode_rationale: Single convention: error messages must describe the actual failed operation

rule: Do not commit empty files to the repository; remove or populate them before merging.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: don't commit empty files

rule: Never suppress or omit React hook dependency warnings; instead, stabilize function references (e.g., via useCallback or moving definitions outside the component) so all dependencies can be listed.
mode_rationale: Single convention: never suppress React hook dependency warnings

rule: Always use proper TypeScript types for data structures; avoid loose typing (e.g., any, unknown, or untyped objects) that could mask real errors.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use proper TypeScript types, avoid any/unknown

rule: Avoid @ts-ignore directives; fix the underlying type issue instead of suppressing it.
mode_rationale: Single convention: fix type issues instead of using @ts-ignore

rule: Do not accept parameters that can be derived from other parameters; compute them inside the method to prevent caller misuse.
mode_rationale: Single convention: compute derivable values inside the method

rule: Use the same field/property name across all layers (API DTOs, service layer, database, permissions) so that read and write interfaces stay consistent and predictable.
mode_rationale: Single convention: same field names across API, service, DB layers

rule: Prefer a concrete default value (e.g., false) over Optional/None when the absence of a value is semantically equivalent to a specific value, to avoid forcing callers to handle three states.
mode_rationale: Single convention: prefer concrete defaults over Optional when absence equals a specific value

rule: Grant permissions only to the roles that genuinely need them; service-level operations (e.g., archiving backing resources) should be restricted to service principals, not user-facing roles.
mode_rationale: Single convention: restrict permissions to roles that need them

rule: When renaming a concept, update all related constants, action names, and system/integration tests to maintain consistency end-to-end.
mode_rationale: Single convention: update all related names and tests when renaming a concept

rule: When a domain concept is renamed, remove all variations of the old term from the codebase to prevent confusion between the old and new mental models.
mode_rationale: Single convention: remove all old term variations after a rename

rule: Use data validation logic to prevent state combinations that lead to unrecoverable situations, rather than restricting actions per-role in ways that overfit specific requirements.
mode_rationale: Single design principle about preferring data validation over role-based action restrictions

rule: Use existing authorization primitives (e.g., PrivateRoute's built-in 403 handling) instead of implementing custom authorization checks that duplicate framework functionality.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to use built-in auth primitives instead of custom duplicates

rule: When modifying core shared components used across the application, run the full end-to-end test suite to verify no downstream breakage.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence testing guideline triggered by modifying shared components

rule: When gating UI elements behind permission checks, also suppress the underlying API requests to avoid unnecessary errors and unauthorized network calls.
mode_rationale: Simple rule to suppress network calls alongside UI gating

rule: Use authorization APIs directly instead of wrapping them in redundant conditional expressions that add no logic.
mode_rationale: Simple code style rule about avoiding redundant conditionals around auth APIs

rule: Name getter-style functions with a prefix that describes what they return (e.g., getButtonType) rather than vague or action-oriented names.
In audit/telemetry events, the target should be the entity that receives the verb's action (e.g., the file being downloaded), not the container or context (e.g., the repo).
mode_rationale: Simple modeling rule for audit event targets

rule: View and download events should not use fieldChanges or scalar change tracking, since the entity was not modified — only observed.
mode_rationale: Simple constraint: read-only events should not track field changes

rule: When a domain concept (e.g., feature flags) appears in multiple event types, model it the same way (same target type, same field structure) for consistency and queryability.
mode_rationale: Consistency convention for domain concept modeling in events

rule: Define button and element styles as their own styled components rather than nesting class selectors inside a parent styled component; reserve parent-level nesting only for state-dependent styles like hover.
mode_rationale: CSS-in-JS style convention about component structure

rule: Review diffs for auto-generated files (timestamped configs, build artifacts, IDE files) and exclude them from commits.
mode_rationale: Simple hygiene rule to exclude generated files from commits

rule: When modifying a shared data schema or format, identify and update all dependent components that consume or persist that data.
mode_rationale: Single principle about propagating schema changes to dependents

rule: Do not rename tests that cover a different method than the one being changed — test names should accurately describe which method or behavior they verify.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for test methods

rule: Always add aria-label attributes to icon-only buttons to maintain accessibility score.
mode_rationale: Single accessibility rule for icon-only buttons

rule: Use the established shared layout components (e.g., AppContent) for page structure instead of creating custom one-off layouts, to maintain consistency across the application.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to reuse established layout components

rule: When adding filter-based endpoints, write tests that verify each filter value returns the correct subset, and that omitting the filter returns all expected results.
mode_rationale: Single testing guideline for filter-based endpoints

rule: When changing a field's type (e.g., from required to optional), explicitly assess the impact on downstream event consumers and integration tests before merging.
mode_rationale: Single review checkpoint before merging type changes

rule: Test fixtures should use the semantically correct domain type for each entity rather than a convenient substitute that doesn't match the real domain model.
mode_rationale: Simple rule about using semantically correct types in test fixtures

rule: Mark helper classes and methods as private when they are only used within the enclosing class or file.
mode_rationale: Simple encapsulation convention for helper classes

rule: Order import statements with absolute/package imports before relative imports.
mode_rationale: Simple import ordering convention

rule: In data-fetching hooks that feed tables or lists, track a hasLoaded state to distinguish 'never loaded' from 'loaded but empty', preventing a flash of the empty state on initial render.
mode_rationale: Single UI pattern to distinguish never-loaded from empty

rule: When adding action buttons (e.g., download) in a section that already has similar buttons, use the same icon collection and style for visual consistency.
mode_rationale: Visual consistency rule for action buttons in a section

rule: Table empty state components should include a maxContentWidth prop to prevent the empty message from stretching across the full table width.
mode_rationale: Simple UI prop convention for table empty states

rule: Before defining an ad-hoc type or interface, check if an existing domain type (e.g., ArtifactFile) already models the data; reuse it to reduce duplication and drift.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to check for existing types before defining new ones

rule: Use theme color tokens instead of hardcoded hex values for font colors and other styling, even when matching a third-party library's colors.
mode_rationale: Simple styling rule to use theme tokens

rule: Do not disable or ignore accessibility lint rules; fix the underlying issue to maintain the accessibility score.
mode_rationale: Simple rule against disabling a11y lint rules

rule: Use existing shared constants (e.g., NOT_AVAILABLE_PLACEHOLDER) instead of defining inline literals for common placeholder or sentinel values.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to use shared constants over inline literals

rule: Name audit/telemetry events using a dot-separated hierarchical convention (e.g., `admin.audit.trail.view`) consistent with existing events in the system.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for audit/telemetry events

rule: When modifying logic, update or remove nearby comments so they still accurately describe the code.
mode_rationale: Simple hygiene rule to update comments when changing logic

rule: When transforming and filtering a collection, prefer a single `reduce` pass over chaining `map` and `filter` to avoid unnecessary intermediate arrays.
mode_rationale: Single performance convention for collection transformations

rule: Global authorization and shared state hooks should be provided through the centralized `useStore` rather than consumed as standalone hooks in individual components.
mode_rationale: Simple architecture convention for shared state access

rule: TypeScript interface names must start with an uppercase letter (PascalCase).
mode_rationale: Simple TypeScript naming convention

rule: Always add explicit type annotations to function parameters, return types, and variables rather than relying on implicit `any`.
mode_rationale: Simple typing convention against implicit any

rule: When async operations fail in a global store action, capture the error in the store's error state (e.g., via `setError`) so the UI can display it, rather than silently swallowing it.
mode_rationale: Simple error handling convention for store actions

rule: Do not import lodash utilities when an equivalent native JavaScript method exists (e.g., use `Array.prototype.join` instead of `_.join`).
mode_rationale: Simple dependency convention to avoid unnecessary lodash imports

rule: A REST API controller (presentation layer) must depend only on its own domain service, never on external services directly; new logic belongs in the service layer to keep domain logic separate from REST concerns.
mode_rationale: Single layering rule for controller dependencies

rule: After triggering an async action (start, stop, update), refresh the relevant data so the UI reflects the new state, including updating action buttons and status indicators.
mode_rationale: Simple UI convention to refresh data after mutations

rule: Generate internal navigation links using the centralized route helper functions (e.g., `govern/routes.ts`) instead of constructing URL strings inline.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to use route helpers instead of inline URL strings

rule: Reference design-system palette tokens (e.g., `palette.textPrimary`) for colors instead of hardcoded hex values or non-semantic color constants.
mode_rationale: Simple styling convention for design-system palette tokens

rule: Do not create an intermediate function or variable when an inline arrow function or direct reference achieves the same result with less indirection.
Always use colors from the design system palette rather than hardcoded color values.
mode_rationale: Simple styling convention to use design system palette

rule: Import styled-component files using `import * as Styled` for consistency across the codebase.
mode_rationale: Simple import convention for styled-component files

rule: Read shared application state from the centralized store (useStore) rather than deriving it locally or passing it through props when the store already provides it.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to read shared state from the centralized store

rule: Extract non-trivial render logic (especially from utility files) into dedicated components.
mode_rationale: Simple refactoring guideline for non-trivial render logic

rule: Do not mix authenticated and unauthenticated operations in the same service; keep authorization at a consistent layer so new contributors know where auth is enforced.
mode_rationale: Simple architecture principle about consistent auth layering

rule: When granting authorization roles, verify the list is complete and consistent — check that no roles are missing and that newly granted roles match what other equivalent actors receive.
mode_rationale: Simple review checkpoint for role grants

rule: When modifying cache keys or the data a cache stores, evaluate whether cache invalidation logic also needs to change and get sign-off from the cache owner.
mode_rationale: Simple review guideline when modifying cache keys

rule: Define local DTOs with only the fields you need rather than depending on DTOs owned by another module, to avoid coupling to upstream changes.
mode_rationale: Simple decoupling convention for DTOs across modules

rule: Use existing test helper factories and fixtures instead of creating ad-hoc test data that duplicates their logic.
trigger: When writing test setup logic that creates users, projects, or other common domain objects
rationale: Without reusing existing helpers, tests accumulate duplicated setup code that drifts out of sync with the actual object construction logic, leading to brittle tests and inconsistent test data.
good_example:
val user = create_user(role = Admin)
val project = create_project(owner = user)
bad_example:
val user = User(id = UUID.randomUUID(), name = "test", email = "test@test.com", role = Admin, createdAt = Instant.now(), ...)
val project = Project(id = UUID.randomUUID(), name = "proj", owner = user.id, ...)
mode_rationale: Simple convention to use existing test factories over ad-hoc data
steps: Step 1: Search the test codebase for existing helper/factory functions (e.g., grep for create_user, createProject, buildFixture)
Step 2: Review the helper's signature and defaults to confirm it supports your use case
Step 3: Call the existing helper with only the overrides you need instead of writing inline setup
Step 4: If the helper is missing a parameter you need, extend the helper rather than bypassing it

rule: In microservice architectures, do not assume the authenticated principal is the end user — service-to-service calls may use a service principal, so preserve explicit user ID parameters when the caller is a service acting on behalf of a user.
mode_rationale: Simple architecture rule to preserve explicit user ID in service-to-service calls

rule: Avoid explicit `any` types in TypeScript; properly type all values to preserve type safety.
Use a proper UUID library (e.g. `uuid`) for generating random identifiers instead of ad-hoc methods like Math.random().
mode_rationale: Simple library preference for ID generation

rule: Do not wrap components in an Ant Design Form unless there are corresponding Form.Item elements that provide validation — an empty Form with no Form.Items serves no purpose and misleads future readers.
mode_rationale: Single rule about Ant Design Form usage

rule: Extract complex UI sub-sections (e.g. an inline editable table) into their own components, and define helper functions outside the component body to avoid unnecessary re-creation on every render.
mode_rationale: Component decomposition guideline

rule: When a component accumulates many useState hooks, consolidate them with useReducer to improve maintainability and make state transitions explicit.
mode_rationale: State management convention triggered by a threshold

rule: In multi-step wizards/steppers, validate all forms across all steps on final submission, not just the current step, since step navigation may allow skipping validation.
mode_rationale: Single validation rule for wizard forms

rule: Do not use Promise.all when resolving a single promise — it adds unnecessary complexity with no concurrency benefit.
mode_rationale: Simple anti-pattern to avoid

rule: Ensure a state variable's initial value type is consistent with the type returned by its update handler to avoid runtime type mismatches.
mode_rationale: Type consistency convention for state variables

rule: Prevent form submission while asynchronous operations (e.g., adding a collaborator) are in progress to avoid partial or inconsistent data being submitted.
mode_rationale: Single guard rule for async form state

rule: Every interactive form control (radio buttons, selects, checkboxes) should be managed through the form library (e.g., Ant Design Form) rather than with standalone local state, to ensure consistent validation, submission, and reset behavior.
mode_rationale: Convention to use form library for all controls

rule: Every Ant Design Form.Item component must have its `name` property set so the form can track, validate, and submit its value.
mode_rationale: Simple required-property rule for Form.Item

rule: Do not fetch data that is never used in a component. When multiple sibling components within the same form need the same data, lift the fetch to a shared parent or context instead of duplicating the request.
mode_rationale: Data fetching convention about lifting shared data

rule: Do not add eslint-disable comments to suppress the exhaustive-deps rule in useCallback or other hooks. Ignoring dependencies in useCallback is especially dangerous because the function will silently use stale values. Instead, fix the dependency array.
mode_rationale: Single lint rule enforcement convention

rule: React useState setter functions are stable across renders and do not need to be listed in hook dependency arrays. Understand React's stability guarantees before adding unnecessary dependencies or suppressing lint rules.
mode_rationale: React knowledge rule about stable references

rule: Async operations (API calls, data mutations) must be wrapped in try/catch blocks to handle failures gracefully and prevent unhandled promise rejections.
mode_rationale: Simple error handling convention for async code

rule: When the same permission concept exists at both global and project scope, use the same name (e.g., 'QueryAuditEvents') to maintain alignment and reduce confusion.
mode_rationale: Naming consistency rule for permissions

rule: When roles are additive (e.g., projectContributor includes resultsConsumer), do not re-declare the same permissions on the parent role that are already granted by the inherited child role.
mode_rationale: Simple rule about role inheritance deduplication

rule: When adding new feature permissions, create a dedicated role rather than adding them to an existing broad role, to avoid granting permissions to users who do not need them.
mode_rationale: Security convention for permission scoping

rule: Do not introduce abstraction frameworks around simple operations; keep things straightforward until complexity genuinely warrants it.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence design philosophy

rule: When adding new resources, follow the naming pattern established by existing resource types in the same file or module.
Reference existing functions or constants for derived values instead of hardcoding them; if a value is already computed elsewhere, call that function.
mode_rationale: Code reuse convention against hardcoding

rule: Update the PR description with concrete testing evidence (e.g., YAML dumps, API responses) so reviewers and cross-team stakeholders can verify correctness.
mode_rationale: PR description convention for testing evidence

rule: Each domain entity (apps, model APIs, etc.) should have its own dedicated configuration rather than reusing another entity's config, to maintain clear boundaries and avoid coupling.
mode_rationale: Architecture guideline for config boundaries

rule: Prefer idiomatic Scala patterns like returning tuples or multiple values from expressions instead of using mutable variables.
mode_rationale: Idiomatic Scala style convention

rule: When the same construction logic is repeated in 3 or more places, extract it into a shared function so the logic can be changed in one place.
mode_rationale: DRY convention with a clear threshold

rule: Do not add new entries to monolithic configuration classes like Settings; find or create a more scoped location for new configuration.
mode_rationale: Architecture rule against monolithic config classes

rule: Do not add eslint-disable comments to suppress linting rules; fix the underlying code issue instead.
mode_rationale: Blanket rule against suppressing lint

rule: Remove unused variables from useEffect dependency arrays; dependencies should only include values actually referenced in the effect body.
mode_rationale: Simple dependency array hygiene rule

rule: When a shared utility exists for a common operation (e.g., getAppName from whiteLabelUtil), use it instead of writing custom inline logic.
mode_rationale: Code reuse convention for shared utils

rule: Avoid the `any` type in TypeScript; use generics or specific types to preserve type safety.
mode_rationale: Type safety convention (duplicate of no-explicit-any)

rule: Do not specify both a fixed height and a min-height on the same element when the min-height is redundant.
mode_rationale: CSS hygiene rule about conflicting properties

rule: When the same styled-component styles are repeated across multiple files, extract them into a shared styled component to reduce duplication.
mode_rationale: DRY convention for styled-components

rule: When a function has many Optional parameters, replace them with a typed configuration/filter object to improve readability and prevent positional argument errors.
mode_rationale: API design convention for parameter clarity

rule: When introducing new abstractions or frameworks (e.g., custom query builders), document them with tutorials and examples in Confluence so the team can maintain them independently.
mode_rationale: Documentation convention for new abstractions

rule: Name filters, methods, and query objects to explicitly describe their actual behavior and scope (e.g., `OwnedAndCollaboratingProjects` instead of a generic name) to prevent incorrect assumptions.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for descriptive method names

rule: If a field is required, make it non-optional and enforce it at the type/compiler level rather than throwing runtime errors when it is missing.
mode_rationale: Type system convention favoring compile-time enforcement

rule: When using numeric values in styled components (e.g., z-index 99, pixel sizes), ensure the value is intentional and documented or use a named constant to clarify its purpose.
mode_rationale: Style convention to name or document numeric values

rule: Prefer creating styled icon resolvers or dedicated styled components instead of applying classNames and then styling child elements from a parent selector.
mode_rationale: Styling convention preferring styled-components over classNames

rule: When a Kubernetes operator owns a CRD, the operator should own cleanup of all related resources; avoid coupling external services to trigger resource deletion.
mode_rationale: Architecture rule about K8s resource ownership

rule: When refactoring Future/async error handling (e.g. replacing onComplete with map/recover), verify that all exception paths from upstream calls are still handled.
mode_rationale: Error handling convention for async refactoring

rule: Use unique data identifiers (e.g. object keys) as React component keys instead of array indices when the data provides a natural unique key.
mode_rationale: React key assignment convention

rule: When a UI component can render with empty data, show an informative message instead of rendering an empty container (e.g. an empty collapse panel).
mode_rationale: UI convention for empty data states

rule: Default boolean component props (like autofocus) to false and require explicit opt-in; avoid defaulting behavioral props to true.
mode_rationale: API design convention for boolean prop defaults

rule: Remove unused functions and imports before submitting a PR; dead code can cause CI build failures and adds confusion.
mode_rationale: Code hygiene rule to remove dead code

rule: When using withScalarChange for audit events, respect the parameter order (fieldName, before, after, unit) and use None for the 'after' value on deletions.
mode_rationale: API usage convention for parameter order

rule: Audit events should designate one primary target entity and use the 'affecting' field for secondary entities, following the single-target policy.
mode_rationale: Audit event design convention

rule: When logic moves from frontend to backend, delete the now-redundant frontend functions rather than leaving them unused.
mode_rationale: Code cleanup rule after migration

rule: Always check error return values from fallible operations; do not silently ignore error variables.
mode_rationale: Simple error handling convention

rule: Never log authentication tokens, secrets, or credentials; review log statements to ensure sensitive values are not included.
mode_rationale: Security convention against logging secrets

rule: Remove leftover debug log statements before merging; review all added logging to ensure only intentional production logs remain.
mode_rationale: Code hygiene rule for debug statements

rule: Name event handler functions after the semantic action they perform (e.g. onConfirm, onSave) rather than generic UI actions (e.g. onClose) when the handler represents a specific business action.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention for event handlers

rule: When a component is rendered per table row, fetch shared data once in the parent/wrapper component and pass results down, rather than making one request per row.
mode_rationale: Single performance guideline about where to fetch data
