rule: Do not store derived data in component state; compute it directly from the source value to avoid sync bugs and unnecessary re-renders.
mode_rationale: Single rule against storing derived data in state

rule: Use a <button> element (or an existing icon button component) for clickable actions instead of a <div> with an onClick handler, to preserve keyboard navigation and screen reader accessibility.
mode_rationale: Single accessibility convention about element choice

rule: Remove or do not commit empty source files; they add noise and indicate incomplete work.
When renaming or naming functions and events, ensure the name accurately represents the full scope of the behavior (e.g., plural if it handles multiple items).
mode_rationale: Single naming convention about accuracy

rule: When building a URL or configuration, use and extend existing utility functions rather than appending parameters inline in a consuming component.
mode_rationale: Single code reuse guideline

rule: After updating dependencies (e.g., domino-api), restart the dev server before committing so the lock file reflects the correct resolved state; stale lock changes will break CI.
mode_rationale: Single operational rule about lock file consistency

rule: Include all referenced variables in useCallback/useEffect/useMemo dependency arrays; skipping dependencies leads to stale closures and unexpected behavior.
mode_rationale: Single React convention about dependency arrays

rule: Use styled-components or CSS classes instead of inline style attributes to maintain consistency and leverage the project's theming system.
mode_rationale: Single styling convention

rule: Before adding a new component or file, verify that an equivalent one does not already exist in the codebase to avoid unnecessary duplication.
mode_rationale: Single guideline to avoid duplication

rule: When removing code that behaves differently based on a feature flag, verify the behavior is correct in both the flag-on and flag-off states before removing the mapping.
mode_rationale: Single safety rule about feature flag removal

rule: When leaving a TODO or referencing future work in code, include concrete examples or link to a ticket so the intent is actionable by anyone who encounters it later.
mode_rationale: Single documentation convention for TODOs

rule: When adding or changing behavior (e.g., aria labels) on a shared component, run integration/E2E tests (e.g., cucu tests) to ensure the change does not break consumers that rely on specific selectors.
mode_rationale: Single testing reminder, not a multi-step procedure

rule: When renaming a type or class, also rename all variables and parameters that reference that type to maintain consistency between type names and their usages.
mode_rationale: Single consistency rule for renames

rule: API specs should define a single canonical response code per operation; do not add a duplicate status code as a workaround — replace the incorrect one instead.
mode_rationale: Single API spec convention

rule: Use the project's established CSS unit convention (px) consistently; do not mix in rems or other units unless the project has adopted them.
mode_rationale: Single styling consistency rule

rule: Static preset values, configuration objects, and constants that do not depend on props or state should be defined outside the React component to avoid unnecessary re-creation on each render.
mode_rationale: Single performance/organization convention

rule: When catching all errors as a best-effort cleanup strategy, make the intent explicit with comments or logging so reviewers and future maintainers understand the errors are intentionally swallowed.
mode_rationale: Single convention about documenting swallowed errors

rule: Do not use setTimeout to defer state changes in order to bypass form validation timing issues; find a proper solution such as conditional validation or explicit state flags instead.
mode_rationale: Single anti-pattern prohibition

rule: Changes to shared color palettes, design tokens, or theme values that affect the entire application must be validated with design stakeholders before merging.
mode_rationale: Single process rule about design token changes

rule: When a field contains multiple values (e.g., a list of user IDs or names), use a proper collection type (Seq, List, Array) instead of a comma-separated string in a single field.
mode_rationale: Single data modeling convention

rule: When adding user-identifying information (usernames, emails) to telemetry or analytics events, verify whether those fields need to be added to the redacted fields list to prevent PII leakage to third-party analytics platforms.
mode_rationale: Single security/privacy rule

rule: Configuration variable names for durations should include the unit (e.g., `cacheTtlSeconds` instead of `cacheTtl`) to prevent ambiguity about whether the value is in seconds, milliseconds, or another unit.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention for duration variables

rule: Configuration parameters representing durations, sizes, or other measured quantities should include the unit in their name or an adjacent comment.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention for measured quantities

rule: When implementing HTTP response caching, cache based on the presence of cache-control headers rather than hardcoding status code checks, to avoid confusing conditional logic that implies impossible states.
mode_rationale: Single implementation guideline for HTTP caching

rule: When the same permission name exists at different scopes (e.g., project-level vs global), ensure the naming or documentation clearly distinguishes them to avoid confusion during code review and maintenance.
mode_rationale: Single naming clarity rule for permissions

rule: Actions exposed through an 'authorized actions' endpoint should actually check authorization; if an action is configuration-based rather than auth-based, expose it through a separate configuration API.
mode_rationale: Single API design principle about semantic correctness

rule: When copying CRD or schema definitions across repositories, add a comment noting the source and ensure changes in the source are propagated to all copies.
mode_rationale: Single maintenance rule about cross-repo copies

rule: State enums used as 'target status' should only contain terminal/desired states, not transient states like 'starting' or 'updating' that represent in-progress transitions.
mode_rationale: Single enum design convention

rule: When a service method performs a side-effecting operation (e.g., updating a K8s custom resource), add a comment warning that subsequent code additions should include a rollback for that operation.
mode_rationale: Single documentation convention for side-effecting code

rule: Use themeHelper utilities or theme hooks for colors and spacing instead of importing raw color constants, to ensure consistency with the design system and support theming.
mode_rationale: Single styling convention about theme usage

rule: When writing CSS-in-JS styles, use existing helper functions (e.g., getMargin, getPadding) instead of raw values to maintain consistency and reduce duplication.
mode_rationale: Single code reuse rule for CSS-in-JS

rule: When a function returns a tuple, destructure it into named variables at the call site for readability instead of accessing elements by index.
mode_rationale: Single readability convention

rule: When a common operation (like executing an aggregation pipeline and converting from BSON) is repeated across persistence methods, extract it into a typed helper method to eliminate repetitive conversion boilerplate.
mode_rationale: Single DRY guideline for repeated conversions

rule: Combinator functions like And/Or should accept variadic arguments rather than requiring callers to wrap arguments in a Set or List, to improve ergonomics at the call site.
mode_rationale: Single API ergonomics guideline

rule: Validate all inputs before making any state changes so that the operation is atomic — either all changes succeed or none are applied.
mode_rationale: Single atomicity rule for mutations

rule: When recording audit or tracking events, include all available identifying information (e.g., user IDs, entity IDs) even if not strictly required, to make events useful for debugging and auditing.
mode_rationale: Single logging completeness guideline

rule: Pass actual runtime values as parameters to audit/event-tracking functions rather than relying on hardcoded defaults, so audit logs remain accurate even if defaults change.
mode_rationale: Single audit accuracy rule

rule: When modifying or removing metric fields, verify that existing monitoring dashboards (NewRelic, Grafana, etc.) that query those fields are not broken by the change.
mode_rationale: Single verification rule when modifying metrics

rule: Name variables and fields to accurately reflect their actual usage pattern — do not call something a 'cache' if it is not used for lookup-based retrieval.
mode_rationale: Single naming accuracy convention

rule: Protect mutable shared state with synchronization even when concurrent access seems unlikely, to prevent subtle race conditions with virtually no performance cost.
mode_rationale: Single concurrency safety rule

rule: Use stable, deterministic keys for React components — never generate new UUIDs or random values on each render, as this forces unnecessary unmount/remount cycles.
mode_rationale: Single React performance convention

rule: Do not reuse a variable name that shadows an outer-scope variable — use a distinct name to avoid confusion and subtle bugs.
mode_rationale: Single naming/scoping convention

rule: Do not bundle unrelated changes into a single PR — separate concerns so that individual changes can be reverted independently without collateral impact.
mode_rationale: Single PR discipline guideline

rule: When a multi-step operation partially succeeds, surface the failure to the user as a warning with clear guidance on what to do manually, rather than silently swallowing the error.
mode_rationale: Single error-handling convention

rule: When multiple code paths log similar errors, unify them with a shared log message prefix so that searching logs and creating alerts does not require matching multiple distinct strings.
mode_rationale: Single logging convention for searchability

rule: When using React hooks (useEffect, useCallback, useMemo), include all referenced state setters and values in the dependency array to avoid stale closures.
mode_rationale: Single React hooks convention (overlaps with never-skip-dependencies)

rule: Keep JSX component props and exported TypeScript interfaces sorted alphabetically for readability and consistent diffs.
mode_rationale: Single ordering convention for readability

rule: Magic strings used as sentinel or filter values (e.g., "All") should be extracted into named constants to prevent typos and enable single-point changes.
mode_rationale: Single code hygiene rule about magic strings

rule: Async operations that can throw (API calls, mutations) must be wrapped in try/catch with user-facing error feedback so failures are not silently swallowed.
mode_rationale: Single convention: wrap async ops in try/catch with user feedback

rule: Review compound conditional expressions for logically redundant terms — e.g., `(A && B) || B` always reduces to just `B`.
Do not use hardcoded pixel heights to vertically center content — use flexbox or grid instead, as fixed heights break on smaller viewports and cause unnecessary scrolling.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use flexbox/grid instead of hardcoded heights

rule: Do not use `position: absolute` for main screen content that can be laid out with normal document flow — absolute positioning should be reserved for overlays, tooltips, and floating elements.
mode_rationale: Single convention: prefer normal flow over absolute positioning for main content

rule: When emitting events or metrics, be aware that empty-string defaults (via `.getOrElse("")`) can change how monitoring tools like New Relic index fields — an empty string may omit the key entirely, which is often the desired behavior for optional fields.
mode_rationale: Single convention: be aware of empty-string defaults in metrics/events

rule: When adding Prometheus metrics or structured log fields, use the same field names as existing events/telemetry for the same concept so engineers can correlate data across systems without confusion.
mode_rationale: Single convention: reuse existing field names for cross-system correlation

rule: Metric and counter names must use the correct domain term for the scope they cover — e.g., use "runs" not "jobs" when the metric tracks all run types, since "jobs" is a sub-category of runs.
mode_rationale: Single convention: match metric names to the correct domain scope

rule: When a constant represents a value with units (bytes, seconds, pixels, etc.), append the unit to the constant name for clarity.
mode_rationale: Single convention: append units to constant names

rule: Component properties that have sensible defaults or are not always needed should be declared as optional rather than required.
mode_rationale: Single convention: default props with sensible values should be optional

rule: When two callbacks or state setters appear to do the same thing, unify them into one; when an expression is repeated, extract it into a named constant.
mode_rationale: Single convention: deduplicate callbacks and extract repeated expressions

rule: When transitioning to a loading state, clear any previous error state so stale errors are not displayed alongside a loading indicator.
mode_rationale: Single convention: clear stale errors on loading transition

rule: When URL-encoding path elements, encode all special characters comprehensively using a standard library function (e.g., URLEncoder.encode), not just specific characters like spaces.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use standard library for full URL encoding

rule: Exception and error messages should include the relevant context variables (e.g., file path, ID, input value) so that issues can be diagnosed without additional logging.
mode_rationale: Single convention: include context variables in error messages

rule: When the same expression (e.g., `isDownload.getOrElse(false)`) is evaluated more than once, extract it into a named val or constant to reduce duplication and improve readability.
mode_rationale: Single convention: extract duplicated expressions into named constants

rule: Metadata such as failure reasons should be recorded only at the final status transition, not at every intermediate stage, to avoid noise and redundancy.
mode_rationale: Single convention: record metadata at final transition only

rule: Input normalization (e.g., URL decoding, trimming, case conversion) should be performed at the controller or entry-point level so that downstream functions receive consistent, already-normalized data.
mode_rationale: Single convention: normalize inputs at the entry point

rule: When a shared wrapper component exists (e.g., TooltipWrapper), use it instead of reimplementing tooltip behavior inline.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use existing shared wrappers instead of reimplementing

rule: Route parameter keys passed to generatePath must be validated as non-empty, since generatePath throws a runtime error if any param is empty.
mode_rationale: Single convention: validate params are non-empty before generatePath

rule: When fetching data in a view, always handle the error state using the module's existing error component (e.g., FlowsError) rather than ignoring failures silently.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use module's existing error component for fetch failures

rule: UI labels and copy must use the correct domain term for the entity being displayed (e.g., 'file' vs 'artifact') to avoid confusing users.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use correct domain terms in UI copy

rule: Date and time formatting functions should live in the shared useDate utility so they respect locale settings, rather than being defined ad hoc in individual component utils.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use shared useDate utility for date formatting

rule: When extending a feature to a new role, create a new, narrowly-scoped permission rather than reusing an existing broad permission that grants more access than intended.
mode_rationale: Single convention: create narrow permissions rather than reusing broad ones

rule: Authorization checks must be enforced on the server side; relying solely on UI restrictions (e.g., limiting dropdown options) leaves the API vulnerable to direct calls that bypass the UI.
mode_rationale: Single convention: always enforce auth on the server side

rule: Test names should precisely describe the behavior and constraints being verified, including boundary conditions like 'to org that they are a part of'.
mode_rationale: Single convention: test names must precisely describe behavior and constraints

rule: When testing authorization changes, include negative test cases that verify users cannot perform actions outside their allowed scope (e.g., transferring to an org they don't belong to).
mode_rationale: Single convention: include negative auth test cases

rule: When assigning ports for internal sidecar or proxy services, check that the port number does not collide with common defaults used by user-facing frameworks and languages (e.g., 8180, 8888) to prevent conflicts in user workloads.
mode_rationale: Single convention: avoid port collisions with common framework defaults

rule: When modifying route structures, verify that breadcrumb navigation resolves correctly for all nested sub-routes (e.g., add/edit pages), not just the base route.
mode_rationale: Single convention: verify breadcrumbs for all nested sub-routes

rule: Do not combine useEffect-triggered fetches with explicit refetch calls, as this causes duplicate simultaneous requests for the same data.
mode_rationale: Single convention: don't combine useEffect fetches with explicit refetch calls

rule: Combine multiple filter conditions into a single filter function call instead of chaining multiple passes over the same collection.
mode_rationale: Single convention: combine multiple filters into one pass

rule: In functional components, use React hooks (useNavigate, useParams, useStore) instead of higher-order components (withRouter, withStore).
mode_rationale: Single convention: prefer hooks over HOCs in functional components

rule: Use semantic color tokens from the design system palette (e.g., palette.textPrimary) instead of hardcoded or ad-hoc color values.
When the same function logic (e.g., getTitle, sort comparators) appears in multiple components, extract it into a shared utility to avoid duplication.
Use established utility libraries (e.g., lodash sortBy) instead of reimplementing common operations like sorting, especially when multiple identical implementations exist.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use lodash etc. instead of reimplementing common ops

rule: Use existing shared component aliases (e.g., @domino/core-ui/EllipsisText) instead of reimplementing or directly importing internal paths.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use existing shared component aliases

rule: Import modules from their source (src) paths, not from compiled dist/build output directories.
mode_rationale: Single convention: import from src paths, not dist/build output

rule: Use Array.isArray() to check if a value is an array instead of other type-checking approaches.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use Array.isArray() for array checks

rule: Return at the end of an if statement instead of using an else block to reduce nesting and cyclomatic complexity.
mode_rationale: Single convention: return early to reduce nesting

rule: Never use explicit `any` type in TypeScript; use proper types, generics, or `unknown` with type narrowing instead.
Use explicit if statements instead of complex ternary or inline conditional expressions for better readability.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use if statements over complex ternaries

rule: When throwing or wrapping exceptions, always include the original cause to preserve the full error chain for debugging.
mode_rationale: Single convention: always include original cause when wrapping exceptions

rule: When code is unused and compiles without it, delete it outright rather than leaving it in place; document the removal in the PR description.
mode_rationale: Single convention: delete unused code outright

rule: Use the established error-handling utilities (e.g., getError from a shared provider) instead of ad-hoc error extraction logic to keep error handling consistent.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use shared error-handling utilities

rule: When a default or fallback value depends on implicit context (e.g., undefined means 'everyone' in create mode vs. a real value in edit mode), add a code comment explaining the reasoning for future maintainers.
mode_rationale: Single convention: add comments explaining implicit default value reasoning

rule: Replace nested or multi-condition ternary expressions with if/else statements for readability and maintainability.
mode_rationale: Single convention: replace nested ternaries with if/else

rule: Before writing custom test assertions, check for existing shared test utilities (e.g., checkBtnDisabledState) that already handle common verification patterns.
mode_rationale: Single convention: check for shared test utilities before writing custom ones

rule: Always reference the theme object for colors, spacing, and typography in styled-components rather than hardcoding values, even inside conditional expressions.
mode_rationale: Single convention: reference theme object instead of hardcoding values

rule: Aria labels should be natural, human-readable phrases (e.g., 'Save evidence') rather than id-like strings with dashes or camelCase.
In Scala, place utility functions that are specific to a class in its companion object rather than leaving them as loose functions or mixing them into unrelated classes.
mode_rationale: Single convention: Scala utility functions go in companion objects

rule: Always URL-encode user-supplied values when constructing query strings to prevent injection and encoding issues.
mode_rationale: Single convention: always URL-encode user-supplied query values

rule: Do not copy-paste utility functions across modules; either fix dependency issues to reuse the existing utility or write a minimal inline version if only a fraction of the logic is needed.
mode_rationale: Single convention: reuse existing utilities instead of copy-pasting

rule: When changing feature flag or config-driven behavior, verify the change across all deployment scenarios including when the flag is absent, not just when it is explicitly set.
mode_rationale: Single guideline to verify flag behavior across deployment scenarios

rule: Do not leave functions or exports that are never called; remove dead code before merging.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to remove dead code before merging

rule: When a function accepts parameters that serve the same role (e.g., all are query params), pass them consistently through the same mechanism (params object vs positional args) rather than mixing styles based on whether they are required or optional.
mode_rationale: Single convention about parameter passing consistency

rule: When catching a broad or non-fatal error, do not silently assume it was caused by one specific condition (e.g., uninitialized repo); the same error path may be triggered by unrelated failures.
mode_rationale: Single guideline about not over-interpreting broad errors

rule: When catching and handling an error, always log the original error so that if the assumption about the cause is wrong, engineers can diagnose the real issue from logs.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to always log original errors when catching

rule: Recursive functions that make external API calls must enforce a maximum depth or iteration limit to prevent unbounded API usage, rate limit errors, and UI hangs.
mode_rationale: Single rule to enforce depth/iteration limits on recursive API calls

rule: Create mutable data structures (buffers, accumulators) inside the function that owns them rather than allocating them externally and passing them in unused.
mode_rationale: Simple convention about data structure allocation locality

rule: In recursive functions, use a mutable accumulator as either a parameter or a return value, not both — dual use leads to duplicate entries and confusing ownership semantics.
mode_rationale: Single guideline about accumulator usage in recursive functions

rule: When changing behavior that is tied to a feature flag, check all other logic gated by the same flag to ensure the change does not produce inconsistent state when the flag is enabled or disabled.
mode_rationale: Single check to verify all logic gated by the same flag

rule: When serving files to the browser, set the content type to the correct MIME type for the format (e.g., `text/xsl` for XSL files) rather than defaulting to `text/plain`, so the browser can render or highlight the content appropriately.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to use correct MIME types when serving files

rule: Function names should clearly describe what the function returns or does; prefer specific names like `getFileAbsolutePath` over generic or ambiguous names.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for functions

rule: Extract inline magic values (such as lists of allowed file extensions) into named constants or sets to improve readability and make future maintenance easier.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to replace magic values with named constants

rule: Do not re-fetch the same entity from the database in multiple classes along a call chain; fetch it once and pass it through, preferably only when the data is actually needed.
mode_rationale: Single rule to fetch once and pass through

rule: Provide visible user feedback (error messages, toasts, status indicators) when state-changing operations occur, especially on failures.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to show feedback on state changes

rule: Wrap API calls in try-catch to handle cases where intermediaries (proxies, gateways) return failures before the request reaches the target service, not just service-level error responses.
mode_rationale: Single guideline to wrap API calls in try-catch for proxy failures

rule: Set a default human-readable error message and attempt to enhance it with details from the API response; do not replace standard response fields like statusText. Follow existing error handling patterns in the codebase.
mode_rationale: Single convention about default error messages with enhancement

rule: When designing audit/domain events, choose a consistent convention (unified event with a changes field vs. separate events per action) and apply it across the codebase to ensure queryable and coherent audit trails.
mode_rationale: Single convention to use consistent audit event patterns

rule: When fetching a record that is expected to exist, validate its presence and throw a descriptive RecordNotFound error rather than silently proceeding with a missing value.
mode_rationale: Simple rule to validate presence and throw descriptive errors

rule: Domain events and UI-facing operations should use the application's canonical user ID, not IDP-specific identifiers (e.g., Keycloak IDs), to avoid leaking infrastructure details into the domain layer.
mode_rationale: Single convention to use canonical user IDs, not IDP-specific ones

rule: Add a log message when changing or removing operational behavior (such as disabling health check pauses) so that on-call engineers and debuggers can understand the system's current mode from logs.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to log when operational behavior changes

rule: Incorporate feature flags and access control checks into centralized auth contexts rather than scattering them across individual service methods, to ensure consistent enforcement across all access paths.
mode_rationale: Single architectural guideline about where to place auth checks

rule: Return distinct, resource-specific authorization error messages that identify which resource the user lacks access to, rather than generic 'not authorized' responses.
mode_rationale: Simple convention for resource-specific auth error messages

rule: Use proper types or type inference instead of explicit `any` annotations in TypeScript; leverage existing type definitions or generics to maintain type safety.
mode_rationale: Simple type safety convention

rule: Instead of rendering the same component twice with different props based on a condition, use a single instance with a conditional prop value.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to use conditional props instead of duplicate components

rule: Always return a cleanup function from useEffect to unregister event listeners when the component unmounts.
mode_rationale: Simple React convention about useEffect cleanup

rule: When code contains non-obvious assumptions or workarounds, leave a comment explaining why, so future developers understand the reasoning.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to comment non-obvious code

rule: Use existing design system components (e.g., ActionDropdown.Button) instead of building custom elements with manual ARIA attributes and roles.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to prefer design system over custom elements

rule: Do not use arbitrary timeouts to wait for server-side state changes; implement polling or event listeners to track status updates accurately.
mode_rationale: Single rule against arbitrary timeouts for status tracking

rule: View components should not contain API call logic; move API interactions to custom hooks or container components.
mode_rationale: Simple separation-of-concerns convention

rule: Do not nest multiple ternary expressions; use early returns, if/else blocks, or a lookup map for readability.
mode_rationale: Simple readability convention

rule: Do not use useEffect to synchronize state that can be computed or updated directly within an event handler or callback.
Always use state setter functions to update React state; never mutate state objects directly, as it leads to unexpected rendering behavior.
mode_rationale: Fundamental React convention about state immutability

rule: Before adding values to shared enums or type definitions, verify the change does not alter behavior in other screens or features that consume them.
mode_rationale: Single check before modifying shared types

rule: Do not use fetch calls or state setters within boolean expressions; side effects should be in explicit control flow statements.
mode_rationale: Simple convention about side effect placement

rule: Do not use React state for values that can be easily calculated or derived from existing state or props.
mode_rationale: Simple React convention about derived values

rule: Split large components into container (business logic) and view (presentational) parts, using hooks or wrapper components to separate concerns.
mode_rationale: Single architectural convention about component structure

rule: Split components that handle multiple distinct actions (e.g., start AND stop) into separate, simpler components with a single responsibility.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to split multi-action components

rule: When a component needs additional data for a new use case, create a specialized wrapper that composes the original component rather than modifying it to fetch the data internally.
mode_rationale: Single architectural preference for composition

rule: When adding data fetching to a component, always handle loading and error states to prevent broken or misleading UI.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to handle loading/error states when fetching

rule: When adding new logic to a class component, convert it to a functional component rather than continuing to extend the class.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to convert class components when touching them

rule: Avoid type casting; if a cast is needed, fix the upstream typing so the payload is properly typed.
mode_rationale: Simple type safety convention

rule: Remove TODO comments once the referenced work is complete or no longer relevant; do not leave stale TODOs in the codebase.
Use the correct semantic HTML elements (nav, main, header, etc.) instead of generic divs or spans, and update styled components directly rather than wrapping with new tags.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to use semantic HTML

rule: When changing an HTML element type in a styled component, verify that default display properties (e.g., inline vs. block) do not break the layout.
mode_rationale: Single check when changing element types in styled components

rule: When modifying shared layout components, verify that changes render correctly under all active themes and feature flag configurations.
mode_rationale: Single testing convention for shared layout components

rule: If console.log statements are intentionally left in production code, add a comment explaining why they are needed.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to comment intentional console.log statements

rule: Use theme color tokens instead of hardcoded color values in UI components.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to use theme tokens instead of hardcoded colors

rule: Capitalize domain entity names consistently in user-facing strings and enum values (e.g., 'Bundle' not 'bundle').
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for domain entities in UI strings

rule: Do not include unrelated changes to auto-generated files in a PR; if unavoidable, explain why the changes appeared.
mode_rationale: Simple convention about explaining generated file changes in PRs

rule: When adding audit trail v2 events, use normalized fields (target, affecting, location) instead of flat metadata keys for entities with IDs and names.
mode_rationale: Single convention about audit event field structure

rule: Add UI permissions and authorized actions in the correct scope — sidebar auth configs are only for sidebar menu visibility, not for feature-level permissions elsewhere in the app.
mode_rationale: Single guideline about where to place permission configs

rule: Name authorized actions without the 'can' prefix since capability is implicit in the authorized actions endpoint (e.g., 'project.override_dataset_limits' not 'project.can_override_dataset_limits').
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for authorized actions

rule: When adding new service actions or permission checks, add corresponding test cases in the relevant spec file.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence testing convention

rule: Prefer if/ternary expressions over chaining && expressions when it avoids the need for non-null assertions (!).
mode_rationale: Simple code style preference for conditionals

rule: Before creating new test utility helpers, check for existing test utils in the same module and reuse them to avoid duplication.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence convention about test util reuse

rule: Verify that a directory or resource does not already exist before attempting to create it to avoid errors or unintended overwrites.
mode_rationale: Simple defensive coding guideline

rule: When modifying configuration that affects multiple services or codepaths (e.g., KubernetesConfigurationGenerator and WorkloadServiceHelper), ensure all consumers are updated to maintain exact parity.
mode_rationale: Single convention about keeping configs in sync

rule: Never leave tests ignored or disabled in a PR without explicit justification; temporarily skipping tests to focus on main code should be resolved before merging.
mode_rationale: Simple rule about not merging disabled tests

rule: When pushing transient state (filters, tabs, selections) to the URL, use history.replace instead of history.push to avoid polluting the browser back-button history.
mode_rationale: Single convention for URL state management

rule: When the same logic pattern appears in multiple components, extract it into a reusable custom React hook to reduce duplication and improve maintainability.
mode_rationale: Single refactoring guideline for React hooks

rule: Keep the root of a feature directory shallow by nesting new component folders under an existing 'components' directory rather than creating new top-level folders.
mode_rationale: Simple directory structure convention

rule: Always import shared components via their scoped package path (e.g., @domino/core-ui/<Component>) rather than deep relative paths into another package's internals.
mode_rationale: Single import path convention

rule: For imports between files within the same feature module, use relative paths rather than absolute or aliased paths to keep the module self-contained.
mode_rationale: Single import path convention for intra-module files

rule: Move reusable helper functions and transformation logic out of component files and into dedicated utils files to improve testability and reuse.
mode_rationale: Simple code organization guideline

rule: Do not throw exceptions in callbacks or handlers unless the caller explicitly catches them; uncaught throws in modal onOk or similar props silently fail or cause runtime errors.
mode_rationale: Single error-handling convention about throw/catch pairing

rule: Use react-router's Link component instead of native anchor tags for internal app navigation to enable client-side routing and avoid full page reloads.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to use Link over anchor tags

rule: Avoid oversized components by extracting API calls and request logic into a container component or custom hook, keeping the presentational component focused on rendering.
mode_rationale: Single guideline about component size and separation

rule: Always use the theme helper for color values instead of hardcoding hex codes; verify any non-standard color with the UX team before using it.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to avoid hardcoded colors

rule: Never invoke state setters or trigger API requests inside useMemo; useMemo is for derived computations only — side effects belong in useEffect or event handlers.
mode_rationale: Single React anti-pattern rule

rule: Use `principal.eventUser` for audit trail events instead of fetching the user via a persister, to avoid unnecessary database lookups.
mode_rationale: Simple convention for audit trail user references

rule: Leverage audit trail entity preprocessors (e.g., `UserNameFetchingEntityPreprocessor`) to resolve entity names from IDs automatically, rather than manually injecting and calling persisters.
mode_rationale: Single convention to use built-in preprocessors

rule: Always use canonical entity types (e.g., `EntityType.User`) in audit events, not context-relative names like `"assignee"`, to enable consistent global filtering and querying.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for entity types

rule: Store raw identifiers in audit events and defer display formatting (e.g., fallback labels like "Unassigned") to read/display time, not write/storage time.
mode_rationale: Single data storage convention

rule: Use `SequentialFieldChange` (not `withScalarChange`) for audit event fields that reference other entities by ID, as this stores both ID and name and enables preprocessor resolution.
mode_rationale: Single API usage convention for audit fields

rule: Name audit event classes to clearly distinguish metadata changes (e.g., renaming a stage) from state transitions (e.g., moving to the next stage).
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for event classes

rule: When using nested Option types like `Option[Option[T]]` to represent distinct states (no change vs. explicit unset vs. new value), add scaladoc or inline comments explaining the semantics of each case.
mode_rationale: Single documentation convention for complex types

rule: Do not apply `distinct` to queries on fields that are already guaranteed to be unique (e.g., primary keys), as it adds unnecessary overhead.
mode_rationale: Simple performance guideline about redundant queries

rule: Use sequential changes for entity-related fields (anything with an ID tied to a collection) and scalar changes only for primitive values in audit trail events.
mode_rationale: Single convention for audit trail field types

rule: Use schema-validated fields instead of the metadata catch-all in AuditableEvents; reserve metadata only for event-level details that cannot be captured any other way.
mode_rationale: Simple convention about structured vs catch-all fields

rule: Model audit events with a single target whenever possible; emit one event per entity rather than bundling multiple targets into a single event.
mode_rationale: Single modeling convention for audit events

rule: Move compiled regex patterns and other constant values outside of function scope to avoid recomputation on each invocation.
mode_rationale: Simple performance convention about constant placement

rule: When adding a new authentication mechanism, add dedicated test cases covering the new auth flow.
mode_rationale: Single testing convention for auth flows

rule: Use the AppContent component with its built-in title and pageActions props for page headers instead of building custom layouts.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to use AppContent for layouts

rule: Move inline boolean expressions into descriptively named variables to improve readability.
mode_rationale: Simple readability convention

rule: Use Option types rather than passing null values to avoid null reference risks and improve type safety.
mode_rationale: Single type safety convention

rule: Disable action buttons while their associated async operation is in progress to prevent duplicate submissions.
mode_rationale: Simple UI convention to prevent double-submit

rule: Use the canonical domain term in error messages and code (e.g., 'bundle' not 'deliverable') to maintain consistency with the product vocabulary.
When fixing a bug, add a unit test that would have caught the bug to prevent regressions.
mode_rationale: Single testing convention for bug fixes

rule: When fixing a bug, search the codebase for similar code paths that may have the same issue and fix them in the same PR.
mode_rationale: Single convention to check for related bugs

rule: Fix the root cause of TypeScript errors (e.g., make a prop required) rather than replacing one suppression mechanism with another (e.g., swapping @ts-ignore for a default value).
mode_rationale: Single guideline about fixing errors properly

rule: When changing optional props to have default values, verify that the default (e.g., [] is truthy) does not alter existing conditional logic that depends on falsy checks.
mode_rationale: Single convention about verifying default value impacts

rule: When a column or render function operates on a specific field of a generic type, use indexed access types (e.g., Row['fieldName']) to keep the type tied to the base generic rather than using standalone type annotations.
mode_rationale: Single TypeScript typing convention

rule: Place utility functions in the module that owns the domain concept they operate on, not in a shared generic utils file, especially when they are specific to one feature (e.g., URL parsing for file browser).
mode_rationale: Single code organization convention

rule: When changing code behavior (e.g., changing a default value), update or remove comments that described the previous behavior so they don't become misleading.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation hygiene guideline

rule: When modifying behavior gated by a feature flag, verify that the change doesn't unintentionally expand the flag's scope to cover additional cases beyond its original intent.
mode_rationale: Single convention to verify feature flag boundaries

rule: When wrapping content in new DOM elements (e.g., adding a span for aria-label), verify that the element's default styles (e.g., display: inline) do not cause unintended visual regressions.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to check for visual side effects

rule: Use the semantically correct ARIA role for the element's function (e.g., use 'menuitem' for items inside a menu, not a generic role).
mode_rationale: Single accessibility convention

rule: Do not override Ant Design (or other library) CSS classes directly from custom components; instead use styled-components wrapper with specificity selectors (e.g., &&&) and pass configuration through component props like btnType and size.
mode_rationale: Single styling convention for third-party components

rule: Use useMemo for computed values derived from props or state to avoid unnecessary recalculations on re-render.
mode_rationale: Simple React performance convention

rule: Replace nested ternary expressions with switch statements or early returns for readability, especially when matching against enum or state values.
mode_rationale: Simple readability convention for conditional expressions

rule: Avoid nested ternary expressions as they make code hard to read and maintain.
mode_rationale: Simple readability convention against nested ternaries

rule: Define magic string values (resource names, file names, paths) as named constants in the relevant config object rather than using inline string literals.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence naming convention for magic strings

rule: Remove unnecessary encode/decode round-trips and other debugging artifacts before merging; if the operation has no functional purpose, simplify it.
mode_rationale: Simple cleanup rule before merging

rule: When a value is already defined as a constant elsewhere in the codebase, reference that constant instead of duplicating the string literal.
Remove commented-out code before merging; version control preserves history if the code is needed later.
Place helper functions and utilities in a dedicated utils file rather than co-locating them in the component file.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence file organization convention

rule: When the same conditional expression is used in multiple places, extract it into a named variable to avoid code repetition and improve readability.
mode_rationale: Simple DRY convention for conditional expressions

rule: Before implementing common UI patterns like loading spinners, check for existing shared wrapper components (e.g., SpinnerWrapper) that handle the pattern consistently.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence convention to check for existing components first

rule: Use the automock functionality instead of defining manual mock files for components and modules.
mode_rationale: Simple testing preference for automock over manual mocks

rule: Avoid nested ternary operators; use if/else blocks, early returns, or helper functions for readability.
mode_rationale: Simple readability convention (duplicate of nested ternary rule)

rule: When accessing deeply nested response properties (e.g., err.response.data), add null/undefined guards or use optional chaining to prevent runtime errors.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence defensive coding convention

rule: When modifying a shared or reusable component, verify that the change is compatible with all existing usages.
mode_rationale: Simple review-time convention for shared components

rule: When capturing errors, store both the error code (if available) and the error message to enable better debugging and user feedback.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence error handling convention

rule: Never use truthiness checks (!! or Boolean()) directly on a Promise object; always await or resolve the promise first, since !!Promise is always true.
mode_rationale: Simple correctness rule about promise truthiness

rule: Replace @ts-ignore directives in test mocks with properly typed alternatives, such as a mutable variable assigned to the mock factory.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence convention about typed test mocks

rule: Resolve icons through the IconResolver component instead of importing Ant Design icon components directly.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to use the project's icon abstraction

rule: When code references an API without a proper schema, create a tracking ticket to add the missing schema rather than leaving untyped workarounds in place.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence convention to create tickets for missing schemas

rule: Set aria-label values to accurately describe the component's current function, not the broader workflow it belongs to (e.g., 'select collaborators' for a selection input, not 'invite collaborators').
mode_rationale: Simple accessibility naming convention

rule: When a referenced Jira ticket is closed as 'won't do', remove associated TODO comments, placeholder types, and dead code paths that existed only for that ticket.
mode_rationale: Simple cleanup convention for dead ticket references

rule: Do not use the __mocks__ directory convention for manual component mocks; prefer co-located jest.mock() calls with explicit factory functions.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence testing convention for mock placement

rule: Add docstrings to methods that return tuples or composite values where the meaning of each element is not self-evident; prefer adding named boolean fields to case classes over returning ad-hoc tuples.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation convention for complex return types

rule: When a code change broadens the scope of an operation (e.g., from template repos only to all repos), explicitly note the behavioral change and assess potential unintended consequences.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence review convention about scope changes

rule: Remove commented-out lines left behind during refactoring and audit surrounding components for the same legacy pattern (e.g., legacy icon imports) to track follow-up cleanup.
mode_rationale: Simple cleanup convention for refactoring leftovers

rule: When an established library already provides a method for an API call, use it instead of writing custom HTTP requests, so you can reuse existing error handling and warning logic.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence convention to use existing library methods

rule: Analytics and tracking events must include comparison guards to ensure they only fire when the specific tracked state actually changes, not when unrelated state changes occur.
mode_rationale: Simple correctness convention for analytics events

rule: When adding new conditional checks to an existing guard clause, preserve the original conditions rather than replacing them — combine old and new conditions together.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence convention about combining guard conditions

rule: Wrap sessionStorage and localStorage access in try-catch blocks because they can throw exceptions in certain browser contexts (e.g., security restrictions, storage quota exceeded).
mode_rationale: Simple defensive coding convention for browser storage

rule: Variable and parameter names must accurately reflect what they contain — rename identifiers when the underlying data type changes (e.g., from ID to name).
mode_rationale: Single-sentence naming convention about type accuracy

rule: When the filtering, querying, or API capabilities of a model or endpoint change, update its documentation (scaladoc, JSDoc, comments) to reflect the new behavior.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation convention for API changes

rule: When the same logic is repeated multiple times with only parameter differences, extract it into a helper function with appropriate arguments to reduce duplication and lines of code.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence DRY convention for repeated logic

rule: Do not call .toString or other type conversion methods on values that are already the target type — check the declared type of upstream variables before converting.
mode_rationale: Simple convention against unnecessary type conversions

rule: Use the same domain term across backend and frontend layers — do not introduce an alternate name in one layer (e.g., 'tasks' in the UI vs 'goals' in the backend) without a planned migration to rename everywhere.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence naming convention for cross-layer consistency

rule: When adding a new actions or service class, add a corresponding test class following the existing naming convention (e.g., ProjectGoalActionsSpec for ProjectGoalActions).
mode_rationale: Simple convention to add tests alongside new classes

rule: Action codes must follow the established naming convention of the codebase (e.g., 'project.view_goals') — match the pattern used by existing actions for consistency.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence naming convention for action codes

rule: Terminate background polling as soon as the UI state it serves is no longer relevant, not just when the underlying process completes.
mode_rationale: Simple performance convention about polling lifecycle

rule: When tests are slow or flaky, investigate and fix the underlying cause (e.g., unmocked API calls, missing mocks) rather than increasing timeouts.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence testing convention against timeout workarounds

rule: When an index file only re-exports a default export, use the shorthand `export { default } from './Module'` instead of importing and re-exporting separately.
mode_rationale: Simple code style convention for re-export shorthand

rule: Name service interfaces based on their actual responsibility (e.g., 'ProjectRepository') rather than implementation details like authorization bypass (e.g., 'ProjectServiceWithoutAuthorization').
mode_rationale: Single-sentence naming convention for service interfaces

rule: When fixing layout or overflow bugs, ensure the solution works for all viewport sizes, not just the ones tested — avoid hardcoded values that only fix a specific screen width.
mode_rationale: Simple convention about viewport-agnostic CSS fixes

rule: Name React props to clearly indicate the type of value they accept — boolean-sounding names (e.g., `isVisible`) should not accept components, and component-accepting props should use names like `renderX` or `xComponent`.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence naming convention for React props

rule: Prefer styled-components (or CSS modules) over inline styles for component styling to maintain consistency and separation of concerns.
mode_rationale: Simple styling convention preferring styled-components

rule: Use configured module aliases instead of long relative paths when importing from the same package or section.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence import style convention

rule: When refactoring code used in Storybook stories, verify the stories still render correctly — especially check that expression changes produce equivalent output.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to check Storybook after refactors

rule: When returning collections from API endpoints, prefer ordered types (Seq/List) over unordered ones (Set) to ensure deterministic iteration order across requests.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence API convention for deterministic ordering

rule: When filtering data displayed in tables, prefer server-side filtering via API calls over client-side filtering to handle large datasets and reduce client memory usage.
mode_rationale: Simple performance convention for data filtering

rule: Wrap event handler and callback functions passed as props with React's useCallback to prevent unnecessary re-renders of child components.

Place state mutation logic (e.g., status transitions with success/error messages) inside the relevant context provider or RPC layer rather than standalone utility functions, and parameterize by target state.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence architecture convention for state management

rule: When interpolating optional values into user-facing strings, ensure the message remains clear and grammatically correct when the value is undefined — add explicit fallback text if needed.
mode_rationale: Simple defensive coding convention for UI strings

rule: Use existing API endpoints to update state rather than manually constructing state changes in the frontend; pass undefined for unused params to reuse a single API call.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence architecture convention to use existing APIs

rule: When modifying shared UI components, check that existing integration and e2e tests still pass and link failing test evidence in the PR.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to check test coverage before merging

rule: Use existing shared components (e.g., SpinnerWrapper for loading states) instead of re-implementing conditional rendering logic inline.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to prefer existing shared components over inline reimplementation

rule: Do not add explicit type casts when the type system already provides the correct type; check the existing type definitions before casting.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence rule about trusting the type system

rule: When a method or class can accept multiple value types, use generics or the most general type (e.g., Map[String, Any] instead of Map[String, String]) to avoid silently relying on type erasure.
mode_rationale: Coding convention about choosing the right generic type

rule: When writing validation logic for structured data (e.g., YAML, JSON), ensure it handles nested and complex structures, not just flat key-value maps.
mode_rationale: Simple guideline to handle nested structures in validation

rule: When cherry-picking or backporting changes, call out any diffs that are not directly related to the stated PR objective so reviewers can confirm they are intentional.
mode_rationale: Simple PR hygiene convention

rule: Review compound boolean conditions for redundancy; e.g., `(loading && !hasLoaded) || !hasLoaded` reduces to just `!hasLoaded`.
mode_rationale: Code style rule about simplifying boolean logic

rule: When a frontend or backend change depends on authorization, API contracts, or config from another service, explicitly confirm those dependencies are met in the target release.
mode_rationale: Simple review-time check, not a multi-step procedure

rule: When code makes assumptions that will change (e.g., local storage always being ready), add a comment explaining the current assumption and what will change in the future.
mode_rationale: Single convention about commenting on temporary assumptions

rule: When modifying templates or configuration generators (e.g., nginx configs), include the fully rendered output in the PR description so reviewers can verify the final result.
mode_rationale: Simple PR description convention

rule: When changing ports, constants, or configuration in one repo, identify and create matching PRs in dependent repos (e.g., workload operator, compute services) before merging.
mode_rationale: Simple rule to create matching PRs in dependent repos

rule: When setting explicit column widths in Ant Design tables, verify the total width does not exceed common viewport sizes; leave at least one column without a fixed width to absorb remaining space.
mode_rationale: Single layout guideline for Ant Design tables

rule: When a ternary or conditional expression produces different arrays or values and is repeated, extract it into a named constant (e.g., `computedStates`) to eliminate duplication.
mode_rationale: Simple DRY convention for conditional expressions

rule: Service interface methods should have documentation explaining their purpose, and parameters should use descriptive names (not generic names like 'body').
mode_rationale: Naming and documentation convention for service interfaces

rule: Before creating a new abstraction (provider, hook, utility), check if an existing one already serves the same purpose and use it instead.
mode_rationale: Simple check-before-creating convention

rule: Use a consistent async style within a function — either async/await with try-catch or promise chains with .catch(), but do not mix both patterns.
mode_rationale: Style rule about not mixing async patterns

rule: Every async request must have an exception handler; do not fire requests without catching possible failures.
mode_rationale: Simple error handling rule for async calls

rule: Append '|| true' to non-critical cleanup commands in shell scripts so a cleanup failure does not mask the exit code of the preceding critical command.
mode_rationale: Simple shell scripting convention

rule: Replace repeated string literals (e.g., "keytab") with named constants or parameters to avoid typos and ease future changes.
mode_rationale: Standard coding convention about named constants

rule: Remove all console.log statements and debugging artifacts before submitting a PR for review.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: remove console.log, use proper logging

rule: When the same logic appears in multiple places, extract it into a shared utility function and reuse it.
mode_rationale: Standard DRY convention

rule: Remove code that has no effect — if it doesn't break anything but doesn't do anything, it should not be in the codebase.
Do not prefix variable names with underscores in frontend code; use descriptive names instead.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for frontend code

rule: Use a single, consistent encoding strategy (e.g., encodeFilePath vs encodeURIComponent) across all routes; do not mix approaches without justification.
mode_rationale: Consistency convention for encoding strategy

rule: When handling file or directory paths, verify behavior with special characters (spaces, question marks, etc.) in every segment of the path.
mode_rationale: Simple testing guideline for path handling

rule: Do not pass implicit arguments explicitly when an implicit value is already in scope; let the compiler resolve them.
mode_rationale: Scala idiom convention about implicit resolution

rule: When upgrading CLI tools or dependencies that interact with versioned infrastructure (e.g., kubectl ↔ Kubernetes), verify the version skew policy and check with affected teams before bumping.
mode_rationale: Simple pre-upgrade check convention

rule: Before encoding a URL or path, check whether downstream utilities already apply encoding to avoid double-encoding bugs.
mode_rationale: Simple defensive check before encoding

rule: Use encodeURIComponent only on individual path segments or query values, never on full URLs or paths containing slashes.
mode_rationale: Single rule about encodeURIComponent scope

rule: When a utility function already exists for an operation (e.g., encodeFilePath), use it instead of inlining equivalent logic.
When changing code behavior, remove or update comments that no longer accurately describe the code.
mode_rationale: Comment hygiene rule

rule: When applying string transformations like encoding or replacement to URLs or paths, verify the transformation only affects the intended segment and does not corrupt other parts of the string.
mode_rationale: Simple correctness guideline for string operations

rule: When code compares two values that seem equivalent on the surface (e.g., locationContext vs window.location), add a comment explaining why the comparison is necessary.
mode_rationale: Documentation convention for subtle comparisons

rule: Always await async function calls when they are inside try/catch blocks; without await, the catch block will not handle rejected promises.
mode_rationale: Simple async/await correctness rule

rule: Fetch data in the component that renders it, not in a parent; use tab lifecycle hooks (e.g., destroyOnInactiveTab) so data refreshes when the component becomes active.
Router components that are mounted at all times must not trigger global side effects like loaders or spinners scoped to a particular section.
mode_rationale: Single routing architecture rule

rule: Do not define interfaces or types with no properties unless there is a concrete, immediate plan to populate them in a follow-up PR.
mode_rationale: Simple code organization rule

rule: When adding items to a navigation sidebar or tabbed view, verify the same page is not already reachable from another menu point to prevent confusing duplicate entries.
mode_rationale: Simple UI architecture check

rule: When implementing permission checks for a resource type, design the check to handle multiple instances of that resource rather than hard-coding for a single one.
mode_rationale: Single design guideline for permission checks

rule: Operational parameters like cache settings, timeouts, and thresholds should be configurable via feature flags (CC Flags) rather than hardcoded, so they can be tuned without redeployment.
mode_rationale: Configuration convention for operational params

rule: When defining metrics or timers, ensure each description accurately describes the specific time range or quantity being measured — do not copy-paste descriptions between metrics that measure different things.
mode_rationale: Simple accuracy rule for metric definitions

rule: When code behaves differently for specific cases (e.g., skipping redaction for one entity type but not others), add an inline comment explaining why to preserve context for future readers.
mode_rationale: Documentation convention for special-case logic

rule: When a boolean flag controls state transitions (e.g., promoting/unpromoting), write tests for both directions to ensure the flag doesn't incorrectly trigger behavior for the reverse transition.
mode_rationale: Simple testing guideline for boolean flags

rule: Name classes, events, and methods using the same domain terminology used elsewhere in the codebase rather than introducing synonyms (e.g., use 'MarkAsOfficial' consistently instead of mixing in 'Promotion').
mode_rationale: Naming consistency convention

rule: Before adding implicit formats, serializers, or other boilerplate to a class, check if the parent trait or class already provides them — avoid redundant declarations.
mode_rationale: Simple inheritance hygiene rule

rule: When an entity requires non-trivial or custom serialization logic, keep the serializer in a separate class (e.g., implementing a Serializer trait) rather than coupling it directly to the domain event or entity class.
mode_rationale: Single separation-of-concerns guideline

rule: When a function has many arguments (especially optional ones), use an object/dictionary parameter instead of positional arguments to avoid fragile call sites and trailing undefined values.
mode_rationale: API design convention for function signatures

rule: When wrapping an API that uses `any`, use `unknown` in your own code to prevent propagating type unsafety — even if the upstream type is `any`.
mode_rationale: Type safety convention at API boundaries

rule: Always use primary/secondary text colors and other design tokens from the theme instead of hardcoding color values, to ensure consistency and support theming.
mode_rationale: Simple styling convention for design tokens

rule: If a variable is already defaulted to a non-nullable value (e.g., `= []`), do not use optional chaining (`?.`) on it — the guard is redundant and misleads readers about nullability.
mode_rationale: Code clarity rule about unnecessary guards

rule: Files within a package must not import from their own package's published name (e.g., `@domino/ui`); use relative imports instead to avoid circular dependencies and bundling issues.
Move visibility and rendering logic inside the component itself rather than keeping it in the parent; the component should decide whether it renders.
mode_rationale: Single convention about where rendering logic lives

rule: Use lowercase for file names and kebab-case for directory names in the frontend codebase.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention

rule: Do not merge PRs with temporary or unpinned dependency versions; re-pin versions once the upstream branch or package is merged.
mode_rationale: Single rule about dependency version hygiene

rule: When implementing features that reference an external provider (e.g., GitHub), verify the behavior is correct for all supported providers (e.g., Bitbucket, GitLab) or scope the feature to the applicable provider only.
mode_rationale: Single guideline about multi-provider correctness

rule: Avoid explicit `any` in TypeScript; prefer `as const`, proper type assertions, or narrower types.
mode_rationale: Simple TypeScript typing convention

rule: Use the AppContent component for page layout (title, breadcrumb, page actions) instead of building custom page chrome.
mode_rationale: Single rule about using AppContent for page layout

rule: Use consistent field names across related entities in the codebase; prefer the naming convention already established by sibling entities (e.g., use `name` instead of `title` if other event entities use `name`).
mode_rationale: Simple naming consistency convention

rule: Remove debug utilities, test scaffolding, and temporary code changes before merging a PR.
mode_rationale: Single cleanup rule before merge

rule: Use already-available contextual data (e.g., `principal.eventUser`) instead of making additional service calls to fetch the same information.
mode_rationale: Simple performance convention about avoiding redundant fetches

rule: When displaying optional values in user-facing contexts (e.g., audit trails), resolve None/null to a human-readable default like "Unassigned" using a helper function.
mode_rationale: Single display convention for null/absent values

rule: Component names should omit generic suffixes like 'Page' or 'View' to match existing naming conventions in the codebase.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention

rule: Async data-fetching functions should return their results so callers can use them directly, rather than relying on state updates and extra useEffects to chain dependent calls.
mode_rationale: Single convention about async data flow in React

rule: Avoid disabling or overriding ESLint rules in source files; fix the underlying issue instead.
mode_rationale: Simple rule about not disabling ESLint

rule: Initialize optional values as undefined rather than empty objects when downstream code uses Boolean filtering, since Boolean({}) evaluates to true.
mode_rationale: Single convention about optional state initialization

rule: Files and route references must be placed in the correct domain module; do not add cross-module references that violate module ownership.
mode_rationale: Single architectural boundary rule

rule: When code branches on a feature flag, add a TODO comment with consistent text across all affected locations so the code can be easily found and removed when the flag is retired.
mode_rationale: Single convention about feature flag cleanup tracking

rule: Source files must live in the module directory that owns their domain concept, not in an adjacent or related module.
mode_rationale: Single file placement convention

rule: When a function has more than 2-3 parameters, pass an options object instead of positional arguments to avoid calls with multiple undefined placeholders.
mode_rationale: Simple API design convention for parameter passing

rule: When removing a feature or component, find and remove all related references, types, and dead code paths rather than leaving orphaned remnants.
mode_rationale: Single cleanup convention about thorough dead code removal

rule: Replace magic numbers with named constants to improve readability and make the intent of the value clear.
When executing multiple async calls with Promise.all, destructure and use the returned values directly rather than reading from state that may not have updated yet.
mode_rationale: Single convention about using fresh async return values

rule: In OpenAPI/Swagger specs, only document expected status codes that clients should handle; do not routinely document generic 500 errors unless existing endpoints already do so for consistency.
mode_rationale: Simple API documentation convention

rule: When tests are flaky or slow, investigate the root cause (unmocked API calls, missing test utilities, inefficient selectors) rather than simply increasing timeouts.
mode_rationale: Single guideline about debugging flaky tests properly

rule: Prefer data-test attributes over CSS query selectors for locating elements in tests to improve test resilience and readability.
mode_rationale: Simple testing convention for element selection

rule: Use shared test utility functions (e.g., for picking select values, clicking checkboxes) rather than reimplementing common interaction patterns in each test file.
mode_rationale: Single convention about reusing test helpers

rule: When a display value may be absent, provide a meaningful human-readable fallback (e.g., derived from another field) instead of an empty string, especially for audit trails and user-facing logs.
mode_rationale: Single display convention for absent values

rule: Put changes to external service integrations (especially cloud provider APIs) behind feature flags/central config to allow rollback, since corner cases often surface only in production.
mode_rationale: Single convention about feature-flagging risky integrations

rule: When adding a new prop to a shared component, make it optional (with a default or callback) if not all consumers need it, to avoid breaking existing usages.
mode_rationale: Simple API design rule for shared component props

rule: Remove unused code (commented-out blocks, state without setters, unreachable paths) rather than leaving it in the codebase; version control preserves history.
mode_rationale: Single code hygiene convention

rule: Child components should not bubble errors up to parent containers via prop drilling; use context providers or localized error boundaries instead so failures are scoped to the affected widget.
mode_rationale: Single convention about error boundary scoping

rule: When a code change alters navigation behavior (e.g., introducing a full page reload where there was none), validate the UX impact with the design team before merging.
mode_rationale: Single rule about design validation for nav changes

rule: Every user-facing operation that can fail must display error feedback to the user; never silently swallow errors in UI code.
mode_rationale: Single convention about never swallowing UI errors

rule: When submitting user-generated content (e.g., markdown with mentions), apply all required formatters (like formatMentions) before sending data to the backend, consistent with existing usage patterns.
mode_rationale: Single data formatting convention

rule: New React components should separate the container (logic/state) from the view (presentation) into distinct layers.
mode_rationale: Single architectural pattern for component structure

rule: When persisting user preferences or state to storage, use the existing Preferences utility (or UiUx state API) rather than writing custom localStorage logic.
mode_rationale: Single convention about using existing Preferences utility

rule: When adding new functionality to an already large file, extract reusable logic into a separate global utility file instead of making the file longer.
mode_rationale: Single convention about file size management

rule: When implementing a pattern that must be updated when the code is extended (e.g., adding a new sortable column requires updating sort persistence logic), leave a comment explaining the maintenance requirement.
mode_rationale: Single documentation convention for extensible code

rule: When a directory has an index.ts barrel file, import from the index rather than directly from the underlying module files to maintain proper encapsulation.
mode_rationale: Simple import convention for encapsulation

rule: Do not leave commented-out code (especially console.log statements) in production files; remove them before merging.
Extract hardcoded constants (URLs, config values, magic strings) into dedicated constants files rather than defining them inline in hook or component files.
mode_rationale: Single convention about constant placement

rule: When a code change breaks existing tests, fix the tests rather than disabling or deleting them; if a fix isn't immediately possible, document the plan to restore them.
mode_rationale: Single rule about maintaining test coverage

rule: When using utility functions that may return a promise or a resolved value, add explicit strict checks rather than relying on truthy/falsy coercion, as a Promise object is always truthy.
mode_rationale: Single convention about strict checks on async returns

rule: When fixing a bug for a specific case, look for a generic solution that handles the entire class of problems rather than patching only the immediate instance.
mode_rationale: Single guideline about fix scope

rule: Use language-provided URI/URL builder classes to construct URLs with query parameters rather than manually concatenating strings, which can produce invalid URLs when parameters already exist.
mode_rationale: Single convention about URL construction safety

rule: Name function parameters to describe their purpose (e.g., `defaultResult` or `defaultRedirect`) rather than using generic names like `fn` that give no indication of the parameter's role.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention

rule: When importing multiple items from the same module, combine them into a single import statement rather than using separate import lines.
mode_rationale: Simple code style convention

rule: When creating a new file for a specific feature, consider whether the file could host related functionality in the future and name it generically to avoid premature renaming.
mode_rationale: Single file naming convention

rule: When a configuration value controls a limit (e.g., max file size), do not default to 0 if undefined — a zero default can silently block all operations unless explicitly handled as a special case.
mode_rationale: Single convention about safe default values

rule: Define shared constants for common unit conversions (e.g., MB_IN_BYTES = 1024 * 1024) and reuse them across the codebase instead of inlining magic numbers.
mode_rationale: Single convention about shared conversion constants

rule: When comparing sizes or measurements, convert the limit to the base unit and compare directly (e.g., file.size > limitInMB * MB_IN_BYTES) rather than dividing and rounding, which introduces precision errors.
mode_rationale: Single convention about precision in comparisons

rule: When a React component makes multiple API calls that control rendering, extract the data-fetching and state logic into a custom hook to keep the component focused on presentation and simplify testing.
mode_rationale: Single guideline about where to place data-fetching logic

rule: When a component conditionally renders based on an API response, always handle and surface errors to the user (e.g., via a toast notification) — otherwise a failed request results in a blank screen with no explanation.
mode_rationale: Single rule: always surface errors to the user

rule: In component unit tests, mock the custom hook rather than the underlying API calls — this avoids unnecessary async waits for mocked requests and keeps component tests focused on rendering logic, with hook logic tested separately.
mode_rationale: Single testing convention about what to mock

rule: Custom React hooks should be organized in a dedicated `hooks` directory rather than placed alongside component files, to maintain a consistent and discoverable project structure.
mode_rationale: Simple file organization convention

rule: Always reset loading/fetching state (e.g., setIsFetching(false)) in catch blocks to prevent the UI from being stuck in an infinite loading state when an API call fails.
mode_rationale: Single rule about resetting state in catch blocks

rule: Use useMemo for objects passed as context provider values to prevent unnecessary re-renders of all consumers on every parent render.
