mode_rationale: Single performance convention about useMemo

rule: When calling refetch after a state update, verify the new state has been applied before the request fires to avoid race conditions where the request uses stale state.
mode_rationale: Single guideline about verifying state before refetch

rule: Move reusable logic out of components and into dedicated utility modules to improve testability and reuse.
mode_rationale: Simple code organization principle

rule: Order imports with third-party/library imports before relative/local imports to maintain consistent and readable import blocks.
mode_rationale: Simple import ordering convention

rule: Common/shared components should use TypeScript generics instead of hardcoded types like string to maintain flexibility for all consumers.
mode_rationale: Single typing guideline for shared components

rule: Pure presentational components (containers, atoms) that are not connected to business logic should live in core-ui, not in feature-specific UI packages.
mode_rationale: Simple rule about where presentational components belong

rule: Use the dataTest prop (which renders as data-test) for test selectors instead of other data attributes or class-based selectors.
mode_rationale: Single convention for test selector attributes

rule: Use the shared useAuthorization and useGlobalAuthorization hooks for permission checks instead of manually resolving authorization promises in components.
mode_rationale: Single rule: use existing auth hooks instead of manual resolution

rule: Combine multiple import statements from the same module into a single import to keep files clean and avoid redundancy.
mode_rationale: Simple import hygiene rule

rule: Review and remove TODO comments that are no longer valid or have already been addressed before merging a PR.
mode_rationale: Single pre-merge cleanup convention

rule: Consolidate all imports from the same module into a single import statement; never have duplicate import lines from the same path.
mode_rationale: Simple import deduplication rule

rule: When moving or restructuring code, audit existing comments and TODOs to verify they are still accurate; remove or update any that no longer apply.
mode_rationale: Single guideline about comment maintenance during refactors

rule: Explore type-safe alternatives before resorting to @ts-ignore; if unavoidable, document why the suppression is necessary.
mode_rationale: Single rule about avoiding type suppression

rule: When multiple API calls depend on each other or must not run concurrently, ensure they are properly awaited and executed sequentially to prevent race conditions.
mode_rationale: Single concurrency guideline about sequential awaits

rule: Never modify antd library CSS classes from application code; instead, create wrapper components (e.g., IconWrapper) to apply custom styling without touching library internals.
mode_rationale: Single styling convention about not touching library CSS

rule: Never use div elements styled as buttons; use the design system's Button component with the appropriate props (btnType, isDanger, icon, etc.) for correct semantics and accessibility.
mode_rationale: Single accessibility/semantics rule

rule: When modifying shared/base components (e.g., CodeEditor), manually validate that changes do not introduce regressions in existing consumers, especially for interactive behaviors like auto-close brackets.
mode_rationale: Single testing principle about checking consumers

rule: When re-enabling a previously skipped flaky test, include an actual code fix that addresses the flakiness and remove the skip comment; do not simply un-skip the test without evidence of a fix.
mode_rationale: Single rule: include a real fix when un-skipping tests

rule: Preserve the existing correct import ordering when making changes; do not reorganize imports that were already correctly placed as part of an unrelated change.
mode_rationale: Simple rule about not reorganizing unrelated imports

rule: Never use dependencies that are not listed in package.json; if a polyfill or library is needed, explicitly add it as a dependency (or devDependency) before importing it.
mode_rationale: Single dependency management convention

rule: In Scala, use == instead of .equals() for equality checks; == is null-safe and more idiomatic, improving readability.
mode_rationale: Simple Scala idiom preference

rule: Each React component should be defined in its own file; do not co-locate multiple components in a single file.
Organize import statements with absolute (third-party/library) imports first, followed by relative (local) imports.
mode_rationale: Simple import ordering convention

rule: Do not add eslint-disable comments when the violation can be resolved by restructuring code; for example, omit unused function parameters instead of suppressing the lint warning.
mode_rationale: Single lint hygiene rule

rule: Remove all console.log statements from production code before merging; use proper logging utilities instead of console output for debugging.
Move CI setup steps (like tool installation) into the specific jobs that use them rather than defining them globally, to prepare for extraction and keep CI configuration clearly separated by concern.
mode_rationale: Single CI organization principle

rule: When adding or modifying API endpoints, update the route summary/documentation to reflect the new behavior.
mode_rationale: Single documentation maintenance rule

rule: When conditional branches are mutually exclusive, use if/else rather than separate if statements to make the logical relationship explicit and prevent unintended fall-through.
mode_rationale: Simple coding style convention

rule: When bumping package versions for a release branch, ensure the version string matches the release branch naming convention (e.g., 6.0.2-... for a 6.0.2 release branch).
mode_rationale: Single versioning convention

rule: When tests hit timeouts, investigate and fix the root cause rather than masking the problem with jest.retryTimes or further timeout increases.
mode_rationale: Single testing principle about root-cause fixes

rule: Use Jest automock for module mocking in tests instead of manually mocking each dependency.
mode_rationale: Single testing convention about mocking approach

rule: Do not use deprecated HTML attributes like frameBorder; use CSS equivalents instead.
mode_rationale: Simple rule: use CSS instead of deprecated attributes

rule: Never import from a package's dist output from within the same package; use source-level imports instead.
mode_rationale: Single import convention about source vs dist

rule: When creating wrapper components for HTML elements, extend the native HTML prop types (e.g., React.DetailedHTMLProps<React.IframeHTMLAttributes<HTMLIFrameElement>, HTMLIFrameElement>) instead of manually listing individual props.
mode_rationale: Single typing guideline for wrapper components

rule: Add event listeners inside useEffect hooks and return a cleanup function to remove them on unmount; never add listeners directly in the render body of a function component.
mode_rationale: Single React lifecycle convention

rule: Extract magic numbers and repeated literal values into named constants; if a value is used in multiple places, define it once and import it.
Do not return the result of an assignment expression; split the assignment and return into separate statements for clarity.
mode_rationale: Simple code clarity rule

rule: Include the unit of measurement as a suffix in constant names (e.g., COUNTDOWN_MS, TIMEOUT_SECONDS) so the unit is unambiguous at the call site.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention for constants with units

rule: Remove commented-out code before merging; rely on version control history instead of keeping dead code in comments.
Sensible default styles (like removing borders) should be built into the component itself rather than requiring every consumer to override them.
mode_rationale: Single component design principle

rule: Extract hardcoded timeout values into named constants and expose them as optional props so consumers can configure them without modifying the component.
mode_rationale: Single API design guideline about exposing configuration

rule: Use the project's theme helper or design token system for colors instead of hardcoding hex values directly in components.
mode_rationale: Single styling convention about design tokens

rule: Use java.time.Instant instead of legacy date types for timestamps in Scala/Java code for smaller memory footprint, built-in comparison support, and modern API compatibility.
mode_rationale: Single type choice convention for timestamps

rule: Remove documentation files that have been superseded by newer docs or tooling changes to avoid misleading developers.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation hygiene rule

rule: Use the `history` object (from React Router) for in-app redirections instead of direct window.location assignments.
mode_rationale: Single navigation convention for React Router apps

rule: Use the `safeGetErrorMessage` utility to extract error messages instead of ad-hoc error parsing.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence convention to use a specific utility for error message extraction

rule: Never use `any` as a type annotation; use specific types like `Record<string, unknown>` or a custom interface instead.
New Storybook stories should not use the `.mdx` format; use the current CSF (Component Story Format) instead.
mode_rationale: Single convention about which Storybook format to use

rule: Use the `uuid` library to generate unique identifiers instead of custom ID-generation logic.
mode_rationale: Simple rule to use a standard library for ID generation

rule: Delete unused code rather than commenting it out; version control preserves history.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence code hygiene convention

rule: When moving or refactoring imports, ensure `eslint-disable` comments remain attached to the specific import they were suppressing.
mode_rationale: Simple convention about maintaining eslint-disable comments during refactors

rule: Import shared modules from their canonical package (e.g., `@domino/core`, `@domino/core-ui`) to keep a single change point for future package extractions.
mode_rationale: Single import convention rule

rule: Use the `useDate` hook for all date operations instead of calling date libraries (dayjs, moment) directly, so the underlying library can be swapped in one place.
mode_rationale: Simple rule to use a specific hook for date operations

rule: Use semantic color tokens from `palette` (e.g., `palette.danger`) instead of raw color values or the legacy `colors` object.
mode_rationale: Single convention about using semantic color tokens

rule: Before creating a new UI element, check if a shared component already exists (e.g., `Search`) and use it instead of building a duplicate.
Name section-level components with their parent context (e.g., `CostCenterHome` instead of `Home`) to avoid ambiguity with platform-wide concepts.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention for section-level components

rule: Add TypeScript types to all component props and data structures, even when using mocked data during development.
mode_rationale: Simple rule to always add TypeScript types to props and data

rule: Use `unknown` instead of `any` for component props that are passed through without being accessed, to maintain type safety.
mode_rationale: Single type safety convention for pass-through props

rule: Use styled components instead of inline styles, especially when the same styles are repeated across multiple components.
mode_rationale: Simple styling convention to prefer styled components

rule: Use theme helper functions and tokens (e.g., font-weight, spacing) instead of hardcoded values to maintain design system consistency.
mode_rationale: Single convention about using theme tokens over hardcoded values

rule: Export anything used outside a module through its index file so external consumers don't depend on internal module structure.
mode_rationale: Simple export convention for module boundaries

rule: Tab components should persist their active tab in the URL and support setting the initial tab from URL parameters for shareability and navigation.
mode_rationale: Single convention about persisting tab state in URL parameters

rule: For API-backed tables, plan for server-side pagination and filtering rather than filtering results locally, as endpoints will eventually support pagination.
mode_rationale: Simple architectural guideline for API-backed tables

rule: When a shared component already exists, use it instead of writing an equivalent inline implementation to avoid code duplication.
mode_rationale: Single convention to avoid duplicating existing shared components

rule: Remove empty files from commits; placeholder files with no content add noise to the codebase.
Use components from shared packages (e.g., `@domino/core-ui/Breadcrumbs`) instead of building custom equivalents.
mode_rationale: Single convention to use shared packages over custom equivalents

rule: Use configured path aliases (e.g., `ui/`, `components/`) for imports instead of relative paths to improve readability and avoid deep relative traversals.
mode_rationale: Simple import style convention

rule: Use UPPER_CASE (SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE) naming for constant values to distinguish them from mutable variables.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention for constants

rule: Extract repeated utility functions into a shared location rather than duplicating them across modules.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to extract repeated utilities into shared locations

rule: Extract repeated or complex inline styles into named styled components (e.g., StyledDatePickerWithInput) for readability and reuse.
mode_rationale: Single styling convention for repeated/complex inline styles

rule: When a default value is available (e.g., from a theme provider), use it directly instead of wrapping the component in a higher-order component like withTheme.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to avoid unnecessary HOC wrapping

rule: Use purpose-built wrapper components (e.g., TooltipWrapper) instead of lower-level renderer helper functions for better composability and consistency.
mode_rationale: Single convention about component composition pattern

rule: When multiple methods perform similar operations (e.g., parsing API responses), ensure error handling is uniform across all of them rather than having ad-hoc handling in individual methods.
mode_rationale: Simple guideline to keep error handling uniform across similar methods

rule: Resolve colors from the theme at the component that owns the rendering and pass them as props, rather than wrapping components in withTheme or resolving colors deep in helper functions.
mode_rationale: Single convention about where to resolve theme colors

rule: Always use colors from the design system palette or theme helper instead of hardcoding color values; if a color exists in the palette, reference it by key.
mode_rationale: Simple rule to reference palette colors by key

rule: Files inside a package (e.g., @domino/ui) must not import from that package's public entry point; use relative imports to the source files instead.
mode_rationale: Single import convention to avoid circular package references

rule: Wrap debounce functions in useMemo (or useCallback) so the debounce reference is not regenerated on every re-render, which would break the debounce behavior.
mode_rationale: Simple performance convention for debounce in React

rule: For search components, prefer the onSearch prop (triggered on Enter or search icon click) over manual debounced onChange handlers, for consistency across the app.
mode_rationale: Single convention about using onSearch over debounced onChange

rule: Number formatting, date formatting, and other localization concerns should be addressed as an integral app-wide solution rather than applied ad-hoc in individual components.
mode_rationale: Simple architectural guideline against ad-hoc localization

rule: Do not spread entire objects into React hook dependency arrays when the individual properties are already listed as dependencies; remove redundant object-level entries to avoid unnecessary re-renders.
mode_rationale: Simple React performance convention for dependency arrays

rule: Instead of wrapping components in the withTheme HOC to access theme colors, use IconResolver (which accepts palette color keys directly) or component color props to eliminate the need for the HOC.
mode_rationale: Single convention to avoid withTheme HOC in favor of direct props

rule: Tests that have been skipped for an extended period (months or more) with no plan to re-enable should be deleted rather than carried forward as dead code.
mode_rationale: Simple test hygiene rule about long-skipped tests

rule: Use components from the internal packages/ui library (e.g., Tag from packages/ui) instead of importing directly from Ant Design, as the internal components expose theme-aware props like color keys.
mode_rationale: Single import convention to use internal wrappers over Ant Design directly

rule: When a component accepts color key props (e.g., text color and background color), pass the palette key string directly rather than resolving it to a color value in an intermediate variable.
mode_rationale: Simple convention about passing palette keys directly

rule: When styling theme-specific elements, use semantic palette colors (e.g., bgDanger) instead of raw color tokens or system tokens to maintain consistency with the design system.
mode_rationale: Single styling convention for semantic palette usage

rule: When an API request fails, always provide visible feedback to the user rather than only logging to console.error; the screen should never silently remain empty.
mode_rationale: Simple UX convention to always show visible error feedback

rule: Ensure the initial loading state displays a loader or skeleton; do not rely on checking whether data is empty, as that conflates 'loading' with 'no results'.
mode_rationale: Simple UX convention to display loaders during initial fetch

rule: When navigating between React pages, use React Router Link components instead of anchor tags or window.location to avoid full-page reloads.
mode_rationale: Single convention to use React Router for in-app navigation

rule: Define table column configurations outside the component's render function to avoid unnecessary re-creation on each render cycle.
mode_rationale: Simple performance convention for table column definitions

rule: When a component exposes a dedicated prop for a style property (e.g., iconSize), use the prop instead of applying an inline style override.
mode_rationale: Single convention to prefer component props over inline style overrides

rule: When wrapping a component, omit props from the exposed type definition that are managed internally and not intended to be controlled by the parent (e.g., disabled, options populated from a fetch).
mode_rationale: Simple API design convention for wrapper component type definitions

rule: When a string literal is used in production code and also asserted in a unit test, export it as a named constant so both share a single source of truth.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to share string literals between production code and tests

rule: Export error message strings as named constants rather than inlining them, enabling reuse and consistent error messaging across modules.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to export error messages as named constants

rule: When a value is limited to a known set of options, define a union type rather than using a generic string or number type.
mode_rationale: Single type safety convention for known value sets

rule: Dynamic UI text (e.g., 'Login with SSO') must be conditional on whether the referenced feature is actually enabled, to avoid misleading users.
mode_rationale: Simple UX convention to make dynamic text conditional on feature state

rule: Use color tokens according to their semantic purpose — text palette colors for text, background palette colors for backgrounds — never cross-purpose system tokens.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence convention about matching color tokens to their semantic purpose

rule: Input components should support a controlled mode where the parent provides the value via props, not only an uncontrolled internal-state mode.
mode_rationale: Design guideline for input component API surface

rule: In Storybook stories, use the built-in Storybook actions addon for event logging instead of plain console.log calls.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to prefer Storybook actions over console.log

rule: Async permission-check functions that return promises must be awaited before using the result in conditionals, because an unresolved promise is truthy.
In styled-components, access theme colors directly in the component definition via the theme object rather than passing color values as props from the parent.
mode_rationale: Style convention for accessing theme directly in styled-components

rule: For error or warning UI states, use the semantic palette colors (danger, warning) instead of raw color tokens or hex values.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to use semantic palette for error/warning states

rule: Color tokens designated for font color must not be used for backgrounds, and vice versa — each token category has a defined purpose that must be respected.
mode_rationale: Single rule about respecting color token category boundaries

rule: When you only need part of a composed component, use the exposed base component directly rather than hiding UI elements of the higher-level wrapper.
mode_rationale: Architecture guideline preferring base components over hiding parts

rule: Use the predefined elevation and shadow styles from the design system (Storybook) instead of defining custom box-shadow values.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to use design system shadows instead of custom values

rule: Use color tokens that match the semantic purpose of the element — e.g., use border color tokens for grid lines and borders, not text color tokens.
mode_rationale: Convention about matching token purpose to element purpose

rule: When rendering a list of items, avoid making one API call per item; consider batch endpoints or aggregated queries to prevent request storms.
mode_rationale: Single performance guideline about batching API calls

rule: Wrapper and composed components must accept and forward data-test (or dataTest) props to their root container so Cypress tests can target them.
mode_rationale: Testing convention to forward data-test props for Cypress targeting

rule: When a styled component accepts a color prop, type it to accept design token keys rather than raw hex values to maintain consistency with the design system.
mode_rationale: Type convention for color props to accept token keys not hex

rule: When transforming an array into another array of the same length, use map() instead of reduce() for clarity and intent.
mode_rationale: Simple code clarity convention for array transforms

rule: Expensive data transformations and computations inside React components should be memoized (useMemo) to avoid recalculating on every re-render.
mode_rationale: Performance convention to use useMemo for expensive computations

rule: When a styled component already exists for an element, add styles there instead of using inline style attributes — avoids duplication and keeps styling co-located.
mode_rationale: Style co-location convention

rule: Use the project's existing useDate utilities for date formatting and localization instead of writing custom date logic, to ensure consistent locale-aware formatting.
mode_rationale: Convention to use project's useDate utilities over custom logic

rule: Remove all console.log statements before submitting a PR; they are debug artifacts that should not ship to production.
When data will be consumed by a standard UI component (e.g., Select), shape it using the component's expected interface (e.g., OptionProp) rather than inventing a custom shape.
mode_rationale: Convention to shape data to match component interfaces

rule: TypeScript interface and type names must start with an uppercase letter, following PascalCase convention.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for TypeScript types

rule: When a component pattern is duplicated across many files with only minor variations, extract it into a single reusable component that accepts props for the varying parts.
mode_rationale: DRY convention for duplicated component patterns

rule: If a function is responsible for one side of a state transition (e.g., turning something off), it should also handle the symmetric side (turning it on) to keep related logic co-located.
mode_rationale: Code organization guideline to co-locate symmetric logic

rule: Import from the directory path, not from the explicit index file — write `import X from './module'` not `import X from './module/index'`.
mode_rationale: Import style convention

rule: Delete code, styles, and imports that are no longer needed rather than leaving them in place.
mode_rationale: Simple cleanup convention

rule: When logic in a component could be reused elsewhere, move it to a shared utility module rather than keeping it inline.
mode_rationale: Convention to move reusable logic to shared modules

rule: Use the project's internal wrapper components (e.g., RouteLink) instead of directly using the underlying library components (e.g., react-router Link).
mode_rationale: Architecture convention to prefer project wrappers over raw libraries

rule: Use the component's semantic props (e.g., iconSize) instead of overriding with generic CSS properties (e.g., fontSize) to ensure consistency and future-proof styling.
mode_rationale: Convention to use semantic props over CSS overrides

rule: Always use the design system's spacing theme values (e.g., spacing.sm, spacing.md) for margins and paddings instead of hardcoded pixel values.
mode_rationale: Design system convention for spacing values

rule: Use CSS flex layouts with flex-grow to dynamically fill available space rather than using fixed dimensions or manual calculations.
mode_rationale: Layout convention preferring flex over fixed dimensions

rule: When adding new fields to a data model or event, add at least one unit test that verifies the new fields are correctly extracted and populated, not just left as None.
mode_rationale: Testing convention to cover new data model fields

rule: Use colors from the design system palette or system tokens instead of defining custom color values, especially as custom colors are being deprecated.
mode_rationale: Convention to use palette colors instead of custom values

rule: Use the design system's Typography component for rendering text instead of raw HTML elements like span or div to ensure consistent font styles across the application.
mode_rationale: Convention to use Typography component over raw HTML elements

rule: When using component props that may have interdependencies, verify that the specific combination of props produces the expected behavior before submitting.
mode_rationale: Testing convention to verify prop interdependencies

rule: Define static configuration objects outside of React components, or use useMemo if they depend on props, to avoid unnecessary re-creation on every render.
mode_rationale: Performance convention to avoid recreating static objects on render

rule: Move helper functions out of component files into dedicated util files to separate concerns and keep components focused on rendering.
mode_rationale: Code organization convention to separate helpers from components

rule: Prefer existing project utility hooks (e.g., useDate) over raw browser APIs (e.g., Date) to ensure consistent behavior and formatting across the codebase.
mode_rationale: Convention to prefer project hooks over raw browser APIs

rule: Use optional parameters (param?: Type) instead of explicit union types (param: Type | undefined) for cleaner, more idiomatic TypeScript signatures.
mode_rationale: TypeScript idiom convention for optional params

rule: Avoid using `any` in TypeScript; find or define the correct type for variables and parameters to preserve type safety.
mode_rationale: Type safety convention to avoid any

rule: New or custom color values must be validated with the design team and should come from the standard design palette, not be introduced ad-hoc.
mode_rationale: Process guideline for color approval

rule: Never hardcode color values in components; always reference colors from the theme or design palette to ensure consistency and support theming.
mode_rationale: Convention to always reference theme colors

rule: Use the useThemeColor hook to access theme colors by key instead of manually reading from the theme object, as it encapsulates the lookup logic.
mode_rationale: Convention to use the useThemeColor hook for color lookups

rule: Only add resource requests (e.g., GPU) to Kubernetes specs when the requested amount is greater than zero, matching the pattern used for scheduling workloads.
mode_rationale: Single guard condition convention for Kubernetes resource specs

rule: Pass query parameters as explicit arguments in route definitions and controller methods rather than extracting them manually from the request, following the Play framework convention.
mode_rationale: Play framework convention for query parameter handling

rule: When checking that all values in a collection of Options are defined, use `forall(_.isDefined)` instead of `exists(_.isDefined)` — exists returns true if any single value is defined, which can cause bugs when all values are required.
mode_rationale: Scala convention to avoid subtle exists vs forall bug

rule: When handling errors, pass the actual error message through to the UI or caller rather than silently swallowing it, so users can understand what went wrong.
mode_rationale: Error handling convention to surface messages instead of swallowing

rule: Use Apache URIBuilder (or equivalent) to construct URLs with query parameters instead of manual string concatenation, ensuring correct encoding and cleaner code.
mode_rationale: Convention to use URIBuilder over string concatenation

rule: When working with Option[String] or other Option types in Scala, use .getOrElse() to provide a default value or handle the missing case explicitly rather than assuming the value exists.
mode_rationale: Scala convention for handling Option types with defaults

rule: Use the `query-string` library's `stringify` utility for constructing URL query strings instead of manual string concatenation or template literals.
mode_rationale: Convention to use query-string library for URL construction

rule: Use camelCase for all JavaScript/TypeScript variable and prop names in React components.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for JS/TS variables and props

rule: Name boolean props using positive framing (e.g., `keepValuesOnSubmit`) rather than double negatives (e.g., `doNotResetValuesOnSubmit`) to improve readability.
mode_rationale: Naming convention to avoid double negatives in boolean props

rule: Do not add props to a base or parent component that are only handled in a subclass or derived component; it is misleading for consumers who expect the prop to work at the base level. Scope such props to the component that actually implements the behavior.
mode_rationale: Single convention about prop scoping in component hierarchies

rule: Ensure implementation changes are scoped precisely to what the ticket describes — do not apply fixes to both success and error paths when the ticket only asks for the failure case.
mode_rationale: Single guideline about keeping changes scoped to ticket requirements

rule: When implementing a plugin or field registry pattern, centralize the registry keys to prevent collisions between independently registered components, especially when registration order is non-deterministic.
mode_rationale: Single convention about centralizing registry keys to prevent collisions

rule: When auto-collapsing or hiding UI based on empty data, ensure the behavior only applies on initial render and not on every subsequent data change (e.g., after applying filters), as this can confuse users.
mode_rationale: Single guideline about limiting auto-collapse behavior to initial render

rule: Do not use useEffect to synchronize state that can be handled by passing a callback prop directly; prefer prop-based handlers over effect-driven state synchronization.
mode_rationale: Single convention preferring prop callbacks over effect-driven state sync

rule: Before conditionally rendering or collapsing UI based on data emptiness, verify that the loading state has resolved to avoid acting on data that simply hasn't arrived yet.
mode_rationale: Single guard condition to check before acting on data emptiness

rule: UI copy must use the correct domain-specific action verb for each entity type (e.g., 'unregistered' for data plane storages, not 'completed or shutdown') to avoid confusing users.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention for domain-specific UI copy

rule: Avoid merging commented-out code intended for future integration; instead, add the code when the dependency (e.g., API) is ready, or use feature flags.
mode_rationale: Simple code hygiene rule about not merging commented-out code

rule: Table column definitions, configuration objects, and other static data that do not depend on component props or state should be defined outside the component to avoid unnecessary re-creation on each render.
mode_rationale: Single performance convention about static data placement

rule: API responses should include user IDs (in addition to or instead of usernames) because IDs are more stable and usable for programmatic consumers and UI clients.
mode_rationale: Single API design convention preferring stable identifiers

rule: Choose identifier names that are specific to the domain concept they represent (e.g., `reasonForChangeId` over `eSigId`) so they don't conflict with future features that may share the broader concept.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention about domain-specific identifier names

rule: When an API provides an overload that omits an optional parameter, use that overload rather than passing an empty or placeholder value to the full-parameter version.
mode_rationale: Single convention about using proper API overloads

rule: When a PR includes changes that appear unrelated to the stated purpose, reviewers should explicitly confirm intentionality to prevent accidental regressions.
mode_rationale: Single review practice about confirming unrelated changes

rule: Leave a TODO comment when introducing temporary code or workarounds that should be removed later, so future developers know it is intentional debt.
mode_rationale: Single documentation convention about marking temporary code

rule: A value passed to a component should have a type the component actually accepts; never pass a mismatched type and rely on implicit coercion.
mode_rationale: Single type safety convention about matching component contracts

rule: Store values in the native type the UI component understands (e.g., Date objects for date pickers) and only transform to string/serialized format when sending to the backend.
mode_rationale: Single convention about storing native types and serializing at boundaries

rule: Keep presentation components free of API request logic; separate data-fetching concerns from rendering concerns.
mode_rationale: Single architectural convention about separation of concerns

rule: Use the project's shared form abstraction (e.g., DynamicField) consistently across a single form rather than mixing it with raw library inputs, which fragments state management and breaks UI consistency.
mode_rationale: Single convention about using the shared form abstraction consistently

rule: Add explicit TypeScript types to variables and parameters instead of leaving them implicitly typed as 'any'.
mode_rationale: Single convention about adding explicit TypeScript types

rule: Do not wrap trivial computations (e.g., a simple boolean expression) in useMemo; reserve memoization for genuinely expensive calculations.
mode_rationale: Single performance convention about reserving memoization for expensive work

rule: Use the design system's theme tokens (e.g., spacing.spacingMedium, margin.large) for spacing values instead of hard-coded pixel values.
mode_rationale: Single styling convention about using theme tokens for spacing

rule: Use configured module aliases (e.g., `ui`, `@domino/core-ui`) instead of deep relative paths like `../../../` to improve readability and reduce fragile path coupling.
mode_rationale: Single convention about using module aliases over deep relative paths

rule: Before creating custom layout workarounds, check if the shared component already supports your use case (e.g., nested elements for layout alternation in DynamicField).
mode_rationale: Single convention about checking existing component APIs before custom workarounds

rule: Functions used inside useEffect should either be defined inside the effect, or accept dependencies as parameters and be wrapped in useCallback with a stable dependency array, so they can be safely listed in the effect's deps.
mode_rationale: Single convention about proper useEffect dependency management

rule: When two code branches differ only in text or small values, use a conditional expression (ternary) to switch the differing parts rather than duplicating the entire block.
mode_rationale: Single DRY convention about using ternaries for differing values

rule: Do not wrap static strings in JSX expression containers (`{'some string'}`); use the string directly in the JSX template.
mode_rationale: Simple syntax convention about static strings in JSX

rule: When conditional branches only differ in text content, assign the text values to variables conditionally and write the JSX once instead of duplicating the entire block.
mode_rationale: Single DRY convention about extracting conditional text from duplicated JSX

rule: Use theme helper functions (e.g., `themeHelper('palette.danger')`) for colors instead of importing from a static colors constant.
mode_rationale: Single styling convention about using theme helpers for colors

rule: Do not combine unrelated boolean conditions into a single validation variable; each validation check should test exactly one concern to avoid unexpected interactions when individual conditions change.
mode_rationale: Single convention about separating unrelated boolean conditions

rule: When a variable represents a measurement, include the unit in the name (e.g., `maxDateRangeDays` not `maxDateRange`) so the unit at the definition site matches how the value is consumed.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention about including units in variable names

rule: When adding a new route, explicitly evaluate which controller owns the domain concept and discuss alternatives before defaulting to the most convenient location.
mode_rationale: Single architectural convention about evaluating controller ownership for routes

rule: Field names within audit event targets must use camelCase to follow the established convention.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention for audit event field casing

rule: Map audit event data to semantic fields like `eventTo` and `targets` rather than stuffing everything into the generic `metadata` map; if an entity is the target, use the target field and remove it from metadata.
mode_rationale: Single convention about using semantic fields over generic metadata

rule: Avoid adding new events or functionality to deprecated API versions; add new work to the current version only, even if the deprecated version has existing similar entries.
mode_rationale: Single convention about not adding features to deprecated API versions

rule: Do not rename analytics or telemetry event names that are already in production, as downstream consumers (e.g., Mixpanel dashboards) may depend on the existing names.
mode_rationale: Single convention about not renaming production event names

rule: Avoid duplicating information across telemetry event fields; if a value is already captured in one field (e.g., project as the location), do not repeat it in another field (e.g., affecting).
mode_rationale: Single convention about avoiding duplicate telemetry data

rule: For service-to-service authorization, check against specific fine-grained permissions rather than broad superuser checks to limit the scope of service accounts.
mode_rationale: Single security convention about fine-grained permission checks

rule: Place authorization checks before database queries or other expensive operations to avoid unnecessary work when the user lacks permissions.
mode_rationale: Single performance/security convention about authorization ordering

rule: Use a consistent format and casing for error messages and exception strings across the codebase (e.g., 'could not find project for Dataset ${dataset.id}').
mode_rationale: Single convention about consistent error message formatting

rule: Do not use an '/all' suffix on REST collection endpoints; the collection URL itself should return all resources.
mode_rationale: Single REST API naming convention

rule: Use hyphens (kebab-case) instead of camelCase for casing in REST API URL paths.
mode_rationale: Single REST API URL casing convention

rule: When the same logic appears in multiple methods, extract it into a shared helper function to reduce duplication.
mode_rationale: Single DRY convention about extracting shared logic

rule: Do not include unused fields in data structures or event payloads; remove fields that are not actively consumed.
mode_rationale: Single code hygiene convention about removing unused fields

rule: When using string-based identifiers with embedded metadata (e.g., 'dataset-<name>-<id>'), ensure the naming scheme cannot collide with real user-created names.
mode_rationale: Single convention about collision-safe identifier schemes

rule: When wrapping one entity as another (e.g., exposing a dataset as a datasource), verify that the original entity's access control invariants are maintained in the new abstraction.
mode_rationale: Single security convention about maintaining access control through abstractions

rule: When None and an empty collection (e.g., Some(Seq.empty)) have different business semantics, make the distinction explicit through helper methods, comments, or descriptive variable names so the code is self-documenting.
mode_rationale: Single convention about making None vs empty semantics self-documenting

rule: Function names should not reference concepts from other architectural layers; a service-layer method should not mention web-layer concerns like cookies or HTTP headers.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention about layer-appropriate function names

rule: When a higher-level abstraction exists (e.g., useThemeColor hook), prefer it over composing lower-level utilities (e.g., themeHelper with manual theme threading).
mode_rationale: Single convention preferring higher-level abstractions

rule: Do not wrap expressions in template string backticks when the value is already a string; template strings should only be used in styled-component interpolations or when concatenation is needed.
mode_rationale: Simple syntax convention about unnecessary template literals

rule: In React function components, access the theme via hooks (e.g., useTheme) instead of injecting it as a prop via the withTheme HOC, which is being deprecated.
mode_rationale: Single convention about using hooks over deprecated HOC patterns

rule: When a code change resolves the type error that a @ts-ignore or @ts-expect-error was suppressing, remove the suppression comment so TypeScript can resume checking that line.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence convention: clean up suppression comments when the underlying issue is fixed

rule: Use theme spacing tokens (based on a 4px grid) for all spacing values instead of arbitrary pixel values.
Use styled components for styling instead of CSS class names when possible to keep styles co-located and type-safe.
mode_rationale: Simple style preference for co-located, type-safe styling

rule: When wrapping code in a try-catch, document or be able to explain what specific error conditions justify the error handling.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence guideline about intentional error handling

rule: When a callback is only used inside a useEffect, move the function definition inside the effect to avoid an unnecessary useCallback wrapper.
mode_rationale: Simple React convention to reduce unnecessary wrappers

rule: Do not write tests for components where all functionality is mocked, as the tests validate the mocks rather than real behavior.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence testing principle about mock validity

rule: Before building a custom component, check if a standard reusable component already exists in the design system and prefer it for consistency.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: check design system before building custom

rule: Use themeHelper spacing entries (e.g., getMargin) for gap and margin values instead of hardcoded pixel values in styled components.
mode_rationale: Simple style rule: use themeHelper instead of hardcoded pixels

rule: Place UI state (e.g., search, filters) at the container level rather than in leaf components, anticipating future needs like pagination that would require lifting it later.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence architecture guideline about state placement

rule: Use the iconSize prop with predefined iconSizes values from the design system instead of inline size overrides.
Route-based access control and redirects should be handled at the router level rather than in individual components.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence architecture convention about routing responsibility

rule: When a feature is behind a feature flag, ensure all related UI surfaces (descriptions, metadata, navigation entries) are also gated by the same flag.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence rule about comprehensive flag gating

rule: When adding feature-flagged functionality, verify the behavior with the flag both enabled and disabled, especially when multiple flags interact.
mode_rationale: Simple testing convention for flag-gated features

rule: When API and UI changes must ship together, coordinate the dependency version pinning — merging to develop triggers a new API version that requires a follow-up PR to pin.
mode_rationale: Multi-step coordination workflow: merge triggers version, then follow-up PR to pin

rule: Before implementing new helper logic, check if an existing utility already handles the same concern and can be made globally available.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: check for existing utilities first

rule: Default new methods to the most restrictive visibility (private) and only widen access when explicitly needed.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence encapsulation principle

rule: Extract inline literal values into named constants to improve readability and make future changes easier.
Every @ts-ignore or type suppression comment must include an explanation of what the type issue is and why the suppression is necessary.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence documentation convention for suppression comments

rule: Avoid accumulating @ts-ignore comments; fix the underlying type issue or, when suppression is unavoidable, document the specific error being suppressed.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: prefer fixing types over suppressing them

rule: When a custom hook has been created for a concern (e.g., theme access), use it consistently across the codebase instead of falling back to older patterns.
mode_rationale: Simple consistency rule: use the team's custom hooks

rule: Do not wrap JSX in a React Fragment when the component already returns a single element.
mode_rationale: Simple cleanup rule for redundant JSX wrappers

rule: After refactoring code, check whether existing eslint-disable or lint suppression comments are still needed and remove them if the underlying issue is resolved.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: clean up suppressions after refactoring

rule: When migrating code (e.g., to design tokens), actively remove unused styled components and dead code rather than leaving them behind.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: delete unused code during migrations

rule: Chart and data visualization colors should come from the design system palette/tokens rather than hardcoded values, to ensure dark theme compatibility.
mode_rationale: Simple style rule: use design system palette for charts

rule: Use the design system's shadow/elevation tokens for box shadows instead of raw CSS values to maintain visual consistency.
mode_rationale: Simple style rule: use shadow tokens not raw CSS

rule: When data is already present in the URL (route params or query string), read it from there rather than threading it through props or component state.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence architecture guideline about state source of truth

rule: Modal and form components should expose generic callbacks like onSuccess or onEntityCreated rather than directly calling parent-specific functions like refetch, so consumers can customize behavior.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence decoupling principle for component APIs

rule: Use optional chaining (e.g., `refetch?.()`) when invoking callbacks or functions that may be undefined.
mode_rationale: Simple coding convention for nullable function calls

rule: TODO comments in code should include the associated ticket number so they can be tracked and prioritized.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation convention for TODO comments

rule: Use Array.filter instead of Array.reduce when the intent is to filter elements from a collection; it is more readable and expresses intent directly.
mode_rationale: Simple code style rule about array method readability

rule: Use the design system's RouteLink component instead of plain anchor tags or generic Link components to get consistent navigation styling.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: use RouteLink instead of raw anchors

rule: When combining one-time DOM events (like onLoad) with asynchronous data resolution, verify the async data is available before making decisions, or handle the case where the event fires before data is ready.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence defensive coding guideline for async+DOM timing

rule: Section-level actions (like 'Create' buttons) should follow the established page action design pattern rather than being embedded inline in child components.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: follow existing page action design patterns

rule: In a monorepo, an app's tsconfig or build config should not directly reference the internals of another package; packages should be consumed through their public API.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence architecture rule about package boundaries

rule: Use RESTful resource-oriented routes for CRUD operations and /rpc/ prefixed routes for RPC-style actions; choose the style that matches the API's semantics.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention: REST for CRUD, /rpc/ for actions

rule: Code identifiers and config keys should use the user-facing product name (e.g., 'netApp') rather than internal or vendor-specific terms (e.g., 'ontap') that aren't surfaced in the UI.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention about user-facing terms

rule: Configuration retrieval functions should return raw config values without embedding permission checks; authorization logic should live in its own layer or service.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence separation of concerns principle

rule: Every TODO comment in code should include a ticket URL or ticket ID so the work can be tracked and prioritized.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation convention for TODO comments

rule: When migrating to design tokens, verify that the token type matches the CSS property — do not use a shadow token where a color value is expected.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: match token type to CSS property

rule: A package must not import from its own published path (e.g., domino/ui should not import from @domino/ui within itself); use relative imports instead.
mode_rationale: Simple import convention: use relative imports within own package

rule: When performing async operations (e.g., navigation followed by state updates), ensure the component is still mounted before updating state to avoid React warnings.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence React convention about async state safety

rule: Page-level action buttons and controls should be extracted into their own component rather than inlined in the main view component.
mode_rationale: Simple code organization rule about component extraction

rule: The core routes file should only contain top-level base routes; feature-specific inner routes should be defined within their own module.
mode_rationale: Simple architecture rule about route file scope

rule: When removing the last usage of a component, also delete the component file itself to avoid dead code accumulating in the codebase.
mode_rationale: Simple cleanup convention: delete dead component files

rule: Import from the package's canonical alias (e.g., ui/) rather than reaching into the dist build output (e.g., @domino/ui/dist/).
mode_rationale: Simple import convention: use aliases not dist paths

rule: Do not include empty files in a commit; either add the intended content or remove the file entirely.
mode_rationale: Simple commit hygiene rule

rule: Methods that skip authorization checks should be named with a clear suffix like `withoutAuthz` to make the security implications obvious at the call site.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for security-sensitive methods

rule: When writing tests for access-controlled features, include test cases for all relevant user roles including admin users.
mode_rationale: Simple testing convention about role coverage

rule: When moving or refactoring code, verify that all existing test assertions are preserved in the new location — don't silently drop test conditions.
mode_rationale: Simple testing convention: don't drop assertions when moving code

rule: Validate parameters at API boundaries where inputs are untrusted; trust internal code paths where values are known and verified through integration tests.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence architecture principle about validation placement

rule: When a data fetch fails, show a meaningful error state rather than leaving the page blank — even if a toast notification has been shown.
mode_rationale: Single convention: show error state instead of blank page

rule: Name callback props after the semantic event (onSuccess, onUpdated) rather than the implementation detail (onRefetch) so child components don't leak knowledge of parent behavior.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for callback props

rule: Declare React components outside of the parent function body or memoize them to avoid re-declaration on every render cycle.
mode_rationale: Single rule about component declaration placement

rule: Move calculations and business logic out of React components into standalone utility functions that can be unit tested independently.
mode_rationale: Single principle about separating logic from components

rule: Fetch data as close as possible to the component that uses it; push selection state up to the parent rather than fetching in the parent and threading data down.
mode_rationale: Architectural convention about data fetching placement

rule: When multiple consecutive elements share the same condition, group them under a single conditional check rather than repeating the condition.
mode_rationale: Simple code style rule about grouping conditions

rule: When using styled-components, avoid combining them with CSS class selectors; convert elements to styled components to eliminate nested class-based styles.
mode_rationale: Single styling convention

rule: Layout components with fixed-width elements must define minimum supported viewport widths using breakpoint constants to prevent content overflow.
mode_rationale: Single rule about breakpoint definitions

rule: Use `unknown` instead of `any` when a value is simply passed through without being accessed — it provides type safety while still allowing forwarding.
mode_rationale: Type safety convention for passthrough values

rule: Exclude build artifacts, IDE-generated files, and temporary config files (e.g., Vite timestamp files) from version control.
mode_rationale: Simple version control hygiene rule

rule: Do not use type assertions (e.g., `as SomeType`) to bypass TypeScript's null/undefined checks — verify the value exists at runtime or use proper type narrowing.
mode_rationale: Single type safety convention

rule: Avoid overloading domain types to represent unrelated concepts; each enum or sealed trait should model a single coherent domain category.
mode_rationale: Modeling convention about type cohesion

rule: Fields that depend on data migrations should use Option types to gracefully handle cases where the migration has not yet run or fails for some records.
mode_rationale: Single typing convention for migration-dependent fields

rule: When a type extends a base interface, do not redundantly specify properties already defined in the parent type.
mode_rationale: Simple type inheritance rule

rule: Use the project's typography tokens or components for text styling rather than defining ad-hoc font styles, even for buttons or non-body elements.
Define explicit TypeScript types for data records and function return values rather than using untyped objects or `any`.
mode_rationale: Simple typing convention

rule: Do not duplicate cleanup or state-reset logic in both catch/then blocks and a finally block; let the finally block handle it once.
mode_rationale: Single code organization rule about finally blocks

rule: When splitting a configuration string by delimiter, trim whitespace and filter out empty strings to avoid processing blank entries.
mode_rationale: Simple defensive coding convention

rule: Define default values and magic strings as named constants at a shared scope so they can be reused consistently across the codebase.
mode_rationale: Single rule about extracting constants

rule: Place public methods above private methods in class files to maintain a consistent top-down readability order.
mode_rationale: Simple ordering convention

rule: Add authorization check tests for every new endpoint or service method that handles user requests.
mode_rationale: Single testing convention for new endpoints

rule: Include at minimum a basic authorization check (e.g., rejecting anonymous users) even in stubbed or incomplete implementations to prevent security gaps from shipping.
mode_rationale: Single security convention about stub implementations

rule: Do not leave empty or placeholder docstrings/comments; either write meaningful documentation or remove the block entirely.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation hygiene rule

rule: Extract styled components into a dedicated file (e.g., styled-components.ts) rather than co-locating them inline with component logic.
mode_rationale: Single file organization convention

rule: Use standard spacing tokens from the design system instead of arbitrary pixel values to maintain visual consistency.
Use design system typography components or tokens instead of directly referencing raw theme helpers like themeHelper('fontSizes.tiny').
mode_rationale: Single styling convention about typography access

rule: Declare related variables or fields adjacent to each other so their relationship is immediately visible.
mode_rationale: Simple code organization rule

rule: When writing new components or code, use the latest agreed-upon terminology rather than legacy names to avoid immediate tech debt from planned renames.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention about terminology

rule: Include parent resource identifiers in API route paths (e.g., /snapshot/:snapshotId/import-blobs/:taskId) for consistency and to enable authorization checks against the parent resource.
mode_rationale: Single API route design convention

rule: React context providers should only contain state and logic that directly affects the provided values; extract formatting utilities, helper functions, and use-case-specific logic into separate hooks or utility modules to prevent provider bloat.
mode_rationale: Single architectural principle about provider scope

rule: Do not use type casts to work around loose or incorrect type definitions; instead, fix the underlying types so TypeScript can provide actual safety — excessive casting is equivalent to using `any`.
mode_rationale: Single type safety convention

rule: Split functions that both transform data and make API calls into separate concerns: dedicated mapping functions for value transformations and a single function for the network request.
mode_rationale: Single separation-of-concerns convention

rule: Before creating a new UI component, check whether an existing component with similar functionality already exists in the codebase and can be reused or extended.
mode_rationale: Single convention about component reuse

rule: Application-level configuration files (e.g., tsconfig.json in apps/) should not reference internal packages directly; keep dependency boundaries clean and revert accidental config changes.
mode_rationale: Single config hygiene rule for monorepos

rule: When solving a problem that the codebase has solved before (e.g., path handling, URL encoding), reuse the existing helper or approach rather than inventing a new one.
mode_rationale: Single convention about reusing existing solutions

rule: Route-building and navigation helper functions should live in a shared utils or routes file for the feature area, not inline in individual components, so they can be reused from other entry points.
mode_rationale: Single file organization convention for route helpers

rule: When adding new functionality that depends on a feature flag, ensure the code behaves correctly when the flag is disabled — do not render or include flag-gated features unconditionally.
mode_rationale: Single convention about feature flag correctness

rule: When designing internal service APIs, place contextual parameters (IDs, metadata) in the same location (header or body) as similar parameters in the same request for consistency, preferring the simpler option.
mode_rationale: Single API design consistency convention

rule: Custom HTTP headers should follow the established naming convention in the codebase (e.g., `X-Domino-Api-Key` capitalization style) rather than introducing inconsistent casing.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention for HTTP headers

rule: In integration test configuration, assign a unique port to each service rather than sharing ports between unrelated services to avoid conflicts and confusion.
mode_rationale: Single test configuration convention

rule: Remove or replace `console.log` statements with appropriate logging (e.g., `console.warn`) before merging; debug logging should not ship to production.
mode_rationale: Simple code hygiene rule

rule: When refactoring test logic, update the test name to accurately describe the current behavior being tested; misleading test names obscure intent and cause confusion during failures.
mode_rationale: Single testing convention about naming accuracy

rule: When building UI components that display variable-length content (file names, paths, nested structures), verify rendering behavior with edge cases like deeply nested paths and long names.
mode_rationale: Single testing convention about edge case verification

rule: Do not rename default exports from the shared component library or use underlying framework props (e.g., Ant Design) when the wrapper component defines its own prop types.
mode_rationale: Single convention about respecting library APIs

rule: Entities that may require deep linking should be represented in the URL path or query params, not solely as internal component state.
mode_rationale: Single architectural convention about URL state

rule: Always use the design system's Typography component for text styling; if designs use non-standard typographies, check with UX before implementing custom styles.
When two code paths differ only in a single value, extract the varying part into a conditional expression rather than duplicating the entire statement.
mode_rationale: Simple DRY code style rule

rule: Use domain-specific prefixes for identifiers that could be ambiguous across contexts (e.g., prefer `dfsCommitId` over generic `commitId` when referring to the Domino File System commit).
mode_rationale: Single naming convention for ambiguous identifiers

rule: Remove code that is no longer referenced anywhere in the PR; do not leave dead code behind.
mode_rationale: Simple code hygiene rule

rule: Use enums for fields with a fixed set of valid values and domain-specific types instead of raw Strings to reduce validation code and improve readability.
mode_rationale: Single typing convention for DTO fields

rule: Log at `warn` level (not `info`) when the system encounters an unexpected condition that is gracefully handled, such as a resource expected to exist returning a 404.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence logging level guideline

rule: Every new API endpoint must include an authorization check, especially when the code will be deployed in cloud environments.
mode_rationale: Simple rule: every endpoint needs an auth check

rule: API contract mapping (translating between external API models and internal domain models) should be done in the controller/API layer, not in the service layer, because it is a contract concern rather than business logic.
mode_rationale: Architectural convention about where mapping belongs

rule: Method names should explicitly describe what the method does; avoid generic names like `test` when the method performs verification and translation.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for method clarity

rule: When setting parameters for external API calls, verify through logs or tests that the parameters actually appear in the outgoing request, not just that they are set in code.
mode_rationale: Single verification guideline for external calls

rule: Name feature flags to be self-descriptive to consumers rather than mirroring internal enum values; prefer domain-meaningful names (e.g., `enableGenericGitImport` over `enableUnknownProviderImport`).
mode_rationale: Naming convention for feature flags

rule: When removing or changing validation guards, ensure that unsupported operation paths still produce helpful error messages rather than silently passing invalid requests to downstream services.
mode_rationale: Simple rule about maintaining error messages

rule: When adding a new implementation of an existing capability, replicate the same validation rules and edge-case handling as the existing implementation to avoid inconsistent user experiences.
mode_rationale: Convention to replicate validation across implementations

rule: Remove commented-out code and dead code paths before merging; do not leave stale commented blocks that suggest unfinished work or confuse reviewers.
mode_rationale: Code hygiene convention

rule: When refactoring code that includes validation logic, verify that validation is still enforced somewhere in the new code path rather than accidentally dropping it.
mode_rationale: Single guideline about not dropping validation

rule: External service URLs and endpoints that may change per deployment should be exposed as configurable flags rather than hardcoded.
mode_rationale: Configuration convention for external URLs

rule: When a user explicitly chooses to force an operation, bypass expensive pre-validation checks (e.g., full repo clones) that exist to protect against accidental overwrites.
mode_rationale: Performance convention for force operations

rule: When multiple providers implement the same interface, their validation logic should be consistent — don't reinvent validation in each provider when a shared approach already exists.
mode_rationale: Convention for shared validation logic

rule: Use map, flatMap, and other functional combinators on Either/Option/Try types instead of manually matching on left/right or some/none when the transformation is straightforward.
mode_rationale: Code style preference for functional types

rule: Before writing custom error extraction logic, check if the codebase already provides a utility (e.g., safeGetErrorMessage) for safely extracting error messages from API responses.
mode_rationale: Convention to check for existing utilities first

rule: Third-party SDK initialization and app-level setup utilities should be consolidated into a dedicated bootstrap component rather than scattered across the app entry point.
mode_rationale: Architectural convention for SDK initialization placement

rule: Do not maintain separate boolean state variables (e.g., loading and hasLoaded) when one can be derived from the other — consolidate into a single source of truth.
mode_rationale: State management convention to avoid derived state

rule: Before initializing third-party SDKs, verify all required data dependencies have loaded successfully and use a module-level guard flag to ensure initialization runs exactly once, eliminating the need for defensive nullish chaining.
mode_rationale: Initialization safety convention

rule: Do not set a 'hasLoaded' or 'completed' flag in a finally block if it conflates successful loads with failed ones — loading state should accurately reflect whether data was successfully retrieved.
mode_rationale: State semantics convention for loading flags

rule: When embedding third-party scripts verbatim, disable ESLint and TypeScript checks at the file level rather than suppressing individual lines throughout the file.
mode_rationale: Convention for handling vendored files

rule: Use design system spacing tokens or standardized margin/padding values instead of arbitrary pixel values to maintain visual consistency across components.
mode_rationale: Design system spacing convention

rule: Use the design system's typography components or tokens for text styling instead of defining raw font-size, font-weight, and line-height values in component styles.
mode_rationale: Design system typography convention

rule: Use standardized spacing values (design tokens) instead of arbitrary margins and padding in style definitions.
mode_rationale: Design token usage convention

rule: Do not connect or inject a global store into a component that does not use it; remove unnecessary store dependencies.
mode_rationale: Convention to remove unnecessary store dependencies

rule: Omit the `={true}` assignment for boolean JSX props; the bare attribute name defaults to true.
mode_rationale: JSX syntax convention

rule: Custom hooks that perform async operations should expose `loading` and `hasLoaded` flags so consumers can render appropriate loading states.
mode_rationale: API design convention for async hooks

rule: Reserve PascalCase names for React components; use initial camelCase for non-component exports like utility functions, hooks, and constants.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for exports

rule: When the same string literal appears multiple times, extract it into a named constant and reuse it.
mode_rationale: DRY convention for string literals

rule: Create database indexes via migration scripts, not in application startup code that runs every time the app starts.
mode_rationale: Convention for where to create indexes

rule: Name new classes following the established naming pattern in the codebase (e.g., use `XyzPersister` if that is the existing convention, not `XyzManager`).
mode_rationale: Naming consistency convention

rule: When leaving a hack or workaround in the code, add a comment linking to the Jira ticket that tracks its removal.
mode_rationale: Documentation convention for workarounds

rule: Do not memoize table column definitions as it can break column resizing behavior; verify resize still works if memoization is added.
mode_rationale: Performance gotcha for table columns

rule: Use `jest.mock(<path>)` to auto-mock an entire module instead of manually creating mock functions for each export; all exports become jest functions you can spy on.
mode_rationale: Testing convention for module mocking

rule: When removing a feature or capability, create a follow-up ticket to formally deprecate or replace it rather than silently dropping it.
mode_rationale: Process convention for feature removal

rule: Use semantic color tokens (e.g., border color tokens) instead of raw hex values or generic color variables for borders and other styled elements.
mode_rationale: Design system color convention

rule: Carefully spell variable and property names correctly; typos in identifiers create confusing APIs and make code harder to search.
mode_rationale: Basic naming correctness convention

rule: Do not add redundant conditional checks when one state already implies another (e.g., `hasLoaded` being true already implies `loading` is false).
mode_rationale: Code simplicity convention for state logic

rule: Always reference palette tokens (e.g., palette.primary, palette.textPrimary) instead of hardcoded color values when styling components.
mode_rationale: Design system color token convention

rule: Use the standardized box-shadow/elevation styles provided by the theme rather than constructing custom box-shadow values with raw colors.
mode_rationale: Design system elevation convention

rule: Only use themeHelper within the scope of a styled component; when used outside, it returns a function instead of the resolved style value.
mode_rationale: API usage gotcha for themeHelper scope

rule: Remove temporary debugging code (e.g., `|| true`, console.logs, hardcoded overrides) before submitting a PR for review.
mode_rationale: Code hygiene convention for debug code

rule: When accessing properties on a caught error, guard against the error not matching the expected interface to avoid throwing a secondary exception.
mode_rationale: Error handling safety convention

rule: Do not use explicit `any` type annotations; use proper types or `unknown` instead.
mode_rationale: TypeScript type safety convention

rule: Add warning or error logging when an unexpected or edge-case code path is reached, especially around feature-flagged behavior.
mode_rationale: Logging convention for edge cases

rule: Validate that required user selections (e.g., a selected project) are present before sending requests to the backend.
mode_rationale: Input validation convention

rule: When building arrays that may contain empty or falsy elements, use reduce (or equivalent filtering) to avoid empty positions in the result.
mode_rationale: Array construction convention

rule: Do not use the optional chaining operator (?.) on a variable that is already guaranteed to exist by a preceding guard clause or conditional check.
mode_rationale: Code clarity convention for optional chaining

rule: Skip API requests when required parameters are empty or undefined rather than sending invalid requests to the server.
mode_rationale: Request validation convention

rule: When the API supports pagination, sorting must be done server-side; client-side sorting only operates on the current page and produces incorrect results.
mode_rationale: Pagination correctness convention

rule: When two components across different modules perform nearly identical functions, consolidate them into a single shared component rather than maintaining parallel implementations.
mode_rationale: DRY convention for shared components

rule: Shared or common components must not contain hardcoded options or test data; all values should be passed via props or configuration to ensure correctness across all consumers.
mode_rationale: Single convention: shared components must receive values via props, not hardcode them

rule: Memoize filtering, searching, or other expensive computations inside table cell components to avoid re-running the same operation N times (once per row) on every re-render.
mode_rationale: Single performance guideline: memoize expensive operations in table cell renders

rule: Avoid chaining multiple .filter() calls that iterate the same list repeatedly; use .reduce(), function composition, or a transducer to derive results in a single pass.
mode_rationale: Single performance convention: use single-pass iteration instead of chained .filter() calls

rule: Reserve SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE naming for true constants; use standard camelCase for variables whose values change at runtime.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for constant vs runtime variable casing

rule: When multiple derived lists or option sets come from the same source data, compute them all in a single iteration rather than using separate useMemo hooks that each traverse the full list.
mode_rationale: Single performance guideline: compute multiple derived values in one iteration

rule: Use a route switch to encapsulate conditional page or view rendering rather than inline conditional logic in the component body.
mode_rationale: Simple architecture convention: use route switch instead of inline conditionals

rule: Always use the generated TypeScript types from the API client package (e.g., `@domino/api/output/`) for row data and props instead of defining inline or custom types.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: use generated types from the API client package

rule: Every data table must include an empty state component that explains what the table shows, why it is empty, and what the user can do — either inline or tracked via a TODO for a follow-up PR.
mode_rationale: Single rule: every data table must include an empty state component

rule: Do not conditionally render entire pages based on auth state in a way that causes a visible flash or blink when the auth check passes; prefer showing a loading state or keeping the page mounted.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: show loading state instead of flashing content during auth checks

rule: Use string literal union types with a Map (and the `UnionToMap` type utility from `ui/utils/typescriptUtils`) instead of TypeScript enums, which are considered an antipattern in this codebase.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: use string literal unions with Map instead of TS enums

rule: Reference semantic color tokens from the palette (e.g., `palette.danger`) instead of hardcoded hex values or non-semantic theme references when applying colors in styled components.
mode_rationale: Simple styling convention: use semantic palette tokens, not hardcoded hex

rule: Add debug-level logging with the relevant metadata before returning a NotAuthorized result, so authorization failures can be diagnosed from logs.
mode_rationale: Simple logging convention: add debug logging before returning NotAuthorized

rule: When renaming a class or trait (e.g., Manager to Persister), update all corresponding filenames — including test and integration test files — to match the new name.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: rename files when renaming classes/traits

rule: When using `themeHelper` to resolve color tokens, always pass the theme object; omitting it causes the color to silently fall back to `inherit`, producing incorrect styling.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: always pass theme object to themeHelper calls

rule: Use the correct CSS property name `color` for text color — `font-color` is not a valid CSS property and will be silently ignored by browsers.
mode_rationale: Simple correctness rule: use valid CSS property names
