rule: Ensure ESLint and Prettier are configured in your local development environment so formatting issues (e.g., extra whitespace) are caught automatically before code review.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: ensure local linting/formatting tools are configured

rule: Shared/generic components (e.g., DynamicField) must not import or reference feature-specific types; use generics or a registration pattern to extend them without creating coupling.
mode_rationale: Simple architecture rule: shared components must not import feature-specific types

rule: Before adding cross-module imports, verify the import graph to ensure no circular dependencies are introduced; if a cycle already exists, flag it for resolution rather than deepening it.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: verify import graph before adding cross-module imports

rule: When creating a utility that operates on a single value (e.g., a theme color resolver), design its API to also accept multiple values to avoid forcing consumers to compose many single-value calls.
mode_rationale: Simple API design guideline: design utilities to accept multiple values

rule: Reference route paths from the canonical exported PATHS constant rather than hardcoding path strings, to ensure consistency and catch breakage at compile time.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: use PATHS constant instead of hardcoded route strings

rule: Tables should remember and restore the user's last sort/filter state across mounts rather than resetting to a default sort on every render.
mode_rationale: Simple UX convention: tables should remember sort/filter state across mounts

rule: When renaming an API field or attribute, search the codebase for the old name to ensure no usages are hidden behind an `any` type that would silently break at runtime.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: search for old name behind any types when renaming API fields

rule: When renaming fields in API response models, assess backward and forward compatibility risks — especially in deployments with multiple frontends that may temporarily run different versions.
mode_rationale: Simple guideline: assess compatibility risks when renaming API fields

rule: Define color values in a dedicated colors file rather than scattering them across utility or component files.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: define colors in a dedicated file, not scattered across modules

rule: Use locale-independent string methods (toUpperCase) instead of toLocaleUpperCase when transforming strings for API values, since locale-specific casing rules can produce unexpected results.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: use locale-independent string methods for API values

rule: Use the design system's spacing tokens instead of raw margin or padding values to maintain consistent spacing across the UI.
Move non-rendering logic (data transformations, constants, helper functions) out of React component bodies into separate modules or hooks.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: move non-rendering logic out of React component bodies

rule: Ensure inner component state and outer/parent state use the same type representations (e.g., both use undefined or both use empty string) to avoid subtle inconsistencies.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: inner and outer state should use matching type representations

rule: When catching or suppressing errors, explicitly consider whether the failure affects the user's workflow and provide appropriate feedback if it does.
mode_rationale: Simple guideline: provide user feedback when suppressed errors affect workflow

rule: Use styled-components (or the project's CSS-in-JS solution) instead of inline styles for component styling.
mode_rationale: Simple styling convention: use styled-components instead of inline styles

rule: Avoid unnecessary wrapper elements (e.g., extra span or div) in JSX when the child element can stand alone.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: avoid unnecessary wrapper divs/spans in JSX

rule: When the same utility function appears in multiple places, extract it to a shared module within the relevant feature area to avoid duplication.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: extract duplicated utility functions into a shared module

rule: Domain entities should not know how they are returned in APIs; DTOs should map themselves from/to domain, not the other way around.
mode_rationale: Simple architecture rule: domain entities must not reference DTOs

rule: Feature flags with long lifespans should be referenced in as few places as possible to reduce state inconsistency risk and simplify future removal.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: reference long-lived feature flags in as few places as possible

rule: Public-facing API field names should use snake_case to follow the established API naming standard.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention: public API fields use snake_case

rule: Use HTTP PATCH (not POST) for endpoints that partially update an existing resource.
mode_rationale: Simple API convention: use PATCH for partial updates, not POST

rule: When code is gated behind feature flags or central config, create cleanup tasks to delete it once the flag is removed or the code is no longer used.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: create cleanup tasks for flag-gated dead code

rule: Modals and child components that need global state should consume the store directly rather than having parents pass it down as props.
mode_rationale: Simple architecture rule: child components should consume store directly

rule: Standalone utility functions that contain logic should have accompanying unit tests.
Declare optional props with `?` syntax (e.g., `prop?: Type`) instead of explicitly adding `| undefined` to the type.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: use ? syntax for optional props instead of | undefined

rule: Do not create wrapper functions around values that can be accessed directly from props or context.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: access values directly instead of wrapping in functions

rule: Use theme tokens (spacing, border radius, colors) instead of hardcoded values in styled components to maintain design system consistency.
mode_rationale: Simple styling convention: use theme tokens instead of hardcoded values

rule: Do not use the `.styled` suffix in file names for styled component files.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention: do not use .styled suffix in filenames

rule: Use the `spacing` design token for margins and padding instead of raw pixel values.
mode_rationale: Simple styling convention: use spacing tokens for margins and padding

rule: When accepting user-provided URLs or URL segments, validate characters against the relevant RFC 3986 section (e.g., unreserved characters for path segments).
mode_rationale: Simple security convention: validate URL characters against RFC 3986

rule: When a field must be unique (e.g., vanity URLs, slugs), add a unique database index rather than relying solely on application-level checks to prevent race conditions.
mode_rationale: Simple architecture rule: enforce uniqueness with DB indexes, not app-level checks

rule: Move inline styles into the corresponding styled component definition rather than applying them directly on elements.
mode_rationale: Simple styling convention: move inline styles into styled component definitions

rule: Define union types for known sets of values instead of using the loose `string` type in interfaces and function signatures.
mode_rationale: Simple typing convention: use union types for known value sets instead of string

rule: Functions that return color values should have their return type annotated with the Palette or color token type, not a generic string.
mode_rationale: Simple typing convention: annotate color return types with Palette type, not string

rule: Use the `IconResolver` component for rendering icons instead of importing and rendering icon components directly; it provides consistent sizing and color props.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: use IconResolver component instead of direct icon imports

rule: When adding new entries to the color palette or design tokens, ensure they are also added to the corresponding Storybook story.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to keep Storybook in sync when adding design tokens

rule: When migrating raw color values to design tokens, verify the replacement token has the correct semantic meaning — not just a similar hex value.
mode_rationale: Single-check guideline about semantic correctness during token migration

rule: Test fixtures should use realistic data formats (e.g., mock UUIDs for UUID fields) rather than arbitrary placeholder strings.
mode_rationale: Simple convention about using realistic data formats in test fixtures

rule: Remove temporary or debug log statements before merging a PR.
mode_rationale: Simple hygiene rule about removing debug statements

rule: Test descriptions should clearly and specifically state the scenario being tested, including the relevant condition or trigger.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for test descriptions

rule: Add documentation that describes what a function does (its intent and purpose), not how it does it; the code already shows the how.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation style guideline

rule: Include test cases for records that should be excluded from query results (e.g., archived, deleted, or inactive entities) to verify filtering logic.
mode_rationale: Testing convention to include exclusion cases

rule: Use either await or .then() consistently within a function; do not mix both async patterns as it creates confusion and potential double-promise issues.
mode_rationale: Simple style rule about async pattern consistency

rule: New user-facing UI features should be gated behind a feature flag so they can be toggled independently of deployment.
mode_rationale: Single convention about feature flag gating

rule: Use descriptive variable names instead of single-character abbreviations, even in short callbacks or lambdas, to maintain readability.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention about descriptive variable names

rule: Do not fire API requests when required filter parameters are undefined; guard the fetch to prevent unfiltered queries and race conditions with loading state.
mode_rationale: Simple guard pattern for API calls with missing parameters

rule: When handling external service errors, match on structured error codes (e.g., AWS error codes) rather than parsing exception message strings, which are fragile and may change.
mode_rationale: Simple error handling convention about matching on structured codes

rule: When performing cleanup or deletion of resources, handle possible failures in the deletion itself to avoid silent errors or orphaned state.
mode_rationale: Simple error handling guideline for cleanup paths

rule: Replace magic numbers with named constants that explain what the value represents, improving readability and maintainability.
Utility functions should always return a consistent type (e.g., always a string) rather than sometimes returning undefined or null, to simplify consumption and avoid null checks downstream.
mode_rationale: Simple API design convention about return type consistency

rule: Do not create objects, arrays, or other reference values inside a React component body; define them outside the component or memoize them to avoid unnecessary re-renders.
mode_rationale: Simple React performance convention about reference stability

rule: Repository names should match the artifactId (not the groupId) so that developers can trace dependency coordinates back to their source repository without ambiguity.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for repository-to-artifact traceability

rule: When checking entity status (e.g., snapshot readiness), compare against the exact expected terminal state (e.g., `=== 'Active'`) rather than a looser check, to avoid acting on intermediate states.
mode_rationale: Simple convention about comparing against exact terminal states

rule: Authentication redirect logic should be placed inside the Route component that handles the protected path, not at the module top level, to prevent unintended redirects on unrelated routes.
mode_rationale: Simple architectural convention about auth redirect placement

rule: Avoid using inline object literals like `{}` as default values in React props or hooks, as they create a new reference on every render and can cause unwanted re-renders or effect re-executions.
mode_rationale: Simple React convention about avoiding inline object literal defaults

rule: Do not hold the same piece of state in multiple places (e.g., form library state, component state, and a custom hook); consolidate to a single source of truth to prevent synchronization bugs.
mode_rationale: Simple architecture convention about single source of truth for state

rule: When business logic is complex, invest in stronger TypeScript types to catch errors at compile time rather than relying on runtime behavior.
mode_rationale: Simple guideline to invest in stronger types for complex logic

rule: Do not conflate HTTP authentication/authorization errors (401, 403) with feature-disabled states; each status code has specific meaning and should be handled distinctly to avoid incorrect application behavior.
mode_rationale: Simple convention about using HTTP status codes correctly

rule: Ensure error messages, toast notifications, and status text reference the correct entity or feature — copy-paste from other modules often leaves stale references.
mode_rationale: Simple quality check for copy-paste errors in user-facing text

rule: Review your PR diff to ensure no unintended files (especially those that should be gitignored) are included; discard changes to files unrelated to your feature.
mode_rationale: Simple hygiene rule about reviewing PR diffs for unintended files

rule: When exposing identifiers in API response models, make the ID type explicit (e.g., DominoId, MongoId, UUID) so consumers do not have to guess the format or provenance.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for explicit ID types in API models

rule: When converting numeric types (e.g., `.toDouble`, `.toLong`) in pagination or comparison logic, apply the same conversion consistently across all code paths to avoid subtle bugs.
mode_rationale: Simple convention about consistent numeric type conversions

rule: Validate that required fields exist before making API calls; if a field accessed via optional chaining (e.g., `file.name?.fileName`) may be undefined, either throw an error or skip the call rather than sending an invalid request.
mode_rationale: Simple validation guideline before making API calls

rule: When accepting user input for file operations like rename, validate the input to reject invalid characters and path separators (e.g., slashes) that could cause unexpected behavior.
mode_rationale: Simple security convention to reject invalid characters in file input

rule: Test names should explicitly describe the conditions under test and the expected outcome, especially when behavior may be confusing to future readers.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for test descriptions with conditions and outcomes

rule: Remove mock setup for code paths that the test asserts are never reached, making the test more realistic and its intent clearer.
mode_rationale: Simple testing convention about minimal mock setup

rule: When a parameter or code path exists for non-obvious reasons, write a detailed comment explaining the real-world scenario that necessitates it, not just what it does.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation convention about explaining real-world reasoning

rule: When adding a trait or mixin that changes behavior, apply it at the individual class level rather than a shared parent trait, to avoid unintended side effects on sibling classes.
mode_rationale: Simple architecture convention about trait/mixin granularity

rule: When pattern matching on related values, match them as a tuple in a single match expression rather than nesting matches, for conciseness and readability.
mode_rationale: Simple code style convention about tuple matching vs nested matches

rule: Do not leave commented-out code in production files; remove it and rely on version control for history.
Use Typography type components for text styling instead of raw fontSizes values to maintain consistency with the design system.
mode_rationale: Simple design system convention about using Typography components

rule: When an API call can fail, display a toast notification or error state on the page so users are aware of the failure rather than silently swallowing it.
mode_rationale: Simple UX convention about surfacing API failures to users

rule: Load data that multiple child components need at the parent container level and pass it down, rather than making individual API requests per cell or child, to avoid N+1 request problems.
mode_rationale: Simple performance convention to avoid N+1 request problems

rule: Use `command -v` instead of `which` to check for executable availability in shell scripts, since `command` is a POSIX shell builtin guaranteed to be present.
mode_rationale: Simple portability convention for shell scripts

rule: When configuration files are written to user-visible locations (e.g., workspaces), document the reasoning behind non-obvious settings in external documentation rather than inline comments, and remove commented-out defaults.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation convention for user-visible config files

rule: Before executing a script or binary, verify that the file exists and is executable to reduce blast radius on unexpected configurations.
mode_rationale: Simple defensive coding convention before executing scripts

rule: When catching errors, extract and surface the actual error message rather than swallowing it or showing a generic message.
mode_rationale: Simple error handling convention about surfacing actual error messages

rule: Define named constants for magic numbers (like validation limits) and reuse them in both logic and user-facing messages to keep them in sync.
mode_rationale: Simple convention about named constants for validation limits and messages

rule: Add dedicated unit tests for repository query methods (sort order, filters) even if they are implicitly covered by higher-level integration tests.
mode_rationale: Simple testing convention about dedicated tests for repository queries

rule: When multiple sequential database queries and writes occur in a single operation, consider batching them to reduce the total number of round-trips.
mode_rationale: Simple performance convention about reducing database round-trips

rule: When passing callbacks to hooks, wrap them in useCallback to produce a stable reference, even if the consuming hook doesn't currently depend on reference stability — treat hooks as black boxes.
mode_rationale: Simple React convention about stable callback references for hooks

rule: Track loading state with an explicit flag (e.g., hasLoaded) rather than inferring it from the absence of data, since !result does not reliably equal loading.
mode_rationale: Simple state management convention about explicit loading flags

rule: Drive data refetching through reactive state changes rather than imperative request functions, because imperative patterns scale poorly when external inputs like search fields or filters are added.
mode_rationale: Simple architecture convention about reactive vs imperative data fetching

rule: Ensure callbacks are listed in useEffect/useMemo dependency arrays when they reference external state setters not controlled by the component, to avoid calling stale closure versions.
mode_rationale: Simple React convention about complete dependency arrays

rule: When a utility method accepts optional parameters but most callers always provide values, offer a strongly-typed overload that takes non-optional parameters for convenience and type safety.
mode_rationale: Simple API design convention about convenience overloads

rule: When a function takes an optional partial override object, default it to an empty object so callers are not forced to pass {} when using the defaults.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence convention about defaulting optional parameters

rule: Never add frontend routes under reserved backend path prefixes like /cloud-admin to avoid routing conflicts.
mode_rationale: Simple rule to avoid routing conflicts with reserved paths

rule: When a page has multiple independent API updates with different endpoints or payloads, use one form per update rather than nesting forms or sharing a single form across unrelated mutations.
mode_rationale: Architectural guideline about form-to-API mapping

rule: Use terminology that is consistent with URL patterns and codebase conventions (e.g., prefer 'allowlist' over 'whitelist' when URLs already use 'allowlist').
mode_rationale: Naming convention to keep terminology aligned with URL patterns

rule: Avoid merging to develop while dependencies point to a custom branch; pin dependencies to the develop version or merge a follow-up PR immediately after to correct the reference.
mode_rationale: Simple rule about dependency pinning before merging

rule: Do not use the generic `object` type; define or generate a specific type from the API schema (e.g., Swagger) so consumers get type safety.
mode_rationale: Type safety convention to avoid generic object types

rule: Be aware that the spread operator only performs a shallow clone; nested objects retain the same references, which can break immutability guarantees.
mode_rationale: Awareness rule about shallow clone behavior

rule: Use Intl.NumberFormat for number formatting and template literals for string interpolation instead of manual concatenation.
mode_rationale: Simple convention preferring standard formatting APIs

rule: Keep all changes in a PR related to the stated purpose; unrelated changes should go in a separate PR to keep reviews focused and diffs auditable.
mode_rationale: Process convention about keeping PRs focused

rule: Avoid prefixing variable names with underscores; use explicit, descriptive names instead.
mode_rationale: Naming convention about variable prefixes

rule: Split components that grow too long or mix multiple concerns into smaller, focused components; extract injected logic into dedicated child components.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence guideline about component decomposition

rule: Always use predefined Typography components from the design system instead of custom fontSize or fontWeight values in styled-components.
Use standard margin and padding values from the design system's spacing scale rather than arbitrary pixel values.
mode_rationale: Style rule to use design system spacing scale

rule: Use palette colors from the design system for backgrounds and foregrounds; avoid system tokens and direct access to the `colors` object.
mode_rationale: Style rule about correct color token usage

rule: Use the design system's iconSize tokens instead of hardcoded pixel values for icon sizing.
mode_rationale: Style rule to use icon size tokens instead of hardcoded values

rule: Use theme helper spacing tokens (e.g., spacing.xSmall, spacing.xXSmall) instead of hardcoded pixel values for margins, padding, and gaps.
Use theme helper border radius tokens (e.g., spacingBorderRadius.radiusSmall) instead of hardcoded pixel values.
mode_rationale: Style rule to use border radius tokens

rule: Never use hardcoded color values in styled components; always reference design system color tokens via the theme.
mode_rationale: Style rule against hardcoded color values

rule: When a design system token cannot be used due to component context limitations, leave the hardcoded value with a TODO comment explaining why and what needs to be refactored.
mode_rationale: Documentation convention for when tokens can't be used

rule: When performing optimistic UI updates, always implement a rollback or refetch strategy for when the backend operation fails.
mode_rationale: Error handling convention for optimistic UI patterns

rule: Always wrap if expression bodies in curly braces for readability; do not use shorthand single-line if statements without braces.
mode_rationale: Simple code style rule about brace usage

rule: Do not add @ts-ignore comments unless they are actually needed to suppress a real type error; verify the error still exists before adding the directive.
mode_rationale: Code hygiene rule about ts-ignore comments

rule: Avoid calling .get on Scala Option types; instead handle the Option explicitly to account for edge cases where the value may not exist.
mode_rationale: Simple safety rule about Scala Option handling

rule: When .get on an Option is unavoidable, add a comment explaining why the value is guaranteed to exist at that point.
mode_rationale: Documentation convention for forced unwraps

rule: Use named parameters (e.g., commitId = request.dfsCommitId) when constructing objects to improve readability and make the mapping between source and target fields explicit.
mode_rationale: Readability convention for object construction

rule: All controller methods should extract DominoContext via `implicit val dc: DominoContext = request.dominoContext` and service method signatures should accept it as an implicit parameter.
mode_rationale: Architectural convention for controller method signatures

rule: New controller and service methods should include debug or trace logging from the start, especially when test coverage is not yet in place.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to include logging from the start

rule: Never call `.get` on an `Option` type; use pattern matching (`match { case None => ... case Some(v) => ... }`) or a safe alternative like `.getOrElse`/`.fold` to handle the empty case explicitly.
mode_rationale: Safety rule against unsafe Option.get calls

rule: Use locale-aware date formats (e.g., dayjs `l`/`lll` tokens or `toLocaleDateString` with options) instead of hardcoded format strings, so dates are consistent with the user's system locale across the UI.
mode_rationale: Formatting convention to use locale-aware dates

rule: Do not merge unused methods or code; if it's needed for follow-up work, add it in the follow-up PR.
mode_rationale: Code hygiene rule against merging unused code

rule: Client input validation should live in the controller layer, not in service or domain layers.
mode_rationale: Architectural convention about where validation lives

rule: Validation errors from client input must return 4xx status codes, not 500; use exception types that map to the correct HTTP status.
mode_rationale: Simple rule about correct HTTP status codes for validation

rule: Always include all referenced values in React useEffect dependency arrays to prevent stale closures and missed re-renders.
mode_rationale: React convention about useEffect dependency arrays

rule: Use the project's standard loading component (WaitSpinner) instead of custom or inline loading indicators.
mode_rationale: Convention to use project's standard loading component

rule: Extract magic numbers and repeated string literals into named constants for clarity and maintainability.
Use TypeScript optional property syntax (?) instead of explicitly adding undefined to union types.
mode_rationale: TypeScript style preference for optional properties

rule: Use the project's standard error message utility (safeGetErrorMessage) instead of manual error string extraction.
mode_rationale: Convention to use project's standard error utility

rule: Do not use template literal backticks when a plain string suffices; backticks should only be used when interpolation or multiline is needed.
mode_rationale: Style rule about when to use template literals

rule: Use the design system's spacing tokens for margins and padding instead of hardcoded pixel values.
Omit explicit type annotations when TypeScript can reliably infer the type; only annotate when inference fails or is ambiguous.
mode_rationale: Style convention to omit redundant type annotations

rule: Prefer useMemo with JSX over defining inner render functions for memoizable UI fragments within React components.
mode_rationale: Performance convention preferring useMemo over render functions

rule: When parsing or aggregating data, prefer returning partial results over throwing exceptions when possible, to avoid total failure on minor mismatches.
mode_rationale: Error handling convention to prefer partial results over throwing

rule: Use the correct semantic token category from the theme palette — border colors for borders, background colors for backgrounds — instead of cross-referencing token categories.
mode_rationale: Style rule about using semantically correct token categories

rule: Use the design system's standard typography components and styles instead of custom inline text styles.
mode_rationale: Style rule to use design system typography components

rule: Use the `spacing` theme key for all spacing values; the `margins` theme key is deprecated.
mode_rationale: Convention to use spacing instead of deprecated margins key

rule: Avoid adding a boolean toggle prop that only has effect when a companion prop is also provided; combine them into a single optional prop instead.
mode_rationale: API design rule to combine coupled boolean props

rule: Do not add conditional checks for values that are already guaranteed to be truthy by the surrounding control flow.
mode_rationale: Code hygiene rule against unnecessary conditionals

rule: Never call state setters directly in a React component's render body, as this can cause infinite re-render loops; use useEffect or event handlers instead.
mode_rationale: React safety rule against state setters in render body

rule: Do not use TypeScript non-null assertions (!) when a value can be undefined; use default values (e.g., destructuring defaults) instead.
mode_rationale: Safety convention preferring defaults over non-null assertions

rule: Add the appropriate ARIA role attribute (e.g., role="button") to non-semantic HTML elements like divs that act as interactive controls.
mode_rationale: Accessibility convention for interactive non-semantic elements

rule: Do not introduce circular dependencies between build targets; co-locate test specs with their corresponding implementation to keep the dependency graph clean.
mode_rationale: Single convention about dependency graph hygiene

rule: Ensure API route parameter names accurately describe what they represent to avoid confusion from copy-paste errors.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for API route parameters

rule: In tests, assert the expected size of a collection before accessing individual elements to catch unexpected additions or omissions.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence testing best practice

rule: Clearly define and document the semantic difference between None (no filter / all results) and Some(Seq.empty) (empty filter / no results) in optional collection parameters.
mode_rationale: Convention about semantic clarity in optional parameters

rule: When code intentionally differs from a nearby established pattern, add an inline comment explaining why the divergence exists to help future developers.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation convention

rule: When adding feature flags or runtime configurations, verify that they are scoped to the correct entity types and not inadvertently enabled for all execution types.
mode_rationale: Single verification rule for feature flags

rule: Use explicitly named parameters rather than generic container objects (e.g., baseOperationParams) when the caller must know which fields are set; wrap generic helpers with specific-named facades for clarity.
mode_rationale: API design convention about parameter clarity

rule: Every new endpoint that accesses a protected resource must include an explicit authorization/permission check, consistent with similar existing endpoints.
mode_rationale: Security convention applied passively to every new endpoint

rule: When a comment references a concept that could be confused with similar concepts nearby, name the specific system or feature (e.g., 'mixpanel record access' instead of just 'record access') to prevent misreading.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation clarity rule

rule: When adding a new constraint or default for a form field, ensure it is also reflected in the centralized validation rules constant so that UI validation and business logic stay consistent.
mode_rationale: Convention about co-locating validation logic

rule: Never add test library dependencies to production build targets; restructure imports to use the production library instead.
mode_rationale: Single rule about build target dependency hygiene

rule: Wrap function references passed as props or used in dependency arrays with useCallback to prevent unnecessary re-renders.
mode_rationale: Simple React performance convention

rule: Use useMemo for derived values and expensive computations in React components to avoid recalculating on every render.
mode_rationale: Simple React performance convention

rule: Split non-trivial sub-components into their own files rather than defining multiple components in a single file.
mode_rationale: Code organization convention about file structure

rule: Use a stable unique identifier (name, id, or link) as the React key prop instead of array index to avoid rendering bugs on reorder or deletion.
mode_rationale: Simple React rendering best practice

rule: Use palette/token colors from the design system instead of hardcoded color values or raw color keys; validate with UX before overriding existing component styles.
mode_rationale: Style convention about using design tokens

rule: Import shared utility functions (e.g., noop) from existing libraries instead of defining inline equivalents.
mode_rationale: Simple reuse convention

rule: Define React context default values as a named constant and reuse it in both createContext and the provider return to ensure consistent shape and easier extension.
mode_rationale: React context pattern convention

rule: Use reduce when building a new value from a collection; reserve forEach for intentional side effects like RPC calls where no return value is needed.
mode_rationale: Simple coding style convention

rule: Set default values for hook return values and API responses so consumers don't need defensive optional chaining throughout the component.
mode_rationale: Defensive coding convention

rule: Use descriptive variable names instead of single-letter abbreviations (a, f, i) to maintain readability and long-term maintainability.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention

rule: Access store-provided values directly from useStore rather than extracting them through intermediate custom hooks to keep the data source clear.
mode_rationale: Architecture convention about data access patterns

rule: Use design system palette background tokens instead of the raw colors key; only use colors when a specific design requirement demands a system token override.
mode_rationale: Style convention about design system tokens

rule: TODOs left in code should be explicitly linked to a follow-up ticket so reviewers and future developers know when the cleanup is expected.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation convention for TODO comments

rule: Before overriding component styles, check if the design system component has been updated to support the desired appearance natively; validate discrepancies with UX.
mode_rationale: Convention about checking design system before customizing

rule: Always use typography styles from the design system (e.g., Storybook typography foundations) instead of custom font styles; if no existing token matches, discuss with UX before creating custom styles.
mode_rationale: Style convention about typography tokens

rule: Keep business logic (e.g., how to create/add entities) in container components or hooks, and keep presentation components free of domain-specific logic so they remain reusable and single-purpose.
mode_rationale: Architecture convention about component responsibilities

rule: Avoid adding computed values and derived state directly to React context providers; instead use separate hooks that consume the context, keeping the provider as a single source of truth without unnecessary growth and re-render propagation.
mode_rationale: React performance convention about context design

rule: Before implementing new data-fetching or state logic, check if an existing hook or utility already provides the needed functionality and use it instead of duplicating logic.
mode_rationale: Simple reuse convention

rule: When the same literal value (e.g., a template string, magic number, or configuration key) appears in multiple places, extract it into a shared constant to ensure consistency and ease of maintenance.
mode_rationale: Simple DRY convention

rule: When a component already supports a prop (e.g., dataTest for test IDs), use it directly instead of wrapping the component in an extra DOM element just to attach the attribute.
mode_rationale: Convention about using component APIs correctly

rule: Every TODO comment in the codebase must reference a tracking ticket (e.g., JIRA) so the work is visible, prioritized, and not forgotten.
Method names should clearly communicate their behavior (e.g., include 'latest' if the method returns only the most recent result) so callers understand semantics without reading the implementation.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention

rule: When an existing parameter or request class already models the inputs you need, reuse it rather than passing individual parameters — this simplifies method signatures and keeps the API consistent.
mode_rationale: API design convention about parameter reuse

rule: Do not rename established API method names without strong justification; inconsistent naming across versions creates confusion for consumers and breaks conventions.
mode_rationale: Naming convention about API stability

rule: TypeScript state declarations (e.g., useState) should be explicitly typed rather than using `any`, even in legacy code — prefer adding proper types when touching the code.
mode_rationale: TypeScript typing convention

rule: Icons should be sized via the component's `iconSize` prop rather than custom CSS or inline styles.
mode_rationale: Simple component usage convention

rule: API route segments should use the simplest, most intuitive name (e.g., `/vanity/` instead of a verbose alternative) and defer complex naming redesigns to the new API.
mode_rationale: API naming convention

rule: Structure MongoDB queries to leverage existing indexes for performance; reference the index definitions when writing or modifying queries.
mode_rationale: Database performance convention

rule: When resolving user IDs or object IDs, log a warning or skip invalid values rather than silently falling through to send garbage data (e.g., emailing a raw ObjectId string).
mode_rationale: Error handling convention about invalid data

rule: Return boolean expressions directly (e.g., `return x && list.includes(x)`) instead of wrapping them in if/else blocks.
mode_rationale: Simple code style convention

rule: Before writing a new helper, check whether the codebase already provides a utility for the same purpose (e.g., file-extension extraction).
mode_rationale: Simple reuse convention

rule: Pass icons and other content through a component's exposed props (e.g., `icon` on Button) rather than injecting them as children with manual spacing hacks like `{' '}`.
mode_rationale: Convention about using component APIs correctly

rule: Do not use backtick template literals when a plain string with no interpolation would suffice.

Do not remove classes or files solely because they have no explicit callers; frameworks like Play may discover and wire them implicitly (e.g., *Actions classes), and removing them can silently break functionality.
mode_rationale: Convention about understanding framework conventions before deleting

rule: Access color values via the `useThemeColor` hook rather than hardcoding hex values or using inline color references, to stay consistent with the theme system.
mode_rationale: Theme system convention

rule: When migrating or refactoring, remove color constants, styled components, or other exports that are no longer referenced anywhere in the codebase.
mode_rationale: Simple cleanup convention

rule: Name styled components after their semantic role or usage context (e.g., `IconDanger`, `IconFailure`) rather than generic or color-based names.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for styled components

rule: Apply the appropriate design token for each interaction state (e.g., `primaryHover` for hover states) rather than reusing a generic or incorrect token.
mode_rationale: Design token usage convention

rule: Avoid using `any` in TypeScript; always provide explicit types for function parameters, return values, and variables.
mode_rationale: TypeScript typing convention

rule: When an API call fails silently (e.g., fetching data that populates a list), show a toast notification so the user understands why the UI is empty rather than leaving them without explanation.
mode_rationale: Single convention: show a toast when API calls fail silently

rule: Use the design system's Typography component (Text, H1, H2 subcomponents) for all text styling instead of defining custom font styles; reference the Figma design for the correct text variant.
Use the current `spacing` design tokens for all spacing values; the older `margins` tokens are deprecated and should not be used in new code.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use current spacing tokens, not deprecated margins

rule: Extract magic numbers used in validation (e.g., min/max character lengths) into named constants defined near the related validation logic, so they can be reused and maintained in one place.
mode_rationale: Single convention: extract magic numbers into named constants

rule: Use a single button with a `loading` prop to indicate async operations instead of swapping between two different button components based on state; this keeps the UI consistent.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use loading prop instead of swapping button components

rule: When polling for async task status, use a stable identifier (e.g., entity ID) as the task key so that polling can resume after a page refresh, rather than relying on ephemeral in-memory state.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use stable IDs for polling keys, not ephemeral state

rule: Choose polling intervals appropriate to the operation's expected duration; a 15-second default is too long for user-facing progress — prefer shorter intervals (e.g., 2-5 seconds) for tasks the user is actively waiting on.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use shorter polling intervals for user-facing progress

rule: Use idiomatic Option combinators (map, getOrElse, flatMap) instead of verbose pattern matching or nested conditionals when transforming or defaulting optional values in Scala.
mode_rationale: Single convention: prefer Option combinators over verbose pattern matching

rule: Before modifying code in deprecated or legacy route handlers, verify whether those code paths are actually invoked by the current UI or other callers; don't assume deprecated routes are dead code.
mode_rationale: Single convention: verify deprecated routes are still called before modifying

rule: When depending on a branch reference (e.g., a Git branch for a package), pin it to the target branch (e.g., develop) before merging the PR to avoid shipping unstable references.
mode_rationale: Single convention: pin branch references to stable target before merging

rule: Use the Ant Design Menu's `onMenuClick` prop with menu item keys instead of wrapping individual buttons inside menu items; this makes the entire menu item area clickable and simplifies the handler logic.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use onMenuClick prop instead of buttons inside menu items

rule: Use palette colors (e.g., palette.textPrimary, textSecondary) and themeHelper for spacing instead of raw values or system token colors, as system tokens are reserved for specific cases.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use palette colors and themeHelper, not raw values

rule: Avoid naming a function parameter the same as the enclosing function name, as it causes confusion and potential shadowing issues.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention: avoid parameter/function name collision

rule: For REST PATCH/PUT endpoints, place the data being updated in the request body (or query params), not as path parameters; path segments should identify the resource, not carry update values.
mode_rationale: Single convention: update data goes in body, not path segments

rule: When logging or auditing state changes, retrieve the actual previous value from the data source rather than assuming it is the logical inverse of the new value.
mode_rationale: Single convention: retrieve real previous value for audit logging

rule: When testing an actual implementation, name the variable under test accurately (e.g., `authorizer`) instead of prefixing with `mock` when it is not a mock.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention: don't prefix with mock when testing real implementations

rule: Name functions and methods using a VerbNoun pattern (e.g., `checkProjectLevelESignatureWorkflow` instead of `projectLevelESignatureWorkflow`) to clearly communicate intent.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention: use VerbNoun pattern for function names

rule: Authorizer methods should follow the established interface pattern: expose specific `canX` methods that return an AuthorizationResult, keeping shared helpers private.
mode_rationale: Single convention: authorizers expose canX methods returning AuthorizationResult

rule: When the same permission or edit-eligibility check is repeated across components, extract it into a named helper function (e.g., `canEdit(row)`) to reduce duplication and improve readability.
mode_rationale: Single convention: extract repeated permission checks into named helpers

rule: When calling an async callback (like onSelect), await its resolution before triggering dependent operations (like refetch) to avoid race conditions and redundant calls.
mode_rationale: Single convention: await async callbacks before dependent operations

rule: Before adding a new API endpoint, evaluate whether an existing endpoint (e.g., GET /:projectId) can be extended with an additional field to serve the same purpose, reducing API surface area.
mode_rationale: Single convention: evaluate extending existing endpoints before adding new ones

rule: Use the dedicated iconSize prop for sizing icons instead of overriding with CSS font-size.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use iconSize prop instead of CSS font-size overrides

rule: When layout logic around a component is duplicated at multiple call sites, move that logic into the component itself so callers only pass dynamic content as children.
mode_rationale: Single convention: move duplicated layout logic into the component itself

rule: Always include curly braces for if/else blocks, even single-line bodies.
Use specific TypeScript types for domain objects instead of generic Record or any types when a proper interface or type exists.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use specific types instead of Record or any

rule: Use palette or system token colors instead of raw color names or hex values.
Use spacing tokens from the design system for gap, padding, and margin values instead of raw pixel values.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use spacing tokens for gap/padding/margin values

rule: Use kebab-case (mid-dash) for data-test attribute values.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention: kebab-case for data-test attribute values

rule: Changes to yarn.lock or package-lock.json should correspond to a modified package.json; unexplained lockfile-only changes should be questioned.
mode_rationale: Single convention: lockfile changes must correspond to package.json changes

rule: When a component has predefined type variants (e.g., success, error), rely on the built-in styling rather than passing custom colors that override the variant.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use built-in variant styling, not custom color overrides

rule: Do not use raw margins in styled-components; use the themeHelper utility to access spacing values from the theme.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use themeHelper for spacing, not raw margin values

rule: Do not add custom styles to shared UI components inline; if the component's design doesn't match requirements, file a ticket to update the shared component or discuss with UX.
mode_rationale: Single convention: don't add custom styles to shared components inline

rule: Do not explicitly import from index files (e.g., './module/index'); import from the directory path instead.
mode_rationale: Single convention: import from directory path, not explicit index files

rule: Name styled-component files as styles.ts or styled.ts to match the existing project convention, even if an alternative convention is preferable.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention: use styles.ts or styled.ts for styled-component files

rule: Do not create a new component wrapper when the underlying primitive (e.g., a checkbox) can be used directly without additional logic.
mode_rationale: Single convention: don't wrap primitives that can be used directly

rule: Never merge a PR that points a dependency at a custom branch version; pin to a released or develop version before merging.
mode_rationale: Single convention: never merge PRs with custom branch dependency references

rule: Use imperative onSuccess/onFailure callbacks passed to hooks for showing toasts, rather than declaratively rendering toasts inside components based on state.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use imperative callbacks for toasts, not declarative rendering

rule: Do not use useRemoteData (or similar data-fetching hooks) for mutations; these hooks are designed for initial page loads and refetching, not for write operations.
mode_rationale: Single convention: don't use data-fetching hooks for write operations

rule: Ensure IDE autoformatting is enabled so import statements follow the project's style conventions (e.g., single quotes, consistent spacing).
mode_rationale: Single convention: enable IDE autoformatting for import style consistency

rule: Avoid relying on closure values that may change between the time a hook captures them and the time they are used; prefer passing stable callback references instead.
mode_rationale: Single convention: prefer stable callback references over mutable closures in hooks

rule: Use optional chaining syntax (e.g., `fn?.()`) instead of explicit null checks before invoking optional callback functions.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use fn?.() instead of explicit null checks

rule: Do not use workarounds like closing modals in unrelated lifecycle points; instead, restructure the data flow so state transitions are handled in the correct place.
mode_rationale: Single convention: restructure data flow instead of using lifecycle workarounds

rule: Ensure flags and configuration options are applied at the layer where they have effect (e.g., at the API call site), not at unrelated layers like permission checkers.
mode_rationale: Single convention: apply flags/config at the layer where they have effect

rule: Reusable components must not depend on context values (e.g., currentProject) that may not exist in all usage contexts; pass required data explicitly via props instead.
mode_rationale: Single convention: pass data via props, don't depend on context in reusable components

rule: When an async hook makes a request to determine a value (e.g., a navigation link), catch errors and return a sensible fallback rather than letting the error propagate unhandled.
mode_rationale: Single convention: catch errors in async hooks and return sensible fallbacks

rule: Do not use the nullish coalescing or optional chaining operator on values that are already typed as optional, as it adds unnecessary noise.
mode_rationale: Single convention: don't use nullish operators on already-optional types

rule: Reusable components should receive data via props rather than reading route params directly, since they may be rendered in different routing contexts.
mode_rationale: Single convention: reusable components receive data via props, not route params

rule: Configuration keys must be defined with their actual expected type (e.g., Int for numeric values), not as strings that require downstream parsing.
mode_rationale: Single convention: define config keys with actual expected types, not strings

rule: When a function only makes sense with a value present, accept a required parameter instead of an Option so the caller is forced to validate before calling.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use required params when a value must be present

rule: API method signatures should use domain-specific types (e.g., DominoId) instead of primitives (e.g., String) to provide type safety and prevent accidental misuse.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use domain types like DominoId instead of raw String

rule: Use a regex pattern (e.g., `'^[0-9a-f]{24}$'`) to validate DominoId fields in Swagger/OpenAPI definitions, and ensure the pattern is applied to the correct ID type (DominoId vs UUID).
mode_rationale: Single convention about applying regex validation to ID fields in API specs

rule: When checking or retrieving data for multiple items, use a single batch query (e.g., `findByIds`) and filter the results in memory, rather than making a separate database call for each item.
mode_rationale: Simple performance guideline to prefer batch queries over per-item queries

rule: GET APIs should not use request bodies; use query parameters instead, or change the HTTP method to POST if a body is required.
mode_rationale: Single REST convention about GET request bodies

rule: API endpoint paths should describe the resource being returned, not imply a boolean check when the endpoint actually returns data (e.g., use `/bundle/permissions/view` instead of `/bundle/can-view`).
mode_rationale: Naming convention for API endpoint paths

rule: Function names should accurately reflect what they return; a name implying a boolean (e.g., `canViewBundleUsers`) should not be used for a function that returns a list of users.
mode_rationale: Naming convention about function name/return type alignment

rule: Even endpoints intended for broad access should verify the caller is at least an authenticated Domino user, to prevent anonymous access to internal data.
mode_rationale: Simple security rule about minimum authentication

rule: Use readable enum names (e.g., `ReadableName`) rather than UPPERCASE_SNAKE_CASE for enum values exposed through APIs, leveraging `fromStringOrUnknown` style parsers for consistency.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for API-facing enum values

rule: When parsing input that maps to an enum, reject unknown or invalid values immediately at the entry point rather than allowing bad state to propagate downstream.
mode_rationale: Simple error-handling guideline to reject bad input early

rule: Do not make individual fields optional when the entire parent object is either fully present or absent; unnecessary optionality adds complexity without value.
mode_rationale: Single convention about DTO field optionality

rule: User-facing error messages should describe the issue and suggest actionable next steps in plain language, rather than referencing internal configuration keys or system internals.
mode_rationale: Simple rule about keeping error messages user-friendly

rule: When implementing soft limits with known race conditions (e.g., count-then-act without locks), document why the race is acceptable and reference stronger enforcement mechanisms (e.g., existing quota checkers or distributed locks) for cases where strict limits are required.
mode_rationale: Convention to document race conditions when they exist

rule: When adding new configuration defaults that relate to existing settings, verify the defaults are logically consistent (e.g., a max-active-runs limit should be at least as large as the max-items limit it relates to).
mode_rationale: Simple consistency check for related config defaults

rule: When adding feature flags with a `since` version attribute, verify the version matches the actual release branch cut to avoid incorrect version metadata.
mode_rationale: Single verification rule for feature flag metadata

rule: Always use the project's design system components (e.g., Domino's Button) instead of native HTML elements or third-party alternatives.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to use design system over native elements

rule: Define only one React component per file; extract additional components into their own files.
Do not define props in a component's interface that are never consumed by the component.
mode_rationale: Simple cleanup rule about unused prop definitions

rule: Use styled-components instead of plain CSS classnames; use data-test attributes for test selectors rather than CSS classes.
mode_rationale: Style convention for CSS approach and test selectors

rule: Never type component props as `any`; always provide explicit, accurate type definitions.
mode_rationale: Simple typing rule against any in props

rule: When changing a shared component's styles or behavior, verify that existing usages are not adversely affected.
mode_rationale: Single guideline to verify impact on existing consumers

rule: To force a component remount when a value changes, pass that value as the React `key` prop instead of implementing custom state-reset logic.
mode_rationale: Simple React pattern preference for forcing remounts

rule: Do not assume a value's type by elimination (e.g., 'if not string then object'); use explicit type guards or discriminated unions.
mode_rationale: Single convention about type narrowing approach

rule: Do not wrap text in internationalization or whitelabel translation functions when the text is not subject to whitelabeling; use the string directly.
mode_rationale: Simple rule about avoiding unnecessary i18n wrapping

rule: Make component props required rather than optional when the component depends on them; this surfaces missing data at compile time instead of failing silently at runtime.
mode_rationale: Simple API design convention for prop optionality

rule: When leaving TODO comments or task markers in code, include a ticket reference (e.g., Jira ID) so the work can be tracked.
mode_rationale: Single convention about TODO comment format

rule: When implementation logic is reused or belongs to a specific domain, extract it into a dedicated method on the appropriate service rather than inlining it in callers.
mode_rationale: Simple guideline about extracting reused logic

rule: Remove code artifacts (fields, classes, imports) that are leftover from previous implementation attempts and no longer used.
mode_rationale: Simple cleanup convention for dead code

rule: Use lowercase with hyphens (kebab-case) for data-test attribute values, not camelCase or PascalCase.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention for data-test attributes

rule: Write test descriptions, variable names, and fixture data in English to maintain consistency and readability across the team.
mode_rationale: Simple convention about test language

rule: Use a loading wrapper component to resolve all async data before rendering, rather than allowing components to render with partially loaded state that may cause race conditions.
mode_rationale: Single pattern preference for resolving async data before rendering

rule: When logging audit trail changes, convert None/null values to a visible placeholder like '-' so that additions and removals are clearly distinguishable from changes.
mode_rationale: Simple convention for null representation in audit logs

rule: Avoid redundant conditional checks; combine related conditions into a single comparison when possible.
mode_rationale: Simple code style rule about combining conditions

rule: When a function like hasAccessToResource can return a Promise, always await it before using its result in a boolean expression; an unresolved Promise is truthy and will bypass the check.
mode_rationale: Single rule about awaiting async results before boolean checks

rule: Use React Router Link components for navigation between React-rendered pages instead of plain anchor tags, to preserve SPA behavior and avoid full page reloads.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to use React Router Link over anchor tags

rule: Do not mock table column definitions when testing table components, as this reduces the test to verifying a generic table rather than the actual component behavior; only mock specific components that cause test issues.
mode_rationale: Single testing convention about what to mock

rule: When a data-fetching hook throws on error, ensure callers catch the error; otherwise an uncaught exception will crash the view.
mode_rationale: Simple error-handling rule about catching hook errors

rule: Do not include callback parameters (e.g., onSuccess, onError) in a function signature if they are never used in the implementation.
Remove @ts-ignore or @ts-expect-error comments once the underlying type issue has been resolved, to restore type safety.
mode_rationale: Simple cleanup convention for ts-ignore directives

rule: Use ReactNode instead of more restrictive types (like string) for component props that accept renderable content, to allow consumers to pass any valid React element.
mode_rationale: Single typing convention for renderable content props

rule: File uploads and other form actions must go through the same submit function used for form validation, otherwise required fields (like Reason for Change) can be bypassed.
mode_rationale: Simple rule that all form actions go through validation

rule: Do not commit empty files to the repository; remove any files that have no content before merging.
mode_rationale: Simple hygiene rule about empty files

rule: Use the PRE flow (modal before action) only for actions that need user confirmation before proceeding; use the POST flow (confirmation after action) for inline forms and actions where the UI triggers after the user clicks — do not force a PRE pattern where POST is the natural fit.
mode_rationale: Single UX convention about confirmation modal timing

rule: When a hook exposes state that depends on a configuration value (like 'required'), pass that configuration into the hook and encapsulate the derived logic there, rather than spreading it across consumers.
mode_rationale: Simple encapsulation guideline for hook logic

rule: When a function call or method signature has multiple parameters, place each parameter on its own line for readability.
mode_rationale: Single formatting convention for function parameters

rule: API route descriptions should elaborate on the type of artifact or entity the endpoint expects, not just the action it performs.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation convention for API route descriptions

rule: When adding a new conditional branch or edge case in service logic, add a corresponding test case that exercises that condition.
mode_rationale: Single testing guideline to cover new branches

rule: When a TODO comment is resolved or removed, also remove any associated @ts-ignore directives that were only present because of the unfinished work.
mode_rationale: Simple cleanup rule to remove ts-ignore when TODOs are resolved

rule: Security-critical permission scopes and access controls must have dedicated test coverage to catch missing or incorrect permissions.
mode_rationale: Single rule that permission logic must have test coverage

rule: Use standard library or built-in methods for common operations like date formatting (e.g., toISOString) instead of manually constructing output strings.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to prefer stdlib over manual implementations

rule: Remove code that has no effect or is no longer needed rather than leaving it in place; verify that leftover imports, hooks, or statements are still required.
Import from a module's public index file rather than reaching into its internal file paths to maintain proper encapsulation and module boundaries.
mode_rationale: Single encapsulation rule about import paths

rule: Use React Router's Link or RouteLink components for in-app navigation instead of plain anchor tags, unless the link targets a non-React page (e.g., Scala-rendered).
mode_rationale: Single convention for choosing Link over anchor tags

rule: Do not use the explicit 'any' type in TypeScript; use proper types, generics, or 'unknown' to maintain type safety.
mode_rationale: Simple type safety rule to avoid 'any'

rule: Before creating a new UI component, check if a shared or common component already exists that can be reused or extended.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence guideline to check for existing components first

rule: Audit and telemetry events must reference the most specific entity (e.g., file paths, not the parent project ID) so events are traceable to the exact resource acted upon.
mode_rationale: Convention for referencing the most specific entity in audit events

rule: When a feature is behind a feature flag, verify that all code paths for that feature check the flag before executing, especially at newly added entry points.
mode_rationale: Single check to ensure all code paths respect feature flags

rule: Use pattern matching (match/case) instead of conditional checks with .get() when parsing or validating results in Scala.
mode_rationale: Style preference for pattern matching over .get() in Scala

rule: Return the result of a function call directly instead of assigning it to an intermediate variable that is immediately returned.
mode_rationale: Simple code style rule to avoid unnecessary intermediate variables

rule: When creating new classes based on existing ones, update class-level comments to reflect the new class's purpose rather than leaving copied comments from the source class.
mode_rationale: Convention to update comments when copying classes

rule: Do not explicitly pass implicit parameters in Scala; let the compiler resolve them automatically to keep call sites clean.
mode_rationale: Scala style rule to let the compiler resolve implicits

rule: Test descriptions must accurately describe the behavior being tested; if the test is inside a describe block for method X, the test name should not describe the behavior of method Y.
mode_rationale: Convention for accurate test descriptions

rule: Include the subject (who or what) in test descriptions to make them unambiguous — e.g., 'authorize the project owner using...' instead of 'authorize using...'.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for test descriptions

rule: Use enums instead of raw strings for fields with a well-known, finite set of values (e.g., certificate types) to get compile-time safety and discoverability.
mode_rationale: Type safety convention to use enums over raw strings

rule: Remove unused code paths (e.g., HTTP method mappings, enum cases) rather than keeping them for hypothetical future use; they can always be added back when actually needed.
mode_rationale: Simple guideline to remove unused code

rule: When testing multiple cases that share setup, group assertions inside the loop with their mocks to reduce duplication and improve readability.
mode_rationale: Testing style convention for grouping assertions with mocks

rule: Do not use the TypeScript non-null assertion operator (!) unless you can guarantee the value is defined; use optional chaining or explicit null checks instead.
mode_rationale: Type safety rule about the non-null assertion operator

rule: Do not place side effects inside render props, as they execute on every re-render; extract side effects into event handlers or lifecycle hooks instead.
mode_rationale: React convention to avoid side effects in render props

rule: When a value is null-checked in one location, it must not be assumed non-null (via !) in another location that receives the same value — handle nullability consistently.
mode_rationale: Convention for consistent null handling across locations

rule: When a user lacks permissions, display a 403 Forbidden page rather than silently redirecting to an unrelated route.
mode_rationale: UX convention for handling unauthorized access

rule: Never use custom font-size values outside the design system's typography scale; wrap text in a typography component to maintain visual consistency.
mode_rationale: Design system rule to use typography components

rule: Use precise terminology for cryptographic concepts — 'signingKey' refers to the private key used to sign, not the public key used to verify.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for cryptographic concepts

rule: When defining new API endpoints, align field names and path segments with terminology already used in related internal systems (e.g., Vault config, other services).
mode_rationale: Naming convention to align with existing internal systems

rule: Maintain exactly one entry per dependency in package.json; avoid duplicate entries or stray empty lines.
mode_rationale: Simple rule to avoid duplicate dependency entries

rule: Keep dependencies in package.json alphabetically sorted to maintain consistency and ease scanning.
mode_rationale: Ordering convention for package.json

rule: When a function's only async operation is the return value, return the promise directly instead of marking the function async and awaiting it.
mode_rationale: Simple performance convention for returning promises directly

rule: Prefer narrow, specific TypeScript types over broad ones (e.g., use a union or specific interface instead of 'any' or overly generic types).
mode_rationale: Type safety convention to prefer specific types

rule: Before adding loading spinners or conditional rendering, trace the component's mount point through the routing tree to ensure it only affects the intended section, not the entire app.
mode_rationale: Single check to trace mount point before adding loading UI

rule: Always verify that useEffect dependency arrays are correctly placed inside the hook call — dependencies placed outside the function body create malformed effects.
mode_rationale: React correctness rule for useEffect dependency placement

rule: Pin package or dependency references to a stable branch (e.g., develop or a release tag) before merging a PR — do not leave references pointing at feature branches.
mode_rationale: Convention to pin dependency references to stable branches

rule: Do not pull in utility libraries like Ramda for operations that can be accomplished with native JavaScript methods.
mode_rationale: Simple guideline to prefer native methods over utility libraries

rule: Use styled-components instead of inline styles to keep styling consistent and maintainable.
mode_rationale: Styling convention to use styled-components

rule: Do not add styled-component files or other boilerplate files that contain no actual content or styles.
mode_rationale: Convention to avoid adding empty files

rule: Do not change HTML element types (e.g., replacing a div with a different element) in fragile layout areas without thorough testing.
mode_rationale: Caution rule for HTML element type changes in fragile areas

rule: Never throw errors from a React component's render function; it will trigger the error boundary at best or crash the UI at worst. Handle invalid states gracefully with fallback UI.
mode_rationale: React convention to never throw from render

rule: Prefer a single source of truth for state with transformations between external and form representations, rather than syncing multiple independent state stores.
Add TODO comments for known incomplete implementations or placeholder code so they are tracked and not forgotten.
mode_rationale: Documentation convention for placeholder code

rule: When a value has a known set of possible strings, define an enum or union type to enforce type safety instead of using a raw string type.
mode_rationale: Type safety convention for known string sets

rule: Use the design system's spacing keys/tokens instead of hardcoded pixel values in styled-components.
Use the design system's Typography component for text rendering instead of raw HTML elements with manual styling.
Import styled-components using the namespace convention `import * as Styles from './styled'` to follow established codebase conventions and enable easy visual search-and-replace.
mode_rationale: Import convention for styled-components

rule: When a method has non-obvious behavior that could be mistaken for a bug, add a warning comment so future debuggers can quickly identify the root cause.
mode_rationale: Documentation convention for non-obvious behavior

rule: Never hardcode the product name 'Domino' in UI strings; always use the white-label brandedText helper so the name is substituted correctly in white-labeled deployments.
mode_rationale: White-label convention to never hardcode product name

rule: Use the team's automock functionality for mocking components and extracting received props in tests, rather than writing manual mocks or using ts-ignore.
mode_rationale: Testing convention to use team automock utilities

rule: Do not use @ts-ignore to suppress TypeScript errors; fix the underlying type issue or use proper type assertions instead.
mode_rationale: Type safety rule to fix types instead of suppressing errors

rule: The core package should only define base routes for each domain; feature-specific inner routing belongs in the feature module, not in core.
mode_rationale: Architecture boundary rule for core vs feature routing

rule: When mounting Kubernetes secrets with restrictive file modes (e.g., 0400), verify that the pod's fsGroup security context does not broaden permissions; if it does, copy the file to a writable path and chmod it.
mode_rationale: Security convention for Kubernetes secret file permissions

rule: When assigning permission flags, use the variable name that matches the actual permission being checked to avoid confusing future readers.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for permission flag variables

rule: Use the 'affecting' field for data types that are related but not the direct object of the audit event; reserve 'target' for the direct object of the verb.
mode_rationale: API convention for audit event field semantics

rule: When passing a boolean prop with value true in JSX, use the shorthand form (just the prop name) instead of explicitly setting it to true.
mode_rationale: JSX style convention for boolean props

rule: In React components, define the props interface above the component definition and export the component as the default export.
mode_rationale: React component structure convention

rule: Use the `spacing` theme key for layout spacing instead of the deprecated `margins` theme key, and never consume theme keys directly.
mode_rationale: Design system convention to use current spacing tokens

rule: Use `import { render } from '@domino/test-utils/dist/testing-library'` which provides `getByDominoTestId` and `queryAllByDominoTestId` for querying `data-test` attributes, instead of writing custom query helpers.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use a specific import for DOM queries

rule: Use `userEvent` instead of `fireEvent` for simulating user interactions in tests, as it more closely mirrors real browser behavior.
mode_rationale: Simple preference for one API over another in tests

rule: Use `mockResolvedValue` instead of `mockImplementation` with fake timers when testing async operations, to keep tests simpler and avoid unnecessary timer complexity.
mode_rationale: Simple preference for one mocking approach over another

rule: Never use `any` as an explicit type annotation; use proper types, generics, or `unknown` instead.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence type annotation rule

rule: Lockfile changes (yarn.lock, package-lock.json) should always correspond to a dependency change in package.json; unexplained lockfile diffs should be investigated and removed if unnecessary.
mode_rationale: Simple validation rule for lockfile diffs

rule: When a feature depends on a feature flag, disable or hide the corresponding UI controls when the flag is off, rather than showing options that won't work.
mode_rationale: Single convention: hide controls when flag is off

rule: When a PR includes changes outside the scope of its Jira ticket, add a note in the PR description explaining what those changes do and why they are included.
mode_rationale: Simple PR hygiene convention

rule: When interpolating optional values into user-facing strings, provide a meaningful fallback to avoid rendering empty or broken text (e.g., trailing hyphens or dangling separators).
mode_rationale: Single convention about fallback values in strings

rule: Avoid calling `.get` on `Option` values; use pattern matching or combinators like `map`/`flatMap`/`fold` to safely unwrap optional values.
mode_rationale: Simple Scala style rule: avoid .get on Option

rule: Define each React component in its own file rather than inlining multiple components in a single file.
Do not pass props to a component that does not use them; only pass what the component's interface expects.
mode_rationale: Simple rule: don't pass unused props

rule: Verify that the router uses exclusive matching (e.g., Switch) before adding a catch-all/404 route, otherwise it will render alongside every other route.
mode_rationale: Single guard rule before adding catch-all routes

rule: When fixing routing issues, scope changes to the affected section's inner routers rather than restructuring the entire top-level routing.
mode_rationale: Simple scoping convention for routing fixes

rule: Log each error once at the appropriate level; do not duplicate error logging calls for the same error.
mode_rationale: Single rule: log each error once

rule: Use the codebase's existing error-handling utilities (e.g., safeGetErrorMessage) before writing custom error parsing logic.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: use existing utils before writing new ones

rule: Expose package contents through the package's index file rather than allowing consumers to import from internal module paths.
mode_rationale: Single module export convention

rule: Use the design system's Typography component for all text rendering instead of raw HTML elements or styled text.
When adding new fields to a DTO, make them optional to avoid breaking changes for existing consumers.
mode_rationale: Single API compatibility convention

rule: Memoize objects and arrays passed as props to child components to prevent unnecessary re-renders caused by new reference creation on each render cycle.
mode_rationale: Single performance convention for React props

rule: When the same literal value appears multiple times, extract it into a named constant to improve maintainability and reduce duplication.
mode_rationale: Simple DRY convention for literal values

rule: Use palette colors from the design system (color-palettes.ts, colors.ts, colorsNew.ts) instead of hardcoded hex values in styled components.
mode_rationale: Single rule: use palette colors, not hardcoded hex

rule: Use semantic text color tokens (textPrimary, textSecondary) for text elements instead of arbitrary color values.
mode_rationale: Single rule: use semantic tokens for text color

rule: Use `currentColor` for SVG and icon fill/stroke colors so they inherit from the parent text color and stay consistent with the surrounding context.
mode_rationale: Single SVG styling convention
