rule: Never set font sizes manually in styled components; use the typography component to ensure consistency with the design system's type scale.
mode_rationale: Single rule: never set font sizes manually

rule: Use purpose-built components (e.g., help link component for support/help links) instead of generic HTML elements to ensure consistent behavior and styling.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use purpose-built components

rule: Never use an invalid path as a placeholder link href; use '#' with a TODO comment instead, or wait until the real URL is available.
mode_rationale: Simple rule about placeholder href values

rule: Reference existing constants instead of duplicating their values; if a const is already defined in the file or module, use it.
Do not use `.map()` that returns the same value unchanged; it adds unnecessary iteration with no transformation.
mode_rationale: Single rule: no no-op .map() calls

rule: When the codebase uses synonyms (e.g., 'run' and 'execution'), keep naming consistent within a bounded context; if a DTO uses 'Run', the corresponding domain class should too, unless the name is scoped to a different local context.
mode_rationale: Single naming consistency convention

rule: When catching exceptions to return error responses, always set an explicit error code — don't leave it unset even for generic Exception types.
mode_rationale: Single rule: always set error codes

rule: Do not use flatMap to silently skip invalid inputs (e.g., invalid tokens returning empty optionals); fail the validation explicitly with an appropriate error response.
mode_rationale: Single convention: fail loudly, don't skip silently

rule: Use 403 Forbidden (not 401 Unauthorized) when a request will never succeed regardless of credentials; 401 implies retrying with valid creds would work.
mode_rationale: Single rule: 403 vs 401 usage

rule: For security-sensitive validation failures, return a generic error message to the client (to avoid leaking information to attackers) but log the full details server-side for debugging.
mode_rationale: Single convention: generic response, detailed log

rule: Return an explicit BadRequest for unsupported or unexpected input rather than silently ignoring it, so callers get clear feedback.
mode_rationale: Single rule: return BadRequest for unsupported input

rule: In functional React components, use hooks (e.g., useThemeColor) instead of patterns designed for class components.
mode_rationale: Simple React convention: hooks over class patterns

rule: When writing new code, do not carry over lint ignores or suppressions that originated from bulk updates; fix the underlying issue instead.
mode_rationale: Single rule: fix lint issues in new code

rule: Reorder early-return conditionals so that later branches don't need to re-check conditions already eliminated by earlier returns.
mode_rationale: Single code style convention for early returns

rule: When the same logic appears in multiple places with only minor parameter differences, extract it into a shared utility function.
mode_rationale: Simple DRY convention

rule: Props that control authorization or permissions should be required, not optional with permissive defaults, to prevent accidental privilege escalation when a parent forgets to pass the prop.
mode_rationale: Single rule: auth props must not be optional

rule: When one API response depends on another, chain the requests so that all related data updates atomically rather than rendering stale combinations.
mode_rationale: Single convention for dependent API calls

rule: Logic that is not component-specific should be extracted into a utility module rather than inlined in a component.
mode_rationale: Simple code organization convention

rule: When a function or hook accepts many arguments, request a single config object parameter to improve readability and extensibility.
mode_rationale: Single API design convention

rule: Use shared constants (e.g., NOT_AVAILABLE_PLACEHOLDER) for common placeholder or fallback values to maintain visual and behavioral consistency across the platform.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: use shared constants for placeholders

rule: Aria labels must be human-readable text, not dash-case or ID-style strings, since they are read aloud by screen readers.
Include the acting principal (user identity) in log messages for operations that modify state, to support debugging and audit traceability.
mode_rationale: Single logging convention for audit traceability

rule: Document the observable behavior of notification and messaging abstractions: what medium they use (email, in-app popup, etc.), who receives them, and what triggers them.
mode_rationale: Single documentation convention for notification abstractions

rule: Add explanatory comments when initialization or default-value logic involves multiple conditions or non-obvious rules, so future maintainers can understand the intent without reverse-engineering.
mode_rationale: Single convention: comment non-obvious init logic

rule: When implementing client-side filtering on tables or lists, verify that selection state (row selection, checkboxes) remains consistent after filters are applied or removed.
mode_rationale: Single testing convention for filter/selection consistency

rule: When a feature depends on multiple configuration flags, guard it behind ALL required flags (e.g., config.enabled && sshConfig.enabled), not just a subset.
mode_rationale: Single rule: check all required flags, not a subset

rule: Always include optional components or plugins in the configuration payload and control their behavior via an enabled flag, rather than conditionally omitting them entirely.
mode_rationale: Single configuration convention: include with enabled flag

rule: When mapping request payloads to internal models, ensure all fields from the payload are mapped — do not silently drop fields (e.g., setting ssh = None when the payload includes SSH configuration).
mode_rationale: Single rule about not dropping fields during payload mapping

rule: When methods operate on specific domain resources (e.g., Kubernetes resources), include the resource type name in the method name for consistency with related methods.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for method names involving domain resources

rule: Move complex or hard-to-read ternary expressions into a named variable or a separate function to improve readability.
mode_rationale: Simple readability guideline for ternary expressions

rule: Prefer built-in browser/platform APIs (e.g., URLSearchParams) over third-party libraries (e.g., qs) when the native API covers the use case.
mode_rationale: Convention to prefer built-in APIs over third-party libraries

rule: Boolean fields in API response schemas should be marked as required with a default value in the transformer, so omitted fields are not silently treated as false.
mode_rationale: Single rule about boolean field schema design

rule: When accessing fields that may be absent during migrations or upgrades, use explicit pattern matching with a fallback branch and a warning log rather than assuming the field exists.
mode_rationale: Defensive coding convention for migration-sensitive fields

rule: Common/shared components should not force consumers to wrap them in specific context providers; document the requirement at minimum, and prefer encapsulating the dependency internally.
mode_rationale: Architecture convention for shared component dependencies

rule: Name boolean variables and props with verb prefixes like `should`, `is`, `has`, or `can` to clearly communicate their purpose (e.g., `shouldDisableHwPicker` instead of a generic name).
mode_rationale: Naming convention for boolean variables and props

rule: Tests should not be left in a skipped or commented-out state; either fix and re-enable them or remove them entirely.
mode_rationale: Simple rule about test hygiene

rule: When refactoring removes all usages of a component, delete the component code rather than leaving it as dead code in the codebase.
mode_rationale: Cleanup convention when refactoring removes usages

rule: Avoid using deprecated API keys (e.g., the `margins` key in styled components); use the current recommended approach instead.
mode_rationale: Simple rule to avoid deprecated API keys

rule: When the design system provides a component (e.g., Link), use it instead of creating custom styled elements that duplicate existing functionality.
mode_rationale: Convention to prefer design system components over custom ones

rule: Aria labels should be written in human-readable format (e.g., 'SSH checkbox'), not in dash-case or code-style identifiers (e.g., 'ssh-checkbox').
mode_rationale: Accessibility naming convention for aria labels

rule: When component logic is reusable across multiple contexts, extract it into an independent custom hook rather than keeping it inline in a single component.
mode_rationale: Code organization guideline for reusable hook extraction

rule: Instead of exporting raw base URL paths for API endpoints, create and export specific named functions for each endpoint (e.g., `getArtifactUrnEndpointUrl`).
mode_rationale: API design convention for endpoint exports

rule: Do not import from a module's internal file paths; expose what is needed through the module's public `index` file and import from that single entry point.
mode_rationale: Architecture convention for module import boundaries

rule: Add comments explaining non-obvious default values (e.g., `true`) so future readers understand the intent without digging into history.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation convention for default values

rule: Write explicit tests for operations on missing or non-existent entity IDs to verify the system handles them gracefully.
mode_rationale: Testing convention to cover missing entity scenarios

rule: Do not use the deprecated `margins` key in styled components; use the project's themeHelper or standard spacing utilities instead.
mode_rationale: Simple rule to avoid deprecated margins key

rule: Define helper functions and variables in lexical order, placing them before the component that uses them for readability.
mode_rationale: Code ordering convention for helper functions

rule: Use plain arrow function components (`const MyComponent = () => {}`) instead of typing with `React.FC` to follow project convention.
mode_rationale: Project convention for component typing style

rule: Define component prop types in the same file as the component rather than creating separate `.types.ts` files for simple prop interfaces.
mode_rationale: Convention to keep prop types in the same file

rule: Before implementing a utility (e.g., clipboard copy), search the codebase for existing implementations and reuse them to avoid duplication.
mode_rationale: Convention to search for and reuse existing utilities

rule: Use the design system's Typography component and text types instead of setting raw CSS `font-size` values in styled components.
mode_rationale: Convention to use design system typography over raw CSS

rule: Create separate styled components for child elements rather than using CSS selectors nested inside a parent styled component.
mode_rationale: Styling convention for child element components

rule: Use the function form for styled-components attrs (`() => ({})`) instead of the object form, per the updated API convention.
mode_rationale: API convention for styled-components attrs syntax

rule: Do not use custom font sizes; use existing typography tokens from the design system, and escalate to UX if the design requires something different.
Use a boolean type for binary on/off states instead of a string enum or flavor prop.
mode_rationale: Type convention for binary on/off states

rule: Before writing inline logic, check if shared utility helpers already exist for the task and use them.
mode_rationale: Convention to check for existing shared helpers before writing inline logic

rule: Scala for-comprehensions with Futures execute sequentially (flatMap), not in parallel; assign Futures to vals before the for-comprehension if parallel execution is needed.
mode_rationale: Performance convention about Future parallelism in for-comprehensions

rule: In Scala tests, explicitly await all Futures (via Await.result or equivalent) to ensure the test framework waits for completion and assertions are evaluated.
mode_rationale: Testing convention to explicitly await Futures

rule: Do not leave Futures unyielded at the end of a test block; the test framework may not wait for them, causing silently passing tests.
mode_rationale: Testing convention to not leave Futures unyielded

rule: Use val instead of var in Scala when the variable is assigned once and does not need to be reassigned.
mode_rationale: Scala immutability convention

rule: Catch specific exception types rather than matching on error message strings, to avoid brittle error handling that breaks when messages change.
mode_rationale: Error handling convention to catch specific types

rule: Combine related imports from the same package into a single import statement rather than separate lines.
mode_rationale: Import organization convention

rule: Format log messages with clearly labeled key=value pairs using string interpolation, especially for optional/collection fields, to make logs parseable and readable.
mode_rationale: Logging format convention for key=value pairs

rule: Name API endpoints for their specific purpose rather than using broad names that could be confused with other functionality (e.g., 'cost activation status' instead of 'cost status').
mode_rationale: Naming convention for API endpoint specificity

rule: Use `None` instead of `Option.empty` in Scala code for brevity and idiomatic style.
mode_rationale: Scala idiom convention for None vs Option.empty

rule: Method and variable names should accurately describe what they return — a method returning a count should include 'count' in its name, not just the entity name.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for method return type clarity

rule: Do not wrap a Seq or other collection in Option unnecessarily — mapping over an empty collection already returns an empty collection, making the Option wrapper redundant.
mode_rationale: Scala convention to avoid redundant Option around collections

rule: Error messages should accurately describe the failure condition — do not assume a specific cause (e.g., 'archived') when the check only verifies a broader condition (e.g., 'does not exist').
mode_rationale: Error handling convention for accurate error descriptions

rule: When adding new public methods or modifying existing ones, add or update unit tests — check for existing spec files in the codebase and extend them.
mode_rationale: Testing convention requiring tests for new public methods

rule: When using immer-based reducers (e.g., Redux Toolkit or useImmerReducer), mutate the draft state directly instead of spreading it — spreading defeats immer's proxy mechanism and can cause subtle bugs.
mode_rationale: Architecture convention for correct immer draft mutation

rule: Do not use backtick template literals when a plain string suffices — reserve template literals for interpolation or multi-line strings.
mode_rationale: Style convention to reserve backticks for interpolation

rule: When a value is already typed (e.g., via a typed parameter or return type), let TypeScript infer the type instead of adding redundant explicit annotations.
mode_rationale: TypeScript convention to avoid redundant type annotations

rule: Only use @ts-ignore when there is a genuine type system limitation — investigate the root cause of the type error first and fix it properly when possible.
mode_rationale: Convention to investigate type errors before suppressing

rule: Before hardcoding navigation paths, check for existing centralized route helpers (e.g., core/routes.ts) and use them to avoid duplication and ensure consistency.
mode_rationale: Convention to use existing route helpers over hardcoded paths

rule: When changing the encoding or format of data written to persistent storage, assess backward compatibility — determine whether old data needs migration or if it is regenerated on each use.
mode_rationale: Convention to assess backward compatibility when changing data formats

rule: Use consistent entity names across API endpoints and models; do not mix similar terms (e.g., 'app instance' vs 'app version instance') for the same concept.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for consistent entity terms across APIs

rule: When creating new entities, initialize numeric fields to their correct default values (e.g., views should be 0 for a newly created app) rather than leaving them null or undefined.
mode_rationale: Convention to set correct default values for new entities

rule: Never use `.get` on Option types; use `getOrElse` with a meaningful exception or error message to make failures explicit and debuggable.
Hardcoded default values like time ranges, limits, and thresholds should be made configurable through central configuration rather than embedded as magic numbers.
mode_rationale: Simple guideline to externalize magic numbers

rule: Never check a Promise for truthiness directly — a Promise object is always truthy. Await the Promise or use `=== true` on the resolved value to get the correct result.
mode_rationale: Single rule about Promise truthiness pitfall

rule: When deleting or archiving an entity, ensure all related resources (e.g., vanity URLs, references, child records) are also cleaned up to prevent orphaned state.
mode_rationale: Convention to clean up orphaned state on deletion

rule: When modifying repository or data access layer code, add or update corresponding integration tests to cover the new behavior.
mode_rationale: Simple expectation to add tests with data layer changes

rule: When a component delegates to a service method, add mock verification (e.g., `verify(service.method, times(n))`) in unit tests to confirm the interaction occurs as expected.
mode_rationale: Convention to use mock verification for service calls

rule: Do not use `useMemo` for simple calculations like counts or string concatenations; reserve memoization for expensive computations or objects that would trigger unnecessary re-renders.
mode_rationale: Single rule about when memoization is appropriate

rule: Keep authorization logic (who can access) separate from feature flag or custom feature logic (what is enabled); do not mix them in the same provider or component.
mode_rationale: Architectural separation principle, not a procedure

rule: Ensure all variables referenced inside `useCallback` or `useEffect` are listed in the dependency array to prevent stale closures and bugs.
mode_rationale: Standard React convention about dependency arrays

rule: Place feature components in modules that match their domain; do not add unrelated features to existing views just because it is convenient.
mode_rationale: Simple placement guideline for feature components

rule: When performing synchronous file operations (especially downloads or reads), evaluate whether the operation could block the main thread or lock resources, particularly with large files.
mode_rationale: Awareness guideline about blocking operations

rule: When verifying signed payloads, assert that critical fields in the signed payload match the corresponding fields in the request to prevent tampering or mismatch.
mode_rationale: Single security assertion rule

rule: Store sensitive configuration values (keys, tokens, credentials) in Kubernetes secrets rather than application config files.
mode_rationale: Simple convention for secrets storage

rule: Authorization checks should be placed at the level that matches the granularity of the operation being authorized — if an action operates on a batch of entities, the auth check should cover the batch, not individual items.
mode_rationale: Principle about auth check granularity

rule: When the same authorization action is applied across multiple endpoints or methods, add a comment explaining why the duplication exists to help future developers understand the intent.
mode_rationale: Convention to comment duplicated auth logic

rule: When adding a security bypass or workaround, the comment must explain the full scope of the bypass, who it affects, the security implications, and the tracking ticket for the permanent fix.
mode_rationale: Convention about documenting security workarounds

rule: Each authorizer should only enforce its own concern; do not duplicate authorization checks (e.g., anonymous user checks) that are already handled by other authorizers protecting the same endpoint.
mode_rationale: Single principle about authorizer scope

rule: Always use Domino's wrapped design system components (tags, tooltips, etc.) instead of importing raw Ant Design components directly, to maintain visual consistency.
mode_rationale: Simple import preference rule

rule: Use the design system's typography tokens and theme spacing values instead of hardcoded styles to maintain consistency across the UI.
mode_rationale: Convention to use design tokens over hardcoded styles

rule: Keep domain-specific code (e.g., governance logic) encapsulated within its own module rather than spreading it across unrelated sections of the codebase.
mode_rationale: Simple code organization principle

rule: Memoize the value object passed to React context providers to prevent unnecessary re-renders of all consumers on every parent render.
mode_rationale: Single performance convention for context providers

rule: New service methods and functionality must be accompanied by corresponding unit tests in the same PR, not deferred to a follow-up.
mode_rationale: Expectation that tests ship with new code

rule: Use consistent log levels across architectural layers — debug in services and trace in controllers — to provide distinct granularity when troubleshooting.
mode_rationale: Convention for log level assignment by layer

rule: Do not reuse a permission-check or authorization method for unrelated purposes; extract a dedicated method so that future changes to one check do not silently break another.
mode_rationale: Principle against reusing methods for unrelated purposes

rule: Name DTOs after the domain concept they represent (e.g., DataPlaneDto for workspace data plane config), not after implementation details or generic terms.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for DTOs

rule: Delete TODO comments when their associated ticket or task has been completed; stale TODOs create confusion about remaining work.
mode_rationale: Simple hygiene rule about TODO comments

rule: When cleaning up credentials, ensure all storage locations are covered — including credential files stored inside .git directories, not just the standard git-credentials path.
mode_rationale: Convention to check all credential locations

rule: Use history.replace instead of history.push when navigating away from a page that users should not return to via the browser back button.
mode_rationale: Simple routing convention for non-reversible pages

rule: Extract default values and magic numbers into named constants rather than inlining them, so they are discoverable, documented, and easy to change.
mode_rationale: Standard coding convention about named constants

rule: Use the established loading UI pattern (confirmLoading prop with useLoadingButton hook) for modal confirmation buttons instead of ad-hoc spinner implementations.
mode_rationale: Convention to use existing loading pattern

rule: Remove commented-out code from PRs; if it's needed later, it lives in version control history.
mode_rationale: Simple code cleanliness rule

rule: Storybook stories must not include custom inline styles or workarounds that make components appear to have capabilities they don't actually have — this creates false expectations for consumers.
mode_rationale: Convention about Storybook story fidelity

rule: When modifying a shared UI component, manually verify the change at all usage sites to catch regressions — changes to shared components can break consumers in unexpected ways.
mode_rationale: Single testing guideline for shared components

rule: Do not call onChange callbacks inside useEffect hooks, as unstable references will cause the effect to fire repeatedly; instead, lift state changes to event handlers or use stable callback refs.
mode_rationale: Single React anti-pattern to avoid

rule: Reusable picker/select components should not include their own label; consumers should wrap them in a form item and control labeling externally.
mode_rationale: Component API design convention

rule: Shared/common components must not depend on ambient context like currentProject; instead accept the required data (e.g., projectId) as a prop so the component works in any scope.
mode_rationale: Convention to accept data via props not ambient context

rule: Files that render JSX are components and should be named and organized as such; only put pure logic helpers (formatters, parsers, validators) in utils files.
mode_rationale: Simple file organization rule

rule: Column definitions, configuration objects, and other static data that do not depend on props or state should be defined outside the component to avoid unnecessary re-creation on every render.
mode_rationale: Performance convention about static definitions

rule: Components intended for reuse within a domain should receive configuration via props rather than reading from URL parameters, to avoid coupling to a specific routing context.
mode_rationale: Convention to decouple components from routing

rule: Use arrow function expressions for component definitions and handlers to maintain a consistent code style across the codebase.
mode_rationale: Code style convention for function syntax

rule: Use the project's named color tokens (e.g., border-specific palette colors) instead of raw hex values or ad-hoc color strings, even if surrounding legacy code uses hardcoded values.
mode_rationale: Convention to use named color tokens

rule: Use theme spacing keys and border-radius tokens instead of raw pixel values, and avoid conflicting shorthand-then-override patterns (e.g., setting margin then overriding margin-bottom separately).
mode_rationale: Convention to use theme tokens over raw values

rule: If a function parameter is always called with the same value, remove the parameter and inline the constant to reduce unnecessary abstraction.
mode_rationale: Simple refactoring principle about constant params

rule: When a picker or selector depends on a parent entity (e.g., project), reset its selected value when the parent ID changes to prevent stale selections.
mode_rationale: Convention to reset child state when parent changes

rule: Move styled-component definitions into a separate styles file rather than co-locating them in the component file.
mode_rationale: File organization convention for styled-components

rule: Each React component should live in its own file; do not define multiple exported components in a single file.
mode_rationale: Simple file organization rule

rule: Before adding an eslint-disable comment, verify that the suppression is genuinely necessary; if all dependencies are listed, the disable is likely unneeded.
mode_rationale: Convention about eslint-disable usage

rule: Add inline comments explaining non-obvious logic, especially when values are concatenated, transformed, or derived in ways that aren't self-evident.
mode_rationale: Convention to comment non-obvious derivations

rule: Never hardcode the product brand name in UI strings; use whitelabelling utilities like getAppBrandedText so the text adapts to white-label deployments.
mode_rationale: Convention to use branding utilities over hardcoded names

rule: Name configuration objects with a suffix like Config or Options to distinguish them from React components (e.g. persistenceCheckboxConfig instead of persistenceCheckbox).
mode_rationale: Naming convention for config objects

rule: Do not create wrapper functions that simply delegate to another function with the same signature; call the underlying function directly.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence convention about avoiding unnecessary delegation

rule: Do not silently swallow errors with only console.error; surface failures to the user through UI feedback (toast, banner, or inline error) especially when the failure affects visible state.
mode_rationale: Single guideline about showing errors in UI instead of swallowing them

rule: Avoid using useEffect with incomplete dependency arrays to sync state from props; prefer React's recommended pattern of adjusting state during render when a prop changes.
mode_rationale: Single convention about preferring React's recommended state-during-render pattern

rule: Ensure inline comments accurately describe the actual runtime behavior; a comment saying 'initial onChange' is misleading if the effect runs on every dependency change.
mode_rationale: Simple rule about keeping comments accurate

rule: Do not duplicate parent-owned state inside a child component; use a single source of truth to prevent the parent and child values from diverging.
mode_rationale: Single principle about maintaining a single source of truth

rule: When a component conditionally renders nothing, ensure that its useEffect hooks do not continue triggering side effects like calling onChange handlers on the parent.
mode_rationale: Single convention about preventing side effects when component renders nothing

rule: Verify that React useEffect dependency arrays match the intended execution timing; including reactive values in the array means the effect re-runs on every change, not just on mount.
mode_rationale: Single guideline about verifying dependency arrays match intent

rule: When a data fetch or operation fails, show an error state to the user with a clear recovery action (e.g., refresh the page) rather than silently swallowing the failure.
mode_rationale: Single convention about showing error states with recovery actions

rule: Default or fallback values for component props (like labels) should be the responsibility of the consuming component, not embedded in shared utilities, to maintain separation of concerns.
mode_rationale: Single principle about separation of concerns for default values

rule: Code disabled behind a feature flag should be retained until the flag is fully removed, then cleaned up in a dedicated PR to avoid premature deletion.
mode_rationale: Single convention about when to clean up feature-flagged code

rule: When configuring lint ignore rules, ignore entire directories rather than listing individual files to reduce maintenance burden and avoid missing new files.
mode_rationale: Simple rule about lint ignore granularity

rule: Do not lint, format, or modify files that are not maintained by your team; ignore them in your tooling configuration instead.
mode_rationale: Single principle about not modifying other teams' files

rule: Shell scripts should prefer POSIX-compliant syntax (e.g., `.` instead of `source`, `[ ]` instead of `[[ ]]`) and pass shellcheck validation to ensure compatibility across environments.
mode_rationale: Single convention about shell script compatibility

rule: Always double-quote shell variable expansions to prevent word splitting and glob expansion bugs.
mode_rationale: Single rule about quoting variable expansions

rule: Use shell built-ins like `export -p` instead of manually parsing and reconstructing environment variables, as manual parsing almost always has edge cases.
mode_rationale: Single guideline about using builtins over manual parsing

rule: Disable service configuration options (like UsePAM in sshd) that are not actively needed to reduce attack surface and avoid environment-specific issues.
mode_rationale: Single security principle about reducing attack surface

rule: Avoid `@ts-ignore` and `as` type assertions as they suppress TypeScript's type checking and can mask real bugs that surface at runtime.
mode_rationale: Single rule about avoiding type suppression

rule: When adding code whose purpose is not self-evident (e.g., data remapping workarounds), leave a comment explaining why it exists so future developers can maintain or safely remove it.
mode_rationale: Single convention about explaining non-obvious code

rule: Use `<button>` elements for actions and `<a>` elements for navigation; do not use links styled as buttons, as it creates accessibility issues.
mode_rationale: Single accessibility rule about correct element usage

rule: Functions and helpers used only by tests must live in test files, not in production source code.
mode_rationale: Single convention about test helper placement

rule: Do not nest unrelated feature toggles inside each other's conditional logic; each feature's visibility should be controlled independently.
mode_rationale: Single principle about independent feature toggle control

rule: When building features that reference environment or infrastructure URLs, validate behavior against both local and remote data planes to avoid configuration-specific bugs.
mode_rationale: Single testing guideline about validating across environments

rule: Pin package dependencies to exact versions before merging to prevent unexpected breakages from version drift.
mode_rationale: Single convention about exact version pinning

rule: Use template literals instead of string concatenation when composing strings with dynamic values.
mode_rationale: Simple style rule about string composition

rule: Do not add extra wrapper DOM elements (e.g., divs) unless there is a clear layout or semantic reason; prefer styling the existing element.
mode_rationale: Single DOM hygiene rule

rule: When you suspect a component is unused, verify thoroughly and create a separate ticket for its removal rather than deleting it inline with an unrelated change.
mode_rationale: Single process convention about safe component removal

rule: Do not migrate code to an intermediate pattern that is itself scheduled for replacement; instead, use the simplest direct approach (e.g., pass values as arguments) to avoid compounding tech debt.
mode_rationale: Single principle about avoiding intermediate migration steps

rule: When executing multiple sequential service calls that must all succeed for a consistent state, handle partial failures gracefully and ensure the UI reflects the actual state (e.g., refetch data after errors).
mode_rationale: Single guideline about graceful partial failure handling

rule: Extract repeated logic within the same component into a shared helper or variable to reduce duplication and improve maintainability.
mode_rationale: Single DRY principle for component-level duplication

rule: When logic is used across multiple components or files, extract it into a shared utility module rather than defining it inline.
mode_rationale: Single convention about extracting shared logic

rule: Complex rendering logic should not be defined as ad-hoc functions inside table column definitions or parent components; extract it into its own component for maintainability and testability.
mode_rationale: Single principle about component extraction for complex render logic

rule: When two components have similar implementations, create a single reusable component that can be customized via props rather than maintaining parallel implementations.
mode_rationale: Single reusability guideline about merging similar components

rule: Use the current design system spacing API (spacings key) instead of deprecated alternatives (margins).
mode_rationale: Single rule about using current API instead of deprecated one

rule: Always reference colors from the theme object rather than hardcoding color values in styled components.
mode_rationale: Single styling convention about theme references

rule: Avoid nesting CSS class selectors inside styled components; prefer creating additional styled components to keep styles flat and composable.
mode_rationale: Single CSS architecture rule about flat styled components

rule: Define explicit typed classes for configuration and API data instead of using generic JSON objects, to enforce structure and catch missing fields at compile time.
mode_rationale: Single type safety convention

rule: Name classes and types to reflect their specific scope and domain context rather than using generic names.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention

rule: When a field may not always be present, use Option types (e.g., Option[String]) so that only explicitly set values need to be stored and checked.
mode_rationale: Single type design convention for optional fields

rule: When displaying data that may be undefined, provide an empty string or placeholder rather than rendering undefined or null.
mode_rationale: Single UI rendering convention about fallback values

rule: Never use the `any` type in TypeScript; use specific types or type aliases to maintain type safety.
mode_rationale: Single type safety rule

rule: When the same logic appears in multiple components, extract it into a shared custom hook to eliminate duplication and centralize behavior.
mode_rationale: Single DRY principle about shared custom hooks

rule: Do not add default values to data model fields when other fields in the model have none, as defaults can mask places where the field should be explicitly set.
mode_rationale: Single data modeling convention

rule: Do not manually override font properties (size, weight, line-height) that are already controlled by the design system's typography tokens.
mode_rationale: Single rule about not overriding design system typography

rule: Use design tokens according to their semantic purpose; do not use background color tokens for text color properties or vice versa.
mode_rationale: Single convention about matching token semantics to usage

rule: When replacing hardcoded colors with design tokens, verify the token's semantic meaning matches the intended use context (e.g., a background token should actually be used for backgrounds, not text).
mode_rationale: Single guideline about verifying token meaning when replacing hardcoded colors

rule: New variables and constants should follow the naming conventions already established in the file (e.g., capitalization, casing style) for consistency.
mode_rationale: Single consistency rule about matching existing file conventions

rule: Do not use optional chaining (?.) on a variable that has already been confirmed to be defined by a preceding conditional check (e.g., a ternary guard).
mode_rationale: Single code hygiene rule about unnecessary optional chaining

rule: If a hook returns only a static constant that never changes, import the constant directly instead of consuming it through a hook.
mode_rationale: Single performance convention about avoiding hooks for static values

rule: Do not pass or accept parameters in a function if they are not used within that function's body.
mode_rationale: Single code cleanliness rule

rule: In React hooks, prefer exposing an imperative function (e.g., save()) rather than a boolean flag pattern (e.g., setShouldSave(true)) that triggers side effects via useEffect.
mode_rationale: Single React API design convention about exposing functions over boolean flags

rule: When building a reusable component, make opinionated behaviors optional via props so the component can serve different use cases without forcing all consumers into one behavior.
mode_rationale: Single design principle about making component behaviors optional via props

rule: A component's callback functions should use the component's own props/context values rather than accepting overriding parameters, to prevent inconsistency between what the component displays and what it emits.
mode_rationale: Single rule about callback functions using component's own props

rule: Properties prefixed with an underscore (_) signal private/internal use; do not access underscore-prefixed properties from outside the owning class or module.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention about underscore-prefixed properties

rule: Use color tokens from the correct semantic category — text color tokens for text, background tokens for backgrounds, border tokens for borders — even if the hex values happen to match.
mode_rationale: Simple rule about matching token category to usage context

rule: Boolean properties should use names that clearly indicate a true/false value (e.g., isPlaceholder); a bare noun like 'placeholder' implies a string value.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for boolean properties

rule: Values that can be computed from existing state should be memoized (e.g., useMemo) rather than stored as separate state, to avoid synchronization bugs.
mode_rationale: Single rule about memoizing derived values instead of duplicating state

rule: Use color tokens from the design system palette instead of hardcoded color values; if a matching token doesn't exist, flag the discrepancy with UX rather than hardcoding.
mode_rationale: Simple rule to use design system color tokens over hardcoded values

rule: Move styled-component definitions into a dedicated file (e.g., styled-components.ts) rather than co-locating them in the component file.
mode_rationale: Simple file organization convention for styled-components

rule: Use the design system Typography component for text styling instead of setting font-weight, font-size, or other text properties manually.
mode_rationale: Simple rule to use Typography component instead of manual text styling

rule: Document public methods with scaladoc (or the language-appropriate doc comment format) rather than plain inline comments above the method.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation convention about using doc comments

rule: When refactoring reduces a method to a trivial delegation, inline it at the call site rather than keeping a thin wrapper that adds no value.
mode_rationale: Simple refactoring guideline about eliminating thin wrappers

rule: Name domain model fields to reflect business concepts rather than implementation details, especially for fields that may surface in APIs.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for domain model fields

rule: When wrapping a base library component (e.g., Ant Design), explicitly restrict the exposed prop types to only those the design system supports rather than passing through the full base type.
mode_rationale: Single rule about restricting exposed props on wrapper components

rule: When implementation colors don't match the design system palette, validate the discrepancy with UX/design before using non-palette values — it may be a Figma mistake.
mode_rationale: Simple process rule to check with UX on color discrepancies

rule: Extract complex boolean expressions into well-named intermediate variables or use pattern matching to make conditional logic self-documenting and easier to verify.
mode_rationale: Simple code clarity convention for complex conditionals

rule: When the same conditional logic appears in multiple places, extract it into a private helper function to avoid duplication and reduce the risk of the copies diverging.
mode_rationale: Simple DRY principle applied to conditional logic

rule: Define mock return values once per test setup; avoid overriding fixture-level mocks in individual test cases, as the override semantics can be surprising and fragile.
mode_rationale: Simple testing convention about mock setup location

rule: Use pattern matching (match/case) on enum or sealed-trait values instead of chained boolean conditions to make business rules explicit and exhaustively checked.
mode_rationale: Simple coding convention preferring pattern matching over boolean chains

rule: Use the design system's spacing keys/tokens instead of raw margin or padding values, especially when custom spacing utilities have been established.
mode_rationale: Simple rule to use spacing tokens over raw values

rule: Before creating inline styles or new styled components, check if an existing shared styled component already handles the same styling (e.g., shared StyledTag with margin).
mode_rationale: Simple rule to check for existing shared styled components before creating new ones

rule: Do not hardcode default values (e.g., severity levels) when the actual value should come from an API response; let the API be the source of truth.
mode_rationale: Simple rule about API as source of truth for default values

rule: Do not leave code in place for anticipated future features that are not yet enabled; remove it and add it back when the feature is ready.
mode_rationale: Simple convention against leaving code for unimplemented features

rule: When deprecating a feature (e.g., a theme), remove all associated CSS classes, modifiers, and conditional logic rather than leaving them as dead code.
mode_rationale: Simple rule about complete cleanup when deprecating features

rule: When the same logical condition is expressed in multiple related template or view files, use the same form of the expression to avoid confusion.
mode_rationale: Simple consistency rule for repeated boolean expressions

rule: When removing or modifying feature flags, verify that newly possible feature combinations (previously blocked by the removed flag) are tested.
mode_rationale: Simple testing reminder when modifying feature flags

rule: When accessing nested object properties via utility functions (e.g., lodash get or custom accessors), pass a path array instead of chaining manual property access.
mode_rationale: Simple convention about using path arrays for nested property access

rule: Shell-based plugins and scripts should declare and verify required tool availability before execution, rather than assuming tools like grep are present.
mode_rationale: Simple rule about verifying tool availability in shell scripts

rule: Follow established infrastructure conventions (e.g., client labels for network policies) to keep services loosely coupled, even when a more explicit approach seems correct for a specific case.
mode_rationale: Simple rule about adhering to existing infrastructure conventions

rule: Common/shared UI components should have descriptive, purpose-driven names rather than implementation-detail names like 'Styled' suffixes.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for shared UI components

rule: Use the design system's typography tokens instead of custom font sizes; consult UX if unsure which typography level to use.
mode_rationale: Simple rule to use typography tokens over custom font sizes

rule: Color, typography, and other visual changes should be verified with the UX team before merging.
mode_rationale: Simple process rule to verify visual changes with UX team

rule: Style components by composing styled-components and forwarding className to the root element, rather than duplicating components or passing style override props.
mode_rationale: Simple styling convention about composing via className forwarding

rule: Styled components should be defined in their own dedicated files, not co-located inline within component logic.
mode_rationale: Simple file organization convention for styled components

rule: Use existing utility functions like getAppBrandedText for app-branded text rather than manually interpolating app names.
mode_rationale: Simple rule to use existing branding utility functions

rule: Common UI components should use the dataTest prop convention (mapped internally) rather than data-testid for test selectors, to maintain consistency across the codebase.
mode_rationale: Simple convention about using dataTest prop over data-testid

rule: Use styled-components for conditional display/visibility logic rather than inline style manipulation.
mode_rationale: Simple rule preferring styled-components over inline style manipulation

rule: Before modifying a shared/common component, assess whether the change will cause behavior changes in all consumers, not just the immediate use case.
mode_rationale: Simple rule to assess consumer impact before modifying shared components

rule: Never suppress or skip useEffect dependency warnings; use a file-level variable or a ref to track mount status instead of an empty dependency array with missing deps.
mode_rationale: Simple rule about not suppressing useEffect dependency warnings

rule: Each React component file should contain only one component to maintain clear separation and discoverability.
mode_rationale: Simple file organization convention for React components

rule: Non-obvious workarounds and hacks must include inline comments explaining why they are necessary and how they work.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation rule for non-obvious workarounds

rule: General-purpose components should live at a shared/global level, not scoped inside a feature module; start local only if the component needs refinement or design review before promotion.
mode_rationale: Simple rule about where general-purpose components should live

rule: When a styled component exists, use it instead of applying inline style overrides or creating empty style wrappers with inline specializations.
mode_rationale: Simple consistency rule about using existing styled components

rule: Use positive logic for boolean permission variables (e.g., canCreate, canEdit) rather than negative logic (e.g., isDisabled, isOverLimit) to improve readability and reduce inversion errors.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention preferring positive boolean names

rule: Default boolean values for permission or limit checks should fail closed (restrict access) rather than fail open (allow access), so that errors don't silently grant unauthorized operations.
mode_rationale: Simple security convention about default permission values

rule: Color values should be defined in the centralized colors/theme list, not hardcoded or hidden in individual component files.
mode_rationale: Simple rule about defining colors in centralized theme files

rule: When replacing icons or changing icon labels, verify that no e2e tests rely on the former icon label to avoid breaking the test suite.
mode_rationale: Simple testing reminder when changing icon labels

rule: Aria labels must be written in human-readable form for screen readers (e.g., 'Create comment tab'), not as IDs or machine-readable strings; they exist for accessibility, not as test selectors.
mode_rationale: Simple accessibility convention for aria label formatting

rule: Use consistent types for ID fields across related parameters in the same domain (e.g., if other IDs use ObjectId, new IDs should too).
mode_rationale: Simple consistency rule for ID field types across a domain

rule: API endpoints should reflect the actual domain model relationships rather than creating shortcuts that flatten or skip entities, so the API remains easy to reason about as the domain evolves.
mode_rationale: Simple API design principle about reflecting domain relationships

rule: Create lightweight 'header' DTOs that return the minimal representation of an entity, so endpoints remain extensible without requiring new API calls or bloated full-entity fetches.
mode_rationale: Simple API design convention for minimal entity representations

rule: Service accounts should go through standard authorization checks (e.g., read access) rather than bypassing auth; add the service account to the authorizer if it lacks access.
mode_rationale: Single rule about how service accounts should be authorized

rule: Don't introduce traits or interfaces solely for API DTOs; traits are justified when decoupling dependencies on full domain or DB objects, not when types are only used at the API layer.
mode_rationale: Convention about when traits/interfaces are justified

rule: Use relative imports when importing from files within the same module or package to maintain encapsulation and avoid coupling to the project's absolute path structure.
mode_rationale: Simple import style convention

rule: All API error responses (including BadRequest) must return a JSON body, not plain text, to ensure clients can parse error details consistently.
mode_rationale: Single rule about error response format

rule: Analytics event properties should contain aggregatable, non-sensitive values (e.g., OS name) rather than raw URLs or host-specific data that hinders aggregation and risks leaking sensitive information.
mode_rationale: Convention about analytics event property values

rule: Component callback props like onClick should be marked optional if the component can function without them, rather than requiring callers to always provide a handler.
mode_rationale: Simple convention about prop optionality

rule: Use custom hooks close to where cross-cutting logic is needed instead of prop-drilling event handlers through multiple component layers that don't need to know about them.
mode_rationale: Architectural preference for hooks vs prop-drilling

rule: Define analytics events as a single global event name (e.g., HelpLinkClick) with distinguishing properties (e.g., location, article) rather than feature-prefixed event names (e.g., SshInstructionsHelpClick), so events are comparable across features.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for analytics events

rule: When defining a props type for a React component, name it after the component (e.g., ConnectionInstructionsProps for ConnectionInstructions) rather than reusing or extending an unrelated type name.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for React props types

rule: Before adding an analytics tracking call, verify the event is not already being tracked by another handler or component to avoid duplicate event firing.
mode_rationale: Simple check-before-adding convention

rule: Track tab navigation as a single generic event with a property indicating which tab was selected, rather than creating separate event names per tab.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for tab navigation events

rule: Organize analytics event type definitions by feature domain (e.g., environments) rather than by UI pattern (e.g., tabs), so related events are colocated and discoverable.
mode_rationale: File organization convention for analytics types

rule: Do not duplicate the same data across multiple React state variables; if a single state object (e.g., editedLauncher) already tracks the full form state, derive values from it rather than maintaining separate parallel state.
mode_rationale: Convention against duplicating state

rule: When a component receives multiple related props (e.g., different volume mount types), verify each is actually used and checked before rendering — unused or unchecked props indicate a bug.
mode_rationale: Simple verification convention for props usage

rule: Add a comment explaining tuple field meanings when the types alone do not make the values obvious (e.g., tuples of ID and name).
mode_rationale: Documentation convention for tuple fields

rule: When generating certificates for Kubernetes services, include the short hostname, namespace-qualified name, and full FQDN as principals to ensure resolution works across all contexts.
mode_rationale: Single rule about K8s certificate principals

rule: Create test factories for commonly constructed test data (e.g., execution headers) to avoid modifying tests across many files when the shared structure changes.
mode_rationale: Convention about test data construction

rule: When the same fallback or default-resolution logic (e.g., falling back to local data plane when an ID maps to None) is duplicated across multiple call sites, centralize it in a dedicated service method.
mode_rationale: Convention against duplicated fallback logic

rule: When adding a new authorized action or permission check, add a corresponding test case in the authorizer spec.
mode_rationale: Simple rule to add tests when adding permissions

rule: When configuration (e.g., env vars) is conditionally included, write both a positive test (present when condition is met) and a negative test (absent when condition is not met).
mode_rationale: Convention about testing both branches of conditional config

rule: Utility and validation functions (especially those handling user input like CIDR parsing) must have unit tests.
mode_rationale: Simple rule that utility functions need tests

rule: In a monorepo, shared third-party packages used by a UI library should be declared as peer dependencies and installed at the root, not as direct dependencies of the library package.
mode_rationale: Dependency declaration convention for monorepos

rule: React hooks that fetch data and set state on error must handle failure states carefully to avoid triggering infinite re-render loops.
mode_rationale: Convention about careful state handling in hooks

rule: When a function's return type changes to include undefined (or becomes nullable), check all consumers to ensure they handle the new possible value.
mode_rationale: Convention to check consumers when nullability changes

rule: Use the team's established domain vocabulary when naming fields and events — prefer the term stakeholders already use over inventing new ones.
mode_rationale: Naming convention to use existing team vocabulary

rule: Telemetry events should include relevant entity identifiers (e.g., executionId) so events can be correlated and traced back to specific operations.
mode_rationale: Convention about telemetry event content

rule: Only Domino-specific entities (Workspace, Model API, Artifacts, etc.) should be capitalized in UI copy; generic terms should use sentence case.
mode_rationale: UI copy capitalization convention

rule: Extract complex UI logic (e.g., floating controls with action handlers) into dedicated components using composition, keeping base implementations reusable and testable.
mode_rationale: Convention about component extraction for reusability

rule: Avoid state synchronization patterns (e.g., useEffect syncing one state to another); prefer a single source of truth for derived or shared state.
mode_rationale: Convention against state synchronization patterns

rule: When a feature is gated behind a feature flag, consider what happens to persisted state if the flag is later turned off — values may become locked and invisible to the user.
mode_rationale: Convention to consider persisted state when flags change

rule: A help/question-mark icon should itself be the clickable link to documentation, not a hover trigger that reveals a separate link — reduce interaction steps.
mode_rationale: Simple UX convention about help icon interaction

rule: Use configured path aliases (e.g., `ui/govern`) instead of deep relative import paths (`../../../..`) to improve readability and reduce fragility.
mode_rationale: Import style convention

rule: A method's return type should reflect its actual behavior — if it doesn't modify and return the object, return Unit instead of the object type.
mode_rationale: Convention about return type accuracy

rule: Use styled-components for styling and the Typography component for text formatting — do not set styles like fontWeight inline or manually.
mode_rationale: Styling convention for the codebase

rule: Type render function arguments using specific DTO field types (e.g., `DominoDataplaneDataPlaneDto['isLocked']`) instead of `any`.
mode_rationale: Type safety convention against using any

rule: Use IconResolver instead of deprecated antd icons, and use themeHelper spacing keys instead of hardcoded margin values in styled-components.
mode_rationale: Convention to use project-specific icon and spacing utilities

rule: Use the IconResolver component instead of Ant Design icon imports, which are deprecated.
mode_rationale: Convention to use IconResolver over deprecated imports

rule: Use the `spacings` design token key for margins and paddings; the `margins` key is deprecated.
mode_rationale: Convention to use current design token key

rule: Use the async `safeGetErrorMessage` utility to extract error messages instead of manual error handling, as it handles extraction failures internally.
mode_rationale: Convention to use existing error utility

rule: Use the `ActionDropdown.Button` component for dropdown actions on table rows instead of building custom dropdown triggers.
mode_rationale: Convention to use existing dropdown component

rule: Use the modal component's default body styling instead of adding custom padding or spacing overrides.
mode_rationale: Convention against overriding modal defaults

rule: Use the `ConfirmationModal` component for confirmation dialogs with warning boxes instead of building custom modal layouts with warning elements.
mode_rationale: Convention to use existing confirmation component

rule: Use a component's built-in size props (e.g., `iconSize="small"`) instead of manually overriding dimensions with CSS or style props.
mode_rationale: Convention to use built-in size props

rule: Do not add explicit return values or unnecessary expressions at the end of Scala methods that return `Unit`.
mode_rationale: Scala style convention for Unit methods

rule: Utility functions and hooks with non-trivial logic should have unit tests, even if added in a follow-up ticket.
mode_rationale: Simple rule that utility functions need tests

rule: Use styled-components for styling rather than applying CSS class strings directly; classes should not be used solely for styling purposes.
mode_rationale: Styling convention against CSS class strings

rule: Before broadly adopting react-query, establish a centralized pattern for defining query keys that accounts for varying parameters, provider placement across Scala and React pages, and caching scope.
mode_rationale: Multi-step planning procedure for react-query adoption involving key strategy, provider placement, and caching scope decisions

rule: Extract data mapping and transformation logic from components into dedicated utility files.
mode_rationale: Code organization convention for data transformations

rule: When a render function has enough structure and length, extract it into its own standalone component.
mode_rationale: Convention about component extraction from render functions

rule: Avoid letting context providers grow too large; split oversized providers into smaller, focused ones.
mode_rationale: Convention about context provider size

rule: Use the Typography component for rendering text instead of manually setting font-size and font-weight in styled-components.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to use a specific component for text rendering

rule: When casting a string to a numeric type, verify and document the guarantee that the string will always be a valid numeric value.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence guideline about verifying type safety on casts

rule: When service-specific configuration closely mirrors a shared library's config structure, merge it into the common library to avoid duplication and drift.
mode_rationale: Architectural guideline to merge duplicated config into shared libraries

rule: Extract complex channel or subscription initialization (e.g., basicConsume) into a separate dedicated method rather than inlining it in the subscription setup.
mode_rationale: Code organization convention to extract complex logic into dedicated methods

rule: Use the classnames library for conditional CSS class composition in React components, and forward className props to allow styled-component wrapping.
mode_rationale: Simple convention about using classnames library and forwarding className props

rule: When modifying or replacing DOM elements in shared components, run the e2e test suite and perform a visual check to catch regressions in downstream consumers.
mode_rationale: Multi-step procedure: run e2e suite then perform visual check when modifying shared DOM elements

rule: Before creating a new UI component implementation, check if an existing one can be extended with the needed behavior to avoid maintaining multiple near-identical components.
mode_rationale: Simple guideline to check for extensible existing components before creating new ones

rule: Use the shared NOT_AVAILABLE_PLACEHOLDER constant ("--") for empty or not-applicable table cells instead of leaving them blank.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to use a shared constant for empty table cells

rule: Always wrap single-line if-statement bodies in curly braces; do not resolve the if statement on the same line.
mode_rationale: Basic style rule about brace usage in if statements

rule: Place resource identifiers like projectId in the URL path rather than the request body to follow RESTful API conventions.
mode_rationale: RESTful convention about placing identifiers in URL paths

rule: When a required resource identifier is missing or invalid, return an appropriate 4xx HTTP status code (400 or 404) instead of allowing a 500 internal server error.
mode_rationale: Simple guideline about returning correct HTTP status codes

rule: Never obscure secret values with placeholder strings in API responses; instead, use separate response types that omit the secret field entirely, and split retrieval into metadata-only and secret-value methods to minimize secret exposure.
mode_rationale: Security convention about using separate response types to avoid exposing secrets

rule: Return 404 (not 400) when a referenced resource does not exist, even on POST endpoints, because it more precisely describes the problem than a generic bad request.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to return 404 instead of 400 for missing resources

rule: Keep API response DTO structures consistent across similar endpoints, even if slightly verbose, so frontend consumers can reuse TypeScript model definitions.
mode_rationale: Consistency guideline for API response shapes across endpoints

rule: Never send secrets in plain text over the network in execution payloads; use vault keys or secret references instead.
mode_rationale: Security convention to use vault references instead of plaintext secrets

rule: Keep execution headers and transport objects as lean as possible — remove redundant fields and avoid duplicating data already available through other fields.
mode_rationale: Simple guideline to keep transport objects minimal and avoid duplication

rule: Verify a resource exists before attempting deletion and return an appropriate error if it does not, rather than silently succeeding or throwing an unexpected error.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to verify resource existence before deletion

rule: Do not import from a package's published name within the package itself; use relative imports to avoid circular dependencies and bundling issues.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to use relative imports within a package

rule: Handle display-level data masking (e.g., obscuring secret values with placeholder strings) in the frontend rather than the backend, to avoid confusing real values with mask sentinels and to keep the API response contract clean.
mode_rationale: Architectural convention about where to handle display-level masking

rule: Do not catch broad exception types (e.g., NonFatal) and map them all to a single HTTP status code; distinguish client errors (400) from server errors (500) by handling specific exception types.
mode_rationale: Error handling convention to map specific exceptions to appropriate status codes

rule: Map the same exception types to the same HTTP status codes across all endpoints in a controller to avoid inconsistent behavior and confusing debugging.
mode_rationale: Consistency guideline for exception-to-status-code mapping across endpoints

rule: Error messages should include domain-specific context (e.g., entity type, identifier) so that engineers debugging production issues can quickly identify which resource and operation failed.
When processing an Optional value with a side effect, prefer forEach over getOrElse to eliminate the Option wrapper and produce cleaner, more idiomatic code.
mode_rationale: Idiomatic Scala style guideline for processing Optional values with side effects

rule: When a function or interface only needs a single field from a larger object, pass just that field rather than the entire object to reduce coupling and clarify dependencies.
mode_rationale: Simple coupling-reduction guideline about passing only needed fields

rule: When adding or modifying methods on an interface, use the same parameter types and ordering as existing methods on that interface to maintain consistency and reduce cognitive load.
mode_rationale: Consistency convention for parameter types and ordering on interfaces

rule: Never store secret values directly in Kubernetes V1EnvVar.value fields; use valueFrom with a SecretKeyRef to reference K8s secrets so that sensitive data is not exposed in pod specs.
mode_rationale: Security convention about using SecretKeyRef instead of plain values in K8s specs

rule: Plugin interface methods should return focused, single-purpose types (e.g., Seq[V1EnvVar] from getEnvironmentVariables) rather than mixed types, so the interface remains extensible for future plugins.
mode_rationale: Architecture guideline about plugin return types being single-purpose

rule: Keep domain-specific logic (e.g., secret-to-env-var conversion) encapsulated inside the plugin rather than leaking it to the orchestrator, so callers don't need to know internal details.
mode_rationale: Encapsulation convention to keep domain logic inside the plugin

rule: When an existing field already holds the data you need, override or delegate to it rather than adding a redundant parameter that duplicates information.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to override existing fields rather than adding redundant parameters

rule: Keep pull requests focused by reverting changes that are not directly related to the PR's purpose, even if they are minor or seemingly harmless.
mode_rationale: PR hygiene convention about keeping changes focused

rule: When a value is Optional/None, omit it entirely rather than setting it to an empty string; use .map/.flatten to conditionally include only present values.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to omit None values rather than defaulting to empty strings

rule: When performing destructive filesystem operations (rm -rf, mv), validate path inputs against edge cases like empty strings, root path '/', and restricted directories.
mode_rationale: Security convention to validate paths before destructive filesystem operations

rule: After destructive filesystem operations (rm -rf, mv), log which items were actually affected to aid debugging, since these operations often succeed silently even on nonexistent targets.
mode_rationale: Logging convention to record what was affected by destructive operations

rule: When code correctness depends on the execution order of steps (e.g., items added to a script), add tests to verify the ordering is maintained.
mode_rationale: Testing convention to verify execution order when correctness depends on it

rule: Error handling behavior (which exceptions are logged, suppressed, or surfaced) should be consistent across all modules that serve the same role, even when they live in different packages.
mode_rationale: Consistency guideline for error handling behavior across same-role modules

rule: Raise specific, named exception types rather than generic ones so callers can catch and handle them precisely.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to use named exception types instead of generic ones

rule: When adding cross-package dependencies, evaluate whether the import could create a circular dependency, especially when the consuming package will later need to reference the provider.
mode_rationale: Architectural awareness guideline about cross-package dependency risks

rule: In API interfaces and documentation, use the most specific domain term available (e.g., 'registered model' not 'model') to avoid ambiguity when a term is overloaded in the codebase.
mode_rationale: Naming convention to use specific domain terms to avoid ambiguity

rule: Return structured data (maps, lists) from APIs instead of delimiter-separated strings, so consumers can format and process the data without parsing.
mode_rationale: API design convention to return structured data instead of delimited strings

rule: When adding a new service or entity that shares a name with an existing package or concept, use a disambiguated name (e.g., prefix with the domain) to prevent confusion.
mode_rationale: Naming convention to prefix with domain when names collide with existing packages

rule: When a user has permission to view a list of resources, the UI should either grant access to resource details or visually indicate (e.g., grey out links) that detail access requires a separate permission — never show clickable links that lead to blank or forbidden pages.
mode_rationale: UI convention about consistent permission-gated link behavior

rule: Integration tests for API endpoints should assert on the actual response body content, not just HTTP status codes or response metadata, to catch subtle data-handling bugs.
mode_rationale: Testing convention to assert on response body content in integration tests

rule: When adding new optional fields to persisted data models (e.g., BSON/JSON documents), add a test that verifies successful deserialization of existing records that include the new field to protect against future breakage.
mode_rationale: Testing convention to add deserialization tests when adding optional fields to persisted models

rule: Before moving or removing a shared action, constant, or interface, confirm that no other consumers depend on it to avoid breaking unrelated features.
mode_rationale: Simple guideline to check for dependents before moving shared code

rule: In audit trails and event logs, store only the immutable identifier (e.g., userId) rather than mutable display values (e.g., userName) to avoid stale data; do not pass unused fields as they confuse future readers.
mode_rationale: Logging convention to store immutable identifiers in audit trails

rule: Audit or telemetry event schemas should be scoped to the correct domain entity — do not extend an event for one concept (e.g., BundlePolicy) with fields belonging to a different concept (e.g., Bundle state) even if they are related.
mode_rationale: Architecture convention about keeping event schemas scoped to their domain entity

rule: Use the established product terminology consistently in code identifiers — if the product calls something 'Apps', do not use 'Applications' in code names, as inconsistent naming creates confusion.
mode_rationale: Naming convention to match code identifiers to established product terminology

rule: Avoid using the `return` keyword in Scala; it is a major code smell and should be reworked to use expression-based control flow instead.
mode_rationale: Scala style rule to avoid return keyword in favor of expression-based control flow

rule: Do not write comments that merely restate what the code already clearly says; remove extraneous comments that add no information beyond the code itself.
mode_rationale: Documentation convention to remove comments that restate what the code says

rule: Combine related happy-path tests into a single test that exercises both the positive and negative cases together, verifying correct behavior is applied to the right entities and not applied to others.
mode_rationale: Testing convention to verify positive and negative cases together in one test

rule: Do not instantiate expensive resources like ActorSystem directly in class bodies; inject them via dependency injection to avoid cumulative latency in test suites that can cause hard-to-diagnose timeout failures.
mode_rationale: Single convention: inject expensive resources rather than instantiating them directly

rule: Validate inputs and throw errors early, before acquiring expensive resources (e.g., Kubernetes pods), to avoid wasting resources on operations that will inevitably fail later.
mode_rationale: Single principle: validate inputs before acquiring expensive resources

rule: Use `Option.when(condition)(value)` instead of if/else with Some/None for conditional Option construction in Scala; it is more concise and expressive.
mode_rationale: Single Scala style preference for Option construction

rule: Maintain consistent 2-space indentation in Scala files; fix spacing issues immediately rather than letting them propagate through the codebase.
mode_rationale: Simple formatting convention about indentation

rule: Place API response fields at the abstraction level where the underlying data is stored; if data belongs to a version-level entity, expose it on the version response rather than the parent entity.
mode_rationale: Single design principle about response field placement

rule: When adding dependencies on external resources (secrets, services, configs), verify the system behaves gracefully when the dependency is absent, not just when it is present.
mode_rationale: Single testing principle: verify behavior when dependencies are absent

rule: Prefer existing library constructs or standard language utilities for string parsing and URL splitting over manual implementations, which reduces the need for additional unit tests.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use library utilities instead of manual implementations

rule: Do not initialize optional parameters to None by default unless there is a clear reason; require callers to pass values explicitly to improve clarity.
mode_rationale: Single convention about parameter defaults

rule: Ensure the declared package in a source file matches its directory path to avoid confusing tooling and developers.
mode_rationale: Simple rule: package must match directory path

rule: When a function or comparison operates on values of varying types, define explicit type parameters or type bounds rather than leaving them untyped.
mode_rationale: Single typing convention for generic parameters

rule: OpenAPI schema fields should include description, nullable annotation, and regex validation patterns for ID fields to ensure clear contracts and input validation.
mode_rationale: Single convention about OpenAPI schema field annotations

rule: When building a Map from optional values, prefer Map(option.map(key -> _), ...).flatten over imperative if/else conditional insertion.
mode_rationale: Single Scala style preference for Map construction from optionals

rule: Remove unused imports, bindings, and dead code rather than leaving them in place; unused code adds confusion and maintenance burden.
mode_rationale: Simple hygiene rule: remove dead code

rule: Before performing a state-changing operation (e.g., cleanup, cancellation), assert that the entity is in a valid state for that transition to avoid corrupting data.
mode_rationale: Single principle: assert valid state before transitions

rule: Error messages for uniqueness and constraint violations should include relevant domain identifiers (e.g., data plane ID) to aid debugging and distinguish scoped constraints.
mode_rationale: Single convention about error message content

rule: Even when transitive dependencies provide the required libraries, declare direct dependencies explicitly in BUILD files so the dependency graph is accurate and resilient to upstream changes.
mode_rationale: Single build convention: declare direct dependencies explicitly

rule: In BUILD files where some source files need different targets, prefer explicit file lists over glob() patterns to prevent new files from silently landing in the wrong target.
mode_rationale: Single build convention: prefer explicit lists over globs

rule: Use domain-specific type wrappers (e.g., DominoId, ModelApiId) instead of raw String for identifiers to communicate intent and catch type mismatches at compile time.
mode_rationale: Single typing convention: wrap primitives in domain types
