rule: Use Scala for-comprehensions with filter/yield to combine collections instead of manual nested matching with headOption.
mode_rationale: Single Scala style preference for collection operations

rule: Specify method parameters as optional with sensible defaults rather than requiring callers to always provide a value.
mode_rationale: Single API design convention about parameter defaults

rule: Use java.time.Instant for new code instead of legacy DateTime types; only use legacy DateTime when maintaining consistency within an existing file that already uses it.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use modern time API for new code

rule: Define enum types for status values and reference them in code instead of comparing against raw string literals.
mode_rationale: Single convention: define enums for status values

rule: Reference defined enum values rather than checking string literals to ensure type safety and catch errors at compile time.
mode_rationale: Single convention: reference enum values not string literals

rule: Do not call toString on strongly-typed identifiers; use the appropriate accessor or conversion method to preserve type safety.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use proper accessors on typed IDs

rule: Use Set equality (converting both sides to Sets) instead of verbose manual key-by-key comparison when checking if two collections contain the same elements.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use Set equality for collection comparison

rule: Use explicit domain types (e.g., Seq[DominoId]) for collections of identifiers rather than leaving them untyped or using generic types.
mode_rationale: Single typing convention for ID collections

rule: When constructing domain objects, use existing conversion utilities (e.g., User-to-Origin converters) rather than manually mapping fields, which is error-prone.
mode_rationale: Single convention: reuse existing converters instead of manual mapping

rule: When sending notifications or making API calls for multiple targets, combine them into a single request with a list of targets rather than looping and firing individual requests.
mode_rationale: Single performance principle: batch requests instead of looping

rule: Eliminate unnecessary Some() wrappers that force downstream .get calls on Options; restructure code so the Option is handled cleanly without calling .get, which can throw at runtime.
mode_rationale: Single convention: handle Options cleanly without .get

rule: API calls made in Scala templates (Twirl) should be wrapped in try/catch to safely handle errors; prefer moving API calls to React component hooks instead of calling APIs in Twirl and passing props.
mode_rationale: Single convention about error handling in Twirl templates

rule: When detwirling Scala templates, move data-fetching logic into dedicated API endpoints (e.g. v4 routes) so React components can own their own data fetching and default state, reducing coupling to Scala templates.
mode_rationale: Multi-step migration workflow: create API endpoints, move data-fetching logic, update React components to own data

rule: When business logic operates on mixed entity types (e.g. users and orgs) but downstream processing only needs one type, flatten to the common type (e.g. resolve orgs to users) as early as possible to simplify all downstream logic.
mode_rationale: Single principle: resolve mixed types to common type early

rule: When a feature flag controls infrastructure behavior (e.g., creating an ingress), capture the flag value into the resource record at creation time and use that persisted value for all subsequent operations, rather than re-reading the current flag value.
mode_rationale: Single principle: persist flag value at creation time

rule: When a feature flag controls infrastructure that persists beyond the flag flip (e.g., ingress/auth configuration), ensure that operations like changing visibility or access mode check the actual deployed infrastructure state, not the current flag value.
mode_rationale: Single principle: check deployed infrastructure state, not current flag

rule: When runtime behavior depends on infrastructure configured at instance creation, check the instance's persisted spec (e.g., ingressSpec) rather than the current feature flag value, since the flag may have changed after the instance was created.
mode_rationale: Single principle: use persisted instance spec for runtime decisions

rule: Avoid adding dependencies on the `server` module from other modules; use narrower service interfaces (e.g., UserStateService) instead of reaching into server internals (e.g., UserPreferencesPersister).
mode_rationale: Single architecture principle: use narrow interfaces over server internals

rule: When grouping items by a boolean property (e.g., pinned vs unpinned) while preserving existing sort order, use sequence partitioning instead of re-sorting to guarantee stable ordering within each group.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use partition for boolean grouping with stable order

rule: When adding client-side sorting or grouping (e.g., pinning) on top of server-side paginated results, verify that the feature works correctly across page boundaries — items may not appear on the expected page if pagination was applied before reordering.
mode_rationale: Single principle: verify reordering works across page boundaries

rule: When the same data is needed at multiple layers (e.g., controller and service), fetch it once and pass it as a parameter rather than making redundant database calls, both for performance and to avoid inconsistency if the data changes between calls.
mode_rationale: Single principle: fetch once and pass rather than re-fetching

rule: When implementing custom sorting or ordering logic (e.g., pinning, prioritization), write focused unit tests that verify the ordering behavior; use package-private visibility annotations to enable direct testing of internal sort functions.
mode_rationale: Single testing convention: test custom ordering with focused unit tests

rule: When adding a new API endpoint call, extend or refactor an existing client class with compatible responsibilities rather than creating a new class responsible for a single endpoint; if reuse is not feasible, document why in a docstring.
mode_rationale: Single convention: extend existing clients rather than creating new single-endpoint ones

rule: When a client class accumulates many injected dependencies for different concerns (e.g., JWT signing, config management, API calls), refactor it into a minimal API client plus dedicated helper classes (e.g., a separate JWT signer) so the client can be reused across contexts without pulling in unnecessary dependencies.
mode_rationale: Multi-step refactoring workflow: identify concerns, extract helpers, slim down the client

rule: When deviating from the expected pattern (e.g., not reusing an existing client), add a docstring explaining the rationale so future readers understand why the seemingly redundant code exists.
mode_rationale: Single convention: add docstrings explaining deviations from expected patterns

rule: Do not declare constructor or method parameters that are always passed a fixed or unused value; remove them to reduce API surface.
mode_rationale: Single hygiene rule: remove parameters that are always fixed or unused

rule: Prefer failing deserialization when a required field is missing rather than silently providing a default value that hides data issues.
mode_rationale: Single convention: fail on missing required fields rather than defaulting

rule: Mapping functions that transform between domain types should have simple unit tests to verify the correctness of the mapping.
mode_rationale: Single testing convention: write unit tests for type mapping functions

rule: When querying metrics or data for a specific entity version, filter by the exact entity identifier rather than an alias like 'latest' that may include data from other versions.
mode_rationale: Single principle: filter by exact entity ID, not aliases like 'latest'

rule: When filtering infrastructure metrics, match against backend service identifiers rather than user-facing routes which may be ambiguous or aliased.
mode_rationale: Single principle: match backend identifiers, not user-facing routes

rule: Pass domain-specific types (e.g., DominoId) through the call chain and only convert to primitive types (e.g., String) at the boundary where they are consumed.
mode_rationale: Single convention: pass domain types through call chain, convert at boundaries

rule: Prefer flat tuple-based pattern matching over nested match/case blocks to improve readability and reduce indentation.
mode_rationale: Single Scala style preference: flat tuple matching over nested match blocks

rule: Ensure docstrings accurately describe what the method returns; do not copy docstrings from similar methods without updating the return type description.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence documentation convention about keeping docstrings accurate

rule: Methods in Authorizer classes should return a proper AuthorizationResult type rather than a boolean or other ad-hoc type to maintain consistent authorization patterns.
mode_rationale: Simple type convention for authorization method return types

rule: When allowing users to supply their own credential or token values, validate format, length, and entropy requirements to prevent weak or inappropriate secrets.
mode_rationale: Single rule about validating credential inputs at the boundary

rule: When allowing users to define or reuse tokens across resources, evaluate whether sharing a token could grant unintended cross-resource access to collaborators on only one of those resources.
mode_rationale: Security consideration to evaluate during token design, not a multi-step procedure

rule: User-supplied secrets and tokens should be validated against a minimum entropy threshold to prevent trivially guessable values.
mode_rationale: Single validation rule for user-supplied secrets

rule: Always include a fallback/wildcard case in pattern match expressions that throws an IllegalArgumentException or similar, so unrecognized values fail fast with a clear error instead of silently falling through.
mode_rationale: Simple coding convention to always include a fallback case

rule: Extract repeated test object instantiation (builders, services, etc.) into a shared test Fixture to avoid code duplication across test cases.
mode_rationale: Single refactoring guideline for test code organization

rule: When a domain-specific exception is needed, throw it at the origin point rather than catching a generic exception and re-wrapping it upstream.
mode_rationale: Simple exception-throwing convention

rule: Service layer code should use storage-agnostic terminology (e.g., 'entity', 'record') rather than database-specific terms like 'document' or 'row', since the service layer should not know about the underlying storage implementation.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for service layer abstraction

rule: For upsert operations, rely on the database's upsert return value to determine if a record was created or updated, rather than performing a separate existence check followed by an insert/update — the latter introduces race conditions under concurrency.
mode_rationale: Single rule preferring upsert return value over check-then-act

rule: When an optimization is valid but diverges from the established pattern in the codebase, prefer following the existing convention for consistency unless the performance gain is significant.
mode_rationale: Simple consistency guideline for code style decisions

rule: Name result types with enough domain context that they are meaningful when imported in other files — e.g., use 'UpsertThumbnailResult' instead of generic 'UpsertResult'.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for result types

rule: Place case classes at the top level of the file or in a dedicated model file rather than nesting them inside companion objects, which forces confusing self-imports.
mode_rationale: Simple code organization preference for case class placement

rule: Define constants (like size limits or configuration values) as vals on the class/controller rather than as local variables inside individual functions.
mode_rationale: Simple convention for where to define constants

rule: Ensure method/class documentation accurately describes what the code actually does — if validation happens in the controller, the service Scaladoc should not claim the service performs it.
mode_rationale: Documentation accuracy convention

rule: Don't add fields (like createdAt) that serve no functional purpose and complicate the implementation — if the data is only useful for debugging, rely on logs or audit trails instead.
mode_rationale: Design guideline to avoid unnecessary fields

rule: After refactoring, verify that no dead code, unused imports, or orphaned test dependencies remain — these are easy to miss and create confusion for future readers.
mode_rationale: Simple hygiene rule after refactoring

rule: Keep default parameter values consistent across similar method signatures in the same class or module to avoid confusing callers.
mode_rationale: Simple API consistency convention

rule: Store binary data (images, bundles, files) in blob storage rather than directly in MongoDB, using the GlobalBlobStore interface which supports all deployment types.
mode_rationale: Single architectural rule for binary data storage

rule: When implementing protocol-level behavior (HTTP caching, conditional requests, etc.), link to the relevant RFC or specification in code comments and add a docstring summarizing the behavior.
mode_rationale: Documentation convention to reference specs in comments

rule: When adding non-standard implementations or workarounds, reference the tracking ticket (DOM number) in code comments so future readers understand the context and can trace the decision.
mode_rationale: Documentation convention to reference tickets in workarounds

rule: Include all relevant entity IDs (e.g., snapshotId, runId) in audit trail events to enable full traceability, adding them to metadata if they are not universally applicable.
mode_rationale: Simple logging convention for audit trail completeness

rule: Use the standard getValidPagination utility from PublicApiUtils for pagination instead of hardcoding offset and limit values in API implementations.
mode_rationale: Single rule to use existing pagination utility

rule: Fix data contract issues (e.g., required vs optional fields) at the API client or proxy layer rather than adding workarounds in the service layer to cast or transform invalid data.
mode_rationale: Architectural guideline about where to fix data contracts

rule: Use the RollbackStack mechanism from domino-common-library for multi-step operations that require cleanup on failure, rather than writing ad-hoc try/catch rollback logic.
mode_rationale: Single rule to use existing RollbackStack utility rather than ad-hoc logic

rule: Rollback and cleanup logic must be conditional on the operation type — only delete a resource during rollback if the current operation created it, to avoid accidentally deleting pre-existing resources during an update failure.
mode_rationale: Single conditional rule for rollback behavior

rule: System tags and internal metadata should be modified using superuser/service credentials, not user credentials, and scoped so regular users cannot override them.
mode_rationale: Security convention about credential scoping for system tags

rule: When handling multiple authentication or configuration types, match on the type discriminator field rather than checking which optional fields are present.
mode_rationale: Simple coding convention for type dispatch

rule: Do not use invalid placeholder defaults for required configuration values like credentials; throw an explicit exception stating which value is missing.
mode_rationale: Error handling convention for missing config

rule: When inline logic becomes hard to follow, extract it into a well-named method with scaladoc or doc comments explaining the business logic.
mode_rationale: Simple readability guideline about extracting complex logic

rule: When adding documentation comments, use the same style (e.g., scaladoc) as other files in the package for consistency.
mode_rationale: Documentation consistency convention

rule: When wrapping errors from external APIs, include the original response body in the exception message to aid future debugging if the upstream API changes.
mode_rationale: Single error handling convention for external API responses

rule: Always set explicit timeout values (connection, socket, etc.) and configure retries for external HTTP clients to prevent requests from hanging indefinitely.
mode_rationale: Single configuration rule for HTTP clients

rule: When transforming identifiers by replacing characters (e.g., replacing '/' with '--'), analyze whether distinct inputs can produce the same output and handle or document the collision risk.
mode_rationale: Design consideration when transforming identifiers

rule: When a feature flag guards creation of a resource, ensure the same flag is also applied to related endpoints (listing, configuration) to prevent inconsistent behavior.
mode_rationale: Single consistency rule for feature flag application

rule: Use proper URI/URL parsing libraries instead of string manipulation when handling URLs, as scheme-less or unconventional URL formats can be silently malformed by naive string operations.
mode_rationale: Simple coding convention for URL handling

rule: When renaming or changing enum/type discriminator values stored in a database, verify that no existing records use the old value to avoid breaking deserialization.
mode_rationale: Single verification rule for enum changes

rule: Avoid listing implementation details (method names, endpoints, etc.) in design documents when those details are likely to change, as it creates an additional maintenance burden.
mode_rationale: Documentation convention to avoid listing volatile details

rule: In Scala, prefer `match` with exhaustive cases over nested `if`/`for-comprehension` chains for authorization and type-dispatching logic, as it improves readability and ensures exhaustiveness.
mode_rationale: Scala style convention for control flow

rule: Replace deprecated `javax.xml.bind` (and other javax packages) with their `jakarta` equivalents to stay current with the Jakarta EE migration.
mode_rationale: Single migration convention for package imports

rule: Internal service dependencies should not be exposed as public vals on a class; instead, inject them directly into the class that actually uses them to preserve encapsulation.
mode_rationale: Encapsulation convention for dependency injection

rule: Use idiomatic Option combinators like `.map().orElse()` instead of wrapping with `Some(.map().getOrElse())` for cleaner and more readable code.
mode_rationale: Scala idiom preference for Option handling

rule: When adding new parameters to a service method, extend the existing method with default-valued parameters instead of creating a new method alongside it, to avoid API clutter and confusion for future developers.
mode_rationale: API design convention to avoid method proliferation

rule: When copying code or comments from one location to another, update all references and descriptions to match the new context to avoid misleading future readers.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation hygiene rule

rule: When adding a new field to a domain entity, ensure it is also added to all related models that derive from or are created from that entity (e.g., adding a field to Run also requires adding it to AppVersion).
mode_rationale: Single consistency rule for domain model changes

rule: When test code contains highly duplicated setup or assertion blocks, refactor them into shared helper methods to reduce noise and improve maintainability.
mode_rationale: Simple test code organization guideline

rule: When using role-based queries like fetchAdmins, verify the scope covers all relevant roles across deployment environments (e.g., SysAdmins vs CloudAdmins in cloud vs on-prem).
mode_rationale: Security consideration for role-based queries

rule: When refactoring service methods that no longer accept or validate a principal, verify that authorization is enforced at a higher layer (e.g., controller) and document where the check happens.
mode_rationale: Security convention to verify auth is enforced somewhere

rule: Prefix class names with the domain context (e.g., ModelImageBuildCancelRequest instead of BuildCancelRequest) when the short name is ambiguous outside the immediate package.
mode_rationale: Naming convention to avoid ambiguous class names

rule: Name data transfer objects to reflect their architectural layer and purpose; avoid calling objects 'DTO' if they are internal service-layer types rather than API request/response objects.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for data transfer objects

rule: In API summaries and descriptions, qualify terms that overlap with existing domain concepts to prevent confusion (e.g., 'Gen AI endpoints' vs 'Domino Endpoints').
mode_rationale: Single-sentence guideline about qualifying overlapping terms in API descriptions

rule: When the scope of an API response field is uncertain, mark it as optional to avoid breaking changes if the field is removed or deferred in a future release.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to mark uncertain fields as optional

rule: Before naming API schemas or models, check for existing names in related services to avoid collisions (e.g., use 'ModelEndpoint' instead of 'Endpoint' when 'NewEndpointV1' already exists in AI Gateway).
mode_rationale: Single check-before-naming convention

rule: When naming API fields, avoid reusing names that already have established meaning in other parts of the product to prevent confusion.
mode_rationale: Naming convention to avoid reusing established terms

rule: Audit events should include human-readable identifiers (like names or numbers) alongside internal IDs so users can understand events without cross-referencing.
mode_rationale: Simple rule to include readable identifiers alongside IDs

rule: Audit events should include all related entity identifiers (e.g., parent volume name/ID for a snapshot event) so users have full context without cross-referencing.
mode_rationale: Simple rule to include parent entity context in audit events

rule: API definitions should only include fields and schemas relevant to the resource being exposed; remove references to unrelated features or legacy concepts.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence guideline about API scope

rule: When gating a feature behind a feature flag, ensure all API endpoints and code paths that expose or depend on that feature are also filtered, not just the primary path.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to gate all paths, not just the primary one

rule: When code or template files are no longer used or integrated, remove them from the codebase rather than leaving them in place.
mode_rationale: Straightforward cleanup convention

rule: When adding TODO comments or temporary workarounds in code, include a reference to the tracking ticket (e.g., Jira ID) so the workaround can be traced and resolved.
mode_rationale: Simple rule to include ticket references in TODO comments

rule: When constructing composite string keys or identifiers, include enough context in each segment so readers can tell what entity each part refers to without checking the surrounding code.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for composite key readability

rule: Reference status values through their defined enum constants rather than hardcoding string literals, to ensure type safety and consistency.
mode_rationale: Simple coding convention to use enums over string literals

rule: When adding defensive handling (e.g., empty-string checks) for one parameter, review and apply the same treatment to other parameters of similar type and risk.
mode_rationale: Convention to apply defensive checks uniformly

rule: When using default or lazy-initialized values (e.g., default clients), verify they cannot cause race conditions or create duplicate instances in concurrent contexts.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence reminder about concurrency for lazy init

rule: Sensitive credentials like API tokens must not be stored in MongoDB or other application databases; use a secrets manager (e.g., Vault) or pass them as one-time arguments.
mode_rationale: Clear security rule about credential storage

rule: Leverage the framework's centralized error handler (e.g., ApiServerErrorHandler) for exception-to-HTTP-status mapping instead of duplicating exception handling in individual controllers.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to use framework error handler

rule: When introducing new domain exceptions (e.g., AccessDeniedException), add them to the centralized error handler so they are consistently mapped to the correct HTTP status across all endpoints.
mode_rationale: Convention to register new exceptions in the central handler

rule: API route definitions should use the correct collection type (e.g., Seq[T] instead of a single T) to accurately represent the endpoint's response contract.
mode_rationale: Simple type-accuracy convention for route definitions

rule: When adding a new tag, field, or classification to an entity, plan a migration to backfill the value on all existing records, not just newly created ones.
mode_rationale: Single reminder to backfill existing records when adding fields

rule: Push format conversion (e.g., toString, toHexString) into the domain type's case class so callers use named fields instead of scattering conversion logic across the codebase.
mode_rationale: Convention to push conversions into case classes

rule: Every public API endpoint must include summary, tags, and other OpenAPI annotations so it appears correctly in generated API documentation.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation requirement for endpoints

rule: When an API endpoint references another entity (e.g., setting roles for a user), handle the case where that entity does not exist by catching NotFoundException and returning an appropriate error.
mode_rationale: Error-handling convention for referenced entity lookups

rule: When extending an API endpoint, prefer making existing parameters optional rather than adding new parameters to achieve the same result.
mode_rationale: Simple API extension convention

rule: For list endpoints that may return large datasets, push filtering logic into database queries rather than fetching all records and filtering in application code.
mode_rationale: Performance convention about where to filter

rule: When boolean parameters interact such that one silently overrides another, extract separate methods with clear contracts instead of adding flags that create unintuitive behavior.
mode_rationale: Convention to avoid flags that silently override each other

rule: When adding a new method with overlapping scope, rename the existing ambiguous method to be more specific so callers don't accidentally use the wrong one.
mode_rationale: Naming convention when adding overlapping methods

rule: Use the established domain term (e.g., 'public') rather than introducing synonyms (e.g., 'global') that create ambiguity in the codebase.
mode_rationale: Simple rule to use established domain terms

rule: When an entity has related enum fields (e.g., category and type), validate that the type value is valid for the given category to prevent inconsistent state.
mode_rationale: Validation convention for related enum fields

rule: When a single method already handles all cases correctly, do not add conditional branching that duplicates or wraps it unnecessarily.
mode_rationale: Code simplicity convention

rule: When adding a new use case to an existing API, prefer adding a new code path rather than modifying existing behavior, to reduce the risk of breaking current consumers.
mode_rationale: Convention to prefer additive changes over modifications

rule: Before creating new feature flags or scoped identifiers, check if an established naming scope or prefix already exists for that domain area.
mode_rationale: Simple check-first naming convention

rule: When adding new routes, follow the conventions established by existing endpoints in the same routes file rather than introducing new patterns.
mode_rationale: Convention to match existing endpoint patterns

rule: Throw domain-specific exception types (e.g., GenAIApiException.HttpNotFound) instead of generic exceptions when mapping API error responses, so the controller can translate them to correct HTTP status codes.
mode_rationale: Convention to throw typed exceptions instead of generic ones

rule: Do not return HTTP 404 for corrupted or invalid internal data — if the resource exists but contains bad data, that is a server error (5xx), not a 'not found' error.
mode_rationale: Simple rule about correct HTTP status mapping

rule: Catch known exceptions at the service level and map them to domain-specific errors; use a global catch block at the API controller level to convert any unmapped exceptions to 500, rather than masking all exceptions in utility methods.
mode_rationale: Convention about where to catch and map exceptions in the stack

rule: Dependencies on the monolith server module should live in the monolith-adapter module, not in the core implementation module, to maintain clean module boundaries.
mode_rationale: Module boundary convention

rule: Only implement API endpoints or features when there is a concrete requirement; it is easier to add functionality later than to remove it.
mode_rationale: Simple principle to only build what is needed now

rule: When splitting mapper logic into smaller classes, maintain a clean dependency tree where shared utilities are leaf-level classes — child mappers should not reference the root mapper.
mode_rationale: Convention about clean dependency trees in mapper classes

rule: When a method creates a domain object with additional validation or lookups beyond simple construction, its name should reflect that (e.g., 'createValidEnvironmentUpdate' rather than a generic name).
mode_rationale: Naming convention for methods that validate during construction

rule: When renaming a field in BSON or database storage, consider writing a migration rather than just changing the field name in code, to avoid breaking reads of existing documents.
mode_rationale: Simple reminder to migrate data when renaming stored fields

rule: Do not create catch-all 'Utils' classes; split distinct responsibilities into their own well-named classes or files.
mode_rationale: Convention to split responsibilities instead of catch-all Utils

rule: Do not combine create and update semantics in a single endpoint; separate them so users understand what action they are performing and avoid surprising side effects like an edit silently triggering a new resource creation.
mode_rationale: API design convention to keep create and update distinct

rule: Name feature flags for the broad capability they gate, not for a specific sub-feature, so the same flag can cover future related work without proliferating flags.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for feature flags

rule: Feature flags used during development should be removed once the feature is tested and shipped; do not accumulate permanent development flags.
mode_rationale: Simple hygiene rule to remove flags after shipping

rule: When a boolean field should only be true or false, make it required with a default of false rather than optional, to avoid an unnecessary None/Some(true)/Some(false) three-state representation.
mode_rationale: Convention to use required boolean with default over optional

rule: When passing boolean literals to functions with long positional parameter lists, assign them to named explanatory variables so readers can understand the argument's purpose without looking up the function signature.
mode_rationale: Readability convention for boolean positional arguments

rule: When permissions vary based on conditions, construct a single permission collection by conditionally appending entries rather than duplicating the authorization check with different argument lists.
mode_rationale: Convention to construct permissions by appending rather than duplicating

rule: When adding new fields to API responses, verify that existing clients using the API will not break — adding a new field to a response object is generally safe for loosely-typed clients but should be verified.
mode_rationale: Backward compatibility convention for API responses

rule: When a value has already been fetched from a service, derive dependent values from it rather than making a redundant service call to fetch the same data again.
mode_rationale: Performance convention to reuse already-fetched data

rule: After refactoring return types (e.g., from throwing to Option), remove now-unnecessary error-handling wrappers like Try that were left over from the previous implementation.
mode_rationale: Cleanup convention to remove stale wrappers after refactoring

rule: Use pattern matching instead of .collect on a single Option value; .collect is idiomatic for filtering and mapping collections, not single values, and pattern matching is clearer.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence coding style preference for pattern matching over .collect

rule: Do not wrap a value that is already an Option in another Option and then flatten; access the Option value directly to avoid unnecessary nesting.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to avoid unnecessary Option nesting

rule: When updating a single field on a database record, update only that field directly rather than reading the entire object into memory, modifying it, and saving it back, to avoid overwriting concurrent updates to other fields.
mode_rationale: Single best practice about updating database fields atomically

rule: Don't wrap code in Try or similar error-handling constructs without a clear justification; reviewers will question unnecessary defensive coding.
mode_rationale: Simple guideline to avoid unnecessary defensive wrapping

rule: Avoid redundant operations on Option types such as `.getOrElse(None)` which is a no-op on Option values.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence rule about removing no-op Option operations

rule: When introducing a new parameter, default value, or behavioral branch, add tests that explicitly demonstrate the new behavior is exercised and produces the expected result.
mode_rationale: Passive convention to always test new behavioral branches

rule: When a change touches multiple modules, verify each module has an existing spec and add relevant tests in every affected spec, not just one.
mode_rationale: Simple rule to ensure test coverage across all affected modules

rule: When using Docker BuildKit secrets, mount them directly as environment variables using the env option instead of reading secret files with cat.
mode_rationale: Single convention for how to handle Docker BuildKit secrets

rule: Do not embed feature-specific concepts (e.g., MLflow secret name overrides) into shared/generic domain code; find a way to use existing generic contracts instead.
mode_rationale: Architectural guideline about separation of concerns

rule: When adding cross-module functionality, ensure dependency direction flows from specific feature modules toward generic/core modules, never the reverse.
mode_rationale: Single rule about dependency direction flowing specific-to-generic

rule: When adding a new field to a model or DTO, audit and update all validation checks that compare versions or detect changes, and add tests for the new field in those checks.
mode_rationale: Multi-step procedure: add field, audit all validation checks, update each, add tests

rule: In OpenAPI/Swagger specs, use $ref references to shared schema types (e.g., DominoId) instead of inline type definitions to maintain consistency and reuse.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to use $ref instead of inline definitions

rule: Use the standard canonical API (e.g., getAccessToken()) for token acquisition instead of internal-specific token acquirers unless there is a documented reason to diverge.
mode_rationale: Single guideline to use canonical API for token acquisition

rule: When adding new data retrieval functions or integration points, include unit and/or integration tests covering the new code paths.
mode_rationale: Passive convention to include tests for new data retrieval code

rule: Use java.time.Instant instead of DateTime (Joda-Time) for all new Scala code, per the Domino Scala Style Guide.
mode_rationale: Simple style rule about which time type to use

rule: When a constant already exists in the codebase for a value (e.g., ports, paths, config keys), reference the constant instead of hardcoding the value.
mode_rationale: Single convention to reference existing constants

rule: When composing user-provided configuration with system-required configuration, ensure critical system arguments are positioned last or otherwise protected so user input cannot override them.
mode_rationale: Single security guideline about config composition order

rule: Run plugins and similar dynamically composed execution components should be versioned so that re-running a job or app version produces consistent behavior across Domino upgrades.
mode_rationale: Single architectural principle about versioning execution components

rule: When building new API endpoints or schemas for a feature that parallels an existing feature, use the same field names and conventions to maintain cross-API consistency, even if a more accurate name exists.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to reuse field names across parallel APIs

rule: Use plural nouns for REST API resource paths (e.g., /endpoints/ not /endpoint/) to maintain consistency with REST conventions and existing APIs.
mode_rationale: Single REST naming convention

rule: Do not embed version prefixes like /v1 in API base URLs when they refer to a third-party API version rather than your own, as users may confuse it with the Domino API version.
mode_rationale: Simple URL design guideline

rule: When reverting or modifying dependency files, ensure version numbers are updated to the correct target value before merging the PR.
mode_rationale: Simple pre-merge check for correct dependency versions

rule: When a feature flag is removed and its behavior defaults to enabled, add a deprecation warning log on any endpoints or code paths that are now obsolete.
mode_rationale: Single convention to log deprecation warnings when removing flags

rule: When a class or method's responsibilities narrow or change, update its name to reflect the current behavior.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention to keep names aligned with behavior

rule: Dependencies should be injected via the constructor rather than instantiated or resolved inside the class body.
mode_rationale: Single dependency injection convention

rule: Avoid deeply nested case/match expressions; refactor into smaller methods, for-comprehensions, or combinators to improve readability.
mode_rationale: Simple readability guideline about nesting depth

rule: Do not wrap synchronous operations in Future; use simpler return types like Option or Either when no async work is performed.
mode_rationale: Single convention to avoid async wrapping of synchronous code

rule: Ensure declared error cases and exception throws are actually reachable; remove dead error-handling code that can never execute.
mode_rationale: Simple rule to remove dead error-handling code

rule: Extract hardcoded magic numbers and durations into named constants to improve readability and maintainability.
mode_rationale: Single convention about named constants

rule: New or modified service adapter files should have accompanying unit tests.
mode_rationale: Simple testing convention for adapter files

rule: Use Optional types or clearly identifiable sentinel values for configuration parameters instead of ambiguous bare values that are hard to distinguish from real data.
mode_rationale: Single guideline about using Optional types for config

rule: Variable names should accurately describe the operation being performed; rename variables when the underlying behavior changes to avoid misleading readers.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention to keep variable names accurate

rule: When a type's fields or semantics change, update the type name to reflect the new shape so API consumers are not misled.
mode_rationale: Single naming guideline to rename types when shape changes

rule: Use specific, domain-qualified names instead of generic terms that could be confused with broader concepts.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention favoring domain-specific names

rule: Default parameter values should not silently override configuration settings; make parameters required when a default could mask a missing or incorrect config value.
mode_rationale: Single architectural principle about default parameter values

rule: When renaming values, constants, or types, update all warning and log messages to reflect the new names so users see accurate information.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to update log messages during renames

rule: When a type belongs to one or more categories, define that relationship directly on the type rather than validating compatibility externally.
mode_rationale: Single architectural guideline about type-category relationships

rule: When looking up an entity by a unique field, use headOption.getOrElse with a descriptive NoSuchElementException rather than silently returning None or using unsafe .head.
mode_rationale: Single error-handling convention for entity lookups

rule: Delete and other destructive API endpoints must include test cases that verify authorization checks are enforced.
mode_rationale: Simple testing rule for destructive endpoints

rule: Extract complex string interpolations or expressions into separate named variables to improve readability, especially when a similar pattern is already used nearby.
Do not allow operations to proceed with sentinel or placeholder values like "unknown"; throw an error instead of silently accepting invalid state.
mode_rationale: Simple error-handling guideline about invalid state

rule: In tests, verify that the correct values were passed to dependencies, not just that the mock method was called.
mode_rationale: Single testing convention about assertion quality

rule: Methods that are not part of a class's public interface or trait contract should be marked private to limit the API surface.
mode_rationale: Simple encapsulation convention

rule: Prefer comparing entire case class instances (result shouldEqual ExpectedClass(...)) over checking individual fields, so new fields added in future PRs are automatically covered by existing tests.
mode_rationale: Single testing convention about assertion granularity

rule: Test names and descriptions must accurately describe the behavior being verified; update test names when the test logic changes.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for test descriptions

rule: API fields with a known set of valid values should be defined as enums, not freeform strings, to enable validation and discoverability.
mode_rationale: Single API design convention about enum types

rule: Every new API endpoint must include explicit authorization checks; do not assume inherited auth covers new routes.
mode_rationale: Simple security convention for new endpoints

rule: Use established library methods (e.g. Json.stringify) for serialization and escaping rather than hand-rolling escape logic.
mode_rationale: Single security guideline about using established libraries

rule: Hardcoded numeric literals should be extracted into named configuration keys with sensible defaults so they can be tuned without code changes.
mode_rationale: Single architectural convention about configurable values

rule: When modifying code around logging statements, update log messages to accurately reflect the current behavior and context.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to update log messages during refactoring

rule: Container builds should use internally maintained base images (e.g. Chainguard-based) rather than pulling directly from upstream Docker Hub, for supply chain security, caching, and reproducibility.
mode_rationale: Single convention: prefer internal base images over upstream Docker Hub

rule: API specifications (Swagger/OpenAPI) must declare the correct HTTP status codes for each endpoint response; verify status codes during review.
mode_rationale: Single rule: declare correct HTTP status codes in OpenAPI specs

rule: When surfacing error messages from backend services, verify what the end user will actually see and confirm the UI handles the message properly.
mode_rationale: Single check: confirm error messages render correctly in the UI

rule: When implementing state-changing operations, consider what happens under concurrent requests and whether duplicate transitions could cause unintended side effects.
mode_rationale: Single principle: consider concurrency when implementing state changes

rule: When a method returns different wrapper types (e.g. Some vs None) to signal different outcomes, add documentation explaining what each return value means.
mode_rationale: Single convention: document what different return values mean

rule: Extract common variables from complex match/case expressions to reduce the number of cases and improve readability.
mode_rationale: Single refactoring guideline: extract shared variables from match arms

rule: When performing pattern matching or inference logic, log which pattern was matched to aid debugging and observability.
mode_rationale: Single convention: log which pattern was matched for debuggability

rule: Use debug level for verbose or frequently-executed logging (such as mapping/inference results); reserve info level for significant state changes or business events.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use debug for verbose logging, info for significant events

rule: Combine related log statements into a single informative line so it is easier to correlate results when reading logs.
mode_rationale: Single convention: combine related log lines into one

rule: Use defined constant sets (e.g., `terminalStatuses`) for state checks instead of hardcoding individual enum values, so new states are automatically handled.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use constant sets instead of hardcoded enum values

rule: Return Option/None instead of throwing RecordNotFoundException for missing sub-entities to avoid cascading 404 errors on parent resource endpoints.
mode_rationale: Single convention: return Option/None instead of throwing for missing sub-entities

rule: API enum string values should not contain spaces; use machine-friendly identifiers (e.g., BuildFailed) and let the UI map them to human-readable display strings.
mode_rationale: Single convention: API enum values should be machine-friendly identifiers

rule: When adding new public methods or services, add corresponding test cases to cover their behavior.
mode_rationale: Single convention: add tests when adding public methods

rule: Add validation to prevent invalid state transitions (e.g., moving backward from a later stage) to guard against race conditions in concurrent systems.
mode_rationale: Single convention: prevent invalid backward state transitions

rule: When using acronyms in code identifiers (class names, config keys, variables), add a comment explaining what the acronym stands for.
mode_rationale: Single convention: explain acronyms in comments

rule: Name variables and fields that represent lists or collections using plural nouns to communicate their cardinality.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention: plural nouns for collections

rule: When an identifier contains words that are easily confused (e.g., 'Approval' and 'Approvers'), restructure the name to create clear separation between the similar terms.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention: restructure names to avoid confusion between similar terms

rule: Use precise verbs that describe the actual operation — use 'unset' when clearing a value, not 'delete' which implies removing a resource entirely.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention: use precise verbs matching the actual operation

rule: When an API endpoint always returns null/empty on success, return HTTP 204 No Content instead of advertising a nullable response schema.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use 204 when endpoint returns nothing on success

rule: Reserve DELETE for actually removing a resource; use POST or PATCH for operations that modify or clear a field on an existing resource to avoid misinterpretation.
mode_rationale: Single convention: reserve DELETE for resource removal, use PATCH/POST for field changes

rule: When catching and re-throwing exceptions, preserve the original exception and its stack trace as the cause to avoid losing debugging information.
mode_rationale: Single convention: preserve original exception as cause when re-throwing

rule: Use DEBUG for detailed operational messages that would be noisy in production; reserve INFO for significant events that operators should see.
mode_rationale: Single convention: DEBUG for detailed messages, INFO for operator-visible events

rule: Name fields precisely to indicate what they contain — e.g., use 'username' instead of a generic 'userId' when the field holds a username string, not an ID.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention: field names should match what they contain

rule: Name methods with a leading verb (e.g., 'fetch', 'create', 'validate') to clearly indicate the action being performed.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention: lead method names with a verb

rule: Do not default query limits or page sizes to Int.MaxValue or similar unbounded values; use a reasonable finite default to prevent future performance issues.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use finite defaults for query limits

rule: When introducing nullable or optional update fields, add explicit tests for the None/null case to verify the field is not inadvertently modified.
mode_rationale: Single convention: test the None/null case for optional fields

rule: When adding validation logic, include tests for both the happy path (valid input passes) and the error path (invalid input is rejected).
mode_rationale: Single convention: test both valid and invalid inputs for validators

rule: When validating user-supplied strings like URLs, check for degenerate patterns such as consecutive dots, leading/trailing special characters, and other edge cases beyond basic regex matching.
mode_rationale: Single convention: check degenerate patterns in user-supplied strings

rule: When backporting or cherry-picking changes across branches, always port the associated tests to maintain test parity.
mode_rationale: Single convention: always port tests alongside backported code

rule: Use a URL building library or framework function to construct URLs rather than manual string interpolation, which is error-prone with encoding and path separators.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use URL builder APIs instead of string interpolation

rule: When a value is required for correct behavior, make it non-optional at the type level rather than silently accepting None and producing incorrect results.
mode_rationale: Single convention: make required values non-optional at the type level

rule: Use PUT for update/replace operations on existing resources rather than POST, which implies creation; choosing the correct HTTP verb makes the API more expressive and RESTful.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use PUT for updates, POST for creation

rule: Review diffs for leftover debugging code, test configuration, and dead code before submitting a PR for review.
mode_rationale: Single convention: clean up debug/dead code before submitting PRs

rule: When updating a record, use a direct update operation rather than a delete-then-insert pattern, which is less atomic and can cause transient inconsistencies.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use direct update instead of delete-then-insert

rule: When an API is defined in multiple spec formats (e.g., JSON and YAML), update all formats together to avoid drift between spec files.
mode_rationale: Single convention: update all spec formats together

rule: Before defining a new schema type in an OpenAPI spec, check if an equivalent type already exists and reuse it to avoid near-duplicate definitions.
mode_rationale: Single convention: check for and reuse existing schema types

rule: When different entity types share an implementation (e.g., service accounts implemented as users), ensure API endpoints enforce the correct permission model for each entity type.
mode_rationale: Single convention: enforce correct permissions per entity type

rule: When creating public API endpoints with generic paths, design the request schema to be polymorphic (e.g., using a discriminator field) so future types can be added without breaking changes.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use polymorphic schemas for extensible endpoints

rule: When naming fields or properties, use the correct casing for compound words — single words like 'autoscaling' should not use camelCase internally.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention: correct casing for compound words

rule: When removing a field from an API or service interface, verify that all known clients do not depend on it before merging.
mode_rationale: Single convention: verify no clients depend on removed fields

rule: Tests for new or changed functionality should be included in the PR itself, not deferred to a follow-up.
mode_rationale: Single convention: tests belong in the PR, not a follow-up

rule: When modifying code that serves multiple features or code paths, manually verify that existing functionality still works correctly.
mode_rationale: Single convention: manually verify existing features still work

rule: When a boolean parameter value appears counter-intuitive in context (e.g., isSnapshot=false for a snapshot mount), add a comment explaining the reasoning.
mode_rationale: Single convention: explain surprising boolean parameter values

rule: Error messages should describe the likely cause and suggest a resolution rather than just stating a condition — e.g., report 'anonymous user' instead of 'user ID missing'.
mode_rationale: Single convention: error messages should describe cause and suggest resolution

rule: When adding new copy/fork options to an API, use the existing extensible mechanism (e.g., an 'include' list) rather than adding new top-level boolean fields that duplicate the legacy pattern.
mode_rationale: Single convention: extend existing mechanisms rather than adding duplicate fields

rule: Prefix OpenAPI schema names with their module namespace to prevent silent collisions where one module's schema definition masks another's.
mode_rationale: Single convention: prefix schema names with module namespace

rule: Config key names should be explicit and self-descriptive so future readers can understand their purpose without looking up external documentation.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention: config keys should be self-descriptive

rule: Use warn log level instead of info or error for conditions that are unexpected but recoverable, reserving error for unrecoverable failures.
mode_rationale: Single convention: warn for unexpected-but-recoverable, error for unrecoverable

rule: When chaining filter predicates, place the cheapest checks (e.g., simple equality or inequality) before more expensive ones to short-circuit evaluation early.
mode_rationale: Single convention: place cheapest filter predicates first for short-circuiting

rule: After resolving merge conflicts, check for and remove duplicate imports — consider enabling compiler flags that treat duplicate imports as errors.
mode_rationale: Single convention: check for duplicate imports after merge conflict resolution

rule: Permission names should accurately reflect what they enforce — a permission that only checks authentication should be named 'requireAuthenticatedUser', not 'canListEndpoints', which implies broader authorization.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention for permission identifiers

rule: For list/query endpoints, prefer a principal-scoped authorization context that resolves roles for the requesting user only, rather than a context that eagerly loads all users across all collaborating organizations.
mode_rationale: Single performance guideline about authorization context scope

rule: When you only need to know whether a record exists, use an existence check or limit-1 query instead of fetching the full collection.
mode_rationale: Single query optimization convention

rule: When modifying behavior or APIs, verify that associated documentation (comments, docs, scaladoc) still accurately reflects the new behavior.
mode_rationale: Simple discipline rule about updating docs alongside code

rule: Address linter warnings for unused variables by removing them rather than leaving dead code in the codebase.
mode_rationale: Simple code hygiene convention

rule: When adding new fields to configuration or settings classes, use Option types to maintain backwards compatibility with existing deployments that lack the new field.
mode_rationale: Single typing convention for new config fields

rule: When an entity is in a transitional state (e.g., building), return an empty or default response from informational endpoints rather than throwing a 500 error.
mode_rationale: Single error-handling convention for transitional states

rule: In tests, use representative values (e.g., non-None dataPlaneId for remote data plane tests) instead of defaults that carry implicit meaning in production code.
mode_rationale: Simple testing convention about representative data

rule: When adding new events or features, use the current implementation pattern rather than extending legacy code paths that are no longer needed.
mode_rationale: Simple guideline to prefer current patterns over legacy

rule: Define entity types in a single shared location (e.g., a shared library) rather than scattering definitions across individual services.
mode_rationale: Single organizational convention about type placement

rule: When renaming or changing default values, update all associated log messages and warnings to reflect the new names or behavior.
mode_rationale: Simple discipline rule about log message accuracy

rule: When tagging executions or emitting audit events, include contextual metadata like revision numbers that aid debugging, but avoid internal IDs that provide no user-facing value.
mode_rationale: Single guideline about what metadata to include in events

rule: When transforming optional collections, filter out empty values before applying map transformations to avoid producing unnecessary empty results.
mode_rationale: Simple coding convention for collection transformations

rule: Place all imports at the top of the file so dependencies are visible at a glance.
Avoid default parameter values (e.g., `= None`) unless genuinely needed, as they can mask bugs by allowing callers to omit required arguments silently.
mode_rationale: Simple parameter design convention

rule: Use the appropriate log level for the situation — use warn for concerning-but-recoverable conditions rather than info or error.
mode_rationale: Single guideline about log level selection

rule: Reference configuration settings for paths and mount points instead of hardcoding values, so deployments with non-default configurations work correctly.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to use config over hardcoded values

rule: Implement a dry-run mode for destructive operations (cleanup jobs, bulk deletions) so operators can preview the impact before executing.
mode_rationale: Single design guideline for destructive operations

rule: When cache eviction relies on last-access timestamps, ensure the timestamp is updated throughout the resource's lifecycle (not just at startup) to prevent premature eviction of actively-used entries.
mode_rationale: Single caching convention about timestamp freshness

rule: Do not wrap calls in try/catch when the called method already handles exceptions internally — redundant error handling adds noise and can swallow useful context.
mode_rationale: Simple error-handling convention

rule: Separate service-side mount points from container-side mount paths in configuration so they can be independently configured without coupling infrastructure concerns.
mode_rationale: Single configuration design convention

rule: Do not duplicate URL path parameters in the request body — maintain a single source of truth for each value to avoid conflicts.
mode_rationale: Simple API design convention about single source of truth

rule: Add explicit documentation for non-obvious or surprising API behaviors, such as side effects where modifying one version may replace another.
mode_rationale: Multi-step: identify surprising behavior, document in API contract, and add unit test

rule: Group custom exception classes in a shared location (e.g., an exceptions file in the domain layer) rather than defining them inline in individual services, to promote reuse and discoverability.
mode_rationale: Single organizational convention about exception placement

rule: When modifying a shared API endpoint, identify all consumers (UI, other services) and ensure they are updated to match the new contract.
mode_rationale: Simple discipline rule about checking downstream consumers

rule: Ensure API response metadata (counts, totals, pagination) accurately reflects the filtered or visible data, not the underlying unfiltered dataset, to avoid confusing clients.
mode_rationale: Single API correctness convention

rule: Apply filtering at the service or query layer rather than fetching all records and filtering in the controller to preserve pagination and reduce waste.
mode_rationale: Simple architectural convention about where filtering belongs

rule: Error messages should accurately reflect the actual error condition; do not use misleading messages even if the HTTP status code is correct.
mode_rationale: Simple convention about error message accuracy

rule: Remove unused functions and dead code rather than leaving them in the codebase.
mode_rationale: Simple code cleanliness rule

rule: Prefer returning a result type over throwing exceptions, letting callers decide how to handle the error.
mode_rationale: Single error-handling style convention

rule: When a function accumulates many parameters, group related parameters into a dedicated object to keep signatures manageable.
mode_rationale: Simple refactoring convention for parameter lists

rule: Do not include an 'all' value in REST API filter parameters; omitting the filter should return all results by default.
mode_rationale: Single API design convention about filter defaults

rule: Do not suppress harmless information (like execution IDs) from API responses in the name of minimal exposure when it adds complexity without real security benefit.
mode_rationale: Simple API design convention about information exposure

rule: Remove or update suppression annotations (like @unused) when the suppressed element is actually in use, to avoid misleading readers.
mode_rationale: Simple code hygiene convention about annotation accuracy

rule: In Scala/JVM code, catch only NonFatal exceptions instead of all Throwables to avoid swallowing fatal JVM errors like OutOfMemoryError.
mode_rationale: Single exception-handling convention for JVM code

rule: Do not catch and suppress errors behind a warning log without surfacing any indication to the user; provide degraded responses or error hints so failures are visible.
mode_rationale: Simple error-handling convention about error visibility

rule: Pass already-fetched data as parameters instead of re-fetching it in downstream methods to avoid redundant I/O.
mode_rationale: Single performance convention about data reuse

rule: Use service principal abstractions (e.g., ServicePrincipalMaker) instead of directly injecting superuser credentials, making it easier to remove privileged access later.
mode_rationale: Single architectural convention about privilege abstraction

rule: When constructing objects, use named parameters for all fields consistently rather than mixing positional and named styles.
mode_rationale: Simple coding style convention

rule: Group configuration keys under their logical domain namespace rather than scattering them at the top level.
mode_rationale: Single organizational convention for config namespacing

rule: Do not encode implicit context (e.g., principal, tenant) into method names when all methods in the class already operate under that context.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention

rule: For internal methods, prefer adding an optional parameter to an existing function; for external-facing service APIs, keep separate methods to make the authorization context explicit.
mode_rationale: Single API design guideline about internal vs external method signatures

rule: Use idiomatic Option combinators like getOrElse instead of verbose if/else or match expressions for optional values.
mode_rationale: Simple coding style convention for optional values

rule: Do not reuse a UI default config value as a security validation limit; introduce a separate config key for the maximum allowed value.
mode_rationale: Single security convention about config separation

rule: When matching or parsing user-supplied input against enums or known values, use case-insensitive comparison.
mode_rationale: Simple input-handling convention

rule: The service layer should not know about API response types; only the controller layer should reference *Response classes, and the service should return domain objects.
mode_rationale: Single layering convention about type boundaries

rule: Ensure the terms used in the UI, API, and backend code are consistent; if concepts diverge (e.g., 'owner' vs 'publisher'), resolve the naming before shipping.
mode_rationale: Simple naming consistency convention

rule: Validate user-supplied identifiers (IDs, enums) at the controller layer and return a 400 Bad Request with a descriptive message rather than letting invalid input propagate and produce a 500.
mode_rationale: Single validation convention at controller layer

rule: When introducing a new domain class that extends or enriches an existing one, prefix it with the parent context name and use richer types instead of raw strings.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for domain class hierarchy

rule: When adding new endpoints or refactoring, remove code paths that are no longer needed rather than leaving them in place.
mode_rationale: Basic code hygiene convention

rule: Place all imports at the top of the file rather than inline so that all dependencies are visible in one place.
mode_rationale: Simple code organization convention about import placement

rule: Every new configuration setting should have a clear justification; prefer simplicity and fewer settings unless there is a concrete scenario (e.g., user code causing false positives) that requires a runtime kill switch.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence guideline about preferring simplicity in configuration

rule: When introducing novel operational features with potential failure modes, document known issues, configuration knobs, and troubleshooting steps in the runbook before shipping.
mode_rationale: Single guideline about documenting failure modes before shipping

rule: Use existing constants or named values for well-known paths and identifiers instead of hardcoding string literals.
When reading files from untrusted sources (user code, external input), enforce a size limit to prevent oversized payloads from affecting downstream systems like message queues.
mode_rationale: Single defensive-coding rule about enforcing size limits

rule: Use domain-specific helper methods (e.g., `isGitBased`, `isDfsProject`) instead of reimplementing the same logic inline; this avoids drift and keeps business logic centralized.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to use existing helper methods instead of inline logic

rule: When stripping or parsing delimiter characters (e.g., comment markers like `#`), handle the case where the delimiter appears inside quoted strings to avoid corrupting valid values.
mode_rationale: Single defensive-coding rule about delimiter handling in quoted strings

rule: Acknowledge that regex-based parsing of shell-like syntax will have edge cases (e.g., escaped quotes); document known limitations and prefer a proper parser when complexity grows.
mode_rationale: Simple architectural guideline about parser choice

rule: When adding domain attributes, ensure they are placed on the entity that actually owns the concept; avoid putting child-level attributes on a parent entity where they become ambiguous.
mode_rationale: Single domain-modeling convention about attribute ownership

rule: Remove all temporary test code, debug scaffolding, and experimental snippets before merging a PR.
mode_rationale: Simple hygiene rule about removing debug code

rule: When changing data schemas, permission models, or stored values, explicitly assess whether existing data or permissions will be affected and document the migration path.
mode_rationale: Multi-step procedure: identify affected data, assess impact, document migration path

rule: When a validation rejects user input, the error message should explain what the user should do instead, not just what they did wrong.
mode_rationale: Simple convention about error message content

rule: When using positional accessors like `lastOption` or `head`, verify and document that the underlying collection is guaranteed to be ordered as expected; implicit ordering assumptions break silently.
mode_rationale: Single defensive-coding rule about verifying collection ordering

rule: When triggering state transitions like restarts or redeployments, log the reason for the transition to aid debugging and operational tracing.
mode_rationale: Simple logging convention for state changes

rule: Apply filters before pagination, not after, to avoid returning incomplete pages of results.
mode_rationale: Single rule about ordering of filter and pagination operations

rule: Tests should explicitly assert that excluded or filtered-out items are absent from results, not just verify the count and contents of included items.
mode_rationale: Simple testing convention about asserting exclusions

rule: Comments should explain why code exists or the business reasoning, not restate what the code already clearly expresses.
mode_rationale: Single documentation convention about comment purpose

rule: When the frontend needs state for multiple items, provide a batch endpoint instead of requiring multiple individual API calls.
mode_rationale: Simple API design guideline preferring batch endpoints

rule: When sending a large number of items to an external service, split into configurable batches to avoid overwhelming the client or exceeding request size limits.
mode_rationale: Simple performance convention about splitting large payloads

rule: When calling paginated APIs, always implement pagination handling; do not rely on default page sizes, as they silently truncate results.
mode_rationale: Single rule about always implementing pagination handling

rule: Avoid N+1 query patterns; if unavoidable, parallelize the individual calls to mitigate the performance impact.
mode_rationale: Simple performance convention about query patterns

rule: When setting up a mock in a test, always verify that the mock was actually called to ensure the code under test exercises the expected path.
mode_rationale: Simple testing convention about verifying mock calls

rule: API documentation should describe behavior from the user's perspective, not expose internal implementation details like data point maximization strategies or internal entity names.
mode_rationale: Single documentation convention about user-facing perspective

rule: Only include information in API descriptions that the user can act on; omit internal logic the user has no control over.
mode_rationale: Single documentation convention about actionable information

rule: Define magic numbers and constant values at the top of the file or in a companion object, not inline within method bodies.
mode_rationale: Simple code organization convention about constant placement

rule: Comments explaining algorithmic choices should be specific about what the algorithm optimizes for, not vague about what it does.
mode_rationale: Single documentation convention about comment specificity

rule: Tests should verify that system invariants and constraints hold (e.g., result stays within documented limits), not just check the computed output value.
mode_rationale: Simple testing convention about verifying constraints

rule: When a function must return one of a known set of valid values, test assertions should verify the result is a member of that set, not just that it is non-null or non-zero.
mode_rationale: Simple testing convention about set membership assertions

rule: Add tests for boundary and edge-case inputs (e.g., values below minimums, empty ranges) to ensure correct behavior at the extremes of input space.
mode_rationale: Simple testing convention about edge-case coverage

rule: In Scala, filter collections before mapping to avoid unnecessary transformations on items that will be discarded; prefer `collect` with partial functions for combined filter-and-map operations.
mode_rationale: Simple Scala performance convention about collection operations

rule: When validating file paths for security, also reject null bytes to prevent null byte injection attacks that can bypass path traversal checks.
mode_rationale: Single security rule about null byte validation

rule: When fixing a security vulnerability, identify and patch all other services that handle the same input vector to ensure consistent protection.
mode_rationale: Multi-step procedure: identify the vulnerability, find all services with same input vector, patch each one

rule: When adding a precondition check that prevents a state from being reached, remove or simplify downstream code that handled that now-impossible state.
mode_rationale: Simple refactoring convention about cleaning up unreachable code

rule: Don't execute all queries upfront when the results are only conditionally needed; defer data fetching to the point of use to avoid unnecessary work.
mode_rationale: Simple performance convention about lazy data fetching

rule: When using elevated privileges (e.g., service principal) to check for blocking conditions, do not expose names or details of resources the user lacks permission to see in user-facing error messages.
mode_rationale: Single security rule about error message content

rule: When performing operations that span multiple feature domains (e.g., archival), delegate to each feature's own service method rather than combining them, to avoid surfacing internal implementation details (like internally-linked resources) to users.
mode_rationale: Single architectural guideline about delegation to feature services

rule: Don't leave commented-out code in the codebase; remove it entirely since version control preserves history.
mode_rationale: Simple hygiene rule about removing dead code

rule: When the implementation checks for a non-empty condition or distinct code path, ensure there is a corresponding test case that exercises that branch.
mode_rationale: Simple testing convention about branch coverage

rule: When data has already been retrieved in the current scope, reuse the existing reference rather than making a redundant fetch call.
mode_rationale: Simple performance convention about avoiding redundant fetches

rule: When a variable is referenced in multiple places, give it a descriptive name that conveys its semantic role (e.g., 'unarchivedResources' instead of a generic name) to improve readability.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention about descriptive variable names

rule: Never remove pagination or use unbounded loops that pull all data from a paginated API; always respect client-specified page sizes and limits to avoid performance degradation at scale.
mode_rationale: Single performance rule about honoring pagination constraints

rule: When filtering API results by an entity's domain type, use an enum-based query parameter (e.g., `?projectType=Template`) rather than a boolean toggle (e.g., `?includeTemplateApps=false`), as enums are more extensible, declarative, and consistent with domain modeling.
mode_rationale: Simple API design convention about filter parameter types

rule: When modifying API definitions, regenerate derived spec files using the project's designated tooling (e.g., `dev/bazel/update_api_specs.sh`) to keep generated artifacts consistent with source definitions.
mode_rationale: Simple convention about regenerating derived files after changes

rule: Avoid default parameter values in method signatures; prefer explicit arguments at call sites to make behavior clear and prevent hidden bugs.
mode_rationale: Simple coding convention about avoiding default parameter values

rule: Do not use an empty collection to mean 'no filter' or 'all values'; instead define an explicit constant (e.g., ALL_TYPES) so the intent is clear at every call site.
mode_rationale: Single API design rule about explicit intent over implicit conventions

rule: Constants that are meant to be used across modules should follow capitalized naming conventions to signal they are constants.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for shared constants

rule: When filtering logic depends on non-obvious domain behavior (e.g., one list only containing active items while another contains all), add a comment explaining why the filter exists.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation convention about explaining non-obvious filters

rule: When adding validation to a create endpoint, audit whether the same validation should also apply to update/patch endpoints for the same resource.
mode_rationale: Multi-step procedure: add validation to create, then audit update/patch endpoints for the same resource

rule: When all callers can provide a required value, make the parameter mandatory instead of optional — eliminate None/null handling branches that add unnecessary complexity.
mode_rationale: Simple design convention about eliminating unnecessary optionality

rule: User-facing error messages must use the product's user-facing terminology (e.g., 'Domino Endpoint') rather than internal names (e.g., 'model API').
mode_rationale: Single convention about using product terminology in error messages

rule: When the same operation appears in multiple branches, refactor to call it once before or after the branch so the logic is not duplicated.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence refactoring convention to avoid duplicated logic across branches

rule: Test descriptions must accurately describe what the test actually verifies; misleading test names obscure intent and make failures harder to diagnose.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for test descriptions

rule: Do not redefine constants that already exist elsewhere in the codebase; import and reuse the existing definition to improve maintainability.
mode_rationale: Simple DRY convention for constants

rule: Use existing shared test helper functions and fixtures instead of redefining test data locally; this reduces LOC and improves maintainability.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to prefer shared test helpers over local redefinitions
