rule: Remove empty objects, unused classes, and dead code rather than leaving them in the codebase.
mode_rationale: Straightforward hygiene rule about removing dead code

rule: When backporting a bug fix to a release branch, ensure a corresponding fix is also opened against the main development branch.
mode_rationale: Simple branching convention, not a multi-step procedure

rule: When adding a new metric or event type, ensure it is also added to all related tracking or reporting locations in the codebase.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence consistency rule for metrics/events

rule: When implementing a workaround or non-obvious fix, add a comment explaining the background context, why the simpler approach was chosen, and any caveats for future maintainers.
mode_rationale: Simple commenting convention for workarounds

rule: Place shared code in packages that reflect the owning module (e.g., domino.projects), not generic catch-all names like 'common', unless the code truly belongs in a shared common library.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for package organization

rule: After moving code out of a module, audit BUILD files to identify and remove dependencies that are no longer needed, reducing unnecessary coupling.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence reminder to clean up BUILD files after moves

rule: When removing an existence or duplication check as a workaround, explicitly verify and document that the underlying operation is idempotent so that repeated execution produces the same result without harmful side effects.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence safety check about idempotency verification

rule: When implementing status update logic that depends on message ordering, document the delivery guarantees (synchronous RPC vs async streams) and explain why timestamps are or are not needed to prevent out-of-order processing.
mode_rationale: Documentation convention for ordering assumptions

rule: Repeated string literals (e.g., operation names, status labels) should be extracted into named constants to avoid drift and improve maintainability.
mode_rationale: Simple refactoring convention for string literals

rule: When removing a feature or module, trace and remove all references including config bindings, injection modules, and test fixtures — don't leave hollowed-out shells.
mode_rationale: Multi-step procedure: trace references through config bindings, injection modules, and test fixtures

rule: When a class already owns 'handles this thing' logic, new filtering conditions should be added to that class via a consistent interface (e.g., a handlesEvent method) rather than spreading the logic across callers.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence design principle about encapsulation

rule: When adding a new public method or changing an interface (e.g., a new handlesEvent function), add corresponding unit tests — especially when fixing a bug that the test would catch in regression.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to add tests alongside new public methods

rule: When testing code that delegates to collaborators through mocks, add verify() calls to assert the expected mock method was actually invoked with the correct arguments.
mode_rationale: Simple testing convention about adding verify() calls

rule: Before adding new traces or instrumentation, evaluate the volume of events that will be generated and the cost implications in the observability platform (e.g., New Relic data ingest costs).
mode_rationale: Single-sentence reminder to evaluate cost before adding traces

rule: Avoid using async linking patterns (e.g., token get/expire) when the work is synchronous — use simpler constructs like transaction naming instead.
mode_rationale: Simple coding convention against unnecessary async patterns

rule: When referencing entity names (e.g., saga names, state names) in instrumentation or logging, derive them programmatically or use shared constants so they stay in sync if the source names change.
mode_rationale: Naming convention to derive identifiers programmatically

rule: Tests that guard assumptions about a library's internal behavior should live in that library's test suite, not in downstream consumers — this prevents shipping bugs when the library changes.
mode_rationale: Simple test organization convention

rule: When the same logic appears in multiple places, extract it into a shared utility or service method rather than duplicating it.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence DRY principle

rule: When refactoring makes existing types or code paths obsolete, delete them in the same PR rather than leaving unused code behind.
mode_rationale: Simple hygiene rule about removing obsolete code in the same PR

rule: Do not mix in a trait that is already included in the parent type's definition, as it adds noise without changing behavior.
mode_rationale: Simple Scala convention about redundant trait mixins

rule: Use the same filtering approach (inclusion vs exclusion) across related code branches to avoid confusion and reduce the chance of subtle bugs.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence consistency rule for filtering approaches

rule: When unpacking multiple Option values, use a for-comprehension instead of nested flatMap/map chains for clearer, more idiomatic Scala.
mode_rationale: Scala idiom preference, single-sentence style rule

rule: When using Option[List], document whether None and Some(List.empty) have different semantic meanings, or prefer using plain List with a default empty value if they are equivalent.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation convention for Option[List] semantics

rule: When changing URL routes or API endpoints, ensure backwards compatibility by implementing redirects from old routes and updating all dependent components.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence convention about redirects when changing routes

rule: When testable logic exists at the unit level, add unit tests rather than relying solely on frontend or end-to-end tests to catch regressions.
mode_rationale: Simple test pyramid preference guideline

rule: Use the same constant reference (alias or fully-qualified name) consistently across implementation and test code to avoid confusion about whether different names refer to the same value.
mode_rationale: Naming consistency convention for constant references

rule: When adding a configuration or feature for one entity type, evaluate whether the same capability should be extended to similar entity types for consistency.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence design consideration for consistency across entity types

rule: Code comments should not restate information that is already obvious from the surrounding context; keep comments concise and additive.
mode_rationale: Simple commenting convention to keep comments concise

rule: Do not expose internal implementation details (such as internal tag names, internal IDs, or system-level metadata) in public API documentation, as users may misuse or depend on them.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence API documentation rule

rule: Deprecation notices in API docs should use a consistent format: bold 'DEPRECATED' label, specify the version(s) where deprecation begins, and direct users to the replacement endpoint.
mode_rationale: Simple formatting convention for deprecation notices

rule: When a parameter represents a boolean flag, use an explicit Boolean type with a sensible default (e.g., `true` or `false`) rather than Option[Boolean] where None could be confused with the negative case.
mode_rationale: Simple type design convention for boolean parameters

rule: When combining multiple Option[Seq] values, use getOrElse(Seq()) to unwrap and concatenate, then convert back to Option based on emptiness, rather than nesting match/fold expressions.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence Scala idiom for combining Option[Seq] values

rule: Domain terms (e.g., Dataset, Workspace, Model API) should be capitalized consistently across all API specification files to avoid confusion.
mode_rationale: Naming consistency convention for domain terms in API specs

rule: When authorization or access-control logic involves non-obvious rules (e.g., role-based exemptions, cross-organization restrictions), add inline comments explaining the business rationale and who is affected.
mode_rationale: Simple commenting convention for non-obvious auth rules

rule: API parameter descriptions must accurately describe the filtering or sorting behavior of the implementation; a mismatch between 'or greater' vs 'or less' in a version filter description can cause users to pass incorrect values.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence accuracy rule for API parameter descriptions

rule: In build and CI scripts, check that required files (JARs, binaries, config files) exist before performing operations on them, and exit with a clear error message if missing.
mode_rationale: Simple defensive coding convention for CI scripts

rule: In CI scripts, use `mkdir -p` instead of `mkdir` to ensure directory creation is idempotent and won't fail if the directory already exists from a previous or retried run.
mode_rationale: Simple CI convention to use mkdir -p

rule: Extract repeated lookups on mutable collections into a single val to eliminate time-of-check-to-time-of-use inconsistencies under concurrent access.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence performance/safety rule about caching lookups

rule: When adding or modifying mutable state, explicitly analyze whether concurrent access paths exist and document the reasoning, even if you conclude synchronization is not needed.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to analyze and document concurrency reasoning

rule: Return the semantically correct HTTP status code for each operation — use 200 OK for retrievals/updates and 201 Created only when a new resource is actually created.
mode_rationale: Simple API convention for correct HTTP status codes

rule: Ensure APIs intended for external or customer use are exposed on the public API surface, not accidentally placed behind private/internal-only routes.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence API routing convention

rule: Sensitive configuration values such as signing keys, credentials, and tokens must be stored in vault or Kubernetes secrets, not in plaintext configuration files.
mode_rationale: Simple security convention for sensitive values

rule: Name audit events, permissions, and action identifiers to describe the action's scope rather than coupling them to the current role that performs them, to avoid renames when access policies change.
mode_rationale: Naming convention to decouple names from current role assignments

rule: When documenting external resources like cron jobs or infrastructure components in code comments, reference them by name rather than inlining their configuration details, which can silently drift out of date.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation convention to reference by name

rule: Operational troubleshooting details, deployment procedures, and system interaction workflows belong in runbooks or operational documentation, not embedded in source code comments.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence convention about where operational docs belong

rule: Any list endpoint exposed through a public or external API must support pagination to prevent unbounded result sets; internal-only methods may defer pagination until exposure.
mode_rationale: Simple API design rule requiring pagination on list endpoints

rule: Extract hardcoded values (ports, resource limits, service names) into central configuration to make them easily tunable and prevent them from becoming the root of operational issues.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence convention about extracting hardcoded values into config

rule: When leaving deprecated or temporary code with a TODO comment, always include a reference to the tracking ticket so the cleanup is discoverable and prioritized.
mode_rationale: Simple rule to include ticket references in TODO comments

rule: Do not hardcode ports or service names in configuration templates; use variables or constants so they can be changed in one place.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to avoid hardcoding ports and service names

rule: When performing division, verify that the divisor cannot be zero — even if current callers make it unlikely — to prevent runtime exceptions from unexpected inputs.
mode_rationale: Single defensive coding rule about checking divisors

rule: Use the project's established metrics library (e.g., DominoMetrics) for instrumentation rather than creating ad-hoc metric implementations, even if it requires extending the library for new label dimensions.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to use established metrics libraries

rule: Use the codebase's established naming convention for optional values (e.g., `maybeFoo` prefix) rather than inventing new abbreviations like `Opt` or `optional`.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for optional value prefixes

rule: Always URL-encode user-controlled or dynamic URI components in redirect paths and auth callbacks to prevent injection and malformed URLs.
mode_rationale: Simple security rule to URL-encode dynamic URI components

rule: Tests for infrastructure configuration (e.g., Kubernetes ingress annotations) should assert on actual generated values for security-relevant fields like auth-url and auth-signin, not just verify the keys exist.
mode_rationale: Testing convention to assert on actual values, not just key existence

rule: Use a boolean for binary on/off states rather than creating an enum with only two values; only introduce an enum when there are concrete plans for additional states beyond enabled/disabled.
mode_rationale: Simple API design guideline for binary state representation

rule: When exposing internal service endpoints (hosts, ports) to user-accessible environments like workspaces, explicitly evaluate and document the security implications before proceeding.
mode_rationale: Requires explicit evaluation and documentation steps before exposing endpoints

rule: Scope PRs to a single, bite-sized chunk of functionality (e.g., one endpoint plus its models, DTOs, and tests) rather than bundling multiple features into one large PR.
mode_rationale: Simple convention about PR scope

rule: Domain models should store only IDs for referenced entities; enriched objects with full details belong in the API DTO layer, not the core model.
mode_rationale: Architectural guideline about model layer separation

rule: Only create a separate DTO when there is a real structural difference from the existing model (e.g., a Create DTO that omits the server-generated ID); do not duplicate types that are identical or nearly identical.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to avoid redundant type definitions

rule: Do not add fields to a shared model that only apply to some variants; if not all instances of a type will have the field, it does not belong in the common model.
mode_rationale: Simple modeling rule about variant-specific fields

rule: Do not add API filtering, projections, or performance optimizations until all endpoints are implemented and real query patterns are known.
mode_rationale: Simple guideline to avoid premature optimization

rule: When an API endpoint performs a soft delete, explicitly document that behavior in the API specification so consumers understand the data is not permanently removed.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation rule for soft-delete behavior

rule: Prefer importing individual classes by name over wildcard imports when the number of imported members is small, for clarity.
mode_rationale: Simple import style convention

rule: Use debug or trace level logging for verbose or high-frequency log statements to avoid generating excessive noise in production logs.
mode_rationale: Simple logging level convention

rule: When catching and recovering from errors, always add logging so that caught exceptions are visible for debugging and monitoring.
mode_rationale: Simple rule to log caught exceptions

rule: When a method is used by multiple callers, keep it generic rather than embedding caller-specific concepts (like domain-specific labels) that tie it to a single use case.
mode_rationale: Simple design guideline about method generality

rule: When an authorization check fails or encounters an error, default to denying access (not authorized) rather than granting it.
mode_rationale: Simple security convention to deny on auth failure

rule: Name test classes to clearly indicate which specific implementation they are testing, so the purpose remains unambiguous when implementations change.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for test classes

rule: When a class needs specific implementations injected, prefer injecting the concrete types directly rather than using @Named annotations on the interface, reserving the interface binding for the facade that other consumers should use.
mode_rationale: Simple DI convention about injection style

rule: Name variables and parameters consistently with the class names they reference to maintain clarity and reduce cognitive overhead when reading code.
mode_rationale: Simple naming consistency rule

rule: When a trait or interface has multiple implementations, add a brief comment to each explaining its specific purpose and how it differs from the others.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation rule for interface implementations

rule: Name filter parameters to clearly indicate what entity or level they apply to, especially when multiple filters exist at different levels of a hierarchy.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for filter parameters

rule: Use domain-specific exception types (e.g., DocumentNotFoundException) from shared libraries instead of generic exceptions for clearer error semantics.
mode_rationale: Simple error handling convention about exception types

rule: Assign complex inline expressions (especially query filters and conditions) to descriptively named variables to improve readability.
mode_rationale: Single readability convention about naming complex expressions

rule: Abstract larger matching filters and complex query-building logic into named helper functions to improve readability and maintainability.
mode_rationale: Simple code organization rule about query abstraction

rule: Use parameter names that match the established API standard (e.g., Domino API Standard) rather than inventing new conventions, to maintain consistency across services.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to follow established API naming standards

rule: Define URL patterns in dedicated route objects (e.g., FrontendRoutes) instead of hardcoding URL strings inline, so routes are maintained in one place.
mode_rationale: Simple architectural rule about route definition location

rule: Catch domain-specific not-found exceptions (e.g., UnknownDataPlaneIdException) inside cache loaders to prevent one bad key from breaking the loader for other entries.
mode_rationale: Simple error handling rule for cache loader resilience

rule: Authentication and authorization failures should return 401/403, not 400; distinguish auth errors from input validation errors with appropriate HTTP status codes.
mode_rationale: Simple convention about correct HTTP status code usage

rule: When an enum type exists for a value, use the enum type rather than raw strings to get compile-time safety and prevent invalid values.
mode_rationale: Simple type safety convention

rule: When validating or fetching multiple entities, use a single bulk query (e.g., fetchReferences) instead of iterating and making N individual database calls.
mode_rationale: Simple performance convention about batch queries

rule: When code relies on a current authorization model (e.g., admin-only access), add a comment noting the assumption so future developers know what to revisit if permissions change.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation rule about noting auth assumptions

rule: Interface-level documentation (traits, abstract classes) should describe behavior abstractly without revealing storage or implementation details like specific database fields.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation convention about abstraction level

rule: When refactoring code that removes logging statements, add equivalent warn/debug logs in the new code path to maintain observability.
mode_rationale: Simple rule to maintain logging during refactors

rule: Move stateless methods and constants to the companion object rather than keeping them on the case class instance.
mode_rationale: Simple Scala convention about method placement

rule: Significant constants should live in dedicated parameter or config files, not inline in generator or builder classes.
mode_rationale: Simple code organization rule about constant placement

rule: When invoking external tools like sed or shell commands, run them in a separate step, validate the result is meaningful, and handle exceptions with clear error messages.
mode_rationale: Simple error handling rule for external command invocation

rule: When multiple code sections share similar logic, extract a reusable function or add a method to the relevant domain class to reduce repetition.
mode_rationale: Simple DRY convention about shared logic extraction

rule: When the same logic is repeated across call sites, encapsulate it as a method on the owning domain class rather than duplicating it inline.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to encapsulate repeated logic in domain methods

rule: For partial updates (PATCH), use incremental/atomic database update operations instead of read-modify-write cycles, which introduce race conditions.
mode_rationale: Simple convention about atomic PATCH operations

rule: Update tests should modify a value, then assert that only the targeted field changed and all other fields remain unchanged.
mode_rationale: Simple testing convention for update assertions

rule: During code review, verify that all new declarations, imports, and parameters are actually used; remove any that are not.
mode_rationale: Simple cleanup rule to remove dead code

rule: Routine operational log lines (e.g., indexing progress, normal flow) should use debug level, not info, to avoid noisy production logs.
mode_rationale: Simple logging level convention for routine operations

rule: When processing all entities (e.g., for indexing or batch operations), use lazy/streaming collections instead of loading everything into memory at once to avoid OOM issues at scale.
mode_rationale: Simple performance convention about lazy streaming

rule: When indexing entities, handle the case where the entity has been archived or deleted — clean up the index entry instead of throwing an exception.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to clean up index entries for removed entities

rule: Replace tuples with named case classes when passing multiple related values together, especially at call sites where the meaning of each position is unclear.
mode_rationale: Simple Scala convention to use named types over positional tuples

rule: Record telemetry and audit trail events after the operation succeeds, not before, to avoid logging events for operations that actually failed.
mode_rationale: Single timing rule for when to log telemetry events

rule: Audit trail events should be scoped to intentional user actions, not fired for every internal system call that happens to share a code path — verify the intended scope and consider performance of event fan-out.
mode_rationale: Single scoping principle for audit event emission

rule: Use consistent separators and delimiters within related data structures — mixing semicolons and commas in the same context signals unintentional inconsistency.
mode_rationale: Simple consistency convention for separators

rule: Add logging in catch-all/default match branches so unexpected states are visible during troubleshooting rather than silently ignored.
mode_rationale: Single rule to add logging in default/catch-all branches

rule: When wiring mocks in tests, add verification that the mocked methods are actually called with expected arguments — otherwise the mock only prevents crashes without validating behavior.
mode_rationale: Single testing convention to verify mock call arguments

rule: Disabled states and error messages shown to users should explain both why the state exists and what the user can do to resolve it, so they can self-serve without contacting support.
mode_rationale: Single UX convention for disabled state messaging

rule: TODO comments in code should reference a tracking ticket or documented deprecation plan so the work is discoverable and prioritizable.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation convention for TODO comments

rule: Use type-based pattern matching (e.g., matching on AnonymousPrincipal) instead of checking whether optional fields are defined, to make the intent clearer and leverage the type system.
mode_rationale: Single coding style preference for type-based matching

rule: When a function should only handle specific types or cases, explicitly restrict and fail on unexpected inputs rather than silently returning nothing.
mode_rationale: Single defensive coding principle

rule: Error messages that may be shown in the UI should be clear and actionable for end users, not just developer-oriented technical messages.
mode_rationale: Single convention for UI-facing error message tone

rule: Avoid fetching all records and sorting/filtering in application memory; push sorting, limiting, and pagination logic into database queries to prevent performance degradation as data grows.
mode_rationale: Single performance principle about query placement

rule: When loading related entities for a list of items, use batch queries or database-side joins instead of issuing one query per item (N+1 pattern).
mode_rationale: Single performance anti-pattern to avoid

rule: When fields are required for an operation but optional in the data model, declare them as non-optional in the operation's interface and validate their presence at the boundary, throwing if required fields are missing.
mode_rationale: Single type design convention for required fields

rule: When code relies on an assumption that may change in the future (e.g., only one active instance), add a comment documenting the assumption and what would need to change.
mode_rationale: Single documentation convention for fragile assumptions

rule: When a required invariant is violated (e.g., a commit ID cannot be resolved), throw an error rather than logging a warning and continuing, to surface the problem immediately.
mode_rationale: Single error-handling principle to throw instead of warn

rule: When deriving data from related entities, always consider the case where the relationship doesn't exist yet (e.g., no instances created) and implement explicit fallback behavior.
mode_rationale: Single defensive coding principle for missing relationships

rule: When new behavior is introduced (e.g., allowing an action that was previously disallowed), add a test that specifically codifies the new allowed behavior.
mode_rationale: Single testing convention to codify new allowed behaviors

rule: When a parameter is not used by a method's implementation, remove it from the signature all the way through the call chain rather than leaving dead parameters.
mode_rationale: Simple cleanup convention for dead parameters

rule: When creating new code paths that replace or parallel existing ones, audit the original path for validation steps (guardrails, permission checks) and ensure they are carried over.
mode_rationale: Single refactoring principle to carry over validation

rule: All fields that are resolved at runtime (e.g., from project defaults) must be explicitly resolved and persisted before launching to ensure reproducibility.
mode_rationale: Single architecture rule for reproducibility of runtime values

rule: Do not add validation checks that do not affect correctness — they add maintenance burden and complexity without preventing real bugs.
mode_rationale: Single principle against unnecessary validation checks

rule: API documentation should not reference current implementation details (like specific upsert keys) that may change as new types or features are added.
mode_rationale: Single documentation convention for API docs

rule: API endpoint descriptions should describe the semantic behavior uniformly (e.g., 'absent fields are left unchanged') rather than calling out individual field behaviors that follow the same rule.
mode_rationale: Single documentation style rule for uniform descriptions

rule: Logic that is only used in one place should be inlined rather than extracted into separate helper methods, to keep changes smaller and code easier to follow.
mode_rationale: Single code organization principle against premature extraction

rule: Feature flag names should not include layer-specific prefixes (e.g., 'ui') because the flag will likely be consumed by multiple layers.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for feature flags

rule: Do not write tests that don't assert meaningful behavior; if the test scenario cannot actually occur given the API contract, the test is unnecessary.
mode_rationale: Single testing hygiene rule

rule: Actor system instances should register shutdown hooks to allow clean and graceful shutdowns, even if not needed during normal operation.
mode_rationale: Single infrastructure convention for actor systems

rule: Internal implementation fields (like API invokers and client instances) should be declared private to prevent unintended external access.
mode_rationale: Single encapsulation principle for internal fields

rule: Expensive operations (feature flag lookups, external service calls) should be guarded behind early-return checks for empty input to avoid unnecessary work.
mode_rationale: Single performance convention for early returns

rule: When API schema marks fields as required, mapping None values to empty strings can confuse consumers who expect meaningful values; use proper Optional types or ensure values are present.
mode_rationale: Single API design rule against empty-string placeholders

rule: When a dependency can throw and is caught with NonFatal, write a test verifying the caller degrades gracefully (e.g., returns a default or partial result) rather than propagating the failure.
mode_rationale: Single testing convention for graceful degradation

rule: When an optional collection field has different semantic meanings for absence (None = unknown/legacy) vs empty (Some(empty) = explicitly none), preserve that distinction rather than collapsing both to None.
mode_rationale: Single type design principle for optional collections

rule: When calling external services or APIs that can return null in edge cases (e.g., a repo with no commits), handle the null case defensively, especially if the method may be reused in broader contexts.
mode_rationale: Single defensive coding rule for external API nulls

rule: Add warning-level logging for code paths that are believed to be unreachable, especially when the code is reused across contexts where the assumption may not hold.
mode_rationale: Single logging convention for unreachable paths

rule: When a code path is believed to be impossible, throw an exception rather than silently skipping or returning a default value, so violations of assumptions surface immediately.
mode_rationale: Single error-handling principle for impossible states

rule: Documentation in an interface module should not reference implementation-layer concepts or types from other modules; move implementation-specific edge case descriptions closer to where those details are resolved.
mode_rationale: Single documentation scoping principle

rule: Docstrings in interface/public-API layers should describe contracts and behavior, not reference concrete implementation classes or execution-layer specifics that the interface has no knowledge of.
mode_rationale: Single documentation convention for interface layers

rule: When an API parameter is optional, the API description must specify the default behavior when the parameter is omitted.
mode_rationale: Single API documentation rule for optional parameters

rule: When a behavior depends on integration between multiple systems (e.g., browser requests through middleware), acknowledge unit test limitations and ensure an integration or e2e test ticket exists rather than forcing the assertion into the wrong test layer.
mode_rationale: Single testing principle about choosing the right test layer

rule: In tests, assert against directly known values (e.g., a specific entity's field) rather than values derived through the same code path being tested, which can mask regressions if those paths diverge.
mode_rationale: Single testing convention for assertion targets

rule: Before fixing a long-standing API bug, assess whether any consumers may have built workarounds that depend on the buggy behavior, and plan for a safe migration path.
mode_rationale: Single API change management principle

rule: When extracting values from a map or collection for use in a batch lookup or query, deduplicate them first to avoid redundant processing, even if each individual operation is inexpensive.
mode_rationale: Single performance convention for batch lookups

rule: When a downstream service call can both fail and legitimately return empty results, only log warnings for actual failures — not for valid empty responses — to avoid noisy or misleading logs.
mode_rationale: Single logging convention to reduce noise from valid empty responses

rule: When passing a dynamically-sized list of IDs to a database query (e.g., Mongo $in), verify that the list is bounded, the target field is indexed, and the query document stays within size limits.
mode_rationale: Single performance rule for bounded dynamic query inputs

rule: Always include human-readable names (not just IDs) in audit event payloads for readability, even if it requires an additional DB query.
mode_rationale: Single convention about including human-readable names in audit events

rule: When adding a new API endpoint in one service, evaluate whether a corresponding endpoint needs to be created in related services that expose similar resources to maintain API parity.
mode_rationale: Single API design principle for service consistency

rule: When accessing a field assumed to always be present (e.g., via .get), verify and document that the field is guaranteed to exist; otherwise handle the missing case to avoid runtime exceptions.
mode_rationale: Single defensive coding rule for assumed-present fields

rule: Do not overload status enums with derived or computed conditions (e.g., 'expiringSoon'); instead, use a separate boolean field alongside the canonical status to avoid confusion in filtering and API responses.
mode_rationale: Single API design rule against overloading status enums

rule: When adding new query parameters or feature inputs, update related existing test specs to exercise the new parameters rather than only testing them in isolation.
mode_rationale: Single testing convention to update existing test coverage
